text: The fish wasn't cooked, it took very long to get seated and to get food. To many greeters not enough waiters.
Great food! Was amazed at how simple it looked inside but had A1 service and the most delicious breakfast ever!!!

I'm a frequent customer at Fixin's, and every single time my experience has been amazing. I usually order the oxtails, chicken and waffles, and a Kool-Aid (unless I'm grabbing a drink from the bar), and the food is always sooo good--honestly a 10/10 every time.

The service is consistently great--super welcoming and friendly, which makes the whole experience even better. I do recommend making a reservation so you can get seated quickly.

I love bringing my friends and family here, and they always end up loving it too. The place is super cute, both inside and outside. Definitely one of my go-to spots!

I really wanted to like this place because the vibe and music were great but unfortunately the service and happy hour confusion made the experience disappointing. Stopped by on a Friday after work with a group of about 9 people to celebrate a birthday and catch happy hour. We arrived around 2:45pm and waited until 3pm to order since happy hour starts then. The restaurant wasn't very busy at first but did start filling up shortly after. The outdoor seating and R&B music were nice, good vibes. 

Most of our table ordered from the happy hour menu. I ordered the blue raspberry margarita, a shot of El Jimador, and the mac and cheese balls. Interestingly the food came out before the drinks and we were almost finished eating before the first round of drinks finally arrived. The mac and cheese balls were good but nothing memorable. The blue raspberry margarita looked nice but the flavor was just okay. Others at our table had similar comments about the food, sliders were good but nothing that really stood out.
Service was unfortunately the biggest issue. Our server seemed overwhelmed and it was difficult to get their attention throughout the visit. Drinks took a long time both rounds and we had to signal a few times just to get assistance which started to throw off the mood for the table.

The biggest surprise came when the bill arrived. The website lists $6 margaritas during happy hour which is one of the reasons we waited until 3pm to order. However the margaritas on our bill were $12. If certain margaritas are excluded from the special that should probably be clarified because it was confusing and felt misleading.
With a party our size gratuity was automatically added and the bill ended up being over $400 which made the slow service even more noticeable. The atmosphere and music were nice and the food was decent but the slow service and unclear happy hour pricing made this a disappointing experience for our group.

Tip for future guests: If you're going for happy hour drinks it might be worth asking which ones actually qualify before ordering!

Went to Fixins to celebrate another year around the sun for our main man Matt. I love my motley crew and felt elated when Matt said he wanted soul food from Fixins for his birthday dinner with the gang. The ambiance is lively, cool, and comfortable. Everyone feels like family and the service is top notch. If you have any questions the staff will answer with a smile and you can tell they take pride in what they do. 

5/5  - The deviled eggs were savory with a perfect amount of seasoning. 

5/5 - Smothered Chicken W/ sides of greens, potato salad, and white rice. The chicken breast is succulent and juicy. The ancestors hands were guiding the chef as they seasoned the chicken cause the gravy-&-crispyskin combo were heavenly. It reminded me of my Mom's perfectly seasoned fried chicken, so kudos to the chef over there. 

*Chicken and Waffles 3/5 - This is only referring to a To-Go order I had for this item. It's placed in a package that has ventilation holes. You puncture the holes to keep air ventilation so the waffle does not get soggy and the chicken stays crisp. They did not puncture the holes and my food arrived steamed with a soggy waffle. 

5/5 The Patron tower was a highlight! The drinks were delicious and the bartender def showed love. 

Overall, I hope they continue to cook with love and serve with care. If so, they will continue to have a customer of me.

I've visited Fixins a few times now, and each time our experience has been consistent and the food has been delicious.

Our latest visit was on a Saturday night, date night with my husband and we put in a reservation ahead of time. I'm glad we did because there were a bunch of people outside waiting for a table. Upon entering they were ready to seat us right away at one of the four top booths- but we spotted a couple open seats at the bar and offered to sit there instead so others could take the big table.

Our server/bartender was friendly and we ended up ordering purple koolaid ($5) (their sugar to flavor ratio is perfect). It's a LARGE glass, and probably could be shared. 

We also ordered the Oxtails ($30) and the three piece fried chicken ($23) to share and knew we'd have leftovers.

Their oxtails are fall off the bone and really flavorful in the gravy. The chicken is perfectly crispy and they give you a choice of dark or white meat- we opted for dark because it's more flavorful- and it was cooked perfectly. We both chose okra for our sides, it's not something you see everywhere and Fixins does a great job.

The restaurant was busy and buzzing with energy. There is indoor and outdoor seating. If you're looking for something close by while you wait for a table there are great shops like Strapping to pop into. There's also plenty of street parking in the area.

Fixin's Soul Food has the best Soul food of any we have ever had. It is always fresh and wonderful. They have a great menu so you can go a few times without repeating, but some things you just must have.

They have great fried chicken and the best greens and mac and cheese and their pork chops smothered are wonderful and I am very picky about smothered pork chops. They can be dry and over cooked and tuff. These are perfect. I mean they are just perfect.  Their corn bread is great. If you can't finish it, don't be shy, ask for a bag and they will give you one. They heat up so great.

The service is the best. Now, it takes a minute for them to get your food. That is because it is not laying around in bin somewhere, they have to make it. They never rush you. The place is so clean and cute with great art on the walls.

I don't know if they take reservations, but it can and will get crowded by the line moves pretty fast. Have a the best time

I highly recommend that you make a reservation for this restaurant, because it was very busy. The decor is funky and cute. The service was very friendly. I thoroughly enjoyed the oxtails. They were cooked perfectly, and the gravy was flavorful. The greens were delicious too. The peach cobbler was a little too sweet for my taste. But, I enthusiastically recommend this restaurant.

We happened to be in town so we decided to try this spot as the pictures on here looked really good. We were able to get seated fairly quickly although after we arrived, I noticed a line started to form. 

I will start off by saying that this place is really loud. If you are looking for somewhere that is quiet. This is definitely not the spot for you. You can hardly hear yourself over everyone else's conversation.

We got their hot chicken sandwich as well as the fried chicken, macaroni and french fries. I will say the macaroni was really good. The fried chicken in my opinion lacked a little bit of flavor. The biscuit was also really good as well. 

Service was ok. Our server really didn't talk to us at all. Basically our whole time there we had almost 0 to no interaction with her apart from when we placed our order. 

She also originally gave us straws for our water and then she took one of the straws back, which was a bit weird. The whole time there, she would just come around periodically to refill our water cups, but nothing more was really said. 

Honestly, I'm not sure if I'd be back. The service didn't really feel like you were in a restaurant.

We had a really long wait time for decent enough food, don't think I'll come back.

We got there at around 1:30 on a Sunday during the summer and waited around 40 minutes to be seated. We ended up sitting on a long, shared table with others parties next to us.
For a place supposedly about ambiance, I wasn't much pleased.

 The mains were nice, but I didnt care but for the sides. Mac tasted cold, slaw was average, collard greens were fine. 

I've heard others say it can be hit or miss, maybe it was just a miss that day?

Really felt that Southern love at Fixins! I ordered the shrimp and grits and adults kool-aid and it was the perfect meal after a long day. The roux was really good and complemented the grits. I also thought the texture of the grits was perfect -- creamy, velvety, thick, yet you can still taste the grains. The staff were welcoming and our server made sure our table was taken care of. Everything from their menu sounded delicious, we'll definitely be back to try other food items!

First and foremost the restaurant is clean and the staff were nice, they had really good attitudes in my opinion and thats always a plus for me. Unfortunately I have to rate this restaurant a 2 star, and those stars go to the staff, there the only reason this is not a zero star review. I had heard good things about the food but I was not impressed not one bit. We had the Ox Tail plate with rice gravy and greens. The oxtail was fork tender but lacked that good seasoning and spice, it actually had a sweet flavor that I was not expecting. The greens were good and I would order them again. I also ordered the fried fish plate, I was told it was Catfish (Its not Catfish), with Mac and cheese and potato. The fish was ok but the Mac was best thing on the plate. Just to wrap this up, fixins may have been fire at some point but they did not give there best  performance today.

It saddens me to give Fixins 3 stars. They are more deserving of 3.5, yet not quite a 4 for me. The food was solid, but not a standout. 

I pulled up on a busy Sunday and thankfully there was a seat at the bar. To drink, I ordered the Uncle Nearest Strawberry Cooler and to nibble on, I went with the fish nuggets and fried green tomatoes. The fish itself was mild in flavor and the coating was seasoned well. The tomatoes were overly seasoned in my opinion. This is just a personal preference, the seasoning was similar to what you would find on a fried fish. This is a great option for vegetarians.

The service was good, although the wrong order was put in for me and I had to wait some time for my fish nuggets. Meh, mistakes happen. I may go back to try something different because my overall meal was one note, although good, but not great.

Really cozy interior paired with great service and good food! Made a reservation for NYE lunch but came in a bit early and was able to be seated. There's a bar inside too, so that's nice if you want to grab a drink!

I ordered the smothered chicken, and the server mentioned getting it with boneless chicken thigh. They were HUGE! It came with two pieces, and each piece was the size of an adult hand and also fairly thick. Smothered chicken comes with two sides, so I opted for collard greens and Mac and cheese. Collard greens were salted well, and the Mac and cheese was on the thicker side (just how I like it). 

Didn't get to try dessert but would gladly come back for the great service, taste, and portion size.

I was genuinely thrilled to sample some food. I was particularly eager to taste that crispy chicken that they showcased on social media, along with all the positive reviews from others. 

The atmosphere felt clean and inviting, accompanied by music. We arrived early in the morning when they opened, and after about 15 to 20 minutes of waiting, we received our food. 

The waffle was decent but not crispy; it was soft instead. I had hoped the waffle would be crispy on the outside, but it came with all the toppings like butter and syrup. When the chicken was served, I was really looking forward to trying it. 

The chicken was quite flavorful, but it didn't have the crispiness I had envisioned. If the chicken had been a bit crispy on the outside, that would have been fantastic, but it turned out to be soggy. 

The sides of sweet potatoes or candied yams were delicious, although they seemed to have a lot of butter, as there was oil pooling in the bowl. 

This The greens were also very tasty, along with their mac & cheese.

Fixins Soul Kitchen brings the comfort, flavor, and energy you want in a true soul food experience. From the moment you walk in, the vibe is lively -- great music, friendly staff, and the smell of fried chicken and waffles in the air.

The food? Outstanding. The fried chicken is perfectly crispy and juicy, the mac and cheese is creamy with that baked crust on top, and the collard greens hit that perfect balance of smoky and savory. The cornbread is a must -- slightly sweet, soft inside, and served warm. Portion sizes are generous, and everything tastes made from scratch.

Service is upbeat and professional even when it's busy -- and it does get busy. The staff keeps the energy positive and makes sure everyone feels welcome. The décor gives off a modern twist on Southern comfort -- clean, bright, and full of character that reflects Oak Park's community spirit.

It's the kind of place you come for good food but stay for the vibe. Whether it's brunch, dinner, or a casual weekend stop, Fixins delivers the flavor and warmth that makes soul food special.

Early Weekend lunch with Bae @ Fixins is always a relaxing vibe. We've been coming here every now and then since they opened in 2019. I love the music and friendly atmosphere. The wait staff is always very pleasant and sometimes you'll run into other celebrities at this celebrity owned establishment. Kevin Johnson opened his first Fixins Soul Kitchen in the heart of Sacramento, where he resides in Oak Park. As the former mayor of Sacramento, an All-Star NBA player and fellow Sac High Dragon, Kevin Johnson opened Fixins to Operate Around 5 Core Values: Delicious Food, On-Point Service, Spotless Stores, Tight Operations and Community Love.

My go/to "lil bits" is the cornmeal fried green tomatoes with a smoky chipotle mayo, very fresh and tasty and the perfect starter to any meal. And the star of the show is the golden fried chicken which is brined in a buttermilk marinade for 24Hours. The fried chicken is so crispy and juicy! The best fried chicken in Sacramento. You can also get it smothered in gravy if you prefer. The two sides that come with the fried chicken are called "Fixins" and there are plenty to choose from. Such as collard greens, Mac n cheese, black eyed peas, potato salad, French fries candied yams, Cole slaw, fried okra and grits. The classic buttermilk biscuits are also incredible. We Complimented our meal with an Adult Kool Aid, an alcoholic beverage made with vodka for an extra kick!

With delicious family recipes and boozy kool aid cocktails, this trendy restaurant is definitely top 10 spots in Sacramento, stop by and support local.

I've visited many times and initially I wasn't a fan but I've officially been won over. 

This visit I made a reservation to avoid long wait times, and it was successful. My reservation was at 12 p.m. and I was seated by 12:05 p.m. 

I ordered fried chicken, with red beans and rice and greens. The food was delicious.

Everyone was really friendly!  The Mac and cheese was perhaps the best I've ever had in a restaurant!!

The fried green tomatoes were beautiful to look at and average tasting - not tangy enough tomatoes and the breading and other flavors were just meh.  

The banana pudding is way over priced.  It was fine, but a small serving and $11.  I should have tried the red velvet cake instead.

$45 for iced tea, a side of Mac and cheese, fried green tomato app, banana pudding, and tip.  Unfortunately, it's too rich for my blood.

Really really good chicken, fish, Mac n cheese, shrimp, collard greens, etc etc. the staff is so helpful and friendly, service is fast, it's kid and family friendly. Huge sodas which I love. My baby had fun, my family had fun

Second trip and it was juuuuuust as good as I remembered. First time I got fried chicken with 3 sides (macaroni and cheese, candied yams, & greens) and it was amazing. We stumbled on Fixins during the Sunday Bri ch rush and got lucky with a bar seat. The service was great, the drinks were expensive but tasty. 

Today we brought our kids and tried the fried fish plate. Dammmnmnnnn that was good! 

I could make a meal out of the sides alone. 

Everything is delicious. The hype is real. Just go and enjoy every calorie.

We've ordered from here more than one time now and the flavor were consisted, rich oxtail stew with nice sauce or juice from the meat. Uhmm so mouth watering. The sides were good too. Fast and quick service even if it's a To Go order. The staff were friendly with a welcoming environment. We'll definitely be coming back with family and friends next time.
The shrimp and grits and the fried chicken were 10/10. Our server Kim was so amazing!!

I think this is a great staple for breakfast, lunch, and dinner in the Oak Park area. Been here a few times, but I would like to review my most recent breakfast experience. Overall, I've always enjoyed my experience here. The blue plate special was great. I always scramble my eggs, but I appreciated the handmade turkey sausage. The chicken and waffles was awesome. I appreciate that the chicken breast was not overcooked. It was juicy, while the skin was flaky and crispy. Bette than your standard  Popeyes or KFC. I forgot to ask what type of butter they had on the waffle, but it was phenomenal.

The service is great. I find that everyone is very friendly. It is also pretty quick if I may say. However, there can be a line for dinner and for brunch on Sundays so be prepared to wait.

It has a pretty modern aesthetic inside. There's a modern meets rustic vibe going on. However, my favorite place is outside. I think they have a lovely patio. It is spacious, ample greenery, and heat lamps if you need it. Love that they always have sports on. For my hip-hop and R&B lovers, there is always a great playlist. Always. 

Have I had better southern food? Yes, which is why I can't give it the 5, but this place is worth revisiting and is very quality.

3/3/26 update - made it back with my mom and still so so delicious and amazing service from Terri! Tonight also had fried deviled eggs, fried green tomatoes, collared greens and coleslaw. The bathrooms were so clean it was as though no one had used them.  And I was able to wish the man himself a Happy Birthday! :) 
Finally made it! Yayyyy 
Everything was amazing...one server, not our server, never smiled, maybe not having a great day, but we still smiled. 
Our server was a sweetheart! Everyone was, except the one. Hope their day got better (when they got off work :)).  I love the pictures in the restaurant and the history from Kevin was great to read.  Even the bathrooms were clean and well maintained! 
The food was as amazing as I've been told. Can't wait to take my mom when she recovers from her surgery and my son in law when he is back home. Thank you Fixins for a fabulous visit! See you soon!

Right in the heart of Oak Park southern style comfort food and made fresh! The Mac and cheese was flavorful and loaded with cheese. I also had the oxtails and the portions were generous. My only con was they were a tad bit sweet for my taste as I prefer a more savory and spicy kind of oxtails but overall not bad. 

We had a group of 6 or so and they can only seat you if half of the group is there. If you have to wait you can always snag a drink at the bar while waiting. 

I highly recommend making a reservation for the weekends because it is very busy and the wait for walking in can be long.

Came to Fixins during Black History Month and it felt like the perfect time to support and experience it. The vibe was warm, lively, and full of culture.

I ordered the hot chicken sandwich and I'm not going to lie, it was way spicier than I expected. But in a good way. It had real heat, not just flavorless spice. Crispy, juicy, and definitely not playing around. If you're sensitive to spice, just know they mean it when they say "hot."

Shoutout to our server, Isabella. She was kind, attentive, and genuinely awesome. She checked in at the right times and made sure we had everything we needed without hovering.

Overall, great food, strong flavors, and great service. I'll definitely be back -- maybe next time I'll mentally prepare for that heat.

The best southern food you can get! I think we ordered half the menu and everything was amazing! Great service and atmosphere. We were sat in the back room area which was great bc we had someone with us hard of hearing and it was much quieter back there.

Came here for dinner w my husband on a Friday night around 6:30pm. We were seated right away and our waitress took our appetizer order shortly after. 

The restaurant is pretty spacious. We ordered fried green tomatoes, oxtail + Mac n cheese, and 2 piece fried chicken (thighs), + collard greens + candied yams. 

I can see why they call themselves fixins soul kitchen. Their fixins are rly good!! I personally think the ox tail was ok, but the fried chicken was yummy. I loved the biscuit and butter it came w. And I loved the fried green tomatoes. 

The food was suuuper heavy haha but if you're craving soul food, give this place a try!

Updating my previous review from a year ago. I dined in at Fixins Soul Kitchen on a weekday for lunch and I had a great experience and can't wait to return again soon! 

So first, my mom and I received amazing customer service! From the hostess to Aunt Pearl stopping by to say hello and then to our server, Kim. Everyone was welcoming and friendly! I had a sweet tea to drink and it was so good! We ordered two fried fish plates and everything was absolutely delicious! Hot and fresh and didn't take long to come out to our table. You also get large portion sizes and will likely have some to take home. 

Overall, I'm so happy to have supported this black owned restaurant again! I highly recommend if you're looking for soul food in Sacramento. Can't wait to bring more out of town family and friends to Fixins!

The vibe of this place is so cute and the restaurant layout and decorations itself is very homey. 

I ordered the 2 piece chicken, I love that you can choose what part of the chicken you want. I got a side of Mac n cheese and fries. Overall the meal is very tasty, I think it might be a little salty for my preferences though. The biscuit with the sweet butter was a good way to offset the saltiness. 

Service was friendly and food came out quick

Came in on a Monday about 11:00.  I was the second customer of the day.  I ordered the oxtails with a side of red beans & rice.  The oxtails came with a delicious gravy and rice.  I had oxtails only one other time in a risotto.  I was surprised that the oxtails had the texture of pot roast, it was good.  The decor is really nice, service was excellent.

I came to Fixin's on a Thursday evening around 5 PM, and my party of two got seated right away, great start. There's parking out front, but it's on a busy street strip, and the restaurant is tucked in with several other stores, which made it look smaller from the outside. However, once inside, it's surprisingly spacious with a bar area and outdoor seating as well.

We ordered from the happy hour menu, the mac & cheese balls and beef sliders ($8 each), plus a side of French fries, their biscuits (which come with a strawberry or raspberry butter that's delicious), and the candied yams. The yams were buttery, soft, and flavorful, though they don't come with marshmallows on top, just a heads up if you're expecting that classic topping.

The mac & cheese balls came with four pieces and a cheese sauce. They were really flavorful, bite-sized (about two bites each), and I only wish they were a bit bigger. The fries were seasoned perfectly, crispy on the outside and soft inside. The sliders were solid, simple and tasty, served with pickles on the side along with mustard and ketchup.

Overall, I really enjoyed my visit. The food was a hit, the service was quick, and the vibe was great. I definitely plan on coming back to try more items!

3.5 stars. Good music, decent food, and great service! Our server Alondra was attentive and friendly. She was there when we needed her but she wasn't a nuisance. We would come back for her service alone.

We tried the fried deviled eggs and they were interesting. A great dipping sauce would send those over the top. 

Our entrees were the oxtails and gumbo. Oxtails were plentiful, and both tender and chewy. They came with blackeyed peas that stole the show. The gumbo came with the best corn bread muffin ever! I chose to add shrimp to the gumbo but it wasn't cooked with the dish so the gumbo didn't have that gumbo (filet), seafood flavor. It had a nice roux however and even contained okra. All food was plated nicely, arrived quickly, and was hot.

For dessert we had red velvet cake and peach cobbler. The cobbler was the bomb the next day. Put it in the microwave and loved it. The cake was stale, and had a bit too much salt but the frosting tasted good.

We took Mrs. Ruth's Plate to go. It consisted of 3 sides. We chose red beans & rice, macaroni & cheese, and yams. The sides were small but were pretty tasty! 

Overall we would give them another chance if in the area.

.

This place has amazing service, amazing lemonade but the best chicken and waffles we've ever tasted!!! Super good. A must try Sacramento spot. The only downside is parking, but that's not the restaurants fault. Parking is not so easy to find.

Food was good. We ordered the fried chicken and some sides and the chicken and waffles. The sides tasted great. The fried chicken was cooked well. The taste of the fried chicken was salty for my palate. It was edible but not my preferred fried chicken taste. I look forward to trying other items on the menu. 

The service, from my experience needs improvement. Our server J@€ was not friendly. He looked irritated from the moment he introduced himself, when he took our orders, and until we left. He wasn't disrespectful but no smiles nor welcoming attitude. I'm not sure if he was having a bad day or something else. It looked like he did not want to be there. The whole experience made our group uncomfortable. 

The ambiance was nice. There were booths and table seating as well as a choice of indoor or outdoor. The restroom was clean and well maintained. 

Will I be back? Probably not.

This was our 1st time eating here. The service was great, very quick. 
We started with an order of fried deviled eggs, she ordered the gumbo, I ordered oxtails with a side of collard greens, we shared a side of Mac-n-cheese. 
We washed it all down with Arnold Palmer's made with sweet tea. 
In total our order came up to $94.62
Next time we return we want to try their chicken and waffles. The chicken looked really good as we saw it coming out the kitchen. 
The owner had a beautiful collection of family photos and the story of their journey to Sacramento on the wall. 
Support Black business!
Get to Fixins!!

It is incredibly hard locating a good soul place that we enjoy but thankfully we stumbled across Fixins. This place is great! Good flavors, everything is well seasoned. The only negative at times are the wait times but if you can make it happen on a week day then it shouldn't be too bad. Definitely worth trying if you haven't! Pictured is their Shrimp N Grits.

My daughter has been trying to get me to try this place. Tonight before the Kings game we decided to go. She had the smothered chicken with mac & cheese and I had the smothered pork chop with mac & cheese. Must say they were both delicious. I'm going back to try their gumbo next! We got there about 5 o'clock and they were packed already, but good thing we had reservations. Service was great!

Super nice staff! Loved the family story in the hallway! Our group of 7 had fried deviled eggs, corn muffin, kids Mac n cheese and chicken strips, fried fish plate with red beans and rice, coleslaw, potato salad, shrimp and grits, and the chicken sandwich. Everything was phenomenal! I see why it was packed! Pro tip, get a reservation if you don't want to wait. Once we were seated, drinks and food came out quickly. 

Highly recommend! Can't wait to try again.

This restaurant is such a gem! I wish I would have ate at the restaurant but I was just prepared to grab my food and go to the hotel. The inside and outside seating is so cute.

I'm thankful this food was delicious because I sure had one heck of a time getting my food...nothing that the restaurant did wrong. Since I'm not from the area I wasn't familiar with the location. When I saw on my GPS I was about 20 minutes out, I ordered my food. Well when I got there, parking was so hard to find and I had luggage in my car so I didn't want to risk not parking close and coming back to a smash and grab. So I decided to head to the hotel, check in, and come back. Well my work phone was set to "avoid highways" so it took me some ridiculous way that took forever. After checking in, I made my way back and got my food. 

I was greeted by an employee who just handed a to go order to someone else. I told him my name and order number and he got my bag. He even walked me to the door to open the door for me. I didn't catch his name but thank you! Customer service like that will keep people coming back!

I couldn't wait to dig into my food although I knew it wasn't going to be hot. I was prepared to have to heat it up in the microwave but to my surprise it was still warm.

I ordered the three piece chicken meal that comes with a biscuit: breast, thigh, leg and my sides were Mac and cheese and candied yams. I had two different kind of butters. The fried chicken had great flavor and was crispy, candied yams were delicious and Mac and cheese was good but not my favorite. The meal was a perfect ending to my chaotic Sunday!

Made a reservation to have dinner on a Saturday evening. We got there and checked in and they seated us right at our reservation time. It was pretty busy. Nice vibe and good R & B music playing. Rollin was our server he provided good service. We had fried green tomatoes my first time trying them and they were delicious. We also had the pimento cheese dip which was good. I had the oxtail and rice with collard greens they were very good. The oxtails were very tender. My husband had the fried chicken with red beans & rice and mac and cheese. He loved it and I tried it and it was delicious.  My son had the fried catfish with mac and cheese and fries he enjoyed it and I tried to too and it was very good. My other son had the chicken sandwich with mac and cheese and fries and he really enjoyed it. We tried the peach cobbler and banana pudding for dessert they were both very good. I would like to come back and try more items.
The best fried chicken and catfish in town! We always have a good time here. Great place for some southern comfort food!

I think this place is slightly overhyped and their food is inconsistent. The fried chicken is outstanding, but what's a good southern place without good sides? Every single side we tried was so bad. The Mac and cheese - dry beyond belief, zero flavor or cheese whatsoever. The coleslaw - just tasted like mayo. The fried - overly salted. However, the cornbread was very good and moist. 

I wouldn't return simply for how inconsistent it was. Also, the service is terrible, it took way too long for our server to check on us throughout our night (maybe 20 minutes between each time we saw her).

This is our go-to when we come to visit our daughter at Sac State. This time we went on a busy Saturday night. 

When we drove up, it was packed, so we reserved online using Open Table. Although we had to wait .35 mins once we checked in, everyone was super nice about it so we didn't mind.

Food was slightly better this time! The chicken was so fresh and the waffle was thin and crispy like I like it. 

Service was excellent.

Parking is not too bad. There's no dedicated parking lot but there is a public lot around the corner if you can't find street parking. 

This place is way better than Roscoe's in southern ca and refreshing to eat indoors or outdoors in a nice clean facility.

I'm visiting from out of town and my sisters, cousins and friends suggested Fixins for dinner. We drovr the 48 mins from Stockton to Sac and it's was so worth it. 

We ordered: 
fried deviled eggs - it was good. i'm not into deviled eggs so it was good. 

fried green tomatoes - this was good too

smothered pork chops, grits & mac - the gravy on the juicy grid pork chops was delicious. the grits were great but needed to be warmer that room temp. the mac and cheese was 10/10. my plate was very well balanced. nothing too salty, or too overpowering. just comforting and it's the spot. 10/10

braised oxtail - fall off the bone, delicious, umami, and loved the gelatinous of it in the gravy 

fried catfish - probably the best seasoned fried fish i had. i just love how it's not too salty! it's perfect! 

dessert: we got all three! amazing! delicious! the red velvet was moist! 


Overall, the music, food, service and ambience were great! I will definitely come back for more.

We walked in and got seated immediately, even though it was clearly a full house--great sign of popularity and solid staffing.

Menu & Dietary Notes:
They offer a small selection of vegan options, which my friend chose. Though limited in variety, they seemed thoughtfully prepared.

What We Tried:

I ordered the two-piece meal with collard greens and mac & cheese, plus a side of cornbread.
Everything was absolutely delicious--classic Southern comfort food with great seasoning and consistency.
Our drinks were also refreshing and a good accompaniment.
I took home the banana pudding To-Go, and I'm excited to dig in later.
Service & Atmosphere:

Host and servers were friendly and efficient--seating us promptly despite the crowd.
The overall vibe felt energetic and warm, perfect for a weekend night.
Final Thoughts:
My experience was overwhelmingly positive. While I didn't try the vegan dish firsthand, the rest of the meal hit all the right notes. I'm looking forward to enjoying the banana pudding later!

Tip for Vegans:
If you follow a vegan diet, I'd suggest calling ahead to check availability--options are limited but seemingly well-made.

Best lunch I have ever had! I got fried chicken, greens and red beans & rice. My husband got the gumbo & cornbread. Hot sauce was so good that we purchased two bottles to go! Parking can be tricky but it's worth it for the delicious food!

Make the effort to find a parking
place, who cares how far you have to walk, just get yourselves NOW to Fixins. 
Their motto, "without soul, it's just food," is perfect, and so are the meals.
The gumbo is spectacular, and just go ahead and give me a bathtub full of their shrimp and grits for me to swim in. The Beer Brown Gravy dropped over this is simply fantastic.
Start out with a lil cast iron skillet of Pemto Cheese Dip!
Plenty of indoor seating and a nice outdoor courtyard to choose from.

I can't believe I've never done a review for this place. It is one of our favorites. I've never had a bad meal here. My go-to is the catfish with an extra fillet on the side, cornbread, fries, red beans, and rice. Tonight, I changed it up and went with the two-piece fried chicken meal (dark meat) with a catfish fillet on the side. My wife had catfish, greens, and yams. 

We love the vibe, the staff, and the food. It's definitely worth the trip.

Everything was fresh and seasoned perfectly. The best fried chicken I have ever had. The sweet tea was hard to stop drinking. And all the fixins were top notch. Great service and ambience, highly recommend!

YUM! Excellent food and service. I had the chicken sandwich and friend had the hot chicken sandwich. The chicken sandwich was perfectly crisp and fresh and not overly heavy. The Kool Aid pickle was interesting. Their sweet tea was perfectly flavored (not too sweet). Would come back again to try their gumbo!

My family and I stopped at this restaurant for brunch while we were visiting the Sacramento area on July 22, 2025 (Tuesday). Since it was my brother-in-law's birthday, he actually found and chose this place to eat at. 

I'm very glad he chose to eat here because it was definitely a great choice! The fried chicken that came with my waffle was extremely flavorful and moist. 

The waffle was thin, which meant it wouldn't be too filling. 

The music was great to add to the overall ambiance. The service was also excellent as well!

We will definitely be back here next time we visit Sacramento.

Service- I went in a slower night, during the week, but place was still decently crowded. The service was wonderful, quick, and most importantly friendly. My server made sure to seat and created a sweet atmosphere with genuine kindness and knowledge of their food. She was delightful and constantly checking to see how good the food was treating me.

Food- mmmm, mmm, mmm is what I have to say. Know I had the three piece combo with collard and Mac; and doubled with a biscuit (with grape jelly butter!) and cornbread with honey-Cheyenne butter. Now, everything great: chicken, crispy and succulent | collard | side breads BEST. However, the Mac was lacking severely. 

Over all, I would add to my top choices in sac

I have been to this location multiple times. Please let me start by saying staff are so professional and welcoming! Parking is easy. The menu presents ample options without being overwhelming. They do have a fully vegan burger on the menu which is good! Drinks offered are unique and tasty. The restaurant is a good size offering indoor and patio options and a separate room for private large groups. They have televisions on the wall and they play music! This is a clean, family friendly place that I love to go to when in town! Definitely try them out!

So many positive things to say about this place I don't know where to start. Hubby and I went for lunch  on a Friday at 1:00 after hearing how good it was. It was even better than expected.  I had expected it to be another BBQ joint but it is not. It is sit down service. It is soul food. Comfort food. Fresh food. Beautifully plated. Flawlessly served. 
Food: standout for me because it is so hard to find was the gravy on the pork chops. The best I have had anywhere. Pork chops were delicious!
The fried chicken was juicy, crispy but not oily, and very well seasoned. 
The sides were SO good we could not stop eating them. The collards were very well seasoned and perfectly cooked. Not boiled to death and full of liquid. The okra was amazing!! It is seared so it has a that smoky (not smoked) taste and was dressed with something tart that paired perfectly. The Mac and cheese is out of this world. Real cheese perfectly done. Yams were also excellent as was the homemade biscuit and flavored butter. 
Service: our server John-Nia gave us service to match any high end restaurant we have been to.  She kept an eye on us and checked in at exactly the right times. And not too often. Good service is attentive but not intrusive. We had ordered the peach cobbler at the beginning and she came by just as we finished our dinner and said "I'll put that cobbler order in now."  She was perfectly on point. 
Drink: We did not indulge as it was lunch after all, but they have a nice selection of local beers, creative cocktails and a heritage whiskey that I want to try next time. 
Vibe: we loved the place. It is elevated, without being fancy. It is homey without being sparse or grungy. It has a very cool vibe with art that harkens back to old wisdom. 
The setting is next door to a spacious vintage looking barbershop with plenty of customers and a very nice local coffee shop. 
Highly Recommend!!!
Wow this place was incredible. Everything I ate was amazing and I can't wait to go try the one in LA!
10/10 music was fun everything tasted amazing service was great and got seated pretty quickly on a busy afternoon it is a lot of food so very much worth the price and everything was delicious

The chicken wing waffle, wing was a bit fatty, skin was soft and didnt fry enough through, ox tail was probably the show stopper for me. Service was ok, bit slow on the servers and seemed they didnt card as much if patrons waited. Was busy when we ate there. Corn bread was meh.
Definitely go back here for the ox tail.

A solid Southern restaurant. The staff and clientele of this establishment are always so relaxed and welcoming. It straddles the line of a comfort restaurant with comfort foods but with excellent service and upscale plating. Very comparable to the Porch or South for those Sacramentans that remember. 

Offerings:
- Smothered Fried Chicken: the perfect juxtaposition of textures, creamy gravy and crispy chicken. I will say this was a bit on the saltier side, but it is what you expect from the description. 
- Charred Okra: served with bacon, I would have appreciated a bit more charring but there is a fine line between charred and burnt. 
- Macaroni and Cheese: a solid version, could have used a bit more salt/butter in my opinion. But still very good. 
- Fried Chicken Sandwich: very good!
- Fries: These were fine. What you expect as a side, nothing to write home about. 
- Fried Green Tomatoes: Almost a fish fry batter on the outside. Fried very well and served with sauce. I think the tomato itself was fine, it was all the extras that made the dish good. 
- Mac and Cheese Balls: I think I prefer this to the regular Mac and cheese solely based on a difference in texture. The flavor was very similar, if not less flavorful, but the fried exterior really piqued my interest.

Overall, everything was good, but rather expected.
The service was amazing! The food was delicious.We had a large party and the staff was very accommodating. All of the food was cooked to perfection.

First time! Food was pretty good and came out fast. Eliza was great! Her energy definitely saved our family dinner.
We had a group of approximately 20 guests and it was initially difficult to be seated in the private room. Once seated, a couple of the servers were a bit short with our guests, but again Eliza service and energy was great!!

As for the food, I'd come back. Good soul food.

My first time trying Fixins and i had NO complaints!

The food was nothing short of amazing!

I ordered 'to-go'  online and placing the order was a breeze! The food was ready when i arrived and although i had my food to go i did scope the inside and the ambiance was great it was beautiful inside!

I ordered smothered chicken, yams, and mac and cheese! All was tasty! I do prefer my bones in my chicken for smothered chicken but now i know for the future to order accordingly.

We dipped in on a Sunday afternoon.  Not the best time because you hit the Sunday rush but glad we did because this chicken is bomb. Breast is so succulent and skin is crispy. Jazzy got us seated in about 30 min and Alondra was our server. She was very friendly and did her best to get to us in a timely manner. They were very busy. And a diverse crowd. The game was on and the vibe was right. This food is all that. The greens are well seasoned with turkey meat. The mac and cheese is made with a rue and will have you right. Do yourself a favor and stop by to get your FIX!

Came here with a few of my girlfriends (definitely recommend making reservations if you're with a larger group). Soul food isn't usually my go-to, but I was blown away by the menu--so many tempting options! Luckily, being in a big group meant we could order a variety and sample a little of everything. Here's what we shared:

Chicken & Waffles
Shrimp & Grits
Smothered Oxtails
Collard Greens
Mac n' Cheese
Charred Okra

One thing to note: soul food is hearty, and the portions are generous--so I couldn't finish a full plate on my own. That said, every single dish was seasoned to perfection. My personal standouts were the Shrimp & Grits, the Oxtails, and the Charred Okra--so flavorful and satisfying.

The staff was warm and welcoming, and the music was on point. Honestly, they need to open a location closer to the Bay--it was so good!

This was my second time visiting this place and I didn't know they have a parking lot right behind off 35th Street. This visit was on a Saturday morning just as they opened at 10am--after a girls morning walk.  I enjoy their fried chicken as it's spiced and cooked to perfection! Juicy on the inside-crunchy and flavorful on the outside! Just how fried chicken should be cooked!  They also ask what pieces you want so I got a thigh, wing and breast. This plate comes with cornbread and two sides: greens and Mac dnd cheese. The service was very friendly, attentive and excellent. Of course I didn't finish all that food and the waitress brought us to go boxes-she was so observant bringing exactly what we needed to pack the leftovers.  OH-I also had the sweet tea--yup just like in the south!  I also warmed up the left over breast for my dinner and it was still juicy--very rare that anyplace gets the breast cooked to perfection that even warning it as leftovers it remains juicy n crispy. If I can give a 10 star-I would!  Just be aware--this place gets packed-so plan accordingly.

Ordered a family meal and drove 30 minutes home only to discover the chicken was missing entirely. I called the restaurant and was told to come back, so I drove 30 minutes back. When I picked it up, I checked the order on the spot and saw that I was given only 6 pieces instead of the 8 that were ordered.

When I pointed this out, the server seemed visibly irritated and asked to see both the food and the receipt before finally bringing the remaining two pieces. I understand that verifying is necessary but what made the overall experience negative was the attitude. working in the food industry is demanding, but as a customer who had to make an hour long round trip due to a mistake only for a second mistake, the attitude made an already frustrating situation worse.

That said, the food itself is good, I recommend dine in, as to go orders clearly are not reliable here.

Food
Fried Chicken was crisp just right for me, delicious and juicy. My family got brown, and i got white meat. Mac n Cheese and coleslaw was yummy.

Service
Friendly!

Ambiance
Pretty indoor and outdoor seatings, spacious

First time visit on a Sunday afternoon, and it was fantastic. The outdoor ambiance was perfect, and the service was incredibly friendly and knowledgeable. The grits were the best I've ever had, the butter of all things...was amazing, and the chicken was perfectly crispy and flavorful. The vegetarian omelet was perfection. I'll definitely be back--highly recommend this place every. single. day.

Loved it!

Yes, you should try it all!!! Lol

You can't go wrong with any of the dishes on the menu but I highly recommend the ones we ordered. See photo of receipt to give you an idea of cost. DELICIOUS! 

**they take reservations**

Why I didn't give this place a 5 star.. 
*our food came before our drinks
*took a really long time to get refills on our water

Shout out to Aimee and her spanky personality!!! It was a busy evening and she tried to give us extra attention but her service were needed more in other tables. We were a simple set of peeps and didn't require much but a little love after the initial encounter would have been nice. 

*parking can be challenging. Go up one block and you'll find some

We will be back!

First time here and I'm very impressed. Almost everything I ate was amazing. Jae was our awesome server who was attentive and engaging. We started with the artichoke dip, corn fritters and fried green tomatoes, all which were very flavorful and I would order again. I ordered the 3 sides: grits, red beans & rice and mac n cheese. Let me just say, I have NEVER had mac n cheese this good at a restaurant, it was creamy and delicious and the red beans were extremely flavorful as well. My sis and I didn't care for the grits but would definitely order the mac and red beans again. Sis got the fish nuggets which were fried very well, not hard at all. Some also got the yams which they loved. Overall, I would most definitely return. 
The restaurant is located in the Oak Park neighborhood of Sacramento. The parking was somewhat difficult as I think the theater around the corner was hosting an event. The restaurant has both in and outdoor seating and the decor is quite nice. The staff is pleasant and the wait was not long once we ordered. Again, would return and looking forward to it.
Came and had brunch with my husband. Service was great. Food was amazing. Vibes were great. It was so good that we had to get dinner to go for later. Definitely planning on coming back soon.

I have been here a couple of times now with family. Each time, the service and food has always been good. Even the owner and NBA legend Kevin Johnson has been there. Fun story, I would choose KJ in NBA Jam as a kid. When I saw him here open the door for us, I just froze and was at a loss for words -- big fan moment!

Most recent visit here with family and we ordered a little bit of everything. We lived in the South and familiar with good southern cooking. I got the smothered pork chops with the gravy on the side. Got it with some red beans and rice and collards. 

The ox tails are always good and usually run out. The fried chicken and waffles is always a nice pick. The fried fish dish is solid.

Not a super big knock, but the food is very salty. I do appreciate well-seasoned dishes, but by the end of the meal, we were wanting to chug so much water. 

Nice home-like vibe whether you dine inside or outside. They also have a bar area if you do so choose.

author_location: Downtown Los Angeles, Los Angeles, CA
text: Always a great time! Food is great, nice family atmosphere, and great service as always. Came for a John Lee Hooker Bourbon tasting.

Everything was hella good. Ordered the waffle/chicken natürlich. It came with a strawberry butter that was so damn good. In Oakland, we call chicken/waffles with a side of Mac - Keith's Tackle. Go with that .

Food was great service was great server Terri was nice and helpful. 
Smothered chicken collard greens and black eye peas. Smothered pork chops collard greens yams and Mac and cheese and cornbread. Very delicious will be back

Holy cow, this was great for our 11 person lunch! We were seated in the front where there's a large square table and banquette seating.

The service was great, and Aunt Pearl stopped by to check in on us. Such a sweet woman, who's proud of her restaurant and wants to share with all.

I had a half and half sweet/unsweetened tea. Even at half sweet, it was pretty sweet. And really good. 

Starters:
- deep fried deviled eggs were great. Crispy and good flavor.
- pimiento cheese dip and chips were great. The chips had a nice seasoning on them. 

Entree food:
- the dark meat fried chicken was fantastic. Best fried chicken I've ever had. Better than "the best" from Mew Orleans. Crispy and meat was juicy.
- the fried chicken sandwiches were huge! And also great.
- smothered fried chicken also good.

Sides:
- Mac and cheese was creamy, even when it'd cooled off. 
- okra was delicious, crispy
- black eyed peas, collards, candied yams, coleslaw all good
- cornbread had a nice crust. Served with a strawberry butter. Different but very tasty.

Dessert:
- the red velvet cake (big piece!), banana pudding, and peach cobbler were all very good

Great experience for our group!

Real home feel walking into this place. All the servers and staff were super friendly. The hot chicken sandwich was hella good. Juicy piece of chicken. Skin was crispy with nice coatining. Not crazy spicy so don't worry. The slaw and tomatoes were good but eat it right away before it gets soggy. Definitely can share with another person. Fried chicken was also very good. All the sides were fire. Definitely will be coming back here very soon.

I came here with the kids on our way home from a day of skiing. We have been here several times. The food is good with lots of side options. They have a kids menu that's very reasonable which is a bonus when you are dining out with kids. The service was fast and efficient. There used to be long waits when we first started coming, but that doesn't seem to be the case anymore the last couple times I can't, but I was also able to make reservations one of those times. 

I got the fried catfish. It was a lot of food, but good. The kid plates were also a pretty big portion for younger kids.  The kids got Kool aid for their drinks, and they get to choose the color they want, so that's always fun for them. The place is busy, but not too noisy. 

Overall, this is one of our favorite stops when we need a dinner stop in Sac. It's kid-friendly and the are great options for adults too.

Okay so right out the gate this place gives ALL THE VIBES! Came during the week with a party of 13. Music kept us jammin while we waited for our food. Let me jus say that I was torn with what I wanted to eat. I ended up getting smothered chicken and I'm glad I did, not really a greenz kinda gal but this spot, can I jus say everything was DELICIOUS!! now I grew up with kool-aid and when I asked the flavor and the response was "Red", I knew I was in for a treat. Staff were daft and efficient. Will def come again.

My first time eating soul food was at Fixins in Sacramento- let me tell you, I went back the next day as well! The restaurant is very nice, very clean, seems to be newer, the service was great, and the food, wow, it was out of this world! The candied yams were my favorite! Highly recommend this place!!

My BFF took me here for one of the best brunches ever! 

Food was worth every penny, it was so good that I wished the portions were bigger but then again it is brunch-appropriate portions. 

From the menu, Yes I agree, very very hard to choose from all their good options. We opted for the following: 

-Fried Chicken w/sides: Mac Cheese, Candied Yams, Greens, Grits and Fries 
-Chicken & Waffles 

Their fried chicken is so darn tasty but I wish the portion size was more more more. Sides were good, my fave would have to be mac cheese. Candied Yams could be a little sweeter. 

Will be returning...

Wow, wow & WOW! It's been years since I've had a delicious meal at a restaurant and I was not disappointed. Everything we ordered made me say "WOW, I can't believe it tastes like a homemade meal. 

It was 3 of us. 
I ordered the smothered chicken with Mac & cheese & red beans and rice. That was a 10/10! 

My bf ordered omg the Oxtail with rice plate and the flavor was on point. I usually add salt to everything (I know it's bad) But we didn't need any salt. That's how delicious it was! 10/10

My 3 year old ordered Mac and cheese and a chicken leg. He loved it! 
 Oh and we ordered the deep fried deviled eggs! So good and cornbread 

I'm very socially awkward anywhere we go but this place had an amazing ambiance, I loved seeing the many families enjoying their time. 

And lastly, our server was awesome. She made sure we always had everything we needed. 

We will be back and this time with our other 3 kids! We can not wait

About noon on a Wednesday. Seated immediately, I requested to sit outside since it is a beautiful day today. 

A few large groups today, however I am dining alone. 

My server came within a couple minutes and took my order. I ordered a water, the deep fried deviled eggs and the smothered fried chicken with mac n cheese and cole slaw. 

My water was brought out quickly. And then shortly after my appetizer. Took about 15 more minutes for my entree to arrive after that. 

I think I should have ordered the regular fried chicken and requested a side of gravy, because I missed that crunch of the chicken. But that was a me thing. The chicken itself was delicious. I was just missing the crunch. 

The Mac n cheese was decent and the coleslaw was great. I never thought I would be a person who enjoyed coleslaw, but here I am. 42 and choosing coleslaw instead of fries lol 

Overall the experience was decent. I wasn't bothered by too many people asking me how I was doing, but I never went without anything. 

I wish I had the room in my belly to have some banana pudding though, because it is my favorite dessert. 

Next time.

12/10!  Attended a private party and those can be hit or miss when serving 30 people at once...but EVERYTHING was delicious! We had the deviled eggs (deep fried with bacon!!!), fried green tomatoes, Caesar salad (amazing croutons!), the BEST gumbo I have ever had (tons of meat!), mac and cheese, sweet potatoes, crispy yummy beautiful fried chicken and peach cobbler. It was all delicious!! Can forget the delicious Spiked Pineapple Palmer cocktail. It was all so good. The staff we attentive and helpful and the atmosphere was inviting with comfortable seating and cute decor.

Whew, baby! Let me tell you--this spot right here? FIXINS is fixin' lives one plate at a time!

We were in from out of town, and my cousin said, "You HAVE to try this place." Now, I've been to the one in L.A., and don't get me wrong, it's solid... but this location? Smaller, cozier, and way more intimate. It felt like I walked into a family reunion where everybody could cook!

Let's talk food. EVERYTHING I tried was a spiritual experience--but those sides? Say a prayer before and after. The fried okra? Smokey, crispy, and more addictive than scrolling through TikTok at 2 a.m. The cheesy grits? Creamy little clouds of comfort that hugged my insides like grandma's blanket. And the fried green tomatoes? All I could say was "YAASSS LAWD!" in my best church voice.

Our server was super chill and knew the menu very well. 

The vibe? Immaculate. The art had me snapping pics like a tourist, and the music had me flashbackin' to 2007 house parties where we danced like nobody was recording. 

Fixins, you stole my heart and maybe a button off my jeans. 10/10 will return--and bring more cousins next time!

To be perfectly honest, I actually have no idea how I first came across Fixins Soul Kitchen.  What I do remember is that as soon as I did, I immediately bookmarked them to come here.  Obviously, it was a nice bonus to see that the reviews for this place were so positive, but I kind of expected them to be after seeing all of the delicious looking photos of the food.  At that point, it was never in doubt as to whether or not I'd end up coming here, it was just a matter as to how soon it could happen...and why couldn't I make it happen sooner!

Given their immense popularity, I was genuinely shocked we didn't have to wait for a table when we walked in without reservations for brunch on a Saturday morning.  Not sure if that's typical or if we just got lucky, but I certainly wouldn't recommend temping fate like we did, as this place was packed by the time we left.  It's easy to see why - between the lively atmosphere, the cozy back patio area, and their hip-hop playlist (heard so many of my old jams!), the vibe of this place is great!  Of course, that's without even mentioning the menu, which is chock full of Southern/soul food staples.  From their Fried Chicken & Waffles, Biscuits & Gravy, Smothered Pork Chops, Shrimp & Grits, Fried Fish, Gumbo, all of their fixins, and so much more, there were so many things we wanted to try!

-- SAC TOWN MANGO LEMONADE - Hennessy V.S Peach and Mango Puree, Lemon Juice, Sprite...$16
As tempting as the Uncle Nearest Strawberry sounded, the peach and mango puree in this was too good to pass up.  Thankfully, this didn't disappoint.  The pour wasn't overly heavy, though you could still taste the Hennessy, and I love how well the puree mixed with the Sprite and lemon juice.  Refreshing!

-- DEEP FRIED DEVILED EGGS - Deep Fried Egg Cups Filled with Creamy Yolks Topped with Bacon and Hot Sauce...$12
Pretty tasty, but I still prefer regular deviled eggs, which I love.  The batter had a nice crispiness and the yolk/filling tasted great, thanks to the bacon and the bit of heat it had.  That said, given how it's fried, it had a slight taste of oiliness that I wasn't as fond of.  Still, great starter!

-- CHICKEN & WAFFLES - Two Pieces (Dark) & a Waffle...$16
Quite simply, this was so good!  On the outside, the batter was crunchy (in the best of ways) and well seasoned while on the inside, the chicken was juicy and piping hot.  As for the waffle, it was just about perfect - big yet not dense nor "bready" and so good with the butter and syrup.

-- FRIED FISH - Cornmeal Crusted Fish Served with Corn Fritters and Choice of Two Fixins (Grits and Collared Greens)...$
More solid than spectacular.  The cornmeal crust made this feel a bit dry, but the fish itself was moist and the fritters were delicious (crispy, flavorful).  As for the fixins, I'm not big on collared greens, but these weren't too shabby, and I liked how creamy the grits were, though, we forgot to add cheese.

Along with how good the food was, our server was great - super friendly and very engaging.  Plus, he was so (unnecessarily) apologetic that they were out of certain fixins that we wanted (unless we wanted to wait).  We had to keep telling him it wasn't that big of a deal, as we were curious about most of their entire slate on the menu.  Also, the other thing that I LOVED about our trip here was how easy to was to find parking.  I don't know if we were similarly lucky, but it was both awesome and refreshing to able to find a spot around the corner without having to circle endlessly around the neighborhood.

Now, I just need to figure out when I'll be able to make my way back here to check out their dinner menu!  FOUR AND A HALF STARS rounded down for now.

I love this spot in Oak Park, CA!!  The food was so good if you're craving  for some authentic Soul Food!!  I had the oxtail--to die for!  Service was excellent.  We sat outside in the patio and it was so nice.  We're definitely going back to Oak Park just so we can eat at Fixins Soul Kitchen again!

For whatever reason, Fixins Soul Kitchen caught our eye on yelp and we decided to make the 93-mile each way drive to give them a taste. It was definitely a worthy trek. With the Princess in tow, we headed out.

We lucked into a parking space about two blocks away, giving us time to stretch our legs before tucking into lunch. It was a nice spring day with a mild wind.

We stood outside for a minute and looked over the menu once more. Our routine is that I'll go directly to the patio area with the Princess while she goes inside to order. Today she followed me into the patio and entered through the side entrance. The host told her that if were eating in, they would provide table service, and brought us menus, welcoming us. Our server would be with us shortly.

Jae (I hope I got that right) came over and asked if we had any questions. I told him no and that we were ready to order. Because of the long drive, we decided to try as many flavors as we could knowing that we would end up with leftovers. We ordered:

Deep Fried Devil Eggs - 5 - we've never had Deviled Eggs like this before. The whites are battered and deep fried and then the eggs are put back in place with a wedge of crispy bacon on top. To keep the eggs from rolling around on the plate, a dollop of the eggs was placed under the egg as well.

Fish Nuggets - 5 - Dusted with cornmeal and fried to perfection, these little nuggets were the cat's meow. I could easily eat these all day long with the lightly spiced chipotle dipping sauce they were served with. There was also a tartar sauce for those who preferred. 

Oxtails - 4 - A good portion of oxtails (3 medium), great gravy and rice. The only thing that could have improved this dish was just a bit more cooking time. The oxtails were good but still a bit chewy. I like my oxtails to be completely falling off the bone. 

Gumbo - 5 - Absolutely delicious and adding on the prawns took it over the top.  A very warming and satisfying dish. 

Fried Chicken - 5 - Crispy on the outside, moist and tasty on the inside. We asked for all dark and got three thighs. The crust looked like it had been fried hard but that was an illusion. Just a tad salty overall, especially cold the next day, but still overall delicious. 

Red Beans n Rice - 3 - This was our least favorite dish. There wasn't anything wrong with it, but we've become so accustomed to the Popeye's creamy version that it's become the standard that we use. It may have been wiser not to get this dish since we already had rice with the Gumbo and Oxtails. 

Mac N Cheese - 5 - My wife never orders Mac n Cheese anywhere although I do in the hopes of finding a good one. She actually like this one. It was reminiscent of the Mac n Cheese that Mrs. Jackson used to make when my wife was a child. Mrs. Jackson ran a Cafe in West Oakland two generations ago before the Acorn Projects were built. 

Candied Yams - 5 - Way too sweet for me but she loved them. This dish, along with the Mac n Cheese were similar to the sides that we used to get from Sunday Church Plates when the local churches cooked as fund raisers. 

Peach Cobbler & Vanilla Ice Cream - 4 - Jae asked if we wanted dessert. I didn't because we were already stuffed to the gills. My wife asked what they had and was told: Peach Cobbler, Red Velvet Cake and Banana Pudding. I asked if the peaches were fresh or canned and Jae said fresh. So, my wife ordered it to go. 
When it was presented in two containers, we discovered that the cobbler was in one and the ice cream in another. Naturally we can't waste the ice cream so she ate it. Then we reheated the cobbler for dessert without ice cream. Once again, way too sweet for me. The ice cream would have toned it down some. We took away one star because we felt that more peaches were in order. 

We met Aunt Pearl who came out and introduced herself. She also told us that she was the actual Aunt to Kevin Johnson and his wife Michelle, the founders. It was important to the brand that she made sure that everyone was satisfied with the service and the food. 

I'm an old bay area news guy and I have more trivia filed away in my head than I can possibly catalog. After she walked away, I thought about the name and why it sounded familiar. I looked him up online and found that he had been Mayor of Sacramento.

The restaurant was clean inside and out, including the restrooms. Service was excellent as was the food. There was more than enough on the menu to make us happy. Definitely a place we would return when in the area.

I definitely enjoy coming to this spot  when I am craving some soul food. I recently ordered the fried chicken - 2 piece light & dark, Mac & cheese, and some yams!! Definitely worth it.

The chicken and waffles are also a must if you truly enjoy a little sweet and a little savory. I also loved the fried green tomatoes with their chipotle aioli. The food overall was delicious! 

The ambience was well maintained for a busy Sunday and the server was extremely friendly. Thank you FIXINS!

My husband and I came here for brunch and absolutely loved the food. The fried chicken was amazing, crispy, flavorful, and cooked perfectly. I had mine with candied yams and fries, and both sides were delicious and paired really well with the chicken. My husband ordered the chicken and waffles, which came with a generous portion of two waffles and three pieces of chicken. Everything was filling and satisfying. Service was friendly and attentive, which made the experience even better.

Great food, great venue. Visited with family, only issue was the service being a bit slow, but the quality & quantity of the food made up for it. Highlights for our group included the pimento cheese dip appetizer, the fried chicken, the burger, the spiked kool-aid, peach cobbler & red velvet cake.

In 2019, you couldn't get a seat it was that packed all the time. Now it's 2024 a few years later and you can get a seat. We even got a parking spot. We went this past Friday around 5:00pm. When we arrived there were several tables (it's a small place probably 8 tables were) taken. They greeted us warmly as we entered and seated us near the  back where there were 3 empty tables.

Service was impeccable. Our waitress was always checking on us but, not in an intrusive way. If we needed anything she was there.

Soon after we were seated we were given water and offered drinks. Our order was then taken.

The best dish we got was the appetizer, the fired green tomatoes. They were delicate and crispy and simply delicious. 

For a main dish I had the shrimp and grits. It was delicious. The shrimp were large and succulent.

My husband got the fried fish. It came with 2 sides. We got Mac and cheese and collard greens. It was delicious. The fish was perfectly fried.

We would definitely come back and make sure we have room for the peach cobbler.

Fixin's don't miss!!! 

Been a few times and now I think I'm in love with the cheesy grits and collard greens. Grits were perfect and seasoned well that dollop of cheese sauce was superior. Now those collard greens I had to ask if my grandmother was back there omg so good. Had a red Koolaide as well which reminded me of my childhood. Our serve was very attentive and always made sure we had what we needed. The music in the background set the Sunday brunch off. A vibe!!

3 stars because my server Brian was amazing. 
But honestly I don't understand the hype of this place. I was super excited to try the fried chicken, and  was beyond disappointed. The breading is so much and so tick that's takes 7 business days to  actually get to the chicken. And the chicken didn't tasty right at all. 
The purple koolaid was over sweet impossible to drink. The peach cobbler was gross. 
Spend 104$ for bad food.

The food was great. I had the oxtails and my wife had the shrimp and grits. The oxtails was  served over rice with a nice rich, brown gravy and very tender. The taste was amazing. I got a side of red beans and rice. That was really good. I had a bite of my wife, shrimp and grits, and the grits were creamy and the shrimps were cooked perfectly with a light crunch the atmosphere was laid back. We're definitely going back.

This place was packed on Sunday evening. Our server Cheri was very knowledgeable on the dishes and friendly, she had a large group next to us so didn't check on us regularly but her personality made up for it. My 17 year olds order had the smothered chicken. He loved every bite of it. The yams he said were perfectly cooked soft and seasoned on point. Mac and cheese was deliciously cheesy with good flavor and the chicken was the king of his plate.  My 11 year said all other fried chicken is put to shame. Never disappointed with the food. I had okra as my side and it was delicious as well as their mustard dressing for my side salad. I'm not a fried chicken type of person but it was good chicken without leaving me feeling sick afterward. Always good soul food at Fixins.

Not sure why I haven't posted my review for this place.  We have been here a number of times now.  This place is always busy, highly recommend making a reservation to avoid the long wait times.

Came here after waiting almost 2 hours for food at another soul food place.  We made the 45 minute trip up to enjoy this place.  

Service was attentive despite how busy they were.  Great music playing in the background.  Lots of options.  I ordered the smothered pork chops with collard greens, candied yams and potato salad as a side.  My husband ordered shrimp and grits.  My son ordered the fried chicken with collard greens and grits.

For starters, since we were starving- we had the deviled eggs and pimento cheese.  I think I like the traditional style deviled eggs vs deep fried.  Otherwise, appetizers came quick.

The food came and each dish was delicious.  The gravy on my pork chops had so much flavor.  I thoroughly enjoyed it.  Portion was a bit big for me so we had leftovers.  The collard greens are the best I ever had.  So flavorful.  The yams were not too sweet and I tasted some orange zest in there.  Potato salad was perfect.  Not too tart and just the right amount of seasoning.

My husband's shrimp and grits were delicious too.  The sauce was full of flavor.  I could taste some pimento in it.  Plenty of shrimp to enjoy with the nicely cooked grits.

My son enjoyed the ver well seasoned and delicious fried chicken.  Good portions.

We definitely enjoyed every bite and trek to make it to enjoy some good soul food.  We will definitely keep coming back here.  They have their system dialed in.  Wait time for food was not too long.  Unlike the previous location we went to.  

Service was great.

The oxtail & potato salad came out really sweet 
The Mac and cheese was on the grany side. 
But the kool aid and adult kool aid was delicious. The pieces of fried chicken were really small and some were salty. Service took a while to get to our table and we had got there before a large party came in and they had took their order first so it was a bit upsetting. But everyone was friendly and it was clean inside.

Ordered right before our night out. Food came in a timely manner. 
The food was still a little warm. Very tasty mixture of sauce in the rice. The greens were good too. 

Probably a better experience if we had dined in. 

The food was packaged and left outside our hotel door without a knock to notify us it was delivered.

Back again everything we had was different. Pork chops smothered with gravy, Chicken sandwich and 3 pieces of chicken with beans and salad and candied yams  also oka fried. 
Maya was our waitress and we was well taken care of. 
The place is very fun and unique.

******BLACKED OWNED BUSINESS ALERT***********************

Great place. Great vibe. Great food. 

I am not familiar with Sacramento and what it has to offer. I was excited when my cousin introduced me to this establishment. 

We ordered a few appetizers and some entrees.  The fried deviled eggs were tasty as well as the fried green tomatoes. They were seasoned just right and had a nice crispiness about them. 

I ordered the smothered chicken with fries and yams. All was satisfying. Of course there were some to go bags leaving up out that joint. 

The service was friendly and comfortable, so much so I called her Raquel and her name tag said Desiree. Gawd, it was way past my bed time. 

Oh but there's more... they serve Kool-Aid......Red (as in Red Dye 40), Blue (as in Food Blue 5), and Purple (as in Red Dye 40 and Blue 5 mixed together). I had an unsweetened Arnold Palmer with a splash of sweetened Palmer. LMFAO. 

Drumroll Please..... wait for it.....wait for it.....there's one in Detroit, MI.....now BMF on that!!

First time eating here and was pleased. The food was okay. I had Fish, macaroni and cheese, and the greens, which were very tasty. My grandson had a chicken sandwich which came with one side, he chose macaroni and cheese also. He said it was good.  I gave it four stars because my fish was really greasy, but our server Kimberly was excellent!! I would go again to try other things on the menu.

The service was quick and the restaurant was filled. The sweet potatoes were delicious and the sweet tea was thirst quenching. The restaurant displays photos of family members that helped develop the recipes, very homey.

Atlanta in the house! Get a feel of Atlanta right here in Sacramento. The ambiance is that if you will - a soul spot. 

Know what's so awesome and yet so simple? The different types of butter available for your chicken and waffles.  

There's plenty of dishes to choose from and I've tried just about everything from ox tails, fried catfish balls, fried cat fish, chitlins and the good ol staple, chicken and waffles. 

Overall, food is great and wait times can be long but if you plan in advance by reserving your spot online, you'll be totally fine. Salt can be a bit on the high end but it comes and goes.

Delicious soul food at a lovely restaurant! Also, great customer service!!! We are new to the area, coming in from Los Angeles so this was a new experience for sure. We will absolutely be coming back- we were treated so well here on top of the tasty food. I'm a vegetarian and I gotta say- you've got options here. I had a Caesar salad, which was very delicious, and a generous portion too.

Vegetarian or not though, you must try the fried green tomatoes. Just trust me on this one and thank me later.  5/5 stars!

I enjoyed every aspect of my visit! The food, service and atmosphere were all top notch. Really enjoyed the catfish, chicken and the grits! I highly recommend this restaurant! Be aware that parking is a bit of a challenge in the area.

Our family went to Fixins on a weekend while visiting Sacramento. We were a large group (like 10 people) and were early for our reservation but they were still able to seat us after a brief wait. 

What I liked about this place the most was the vibe: no frills, good music, and good energy! This is the kind of place you go to if you want a real soul food experience in a nice environment. 

I honestly feel like there is no need to say too much about the service or the food because it was all giving what it needed to, but it wouldn't be a review if I didn't give y'all some details~

Talking about the food, the oxtails I got were fall off the bone and very flavorful. We also LOVED the fried green tomatoes which were a sleeper hit. My rule of thumb is if a place has fried green tomatoes, I'm trying it, and I was not disappointed! 

Overall, I was happy with portion sizes for the price and most of us ended up taking some food home because we couldn't finish everything. 

I'm rating it a five because I think about it every time I am in the area and am trying to find my way back.

I had Fixins saved in the date archives with my husband and we came yesterday and was not disappointed. 

We waited 30 minutes after putting our name on the list and the moment we sat down hospitality! 

Good Ambiance, Good Music , Great Food

- fried fish
- chicken and waffles (2 wings)
- mac n cheese (2)
- candied yams
- red kool-aid
- blue raspberry margarita (2)

We ordered our appetizers and drinks and the kool-aid instantly put a smile on my husband's face! My blue raspberry margarita was sooooo good I ordered 2. When the food came out we were blown away the fish, chicken and mac n cheese. Now to the yams...I could actually taste the cinnamon and nutmeg and not only sugar 
Absolutely delightful! the yams were actually peeled soul food spots lately have forgot this step apparently) . 

Parking was on the street but easy to find a good spot. 

Came from the bay area with 0 regrets

We will be back!
We have been to the one in LA and now Sac, Fixins never disappoints. The food reminds you of home, comfort and soulfully delicious with every bite.

Fixins is the only spot I know serving up Southern comfort food in a cool, lively space! Fixins is located in north Oak Park and there's plenty of street parking nearby, even on weekends. My partner and I walked in for a table for two, and we were quoted a 35 minute wait (even though we only waited about half of that). Once seated, we dug into the menu.
