We arrived on time for brunch, but we both decided to order off of the lunch menu. He ordered the fried chicken (gravy on the side) with Mac and cheese and fries as his fixins. I ordered the smothered chicken (dark meat) with charred okra and cheesy grits as mine. After trying both, we're in agreement that the fixins absolutely shine here! The charred okra was the best I've ever had. The chicken was a little soggy, but all the flavors were on point! Prices were reasonable, and it's definitely worth coming back for!

My husband and I went for the first time. The food was perfect! 

We started with deep fried deviled eggs with were so tasty with a drip of buffalo sauce mixed with that bacon. I had the fried fish with Mac n cheese and fries; so delicious... the fish was crispy crust but flaky inside, and seasoned perfectly. Fries were crispy and good, Mac was so cheesy!! My husband had the fried chicken, red beans & rice with collard greens as well as a side of chicken & waffles; where to begin?!! The chicken was super juicy and crispy! The greens were so flavorful. The waffles came with a strawberry buttercream that was the perfect sweetness with the syrup. 
Oh and the Kookaid was on point!!  Every sip of my "purple" Koolaid took me back in time to my childhood. 
We both were impressed with the quality of food as well as the service. Our waiter gave us recommendations and was attentive and friendly. The place was nice... tvs at the bar and plenty of room in between tables. 
We will be back for some home  cooked soul food there at Fixins!!!

11/19/24-

Taking advantage of a very stressful and busy day..

My new gym friend mentioned this morning- that he came down yesterday and had Fixins-

My oldest has always had nice comments about the food-

So, I went ahead and ordered-

Order was for the fried fish- sides of red beans and rice and coleslaw-..

Also ordered the catfish bites and a side of greens-

Amazing fish! Crispy and not oily- seasoned just right..

Loved the red beans and rice-

Catfish bites were devoured .. greens were decent.

So glad I was able to relax and enjoy a meal that I didn't have to prepare..

Fixins is located in Oak Park. There is limited street parking in the area. The interior was somewhat loud, and the service was friendly.

I usually order the fried chicken but this visit I tried the smothered pork chops with okra and mac & cheese for sides. The pork chops were good but i'll stick to the fried chicken in the future. Overall, Fixins is good and I recommend visiting to give it a try.

Everything was perfect here.   Kim was our Server and she was absolutely awesome.   Oh, and the food was exquisite.  The portions are generous and everything is just so pleasant. 

Parking was a bit of a challenge,  we had to walk a couple blocks away, but it was so worth it.

Came here on a Sunday night around 7:30pm. The place was poppin'- I really loved the vibe and music.

We were seated right away even without reservation and our server, Eliza, was super welcoming and sweet. 

I ordered the 2 piece chicken and waffle. My friend ordered the 2 piece and 2 sides (red beans and rice and collars greens). We were able to choose what chicken pieces we wanted. I ordered a drumstick and thigh. 

We got our food very quickly and it was delicious and the perfect amount for me. The waffle was crispy and covered the whole plate, so Eliza brought me a second plate just in case I wanted to divide and conquer. It was topped with powdered sugar and also came with grape jelly butter and delicious maple syrup on the side. Not overly sweet and paired well with the wonderful crispy and moist chicken.

They have their own house made hot sauce and it was pretty good too.

We only had water as drinks, but we did see them bring out some adult kool-aid which came served in large Mason jars which looked bomb.

I would definitely go back! Free Parking right on 3rd street.

This place was absolutely incredible. The environment was really unique, and on the walls by bathroom it gave the whole story on the family and the restaurant. We sat outside, be prepared if you sit outside that you are okay with a little mist. The server was nice and gave us good reccomendations. I got the fried chicken, Mac and cheese, and yams. It was probably the best fried chicken I've ever eaten, the Mac and cheese was also on another level. The yams were also delicious and very sweet. Amazing place!

Wow!  1st time and we went here for my birthday.   Very lively environment.  Food was outstanding.  Taste like homemade.  I had chicken (2 breasts) and waffles.  Biggest pieces chicken that i have had in years.   Really like biscuits with butter too!  Outside crunch and soft inside.  Staff was very welcoming and friendly!  Will come back often and (1) of our spots in Sac now.

Waffles, Candied Yams, Turkey Collards (omg), Cornbread, Potato Salad (holy kittens it was nearly identical to my granny's) and the Peach Cobbler?! I may dream about that cobbler at some point. Live in San Ramon so the drive was not short, already looking forward to another outing. 

Nearly everything was excellent. The *only* slight miss was the macaroni and cheese, but my partner said it was decent and I'm more of a dry mac fan so that may be solely personal preference issue. 

Lavender Lemon Drop - Fantastic! ~ Careful though, snuck up on me. Alcohol taste is nearly undetectable but it's definitely not a weak pour. :)

This was REAL southern food with soul, mouth watering and seasoned to perfection. (Ahem, looking at you Cherie's "Southern" Kitchen). Took me back to my childhood family visits in the south. 

Love the outdoor seating option! Although....the flies tend to be a bit much but if there is any way to tackle that issue it would be awesome. We arrived at 11:00 am for brunch on a Sunday, waited about 10 min to be seated outside. By 12 pm it was fairly packed with a lengthier wait so keep that in mind. Lovely music, people clearly enjoying themselves all around. Excellent food, nice portions, fairly priced. 
Give Fixins Soul Kitchen a shot!

I can't say enough good things about this restaurant! Our whole experience was incredible and encourage everyone to go if you can. 

Food (5/5): I love me some soul food. It's tough sometimes when it's overly greasy or salty but I thought everything was well seasoned and not terribly oily. I was pleasantly surprised at how fluffy and crisp the waffle was and would order a whole one to myself next time! The Mac n cheese was simple but packed with flavor and the coleslaw was my favorite type, vinegar based and not mayo based. The chicken was incredibly juicy and tender and the skin was super crispy but not burnt. 

Service (5/5): Super friendly staff! They were quite busy when we visited but they continued to give us plenty of attention and excused their busyness. 

Ambiance (5/5): I seriously need the playlist they were bumpin in the restaurant. They were playing oldies and I was able to sing and enjoy my meal. Everyone in there was having a great time and added to the welcoming environment. 

Parking was a bit tough. We got lucky with the lot around the corner but otherwise you'll have to look for tight street parking.
The fried chicken!!! Fireworks everywhere on the first bite! Excellent service! Nice bar vibes with exceptional food choices from the South.

Decent service and fair amount of seating between the main dining area, back room, bar, and patio. Husband is from Mississippi and I lived there briefly as well so we're a bit critical of soul/southern cooking. We found the oxtail dish to have a hint of underlying sweetness in the save, and wasn't very gravy-like. The charred okra was simple and what you'd expect. Husband said his smothered pork chops were "okay, not bad but definitely could be better." We liked the cornbread and fried green tomatoes. Drinks were yummy.
Old fashioned that didn't waste my time, learned the meaning of "24 hour brine", well balanced greens and creamy grits. I'm coming back here. Maybe too often....

My food was DELICIOUSSS. Big shoutout to our server whos name of course i forgot... she was so sweet and patient with us, we appreciated her  it was a full house but not loud at all. Foods came out at a good speed, she made sure everybody had everything they need and checked in on us a lot. Everyone else loved their food too we got cheeseburgers for the kids, fried tomatoes 9/10. And friend chicken. Forgot to take pics of others food.

We went there yesterday for the first time ever! Let me tell you something.... THIS IS SOOOOO GOOD!!! The Mac n cheese was best I ever had, the collard greens delicious, chicken was juicy! Anytime I cut a piece off the juice was running down. And there sweet tea 10 out of 10! My husband got there spiked kool-aid also 10/10! We will be coming back for sure! We had a 40 min wait and it was definitely worth the wait!

Met some friends here for dinner. I'm from the south so I was skeptical but.. 

Shrimp n Grits are to die for. The collard greens.... Y'all.... So GooD! Not slimy at all. The perfect taste! I ate every bite of them. 

The Mac n cheese was divine! Definitely not out of a box. 

My child got chicken n waffles. Husband got tenders and waffles. The breast pieces were huge! I think the tenders were best. 

It's fried to perfection. Do stinking good. And the grape butter was interesting but also good. 

Banana pudding... get it. Perfect texture! 

Peach pie was also killer. 

Definitely going back. 

Minus 1 star for leaving dirty/used dishes on the table. Other than that, excellent service.

Got to Fixin's at 8pm on a Wednesday night with a group 4. We were greeted and seated right away with no wait and no fuss about us getting there a little less than hour before closing . 

The vibe definitely was more modern/industrial than what I would imagine soulfood have.

Their menu was simple but had options for everyone. We ordered the family meal which came with 8 pieces of fried chicken , 2 large sides, 4 biscuits. Mac n cheese  and charred okra were delicious , okra was something I've never had prepared this way, but unique and tasty if you can get past the texture .

Over all chicken was delicious and crispy. Their hot sauce was delicious not too spicy. It's a little bit of zing from vinegar.

This was my first time eating soul food, and I can say that it was delicious . We definitely want to come back to try some other items.

Fixins has made it to the top of my list for places to eat in Sac. I think it is one of the best fried chicken + waffles I've ever had -- even competes with other well-known places in other states. The meat is always juicy and flavorful and the flavored butters are the perfect sweetness to complement the savory meal. They can get crowded; personally, I've only been for dinner but I've always been able to get a seat. I love ordering the cheese dip and dipping my chicken in it for another flavor profile!
I love this place!  Food is delish!  Ask for Jae!  Wonderful service! He totally made us feel welcomed!  We will be back!  We had the oxtails and fried chicken.

Came in on a Sunday for brunch. Already expected to be waiting to get seated. Waited about 45 mins when they initially told us 1 hour but that's cool. Went next door to hang out while we waited. Came back and got seated right away. Host sat us at a table that was supposedly already seated by someone else and moved us to another table quick. However, they forgot to give us menus & we sat waiting for the server to attend to us. Took several minutes before they came back to give us water & take our order. Lack of communication between the employees.It's cool tho. It's busy I get it. To avoid long waits, I would recommend coming later in the afternoon after the rush. About 2pm after.

Food was really yummy & great portions! Like the side options. So filling I barely finished my food. Made sure I ate the expensive stuff tho.

Nice staff, very nice decor, and the food is decent. My mother got oxtails and there was a little too much gravy for her she said the flavor was good, and so were her greens. I got a catfish plate, their fish is great, macaroni has good flavor but my noodles were overcooked/mushy and were really oily, red beans and rice were decent. My children got chicken strips and the chicken had great flavor. The koolaid is really good and the peach cobbler is okay too. I wasn't wowed but it's definitely worth a visit if you want soul food and don't know how to make it yourself.

Heading back home from Tahoe and my husband found this spot. We didn't need to make a resy but did, just in case.

Cute location and vibe. All the servers looked official with their ear pieces. They offered paper and crayons for the kids. There was a little weird pause and awkwardness as we tried to flag down our waitress to order but after that, everything was fine.

Fish nuggets - YUM! really well battered and seasoned. I would have ordered this as my kid's main meal instead lol. We all enjoyed this one.
Deep fried devil eggs- it was okay, nothing amazing as some reviews made it seem.
gumbo - a little salty...good but nothing great.
3 piece dark meat chicken - pretty juicy and crunchy. 
collard greens - my husband liked it, I thought it tasted a little weird, but I've never been a fan of it.
candied yam - MMMM! indeed candied, cinnamon, sweet and it felt like the holidays! yummy
the kids got fried chicken and chicken tender meals. Mac n cheese was good. Fries were enjoyed. I'd probably skip the chicken tender next time. It was mostly breading I felt.

Overall a satisfying meal if you know what to get.

I don't typically do reviews but this place is amazing! We ordered the gumbo and it came with corn bread. My husband had the fried chicken platter with collards greens,  fries and  biscuits. Best meal we've had in a long time! I highly recommend this place! We will be back soon! Thank you Fixins Soul Kitchen for an amazing meal!

We ordered on doordash and we will order again. The price is a bit high, but it isn't insane. The chicken wings were pretty good and I enjoyed the waffle though nothing was special. The mac n cheese had a really gross texture and the taste was extremely sharp and plain. I won't order the mac again. The biscuit was good and the fries were fine. We decided that the sides here are underwhelming but the chicken was solid.

Incredible service everytime I come. This was my third time coming. Eliza was our server and she made us feel well attended to. 

I love the deviled eggs here and recommend you start your order with them. This time I ordered the gumbo. It was amazing! Perfect flavor and good sized portion! I also like the option to get kool aid drinks. Very delicious all around.

Highly recommend this place and will definitely be back again.

Came in for lunch since my son had a haircut appointment next door. It is settled in a cute little area that offers shade outside and an area to sit if you have to wait. There is a bar area upon entering;it is very inviting and everyone was so nice. They offer indoor and outdoor seating. They had some old school R&A playing which was cool and I really liked since it is what I listen to. They have sweet tea which you don't find all that often here and it comes out in a mason jar. The food came out fast and hot and it was all delicious and good portions. The fried chicken was crispy and juicy and the sides were all good. My son and I ended up getting different sides so we could taste different stuff. The candied yams were sweet and yummy and the mac and cheese was creamy and flavorful. Overall not a bad place to stop and find.

Found Fixin's on tik tok as we were driving up to Sac from the Bay, ordered to-go online and food was ready before the suggested time frame.

Pulled up and the outside of the restaurant is aesthetically pleasing.
Inside was clean and a vibe. Very busy.

Experience and pickup customer service was awesome, everyone seemed happy to be at work during pickup, and food was actually ready and waiting in front! 

Ordered at 11:43am, said it'd be ready at 12:16, was ready at 12:05pm.

What we got:
Fried deviled eggs with bacon
Oxtails w/ rice, side of candied yams
3pc fried chicken & waffles (2 thighs 1 drum - love that they let you pick your cut!!)
Corn fritters and corn bread
Requested the grape butter on the side
Banana pudding
(Bonus : they threw in a peach cobbler & ice cream!!!)

We loved everything!
Have never tried the fried deviled eggs with bacon before - really enjoyed this. As it is, would give this a 8.5/10, only change I would add is a bit of Maldon sea salt on top to make it a 10.

My husband is Polynesian so any chance at "Keke" (fried dough balls) or in Fixin's case "corn fritters", we are taking it! Loved that they put bits of corn in these. It's a staple IMO.

Cornbread looked a little overcooked but it was not. It was slightly sweet and I wished I'd had some milk and honey to go with it.

Oxtails were flavorful!!! I love that they were able to braise and add spices to this but kept it from being hot/spicy! I can't handle any form of spicy but this was seasoned perfectly. (Have to mention that they included crystals hot sauce - and a lot of it, for those who want to spice it up).

Candied yams were good, no marshmallows though. (Not an issue for us).

Waffles were good, sweetened with the powdered sugar and really good with the grape butter! Fried chicken on top was great, crispy even though it sat in the to-go box for about 10-15 min before we opened it. I think this is missing a bit of salt too, but I also think if this is eaten with everything as a combined dish with syrup or hot sauce, that it's probably not noticeable.

Banana pudding was your standard pudding, I appreciated that the nilla wafers were fresh and crunchy and not soggy.

Cobbler was hella good! Flavorful. Only thing to make it a 10 in my book is to make the crust crunchier, but at this point I'm nitpicking. 

Overall would rate all of the above combined as a 9/10. 

Highly recommend this place. You know when the to-go food is good, it's all good! 

Will run it back when we are in Sac next!
Cool environment. Great mac n cheese. Service was great. Parking can be tough tho. Will come back

I had wanted to eat here for a very long time as I have enjoyed good soul food on my travels thru various southern states.  Finally, went last night with two companions. Our table wasn't immediately ready upon arrival so went to the bar for a beer. Service was a bit slow but otherwise friendly. Once seated, again a little slow but sped up once our server got to us. Ordered the gumbo and also a couple pieces of fried chicken, collard greens and candied yams to share.  The gumbo was very good, much like gumbo I recall having in New Orleans. The chicken was good but really not exceptional, I think Popeye's got them beat I'm afraid to say.  Collard greens were too salty, candied yams were good. So overall, the gumbo is a winner but the chicken isn't worth the price. I'd return for the gumbo and to try other menu items.

Food was delivered by DoorDash, was packaged so well it was all still warm when delivered.  The food was amazing no beyond amazing!  Green fried tomatoes were so crispy and delicious.  The shrimp and grits was the best I've ever had!

My friend and I made reservations at 1:00 today (Thursday) for outdoor seating.  The staff were very friendly.  They fired up the heaters after leading us to our table.  The outdoor seating is covered by shades.  

Our server Kim was very personable and provided prompt service.  I had the fried chicken salad and my friend had the burger and fries.  Both were terrific.  We will definitely go back.

Now this is what I call comfort food. My boyfriend I went on a Thursday afternoon. The ambiance was modern and comfy with southern accents. We got seated immediately and was greeted by Kim. We ordered the Fried Fish and the fixins being Grits with Cheese and Collard Greens, the Smothered Chicken with the gravy on the side and the fixins being Mac N Cheese and Candied Yams, the corn bread muffin and biscuits. The food came in a couple of minutes and piping hot. All we can say is it was delicious, and that our souls were fixed after it. The tartar sauce that came with the fried fish was like no other tartar sauce we've had before. The maple cayenne butter and grape jelly butter that came with the cornbread muffin and biscuits were to die for. The chicken was crispy and seasoned to perfection. All the sides were phenomenal, if we could get a bucket of it we would. The service was lovely, we met the owners Aunt and she was so sweet and inviting. This experience felt personal and comforting, making us want to come back again. The music was popping and so was the food, coming back ASAP.

Fixins is an authentic southern soul food restaurant near UC Davis med school. It's a great place for big groups or for your family. The casual ambiance combined with flavorful food, makes it ideal for a nice lunch or dinner. The service was quick, and the servers did a good job handling our big group with dietary restrictions. All the food was presented well, seasoned nicely and portioned nicely for the price. I really liked the pick 3 sides concept, which was the perfect amount of food. I'd definitely recommend coming here at least once!

Great restaurant in Sacramento for soul food brunch. I tried Fixins on a Sunday for brunch and luckily, because we had reservations, we didn't have to wait. The restaurant was crowded and there were plenty of people waiting outside for a table. 

We ordered the Oxtails ($28), which comes with rice, gravy, and a choice of Fixin. The oxtail was tender and delicious! We also tried the three piece fried chicken ($21), which comes with 2 Fixins and a biscuit. The fried chicken was amazing, definitely a fan favorite. For the Fixins, we chose the collard greens, potato salad, and red beans with rice. They were all good and added to the meal nicely. The biscuits were good! Especially with their raspberry butter. We were stuffed and had plenty to take home. 

Our waitress was very friendly and provided good service. The atmosphere was lively and homely. I had a great first visit to one of Sacramento's most popular brunch spots.

Comfort food with great service.
Brought my aunt who visits from two hours away to her favorite place, she loves the fried chicken and all the Fixins they offer.  The staff are always so friendly regardless if they are busy. The music is always jamming and the vibe is great. During this visit, the restaurant wasn't too busy so we got seated right away. The waiter connected with us and had some great conversations. Ordered the smothered chicken plate with greens and mac & cheese. Gravy tasted great and full of flavor. Greens are my number one fixin I get every time. Mac & cheese real cheesy And food is always served hot. We also ordered the two piece fried chicken plate. Can never go wrong with the fried chicken And that strawberry butter they serve is so good. I can eat it alone. But of course, tastes great on the biscuit or cornbread muffin. A must try restaurant went in the mood for some good comfort, soul food, and in the Oak Park area.

Here's an update to my previous review.  I didn't plan to return but I attended an event at the theater around the corner.  It was advertised to include a reception and meet and greet with an author at the nearby brewery. I purchased that add on.  During the event, they announced the reception would be held at Fixins'.  

The food would have been great but it was not hot.  They served two nuggets, mac and cheese, a biscuit and lemonade for $20. After about 20 people in line to met the author had their photos taken, the owner declared the line was "cut off", leaving me ant two others, all wearing our VIP wristbands, without the meet and greet opportunity. The book talk and the author presentation was exceptional but Fixins is missing the mark on customer service.

Get you some shrimp and grits. That's all you need to know. Servers were friendly and the cocktail I had was nice. But this was the best shrimp and grits I've had in years especially on the west coast. Try em-- you won't be disappointed.

I was referred here by an old boss and it does not disappoint. Man!! This place was good. I ordered Chicken and Waffles. My little one ordered the chicken tenders and my partner ordered the burger. All of it was so delicious. I mean, it had me in a food coma afterwards. The drinks were big and yummy. We all loved the food.

The waitress was so kind and helpful. They were patient and helped us order. Great vibes. Great service. Great food. A must try in the area.

My review may be a little biased. Only because our friendly waiter Jae did a great job, and those who do a great job and ask for 5 stars, get five stars! He kept our drinks full, checked in on us and brought our food out pretty prompt. The food was flavorful and everything came out at perfect temperature (HOT and fresh). I would absolutely come again for the greens. The Mac n cheese wasn't my favorite but the chicken was on point. 
My friend enjoyed her gumbo also. 

If this place is busy on an average day, I'd love to see how busy it gets on a popular day.

I was back up from the Bay and had to stop by! It was Sunday and crowded as I expected! I was smart enough to make a reservation! In the past, I was seated in the back. This time, I was in the front. They've got a beautiful bar, with plenty of TV's. It's got a great comfortable feel. I got the oxtails this time! SO many things to choose from, but had to go with the Ox! Great tail portions, and great deep flavor! I paired it with the greens, which I love! The grits were a first here for me and did not disappoint! So many other "soul food" restaurants fall short, and are unnecessarily expensive! This spot brings the flavor every time, and is very reasonable for what you get! A definite must go to place when in Sacramento!!!

Every Soul Often

Soul food joint founded by Oak Park native, ex Sacramento Mayor, and NBA All-Star Kevin "KJ" Johnson and his wife, Michelle.

Their aim is to elevate Soul Food in a classy, casual, and fun environment with fantastic food and great service. So, far they're well on their way as they now celebrate four locations nationwide, with this Oak Park one being the OG.

The Good:

*Reservations via OpenTable 

*Terrific service

*Nice selection of local brews from literally just down the street from Oak Park Brewing, Co. 

*Purple Kool-aid® was an excellent mix and a *must* with Soul Food, imo. 

*My picky son tore thru their Fried Fish plate. The fish was perfectly crisp, the fries were fantastic, and that mac and cheese was ooey-gooey goodness. 

*I'd had a hankering for Oxtails and Fixins delivered in spades. The melt-in-your-mouth Oxtail in a delicious umami gravy was heaven on a plate. The salty, earthy collard greens were the perfect texture and a great foil to the Oxtails. 

*Great ambiance and vibe 

*Dog-friendly outdoor dining

The Meh: 

*I'd read that parking can be an issue, but we didn't have much issue after circling the block a bit. Street parking only and be sure to read the signs. 

The Ugly:

*Why do I have to drive two hours for this?! C'mon, KJ, open one up in The Bay! Pretty please!!! :'(

Conclusion: Fantastic Soul Food and can't wait to try more of their menu in the future. Highly recommended.

If you're going to come on a weekend, I'd recommend making a reservation just in case. You never know when big groups might show up! Luckily, on the Saturday night we went for dinner, we got to sit down pretty much right away. I ordered the oxtail with mac and cheese. My friends ordered the fried fish with a cornbread muffin and then the fried chicken with cole slaw and potato salad. Everything was DELICIOUS! The oxtail fell off the bone. I am not the biggest fan of gravy on rice, so I do wish it was separate, but not a big deal. 
I think the fried chicken was probably my favorite. The batter is so crispy and crunchy and so flavorful. Dark meat is the way to go. So juicy. The potato salad had a good mustard flavor to it. I wasn't the biggest coleslaw fan, but my friends really liked it. I'm not usually into cornbread, but I would come back for this cornbread. SOOOOOO moist. Most places do not make it right. This place does! 
Everything was a hit. Definitely worth coming back to!

DONT SLEEP ON FIXINS HOLY MOLY THIS PLACE IS SOOOO GOOOD! The food is just so flavorful and the customer service is great! Very friendly and accommodating!! Try the seeet tea and potato salad and the CHICKEN!!! Best friend chicken in sac, they play the best music as well it's not too loud just the right vibe. 
We live in Folsom now so it's hard to find good soul food. We love it here and coming back omg this place don't skip!!!!!!
Liked this spot!!! Their breaded catfish was my fave - flaky but still crispy and soft on the inside. COLLARD GREENS AND MAC AND CHEESE!!

YOWZER  this place was a MUST!!
 collard greens
 Fried chicken 
 Corn muffins 
 Mac & cheese 
 sweet tea

We went for brunch and there was no line to get in. The service here was super slow and the food was mediocre at most. I've tried other chicken & waffle places out there and there are others that are significantly better than this place. 

I told the waiter that I was going to be sharing  My meal between three people. It took approximately 10  minutes for someone to come by and provide us with extra plates to share with. I even looked at a few waiters, tried to wave them down, and they just walked past me. This is a nice local brunch spot where people bring their kids and family. 

This restaurant is located on a major street and parking is red will be hard to find I found meter street parking on the street.

The fixins have to be top notch to stand by the delicious fried chicken - and they are! 

We always have to order the family meal even if there are only two of us dining because we will want left overs.

I was at a friend's birthday party and we were talking about our favorite places to eat. People brought up restaurants from everywhere but California. I am fairly well traveled and understood what they meant by the comparisons. I mentioned Fixins in Sacramento was solid and could hold up to some of the best soul food I've ever had. They went on like I didn't say anything. 

Fast forward a few weeks and the birthday celebrator is with me in Sacramento, so we go to Fixins. To make a short story shorter, anytime they comes to Sac it's the only place they want to eat. 

I've been coming since they've opened and have experienced them at every phase. I've mostly had my hiccups in take out service but that's to be expected with the amount of orders they take and that I have made.

Not I stamp I give up regularly but everything is good, there I said it. 

Soul Caesar depends on who's in the kitchen that day. The Smothered chicken is fantastic and the oxtails are my favorite, that gravy is good on anything. Shrimp and grits are tasty and flavorful, not too salty or bland without the gravy but hold up on their own. Chicken sandwiches are really good and filling.  

Mac and cheese is unique to them and good eats. Yams aren't overly sweet but very tasty. Greens are good and that's a lot from me because I don't eat everyone's greens. 

I will eventually update this as I come here a lot. With that said, if I had someone I was trying to show the best of Sacramento, I would definitely bring them here.
The food is decent but not the greatest soul food ever. Service was on par and the food came out pretty fast.

Food, Service and Ambience was very good for a party of approximately 65+ people
Thanks, will come again 
Elen

Ordered Fish with 2 sides...  greens and black eye peas $24 - Food was good. Nothing special. Greens were seasoned well. No hot sauce needed. Black eyed peas were "warm" I added hot sauce. 
Fish.... Well portion was unexpected at just (1 pc) and it was not Catfish... 
Atmosphere was nice and looked peaceful especially the outdoor seating. Oh and I ordered it Togo since it was 45min wait for 2. Food was not as hot as expected. We ate it at the park around just around the corner.

Came w a group of girls to do lunch. I had the fried chicken salad no Dijon and blue cheese dressing on the side.  I had an old fashioned, friend had the prickly pear, shared catfish nuggets and Mac and cheese. Great food good sides and vibe is chill.

Been to Grapewood Grill, Chef Paul's, & Dolls Kitchen. This place is amazing and the best soul food I've had. I dream of eating here again. The collard greens are the best ever. Black eyed peas are creamy & flavorful.

Absolutely had to shout out NyshiaGirl at Fixins in Sacramento--she made our visit unforgettable! My family and I came up from LA to celebrate my mom's birthday, and from the jump, NyshiaGirl set the tone with her warm energy and top-tier customer service. She knew the menu inside and out, and every recommendation she gave us was spot on--from the perfectly crispy fried chicken to the soul-warming mac & cheese.

Beyond the food, Fixins has a dope, down-home vibe that feels like a mix of Sunday dinner at Grandma's and a classic cookout with family. The music, the atmosphere, and the love in the food? 10/10, no notes. But it was NyshiaGirl who truly elevated the experience--she made us feel like VIPs the entire time. If you're in Sacramento, ask for her and trust her recommendations--you won't regret it. We'll definitely be back! Five stars all the way!

Wow!  This place was fabulous!  Super happy we had a reservation because they were already packed at 5pm on Saturday. All of the food was amazing!  The standouts were the fried green tomatoes, fried chicken breast, and the best collard greens I have ever had!  We also loved the fried fish and the fried deviled eggs!  Our server was super friendly and attentive. Very clean and nice restaurant!  We will be back!

The food, the service, the ambiance were everything. 

Had some LA natives visiting from out of town who were no strangers to great Chicken n Waffles, as they grew up eating Roscoe's Chicken n Waffles. The bar was set high for a group curious of what the soul food scene in Sacramento had to offer. Seeing that Sandra Dee's, my old go-to spot was now closed, I had to quickly find an alternative for my hungry guests. After quickly conducting a brief search on my reliable Yelp application, Fixins Soul Kitchen was the first to pop up. I quickly called to make a reservation, as I have heard great things via word of mouth of this place in the past and understand how quickly this place can get packed. 

When we arrived at our estimated seating time, we waited but five minutes before getting seated and were off to a great start.

From the fish fry, to the oxtails, to the chicken n waffles, Fixins Soul Kitchen did everything right. "The itis" shortly kicked in after we finished our meals, and everyone was then ready to go. 

Fast forward, we found ourselves visiting the LA location the next time we met up, as we needed our fixins. Needless to say, my group was quite impressed. 

An excellent establishment nestled in Oak Park, where former All-Star NBA player and Fixins Owner Kevin Johnson himself grew up.

The menu choices were slim as well as the food was just a little too spicy for my taste. I got the Smothered Chicken which was a little messy for me and the Black Eye Peas were a little watery.
However the ambience and the service was great. So if you love Spicy food I suggest making a reservation so you don't have to wait because it'll take a minute for you to get a table.

Great soul food in an upscale restaurant! Coming from Chicago, I've had my share of soul food and this is one of the nicest and modern soul food restaurants I've been in. I'm a fan of this restaurant that anchors Oak Park. Food is delicious and lots of flavorful classic "fixings" (including collard greens with turkey neck, fried okra, grits with cheese) that compliment the staples like fried chicken (dark meat an option which is great) and fish. Service is nice, especially when "Auntie" comes around!

**5 Stars - Just as Good as the First Time!**

This was my second time visiting Fixins Soul Kitchen, from out of town -- that alone should tell you how amazing this place is. From start to finish, everything was absolutely top tier.

The service is unmatched. The staff is friendly, attentive, and truly makes you feel at home. The restaurant itself is immaculate -- clean dining areas, spotless bathrooms, and a sleek, well-kept bar that's perfect for relaxing with a drink.

The food? Still unforgettable. Every dish is packed with flavor and made with love. You can taste the soul in every bite -- it's comforting, authentic, and consistently delicious.

Fixins is more than a restaurant -- it's an experience. I'll be back every time I'm in town. Highly recommend to anyone looking for top-notch soul food in a clean, welcoming environment.
Everything was sooooo gooood!! I'm from LA and didn't realize we have one here. I'll have to go. Service was fast and friendly. Lots of people the in the middle of the day on a Wednesday!!

Never been here before but I will not forget about this place moving forward! Menu was creative, tasty but approachable and fairly priced. The ambiance was clean and welcoming even though we got there on the earlier side. The real star of the show, however, was our server Cat. She was so cheery and helpful, suggesting ways to improve our selections. Knowledgeable about the menu too! We got the three happy hour food selections which were incredible portions for the price and the oak park brews on tap were super tasty. We each had entree selections and everything was so tasty and a great value for the price. Very very good! Will definitely be back.

The food was so good. The service was excellent. I always have food to take home because the portions are large. 
I have tried breakfast, lunch and dinner. I have never been disappointed.

The food here was so good, at times it was reminiscent of me childhood memories. The cool aid drinks and the fried fish were just fantastic. The service and the ambiance were great as well. I just felt like I could order my food and hang out.

This place was definitely food for the soul evoking so much nostalgia and elevated classic recipes from our rich black heritage. The shrimp n grits was divine. The gravy for the dish was **chef's kiss**. The gumbo, like everything else was rich and just as tasty as everything. It came with cornbread to soak up the gumbo sauce. I really liked the cornmeal fried green tomatoes and they tasted great with the gravies/sauces too and had a little kick of spicy.

Everything paired so good. I can't wait to go back!
We went for brunch on a Saturday and it was not too busy. The service was phenomenal. 10/10!

Yes, yes, yes, and another yes. I totally loved my experience at Fixins today. The outside of the restaurant was clean, the windows were clean, as soon as you walked in the environment was super inviting. I love the wall of shirts and all the pictures that they had displayed in the restaurant.

Everyone there was super inviting and so warm and hospitable. Everyone from the greeter, to our waitress, to the manager walking around. I looked over at the bar and the bartender was having a good old time with the customers socializing and making everyone feel welcome.

Made a trip to the restroom. It was super clean. No water spots anywhere, fully loaded toilet paper. 

The food was so good. Everything that each of us had was cooked perfect. The fish was crunchy on the outside light and fluffy and juicy on the inside. The macaroni and cheese totally reminded me of my great grandmothers, homemade macaroni and cheese. The greens were good, they had a little sweetness to them, which was a little different. The Caesar salad with the catfish nuggets was so good. Perfect amount of Caesar dressing, the crunchy and fluffy catfish nuggets was the perfect addition to the Caesar salad. I really loved the cornbread croutons. That was a unique spin on croutons.

100% again on my next trip up north.

Overrated. 

Fish - the batter did not stick to the fish. 

The yams were okay. 

I would not eat here again

I came in to celebrate my birthday with some coworkers on a Thursday around lunch time. Fixins was surprising busy! Everyone was kind but the star of my experience was Auntie Pearl. She was radiating kindness and she really made me feel at home. They created a vibe here that makes you feel like you are visiting a friend for a home cooked meal and I think we need more of that. The food is AMAZING! I got the chicken and waffles and it is my absolute favorite in ALL OF SACRAMENTO. I say this as a self proclaimed chicken and waffles fan girl. The spicy chicken sandwich was also delicious (I had a bite of my friends). Lavender lemon drop was very tasty. Please go here and give them all your money! I love Fixins!!!!

So delicious!! Everything we got was a hit! The oxtail was filling so well cooked. The candied yams OMG, melted in my mouth!! The red velvet cake too was omg HUGE for one and every bite made me want more!!

I love their fried chicken plate. I don't love how you get charged extra to swap out corn bread for the biscuit that comes with the plate. The sides are delicious, greens, Mac & cheese, potato salad and yams . I like to drink the tea half sweet, half regular with lemon. The music & vibes are always on point. The spacious seating & clean restrooms are a plus. The service is typically good.

As a first time visitor, I noted that this place was fun and constantly busy, which is always a great sign. It has a really full menu (including bar), a really nice vibe, and an exceptional staff. Customers were seated and served quickly, despite the line of people patiently waiting to get in. You could tell that they knew that it was worth the wait!!! The only issue that I had was with the chicken and waffle dish. The chicken was seasoned and fried to perfection. However, the waffle was literally as flat and soggy as a pancake! Not a fan. And upon reading the menu again, the restaurant even states that ordering this dish to go is not advised, as the waffle doesn't "travel well." My suggestion... improve the waffle recipe or serve readymade because I think that the chicken was upset that the waffle failed to hold up their end of the bargain! LOL

Otherwise, I highly recommend!

Delicious!
I truly enjoyed my meal. The service was very good. The drinks were a Delight. I will definitely be back there soon.

Food excellent.
Service friendly.
Ambiance clean, neighborly, fun.
Value very good.
I did take out, but had a chance to observe the operation while waiting.
Great soul food in a cute little restaurant with family vibes. Everything we had was delicious. Service was amazing. Will definitely be back again soon

First time coming here 
The decor is cute and the service was amazing. The bartender was super friendly and super funny. 
The rose martini is floral yet still sweet. Really adorable with a rose shaped ice cube.
The fried deviled eggs were delicious and a unique way of making them.
I got the fried chicken plate and the portions are HUGE. It was very delicious. 
My friend got the The gumbo and looked very good and he finished the whole plate.
Definitely will be coming back!!!!

This is our 2nd trip to dine here. We came years ago when it was fairly new but the service was atrocious. Things went much smoother today.  

We weren't really hungry so we ordered the 3 pc fried chicken with a salad side and red bean and rice. It came with a cornbread and strawberry butter. 
Everything was delish especially the fried chicken which was moist and a wonderful crispy, crunchy crust on the 2 thighs and one wing.  The fried chicken is the star.  

We thought we might get the gumbo if we were still hungry but we were not.  The staff was polite and checked back on refilling hubby's soda.  Thank goodness we stayed at the same hotel nearby so we could dine here again.

I had a really great experience at Fixins tonight! The food was amazing, as usual. Our server was friendly and attentive. 

I ordered a 3 piece fried chicken and boxed up my leftovers to take home. I forgot this box on the table after my meal, and by the time I remembered, our table was cleaned and the box of food thrown away by Fixins staff. One of the workers asked me what was in my box and replaced it for me! It was so kind!!!  I wish I got his name so I could mention it here!
Both the quality and size of portions were very impressive. The burgers were fat and the fries were extremely crispy. A good selection of casual-dining

I was in town for work and wanted some good home cooking. Read the reviews and decided to give this place a try. It was on a Wednesday night and they were crowded. 
Tried the fries green tomatoes which were excellent, just wish they were cut thicker. 
For dinner, oxtails with grits, how i judge a soul food place is by the oxtails, smothered pork chop and grits.  This place did not disappoint, the grits were creamy and had lots of flavor, the oxtail was tender and delicious.  My only issue was the gravy, the description said it was sweet, there was no sweetness at all.  
The place had a homey feeling to it, something you would find in a small southern town but with a modern flair.  

Over all excellent food, ill be back as I'm working in Sacramento off and on for the next two months. Just wish i took photos.

For context: The previous time I was here we had an amazing experience.  Chicken - good. Biscuits - good. Kool Aid - good. Service - good.

My girlfriend and I went just for their banana pudding on a Sunday night a few weeks ago. We got seated right behind the host's podium fairly quickly with menus in hand.  Unfortunately, no one checked in on us after getting seated.  We sat there for 10 minutes and no one took our order or asked if we wanted anything to drink.  We watched as another party got seated after us and a server got them situated pretty quickly. So at the fifteen minute mark we left.  Really disappointing since we had such an amazing experience the previous time.  

We went to another restaurant in midtown and had the polar opposite experience where we got seated, were handed menus, asked for drink orders and where the server asked us if we had any questions.

The staff are all friendly and helpful! I appreciated them explaining the menu and offering recommendations. I chose to order the sweet tea and my husband ordered the red kool aid. Super sweet and refreshing, they were perfect for a hot Sacramento day! 
The chicken was crispy and moist ... and fried to perfection. My sides of coleslaw & mashed potatoes complemented the chicken perfectly.
The biscuit on the other hand was over baked and hard on the outside. This may be some people's preference, but it is personally not mine. Left me wishing for a softer biscuit!

The service here is good. The staff are attentive, accomodating and friendly. The food and drinks are tasty. I have eaten here several times and love the soul food, family vibe, and the good service. I have tried the chicken and waffles are fire, the fried green tomatoes and cornbread are delish and the greens and Mac n cheese are on point. And yes, the bathrooms are clean bathrooms. Indoor/outdoor seating and a room for a small group (like 10-15 people). TVs for the game and a full bar. Street parking. Definitely recommend Fixins.

Update Review: 08/23/25 I'm not going to make this a long update. Just want to give credit where it's due, and I have to give it the Chef on duty today. My experience over 3 years ago was horrible and I said I WOULD NOT step foot in here again. But met up with my community group to discuss next steps in feeding the displaced people here in Sacramento, I ordered the 3 piece & 2 waffle. The chicken was juicy and very flavorful, the crispy skin was on point and I don't normally eat the skin. The bad taste I had in my mouth about this place was DEFINITELY removed today. And thank you to our server, you did an amazing job!

PORTIONS: filling and hearty
PRICE: appropriate
SERVICE: table side - fast!
SURROUNDINGS: clean and nostalgic 
FLAVORS:
Fried chicken (4/5) - batter is a little thick
Collard greens (5/5)
Okra (4/5) - we asked for less salt, but that could be a preference 
Mac n cheese (3/5)
Red beans and rice (3/5) 
Biscuits (3/5) 
Strawberry butter (5/5)
Sweet tea (5/5)

The food was really good! Fish batter was well seasoned and perfectly crispy. I wish I just got the fried fish since it also came with hush puppies!! I ordered the fish sandwich and it came with a koolaid pickle, which was my first time trying it. It was yummy! The service was quick and good! The ambiance is a family style diner and brightly lit.

I come here for their oxtails & cornbread. Gotta ask for the grape butter too! Wasn't no meat left on my bones. Tore it up! I washed it all down with a red Kool-Aid. They always play good music too, Jagged Edge, Genuine, music that you light a candle & clean your house too.
Wonderful black-owned establishment in downtown Sacramento! Delicious food and fabulous vibes!!
The best place to come when you're craving soul food. Their sides are my favorite specifically the greens and yams. They also sell koolaid which is a huge throwback

I ordered from Fixins Soul Kitchen for the first time last night via Doordash. Absolutely Delicious!!!! The fried chicken, potato salad, macaroni and cheese, and the biscuit! Divine! Can't say enough how delicious it was!
Great food, great customer service and an awesome vibe inside. One of the best in Sacramento
Good customer service after seated food came quick ,nice atmosphere went here with my hubby and son

Me and my wife had joined some friends for breakfast and came to Fixins. When we came into the restaurant we were greeted by staff and the staff found us a table. The staff were friendly and very attentive. My wife ordered the three piece fried chicken meal and I ordered the chicken and waffles. My wife ordered yams, greens and black eyed peas for her side. My wife thought the chicken was seasoned well but the batter was not sticking to the chicken. She thought the sides were mid. My waffle was good but a little flat. The chicken was seasoned well but the skin was very loose and messy. My wife ordered the deviled eggs. She looked the deviled eggs. Ambiance was customer service friendly.

One of my favorite restaurants in Sacramento the service is usually always pretty good and the food is so good! Its always hot & fresh! It does take a while if you go on a weekend because it's usually busier. Their mac & cheese is my favorite and the prices are pretty reasonable. It's always clean inside & they are always playing good music.

f you're craving comfort food that hits all the right notes, this soul food spot needs to be on your radar. From the moment I walked in, the menu had me struggling to choose because everything sounded incredible, from chicken and waffles to smothered pork chops.

I finally settled on the smothered fried chicken. The chicken stayed perfectly crispy even under a rich, flavorful gravy. Every bite was juicy, crunchy, and packed with that homestyle goodness you hope for in soul food.

Now, let's talk about the sides because they deserve their own spotlight. The greens were seasoned to perfection, with that savory, slow-cooked flavor that feels like a warm hug. And the yams? Buttery, sweet, and decadent, with just the right touch of richness. Honestly, they could've been dessert.

Speaking of treats, this place has a full bar and I couldn't resist ordering the Adult Kool-Aid. Not only was it delicious and refreshing, but they also use frozen Kool-Aid ice cubes so your drink never waters down. Such a clever touch that makes a big difference!

A quick pro tip: while there's plenty of seating, this spot is super popular. If you're on a schedule, definitely make a reservation to avoid the wait.

Overall, this soul food restaurant delivers on every level--flavor, vibe, and service. I'm already planning my next visit, and trust me, you'll want to do the same!

Fixins was everything I wanted it to be! The space is clean, modern and cozy. Would definitely come here often if I lived in Sacramento.

I had the Oxtails with potato salad and the adult kool-aid to drink. The kool-aid was exactly what you would imagine and was great. The oxtails were perfection! You should order this only amongst close friends/family because you will need to use your fingers to enjoy every piece of meat off the bones. 

Our table shared the pimento cheese dip and it was delicious I could easily keep munching on this stuff nonstop. My friends enjoyed the smothered chicken and fried catfish as well. The fish was fried with the perfect loud crisp and the chicken was smothered in flavorful gravy. 

Staff was friendly and service was a fair speed. Can't wait to come back sometime!
I've been a few times and service has been good from the time we arrived and until we left. The food is so good and well worth the drive to Sacramento.

I've been to Fixins in another city before and loved it so I wanted to try while in Sacramento for my niece's birthday. The restaurant was nice, clean, and in a trendy part of town. We were able to get a table right away and just in time for happy hour at 3pm. The waitress was nice and accommodating. We ordered shrimp and grits, Hennessy wings, greens, cornbread, and 2 lavender lemon drops. The food was hit and miss. My niece liked her shrimp and grits but noted that there was more gravy than grits. My greens were ok but the Hennessy wings were a total miss as the taste and texture were totally off. When I got my bill, bbq wings were indicated so maybe I got the wrong thing. The lemon drops seemed to be all lemon. I believe this may have been an off meal for them and will return the next time I'm in the vicinity. I know the food has the potential to be very tasty.

Heavy but amazing food. The oxtail was a bit too fatty/saucy for our preference but overall the food came out spectacular. We were able to be immediately seated for 2 at 6pm on a Friday. We were constantly checked up on throughout the night to ensure we were accommodated.

To start the atmosphere was great. A VERY diverse crowd.

The service was amazing! It was a sight to see, not only the staff working hard but Kevin Johnson right there bustin tables!!

Our food came hot and delicious!  I ordered the catfish and let me tell you absolutely LOVED it!! Hot moist and flaky.

Deep fried deviled eggs... spot on. Great appetizer.

All of our drinks were made to perfection!

We will definitely be going there again!!

Came with some friends who wanted to try out this spot. Cute nicely decorated restaurant with a lot of seating available. Street parking wasn't too bad on a Tuesday evening. Reservations can be easily made on their website via OpenTable. Service was good, no complaints.

We shared some appetizers. Deep Fried Deviled Eggs were pretty good. I didn't like the Fish Nuggets too much, texture was weird for me. I liked the Artichoke Dip the best. It had a nice kick to it, which the menu didn't warn of that at all. Thankfully, I can tolerate some spice.

I originally wanted to try the chicken and waffle, but for $1 more, the 2 piece fried chicken meal offered more and seemed like a better bang for my buck.
I ordered the 2 piece dark fried chicken with coleslaw and mac and cheese. The meal also came with a biscuit and uniquely tasty butter. The fried chicken was good. Crispy skin, and the meat wasn't dry, but I wouldn't say juicy. The biscuit was okay a little hard, but the butter made up for it. Is it some type of berry butter, raspberry? strawberry? Im not entirely sure, but I really liked it, and I even put it on my chicken haha. Coleslaw was a little on the drier side. I liked the Mac and Cheese. Good flavor.

I'm not really too into soul food in general, but I thought this place was okay and was what I expected. Overall, I had a good experience. I appreciated that we weren't rushed out as we kept chatting for awhile. I'd go back if it was convenient or if someone else really wanted to go. It's not my type of food I'd crave to come back for, and I dislike street parking.

Had an amazing experience. Appetizers tasted decent, definitely enjoyed the deviled eggs more than the tomatoes (7/10). Chicken was juicy and well seasoned, with a nice crunch. This place is definitely not skimpy on the portions at all! (9.5/10). Candied yams had chewy texture and was not too sweet (8.5/10). Mac and Cheese had good noodle consistency and very cheesy- exactly what you want from this dish (9/10)

Eliza was our server. She was consistently attentive and provided timely service, always checking in on us to make sure we were okay. The restaurant is live, with hip-hop music playing in the background. Good food and vibes! I'll be back again next time I visit Sacramento.

I couldn't have family visiting from out of town & not being them to Fixins. It's a must!
It gets busy even on weekdays so reservations are always recommended or you will have to wait. 

Once seated, staff are nice, food comes out hot & fresh, & Auntie Pearl might stop by to say Hi to check in. 

10/10 - Fried Chicken & Waffle: oommmgg you can't go wrong with this & it's a must order every time I visit. Chicken was seasoned & fried perfectly; waffle wasn't too thick & everything pairs well with the strawberry butter. 

10/10 - Fried Fish: yuummmyyy crunchy fish that was breaded & fried to perfection; they gave a good amount of fried fish along with delicious sides like mac & cheese, coleslaw, & hush puppies.

10/10 - Charred Okra: if you like okra these are for you! Char on the okra was just enough to not overpower the okra flavor & can't go wrong with bacon on it too. 

10/10 - Candied Yams: these were dripping with all the yummy goodness of being candied; yams weren't too sweet & just enough spice to give it warmth.

10/10 - Pimento Cheese Dip: this was super gooey, cheesy, & pimento flavor really came through; it's was a nice change from a traditional cheese dip.

9/10 - Fried Green Tomatoes: coated nicely & fried well; tomatoes were cut a bit too thin, but the flavor was dynamite. 

7/10 - Banana Pudding: it's tasted like a traditional banana pudding using boxed pudding which was a bit disappointing; nothing made this dessert different.
One word: flavor! Delicious, I get why this is a popular eatery in Sacramento. I'll be going again to try the rest of the menu!

DELICIOUS!!! The food is just amazing and it pairs well with their amazing servers. We had such great service since we sat down. I mean we ordered and our food was ready in 10 minutes. This place is no joke. I mean I would love to try everything on the menu because all the sides we tried were sooo good. Chicken is seasoned just perfectly and so crispy. This place had a line of people waiting to get in and was packed. It was very loud and was a fun place to be on a Sunday night. Highly recommend for food, service, and ambiance.

This is definitely a place to try at least once if you're a fried chicken lover. Fixens offers a very simple menu and a few selections of southern sides to pair along with your entree. I ordered the 3 piece chicken with gravy on the side which is a must. The quantity and for what you pay is definitely well worth it in my opinion.

However, although the chicken was extremely juicy and tender due to the 24 hr brine. I found the chicken to be bland with no flavor. After consulting with my partner, she agreed. I recommend ordering the gravy on the side to add another depth of flavor. All in all, if you live in Sacramento, you have to go here at least once.
Pretty solid soul-food! I'd say this place is a good entryway into the world of soul-food. Seasoning could be more on-point. Been here 3 times so far. Ambiance is lovely!

Worth the wait!
I've been to Fixins twice. The first time I had the 2 piece fried chicken plate with greens and mac & cheese. It comes with your choice of corn bread or biscuit. I had the biscuit and will always opt for the biscuit! It comes with sweet butter. Dee-lish! 

The fried chicken was tasty and juicy. The greens ate so good as well as the mac & cheese. 

On my second visit I ordered a blueberry margarita, with the choice of salt or sugar on the rim. I chose to try sugar, but unfortunately the sugar with the condensation of the drink made the glass and my fingers sticky. We also had the deep fried deviled eggs. Sooo good! I highly recommend. 

For my main, I had the smothered pork chop which does not come with corn bread or biscuit, so we ordered a side of biscuits. My sides were potato salad and the greens. Everything was delicious, as expected, but I will not be ordering the potato salad again unless I order a sandwich. It was good, but didn't really go with smothered pork chop. There was not any room left for dessert. But that's another reason to return. 

The staff has been very nice and friendly. And the atmosphere is lively, with good music playing. 

I highly recommend this restaurant for groups, date night or if you want to sit at the bar on your own! It's lively and delicious! 

On my first trip, the tab was around $55, for 2. We had 2 dinners and my bf had a beer. 

The second time, our tab was $88 for 2. We had 1 cocktail, 1 beer, and appetizer, 2 dinners plus a side of biscuits.

My mouth is salivating as I write this review mmm! First time trying this place and I tell you definitely tasty finger licking good! I had the fish nuggets! Definitely delicious. I actually should've just ordered the fish meal. For so food place the ambience was pretty cute. Definitely a place to go if you're catching up with friends and you like to hear music! The service was great! Definitely recommend

Auntie Pearl is such a lovely person! She welcomes you in as soon as you are seated and makes you feel like you are having a fun family dinner. 

The food here is absolutely delicious! Oxtails, Shrimp N Grits, and definitely the fried chicken (dark meat is my favorite). The fixins (side dishes) are phenomenal! 

There is tons of seating indoors. Definitely coming back here.

The food was delicious. The shrimp and grits were better than in New Orleans. The catfish was AMAZING. Everything is delicious including the drinks. I love the style of the restaurant, the music and the staff was great.

Tried this place out today for lunch and was pleasantly surprised. I ordered the chicken and waffles, a side of fish nuggets, red beans and greens.

The chicken portions are huge! Definitely didn't disappoint on flavor. The waffle butter was also great!

The sides were just ok. I found the greens to be a bit tangy and didn't care as much for the flavor of the red beans. She catfish nuggets however were amazing.! 

The service was friendly and our server came to check on us often. The place is small with a patio area but definitely had an amazing vibe. 

If I am in the area again I will definitely be back to try new dishes!

Visited this restaurant today and as this was my first time here, I am extremely disappointed and will never return. 

Arrived well after brunch hours on a Sunday afternoon (3pm). Wait was around 30 minutes so not too bad. My daughter and I were seated towards the back of the restaurant where only 1 other table was seated. About 10 minutes in our waiter greeted us and said he'd be right with us, then proceeded to take care of another table on the patio. Server was rude and unwelcoming. A couple minutes later another table was seated in our area and their waiter greeted them and gave them water. Another few minutes passed and the other server graciously gave us waters as well (no sign of our designated waiter still). It's now been over 20 minutes and our server returned to take our order. We order the fish plate, hot chicken sandwich, regular and adult koolaid. Another party is seated at this time and given waters by the other server. 

Literally 20 minutes pass and our server only brings out the regular kool-aid (but he has time to continue to check on his one other table) Meanwhile, the other 2 tables have received their meals BEFORE we have received anything, even my adult drink!. At this point almost 45mins have passed and I ask the other server for the manger. I speak to the manager and inform him of the situation. He brings our original server to our table and give some excuse of the order wasn't placed in the POS due to some system issue. Complete BS. I tell them forget the order, charge me for my daughter's juice and I'll leave. The manager does nothing to rectify the situation or the terrible customer service. He just goes on the patio and chit chats/laughs with the server.

 I've seen this restaurant on TikTok and was excited to finally give it a try. The lack of customer service and simplicity of ensuring every guests leaves satisfied is the reason why I will NEVER dine at this establishment again. Spend your money elsewhere!

I was ready to enjoy the smothered chicken, my favorite. I arrived a bit late and before I ordered a family member insisted that I take a bite of her smothered chicken. I was soooo glad that I did. WTH? It was so spicy! Her lips were burning and so was mine from ONE bite. I asked the waitress to ask the chef if this was a mistake. She came back and said she was told it a NEW recipe. Clients should be able to add hot sauce but to come this spicy with no notice on the menu? NOPE, not good. I was so disappointed. I've been telling friends don't order the smothered chicken/pork chops because it's not what it used to be. I ended up with fried chicken. Ugh. Why change a good thing? This is how you start losing clientele. The old saying "if it's not broken, don't fix it" applies here!!!!

This place did not live up to the hype........... IT SURPASSED IT!!!!! Wow. That fried chicken is UNREAL. Every single piece is coated with the most phenomenal crunchy skin I have ever had, and the seasoning of the breading has so much depth and consistency. Regarding the meat, each piece is cooked perfectly with outstanding levels of juiciness and flavor from their 24 hour brine. It did not go unnoticed at our table that each part must be cooked for a different amount of time in order to achieve the same exemplary result across the board. Choosing a favorite piece is impossible. Seriously, that fried chicken is UNREAL, and they crank out those platters. My close friend and I went on a Wednesday. It was pretty packed, as there were only two or three tables available when we walked in. We ordered the Family Meal, which includes eight pieces making up an entire chicken, two large sides, and then we opted to get two cornbread muffins and two biscuits instead of the standard four biscuits. The fries and the mac and cheese were both superb. The biscuits were very nice and the cornbread muffins were off the hook. We ended up getting three additional pieces of their magnificent fried chicken. My mouth is literally watering right now as I am recalling all of the details and describing the experience. The atmosphere at Fixins is relaxed and inviting, and each team member is friendly, but our server deserves special recognition. Isis has a warm smile, a considerate and engaged presence, and a personable aura that seems completely natural. She is terrific. Regarding our feast, only two pieces of chicken came home with me. The next morning, I made my own waffles and heated up the chicken in my air fryer low and slow. They somehow retained the ridiculous layers of crunch after sitting in a container in the fridge overnight. What a bonus treat to have such a breakfast at home. IF YOU ARE LOOKING FOR THE BEST FRIED CHICKEN, Fixins is the answer!!!!! Hands down. Some people I know are already trying to figure out when they will be going. This authentic soul food restaurant has earned the highest recommendation that I can give!!!!!

Fantastic food and even better service! Our meals were hot, delicious, and overall wonderful. I've only had the opportunity to come twice, but both times I've left with my expectations exceeded.

Thank you to our server Nyshia for giving us fantastic service the entire evening! Especially by going above and beyond to ensure our party was more than  satisfied. 

Absolutely would recommend anyone to come by, not only will you enjoy your food (it's a given.. it's just that good) but you will enjoy yourself, and felt taken care of. 

(I would add pics of our food, but we were too eager to eat!)
Service was great!  Ordered the ox tail and it was so tender and flavorful.  Mac & cheese was solid.  The greens were so good.

I haven't been here in a couple years - my how time flies!  My 15 year old son who's volunteering at Mercy General Hospital opted to come here over Obo for lunch. And I'm so glad we came.  This is hands down the best Southern restaurant in Sacramento.  Not just because of the food which is legendary.  But because this restaurant is on another level.  The restaurant opened in 2019 one year before the pandemic and was modernly designed.  But today as I walked to the bathroom I read the history of the Johnson family in Sacramento and wow!  Kevin Johnson the owner was born and raised in Sacramento and gained fame as an NBA all-star for the Sacramento Kings and went on to become the Mayor of Sacramento. And he is descended from antebellum slaves in South Carolina - wow!  I'm a huge history buff and this place has history and tradition oozing from all corners.  The service is friendly and attentive.  The ambience is excellent and relaxing with hip hop / R&B music playing.  I had a wonderful experience eating lunch here and spending time with my son.

I ordered pickup from Fixins and got a text saying my food was ready, so I showed up on time. But when I arrived, my order wasn't ready because they said they didn't have containers for my food. Meanwhile, other customers were picking up their orders just fine.

I ended up waiting an extra 25 minutes, at which point I said, "If you can't find containers you can just refund me." They discouraged me from doing that, telling me calling the manager for a reimbursement would take longer than just waiting for my food. Eventually they brought out my order, but by then it was no longer hot.

It's really frustrating to have a to-go order handled this way. Fixins is great when things go smoothly, but this experience was pretty disappointing.

Great atmosphere cute place
Artichoke dip 20 stars sooo good

But the food was way over seasoned
amazing food and appetizers. could not get enough of their mac and cheese, great service and friendly atmosphere!

For southern fried chicken and sides, this place is pretty good. The Los Angeles location might have slightly better fried chicken but it's not a huge difference. Biscuit here also seemed smaller than the LA location.

Mixed it up and got slightly difference sides, have to say the potato salad (which seems to use its share of mustard) was more okay than memorable.

Seated at the bar, service was quick if a bit inconsistent, maybe they're a tad short of staff?

I was born and raised in Bama and when I say they nailed it on the soul food, I mean it! We did a family style and they did it RIGHT! It is sooo good! I'm bringing some people back with me next time, for sure. The best fried chicken I've had in a minute and the greens sent me to another planet!! Definitely get the friend green tomatoes-they tasted just like the ones my dad made when I was a kid. Felt like home, from the food, to the ambience and the kindness of the staff. Aunt Pearl stopped off at our table and had a nice chat. She was an absolute delight! Loved every minute of it! My mouth is watering typing this! LOL

We had an exceptional meal! My wife had the catfish with sweet potato and collard greens. My son had the kids meal with a drum stick and mac and cheese which also came with a huge chocolate chip cookie. I had the spicy chicken sandwich with grits and cheese. 
The portions were enormous, the flavoring was delicious and the price was reasonable. 
Inside there is a very southern ambiance, with unique artwork on the walls. And towards the bathroom there is an entire wall filled with photos of the family that has operated this business for generations. 
Parking was easy and the service was fantastic, so do yourself a favor and check this place out!
Took fam that came into town for lunch. Their first time. Got there around 1pm and was seated at the big table. Food, drinks and service always good

Vegan friendly soul food that's so flavorful! First time here and the place is so cute. Great service, multiple vegan options (go online to check it out as it's not on their regular menu) and fun place, I'd go back for more for sure

If you're looking for some good Southern cooking this is the place! It's in the downtown area of Oak Park. We chose patio seating and the weather was perfect for it. Upon checking in, we waited maybe 15 minutes and got a text that our table was ready. Our waiter was Jae and he was great! He came by to check in on us to see how we were doing. Food was all good and service was great! Would definitely recommend this place and would love to come back.

Ok so lots of places have chicken & waffles right? But NOT LIKE THIS! That "R_sc_es" in L.A. will never see me again. Fixins brines their chicken 24 hours and OHHHH BAYYBEEE! The flavor is amazing! And it's perfectly fried, so light and crispy! If you go, please just pick it up & bite it!! It's painful to watch people take a fork & a knife to fried chicken...LOL don't  cheat yourself out of the experience, be a real FOODIE & FEEL IT! Let's talk about that waffle...I'm about to tell you it came with a dab of "grape kool-aid" butter and hot syrup.  Typically something I would NOT try but I'm SOOOooooo glad I did! The combination is completely UNEXPECTED and delightful! It doesn't sound good, well, it didn't initially to me but I decided to go for it! And it was worth it! The omelet drew me in because it had collard greens, I would have liked more collards in the omelet and there was a huge GOB of cheese that made me send it back. They made me a new one & I took it home to enjoy. So I recommend you say "easy cheese" on the omelet. The gravy/sauce the shrimp & grits  came in was phenomenal! They were playing all my favorite gospel songs and the service was great! Oh and the sweet tea was authentic like right out a Georgia kitchen! We actually poured it into our water glasses so one of them makes 3 or 4 glasses if you don't want it too sweet! I'm FIXIN to go back to FIXINs...
Love this restaurant the vibe was really nice. The grits and the fried catfish were my favorite. If you don't like spice, don't get the fried chicken. It was really good but too spicy for me.

Run, Don't Walk to Fixin's!

On a recent trip to Sacramento, I found myself going back to Fixin's not once, but twice because the food was that good. This Black-owned gem brings soul food to the table in a way that's both satisfying and surprisingly light, drawing patrons from all walks of life. It's the kind of place where family, friends, and big groups feel right at home, filling the lively, bustling atmosphere with good vibes.

Let's talk about the fish sandwich. It came piled with all the "fixin's" alongside a side of collard greens. Now, greens have never been my favorite--until I tried their greens! I even called my mom and told her I'd been transformed. This is soul food that feels fresh and hearty without weighing you down.

The staff at Fixin's are another highlight, seemingly floating around to help customers with ease and smiles, handling the busy flow with grace. Whether you're celebrating a birthday or just looking for an incredible meal, this is a must-stop spot if you're in Sacramento. Don't miss it!

If you're anywhere within driving distance of Fixins, I highly recommend that you make your way out there to try some amazing food!

We don't go to the Sacramento area often so we had to try a few of their dishes and I'm telling you that there was not one thing that we didn't like.

Hot chicken sandwich & fried chicken were so perfectly breaded and fried--even though we got takeout, we didn't miss out on the crispiness! Mac and cheese & Red beans and rice were perfect sides. Ohh the corn fritters--hush puppies are my favorite so this is like an even better version. We loved everything that we ordered because of the great flavors!

There was a wait for takeout and looked like a wait for dining in but the food is very much worth it. Can't wait to try more from their menu!

I can't believe I haven't wrote a review for this already. I absolutely love this soul food place. The customer service is great, food is piping hot fresh, the music and ambience is refreshing. We don't have too many great soul food restaurants out here but this place is my go to. I refuse to come here during the weekend as it will be packed, not looking forward to having a seat outside during the cold or heat.. My favorite is the fried catfish (comes with hush puppies) the shrimp and grits with light gravy, the macaroni, greens and candied yams. Their kool-aid and alcoholic drinks are great as well but I'm a sweet tea person and boy is it sweet (ask for a side cup of water to water it down or an Arnold palmer). I usually spend $45-$50 (that's per person) with meal, drink, and appetizer. My favorite appetizers are the catfish nuggets, and the fried deviled eggs (definitely a must try). This place is always my go-to for soul food, I'm so thankful that it's in Sacramento.

The food was outstanding but the wait to just order the food was ridiculous. I'm giving 3 stars out of sympathy for our server, Kymberly (I think). She was a sweetheart the few times we were able to catch her to place our drink and food order but I could tell she was very very overworked and overwhelmed. It was just her and one other server on this busy Tuesday night. I believe that if folks are calling out or what have you then the owner(s) and managers should roll up their sleeves and be working the floor too. From beginning to end we stayed at the restaurant for about 2.5 hours so unless you just got hella time to kill - I would reconsider before sitting down. I can't say the food was worth the wait but when you're hungry af - you might end up cursing someone out who's trying their damnest. BTW - the mac n cheese is cooooool but it doesn't really have much seasoning to it and there's something gritty about the texture that makes me feel like I need to try it again but I'm hesistant. Its giving the cheese roux didn't cook down but I might have to give this place another go to find out.

The yams and greens (without hot sauce!) are certified and the chicken is crispy af!

I have been wanting to try their fried chicken in forever, and it did not disappoint. The meat is juicy and flavorful. Great portion. I love that there are a variety of sides (Fixins). It was hard to pick two for my sides. I got the fries, which was so crispy. I had to get the charred okra with bacon because hello..bacon! The freshly baked biscuit, which came with special butter, was incredibly savory.  

I was told to must try the catfish nugget. It was very good but the batter was a little much for me. Although, I think they need the thick batter, or the fish will fall apart.  My friend had the shrimp salad, and the portion is very generous.
