I didn't order any cocktails, but the drink menu looks very good. The gal next to our table had the Spiked Pineapple Palmer, and it looks delicious. I have to try one of those the next time. 

In case you didn't know, Fixins is owned by former NBA All-Star and Mayor of Sacramento Kevin Johnson and his wife, Michelle. Don't forget to check out the wall with Johnson family photos. It's pretty cool.

Consistently delicious food, friendly and attentive service in the perfect location.   I'm sensitive to loud noise due to hearing loss and nerve damage in the Army but I wasn't bothered by the chatter of the crowd.  It's definitely a destination restaurant to take  friends visiting from out of town.

I previously rated this restaurant and gave it a much better rating in the past than I am now. My family and I came here after church for brunch. We noticed it being unusually empty for a Sunday mid-morning. We were promptly seated. Our server Alize came and introduced herself-and brought us waters. We ordered our drinks (coffee, lemonade and an IPA). All was going well until.....it wasn't 

My silverware and napkin bundle had hair in it. She said she'd bring a new one (but she didn't and left the soiled one on the table).
I had to request a refill on my coffee from another server because ours disappeared. We didn't see our server for at least 25-30 minutes. Our food actually came first before she came back to check on our drinks or status while we waited.

I ordered chicken wings and a waffle. When I went to pull the chicken wings apart, not only was it cold to touch but it almost took superhuman strength to do. The inside was raw, which is dangerous and deadly. See below. The waffle was soggy. It's ironic that after I took a picture of the inside of the wing, Alize promptly appeared. I told her the issue, she (speaking in her rapid dialect) said I'll get you some more. I told her no. Let's change it to 2 eggs scrambled with cheese and asked her if they had turkey bacon. She said yes. So I added the turkey bacon and a biscuit. They brought the biscuit first. Then along comes a plate with 2 eggs scrambled with cheese and a piece of sausage. I told them I asked for turkey bacon. The person delivering the food, (a very nice lady) asked, "You did?" I confirmed. 30 seconds later, Alize appears and says, "I'm sorry we don't have turkey bacon. We only have turkey sausage." I told her about my dislike for sausage and I specifically asked about turkey bacon. She then said, "Oh. Would you like to look at the menu for something else?" Being hopeful, I did. But nothing appealed. Scared to try something else. 

This place has really gone downhill and rapidly. My son's pancakes were soggy but he didn't complain. My husband's shrimp and grits meal barely contained any grits. Just a bad decision altogether. 

It took the server a whole 20 additional minutes to bring the check. Although she placed customer dislike instead of raw, cold food, she did comp my attempts at a good meal.

This place has run its course. A surprise inspection by the health department and OSHA should take place.

It's also important to note, I'm from the Deep South and this definitely isn't southern soul food. Maybe it's a west soul flair.

I recommend... nice vibe, cool people, hip area.
I definitely plan to visit again when I'm in the Sacramento area.

Food:  the food was very tasty and seasoned perfectly. I had the delicious fried green tomatoes; I ordered the two piece (white meat) chicken with waffle meal. The brine on the chicken allowed all the seasoning to marinate and tenderize the meat of the chicken  it was very tasty and crispy. I would have enjoyed it better if the temperature was hotter. The waffle was average and I wished it was a little more fluffy and crispier but the complete dish was wonderfully executed.

Service: food was served timely. Wait staff was attentive and aware.

Ambiance: nice vibe, perfect lighting, and nice patio seating,  we sat at the bar and enjoying chatting with the bar tender and other guests 

Parking: plenty of street parking

Visited Sunday afternoon, nice inside restaurant, hard to find parking. it was packed the wait was about 45mins.  Food was good we order fried deviled eggs, boyfriend ordered chicken n waffles boyfriend he really liked.  I ordered fried chicken plate with greens, Mac and cheese a side of red beans and side of yams.  He took the red beans and rice  the tans were mediocre however, they give you a healthy portions on the chicken which he took for lunch for two days which included the chicken I didn't finish.
Food was great, service and ambiance next level. Will definitely eat here again. We need one in Tracy.

For my beverage. I ordered a sweet tea. For my meal, I had fish nuggets, two pieces of fried chicken, and a side of macaroni and cheese. The tea was pretty good, and the fried chicken was very juicy and flavorful. The fish nuggets had fantabulous flavor as well. The mac and cheese was completely legit. I had the peach cobbler for dessert, and it was off the chain. The ambience was very lively, and the server was very responsive and accommodative.

DELICIOUS!
I've only been here once (so far) and came with my husband and grandmother for brunch on a weekend. They were busy, but service was still fast! We waited about 15 minutes for a table and when we were seated, our waiter was very prompt to get our order started with some drinks. We ordered the Oxtails, 3-piece Fried Chicken plate, and an order of a Wing & Waffle.
The portions were VERY generous. I loved the Mac n cheese and the Oxtails - *chefs kiss* SO GOOD!!!! Like.. falls-right-off-the-bone good!!!
Rice that came with it was also very flavorful.

Chicken was also crispy and delicious, very tender/moist and tasted great with the hot sauce. Biscuit was sweet (yum!!) and yams also good (and I'm not typically a fan of yams). I didn't try the Waffle, but my husband seemed to really enjoy it.

We sat indoors, but their outside patio also looked really nice! Bathrooms, also kept very clean.
It's close to school, so I'm sure I'll definitely be back again, soon!

Yummy and tasty.  I've only been once but I look forward to going again.  The staff was pleasant and the food was seasoned perfectly.  I highly recommend you check out this place next time you are in the area.
Small disappointing fish sandwich for big price tag. So sad, and this was after at 40 minute wait at 3 pm on a Saturday.  Bummer, I had heard good things.

This place far exceeded my expectations. The food was delicious, we had the perfectly fried chicken, the gumbo, artichoke dip and oxtail. I couldn't tell you which one was better, so much flavor in all the dishes. My only regret was not saving room for dessert. Highly recommend this place for authentic, delicious food. Isis, our server was helpful with food suggestions and gave our big party great service.

Came for dinner since there was a Yelp event going on and because my wife said to try it because I would like it and let me tell you...I loved it. They really have a good cook because everything was flavorful and good. The food came out hot and quick. They were playing some throwback 90's old school music which was so good and the environment was so happy, relaxed, and fun. We had the smothered chicken, smothered pork chops, Mac and cheese, coleslaw, and banana pudding. The portions are a very good size and the prices are not outrageous. Props to the cook. Our server was sweet but had a soft voice but still a good server and always had a smile.

The best fried chicken I've ever had! don't know what else there is to say about it the thigh and breast were extremely juicy and crunchy. The waffles were perfectly crisp but also had a great chew and flavor. The biscuit was great with the grape jelly butter....? (I think that's what it was ). The fish nuggets were extremely surprising it was just the right amount of crispy so the fish inside was tender and cooked perfectly. The service was impeccable as well all the people who helped us were EXTREMELY kind and helpful some of the best service I've had at any restaurant. If you are ever in the area and need some absolutely amazing food i HIGHLY recommend you come here.

Omg this place is so good and don't know how it's taken so long to try it?! 

We attempted to go once but the wait was long so came back when we made a reservation. 

Service was quick and everything was super hot and fresh. Shared with my husband and one year old. The waffle was thin but was soft and tasted really good. They let us choose the chicken pieces we wanted. Was a fairly light breading and amazing crunch. Chicken was juicy. 

This will probably be a go to for biscuits and gravy. Rich flavors and chunky gravy. Yum yum. 

Fries were hot and crispy. 

We got out pretty quick which is helpful having a one year old.

Love this place! It cured my soul food craving ! 

We arrived before they opened and it was already a small crowd outside. When the doors opened, we all scurried inside. The hostess was inviting and calm and quick to seat us! 

We already looked over the menu in the car so we was ready to order! For drinks I ordered the sweet tea and my hubby ordered the grape koolaid , both delicious. Now for grown up drinks I had the lavender lemon drop and it was bomb! Hubby had a Hennessy side car he also enjoyed. 

For appetizers we had the fried deviled eggs! I loved these so much I want to make them at home. For entrees I had the chicken dinner with mac and yams, the hubby had the chicken and waffles with a side of mac! Everything was delicious, hot and flavorful! I will come back for sure!

10/10 would recommend!  We left so full and had left overs. The portions were amazing. Service was top tier. Ambiance was 100% a vibe. Music on brand . The whole experience was smooth from start to finish.

Southern food & alcoholic kool-aid?! Yes please!

They are famous for their fried chicken (you can even order by the bucket!) and the sides are great too. They do offer a vegan menu, although it is somewhat limited. My personal fave was the fried fish sandwich. And don't miss their honey mustard- I've been thinking about it for days!

It has a cozy and fun ambiance, with a full bar. And a great patio to enjoy Sac's sunny weather.
Good service , good food !!! Fresh and  hot food. Waitresses on top of the game good job...

I rarely give 5 stars but I can't think of a single piece of feedback. The vibe is fun, the music loud enough to feel like a party but not a club. Our server, Amy was fast and friendly. There is street parking around. The bathrooms were clean. OK cool. But the food...the food was literally perfect! I had shrimp and grits. The gravy was so flavorful and had a heat but not too spicy to ruin all the flavor. The shrimp was cooked perfectly. The portion was huge and it came out hot. Other recommendations include the deep fried Deviled Eggs and their Old Fashion. The Old Fashion has a little surprise. Do not, and I mean this very seriously, miss the cornbread muffin. Maybe the best I've ever had. I don't know what the butter spread was but clearly it was made in heaven. We also tasted the banana pudding. It was super light like the pudding was whipped extra. It was sweet but the portion isn't huge.

This restaurant has all the vibes! It's atmosphere is perfect for either a dinner date, or even sitting at the bar with friends. The staff we're all very friendly and helpful when it came to picking things off the menu. We went on a Saturday night and they were very busy so I recommend a reservation. 

The Food: 

Deep-fried green tomatoes (4/5 stars) delicious! I love the sauce and all the little pieces of veggies on the side to eat with it.

Deep-fried, deviled eggs (5/5 star) I have never had anything like this! I loved it. I wasn't sure how I would feel about hot, deviled eggs, but they were delicious.

Fish Sandwich (5/5 stars) amazing! I loved the flavor and it melted in your mouth. The fries and the Kool-Aid pickle were amazing too! 

Adult Kool-Aid (5/5 stars) a fun twist on an adult version of Kool-Aid. I love that it was like a slushy and it wasn't super strong either way with tasting too much light Kool-Aid or alcohol.

Pork Chops (5/5 stars) as rated by my friend she said both that and the mac & cheese were amazing and she killed the whole plate

Coleslaw (5/5 stars)
Sweet potatoes (4/5 stars)
Potato salad (5/5 stars)

Artichoke Dip (5/5 stars) serviced with chips

Peach cobbler (5/5 stars) served warm with vanilla ice cream

My friend recommended this place since I have never been here. Came here on Wednesday night and there was 10 mins wait.

Great selections on their menu and straightforward. I love fried chicken so of course I had to order that! Got the 2 piece with fries and coleslaw. Chicken skin was super crunchy. Chicken was tender and juicy. Their coleslaw is not the ordinary coleslaw. It was a bit citrusy and had bell peppers. Fries were awesome. Cornbread muffin was good size. Was too full from my main entree so didn't get to try it while it was fresh. Took the cornbread home. 

Service was great. Food came out pretty fast. Place was vibing. Parking may be an issue but I will come back again.

Best meal I've had in a long time!  Our group tried the fried deviled eggs, fried fish, ox tails, fried fish. Sides we tried the Mac n cheese, grits, candied yams, collared greens. Everything was amazing! And the biscuits!!!!
Great service and fun atmosphere. Highly recommend!
Excellent service food was originally soulful and very tasty. Remind me of eating with family members in Texas. I recommend this place for anyone who enjoys eating real Soul Food.

Food  s great!
Ambiance is dope, I love a clean establishment with an awesome playlist.
Service was superb, Nataya was top notch!

The restaurant is fabulous. It was clean, service was just OK.

My favorites were the green Black Eyed Peas and fried fish

WHY WE WENT HERE:
- dinner reservation for celebrating a mini family reunion
- had other family events for the weekend and wanted to make sure the cuisine we ate the night of our dinner reservation was different from the other cuisines we'd be having that weekend (and that had a cocktail menu)

THE LOOK (FROM THE OUTSIDE):
- located off 3rd Ave in between Broadway and 34th St
- very minimal parking out front, so you're likely to find parking in the neighborhood and having to walk over
- brick exterior
- some enclosed patio seating

THE LOOK (FROM THE INSIDE):
- industrial, brick, concrete with wood accents/art
- full bar with wall mounted screens
- various seating (bar, freestanding tables and chairs, booths)

THE MENU:
- available via QR code 
- drink menu: swigs, liquid fixins, spirits, beers, wine
- food menu: lil' bits, grub, chicken & waffles, for the family, handhelds, greenz, fixins, and 1's, sweets

WHAT WE ORDERED:
- Sac Town Mango Lemonade
- 40 Acres
- Side Car
- Deep Fried Deviled Eggs
- Fried Green Tomatoes
- Pimento Cheese Dip
- Artichoke Dip
- Fried Chicken (3 piece dark)
- Oxtails
- Gumbo
- Smothered Pork Chop
- Collard Greens w/Turkey Necks
- Mac & Cheese
- Candied Yams
- Charred Okra w/Bacon
- Red Beans & Rice

THE TASTE:
- out of the cocktails ordered, only Side Car was extremely good; all other cocktails were VERY watery and we couldn't taste or feel the alcohol in them
- all of the food was a hit EXCEPT for the Pimento Cheese Dip; would not order that one again

CUSTOMER SERVICE:
- a bit on the slow side (for the order to be taken and for the drinks to come out), but at least all the food arrived around the same time

On numerous occasions, we've came here. I must express that my latest experience has been the most exceptional thus far. This is largely attributed to the exemplary service provided by our server, Austin. Throughout the duration of our visit, Austin exhibited remarkable patience in accommodating our sizable party, for which I am sincerely grateful. His meticulous attention to detail did not go unnoticed, and we were reassured by his unwavering availability whenever needed.

In addition to the superb service rendered by Austin, the culinary offerings surpassed expectations, delivering a delectable dining experience. The ambiance was further enhanced by the impeccable selection of music, contributing to the establishment's consistently inviting atmosphere.

In summary, I am compelled to commend the establishment for an unforgettable evening, predominantly due to the outstanding service provided by Austin, and the overall excellence in every aspect of our visit.

We had Dinner on a Tuesday night. Not as busy but I'm sure people were still getting off work. There's indoor and outdoor dining. We chose outdoor dining. it was a nice warm day and so it felt nice eating outside. We were seated right away. The menu was pretty straight forward - Just a front and back vs a "novel book" menu. I got the "Uncle Nearest  strawberry cooler" which was refreshing to drink without a strong alcohol taste, which is dangerous if you know what  I mean. Food arrived  on a timely manner. For the dishes, we ended up getting the chicken and waffle - it had a dollop of (what tasted like) strawberry spreadable butter that was good compliment to the buttermilk waffle with the maple syrup on the side if you want a little more extra sweetness. The other dish we got was the shrimp and grits. I don't think I've had this dish before but I thought it was really good. The gravy wasn't too spicy nor salty. a good amount of shrimp. I just wished they removed the tail. For the "Fixins" (side dishes) we got the MAC, Red beans & rice and the corn bread. I thought the first two was good but wasn't amazing. Both MAC and RB&R tasted like the you get from other places, it definitely satisfies your cravings but didn't stand out for me. The cornbread was where it was at. It was really good! Always nice to eat a moist corn bread that doesn't crumble too much. What turned it up a notch was the butter that came along with it. It tasted like a honey cinnamon jalapeño/pepper butter, with the right balance of "sweet heat" with a kick of spicy but not overbearing. 

Service was great. I believe our Server, first initial N was great. She helped us with our orders, recommended drinks and some dishes. Also recommended us to watch Sinners afterwards. unfortunately we didnt lol. We left very satisfied and thought "Nice we have a soul food spot nearby." We will definitely come back.

I don't mind paying a lot for good food but for a $135 for 3 people to eat canned beans is unheard of I'm a old G from the south and I cook beans all the time so I definitely know when I'm eating canned beans if you're taking shortcuts on something so simple makes me question everything the fried chicken was good the service was cool but you hit me with the canned beans want be returning to this location the one in LA every thing was cooked to

Ambiance was solid, loved all the signs everywhere and the music was great. Service was a bit slow at the start but the food made up for it I completely forgot about the wait before ordering. The food got out after we order relatively quick. Me and my girl but got the hot chicken sandwich and fries. 10/10 on the fries and the sandwich, everything you want in a chicken sandwich meal it had. Also got a small side of mac n cheese which was a good choice especially since it was only $6. I used to get the chicken n waffles here a lot. And the sandwich lived up to my already high standard for this place.
Very good. Both times we got food to go. We live over an hour away was still really good.

I ordered the smothered pork chops with rice and beans. Delicious! My sister had the oxtail and omg! It was divine! It was packed so we got a table outside. It was a little chilly but luckily, there was a heater. Our waiter was super nice and even though it was super busy, he still came to check up on us. Definitely coming back with more people!
Wow. This is great place to eat fried chicken and waffles. The chicken is moist and seasoned perfectly. We recommend this place highly.

Oooohhhh Man! I have never been to a soul food restaurant where I left having to call somebody, anybody to tell them how good the food was! 

Came for brunch, there was a reasonable wait. Always a good sign when there's a line.  We were seated in the back room where there were tv's. I instantly noticed how clean everything appeared and felt.  

Drinks served in mason jars were aesthetic and there was an instant vibe of "good food is served here". 

We ordered the shrimp and grits, chicken and waffles, and oxtails.

Shrimp and grits: this ain't no ordinary grits, something creamily, deliciously special.  Shrimp was perfectly cooked and gravy was rich and had some heat. 

Oxtails:  I wouldn't mind if it was more tender but taste was stellar and the mac and cheese side??? Again, no regular kind of mac n cheese - i appreciated some of the uncooked shredded cheddar mixed in with the cooked cheeses with the irresistible "pull"z. 

Fried chicken and waffles: chicken is excellent, waffles were aight (i have yet to find a spot that has their waffles with that perfect crunch). 

Will definitely be a regular here...

The food and drinks are good and reasonably priced.  The restaurant is crowded each time I go and there's await to be seated but the food is good. Parking is on the streets. The personal are nice and helpful. The drink is good. The inside is clean and open bar. There's seating inside as well as outdoors. If in the area of Sacramento, Ca. I would recommend this restaurant if you're not pushed for time.

This is one of my favorite spots in Sacramento area. I've been here 3-4 times the last few month, and I live like 2 hours away!

Try everything they have, it will be worth it. But I've narrowed my favorites to the Hot Chicken Sandwich (mostly because the amazing sauces on it, and the amazing fried chicken), and the fried chicken combo with the sides.  My favorite sides are the coleslaw and candied yams.

The place is in great spot in town and the service is excellent.  Must try in Sacramento.

Fixins has been great every time I've visited!

I got the 3 piece chicken with Mac n cheese and candied yams as my sides. The fried chicken is excellent--extremely crispy on the outside and juicy on the inside. Fried to perfection! I really liked the Mac n cheese a lot as well, very rich and creamy. In my opinion the candied yams was my least favorite of the meal, but still very solid! 

They have so many options on their menu that I hope to go back again soon to try more great food! I can't recommend Fixins enough.

Yum! We came for a friend's birthday dinner with a large group and the waitress did such a good job, she was quick and accurate, the food was delicious, some of the best fried chicken I've ever had. We definitely plan on coming again! It can get a bit out but that's a sign it's a popular spot.
I did not go there but I had some of their food at a catered. Funeral  the gumbo was great the chicken was also delicious now I do want to go there  looking forward to the experience

Really really good and I mean good fried chicken! Just wow!!! My fave are the fried chicken tenders but the fried breast is really good also! The sides are pretty amazing as well my go to are the yams (my favorite!) and the mac and cheese, the greens and fried okra are great as well! They also serve a yummy biscuit with the best sweet butter! Definitely give this place a try if you haven't already. You won't be disappointed, everything I have tried here has been good.
Everything about this Experience was amazing. The gentleman at the front to the kind waitress. If you are in the Sacramento area you have to make a stop here. The FRIED DEVILED EGGS my favorite!!!
Fixinuns obtains and greens  are the best on the menu in my opinion. I drive all the way from the bay for them

Fixins Soul Kitchen caught my eye when I saw a high 4 star rating on Yelp. When you first enter the restaurant it has a great smell and vibe. The servers are extremely friendly and helpful with any questions or requests you have! 

Foods
- Chicken: 4.5/5 | The chicken is extremely flavorful and with the added hot sauce it tastes amazing. 
- Mac & Cheese: 5/5 | Fire can't complain 
- Biscuit: 5/5 |The best biscuit I've had in the US tbh
- Collard Greens: 2/5 | Personally, I might just not be a collard greens guy but I wasn't a fan.

My husband and I came here for his birthday dinner based off of a recommendation from a friend. And I'm so glad we ended up eating here! The restaurant is beautiful with a modern touch but you still get a bit of farmhouse here in there with all the southern decor. From outside to inside it's immaculate throughout and staff are just as friendly as ever. Loved loved loved our server, JC! You made my daughter's night :) 

Seeing as this was our first time here we wanted to get their popular main dishes but still played it safe with the sides, Mac & cheese and fries (don't judge). LOL. I got the Gumbo which came with cornbread and the birthday boy got the Smothered Chicken, thigh & wing. We were so stuffed and still had leftovers to bring home. My husband specifically said his chicken was "finger lickin good"! 

The Gumbo had a subtle kick packed with amazing flavor. My cornbread was the best I've ever had. As simple as it was it didn't taste super dry or crumble as you broke it apart to eat and dip. The Smothered Chicken had an unbelievable crunch despite being drowned with the gravy. I kid you not, after sitting for 15+ mins the chicken withstood it all and kept us very satisfied! After how pleased we were we will come back again with an open palate to try the traditional sides. I'm ready!!! 

Plenty of seating inside (booths + tables) where there is also a bar with bar seating, a private room, and patio seating. I heard they even provide catering. This I gotta try. Thanks for such a wonderful first time experience, Fixin's!

1st time in and on a cold Wednesday night the restaurant was crazy busy. Didn't expect that. The food is really good. The fried chicken was nice and crispy without being dry. The portions were large too. The fried chicken came with 2 sides, the standout being the grits. WOW we're they buttery and amazing! The servers were friendly and attentive. Overall a nice 1st impression. Will be back.

I had high hopes for this place. I was specifically looking for soul food...and some places were closed on the holiday (I went on Mon Labor Day 2024)...and there was lots of people traffic in & out. I took that as a good sign. My server was super sweet. However, my table ordered the pork chops without the gravy. The breading completely fell off the chops. I didn't enjoy the flavor of the pork chops. Sides: Mac & cheese and yams. The sides were both delicious! 

We got fried fish also. I was disappointed that only 1 piece comes with the order. It was good...just wish I had another piece. The sides I got were collard greens & red beans & rice. Neither were seasoned enough for me...rather bland.

Unfortunately the only reason I'd go back would be for the Mac & cheese and candied yams. I really wanted to give this place higher reviews.
The BEST chicken and waffles I have had. The owner is the sweetest, always checking in on table to table to make sure everything is good. If you want soul food, Fixins is the best place to go.

I propose another location ... come to the DMV!!!!! I visited the Sac location while in the area for work. I was curious to see if the experience I had in Detroit would be the same here. I arrived at around 4:30pm on a Tuesday. The place was super vibey with hip hop music coming through the speakers and a somewhat busy restaurant, guests occupied booths and tables. The bar was empty so I opted to sit there. I was greeted by the hostess and both bartenders when I sat down. The bartender gave a few suggestions (fried chicken, oxtails or fried fish). In an effort to break the hold that fried chicken has on me, I went for the fried fish. I received a very generous portion of a perfectly fried fillet of catfish, red beans & rice, and collard greens and three hush puppies. The catfish was love at first brute, crispy, crunch and well seasoned fish that didn't need anything I added the fixins' hot sauce to get a little kick and u was in heaven. The collard greens were piping hot and full of flavor. And those red beans and rice ... if I could have shipped some home I would have. They were so filling flavor with a slight bit of spice. The hush puppies were too much for me to add but I took a bite and they were ok, nothing special or perhaps not fresh. Everything was excellent, the ambiance, service and food were an A+

I went here for lunch on a Saturday after getting matching tattoos with my best friend. We were seated outside within 10-15 minutes after arriving. It was a bit windy, but there were plenty of patio heaters. To start, we ordered the Deep Fried Deviled Eggs and cocktails... I got the Hurricane. Then we ordered entrees - Smothered Chicken and Wing & a Waffle. Of course, we both got Mac & Cheese and Collard Greens as our sides. Everything was delicious! The portions were perfect. I hate bringing home leftovers because they don't taste the same later. My only grip is parking. Luckily we were already in the area and decided to drop in.
Food Was Good , But When i ordered my food off grub hub they didnt send my drinks i paid for and they didnt send the utencils

Food was solid. I will say it had a pretty small menu but it was all pretty tasty. Only thing I will say was kinda lol was the kool aide for 9 bucks.  I don't even know what to say about that...it's like I get it..they are riffin...but I dunno man that's just kinda cornball. 

I had the oxtail, it was good. It was served over rice and had a savory gravy. The Mac and cheese on the side was tasty. 

I was surprised about the fried tomato's.  They did a real good job with the batter which I wasn't used to having with fried tomato's.   It was good. I was also surprised by the shrimp and grits.  They did a really good job with that. 

I'd pass on the cornbread and get the biscuit instead.  The cornbread was meh but the biscuit was one of the better ones I've had in recent memory especially in sacto. The greens were good, some folks at my table were raving about them but it's not a side that ever got me super excited. It's like getting excited about a preview to me when I'm here for the movie. 

Service was ok, on point when they were there. I drink a lot though so my cup was empty quite a few times till I called someone over for it. To be fair the place was hoppin, but they seemed adequately staffed so no excuses. 

Chicken was solid. I've been disappointed in chicken ever since I got back from Nashville. It's no Nashville chicken but it was definitely tasty. 

Worth a try if you've never been!

My friend and I were excited to try this new soulful restaurant.  It was another hot 100 degree that  day in Sacramento but we asked to sit outside in the patio to keep an eye on our e-bikes.  We were the only two ladies there and sat across a large fan because of the heat.  

A server came out and said that the patio is under serviced but he'll try to find someone to help us.  Why allow us to sit in the patio in the first place then?

Then a different server came out to tell us that the manager didn't want us to sit outside because that area is not serviced, although there was salt and pepper and hot sauce on every table and the fan is going.  We told the server we have a foldable scooter and we can sit inside if the manager was okay with us bringing it in.  Server came back and told us that manager was okay with us staying outside.  

Shortly after we received our dishes, a third server came out to tell us that the large fan we were sitting across had to move across the patio where no one was sitting.  Again, we're the only two ladies sitting in this empty patio in 100 degree heat.  He said the fan can stay for 20 more minutes or we can move our seats inside.  We said we'll be gone in 20 minutes so please keep the fan.  

We felt unwelcome here, lost our appetite and asked for the check.

In terms of food, dark fried chicken and collard greens were good.  I wasn't a fan of the deviled egg , it was a bit rubbery, flavor was meh.

I like this place.. Their fixings are definitely worthy of their name. Their chicken is okay. It's not dry but it's just good to me. Breading is seasoned well and not too much breading where it's overwhelming. A good amount. Their fixings is where it's at though. I always get their collard greens, ocra, and Mac n cheese. Their cornbread is amazing! It just melts in your mouth. Fish nuggets are good too. Nothing super amazing though. Their service is okay.. the wait staff is really nice when they come around but there was a few times where I feel like they're so busy that they forget about you.. multiple occasions I've been here, they have bought out our main dish before the appetizers etc,.. their banana pudding  so good!!!!

Wow, I forgot how good this place is! First time being back in years and Fixins has only gotten better  with time! Came here for a celebration dinner with the family and we all loved it. 

Started off with fried green tomatoes and artichoke dip (it's a lil spicy fyi). We also got a couple of teas (one sweet, one not because the sweet tea is, well, really sweet). The drinks are HUGE. We all got fried chicken plates; their fried chicken is hands down the best in town. Not to mention their sides are equally as great. The greens are a tad salty but the mac n cheese and potato salad are my go tos; I could eat them by the gallon! 

In the end, we ordered way too much food and took it all home. We also couldn't resist ordering dessert to-go. Their peach's cobbled and banana pudding are bomb! Overall, food is amazing, service was friendly, and the vibe of the place is down to earth and fun! We'll be back more often!!
Everything was 10/10 I left so full asf couldn't breath but food was so fire no regrets. Will be back

I love Fixins and I can't believe I haven't reviewed it until now. We love coming here!

I like it that we get to pick which chicken pieces for their fried chicken, which is so delicious! Our family always orders the same thing- shrimp and grits, oxtail and the fried chicken... oh and the blue koolaid too!

If you are lucky enough to get Nyshia as a servicer, you will have excellent service! She is the sweetest and best!!!!

Fixins is hard to review for me because I can only compare it to my mother's cooking. Also, soul food preparation differs regionally so things can taste different from what you are used to. That said - the fried chicken and waffle is a can't miss so if you go to Fixins this can't miss! I am VERY picky about oxtail preparation. I like it to fall
off the bone and be in gravy. I found their oxtails to be covered in a glaze which caught me and my aunt off guard. This isn't how my mom or grandmother prepared it and I am a hard pass on having it again. The cobbler was yummy! The cornbread is more like cake than cornbread which also caught me off guard. The greens were good - a little sweet for me - but still good. The RED KOOL-AID was the bomb and took me back to the 70s! I'd say come for the chicken and waffles and leave with a big wide happy ear to ear Kool Aid smile!

My brother has been craving shrimp and grits and not many places in Sac have it. We went Sunday around 1230 with reservations and had to wait about 15 min to get seated outside. It was busy especially with what looked like people coming right after church. Staff was friendly and service was fast too. I've never had fried deviled eggs. It was good but kind of rubbery texture. Shrimp and grits was flavorful too but I'd personally want more grits on the plate. My oxtail was also flavorful but collard greens were way too salty for my liking. I'd come back again and try a different entree. Oh warm peach cobbler with vanilla ice cream was tasty too!

Went to Sacramento Ca. fora baby shower. And this place was highly recommended by my family member. The service was on point and the food was phenomenal. 
I would go back here on my next visit to Sacramento Ca.
Everything about this restaurant was absolutely amazing. The waitresses , cleanliness and the food.

Fixin's Soul Kitchen really delivers on flavor and atmosphere. The service is excellent--Nyshia is friendly, attentive, and makes you feel right at home. The food is packed with flavor, especially the oxtails and fried chicken. The fried chicken is perfectly seasoned and crispy, and the oxtails are rich and tender. My only note is that the portion size for the oxtails feels a little small if you come in really hungry, but I understand that oxtail has gotten expensive these days. Overall, definitely worth a visit if you're craving soulful, comforting food with great vibes and service
The best Soul food in Northern California Kevin Johnson has did a fantastic job with his restaurant fixins comes highly recommendJust stop by and see

Almost EVERY. SINGLE. TIME we come to Sacramento we swing by Fixins. We live about 50 miles away and when friends come to visit we try and think of a reason to go into Sac just to eat here! Friendly staff, incredible food (I married a Cajun-I should know!), and very reasonable prices. Highly recommend to anyone who loves southern comfort food! You will not be disappointed!!

It's been a while since we've visited. Smothered pork chops with greens and yams- didn't disappoint. Oxtails were also good! Topped it off with strawberry kool-aid, margaritas and a to-go box. All the staff were friendly and attentive. Great vibe!
Haven't been back in a while now and was a little disappointed with this visit. Everything was okay, but not great. Very busy (as always), so we expect the staff to be overloaded.

All I have to say is Country Fried Chicken is top notch. I walked in and saw retired NBA player behind the bar. Upon receiving my order I looked up and saw him bringing my food to me. lol. It's refreshing to see a Millionaire,NBA player and former Government official willing to help his own restaurant grow. Great place for a nice breakfast and very friendly atmosphere. Definitely a must try.

I've never dined in but have ordered takeout twice... both times too hungry to snap food photos.

Have ordered the catfish dinner twice.  Delicious. Fried to a crispy cornmeal crusted perfection with sweet, clean taste
Nice sized filets.

All dinners come with deep fried balls of cornbread that will spoil you for all other preparations, and your choice of two sides.

I was underwhelmed by the Mac and cheese- watery/oily without much flavor. I was shocked.  

I've ordered the greens twice.  Nice amount of meat and packed into the cup. Add some Crystal Hot Sauce and they're my second favorite in town.  

Tonight I got the charred okra with bacon. DAMN. Almost reminded me of blistered shishitos. Served whole. Fan-freaking-tastic.

I've really enjoyed Fixins both times. They do soul food justice. The place was packed but tables turned quickly and efficiently. 

They do seem to have some issues with online ordering and they DO NOT take orders over the phone.  Kind of inconvenient, but the food is worth it

I went back to Fixins last night... this restaurant is even BETTER than I had previously remembered! 

Fixins is located in North Oak Park off Broadway and 34th. Its a fun, walkable area with kombucha, ice cream, breweries, a theater, and more all within a block or so. 

I went with my fiancee and her Mom, and to start we ordered fried tomatoes and spinach/artichoke dip. This was the BEST spinach/artichoke dip I've ever had. Spicy, flavorful... oh my lord! So good! To drink we ordered Fixins' 'sweet tea' which was just the right amount of sweet!

For my main course I ordered the three piece fried chicken meal with collard greens and mac n' cheese. First off, I'd like to say how much I appreciate Fixins using turkey-neck instead of bacon in their greens. I know at most soul food restaurants they cook collard greens with bacon/pork which I don't eat due to religious reasons. So I was pleasantly surprised that I could finally order greens (to somewhat trick my brain into thinking this meal was healthy lol). Th mac n cheese was also incredible!

But for the main attraction, their fried chicken... home run. Actually scratch that- a grand slam! The skin was SOOO flavorful and crispy with ultra tender chicken beneath. I was so sad when my meal was over, I loved it that much! I also ordered dessert but was so full I had to take it to-go. I got their banana pudding which was fantastic (no surprise).

The service is incredible here like always. Such friendly and kind staff! Ambiance is also fun. I love how they play bangers here! No dumb elevator music (lol).

In a nutshell, Fixins is the GOAT fried chicken restaurant in Sacramento! It somehow was even better than last time! Thank you KJ for this incredible restaurant! 

A++++++

Went as a unit holiday luncheon,  3 ordered the fried chicken with sides which included, mac & cheese, beans & rice, French fries,  greens, yams,  side salad.  1 ordered the fried chicken sandwich. 
Everyone was pleased with their choice. 
The cool aid pickle was a first for me.
The restaurant was clean.  Service was great,  "bathrooms " were clean.
I'll return and try other items and update review. I didn't see Ribs or Tri tip on the menu, but had most of the soul food comfort foods I like.
Was glad to see the restaurant had a diverse crowd and was full.
Recommend trying it out with family or friends.

This was our second visit. Sadly it did not measure up to the first visit. This visit our collard greens were over salted, the cornbread was over cooked and dry, and the oxtails were not the same as our first visit. You can see from our pictures there was a huge difference. For $236.00 for a group of four. I really wish it would have been as good as it was the first time.

The food, the service, the music YES YES YES! I've been wanting to try this place for a long time but since I moved away. I didn't get a chance to try it but OMG I loved the entire experience. I will definitely be back once I'm in town. The greens were SO good and that's not easy to do. The Mac and cheese was good. The sweet tea was too sweet for us but we watered it down lol. Chicken was bomb. Cornbread was tasty. Everything was so good. I can't wait to go back.
Food was so good I really enjoyed it. This was my second time eating here and they did not miss

The food was delicious! Outstanding service. She was friendly and helped with my menu choices. The inside and outside seating as very nice. 
Will be back!

I happened to be in the area & saw the great reviews so I decided to try their brunch - I was not disappointed!!
My husband had the smothered chicken (delicious) with a side of red beans & rice (nice kick to them) & potato salad (yum). I had the fried catfish (hot & flakey) with a side of French fries (sooo good) & candied yams (felt like a little dessert). 
The restaurant was at full capacity, but the acoustics weren't too overwhelming. It also had a full bar & lots of different seating choices, whether inside or outside. 
The only downside was the wait time was a bit on the long side, but it was worth it!! We will most certainly be back again & get an early reservation!

Everything we had was delicious. My brother broke his vegan diet here and got the shrimp and grits with the Fried Green Tomatoes as a side dish. The tomatoes were juicy and flavorful and perfectly fried in a classic crispy cornmeal. My smothered pork chops were kinda thinner ones like a chicken-fried-steak-style but the gravy tasted like homemade and it was a big plate.
My brother made what he called a perfect bite with a shrimp, grits, and then a big piece of the fried green tomato on top- he low key got emotional after he ate it lol. Like I saw his eyes tear up, he said it's his favorite meal all year. 
Bonus points for purple koolaid.

This place is top notch! From the great patio ambiance to the fantastic service I was already sold by the time the food arrived. The food was so delicious the rest wouldn't have mattered anyway! Plus I had a tasty beverage on top of it so I was in heaven. Great spot for a date or dinner with friends.

Delivery Review: Great southern Food!!
Collard greens flavorful, not too salty, tender and fresh!
Fried Chicken Juicy, Spicey and crispy- A HomeRun!
Outstanding Southern Food here in SAC!!!!

visited Fixins with my family for dinner today -10/20/25 - Customer Service was top tier and the food wasserved hot. The fried deviled eggs and fried green tomatoes we're delish. I had the smothered pork chop with a corn muffin, Mac n cheese and red beans and rice. The gravy was a smidge salty. It was good but not amazing. The red beans and rice was tasty and so was the Mac n cheese. The happy hour drinks were a winner with a great variety to choose from. My family had the hot chicken sandwich which they said was delicious. The food, ambience and drinks makes "Fixins" an all around fantastic choice any night of the week.

Everything I ate was phenomenal! The food is soooo flavorful! Portion size is great too. The restaurant has a very nice aesthetic, brick and wood, very tastefully done in my opinion. Our server was wonderful, Eliza was very knowledgeable about the food and had great recommendations.

This review is late and very much over due. I had to leave a review because I can't stop thinking about their shrimp and grits. While
Attending orientation for my daughter at Sac State we visited the infamous Fixins!!! They did not disappoint, I can't wait to go back! The service was A1, the drinks were beautiful and perfectly balanced. The ambiance was nicer than I expected and the staff was on it!!! As a chef myself I wasn't sure how I'd like this food, it was the BOMB!!!!!
wow, this place from the staff to the atmosphere to especially the food, just amazing. A shout out to Aunt Pearl, really made us feel at home.
Very good! The butter and biscuits went crazy. Service was nice too. Would take someone

Very excellent service, they were packed and still food came out sooo quick like I was shocked. Drinks were creative and fun. I had the pork chop. Super good, I had Mac and cheese and candied yams, both great choices! My fiance got the chicken with collard greens and red beans and rice and it was spectacular! For app we had the fried deviled eggs, what a good taste, wish there was more than two eggs tho.

This place has been in my list of places to try and was brought to my attention by my lady. I'm glad she found it and we tried them because it was amazing. Great atmosphere. It would be a great place to watch a game. Seating was available inside and outside. We sat inside because it was warm but being outside would have been a nice experience as well. Started with the fried deviled eggs which were good but I felt the batter took away from the flavor of the egg. The fried green tomato's were hella good. Fried perfectly, crispy, sweet and the pepper and mayo combo was a perfect combination. I got the fried fish with greens and potato salad. It was cooked perfectly and full of flavor. The greens were also packed with flavor and one of my favorites of the whole meal. The potato salad was good but I'm used to larger chucks of potato and it had a bit too much of a vinegary taste. My lady got the ox tails and they were so tender and juicy with flavor for days. We had to taste the chicken and that was probably my favorite item off the menu we tried. Crispy, so much flavor, tender and juicy meat. So damn good. The cornbread muffin was another highlight for me with their maple syrup butter. Can't forget about the koolaid. It was so refreshing and took me back to my childhood. Excellent service, ambiance, and quiet because everyone was grubbin! Def return worthy. Looking forward to checking out other menu items

I have been to Fixins Soul Kitchen on several occasions.  The atmosphere is always lively, people enjoying good conversation and food.  The customer service has been pleasant each time. I am a creature of habit so I always order the same thing, hush puppies, fries, cat fish and grilled okra. The hush puppies, fries and fish are always consistent in flavor and never overcooked however depending on who's cooking the Okra is not consistent some days it is way to salty or undercooked.  The prices are ok and so are the portion sizes. I would advise not to go when you it's a hour before closing my friend ordered Ox Tails once and got the bottom of the pot. It was not tasty at all. Overall it is good enough that I have returned several times.

One of the few places I haven't really found fault in. I've been anywhere from 3-5 times and I have yet to be disappointed. My favorite thing there is the oxtail. It's so flavorful and tender. The fried green tomatoes are vibrant and lightly fried. I also love the unique cocktails on the menu. The adult Kool-Aid is probably my favorite because the use Cherry Kool-Aid for the ice cubes. 

Everyone else that I have come here with over the years have never had a bad thing to say about them as well. It's always been a great experience and the service is always on point. He will definitely love the vibe when you come to this restaurant. 

One of my favorite spots in Sacramento that just never disappoints.

Great food and service. The ox tails, chicken&waffles, and the gumbo were all winners on our two visits to this restaurant owned by former NBA star and former Sacramento mayor - he is also a local son - Kevin Johnson. Service was provided by Jae, a great dude reminiscent of a West Coast version of the Rock. Outdoor seating and indoor were both comfortable. KJ was having lunch on our second visit and Jae brought him over for a quick "hello". Jae then gave a big smile for a photo to top off a fun experience.

Fixins delivers flavor with intention, every dish was on point, rich with soul and history. The sweet tea was simple perfection, refreshing and balanced. Every dish we ate left us full and fulfilled. We came for my wife's birthday with a small group, and the staff couldn't have been more attentive or kind. It wasn't just good service, it felt like genuine hospitality. Fixins is more than a restaurant, it's a place where food, genuine community, and joy meet. A wonderful institution to be visited in the Oak Park neighborhood. Come in and have some sweet tea and hang out on the patio during a nice breezy night, you won't be disappointed!

So good I ran it back twice in a week. Both times the restaurants appeared full but magically there was seating available for me. The staff was super nice and accommodating. I placed my order quickly and food came out quickly. Both times I ordered catfish dinner w/ Mac n cheese, yams, and waffle. Catfish was perfectly fried and crunch. The filet was the perfect size and not too thick. The Mac n cheese might be the best restaurant Mac n cheese I've had, very close to home cooked. The waffle was crispy and the grape jelly butter was a great addition. The yams were just like I like, chunks and not mashed.

Today, my family and I decided to celebrate Juneteenth by visiting a black-owned establishment. We walked in, informed the host of our reservation, and were seated immediately. We passed through the clean, organized dining room to a charming patio outside. Our server, Bryan, was very attentive and helpful. We ordered a variety of drinks, from the lavender lemon drop to the margarita tower. When our food arrived, everything was hot, fresh, and well-seasoned. My only complaint is that I wish the catfish had been seasoned more heavily. However, everything we ordered--including the chicken sandwich, the oxtail plate, and the gumbo--was delicious! My friends and family enjoyed their time, and I will definitely be more than happy to visit this establishment again in the future.

I've visited  fixins about 3 times since its opening .our past two experiences and rhe food was excellent. . So 3 days ago our family of 5 we went  to dine in the evening with them we wanted soul food.. fried chicken collard greens canned yams Mac n cheese  red beans n rice  and waffles. Is what our table was served 
 For dessert   banana  pudding and peach cobbler n ice cream.   My collard greens was too salty my Mac n cheese  was also salty . My peach cobbler the peached was blanched  meaning not all the way cooked thats why iknew thea were fresh  peaches and not canned  and the dough  was just thrown in the sauce...  and fixing I've had some peach cobbler but that wasn't it at all . It was like I had some peaches n dumplin .that wasn't peach cobbler... but our waiter and thw service we received was again awesome.. except my remarks every thing  else was good.

Their food is VERY good!!  I had the gumbo and ordered the chicken dinner plate, the chicken was crispy and very tasty! This place is my new go to for really great crispy chicken!  The service was good, the person was very friendly and oh my goodness, they had cool-aid!  I haven't had cool-aid in years!....that too was good.
The food here is consistently delicious! We have ordered everything on the menu and it is all delicious. We frequently drive 1-1/2 hours to eat here.

11am lunch reservation for six.  Seated promptly, drinks ordered pretty promptly also. Good service in their lively, bustling dining room. Everyone enjoyed their food and cocktail choices and some had a bit to take home for a tasty snack later. Glad we got there at opening.  There were plenty of folks waiting for tables as we left.

The food was delicious. The customer service was great. They were very attentive and make sure that the table was cleared promptly. I sampled a little of everything and none of it missed. It can be a little busy so would recommend a reservation.

The atmosphere is quite nice, but as someone who deals with sensory overload, I found the loud music and somewhat cramped seating arrangements challenging. While the space is optimized, it's hard not to notice other patrons and servers squeezing between tables. The outdoor seating looked inviting, but unfortunately, we were seated inside. If you're walking in without a reservation, be prepared for a wait--we had to wait about an hour, though the delicious smell of food kept us hopeful.

We ordered the artichoke dip, fried chicken, candied yams, fries, and mac n' cheese. The artichoke dip was good but not really my thing; I prefer when the cheese is the star of the show, and the pickled jalapeños here overpowered the dish a bit for me. The fried chicken was juicy and fresh, but I felt it lacked depth of flavor. Growing up in the East Bay, I have high standards when it comes to soul food, and this didn't quite hit the mark compared to places like Minnie Bell's Soul Movement. That being said, it was still a comforting meal.

As for drinks, I had the Pomelo Bonita and my partner got the Uncle Nearest Strawberry Cooler. The drinks were very strong, almost too much so--the alcohol really overpowered the other flavors. I'd prefer a more balanced cocktail where the alcohol complements rather than dominates the drink.

Service was friendly but on the slow side. Our drinks arrived just as we were finishing our food, and it took longer than comfortable to get our server's attention when we needed the check.

Overall, while the food was good, I don't think I'll be returning, mainly due to the price and the general disappointment compared to the hype surrounding this restaurant. If you're craving comforting soul food and don't mind spending a bit extra, it's worth checking out--just be sure to make a reservation to avoid the wait.

Came on a Thursday night in a party of 3. We had a reservation, but it probably wasn't necessary even though it was busy. We were quickly seated and had our order taken.

I got the fried catfish sandwich with french fries. Highly recommend the fish sandwich. The fish itself was perfectly cooked and the breading was crispy but not overwhelming. My only complaint is that there was too much mayo/tartar sauce so if you're a slow eater like me it means the sandwich might get a little soggy after a while. The fries were excellent - the perfect amount of crunch, soft insides, and tasty seasoning.

Service was friendly and the atmosphere inside was lively. I'd be happy to come back and try something new.

Let me tell you this place is so good! It's quaint,very nice atmosphere. Definitely a hip family vibe. We didn't have reservations and they were busy. They had tables outside but it was Hot and we didn't want to sit outside. We were just gonna try it another time. They told hold on a sec. They found a table and chairs somewhere don't ask me lol but I'm so glad they did. We sat down and looked over the menu. It was incredible. We ordered oxtails, meatloaf, macaroni and cheese mashed potatoes, fried green tomatoes, okra cornbread yams did I tell you we were hungry oh yeah, we were hungry. Everything was so delicious. The service was outstanding. They kept checking on us to make sure we were OK. Our glasses were never empty. They made our visit an excellent one so much so but I went back again for round two and it was just as good the second time around!

Do you have an EBT menu for those alumni who graduated from Sac High. We need to know what it's like to eat good food. Sincerely 1985 Alumni
 God Bless you.
The food was good and portions were decent. Good service. Good ambiance, but the music was slightly on the louder side.
Everything was so delicious, I can't wait to come back! It was so good I didn't mind having to sit at the bar.

Great ambiance, good location, friendly staff. 
The waitress took a while to take my order, but once I ordered, the food came shortly thereafter. 

The fried chicken is fried very crispy and has a great flavor. I enjoyed it. The yams were tasty as well. 

I'm from Louisiana and expected Louisiana-style red beans and rice. However, their beans were cooked differently, kinda like field peas. They had a decent flavor, but not what I expected. Also, I should have asked for the cornbread which I noticed on someone's fish order. The cornbread was fluffy and looked delicious. My biscuit, on the other hand, was hard and dry. It did come with a cinnamon style butter. 

Overall, it was a good experience and I may go back if I'm in the area to try the fish. I took off a point for the beans and biscuit.

This is an amazing restaurant! The food was delicious and so hot. The food came out so quickly and the staff was pleasant, professional, and kind. Our server, Jae was so accommodating , pleasant, as well as accurate. BJ, the manager, runs a tight ship that consist of exceptional cuisine and a pleasant staff! The restaurant is clean
with a nice calm vibe! We will definitely be back and spread the word to family and friends!

In Sacramento for work and found Fixins just through searching food in Apple Maps. When traveling for work I usually always eat the same thing when I go out. I had to get some soul food on this trip.
I went during lunch time so we were seated immediately. The restaurant is nice. We sat outside to get a nice cool breeze. Our waiter JC (2.0) lol was friendly and took care of us. After ordering our food did not take long to come out. Our meals were freshly made and delicious!! I can't wait to get to my left overs later :D
This is your grand momma's soul food.  Love the music-had to re-download Shazam to add to my playlist.  Service is friendly and attentive.

The location is horrible. The atmosphere is cute, but the area is filled with homeless and sketchy individuals. Parking is on the street, so expect to walk a little bit. We came here on a Thursday at 6pm. 

The service is extremely slow. It took too long to take our order, bring water, and serve the food. We received our appetizer before even getting our waters. We also waited 30 minutes for our table.

The food is mixed results. 
- Chicken- Extremely bland and stringy. Needed salt atleast
- Deep-Fried Deviled Eggs: Completely flavorless
- Mac and Cheese, Biscuit with sweet butter, and Fried Fish: Good, but not worth returning to this area
-Collard Greens: Delicious

The pricing is reasonable but I'd rather pay more for better food and location.

Fixins! I love you so much! My best friend and I have been coming here the past couple of years for our birthdays. Every time we have come has been a total vibe...great food, service, and drinks. Tonight...was awful.  We should have left after 20 minutes of no service, but I would've felt guilty because we already had our waters. Our "server" Jae, avoided us for 20 minutes and acted annoyed that we wanted to order. (I looked thru other reviews. People seem to like him, so maybe he just didn't like us?)
Oddly enough, Kevin Johnson was sitting in the middle of the restaurant. 
Maybe service isn't their priority and manners aren't required? Either way...bummer. The food is fantastic, but I won't pay that price for crappy Jae service again.

My first time trying this place after wanting to try it for years. I was so impressed. Did not have to wait for a table. I had my baby with me and she slept through the whole lunch. The food was delicious and the chicken was so juicy and moist. The best fried chicken I've ever had.

First stop of my Sacramento food tour started with a bang! Fixins was packed, luckily we had reservations and once I was done with my meal I figured out what the hype was about. My meal was incredible! Now I'll keep it real I've had better greens at a food truck in Fresno but these were still top tier. I would come here for the biscuits with the sweet butter alone! The fried deviled eggs and fried catfish were perfection. Service was outstanding! The music were all my favorite throwbacks. If you want food with some flavor, vibes that make you wanna dance, and leave full this is the spot! Don't miss out on Fixins cause then you won't know what great southern food on the west coast tastes like.

Ok let's start with the amazing Oxtails!!!!   First time for me eating Oxtails loved them!  The fried Chicken tasted like home it was fabulous fresh and hot. Smothered Pork Chops were also amazing! If I could be critical about only one thing it would be just a tad less salt in the coating for the pork chops.  Sides Mac n Cheese, Greens,  Cornbread, Biscuits and those butters were all very delicious! Food was served quickly, food was hot and fresh!  First time visit and I will be back again and again sorry it took me so long.

Where do I even start? Wow. What a WONDERFUL soul food establishment. Walked in during lunch break and I was met with kind, welcoming staff. Was sat down quickly and got straight to ordering. Love the attentive detail of the staff here. I ordered the classic chicken n waffles. Food came out in such quick fashion I was thoroughly surprised. Quality was top notch too. Chicken was glistening with it's juices and the waffles paired so well with their syrup and butter. Theres 100% something in that sauce that makes me keep wanting to come back haha!

Will revisit and try out their other dishes! Very fortunate to have high quality soul food in Sacramento! Wish I snagged some photos but I was too hungry to think twice!

Love love love the food here! We had the oxtails and omg, I'm drooling just remembering it now! Also, Fried green tomatoes-chefs kiss! Let's just say we took 5 more banana puddings to take home! Enough said! 10/10 recommend!

"Fantastic Food"
My Daughter And I Enjoy The Food So Much We Come Once A Month Or More! My Favorites Have Turned Out To Be The Smothered Pork Chops, The Gravy Is Lovely And It Tastes Great Together- And The Fried Chicken, It's The Flavor And The Fry That Gets Me - The Mac n Cheese Is So Delicious - The Greens Make The Perfect Impression, Right Amounts Of Every Ingredient! With A Yummy Biscuit Or Great Cornbread Served With Special Butter! Even Their Burgers Are Amazing. My Daughters Favorite Is The Shrimp And Grits, She Loves All The Flavors That Come Together. 
The Oxtails Are So Good It Makes You Want To Sing! The Catfish Is Fresh, Moist And Crispy, Wonderful - Everything Dish Is Always On Point!
Best food ever!  The food was delicious and came quickly! Have the mac & cheese and the grits with the cheese sauce! Also the candy yams while  not a dessert should be a dessert!
It was good!service was also good!I just did not like fish!I think that it was mahi mahi!

Yes!!!!! Solid 5 stars! I tasted a lot of things and everything was flavorful and good quality food. The ambiance is fun and the service was friendly and attentive. 
We had the fried deviled eggs. So sinful and so delicious. My 1 year old CRUSHED them, with the Tabasco and all. We had the red beans and rice. They reminded me of my grandma's. Delicious. The candied yams were good but probably my least favorite of all the things I tried. My 1 year old would probably disagree bc he CRUSHED them during lunch and when we had the leftovers for dinner. The greens were perfectly smoky and tangy. Around Thanksgiving, I was asking my mom if I could make greens with turkey necks instead of ham hocks, but she made me too afraid to stray from the family recipe. Their greens proved that they can be done with turkey necks! We had Mac and cheese and that was also delicious. Perfectly cheesy and they didn't taste like they were made with any cheese products like velveeta. I also had the oxtails. They were cooked well. The meat falls off the bone and the flavor is right on. They actually give a very generous portion of oxtails too. Literally everything I tried was good. That rarely happens, so 5 stars! (I'd probably be like 600 lbs if I lived close to this restaurant because it's legit. Yum!
Delicious food. The menu is wonderful. All the sides were on pint. In particular, the collards were incredible.

I have been to Fixins several times over the years. The location is great though parking can be a challenge. It's exciting to see the revitalization in this part of Oak Park and this restaurant certainly is contributing in a positive way. During a recent visit on a Saturday evening, we enjoyed decent service and our food arrived quickly. We did make reservations though we could not get through on the phone and had to make them online. We were seated outside. My only complaint about the seating is the tables were very wobble and it caused our drinks to spill a few times. 

The food at Fixins is decent. I noticed the menu really has not changed since they first opened. I would love to see new menu items added. The food I will say is seasoned well and has good flavor. The adult Kool Aid only comes in red and I wish they offered more flavors. Also, the kool aid used to come in a larger Mason style glass. The Kool aid was very sweet and not very strong. I actually ordered a shot to add to it to cute some of the sweetness. Overall the experience was ok. I really hope there are some new menu items added to keep customers like me coming back to try new items.

I chose this spot because someone down in LA posted it and I just happened to be visiting Sacramento for the weekend and it wasn't too far from our hotel. Great vibe, music and even better food. We enjoyed everything! My husband loved his koolaid and I absolutely did not. Very sweet & I don't do food coloring. The chicken, waffles, Mac n cheese and biscuits were so good. I got the gumbo and although it is not what I'm used to it was definitely really good. The base was a bit thicker like an ettoufe, and it had okra. I had leftovers and it tasted even better the next day. My tequila drink was great too. Our server was kind and provided all the info we needed. The only downfall was that my husband ordered a side of greens that never came and we ended up still paying for it. I'll def try the one in DTLA soon.
Food is always good, excellent service and the most beautiful black man behind the bar! Professional and kind,  and makes a mean drink to boot! Justin rocks! Thank you, Kevin -- he's a keeper!

We came here last week for the fried chicken and smothered pork chops. In that regard, Fixins met expectations. The chicken was crispy and tender. The pork chops were addictive (the gravy is really something else) and, so far as I could see, underrated. The sides--candied yams (the weight of a fork alone could push through them; great caramelization), red beans and rice (maybe a touch salty), coleslaw (surprisingly good. Not too creamy, and I appreciate the use of romaine for color), and collard greens (cooked with turkey necks but somehow taking on the aroma of smoked ham) were impressive. The biscuit was flaky and the whipped grape jelly butter  interesting. No knocks on the food. 

The service, though, suffered, albeit due to circumstances outside of the front of house's immediate control. The billing system broke down while we were there; I'm grateful to our server for not handing us a $30k bill. One of our original sides--the mac and cheese--was sold out. The coleslaw replacement came not just in a bowl but on top of a napkin on a plate; good attention to detail. But this notification came some twenty minutes after we'd placed our order, and after we'd gotten the rest of our food.

Then also we'd come with a reservation, but weren't seated for another twenty minutes. 

Perhaps there's some miscoordination between the front- and back-of-house; perhaps it's the many takeout orders putting the kitchen behind; in any case, our servers did the best with the hand that they were dealt. But it's a pity to see good food hobbled by operations.
The only thing was good is the Mac and cheese and yams chicken was too hard and the drinks were too sweet

Absolutely fantastic. The fried chicken and gravy was the best I've had, outside of New Orleans. The thigh was the best. The cheesy grits and Mac n cheese were both extremely flavorful. Even the cornbread was full of flavor, much better than any cornbread I've had lately. The deep fried bacon deviled eggs are a must try appetizer and so is the adult kool aid! We had the peach cobbler for dessert and that was great too! Delicious food all around.
Fixins has the best soul food around! Every dish is flavorful, the service is fast, and the atmosphere makes you feel right at home.

Food was amazing. We had the fried chicken, greens, Mac n cheese, coleslaw. The greens were some of the best restaurant greens I've ever had. The Mac n cheese was cheesy and tasted like the noodles were seasoned.  Lavender lemon drop was just okay. Nothing special. But the lemonade was fire. Service was great initially. Maia took our orders quickly and was very friendly. The food came out seriously fast. I think the restaurant got really busy because then we didn't see Maia for a long while and had to finally ask someone else for the check. When she did return she seemed preoccupied but was still friendly overall. She did a great job splitting the check. I would def. return in the future.

Wow. I wasn't expecting much for my first time here. For some context, I had soul food for dinner yesterday. But after tonight, I have determined yesterday's was a pregame. They got nothing on Fixins (out of respect i won't say who of course).

First off, love the blasting of 2000s RnB. Can never go wrong with Chingy and Tyrese haha.

Tonight's experience put the soul in soul food. My brother was shaking his head after every bite, couldn't stop smiling and talking about how good everything was. In total we ordered a chicken family meal (8 pieces), two large fixins of collard greens and candied yams, two orders of oxtail, some gumbo with shrimp and the fried fish. The yellow butter that came with the side of cornbread or biscuit was dope. Had a nice kick to it. My mac n cheese side was great too. Great balance of cheeses. Finally I ordered their special old fashioned. Very nice. 

I would have to try pretty hard to find something critical to say. Gravy was on the saltier side, but what an incredible meal. 

Everything else about my day could go to shit and I'd still be happy.

First time to Fixins Soul Kitchen. I'm gonna be completely honest. I've eaten at a lot of Soul Food restaurants from Cali all the way to Alabama and when we ate here last night the vide was great but the food wasn't great. We order Shrimp and grits and it was ok not great a little under seasoned. They barely give you any grits. This shouldn't be an appetizer. I also order the fried fish, and I was very disappointed. The batter on the fish kept coming off and the yams were not it. They needed more sweetness and love. I also ordered the greens which were overcooked and under seasoned as well. For $23 bucks you would think you get more than 1 piece of fish. The oxtails were good.

Last week was my second time here in years. I'm rarely in the area, but when I am, I have to come back here because they have the best fried chicken and collard greens! The fried chicken is perfectly crispy on the outside and juicy on the inside. The collard greens had the strong flavor of the turkey neck meat and was seasoned well. The red beans and rice were the perfect complement to the chicken. The ambiance of the restaurant was warm and homey, making it the perfect place to come with your family and friends.

Thank you for your wonderful service, Eliza! It was not easy to serve such a huge table, but you were so accommodating and made sure we got the right order and our cups of water were never empty. See you next time, Fixins!

I give the fixings brunch four stars although Yelp would not let me change from 5 to 4 and I do not know why. The fuck  I really like going to Fixin's. They're happy hour is really fun and they have a good selection of food. This review is about their brunch. I will come out and say the only reason I didn't do five stars is the coffee. You know how it taste like you got the bottom of the pot and they need to make a new pot. That's the way the coffee tasted no matter how much cream I put in my in my cup. We asked the very nice and hospitable waitress if we could have another cup out of a newer pot, but it still tasted the same. Their biscuits and gravy is fantastic. I would say almost the best I've ever had and I like biscuits and gravy, although I try not to eat them more than twice a year. The biscuits themselves were very soft and the gravy was very good my friend ordered. I'm not sure what it was called but she ordered potatoes, scrambled eggs, bacon, and a biscuit. The scrambled eggs were very, very good cooked just right the way we asked for them. The potatoes were a little bit salty but they were very good like That. It was worth having my blood pressure go up a bit lol. I try to bite of my friend's biscuit and it was just as fluffy as mine was. I have been there before and had their peach cobbler, which is amazing. I'm not sure where they're getting fresh peaches these days but I think we can get fresh fruit anywhere for mix from Mexico or other spots even though they're not in season in Sacramento. I am completely blind and I've been to fix since many times. Even when I'm alone, the staff is very nice about helping me in inside the restaurant and finding a table. I am a whiskey fan at happy hour, but I decided to try the Kool-Aid drink. I can't remember what is in it? Maybe it's vodka. I went with two girl two other girlfriends and while I thought the drink was too sweet they loved it. The fried chicken is delicious and the fried green tomatoes are yummy I had never had them before and they were certainly delicious.

My fiance and I went there for dinner before a show, and all I have to say is, every last person who works there treats you like you're a family member they haven't seen in a long time. Even Miss Pearl came up and talked to use for at least 15 minutes
Now as far as the food goes, it doesn't get anymore authentic with southern BBQ than this! 100 out of 10 recommend!

If you're looking for some delicious soul food in Sacramento this is the place to get it. The food is delicious, staff is phenomenal and prices for serving size is great. Those shrimp and grits are to die for.

First, I must apologize for the long delay in writing this review, however, as they say "Better late than never" right?  My wife and I have been to Fixins two times and the only reason I actually remembered to write this review is because I was thinking of going back there today.

We decided to grab a quick bite before continuing our trip to Santa Nella for the Mother's Day weekend. Starting with the parking situation, it's quite challenging. You might find yourself circling the block, hoping to catch someone leaving so you don't end up parking a mile away from the restaurant.  Parking aside, dining at Fixins was an absolute delight; the food was phenomenal, and the service was nothing short of stellar.

My wife tried the chicken and waffles for the first time, and she paired it with New Orleans Sweet Tea. We both enjoyed the Sweet Tea so much that it inspired me to learn how to make it at home. As for me, I opted for the fried catfish, collard greens, and candied yams, which were accompanied by hush puppies.  Not a single crumb remained on either plate after we finished eating; I must say, I've been craving some authentic Southern cooking, and Fixins certainly hit the spot.  

Indeed, the experience was so satisfying that we made a point to stop again on our return trip from Santa Nella, only to be delighted once more. However, during our second visit, there was a slight tinge of regret in her drink choice; my wife wished she had opted for the New Orleans Sweet Tea once again, rather than the Adult Cool-Aid, which I agreed was excessively sweet after I sampled it.

I'm about to charm my wife into joining me for another round of indulgence at Fixins Soul Kitchen to satisfy our shared love for comfort food.

Service was excellent. Super fast, friendly and accommodating.

Ambiance: chilled, has cool patio seating but gets very hot during summer

Food: fried chicken was good. Not phenomenal, just good. Had nice crunch and was juicy inside. Flavor was alright 
Mac and cheese: creamy and cheesy. Standard.
Fries: good, stay crunchy for a long time
Okra: aright, sour and stringy, probably wont get again
Biscuits and butter: quite good. Not too dense
Oxtail: not good. Tough and salty. Oxtail should be falling off the bone but this dish giving the over cooked pressure cooker taste. The fat was melted and tender but the meat was tough and dry to the point I couldnt finish the dish. This happened due to the meat got cooked in the pressure cooker for way too long. Common now this is a soul food restaurant, how could you over cook oxtail? For a dish thats $30 I'd expect the basic, falling off the bone oxtail. Also need to tone down on the salt. It was too salty even with rice. 
I guess Id expect more for one of the most popular soul food restaurants but I was quite disappointed. 
Cant praise service enough. Our waiter was so polite and nice. 

Wouldnt come back but worth a shot
Food is incredible. Service is good and the atmosphere is great. A good mix of people. The oxtails were incredible.

The entree of fried chicken was  seasoned and cooked to perfection thank you. 
The appetizer of fried green tomatoes was a disappointment, not cooked, it was like eating a breaded raw green tomato slice sorry to say.
Great food and service! Ordered the 3 piece chicken with candied yams and Mac and cheese. Also ordered the Gumbo, ice tea and banana pudding!  No complaints at all. Highly recommend.
