The service was ok. Took awhile to place an order because all the staff were running around. It was super busy, so I could understand. But didn't understand why the host was missing. We were placing a to-go order. Our order;
- family meal (8pcs Chicken, 4 biscuits, 2 large sides) 
- Catfish plate (includes 2 sides and corn fritters)
When we got home, I was extremely disappointed that my to-go order was missing items. This place isn't cheap. My catfish plate came with 1 catfish filet, and corn fritters, but my sides were missing. (Red beans & rice and collard greens). The food was really good so I was bummed items were missing.

Came to this place before heading out to the movies. I was told that this place is always busy but we were lucky and got seated with no problem.

I wanted to try everything! But of course I'm only one person haha I finally made my choice and ordered the catfish. With this dish you get to choose two fixin's which I got mac and cheese and potato salad. The mac and cheese was delicious! I wasn't a super fan of the potato salad so I probably would  choose a different fixin.

My boyfriend's aunt ordered the Oxtail and for an appetizer she ordered Fried Green Tomatoes. The Oxtail was super tender and it came with okra which is a plus in my eyes. I love okra! I didn't have any of the fried green tomatoes. I've tried fried green tomatoes before and was not a fan. Personally I just don't like tomatoes.

My boyfriend and his cousin ordered chicken and waffles. The chicken and waffles came with a strawberry butter that they both loved! The waffle was so good my boyfriend ordered another one.

When I'm in Sacramento again I would definitely come back to this place and try the Gumbo.

WOO WOO 
Fixins has always been on my list of food spots to try & i am so thankful the universe aligned my Airbnb to be in the same neighborhood! It was definitely fate. 

I checked for reservations prior to leaving my Airbnb and I'm so glad I did because once we arrived there was tons of people outside waiting for tables. 
So I would def make a reservation if possible prior to arriving if you don't mind the wait. They offer tons of outdoor seating as well, this visit we were given a booth inside. 

Our waitress was super fun, personable & very attentive. 
For drinks we ordered water & 2 lavender lemon drops. 
As for food I ordered the shrimp and grits because you can't go wrong there. The dish was exquisite & full of flavor. Don't forget a side of cornbread, Mac n cheese & yams it was so worth it. 
Other dishes we ordered were the waffle and 2 piece chicken with (choice of dark or white meat) The chicken was moist & cooked to perfection - had a great crunch to it. 
The waffle was sooo soft and yummy alongside their house made butter. 

This is comfort food so you will def leave full of comfort, I wish I had more space in my belly for dessert because i initially wanted peach cobbler as soon as I seen it on the menu, but definitely next time! Cannot wait to be back to try other options on the menu.

I was lucky with street parking. But apparently there's a small lot in the back for Fixins, you just have to find it. 

Very airy, warm, and modern inside. Service is very friendly. Auntie was there from the get go telling us the background story about Fixins and the owner. Newbies will have to go through the spiel so if you're an introvert just sit tight. 

Deviled eggs: just ok for me. I wouldn't order it again. I wish the fried part was crispier maybe like a scotch egg texture. It looked fried but didn't make much of a difference in taste of texture. 

Oxtail with grits:  get this. Oxtail was super soft and tender. Very well seasoned, almost too seasoned but I love salt. First bite made me drool it was so tasty and beefy. Grits I know had a million pounds of butter in it because it was the best grits I've ever had. So creamy and buttery and rich. I don't do grits often but I think about these grits every once in a while.  

Friend got gumbo. I don't care about gumbo but she was raving about it. 

Biscuits with strawberry butter:  delicious. Soft flakey and lovely. Butter was fantastic. 

Friend watered down her sweet tea so if you like it sweet you'll enjoy it here. 

Fried chicken was lovely and better than kfc. Same said about the Mac and cheese. 

I couldn't take anything to go as we were going to a hotel. Friend took it home and had leftovers for dinner and she said it heated up very well. 

We want to come back to try the fish!!!

Chicken and Chicken and Chicken what else do you need? Oh yea Waffles, best fried chicken in the Sacramento area,excellent cornbread, greens and mac n cheese too! The fish is a great choice too.Well worth the wait to get in! Also don't forget to grab a bucket of chicken to go 
Oh snap, almost forgot about the Kool Aid, Damn I'm full

The catfish is great never lets me down. The Mac is a 7/10 the red beans 6/10, Great hush puppies. We ate outside and it was so nice. We were seated immediately and the waiter was very helpful. I would definitely go back for the oxtail.

This place used to be top notch, but we were so disappointed today.  The place was packed so  my husband and I decided to take our meals to go for our wedding anniversar dinner.  Not sure if it was a one off, but I have celebrated many meals here and this had the flavor of something I would expect from Dennys or Cracker Barrel.  The ox tail meat was impossible to chew as well.  Very disappointed and not sure if we'll be back.

I had a craving for fried chicken and found Fixins Soul Kitchen. This place is awesome. Great fried chicken and the bartender was very attentive. The food comes out real fast and hot. If you want to enjoy your drink before you eat, do not order right away...it's that fast.

I went to Fixins in Sacramento for the first time.. I was pleasantly surprised! The service was top notch. I had the artichoke dip appetizer and chicken n waffles entree. The dip was creamy and flavorful, chips were crunchy with just enough salt to complement the dip. The waffle was fluffy and not overcooked, chicken had just the right amount of seasoning with a nice crunch and juicy inside. The overall atmosphere was calm and inviting even though the restaurant was busy

Fixins has a good vibe when you walk in. Had a really good burger there. Next time I go I will branch out and try other dishes. Good place to come back to. I'll let you know more when I return. Oh and the service was excellent I do remember that.

My wife and I ate ate fried chicken, fried fish sandwich, Mac n cheese, red beans & rice, and grits & cheese. Our friend let us try the oxtails. ALL OF IT DELICIOUS! Chef & his team cooking delicious food there and the front of house is kind & attentive (shoutout Brian with the ill neck tattoos!)

We have gone back several times since my first review.  The food is amazing.  The customer service is awesome.  They will definitely be keeping my business.

Update:   The first visit was definitely a one off with the greens.  I have since had the greens multiple times, and they have been delicious.
Couldn't even enjoy the food they gave me chicken breast in disguise. Was mostly bones and blood spots. Eww last time here do better

Ambiance was pretty chill. We sat outside in the patio. They had some tiki lights burning, maybe to keep the flies away. 
The server was really quick and our food came out really fast. 
Food: 5
What we ordered 
Red kool-aid: let me say. It was refreshing. Haven't had it in years. It brought back a lot of good memories. 
Margaritas was also very delicious and strong. 
Catfish nuggets: 5. You must get it. It was perfectly fired. The fish didn't fall apart. It was perfectly seasoned. Not salty at all. 
 Chicken and waffles: 4.5 only because there wasn't enough waffles!!! It's not your ordinarily waffle. It was a wheat colored and seasoned with some cinnamon. Paired with an apple butter. The chicken was dark meat. We got a drumstick and a thigh piece. More than enough for the waffle. The skin was crispy and the chicken juicy. 
Biscuit and gravy: 4 the biscuit wasn't as flakey as we would've liked it to be but still very good. This is just based on preference. The biscuit even though not flakey was not dense. Would definitely order it again but maybe with a sunny side up egg on it. The gravy had chunks of pork sausages.
I love the ambience the staff is always professional. Dee has been the bartender on a few occasions always professional and  consistent!

The ambiance is great. This restaurant has Great Service. The food was great. I enjoyed the deep fried egg. The catfish was perfect. The chicken was great and the waffle was excellent!!

I usually like my Vegetarian Omelets loaded with fresh vegetables. No can or jar mushrooms. The food portion sides were on the smaller end.

DEFINITELY better than the soul food in Oakland and the Bay Area...BY FAR!!!!  GREAT SERVICE and a beautiful bar!  I haven't had a bad dish yet and would compare some dishes to Louisiana & Mississippi quality as far as the right salt, fat, and overall FLAVOR!

Oxtails w/rice, 2 sides , presentation was great , atmosphere cozy, staff friendly, i was able to eat the smallest of the 3 Tails, very small tender delicious  the other 2 were large in size , dry and stringing on the inside, cold ,I was so looking forward to them falling off the bone the way that first Oxtail did and the way mine always turn out , I was hoping the young lady notices I had not touched the two really nice sized Oxtails an ask me if everything was OK or if i mite need a doggie bag for all the left over food , she didnt ask and I was not thinking of it , I paid the tab an the 7 dollar tip. I guess I should have "spoke up" nexttime ill speak up ,
Always so delicious! Food Service is always fast, and the customer service is always great despite how busy they are!

I've been here numerous times over the years and it's my favorite soul food spot in Sacramento! Took my beautiful girlfriend here recently and she loved it ! Service is always good , ambience is great and of course the food is Devine !
I just had to come back and say that I went back yesterday and the food was really good especially the Mac n cheese!!!

Excellent experience overall! The food was a 10/10 but the service took it over the top. Wish I could give more stars to our server, Rollin. He was so nice, knowledgeable about the menu and went above and beyond to make sure our table had an exceptional experience. I will absolutely be coming back and recommending Fixins to all my friends. 

I had the fried fish nuggets, cornbread muffin, yams, mac n cheese and chicken tenders. Everything was extremely flavorful and had a nice presentation. It came out fast and was hot which I always appreciate.

Wonderful all the way around! I loved the black eyed peas and the greens. I had the catfish plate. The cornbread was bomb also. We had our company Christmas lunch in the room and our waitress was phenomenal. I love the vibe and music. Fun, friendly and amazing food!

one of my favorite soul food spots! I got the Smothered Chicken that came w this flavorful roux gravy smothered on top. The chicken here has never disappointed me... always seasoned, crispy, and juicy!  The two fixins I decided to get on the side were the fries and mac n cheese! I also decided to add a Cornbread Muffin that came w the most delicious house made butter, absolutely amazing!! Filling and delicious! So much flavor! Great atmosphere w amazing food and drinks, I also recommend the Fried Chicken, absolutely scrumptious!! 
One thing I will say is service will usually take a while, I usually go around dinner time so I don't blame them for taking their time to make my food as great as they can but my first time here I did have to wait 40-60 mins for my food to come out. Was the food delicious? Yes, absolutely worth the wait. However if you are in a time scrunch this may not be the place you want to go especially if it is during their peak hours. Make sure you check the wait time before as well.

Finally able to take the lovely wife to this place and it didn't disappoint!

Love this place for the chicken and waffles but so far I have not been disappointed by anything I've had at this place so far. The butter that comes along with the chicken and waffles is just the proverbial cherry on top and really tied it better when you get a little bit of syrup, butter, chicken and waffle in one bite. Quite honestly I am getting hungry writing this and want to head over there right now haha!

The interior really plays to the history oak park and soul food and really seems to be a fixture to the area. They have lots of seating indoor and outdoor and despite how busy it is, it was never loud and overwhelming. The staff was extremely professional and friendly and was quick to bring food and drinks. 

I really love this place and it is moving up my list of taking people when they come visit sac. Don't sleep on this place, you're missing out if you don't visit!

Everything was delish! The fish sandwich was yummy. I would definitely be returning. Service was great, they even gave me an iced tea while I waited. Hospitality was awesome, made you feel very welcomed.
I wanted to order from door dash, but I couldn't remove honey mustard from a chicken sandwich because they don't accept special requests.

The service is friendly, but slow. I had to ask multiple waitresses to get bowls, utensils and a missing drink, just for them to tell our original waitress to bring the items. At the end, it took awhile to get togo boxes and the check..

I ordered the deviled eggs and it's different from what you normally get else where because the egg white is deep fried. I gotta try to make these at home next time cause $11 is a bit steep for two eggs. And it was a bit too mustardy for my taste. 

The fried catfish was flavorful, tender and not overly battered. The potato salad and the mac and cheese were delicious as well. I'm pretty picky with my fish, and I would definitely get this again.

The gumbo was way too salty, but it was difficult to flag a waitress down so it is what it is. Just needed to take it home to add water and more rice. 

Fixins Soul Kitchen was alright, but I would want to try some other items next time.

I texted in an order leaving a friends house arrived in 20 minutes and food was hot and ready. I ordered oxtails, black eyed peas and Mac and cheese. The portion was perfect I had lunch and more than enough for dinner. The oxtail gravy was a little sweeter than I would have wanted but they were tender and fell off the bone. I'll be back.

I can't believe I haven't had fixins until now! We got a chicken sandwich and two piece chicken with candied yams and Mac & cheese! The chicken sandwich was delicious, I loved the sauce on it. There was also an assortment of sauces to try. We got the dill ranch and the spicy mayo. I loved the dill ranch, I was dipping everything in it. I picked both thigh pieces for my two piece chicken, it was so juicy and flavorful! It was crunchy but not break your teeth crunchy. I really really loved the chicken! I can't to come back and get more. We got a banana pudding to finish off the night. We killed this within seconds, it was so creamy and full of banana flavor. 10/10!

My Mom came to visit from Louisiana for Christmas and she approves! The food and service here is consistently great. Music is on point. Highly recommend the fried green tomatoes to share with the table. I also love how family friendly Fixins is; you'll find something on the menu for every generation.
Good food and nice vibe! I love the chicken with waffles and Mac n cheese. I actually want to come here for my next birthday. The greens are good too!
Stopped in after the Oak Park Black Film Festival. Had the fish bites and a side of charred okra w a red Kool-aid. I've had their grits in the past.  Perfectly done.  I will be back again and again.

My wife and I used to love coming here, the food and staff were always great but our last two visits were awful. The food is not the same at all the quality is poor. The chicken wasn't good, the Mac n cheese was very strange texture and very like chalky not creamy at all, it was gross neither of us finished it the only thing that was good was the biscuit and grape butter. The collard greens were decent nothing special. Even the koolaid was just blah. We tried to give this place one last shot even though our last visit was trash so sadly we won't be back again. I used to be consistently good and delicious now it's consistently bad and gross

Good chicken, grits, cornbread, and drinks! Went for my husbands birthday on a Tuesday and it was packed! I can see why because everything we tried was really good. Staff was friendly and it was overall a good vibe. Can't wait to go back.

We came in a few days ago after a long day of snow boarding. There was a 45 minute wait for inside dining but we asked if we can get seated on the patio where there was no wait. 

After we were seated we were greeted by our waitress Nyshia who was so patient with us as we were deciding what we wanted to order as it was our first time dining there. 

We ended up ordering the gumbo, fried chicken and waffles, smothered chicken and waffles and various sides. Everything was so good!! Probably was one of the best gumbos I have ever had. Breading on the chicken was crispy, flavorful and seasoned well. 

It started to rain but we were warm under the heat lamps and the staff constantly came to check on us. The music was great and added another layer to our experience. 

To end off our meal we asked Nyshia for dessert recommendations which she suggested the banana pudding that was so good!! We were all fighting to scrape the sides. Will definitely be coming back again! Thank you Nyshia and everyone else who was so kind to us.

Mediocre establishment, they haven't been good for a while now. I took my mom because she requested to go there, we both left disappointed. Mind you, I only ordered sides from there. Lazy cooks and service that left a lot to be desired.

This place really hit the spot. Smothered pork chops, fried chicken, gumbo, ox tails and shrimp & grits! Needless to say we left full.
Service was great, very helpful and courteous. 
Will definitely be back for me

I'm on the fence of giving this a 3 star vs 4star. Leaning towards the latter because once you get service and sit does. The servers are exceptionally efficient, gracious and warm. My husband thankfully made a reservation otherwise there was no chance a walk-in sans reservation without a long wait. We arrived about an hour earlier than reservation hoping we could be seated earlier. It wasn't the case so we found a local craft brew and drank there while we waited. It would be nice if this place had a bar area where people could wait and cocktails and small bites. 

We ordered smothered mash potatoes and a three piece fried chicken. All sides were collectively shared to include Mac & cheese, collard greens with smoked turkey, red beans & rice and Potok salad.  My husband received a biscuit but there were no other options offered: corn bread or even white bread would have been a nice touch. 

Previously brined before frying were really good, the serving included two chops made better by gravy. Fried chicken was tender, crispy and tasty. The food was good, a bit pricey and it took awhile to be seated. I would go again on a weekday when it's not too busy.

I visited Fixins Soul Kitchen on Monday, 5/22/23, around 5 PM. It was good timing because I found close parking to the restaurant and avoided the dinner rush. 

For the appetizer, I tried the Deep Fried Deviled Eggs. The eggs were fried to perfection and had great flavor.

For the entree, I ordered the Smothered Pork Chop with Potato Salad, Mac & Cheese, and Collard Greens. My favorite part of the dish was the Mac & Cheese and Collard Greens. The Potato Salad was fine. The Smothered Pork Chop tasted good, especially with the gravy. 

My only criticism about the plate was that the pork chop had too much breading, especially for how thin it was. I left some of the breading behind on the plate.

The food paired well with a big glass of Fruit Punch Kool-Aid.

The service was a bit slow. It took a while to get some things upon request. However, the server was friendly and hospitable. 

The overall experience at Fixins Soul Kitchen was decent.

I very rarely give out 5 stars... but this place is GOOD. Everything we ordered was tasty. Price point is fair. They have a unique drink menu as well. I love that their feature alcohol is Uncle Nearest Whiskey. We ordered the catfish with Mac n cheese and red beans and rice and the red beans and rice was soooo good. The Mac was tasty but slightly over cooked. My hot chicken sandwich was HUGE! He prepared to get a lil messy... it comes with hot sauce and a spicy mayo ... 

I'm visiting Sacramento and this was my first time coming here... they have a patio area with classic and current hip hop and r&b music playing in the background... the patio is covered and catches a nice breeze... they have a full bar and classic southern menu! 

Our server was so sweet and helpful.. made great recommendations.. definitely adding this spot to my list of faves.

If you want to feel as though you're in Georgia having a meal with family, Fixins is the place to be in the Oak Park neighborhood of Sacramento. I visited on the 2nd day of grand opening and many times since then. The service is always the same. It's wonderful. The ambiance is clean yet welcoming. The bar is great to watch a game. The mixed drinks : top notch. 

Here's the star of this location (they have a second location in LA): chicken and waffles . The waffles are crispy yet soft. The chicken is buttermilk chicken so tender and flavorful. And the butter is GRAPE JAM butter!! I've tried the full menu but this simple staple keeps me hooked. Don't pass this place up. Indoor or outdoor dining both have excellent service.
Service and food was amazing. Our server  took great care of me and my family. Can't wait to come back!
Come here with an empty stomach cuz you will leave with a food coma. Love the food, vibe, music and people.

I'm just going to say it...... if you have had real soul food this place ain't it!

I have come here a few times and tried a variety of menu offerings and drinks and the nitty gritty is this place is just okay.

I decided to finally write a review after trying: 
-FRIED DEVILED EGGS- HIT but would be better with more seasoning

-SMOTHERED PORK CHOPS-MISS too greasy and seasoned BLAH. The gravy was pretty good 

-GREENS-MISS for me but it can be a hit for those who like sweet greens 

-MAC & CHEESE- MISS. Just okay. Creamy, cheesy okay flavor but southern delight definitely a miss.

-FRIED CHICKEN (& WAFFLES)-HIT well seasoned great portion size to price and flavor. Honestly the best menu item I have had 

POTATO SALAD- subjective to how you like yours..... to me it was MEH! Not good not bad just meh.

DRINKS- I've tried quite a few of their crafted cocktails: Old Fashioned, Rusty Nail, Lemon Drop all 10/10 

RECAP:
Service= good
Parking situation is hit or miss
Pricing is great for portion size 
FOOD is just okay at best

We came in at brunch today and our server was Alizae. She was so kind and attentive to our needs. She was always checking in on us. The chicken and waffles we ordered was amazing. The chicken was crispy and the waffles were perfect. I highly suggest the fish nuggets as a starter to share with the group.
Never had a bad experience at ether Sacramento or Los Angeles location. I love that the staff are efficient and professional.

Food was delicious! I'm guilty of critiquing something about a restaurant and that did not happen. Everything was delicious. Everything was 10 out of 10.  Service was good we were greeted as we walked in. The restaurant was clean. We were checked on several times which is always a plus.
Amazing food! Amazing service! Amazing ambience! This is a must visit spot for a great experience  in Sac!

Found this place while searching for a lunch spot. It was too hot to sit outside but it looked like such a nice patio set up. Servers were all nice and checked on us often. We order the fried fish, 3 piece chicken plate, side of Grits w/cheese, yams, Mac and cheese and mashed potatoes. Everything was well seasoned, crunchy, and fresh. The biscuits were equally delicious. The drinks come in a huge mason jar so you only need one. I can't wait to go back and enjoy again! 10/10 would recommend.

Still a great spot!  We went for dinner last night and sat on the patio for the first time, it was lovely.  Our server, Jae, was helpful and guided my husband to try out a new beer.  Once again, I loved the pimento cheese dip and cheesy grits.  We took home a slice of red velvet this time.

Pulled up to the sacramento location this time the ambiance is chill and nice, the staff is nice and has good energy !

The food is amazing chicken is fresh and crispy and amazing ! I go to both La / sacramento locations ! Make sure you try it out !!

- large salad with sliced fried chicken thigh & cornbread croutons. LOVED everything about the salad except for the salad dressing. 

- the biscuits with the strawberry butter were phenomenal.

- non--alcoholic drinks came in a HUGE mason jar. really appreciated not having to ask for multiple refills. they also have both Diet Coke & Coke Zero. 

- really nice ambiance outside on the patio, especially on a summer night. it's a little loud inside but would absolutely sit on the patio again.

I love this place every time someone comes into Elk grove to visit me. This is the place we go for brunch or breakfast. The line is long because everybody enjoys the weight as well. The work there's a coffee shop or a bookstore you could go to while you wait and just enjoy the neighborhood. Fantastic customer service great food. I recommend it highly, Anita, Anita Richard.
Good food. Great service. Love the decor and how everything is cohesive to the brand.  My only two cons was that the peach cobbler was way too sweet and the wait to be seated was way too long.

A friend told me about this place.  I decided to go try it.  

We decided to start out with some catfish bites.  We liked it a lot.  The fish was moist and fried well.  My husband liked it a lot.   

I ordered some fried chicken.  You had a choice of sides and I wanted to try the potato salad and mac and cheese.  The chicken was pretty good, crispy and moist. The potato salad was more on the mustard side.  You can taste it had too much mustard.  The mac and cheese tasted good. 

My husband ordered the Gumbo.  He really liked it a lot.  There was a lot of chicken pieces and sausages. He also added shrimp, too.  The gumbo was full of flavor. 

As for the service, it could have been better.  Our server was missing in action.   He didn't check on us.  We actually had to get someone else to help us.  I think it was the manager of the place is my guess.  We just asked him to fill our drinks and later we asked to get us a couple of boxes. We didn't complain, because I know it's a hard job to do. No one is perfect.

I would come here again.  I would like to try their brunch.

Great ambiance and fun music playing. Great service. 

Food (3-4/5): For the vegetarians out there: I wish they had better vegetarian entrees. I got the blackened tofu with corn succotash. The tofu was completely soft, seemed not cooked-- would have preferred if it were baked or sauteed or fried. The corn succotash was meh. Both weren't very well seasoned and I had to add salt and hot sauce. Vegan charred okra was okay. 

However, the vegan collard greens were really yummy as were the biscuits and artichoke dip. We also got the peach cobbler and banana pudding which were both really tasty but super sweet.

Desiree, our server,made this dining experience beyond words. The food was ridiculous! Desiree was attentive and humorous throughout the meal!
Coming back for sure!

TLDR: Solid spot for service and ambiance; food was alright. 

The fried chicken with fixins: the chicken was well seasoned but had too much batter for my liking. Mac and cheese was mid, coleslaw was good. The biscuit was ok - didn't have that homemade taste. The butter it came with was divine though. 

Catfish sandwich: Lots of fish which was appreciated but the bun got soggy very quickly. Fries were average. 

The service was great - Auntie Pearl came by to make sure we had everything. The ambiance was great. Seems like a nice spot to hangout with friends and grab a drink. Very clean and loved the decor.
Fantastic food, not a single miss! Chicken sandwich's, mac, deviled eggs all killer. Service was great too - thanks Jae!

Simply wonderful. You can tell if a place got the juice by the wait time and line, which we were able to avoid by making a reservation. The food was superb, their chicken is a strong contender with my mother's. They served biscuits with an apple cinnamon butter and cornbread with a maple pecan, and the biscuit and cornbread were Moist.  The atmosphere had an R&B brunch vibe, which was awesome considering the changes in Oak Park. If you have not been, what are you waiting for. Ohhh, and Cat, our waitress, she's a blessing. Go get you a plate y'all.

Lest I be accused of cultural appropriation I can't comment on the authenticity of the soul food at Fixins Sacramento, but I know I liked it plenty.  Smothered pork chops is a diet buster, rich gravy over breaded fried pork chops, but good stuff - two full size bone-in chops not fried out in the slightest, which I cannot accomplish without cooking sous vide. Accompanied by very creamy grits and tasty red beans and rice, which had lots of onions and was really nice.  Others at the table had chicken and waffles and a fried chicken omelet, something I've not seen before and which was well reviewed by its consumer.  Good size space, clean modern design with open ceilings and a large patio too.  Though there was room in the patio and main dining room, we were seated by ourselves in the back room, with the TVs.  We did not ask for any specific area, we did have a reservation which was probably a help, we were seated immediately.  Street parking, not rough on a Sunday in the commercial section of the Oak Park neighborhood, don't know about weekdays.  Honestly a really enjoyable meal.

1.20.25 The fast seating at bar, great customer service, nice drinks and delicious food was a way I saw God's favor over our day.  We were very pleased and will continue to come back! Oh and baby them yams.......... will make you want to slap the cook.....lol couldn't say my moma. Thank you for such a great experience. 
Sabrina

Food is great and service. Came here with my little ones and it's noisy enough to not bother people around us. We tried the kool-aid and family meal. It was just enough for 4 adults with big appetites. The wait wasn't too bad and we found street parking easily. Definitely will be back if we are around Broadway.
Depends on staffing and Cook of the day. One waiter overcharged us, one cook undercooked vs Great Service and Food. Seems to be hit or miss.

The greens tasted like my grandmother made them.
The chicken, Mac & Cheese was delicious.

This review is different than my other experiences here in which the food was outstanding.  This time the service was excellent. HAYDEN is working out really well as a host and our server was really attentive and welcoming.   I don't get that every time, especially when they are busy.  This time the food was marginal at best.  The gumbo was watery, the sausage (three pieces) was rubbery, the chicken was tender but lacked spice and flavor, and the shrimp (three) was also tasteless.  Maybe a bad night.

oh wow man. i've been dreading to come here for a while after my homie told me about this place and god darn this place did not disappoint at all brother. i ordered the 3 piece of fried chicken which came with two fixins (sides) and it was like 22 bucks which ain't bad. but bro this was some of the greatest fried chicken ive ever had. like jollibee was my #1 for fried chicken but like i think this is up there with jollibee. i would have to put em side to side to determine which is better but wow man. very flavorful and crispy and juicy and just beautiful man. and it also came with a bread roll thing that had some honey butter type thing like the ones that texas roadhouse be giving. pretty damn good bun ngl. and then the two sides i got were the candied yams and the mac n cheese and woah. it was my first time ever having candied yams and it was absolutely amazing. nice and soft and sweet and a perfect cinnamon flavor. bro i loved it man. it was so good. and this was some nice mac n cheese too. i've never had an extremely good mac n cheese before but this might've been the best mac i've ever had in my life man. it was so cheesy bro. i wouldn't doubt if they used atleast 20 diff types of llama or alpaca cheese in here bc of how cheesy it was. so good man. and i added their signature hot sauce to it too n it was fyeeee. and not to mention the service was astounding. the woman who took our order was very nice and called us honey bun n stuff like that which makes you really feel at home in this place ya feel me. and also our food came out like literally 3 mins after ordering. such a fire place yall gotta come here

food is always delicious, they have been consistent with flavors every time I come. I got oxtails with mac & cheese ($28), my partner got smothered chicken with mac & cheese ($18). we also ordered the lavender lemon drop ($12) and sac town mango lemonade ($15). i took off a star bc the drinks came very late, we were more than half way done with our food when we received them and we weren't in the mood to drink them after they came, we were getting stuffed. my only con is that the tables are so close together you can practically hear other customers conversation lol. our waitress was very kind and patient with us while ordering, i didn't catch her name tho. 

oxtails with mac & cheese: 10/10 
smothered chicken with mac & cheese: 8.5/10
lavender lemon drop: 4/10
sac town mango lemonade: 10/10

one of my favorite Sacramento restaurants. 
The chicken is always fresh, juicy, and overall delicious . 
One of the few spots that offers candied yams and for that I thank them. 
The menu has tons of yummy choices but I stick to my usual chicken meal with candied yams, red beans and rice, and some greens . 
Their mimosas are also super delicious and the bottle is a good deal. 
Go you won't regret it.

Came from the Bay Area to try this place. Heard many good things. However wish my review was about the food. We waited over 45 minutes and were never called. Many people checked in after us and were seated before us. So disappointed.

I've been wanting to come here for awhile now and we finally made it. We didn't have reservations but they were able to seat us right away. There were a lot of people here for dinner. They have a bar on the right side of the dinning room which looked nice but I didn't drink tonight. Just got an unsweetened tea. The Remy here was 20 bucks. 

The server said he would be with us in a second which was a little while but no worries. We had time to look over the menu. We started out with the catfish bites and they were melting in your mouth I really liked them a lot and I will be getting the catfish next time we come here. 

My wife was craving for fried chicken and she got the three piece with mac and cheese and potato salad. I love potato salad but this one was really mustardy and very little potatoes. I was so disappointed in it. 

Now the chicken had a weird after taste to it and I didn't like it at all but my wife did which is all that matters . I will pass on it. 

I got the gumbo and liked it a lot. I added shrimp which was perfect. Lots of chicken and sausage in it. Worth trying. 

I really liked the look of the entire place. Nice and clean to. A diverse crew working there to. It was nice.
The fried chicken was dry, skin and meat, like it had been sitting under a  heat lamp for a long time. The black eyed peas were good.

YUMMY, great ambience, a must for visitors. They are very busy... even midweek... consider making a reservation and Open Table. There were four of us and we decided we needed to taste everything. Gabriel was very nice at the front door and apologetic for the delay in seating us.  No worries on our part. JC was an excellent server helped us make decisions and split the check since we were on business and needed receipts. We had fried green tomatoes, cheese pimento spread, cheesy grits, greens which were outstanding, Black Eyed Peas which is made differently than any of us had ever seen, and two salads with fried chicken. Can I tell you it was amazing. We were so full we didn't even ask for dessert I would recommend this location highly.

ALICEA WAS THE BEST!!! Thank you so much for treating me well.  Bartenders(ISABELLA&LUC) thank you too for treating me well/ perfectly. The food was amazing just like my grandma's southern cooking,  I will be back!

Great for lunch. We had a group of 6 and had to wait about ~20mins for a table. Our server was good. The drinks were good. My favorite on the menu were the fried green tomatoes. Very fresh and the breading was crisp. The shrimp and grits were ok. The smothered chicken was delicious!

author_location: South San Jose, San Jose, CA
text:
Wait time for a Saturday night was a bit slow, about an hour. 

Candied Yams: 3/5, very very sweet
Mac and cheese: 5/5, best of the best
Collared greens: 4.5/5
Fried chicken: 3.5/5, but too oily for my taste 
Fried catfish: 3/5, fish was lacking any flavor 

The ambiance was stellar, you feel right at home. The reustraunt was clean inside and out. My biggest complaint is the server. It took him 15 mins to get a side of ranch and over 30 minutes to get our bill. We started seeing other tables just all waiting for their bill as well.
Great food, good price point and great drinks. This is the place to go for a fresh take on soul food.

Wasn't sure what to expect and was pleasantly surprised by the good food and friendly service. Shout out to our server, Rollin! Super nice guy with a sense of humor. Went to this restaurant yesterday as a group of four to celebrate a birthday. We had reservations for 6 pm. It was very crowded inside and on their patio dining area. But we were seated within 10 minutes of our reservation time. There's loud music playing with a lot of bass so If that's not okay with you, you may want to get take out. 

Our group had the fried chicken, fried fish, and sides of greens, grits, black eyed peas, and candied yams. The chicken was crispy, flavorful, and cooked done, not dry. ( you could enjoy the breast without choking it down) you know what I mean . The fish was ordered fried hard and was crispy, flavorful, moist and delicious. The sides were delicious and flavorful, especially the greens. They had an excellent blend of sweetness and acidity, they were made with smoked turkey neck.  This is not health food, there is a good amount of salt but it was not overly salty. 

Also checked out Underground Books right around the corner and Licks ice cream up the street! We really enjoyed our visit to the area and will return! Great job Kevin Johnson and developers!

Fixin's hits the spot if you're craving real Southern comfort food with some upscale flavor. The fried chicken is juicy, seasoned right, and actually reminds you of home, not that chain stuff. Sides like the mac and cheese and greens don't miss either. It's on the pricier side, but you're paying for quality and that soulful vibe. It won't disappoint

Let me tell you, this was the best soul food I've ever had. If you don't try it, honestly, that's on you. Every single bite was pure comfort--the kind of food that feels like a warm hug. The mac and cheese? Perfectly creamy and loaded with flavor. The fried chicken? Crispy on the outside, juicy on the inside, just like it should be. And don't even get me started on the greens and cornbread. This place just gets it right. Trust me, if you're craving authentic soul food, you don't want to sleep on this spot. So, good I only got pictures of the appetizer and dessert  which did not disappoint!!

The first time I came here, I was really not happy. My yams were oily, and overall, I just wasn't impressed at all. I even told a few friends that I wasn't a fan of the place when ever it was recommended to me But they encouraged me to give it another chance, saying I might have just visited on a bad day. So, yesterday, I decided to try again. This time, my experience was much better! I ordered the smothered oxtails, which were delicious. My husband chose the fried chicken, and it was hot and tasty. My oldest child had a bacon burger that looked amazing, while my other little broke homies (kids) had chicken strips. Everyone enjoyed their meals, and I was really happy with my choice. I'm glad I went back because it turned out to be a great meal. It just shows how trying again can sometimes lead to a better experience. My sever was Kat who I can tell was working a lot of other tables but I definitely appreciated her consistency. Only thing I wasn't a fan of was the wait time. I did have reservations and although I was seated at a decent time they were packed that Saturday so it was a wait just to order and receive our food. What I would recommend to anyone is come here when you're really on a chill zone they play music so that's very laid back, and all their workers are very nice. I had my kids with me so it was a whole bunch of stop, wait , no coloring on her paper lol we got through it though.
Delicious. Shrimp n Grits, oxtail, fish fry, gumbo and yes, the fried chicken.  It's delicious.  Collard greens, Mac n cheese, red beans and rice. Delicious.  Staff are fun and friendly.

A Soulful Dining Experience at Fixins Soul Kitchen in Sacramento

After a great round of golf with three buddies, we decided to stop by Fixins Soul Kitchen in Sacramento--and let me tell you, it was the perfect choice! From start to finish, the experience was nothing short of exceptional.

The Service:
Right off the bat, the service was excellent. The staff was friendly, attentive, and made sure we felt right at home. We didn't have to wait long, which was impressive considering how popular this spot is.

The Food:
Now, let's talk about the food--absolutely incredible! We indulged in as much of the menu as we could handle.
 * The oxtails were fantastic, tender, and flavorful.
 * The chicken wings? Perfectly crispy and seasoned just right.
 * The sides--greens, red beans and rice, cornbread--were piping hot with steam rising off them, just the way soul food should be.

Everything we tried was fresh, delicious, and satisfying. Fixins really knows how to deliver on authentic, soulful flavors.

The Atmosphere:
The vibe in the restaurant was warm and inviting, making it a great spot to relax and enjoy good food with good company.

Before leaving, I even brought my wife dinner so she could enjoy the magic of Fixins too. Safe to say, this won't be my last visit--I'll definitely be back.

If you're in Sacramento and craving amazing soul food, Fixins Soul Kitchen should be at the top of your list!.
Lamont 
1/05/2025

This was my first time eating at Fixins, and I have been wanting to try for a very long time. I ordered the gumbo and some sides of mac & cheese and collard greens. My boyfriend ordered the smothered chicken w/ mac & cheese and candied yams. We shared a purple kool-aid. It was all so delicious and flavorful! My gumbo had lots of flavor and was generous with the portion sizes; as well as my sides. The chicken was super juicy and the skin was packed with flavor and super crispy. He got the gravy on the side, as suggested by our very nice server. Our server, Rollin, was super kind, and gave us the best recommendations to make our meal even more enjoyable. The restaurant was nearly at full house, and I can see why, as this place serves great food and has great service. I'll definitely come back for my soul food cravings!

I can saying being from Atlanta this is one of the best soul food places I have found outside of the south 
The flavors and the options are head on , I've been places that felt spaghetti was soul food so this place was refreshing 

We got 3 piece chicken , greens with turkey, Mac n cheese: well seasoned , fresh fried, Mac cheesy but not runny, greens seasoned nice 

Deviled eggs: good could use more salt but over all worth it 

Oxtails: tender meaty and filling , I would have liked more rice but the gravy was good has sweet taste to it 

Drinks : standard but good  
The music is A1 urban old school not too loud for dinner and conversation

I w sh I could find something to knock  this place but I can't. On the other hand I wish I could say something that stood out, but I can't.
It was a cool spot very chic. There is a wait so be patient. I had the oxtails and as a fan of the tail of the ox it was okay (not bad not spectacular) the potato salad was meh and the black eyed peas was okay. Here's what stood out they gave me a huge Mason Jar of ice water now that's soul food not some ol dainty water glass.
It's a cool spot that's all I have to say don't wanna disrespect the Mayor Kevin Johnson

I feel like I'm fixin to be at Fixins all the time!

We came from outside the area. It was a nice lil drive to get some good food I been eyeing to try for a while.

We had fried deviled eggs, fried green tomatoes, catfish nuggets.. Appetizers were fantastic, specifically the catfish/eggs. 

I had oxtails w/rice & gravy, seared okra, yams. Everything was very good & plentiful. I had like 6 oxtails.. where they do that at?.. at Fixins!

Other meals were chicken/waffles, beans & rice, smothered pork chops. Lots of food with enough to take home. Great prices for portion.

Ahhhh.. dessert, YES.. I had the peach cobbler and it did not disappoint. A perfect blend of crust, juice, peaches, cinnamon, and nutmeg.

s/o to our waiter, I believe her name is Sadeyah. Apologies if I'm incorrect.

Fixins is a YES!! Glad I made it.

We ordered the 3 piece wing which comes with 2 waffles & 2 sides (we chose Mac N Cheese and Candied Yams)- added on a side of greens. Big portions, can order family style.. to say the least 1000/10 #getyousome

I was in Sacramento and stopped by fixins for dinner last weekend after seeing a fellow yelper post a review. I hung out at the bar and the service was solid, the bartenders made sure I was constantly good. I ordered the oxtails, they were really good. The meat effortlessly came right off the bone. It was my first experience with non Jamaican oxtails and i enjoyed them. I ll definitely have to check them out again soon.

Location was nice! Made reservations on OpenTable which I highly recommend if you don't want a long wait time. Ambience outside on a nice sunny day was perfect they do have heaters outside if it's a little breezy. 
Waitress was ok can't remember her name but she never refilled our water or asked us for any refills. Although she was nice she lacked the skill of checking on her tables. 
Mac and cheese and fish nuggets were the best things we ordered including the sweet tea not too sweet just perfect. 
The better burger with the bartered fries and chicken waffles was also good. 
I ordered the oxtails which has a good amount however it's too sweet for my liking. I usually like a more savory taste and there black eye peas lack in flavor I tried to spice it up with their hot sauce but there hot sauce was not it. I wish I would've asked for another hot sauce brand but didn't think to ask I'd prefer crystal with my black eye peas and chicken. 
Definitely recommend their Mac and cheese filled with flavor and cheese they put their foot in it!

Had lunch with a good friend , she loves Fixins . We both ordered the Fried Chicken Dinner with various sides , I ordered the potato salad, greens and candied yams, my friend ordered greens and Mac and cheese. I would rate the chicken 7 out of 10 as the white meat was over cooked , batter falling off before I even ate it , the leg and thigh were moist on the inside which was good but still it was fried too hard ! The greens were flavorful and tender 9 out of 10 ,  the yams were sweet and decadent but could have used a little more butter flavor, and if they are cooked in butter make sure it's not unsalted as salted butter would turn up the flavor! 7.5 out of 10 , The potato salad was decent 6 out of 10 , The biscuit was a tad bit dry as well , not as good as I remembered them being a few years ago , 5 out of 10 ... 
The service was good however the hostess seemed very distracted and not very friendly, which may mean she was over extended with some of her duties outside of being the Host , When I enter a restaurant I expected a warm friendly greeting! I don't want to feel like I am bothering someone! Our main server was friendly, however the service was still average 6 out of 10 . The interior was tidy , as tables were cleaned off in a timely manner, the bar could use a little revamping as the seats were so close to one another and the general seating that elbow room was none existent, 7 out of 10 ! Would I go back to Fixins again? Probably, Now that I have moved back to the area after being away for a long while , along with the fact that my family and friends seems to love this place , I will mostly likely come back however I wouldn't choose this restaurant again for myself ! 
In the meantime, 
Happy Eating

We love the one in LA and now this one as well. I ordered the exact same thing as I ordered in LA a year ago and it was just as delicious - if not better.
They clearly have a formula that works across all regions.
I'm sorry they won't be opening one in Oakland, but I will make an effort to visit all the other ones.

BB

Me and my husband went here on a date night. We both got the same thing... the catfish. I got mines with a side of greens and yams, and we ordered a side of red beans and rice to share. It was busy and we had to wait before getting seated but it was worth the wait. 
The cornmeal crust on the catfish was crunchy but I will say that the fish was a tad mushie... but the flavor was definitely there.
The collard greens, yams and red beans and rice had the great flavor! 
My husband has the adult koolaid, that is a must try!

The food is delicious! Everything is well seasoned, no need for hot sauce or additional seasoning. They do have a house hot sauce. We ordered shrimp and grits, fried chicken and black eyed peas. I had Smothered  chicken, black eyed peas, and greens. The greens were cooked with turkey. Smackalicious!!

My Wife and I went on Friday night. They close at 9:00 pm and we made it at 8:30pm. We Didn't have high expectations, but wanted some food. We walked in and we were seated Immediately. The vibes were Amazing. No feeling of we're about to close at All. The music and atmosphere was Amazing. Cat was our waitress and the customer service was beyond Amazing! She was the Best waitress I've had in 2025! I ordered the pork chop meal with my gravy on the side as pictured. The chops, macaroni, greens and cornbread were Amazing!!! My Wife ordered the 3  piece fried chicken, red beans & rice and candied yams. I didn't get a picture of her food. The cornbread was just Delicious and the Best fried chicken we've had in Sacramento. We will be regulars moving forward and we Appreciate we Finally have somewhere to patronize for Good Southern food. Definitely a restaurant that listened to their customers. We also got our sister the catfish meal to go and she said it was Amazing as well.

Seating took about 45 minutes, but there is a nice bookstore a couple of doors down to browse while you wait. Once seated, our waitress told us she would be right with us, and she was!!!

We ordered shrimp and grits with cheddar  cheese on top, chicken, waffles , and fried green tomatoes. I also got the lavender lemon drop. I'm a lightweight, so I wasted most of it lol.

The green tomatoes were delicious.

The gravy on the shrimp and grits was not to my liking. It was chocked full of onion tomatoes and peppers. I would have preferred the gravy by itself, and the scoop of grits was swimming in the gravy. The grits were a wonderful texture, and the shrimp cooked to perfection. More grits and less gravy. Use the tomatoes and onions for garnishment.

The waffles tasted really good. They should give the option of regular butter if you don't want the strawberry kind. Hubby and I actually ended up switching plates. He found the gravy of the shrimp and grits to be too spicy. The chicken was pretty good.

We would come again and try other items on the menu.

It was total word-of-mouth got us here. Rich from UC Davis raved about it so we had to try it out. We started out with a couple of beers and moved on to the artichoke dip. It was perfect to start off, but the main event was even better. We shared a three piece chicken plate with potato salad and collards. I'm really a fan of the collards. Of course we didn't eat it all, we brought home one of the breast and ate it cold sliced up with a salad the next day. It was perfect! Maybe even better cold than hot! Can't wait to come back and try other dishes.

Had a great brunch here with some Bay Area friends a few weeks ago. Had a reservation snafu and ended up waiting for a *really* long time but we eventually were seated so it's all good.

Settled on simple fare - biscuits and gravy, egg breakfasts and omelettes. Food was pretty darn good all the way around!

We had really good service, the ambiance is welcoming with a cool local yet southern aesthetic.

Reasonably priced, and I know I MUST return for some chicken and waffles! Will definitely be back.

Service was great pleasant, attentive and friendly. The food was good, hot and flavorful, I just wished the waffle was crispy instead of soft. The atmosphere is comfortable with neighborhood type of spot and the not more tgan a dull roar. Having a conversation over food is really easy no feeling like yelling.

I've been here before and now came back to Fixins Soul Kitchen. I took my nieces and nephew.)

FOOD
We ordered the deviled eggs as an appetizer. I ordered Gumbo with shrimp. They ordered the following: Oxtail, Chicken sandwich, 2 piece chicken, Gumbo.

SERVICE
Our server, Sean, was attentive; he definitely provided excellent customer service. 

AMBIENCE
This restaurant was so busy.When I went to register with the host, she said there was a 2-hour wait. My nieces and nephew wanted to go elsewhere. I told them it's WORTH IT! Fortunately, when I checked in after an hour, they had a table for us.

I will come back here at Fixins and would recommend it if anyone is looking for a southern/ soul food in the area!

A friend of mine spoke very highly of this place, and very glad I gave it a try tonight.

Very cool vibe walking in with fun and friendly staff. Our server Cat was very welcoming and provided excellent service, even when it seemed like she was juggling plenty of other tables.

My friends and I (party of 4) ordered family style so we could try as much as possible, and I was in love from the beginning. I enjoy anything with eggs, but I had never tried deep fried deviled eggs before. Good flavor and the fry wasn't too heavy. Having just one piece was enough for an app, so 4 to split was perfect.

Cornbread had a nice crisp outer later and not too dry inside. I didn't care too much for the maple cayenne butter, but I could definitely taste the maple for any maple lovers out there.

Friend catfish was excellent but looks bigger than it really is. Would recommend if you're looking for a lighter dish, but honestly it doesn't seem like any of the dishes are light. However, they really deliver on flavor so I didn't mind at all.

Overall, the sides we chose had such a homemade feel to them. I couldn't stop picking at the collard greens and grits. I would recommend dipping everything in the gravy that comes with the smothered chicken too.

We ended the meal with three more pieces of fried chicken, but we probably could've done without it. Very great first experience and would definitely return to try the rest of their menu.
The food and service are amazing. The ambiance is very lively and good vibes! I definitely recommend!

Probably one of the best chicken and waffles I have tried in Sacramento, I love the strawberry butter thing they have going on so delicious! Waffle and 2 Wings are only $9 which I think is pretty good price-wise in my opinion. The ambiance is pretty cool, bathroom is also pretty clean. Nice service!
Great ambience. Staff was super friendly. Loced the vibe in the neighborhood. Food was a tad on the salty side, couldn't finish all of it. Sides were better than mains.

I finally had the chance to try Fixins Soul Kitchen. Don't know exactly why it took so long as I love good Southern and Soul Food. Fixins is located in Oak Park near Old Soul and Faria and is already a mainstay in Sacramento. The location is typically busy to crazy and only has limited street parking available. You may need to walk off your dinner here!

Fixins has a great menu including traditional soul food options with the chicken available family style which is a great option for groups. The interior of the restaurant is modern country with the requisite Edison bulbs and chicken wire. We were able to snag a large table by the window without a reservation on a Thursday lunch which was great. The booths have padded seats which is more and more important as I get less young.

Our group ordered several different options including the gumbo and chicken. One group got a family meal with greens and red beans and rice. Another got the smothered chicken with mac and cheese. My meal was the classic chicken and waffles. The classic combo of chicken and waffles was done very well with a more traditional waffle, not some fancy Belgian liege stuff. I ordered white meat which came with a large breast, but this dish is best with the dark meat since the breast is just too big to handle.

The chicken is really good and prepared well. It has a great breading with real flavor. I did use a lot of the hot sauce. And the syrup! The waffle had powdered sugar, syrup, and a huge butter ball. Please do not ask for the nutritional facts here. The breast was great, but some wings and legs would be perfect. The rest of the group loved the chicken, but the gumbo was not a favorite. Most of us ordered sweet tea as well which is served in a massive mason jar which probably should have come with a side of Jardiance. It was good as well.

I have to caveat that I have been to many restaurants in the south, so Fixins is a great California soul food place. A lot of true southern places may scare off too many locals. I've had better, but this is one of the best in Sacramento. It is a great place to see and be seen, especially for impressing a client or meeting the family for the 1st or 74th time. They have lots of big tables encouraging big parties. Take out kinda ruins the experience and may actually be better with Popeye's. 

Fixins is upscale soul food which like upscale Mexican food must be done in a very delicate and deliberate way. They manage to walk a fine line while still delivering a good and popular product.

This is 'The Place To Be'! I love love love this establishment. The staff, servers, managers, bar staff and all are kind, considerate and attentative to the customers.  Energy and vibration is everything and here, (At this restaurant) good energy is a culture.  The food is good, taste and have the 'Soul Food' dynamic, style and flavor.  The 'Artwork' on the walls of the restaurant are entertaining, culturally enlightening and funny.  i can relate and will be back. 

Good food, Good Music and the staff is the best!  Rollin was my server and he was the best, handsome and polite.

I've ordered from Fixins on delivery platforms but finally had the chance to visit in person and now I'll be telling everyone to go eat there. Located in Oak Park, there isnt much parking near by but the location is big and always full. My friend arrived 10 minutes before they opened and there was already a line forming. 

I dont think you can go wrong with anything on their menu. From the strong drinks to the appetizers, and main entrees you will be stuffed and wanting to come back again. Although we went for brunch and the fried deviled eggs werent on the menu they were still able to prepare them for us. They werent over cooked nor too oily for a delicious starter. 

As for the smothered chicken (drum and breast pictured) you will be using the cornbread muffin to wipe the plate clean. I do have to say that my absolute favorite side is the mac and cheese. It's comfort in a small bowl and I could probably eat that only. The cornbread muffin is also a must, and I'm not sure what is in their butter but it complements it so well. 

The staff is super welcoming and the service is great. The food is served in a reasonable time frame and the place is very well kept and clean.   This is a place I will be coming back to and will tell everyone to go to as well.

Kim was our server and she is fantastic! We love it here! The food is always phenomenal.    Auntie Pearl always comes to check in on us. Thank you fixins for exceeding my expectations every time. See you next time
The food, service,  and ambiance were amazing! The oxtails, fried chicken and pork chops were cooked to perfection! All of the sides were amazing! Only regret was I was too full for dessert.
The music here is popping! The bass is loud. Very fun atmosphere. Food is good and chicken is juicy. Service was okay as they messed up our order but we got our food fast! I would come back.

Ambiance total vibe! Service fast and friendly! 

I recently had the pleasure of trying some fried chicken that was absolutely amazing. From the first bite, I knew I was in for a treat. The chicken was perfectly crispy on the outside, while remaining juicy and tender on the inside.

The texture of the pork chops was outstanding. They were incredibly tender and moist, making each bite a delight. The meat was juicy and succulent, and it practically melted in my mouth. 

Greens, Mac and cheese, grits w/cheese, and candied yams all hit the mark!! 

Oooo forgot about the Fried deviled eggs and kool-aid also met my standards of excellence!  Who doesn't love red kool-aid 

Definitely recommend!!

I visited Fixins for the first time on a Saturday at noon with a reservation and was pleasantly surprised! I had heard about how busy this location was and I truly think I would have been seated just as fast without a reservation. The staff were very friendly and the restaurant looked very clean. It smelled amazing and I was so glad the food met all my expectations! I loved the sweet tea with some fresh lemons and the chicken wings with waffles was AMAZING!! So juicy and tender and I adored the cinnamon butter. Also, the peach cobbler is literally a must have. If you are full by the end of your meal, which you will be, just get it to go!! I highly recommend giving Fixins a try (or three)!

Top tier soul food!

Came in on the night of Veteran's Day with a friend and didn't have to wait that long. The restaurant was filled up, the music was great, and all was well. I ordered the 2 piece fried chicken meal (dark) with yams and macaroni and cheese. For the restaurant being so busy, it did not take that long for the food to come out. Everything was delicious! 

The chicken was seasoned amazingly well and was fried to perfection. Even before I tried the yams, I could smell they were correct. They tasted like something I would have at a family gathering. The biscuit that it comes with was moist and that butter really made it pop! As for the macaroni and cheese - it was good but was not the best macaroni that I have had. I expected it to be a little more seasoned. Needless to say, I still devoured it. The price was also super reasonable! It was only $17 for all that food, which was very surprising.

Service was fantastic and there were plenty of free parking options close to the restaurant.
Being from south carolina this food is what i get if i want to get a taste from home...

Popped in here looking for some solid cozy comfort food. Love the location, decor, and vibe. Place was pretty packed when we walked in to place a to-go order and it was a bit of a wait but worth it. Would've been nice to be offered some water or something while we waited though because it did take longer than we expected. 

The food is good soul food, not all the flavors I'm used to but nice to try something new sometimes too right! Gotta expand your palate and comfort zone occasionally or you just aren't living.

Looking forward to try the dine in experience next round and get a nice community vibe going too.

Recommend ordering ahead rather than walking in. There was lots of to-go orders and pickups so I know they handle that smoothly. Well for sure order ahead next time we aren't dining in,

When asked where my wife wanted to have her birthday lunch, she stated, "Fixins"!  As we headed to the Oak Park area, I was looking forward to fried chicken and other foods, where, "without soul, it's just food".  There were no parking spots on 3rd Avenue where the restaurant is located, but there was ample street parking on surrounding streets.  We were immediately greeted and seated, entertained by the sounds of Lil Wayne, after we entered the restaurant on a Friday afternoon.

We knew we wanted the 3 piece Fried Chicken ($21) and Smothered Pork Chop ($20), but curious about the Deep Fried Bacon Deviled Eggs ($12).  For the two fixins included with each main dish, we chose the Cheese Grits, Charred Okra with bacon, Black-eyed peas, and Collard greens with turkey neck.  The fried chicken required three medium bites to get through the crispy goodness, to reach the moist and tender meat.  The two pork chops, smothered in roux brown gravy, tasted rich and flavorful.  The Deep Fried Deviled Eggs were creatively crafted with fried egg whites and deviled egg yolk added on top with a piece of bacon.  

After this meal, we needed to walk it off. If you like museums, we enjoyed the California Museum to spend the rest of our afternoon.

On our next visit to Fixins, we will try the Oxtails and Fried Fish.

Food - 5
Service - 5
Ambiance - 4

I moved to Sac a few years ago, but didn't get around to going to Fixins until recently...why did I wait so long to try this gem?! I went for a birthday party and had no shame about sampling every single dish at the table. Everything was delicious, including the blackened tofu, which I was not expected from a soul food restaurant. Nice lively vibe.

I can't believe it took me so long to try this place. It's incredible! Great service, great ambiance, super clean, and the food is so good. Portions are huge! 

The chicken in waffles is better than Roscoes! The chicken fry is perfection, the waffles flavor with that butter is so bomb. I can't even really describe it. It was just perfect. 

Shrimp and grits was full of so much flavor. I loved the spice it had. It was a bit rich for my husbands taste but I loved it. 

The Mac is definitely one of the best if not the best I have ever had. Biscuits with the butter just oh my goodness. Perfect potato salad.

I literally can't wait to go back and try the entire menu. We went for first time last weekend and we were back today. The sweet tea is perfection, and I can't wait to try the kool aide. 

Not one bad thing to say about this place. Go!

The food was delicious! I ordered the 2 piece chicken and waffles which were amazing! For $16, it was well worth the price. My sister had the fried fish with 2 sides and corn fritters. Fixins has large portions. We were both stuffed and we still had to use to go boxes for leftovers.  I would have gave it five stars but the service was a little slow but they were really busy. Overall I'll be coming back for seconds!!

Ambiance: 5
Food: 5
Music: 5
Team: 5
Service: 5

5 stars all around! This place is amazing! 

I had the fried chicken, greens, Mac n cheese, yams, deviled eggs, shrimp grits. 

This place didn't miss. The energy was amazing. Our server, Chandra was amazing! 

I'll definitely be back.
Haven't had anything bad on the menu! Great service also!  Nice atmosphere!  I like the soul food here better than any place in Oakland!  Love the chicken, shrimp dishes, and okra!

We visited on saturday around 12 and was seated right away. I'm not sure what was going on but the hosts didn't greet us, and their attitude wasn't great. Both had a mug on their face and seemed like they didn't want to be there :( not the first impression i was expecting. Otherwise service was great, our servers took care of us and checked in frequently. Even the people clearing the plates/bringing the food was very nice.

We got:
-2 pc chicken with waffles. Chicken was 5/5 soooooo juicy omg seasonings were perfect and fried to golden perfection. We got chicken breast and it wasn't dry at all which is awesome. Waffle was 2/5, it was too thin and not enough crisp on them. 
-Smothered chicken with collard greens and mac n cheese. Smothered chicken 5/5 I got dark meat chicken thigh. Again soooooo juicy and very well done. Collard greens 3/5 the seasoning was great but too salty for me. Mac n cheese 4/5 solid mac n cheese, nothing out of the ordinary. 
-Sweet tea 3/5 I forgot that Southern sweet tea are traditionally on the sweeter side and this was def too sweet for me XD but the tea flavor was there. They gave us a huge cup too and even offered to give us a togo cup to take home.

Overall the food was good, the portions were hefty (we ended up eating this again for dinner). Service was good except for the host. Would come back to try other menu items!

The service is first rate! You feel
Like an honored guest in their home. The patio is amazing, and the food is the icing on the cake. Love this place!

Dropped by for lunch on a Saturday afternoon. I've been here before, and it was really good so I decided to bring my folks as well. We had a reservation so we were seated pretty quickly even though they were very busy.

We ordered the fried green tomatoes and fish nuggets as an appetizer. I've had fried green tomatoes before a few times and never liked them, but I was pleasantly surprised here. They were nicely battered, and delicious. They came with a dipping sauce, and we completely devoured these. The fish nuggets were equally impressive. They were light and crispy, not oily at all and very flavorful. Definitely a good start to the meal.

I ordered the fried chicken with the okra and the black eyed peas. The black eyed peas had great flavor, and this was the second time I've ordered the okra here. It's slightly charred instead of fried, smoky with no sliminess and overall a really solid side dish.  

The chicken here is clearly the hero though. It's well seasoned, incredibly juicy and beautifully crispy without being greasy at all. I ordered the dark meat, but I tried some of the white meat that my wife ordered and it was so good also. This is probably the best fried chicken I've eaten anywhere.

Honorable mention goes to the biscuits. They're light and come with what I'm pretty sure is a house made strawberry butter. I took a bite, and felt like I was back in the south for a second. I ended up inhaling one of these biscuits before I even tried anything else on my plate. 10/10

Since we were a group I got to sample some other sides including the mac n cheese, the potato salad, the collard greens, and the yams. The mac n cheese is really good. The potato salad is exactly what potato salad should be, and I'll order a side of this next time. The collard greens are good, and the yams were not too sweet which I like and tasted good also.

To top it all off, the service is excellent. All of the staff that we talked to were really nice and inviting, and made the overall experience even better. 

Everything from the food quality to the service was consistent both times I've been here. Absolutely one of my favorites at this point, and I'll definitely recommend others to try.

I came here and ordered 2 fried chicken plates and an oxtail dinner. They forgot my son Mac n cheese. I also had hair in my fried chicken. They refuse to answer the phone. I'm extremely frustrated because I came here 2 years ago and I had hair in my oxtails, so I never came back until today and here it is another hair in my food. I'm calling and no one is even answering the phone. You guys must not be wearing any hair nets or something for this to happen again! Now I will never be back and this place need to be shut done.

Great food great service. 
Corn bread yams Mac n cheese Pork chops oxtail and fish nuggets are amazing!!

The greeting was well because we made reservations.  2nd after being seating in the back was good however my family and I waited because we were forgot about. But one of the managers came and asked had anyone came and I stated no. The manager apologized and sent someone to take our order. It wasn't  along wait for food but when the food came it was salty. It was season salt heavy. My waffles were dry.  So with thar being my first time there it will be my last. Maybe someone need to speak tp the cook about so much season salt. We'll the atmosphere was good. But the food not all that great. I will wait awhile before I go back.

This place was amazing. Service was excellent and attentive. Food  was amazing and if I could eat more I'd order the whole menu. Music was not too loud to where you could not carry a conversation and I loved the quote theme pictures around the restaurant. I will be going back to try every thing on the menu one trip at a time .

My husband had seen the restaurant since we moved to Sacramento 3 years ago and was curious to try. 
What a great customer service. We were nicely welcomed, seated right away in a large booth, then one of the managers came, she introduced herself, said she was the aunt of the owner, Kevin Johnson (ex NBA player and ex mayor of the city. 
The food was served fast, the plates were huge and everything was flavorful.
The restaurant was not too crowded, it was lunch, nice ambiance with some RnB music. Before we left, Kevin Johnson, came to our table, shook our hands and thanked us for coming. 
Overall we loved our experience in the restaurant and we will be back!

Trying this place out for the first time - other Southern-bred restaurants in Sacramento that I used to frequent have closed - I forewent my preference of catfish for the acid test of fried chicken.  Would the breast meat be tender?  It was!  It was tasty and delicious, some of the best interior chicken-on-the-bone meat I've had.

The breading was very crispy but also very plain and unseasoned.  There was a bottle of hot sauce on the table, but I'm reluctant to anoint crispy breading with a liquid sauce, which even in small quantity might make it soggy.

However, I took one piece home and it was just as good heated up in the microwave the next day, which isn't always true.

The order took 15 minutes to arrive at my table, which is pretty fast for chicken fried to order, which this must have been.

Sides of mixed quality.  The collard greens had a bitter sauce.  The grits were of the consistency of plaster, but I like grits that way.

Also: this place is very clean, even the restrooms.

This is my third time eating here. I had no problems the first two times as everything was good. Last night my order was subpar. The oxtail was so tough I to put it in my crockpot when I got home. I was hungry and disappointed. They were aware the oxtail was not cooked long enough when they picked them up to place over the rice. The meat should have been falling off of the bowl. Unfortunately, I live too far to return it. I expected better for one of the most expensive items on their menu!!! 

See video of oxtails after an hour and thirty minutes in my crock pot. 

Zero stars for this visit. 2 stars added because of good previous experiences.

Over hyped, because the place is owned by a basketball legend. We walked in and was instantly greeted by the hostess and seated within 10 mins. It was a rainy day, so it was not that busy.

Our hostess was definitely a people's person. She was chatty and bubbly, loved it! However, that may have taken away from her attentiveness to service. A few tables had to wait for checks, water, a spoon (me). I am not going to mention her name, because #1 I don't want her to get in trouble, #2 this post is more about the food.

The shrimp and grits was more like gravy with an over excessive amount of onions and peppers. The grits was swallowed up by vegetable gravy. There was so much gravy that you could barely even taste the grits. The jumbo shrimp was nice sized and flavorful, and they give you a good amount of them. 

The chicken for the chicken and waffles was pink with a gooey texture on the inside. The waffle was flatter than a pancake, and flavorless. The collard greens, and the mac n cheese was on point tho!

Maybe we came on a bad day, or maybe we ordered the wrong meals having a certain expectation of how we wanted it to taste. Either way, it is now marked off the bucket list for the experience.
Great service. Great food, Great portion. Room for large groups. Full service bar. Only challenge was parking.

Wanted some good ol' southern comfort food and was so pleased that Fixins popped up on my search. Everything was delicious!! Really liked the fried chicken and corn bread. And sides-- Okra and greens and grits were all tasty too! And the Kool-aid?! Nice touch. Went with my hubby and two of our friends and we all enjoyed everything we ordered. The portions are huge but the price was not :-)
No place better for fried chicken and candied yams!! Mac n cheese is heavenly and I won't order peach cobbler any where else period!! Fantastic staff, friendly and

Been here a few times and I've heard mix reviews, but it has been so yummy every time I've gone. The shrimp and grits, chicken and waffles, oxtails are all yummy! I'm picky about my soul food, but they are pretty good! Definitely, would return and recommend. The only downside is the restaurant gets very busy so make a reservation or plan ahead to wait!

Placed a 123.00 order under Door dash to deliver food from Fixins.  Door dash delivered the wrong order. I called Fixins and was told there was two orders with the same first name and sorry we gave you the wrong order. Told to call door dash for refund and call them back reorder and pick up the order. Fixins took no accountability for giving Door dash the wrong. Terrible customer service.

The food was great and loved the chicken and Mac n cheese.  The butter they have is soooo good!!! We also tried the deviled eggs which were different but good.  We just had to try them.  The service was great our waitress was attentive and nice.  I also tried the adult kool aid for the first time I didn't try it before because I didn't want to pay that much for kool aid but tonight I went for it and it was tasty.  It brought back memories drinking kool aid when I was young LoL!! We took our adult kids tonight who haven't been here before and they enjoyed the atmosphere and the food!! We also got to watch some basketball too! We will be back!!
Great service, very prompt and the food?  So good!  Chicken is incredibly moist m, collard greens (something I've never tried) and okra were both good.
