Overall a really chill vibe, great music, and pretty good food. Surprisingly, the oxtails were really good. I didn't care for the greens, as they had a sweeter taste. The fried fish and hush puppies were "meh," nothing to brag home about. The fried green tomatoes were good! The drinks were decent. 

The crowd is diversified and so is the staff. I'd definitely come back.

Let me tell you, the way this restaurant got me in a chokehold, the way i was so so down bad for this place. I would do the travel from SJ to Sac for this restaurant alone!

The service was so friendly, everyone made you felt so welcomed and at home, perfect for this comforting setting and comforting food.

The fried chicken was a perfect crisp on the outside with some amazing seasoning, and the inside was soft and tender, amazing flavor also.

Hubby got the gumbo with shrimp and was pleasantly suprised with the portion! He loved that the shrimp was so soft along with the stew itself was flavorful with a nice kick to each bite.

Not pictured but the ARTICHOKE DIP was top notch quality cheesy and i strangely would have this by itself. Thats how good their dip is.

Definitey a must try for that comforting soul food craving.
(please open in the bay area nothing comes close to this)
Absolutely delicious, very attentive, I ordered it as pickup and it was delicious and fresh. I will definitely be back!

Man, this food is good and the music is even better! This place hits the spot for perfectly fried chicken and yummy sides. It may sound odd, but their salads are also really good! Who would have thought Fritos make for a great salad topping!  Fixins is a vibe, don't sleep on it!

In the mood for some soul food, so we found ourselves here. We were seated right away. Staff friendly, but didn't seem like there were enough servers as we had to wait quite a bit to order and/or wait for water refills and the check. 

Pros - portions are great. The charred okra was yummy and that cornbread with that butter is an absolute must. 

Unfortunately, the downsides - the oxtail and collard greens were quite salty. I love oxtail but they were just too salty for me to eat and really enjoy. 

Also ordered the peach cobbler, served hot in a skillet with ice cream. Good, but nothing in particular stood out about it. Same with the deviled eggs.

I was very disappointed. Waiter he said my husband had to move his truck. Because it was in front of curby pick up.  He was walking to his truck. I walked in I was standing said he was not going to seat me until my husband moved his truck. I pointed the truck was not there. There was lots of vehicles parked they nether too. Waiter didn't say anything to them.

Excellent food  and service.  Tried almost all the sides, and the fried chicken. 

Berry butter for the biscuits was very light and flavorful.  

For the gravy covered smothered  fried chicken, would recommend getting the gravy on the side to preserve the breading crunch.

Looking forward to a return visit.
My Classic Sunday Evening Soul Food Meal. Fried chicken & waffle. Today, I ordered the Fish Sandwich to go with peach cobbler and ice cream.

Our family loves coming here. Love starting off with the catfish nuggets. One of my favorite dishes are the oxtails. Very good! Smothered pork chops are also good. Today I tried the shrimp
And grits. Pretty spicy. I think I'll just stick to the grits as a side dish because I love ordering it as a side dish when I come here. So creamy! The Mac and cheese is delicious. I don't drink alcohol too much but the adult Kool Aid is always good and hits me faster than a lot of other drinks without tasting too much alcohol. The servers are always really nice and have smiles on their faces. It's decorated nice and very clean.

What a great place for brunch!!  The atmosphere here is so lively and fun.  The servers were friendly and made you feel so welcome.  Just a side note - don't take the waiters word in the pricing of alcohol.  Make sure to check.  

For appetizers, we have the fried green tomatoes.  That dish was delicious.  The chipotle aioli dip was on point!  We also had the Caesar salad which was okay.  We needed some greens to go with the entrees.  The fried chicken was bomb.  The skin was crispy and the meat was juicy and tender.  I love the fried okra.  I love how it was fried naked.  Another great dish was the oxtail served with rice.  The oxtail was perfectly braised.  The meat was tender and easily fell off the bone.  

I would definitely come back here again for more soul food!

I'll be the first to say that I've had this place bookmarked for almost 2 years, and I will take full ownership as to why it has taken me this long to try. I wish so hard that I have tried Fixins Soul Kitchen sooner, this place is so darn popular for so many reasons! Listen, if you haven't tried FSK, then you are sincerely missing out some delicious southern cuisine!!! 

Today I was given the opportunity to try the Oxtail, which is slow braised beef oxtail smothered in a creamy, buttery, thicccccccc sauce. This meat practically melted off the bones and with each bite I couldn't hold back my moans and slow happy dance moves. My Mac n Cheese side is some of the best I've ever eaten. You can tell this bad boy is baked with lotsa cheese, and I ain't mad about it. Everything is just so fresh, has that homemade and made with so much LOVE feelin'. Kudos to the chefs here 'cuz know exactly what they're doing! The fried chicken thigh, is-a-MUST. Oh boyyyyyy! The crispy, crunchy skin is seasoned perfectly. Reminiscent of KFC's extra crispy recipe but 1 million times better with so much more juicy, dripping flavor. OMG. So good! My mouth is watering just thinking 'bout it, ha!

It's so simple: Great food and Good vibes all around. Iselle, our server was great with her bar assistant Luc. Thanks for providing great service you two ;) 

Listen here, the high star ratings and 1800+ reviews speak for themselves. Yes, yes the hype is as real as it gets. Selfishly, I don't want to brag about this place because that will just mean longer wait times for a table, but like the saying goes: "great things are worth waiting for."

This place is busy for a reason! This is one of Sacramentos best restaurants for soul food. Service is great and everyone enjoyed their food. It was definitely busy throughout the entire restaurant which makes sense since the food is amazing! I personally got the 2 piece fried chicken and I will get it every time I come! 

If you do not want to wait I'd recommend making a reservation as we were seated 2 mins after our reservation time, which is perfect!

I've been wanting to try this restaurant for a while. Impressed with the place as soon as I walked in. 
Incredibly attentive staff, especially Kim who my bartender. 
I ordered the fried chicken plate with coleslaw (stellar) and potato salad excellent too. My meal was spectacular. 
Local brews on tap   
Kim is an impressive mixologist and a wonderful hostess. 
I will back and may even put Fixins on my restaurant rotation!

I have tried have tried both the Sacramento and Los Angeles locations and they are both worth it. From the shrimp and grits to the fried chicken and Kool aid drinks. Just go, don't think about it anymore.

Food - we had oxtails, fried fish, mac & cheese, yams, cornbread and kool-aid drinks! 

We highly recommend and would expediently return on our next visit.

Pros:
- Food is excellent! I recommend the 3 piece fried chicken or the fried catfish. The Okra and Collard Greens w/ Turkey Necks are great Fixins (sides)! Also a very generous kids menu for $9 that my kids really enjoy
- Service is great. Servers and hostesses are on point and very friendly
- Great selection of drinks (both alcoholic and non-alcoholic)

Cons:
- Does not take reservations, so be prepared to wait unless you are ok with outdoor seating (watch out for flies!)
- Parking can be a bit tricky with street parking only
- Although worth it, it can be pricey especially since its in the Sacramento area

Overall, this place is a great staple of mine when i visit Sac and will definitely return again!

We were passing through and in the mood for some real food. We didn't get a reservation and waited just over the hour. The wait was worth it. I got chicken and waffles, they were great. I also got an order of Mac and cheese and blackened okra which was good too. My girl got fried chicken and the turkey neck collard greens. We ordered the hard boiled egg appetizer which was good but I'd pass on that next time because of price (understandably so though eggs are expensive) All in all good restaurant. Everyone was welcoming. Sweet tea just like grandma made it. Would definitely recommend. We had to get an ice chest to take leftovers home.

This place is Fire. Bartender Kim made some legit recommendations for Jake and me. The deviled eggs are a perfect starter. The light batter on the eggs was excellent. They really need to sell their hot sauce @ Nugget! I had to hold back from devouring my whole plate of chicken otherwise I would have gone into a food coma, but holy mackerel that Fried Chicken with the greens and yams was delicious! Those yams could be made into a dessert! I paired the meal with Lemonade and the spices popped.  

I can't wait to return to try the rest of the menu!
The food is always amazing here and the drinks are delicious! This place is always packed on the weekends so I recommend making a reservation. The oxtail plate is my go-to s every time!

This place did not disappoint!  The food and service was excellent and it had a great vibe with comfortable decor.  We tried a bit of everything, even the fried green tomatoes, mac and cheese, biscuits, and we were too full for dessert so we got some to go.  The peach cobbler was crazy good.  We will be back when in the area!

Best food, best service. I met some friends here for dinner and I was blown away. The prices are so reasonable as well. I had the fried chicken, mac n cheese, greens and a biscuits. We also shared banana pudding and peach cobbler. I make a very good mac n cheese but theirs is better. The food was amazing. The service was awesome and the price was down right cheap. I will be back. This was the best meal I have had in while.

No one leaves this place hungry. Patrons enjoy generous portions, tasty food, and excellent service.

Alizae was an excellent guide to the menu and was instrumental in helping us pick the right items to suit our picky palates.  

And then there was dessert. 

I think we need to make a return visit soon.
Had a reservation for 6:30 for five and didn't get seated until 6:50.  Food was great the other four had never been there and they loved the food.

Food was good and the restaurant was really cute. The service fell short for us. We were sat at the table closest to the hallway that leads to the kitchen and as soon as we were seated we could hear the man who ultimately ended up being our server calling his employees or coworkers stupid and that immediately made it uncomfortable. He then came to take our order and overall he was fine but whatever happened in the kitchen clearly agitated him and it was clear in his body language. Just made it slightly uncomfortable and awkward for us.

We also had to wait around to get an itemized receipt for purchase and was not thrilled about that either.

The food was bomb AF! Nyshia our server handled us with the best of care and was so sweet! Get the fried chicken plate (was hella juicy) and the oxtail meat was falling off the bone. Overall great experience!

The fried green tomatoes is what caught my eye, and prompted me to travel from Fairfield to Sacramento for brunch. They exceeded my expectations!! They were super crispy on the outside, and juicy and delicious on the inside. The sauce & greenery in the plate just added to the deliciousness of the tomatoes. I also enjoyed the fried chicken & waffles. The waffle was just the right size and thickness, along with the house made butter & warm syrup - truly amazing. The chicken was crispy like crazy, super moist , tender and delicious on the inside. The icing was our server who was super attentive, kind and not overbearing. She knew just when to step in and check in our comfort level. The ambiance & setting simply priceless!!!
I will return!

Eehhhhhhh it was ok. I mean Sac May be proud as it should be but I've had better. I got the fried deviled eggs and did not like them at all. I got the catfish meal with a side of Mac and cheese.  Mac and cheese was decent. 

I forgot to tell them to fry the fish hard so it had some stringy pieces in it. Could have used a touch more seasoning. 

I also had the side of cheese grits and ummmmmm no. Something with the texture was off with the grits. Didn't finished them. 

I don't like hush puppies or hot water corn bread so I didn't eat then balls on my plate.  Didn't order an alcohol drink bc the price was outrageous. My friends enjoyed their look aid.  

Like the atmosphere. Heavily pregnant waitresses was a gem. Will I be back, not on my own accord. If others want to stop in wouldn't mind but I would probably order a salad or something g simple.

Was in a party of 12 and we got seated fast and our food within 15 minutes of getting there. ELIZA WAS WONDERFULLL. We will be coming back just for her!! So attentive and caring. She made the experience that much better. ;)

You're going to need a bigger stomach, a to-go box or just agree to order a bit of everything and share because everything here was delicious. The Ox Tails and Gumbo are my personal favorites. Service is amazing. Very much a younger crowd,  be mindful that parking can be an issue, but you can always walk off some of your calories.

Great food but don't be in a hurry. Apathetic staff.
Got seated at 3pm and food was still not out at 3:45. (Ordered a burger and a fish sandwich so it wasn't complicated) . Place was about 1/4 full.
Waitress checked on our order 3 times but only when we flagged her down. 

We had to ask for it "to go" after 50 minutes or a refund. Waitress Aliea was rude when we asked for a refund and we needed to leave. 70 minutes later, we had food but couldn't keep waiting for a refund

Can you say Finger licking good? It's called Fixins soul Kitchen for a reason. Kevin Johnson and his wife Michelle founded Fixins to combine her love of southern cooking and a black cultural experience at your table.
 It's nestled in the heart of Oak Park in Sacramento on 3rd Ave. There is street parking if you're lucky. If not, there's parking right behind 3rd street. 
Fixins is serving up Grandma's soul food style with something for everyone. 
When you walk in, you immediately see an open spacious bar area. There's also a huge dining area both inside and outside. They also have a private or additional dining space in the back of the restaurant. 
Our server's name was Maia. We had a party of 6. I started with Sweet Tea. My friends all had the Kool-Aide. Flavors are Red (Cherry), Purple (Grape) & Blue. $5 each, Served in a 24oz mason jar. 
The Smorgasbord menu order included...Fried Chicken Salad- mixed greens, tomatoes, cheese, onions, & ranch dressing. 3 piece dark chicken with Potato Salad & Macaroni and Cheese, a biscuit with grape butter. Oxtails with rice and brown gravy with Mac and cheese. Fried Catfish with Mac and Cheese, Collard Greens and Hush Puppies. Catfish bites with Candid Yams. Hot chicken sandwich coated with hot chicken sauce,honey mustard, lettuce, onion and kool-aid pickles on the side.

Everything was delicious. Seasoned perfectly. Cooked to perfection. When you look around the room, everything is just taking story, going with the flow and enjoying the food along with the southern hospitality. 
Maia's interaction was funny, engaging and she connected with each one of us individually. She made the meal great as well. 
This was an excellent experience. The portion sizes are huge. You'll definitely want to take the extra's home. 
If you get a chance to visit Sacramento, add this foodie stop to your collection list.

Last minute lunch today,
Ordered the smothered pork chops, and it took way too long. Our server informed us that she wasn't happy with the way the food had looked or came out so she had them cook it again when she finally brought it to us, the pork chops were awful... They smelled bad, and they were super greasy $60 lunch down the drain

2 stars because of the hostess named SEVLIN. When we arrived, we were told it would be an hour and a half wait. Two hours later, SEVLIN told me it would be another 30min. I politely told her that they may consider telling people it would be 2 1/2 hours instead of 1 1/2. SEVLIN then proceeded to RUDELY explain what an estimated wait meant. I told her I know what an "estimated wait" is, she then told me "you don't know anything!"

Fixins Soul Kitchen being off by an hour is unacceptable. However to allow an employee to speak to paying customers like this is just a slap in the face. Also, to SEVLIN... not only do I know what an estimated wait is....I also know to spend my money where it appreciated. 

The ONLY thing that made our visit worthwhile was Chandra B. being an amazing server.
Absolutely love this place! It's our go-to spot whenever we're in Sacramento. The food is always amazing, the service is on point, and the vibe is just right!

Pretty good. Wait was pretty short to get in thankfully. Service was friendly and helpful with any questions that we had, water refills frequent. The decor of the place was awesome. Played to the food and the vibe.

We had ordered oxtails for ourselves, catfish nuggets to share, collard greens, charred okra, mac n cheese, and cornbread. Everything had good flavor and was cooked well. I'd say the oxtails were a bit heavy with a lot of fat. It did make for a really nice sauce for the rice tho. The ones I liked best tho were the catfish nuggets and the charred okra. The cornmeal in the breading really worked, and the fish was really flaky. The charred okra wasn't slimy and all. I was pleasantly surprised. 

I'd definitely stop by again if in the area to try some of their other offerings.

OMG! Where do I start, we get there and the power is out in the restaurant. Well that did not stop us we made reservations to come back later that day after some shopping. We're back and we ordered more food than we could eat. Started off with the fried devil eggs and fried green tomatoes. One word yummy. Next up was the fried fish with red beans and rice, greens and corn fritters. Also, had the fried chicken that is brined for 24 hours it was so juicy and seasoned to perfection with a side of mac and cheese and candied yams. Had to order the peach cobbler to go to eat at home. The service was excellent and the staff were fantastic. A great place for families, friends or a date night. I cannot wait to come back and try more food on the menu. Oh and don't forget to order a drink you won't be disappointed,

Lucky on a Thursday night to get street parking. close to the restaurant. Wanted to get a booth but the server just got seated and our server only had the mid tables.

The simple menu was only a page but the drink menu in the table was more extensive. Got Lemonade and an Old Fashioned for our drinks. Food orders were Deep Fried Bacon Deviled Eggs, 2 Wings & a Waffle and Three Pcs Chicken with  Candied Yams and Mac N Cheese. Our nice server Alizae was apologetic as our deviled eggs didn't come with the bacon but she made up for it by bringing a generous portion of chopped bacon.

Place wasn't too packed at 8PM. Ambience was great with lot's of wall decors to read and learn, music was piping with "classic" hip-hop dance tunes. Service was great. And this would've been probably the best soul food I've had this side of the country.

Keep it up Fixins!!!

I have come back a few times for fried chicken. Haven't revisited the oxtail, but did get a nice message saying that there may have just been issues with that batch. 

The fried chicken and biscuit are delicious. The mac and cheese and okra are pretty good. The service is awesome and I love the flavored butters. They also have a nice outdoor seating area that is dog friendly.

Our order: fried chicken (thigh + breast), Mac n cheese, biscuit and strawberry butter, okra, collard greens, candied yams, creamy grits, fried catfish, potato salad and adult kool aid. 

What a gem in Oak Park! Amazing soul food that transported me straight to the South. Undeniably the crowd favourite was the adult kool-aid (only comes in red) that is topped with kool-aid ice cubes - tasty, refreshing and shockingly strong. I really liked my fried green tomatoes, the crisp and tartness bade well with the cream spiced sauce. We were pleasantly surprised at the texture of the butter that came with the biscuits. It was like strawberry Haagen-Dazs! The Mac and Cheese maintained its texture and flavor even after it got cold. 

Chill atmosphere. Awesome selection of drinks. Indoor or outdoor, your choice. Our server Aimee was the friendliest and most fun, and her presence definitely elevated our night out. Fixins also sells punny t-shirts. Can't wait to come back for more!

Very delicious! The shrimp and grits were creamy and flavorful. The kid's meal was a great deal at $9 for 1 drumstick, a cup of Mac n Cheese, cookie, a drink. Service was attentive. A really great spot in Sacramento.

I love the food and I love the vibes. This place is a must go when passing through Sacramento. I have been here a handful of times from small to big groups. The place accommodate different party sizes. Although I have limited pictures to share I do want to share how tasty their drink menue is and appetizers. Do not skip out on the fried Devil Eggs & Fried Green Tomatoes. The staff is kind and attentive. With many selections I recommend getting different items to share. I have to admit that I have not tried any dessert due to being stuffed by the end of my meal. The cornbread muffin goes crazy with their butter! Parking in the area is free and not hard to find.

I came to this place on a Saturday night, they were packed with a hour and a half wait. We decided to take the food to go. We ordered Oxtails with rice, the gravy was pretty thick and the oxtails weren't trimmed. The chicken and waffles were interesting, the chicken was yummy but the waffle was spicy. She had to pick out pieces of peppers out of the waffle. The Mac and cheese was not flavorful but at least it wasn't dry, it was manageable. Now the shrimp and grits was amazing and so creamy and delicious. Unfortunately, the 4 of us all has some type of tummy trouble after consuming our meals. I can't pinpoint one thing as we all ate different items but all had tummy trouble. The staff were friendly and the restaurant looks really cute but busy. There was plenty of street parking.

This was our first experience at Fixins. I got a to-go order for my family this past Easter Sunday. The servers were kind and helpful. The food was so good it bordered on a religious experience. We will be back again!

Excellent food and drinks! The waitress was nice and patient with us. We did not have to wait a long time to get our food. Everyone at my table was very impressed. My first time having fried green tomatoes and they were awesome. Looking forward to my next visit when I'm in town.

I would like to give this place 5 stars, but I think their service in terms of seating people could be better. We were given an estimated time of 20-30 minutes, but weren't seated until 40 minutes later. Our group was 1st in line to be seated on the waitlist, but I saw at least two other groups get seated before us. Granted, they were smaller groups and didn't require a booth, but they had a group of two sitting at a booth when there were a bunch of other free tables available. 

Other than that, the food was great in terms of flavor and portions. The gravy with the smothered chicken was tasty. The oxtail was good, only wished there was more rice. Our server was great and the food came out pretty fast, so the night ended on a good note.
Could've been better. The mac & cheese was OK. The Greens were delicious. Chicken should be cooked a little longer. Ambience was nice. Service was good but rushed.

Prior to attending the nearby theater, we decided to stop in for an early dinner at Fixins. This was our first time to this establishment and we were very impressed with the service, food, location, and atmosphere! We were greeted by "Cat" as soon as we walked in the door. She also was our waitress and took care of us most professionally! "Cat" was amazing and would MOST definitely love to enjoy another experience like this the next time we are in the neighborhood! Thank you and hope to be back again!

Cool, happening, spacious, comfy spot. We scored a booth on a Saturday evening. Our server was super friendly and helpful, Alizae. Appreciate her genuine warmth and smiles, she made our first time here great! The manager even swung by to check on us. Everyone from the host and other staff members were friendly. The music was bumpin, it was lively and fun.

The food was good, I wasn't blown away by much. I love deviled eggs and was excited to try the fried deviled eggs, it was tasty and interesting, just ok though. The fried green tomatoes were ok as well. I would've liked more grits in my shrimp n grits that were drowning in the gravy/sauce. The gravy/sauce was good. Oxtail was tender and tasty, thought there should be more of it though. Fried chicken was super, duper moist but not very crispy. Heard the gumbo was good, although I didn't taste it, I should've. Also, I love mac n cheese but was a little disppointed. No ooey, gooey cheese but dried, scattered cheese. I did love their sweet butter on my biscuit! Yummy!! It was pinkish so my guess is a starwberry butter. 

We parked 3 blocks away, no meters, free street parking which is always a plus.

It was a grown up lunch date! We had an amazing lunch! The entire experience was excellent! Home cooked meals for sure! If you don't cook or if you don't feel like cooking, this is the spot either way! The food, the service, the love for their community shows upon entering!! And "Aunt Pearl" was there making sure we felt right at home! NBA All Star and previous Sacramento mayor, Kevin Johnson, is the owner and Aunt Pearl's nephew!! She let us know if we needed anything to flag someone down. We didn't have to, our server checked on us regularly!! Give this local spot a visit. It is in Oak Park which has recently been gentrified to a really cute, comfortable, business and family oriented area! Graduations were being celebrated, elderly couples were enjoying themselves, company workers were sitting at the bar eating and lots of people having a good time over a good meal! We truly enjoyed ourselves, the genuine hospitality and of course the food was top notch! I will definitely return! I will make room for my own dessert next time!

If you love soul food, go visit this restaurant.

For drinks we got sweet tea, and boy was the jar ever big. We could have probably just shared one as refills are free.

For food, I ordered the smothered chicken and the bf ordered the fried catfish.

I got dark meat for my smothered chicken, but I found that there wasn't a lot of meat to it so I would suggest getting white meat. The gravy was delicious and had a nice little kick to it.

My bf thought the fried catfish was good as well and it came with corn fritters.

The two entrées comes with two fixins so we both got different ones so we can try more. We got Mac and cheese, collard greens with turkey necks, grits, and candied yams.

My favourite fixins was the mac and cheese and collard greens. If you like sweet things than I would go for the candied yams which was sweet and cinnamony.

My bf's cousin got the chicken and waffles which is served with syrup and blueberry whipped butter.
Nyshia was the best. She kept us entertained and she took care us. Just because of her service we would most definitely come back. Thank you Nyshia girrrl.

‍‍‍
Just go here...literally tried to eat everything on my plate in one sitting, (I failed) LOL. SOOO GOOD! Got the adult kool-aide, which tbh was very sweet. It comes with ice cubes that are made from kool-aide as well...had to add water to it because of how sweet it was. Couldn't really taste the alcohol, but maybe it was hidden and all of the sugar lol. 

The oxtails were so delicious! They fell right off the bone and melted immediately in your mouth. Unfortunately, they used a minute rice which I am really not a fan of. But they made up for it with the mac & cheese! So cheesy, so creamy, so bomb! And let's get started on the service...my girl Nataya was super busy but equally attentive, gave great recommendations, and was so nice.  

I do have to note that the runner dropped my brothers food (cornbread balls) when delivering it and didn't replace it but other than that no other complaints! Still awesome food and a great experience overall!

I visited Fixins Soul Kitchen recently for the very first time! 

First off, I love their location. It's surrounded by other cute restaurants and shops which makes it more of a destination than a place to grab a quick bite. I also loved their decor. They have a beautiful outdoor patio and a large indoor seating area, complete with a bar, booths and various different table sizes fit for everyone from couples to large groups. 

Our waiter was extremely nice. We got to try all the coo-lade flavors they had on tap as well as their sweet tea, all of which were very sweet and refreshing. For our meals, we got the fried chicken with the charred okra and mac and cheese side, the spicy chicken sandwich and the chicken and waffles. 

The fry on the chicken was superb! It was crispy and well seasoned and the chicken underneath was tender. The mac and cheese was creamy and the waffle was light and fluffy. One of my favorite parts of the meal surprisingly though was the okra. So good! 

I highly recommend, for their welcoming vibe, great service and amazing food!

Best soul food! 
My dad and I drove up from Monterey bay to Sacramento and had a craving for fried catfish and found this place, now we want to go back asap. 
Delicious food, we both had catfish, red beans and rice, Mac and cheese, black eyed peas and cornbread/biscuits. 
Absolutely nothing disappointed about our food, service and atmosphere. Well worth the drive to go again, their food made a difficult week for us much more relaxing and enjoyable.
Damn good food it's taste soooo good specially after a great Sunday and you heard the word now it's time to nourish your body with the food for your soul

Let me start with saying Eliza is the best server out there . She helped us out and was quick and easy. Perfect service. To add, The food is always amazing Smothered Chicken on top. Plus the music and the weather was perfect. Great spot

This place was hands down one of my new favorites owned by Kevin Johnson the mayor himself and ex sac kings player. Do not be alarmed by the location in oak park this is on Stockton blvd very nice place great out door patio with propane heaters great decor and staff was super friendly and polite. I live in Folsom and would drive down to go here anytime highly recommend this place for some good old southern style kitchen it's to die for

The food was amazing! Neisha our waitress had the Best personality, recommendations and was so attentive!!! The Fried fish appetizer, chicken and waffles, smothered chicken, okra and greens  were so savory and the grape jelly butter was a sweet treat!! Definitely returning the atmosphere was fun and the manager even took a photo for us!!

Ask for amieee she was definitely a great waitress/server and was very accommodating when it came to my drink ordered it came out very bitter, and I'm more of a sweet fruity type of girl when it comes down to drinking so when I complain and told her that I didn't really like it, she actually went back to the bar and found a little twist to add to it just to make it better so I applied to her for that because I definitely wasn't gonna drink it when it comes down to the food and stuff my wife can be very picky especially when it comes down to things being fatty or slimy and she made sure that my wife's fish was double fried to where we didn't have to worry about that and as well as maintain a smile on our faces during the whole experience of ordering I highly appreciate it and I'll definitely be back We gotta try the whole menu now. Shrimp and grits was my order and it was immaculate. I damn there wanted to order a plate to go. That's why I had someone I went home. and overall just the atmosphere of the whole entire restaurant was life the music, the people, and even the customers they came in were very, very friendly.

Great food!! Great customer service!! 
I ordered my Thanksgiving sides from Fixings after reading the Yelp reviews. 
I'm from the south so I was a little skeptical!!!
I was pleasantly surprised to find down home southern type food on the west coast!!

They were pleasant on the phone as well as in person!! I will be coming to Fixins whenever I'm in the SAC area. 
The mac n cheese is arguably the best I've ever had!!

Vibes 10/10, service 10/10, & food 10/10! I've always wanted to try and so glad I did. Everything was so good!! Got a hot chicken sando and it wasn't as spicy as I would like but the hot sauce they have on the table was so freaking good! The lemonade and sweet tea was so good I would go back just to order that. The waitress was very attentive and checked in multiple times and she and other staff would clear our table so we would have room on the table. Would highly recommend going!

I am very glad that I had the opportunity to dine at Fixin's. So far, this establishment serves the best soul food in the Sacramento area. 

I really liked that my twins were provided with coloring paper and crayons to keep them busy while we wait for our food and drinks. 

The ox tails were delicious. The meat was really tender. I enjoyed the sweet garlic brown gravy. The gravy tasted more savory than sweet, which is what I prefer. 

The chicken and waffles were indeed crispy, tender, and juicy. 

The red sangria was delicious and refreshing. 

I have no complaints from this dining experience. I am already looking forward to my next visit!

So the fried green tomatoes were OK the red beans and rice. I felt like the beans should've been cooked just slightly a little bit longer. There was no juice in them. I'm not impressed but it's all good.

We live in another state. Found Fixins on yelp. So glad we did. When we walked in, we were greeted by a very pleasant gentleman. He was very engaging which, instantly made us comfortable. He had excellent suggestions regarding the menu. Our server was also top notch. Took excellent care of us. Now... the food!! OH MY DAMN!! Chicken was awesome, grits are life changing, sweet potatoes were perfect, appetizer fried egg were delicious!! Give this place a try!! You won't be disappointed

We were just there, tonight. First time. We had a lovely time! The vibe was great, the food was scrumptious, people were great. Can't wait to go back. We had deep fried deviled eggs, shrimp and grits plus fried chicken. Looking forward to next time.

The service and the food were excellent. Everything we tried was so delicious and you can tell that a lot of soul was put into the dishes and also the drinks. From the time we checked in to the time we left, the service was amazing! Highly recommend!

Unfortunately, Fixins has changed. I believe because they've become so popular they started rushing the food. The last few times I've gone the chicken has tasted old, potatoes were stale and not seasoned, service was not good, recipes have changed. It's sad because Fixins is a one-of-a-kind place for the Black Community but they are starting to slack. It might help if they bought a bigger space and hired more staff. Don't rush the food; if the food is good and FRESH people will be willing to wait and you'll still get the business. 

Not worth the prices anymore. Also noticed the last visit that celebrities are starting to visit this place...ok, that's fine but make the food worth it please. If you guys can open two other locations then buy a bigger building and don't give customers old, stale, freezer-burnt food please. Won't be back for a while.

This place is really DELISH!  Ambiance is lively and fun, service was really good.  Food comes out quick!  I ordered the fried chicken and red beans.  The red beans are legit and the chicken was perfectly cooked.  The biscuit and that butter....scrumptious!  I wouldn't hesitate to recommend a visit to folks and I'll certainly be back!

We had reservations for a large family reunion.  Despite the number, the manager and wait staff was so efficient and very professional during our entire meal.  Now the food - I heard no complaints!   When I said everything was good  - EVERYTHING was good!
Our server was attentive and the food matched the menu perfectly. I left feeling satisfied.
Food is good. You wanna do good, you try and do good, basically you just gotta do good. I'll make like a boat and sail away now, oh lucky charms

Fun Sunday brunch and dinner place while visiting downtown Sacramento. There wasn't a wait to be seated around 2pm and we got all our food hot and fast after we ordered. 

Started with a plate of catfish bites that came with two sauces, tartar and a seasoned mayo. The Peach Sangrias were a plus (would've ordered a second glass if we had to drive back home). A blue koolaid would make you like a kid again. There's a bar and a few tv's. The Sunday church soul music was a vibe.

Nice atmosphere. It's a very popular restaurant which is a good thing of course, so I decided to wait in line.  The staff was great.  Everything on the menu looked delicious to me. I ended up choosing fried chicken, which was delicious. Also, I had the greens which I normally love. Oh my goodness way too salty so I was disappointed since  I was unable to eat them due to having high blood pressure.  I would say all in all if it wasn't for the food being too salty, I would come back.
I think I'll come back during a weekday and try it again it's worth it! Have a great day, everyone.

Had an Amazing experience at this spot. Love what KJ is doing here. I will be back to try the other items on the menu. I had the smothered chicken. My family had the fried chicken and fried catfish. We were not disappointed. Topped it with Blue kool-aid and peach cobbler for desert!
So good. If you're craving soul food.. this is your place. I broke a 48 hour fast with a little fixins and almost cried haha.

The service was literally the best I've had since going here to eat for many years. Their hosts Alicea, the servers isis & mick were kind, attentive and have amazing customer service! I wish they were there every time I go :) Honestly with service like the one they provided to me twice in a row last week (went for a work meeting then took my kid out for dinner) I will continue to enjoy their delicious food and amazing service. The employees I mentioned didn't only do their job but went above and beyond to make sure my request were satisfied. Thank you for the great service.

1st time and Finally getting here, Very crowded But for good reasons. Although the wait was long but after ordering , the food was fast  And Good plus the crowd inside was good !! We Enjoyed Everything that was ordered.  Which means we'll be back to order More & More ... Thank You  it was a Good Birthday Snack

If you are looking for fixin of fried chicken! Definitely check out Fixins Soul Kitchen. 

Their chicken, sides and ice tea are all to die for. 

The ambiance and staff are all so generous and welcoming. It is almost like walking in a southern home. There is a sense of family and love of good food. 

I highly recommend order their standard plate of chicken which includes sides. All of their sides are amazing. Delicious. 

Corn bread was also amazing. 

Overall, rare find, and definitely recommend trying it. 

Best,
Stevie

Rolled around lunch time and got seated right away outside. Drinks were a great start to our meal. The catfish nuggets were sooooo good. The juiciest fish fried to perfection and NOT greasy!! I'd come back for this. We also had mac n' cheese, charred okra, greens, oxtail so juicy and falling off the bones, and fried chicken & waffle. It came with blueberry ish flavored butter - YUM! Service - awesome, food - amazing, ambiance - homey and chill. Will be coming back one day!

The food was delicious.  The service was good.  It was a bit too crowded for me in the inside but I enjoyed the ambiance of the outside dining area.   They had big cooling fans on the patio too because it was a sweltering.  . I had kool-aid which I haven't had in years.  This was a childhood memory that I was able to share with my son.  We had fried deviled eggs which was a first and was really tasty.   He had chicken and waffles. I had the smothered pork chops.  We will definitely go back soon.

Hubby and I came for our anniversary brunch.

We were greated at the door and seated immediately. We sat inside. Alejandra was our waitor and provided us exceptional service. We got our drinks. I ordered a Kool as a Cucumber without the alcohol and two waters for us both 

 I really wanted french toast but since it wasn't on the menu, I ordered the pancake breakfast. Hubby ordered the blue plate special. 

While waiting for our food, we enjoyed the gospel music playing, they had sports on tv as well. One of our friends popped up and surprised us. 

We didn't wait too long for the food. It came and was nice and hot. The pancakes were fluffy, the grits were perfectly creamy and the scrambled eggs were good. Hubby loved the bacon. 

I'm glad we came early a little after opening time. It started getting crowded by time we were leaving.

My friend took me there for the first day first time oh my God, some of the best Soulfood kitchen I've ever seen the plates were beautifully made the food, the shrimp and grits the oxtail. I had both of them the chicken oh my God if you want some good food Good chicken go there. Don't miss out. This place is booming. I just loved it and I don't usually give comments like this, but this this restaurant deserves it.

Two visits to Fixins and we love it! Both times did not disappoint. Let's start with the deep fried devil eggs. Pretty tasty and a great sharing appetizer, the fried chicken is always so well fried and seasoned. Collard greens are a must and the Mac and cheese is the perfect mix of cheese and smooth. You cannot go wrong here, amazing portions, southern vibes and an overall good time! We will be back for sure!

A Thursday dinner, group of six total, arrived after HH ended already. Only my second visit to Fixins, first time was for brunch! 

Parking situation still the same, try 35th Street. 15-minute wait, patio outdoor seating before sundown.

Note that the takeout menu posted on their website is not as extensive as the dine-in menu. We all tried a bunch of things from the menu -- lil' bits including the deep fried deviled eggs, fried green tomatoes, pimento cheese dip, artichoke dip -- every appetizer except the catfish nuggets! Grub items ordered were the 2-piece fried chicken with mac and cheese and candied yams, 2-piece chicken & waffle, oxtails, chicken tenders, and two orders of the hot chicken sandwich, and sides of buttermilk biscuits. Several people also got the Kool-Aid (red flava) which came in a quite large mason jar. For dessert, two of us shared one order of the peach cobbler. 

I liked the artichoke dip more than the pimento cheese, but they were both good starters. I got the same thing I have had before, their signature 2-piece (dark) chicken & waffle, which came with the grape jelly butter, powdered sugar and syrup. The fried chicken was great, the waffle thin and soft but that grape jelly butter is a winner. I did seriously contemplate the oxtails, shrimp and grits, or the 2-piece chicken with a side of charred okra and cheesy grits though. The smothered porkchop, even the gumbo sound wonderful too. 

I will say the hot chicken sandwich was a monster just by the looks of it, and messy to eat, a LOT going on in between those buns. The red-colored Kool-Aid pickle (Koolickle? Pickools?) was strange and something new, I tasted a few bites and I personally didn't mind it but it may not be to everyone's liking. I could just be weird and perhaps in a previous lifetime I was from Mississippi.

The peach cobbler is made in-house from scratch and served with vanilla ice cream, supposedly KJ's grandma's recipe. This was delicious and plenty for two people to share. 

Server Alizae was great, able to split the checks, otherwise we used Venmo. Great experience. Come if you're craving soul food, Southern style with friendly atmosphere, and if you want to leave "satisfied, full, and looking forward to your next visit!"

Received a recommendation to try this Southern comfort food spot. We had a large take out order and everything was hot and packaged correctly- plus one for that! Too many dishes to mention so I'll just hit the highlights: fried chicken bucket- awesome; hot chicken sandwiches- very good; oxtails and collards- really good. Can't wait to dine in next time!

Holy moly Fixins Soul Kitchen is simply amazing! The food and the atmosphere are top notch. This is a MUST VISIT restaurant if you are in the Sacramento area.

Fixins Soul Kitchen serves up elevated southern cuisine. If you love fried chicken, come here and get their version of it with your choice of 2 sides. The chicken is crispy on the outside and juicy on the in. No matter which chicken dish you try, you'll enjoy that same quality.

I had the hot chicken sandwich on my visit and it was absolutely massive. This thing will fill you up real good. It's the largest chicken sandwich that I've ever had in my life. The chicken itself is huge and it is accompanied by coleslaw, a kool aid pickle, and their unique sauces.

Speaking of Kool Aid, they have all the flavors on the menu! You can get red, purple, green, blue, or pink flavor! I got red and I was in heaven. Delicious!

I went with a large group of friends and everybody was raving over what they had - be it the fall off the bone oxtail to the fried devil eggs. There wasn't anything that was a miss.

If you're in Sacramento and haven't been to Fixins, you really need to go. It is totally worth it!

Man listen!! 

This place was on point , we ordered fried chicken the cat fish black eyed peas, greens yams , mac & cheese two drinks and two desserts to go. I'm not sure why people left bad reviews but they are tripping. The food came out fast and the price wasn't expensive at all. I even kicked my shoes on the way home and ya boi was the driver. Stuff!!!

I'm definitely running it back.

I had a long previous day of drinking that ultimately left me hungover and with no mood for an appetite. I was pressured into brunch against my will... I was not aware of where we were going or what we were eatting, but after arriving and experiencing the vibe amd aroma, my mind was changed.

I reviewed the menu and it was straight forward. The items were named and described as how they are made. You knew what you are ordering without doubt of unsure taste. There were some items that were not on the menu, but the server made us well aware of them. We ordered the Devil Eggs as a starter while waiting for our meal, and was not disappointed. The eggs were battered and fried to a crunch crisp. The fried egg plated on what I believe was a well seasoned potato salad complimented the taste.

The main course for me was the fried catfish. Although the batter was a bit too salty for me, the fish was well made. It was well battered and fried whereas the fish was still juicy and soft, not overcooked. The mac n cheese could be more cheesy and melty, but the flavor is still amazing. The fries are made well. It almost taste like Popeyes for some reason, but no complaints there. The hushpuppies tasted doughy with a slight aroma of vanilla extract(?). I could not taste much, but neither am I a fan of the piece. My judgement would be biased and therefore my review stands no ground against it.

Overall, the portion was big. I barely finished the plate, but whatever I was able to devour satisfied my non-existing hunger at the moment and nearly put me to sleep. Would definitely come back!
Love the food and drinks. Their pork chops are amazing. Definitely would recommend and will go back.

I've never been to this part of sacramento before. But my friends love this place. Wanted to give kj spot a shot. Got the fried fish sandwich, adult Kool aid. Everyone else got the chicken and gravy. Everything was really tasty and the portion is fair. Our server was really nice and patient with us. We were waiting on our 4th person but she kept checking in on us. I wanna come back and try other things on the menu so I will Def be back. Can't give it 5 stars due to parking situation. It's hard to find one.

Vibe is excellent! Sunday morning at 10 am there was a line in the front door. Only waited 5 minutes. Once seated I ordered a cup of coffee. It was fantastic! Ox tails were perfectly seasoned! Service was good.

This is a post Covid review.

Located off 3rd Ave in Sacramento is this well known soul food spot that raises everyone's spirit.

I visited this spot again on a random Monday afternoon. I was shocked that even on a random Summer Monday that this place was packed... 

Service: This spot is still second to none. Service with a beautiful smile. We were served quickly and our server was very attentive.

Ambiance: Southern with a Sacramento California twist. The walls was filled with art work that blended Southern ideas with the modern world.

Drinks: One of the reasons why I came by was their home style made Swee Tea and Koolaids. Their sweet tea was insanely good. Don't ask what the ratio of sugar is to tea. Just drink and be happy that they got it right.

Food: Soul food

During this visit I decided to try their smother pork chops. For those who never tried Southern Pork Chops before, I would highly recommend trying Fixins Pork Chops. Pork chops were moist and juicy with a nice crust, smothered with a savory brown gravy. 

If you are ever around the area, this is definitely the spot to visit.

This was our first time here, and we're glad we chose to come. It was worth the drive from Oakland. The customer service was great, and the music was enjoyable. They are kid-friendly 
food came out quickly. I had the sidecar drink with Dusse added, and the food was delicious. The oxtails were tender and fell right off the bone, the greens were flavorful, the yams were sweet, and the fried chicken was crunchy. The mac and cheese was good, though it could use a little more salt and pepper. The Caesar salad was tasty, and the croutons were really good. The deviled eggs were delicious, too. The food was so enjoyable that my child started dancing after eating and said the restaurant deserves 100 stars! We will definitely come back again.
The staff is friendly and the restaurant is comfy and clean. All the foods I've tried here are good- chicken sandwich, fried green tomatoes, and a salad. The roasted okra is soooo good.
Every last dish was amazing. So so so good! Make reservations if you can, but whatever you do, don't skip this place!

Absolutely not worth the prices at alllll. I went here today with my mother hoping to get a good soul food meal seeing as we're from the south and used to my grandmothers cooking. Today my mom got the fries catfish plate($27) and i got the smothered pork chops($21). Fried catfish plate only comes with one piece of fish my mother said soon as she bit into nothing but batter was all that she tasted and only a small thin layer of fish was actually there the greens are bitter and the mac and cheese has no flavor the pork chops were all right but a bit dry.

Love the ox tails at this restaurant.  The fried chicken ain't bad either.  The selection of side dishes are epic for Southern cooking: black eye peas, red beans and rice, collard greens, candied yams. . .

So glad we finally made it here, and really glad we had a reservation!!  There was a crazy long line when we were leaving. Great southern classics. We especially love the collard greens and Mac and cheese. Fried chicken, smothered pork chops, okra and biscuit were very good. Thought the yams were a little too sweet. The red velvet cake was good and big enough for a table!!
The hype is real. Probably the best soul foods I've ever had in California. Highly recommend you eat as much as you can!

Amazing!  Catfish and collard greens and okra. Yum!!!
Oxtail and fried chicken. Oh my!  
Drinks were wonderful and packed a punch. 
So happy to find  such an awesome soul   
 food Restauant close to me. 
Service was friendly, fast and thorough. 
Highly recommend.

So, ok, we went to Fixins today. It was part of the family that could make it. I mean, it was 105 degrees, so some folks can be excused!! Haha. 

But after all 20 of us had made it, along with 3 children, we started with the drink orders. I swear, each time I got up for something quick, when I came back, 'it's was waiting for me. Good deal!!!

Then we started talking, and reminiscing as family does. My Uncle was to one with his lovely wife. My mother was on the other side of me. And of course, we sat outside, so everyone else had shade, but me. They were eventually moved to fan, and wow. It cooled off a lot. 

So we ordered the food, waited a few minutes, I got up AGAIN, and when I returned, there was my food. Good LORT!!

So alright, I'll jump to the end of this vociferous complaint. 

Kevin wasn't there! What!! I can't believe it.

Because the food was GREAT!! Everyone had plates the size of Nebraska, I had the pancake breakfast, there were chicken salads, fish. Everything. And, everyone enjoyed it. 

But, the Real Johnson (Kevin) wasn't there. This was my first time going, but I will be back. If for no other reason than to see if Mr Johnson will be there. 

And while I'm waiting, sure, I'll get a few things. They are super reasonable as well. 

Bottom line, you may not see Kevin in the place, but you will get good food at a wonderful price. 

A

This was our first time eating at Fixins. I had the two piece fried chicken and waffles. The chicken was very moist, even the white meat. The flavor was outstanding. The waffle was good, a perfect combo w the chicken. The service was outstanding, our waitress addressed our questions and prompt w our needs. The food came out pretty fast too. Fixins reminds me of a sports bar, I say that in a good way. The bar is in the middle, tables nearby, lively atmosphere, down to earth.

I wish I could give them more than 5 stars.

It's a great experience. The ambiance is very welcoming and charming. Amazing drink selection, food and service. Staff are extremely knowledgeable and make great recommendations. I can't believe how at home I felt here! Auntie Pearl is making her rounds making sure everyone is having the best experience while dining here. The atmosphere is so chill but very classy at the same time? It's great how they were able to combine the two. Do yourself a favor and visit Fixins. The service is unmatched you can tell the staff truly cares about your experience when you come here. They take your customer experience very seriously and it shows!!! 

I can't wait to have my birthday dinner here! Haha I'm already counting down the days until I am back and get to see Auntie Pearl again!
Great food and service, nice portions! Happy to see they also have a location in So Cal as well!!

Went here for fathers day, very glad we had a reservation! The place was poppin.
Their okra is a MUST order. The red beans and rice were aight.
We tried the fried chicken and fried catfish, both were delicious.

Their cocktails are diverse, yummy, and strong!

This review is for the food, which was tasty. I'd also note that my prior review garnered an outreach from Fixins owner, who took responsibility, apologized, and noted that this is not their practice or policy; it was a ball getting dropped. Own the mistake - I appreciate that! And a very friendly outreach that sought a solution. Also appreciated.

The good news - the food was on point! Tasty fried chicken, REALLY good collards with turkey, good red beans and rice (and I'm a frequent NOLA visitor!) and one of the better soul food mac and cheese in the area. The biscuits were a little dry for my taste, but my wife dug them, so what do I know.

The personal touch / outreach really says a lot. You'd be shocked at the number of "you don't know what you're talking about!" replies one gets when they make a less than glowing note on a business, so it's nice to see a place that cares, responds, and takes responsibility. I'll be visiting Malik and crew again in the future for more tasty eats. Probably for dine-in. :-)
Food was good. No complaints. Loved the ambiance, went on a Sunday. Gospel music played as the restaurant was full, food and drinks flowed. It was a lively place. Cat fish nuggets a must try!
I really like the food here, while it is commercialized "soul" food, the sandwiches are great and I love that they have kool aid. Been here and few times and it's always good

Our server was amazing!! She was professional attentive and fun! She brought out extra plates without us asking and we were a VERY large party! The mac and cheese is very good and the fish was delicious per my boyfriend. The waffles are thin but the chicken is crispy and good. The yams were ok for my taste. Not too fond of the collard greens, bummer we make them and love them. THE GRITS though, those were the BEST! The koolaid was very good too. I have not had it since I was a little girl! Wow, what a wonderful place! The bright lights and joyous envioronment added to the festivities and fun! Oh, the biscuits were very good too! The different butters were fun and light in flavor but some in our party REALLY liked the butters! :) I highly reccomend this restaurant!

I've been here in the past and enjoyed the food and service. Tonight my family and I went and attempted to have dinner here. We did not have reservations. We walk in and they let us know 30-40 mins wait and ask me if I'm sure I'd like to wait. I understand it busy and 30-40 minutes isn't too bad. So take my number. This is at 6:22 pm. We decided to wait in the car because it was a little cold outside. We wait, wait, and wait. It was already 7:00pm and still waiting to get a table. Still waiting on a text message. I get it we have a party of 4. I know it's a wait. Now my kids are super hungry so we start to look for other options. we look up Creole Soul and I decided to call them. They said they would make room for us so we head over less that 10 mins wait drive. We were treated warmly with a smile. Their food was good. But guess what Fixins still hadn't text us for our table now over a hour and NOTHING from Fixins. Guess what it's now 8:19 and almost two hours and NO ONE has even got our table ready at FIXINS. I will not be back. Because if they did not have room to accommodate us they plain and simple could have said so. It's wild that almost 2 hours later I am still waiting on that text as I ride home from a great dinner a CREOLE SOUL. They will definitely be my next stop when I want some soul food. Fixins dropped the ball but I think it was for the better because we had a AMAZING service and great food else where. I guess I'll be waiting in that table forever.

FIXINS SOUL KITCHEN
The spicy chicken sandwich was amazing
So was the cheese n Mac
The fried chicken and biscuit
Salad was good but the ranch dressing was heavy on the dill.
sooo good soo happy sooo delish. parking was nice. our service was good. i loved the music and the manager was nice as well.

Special Thanks to Fixins Soul Kitchen Kris Johnson and the Fixin's Staff for the good food and service for my brother's repass. A buffet was set up on the patio. Food was ready for serving when guests arrived. Our menu: Appetizers: Fried Green Tomatoes & Catfish Nuggets, Entrees: Fried Chicken & Gumbo, we added Shrimp, Sides: Greens, Yams, Mac & Cheese, Green Salad with balsamic dressing and cornbread muffins. Drinks: water-lemonade-sweet tea. It was Food for our Soul and all guests enjoyed the food. Many guests said it was their first time at Fixins and will definitely come back. Sounds like Fixins is fixing to get busier. Thank you Fixins, for the personal touch you gave to all guests, see you soon.

Fried Chicken & Waffle 7/10
Smothered Oxtail 8/10
Fried Catfish 10/10 would order again! 
Candied Yam 8/10
Collard Greens 8/10
Hush puppies 6/10- dry
Cornbread 6/10- dry

Service 10/10 Friendly & Prompt. Great Staff

Ambiance 10/10 Very clean, open and inviting

First time here and the food was off the chain.  Chicken and waffles, coleslaw, and PURPLE koolaid!  Koolaid...just like I used to make it when I was a kid!!  The service was great, and the "KJ" was in the house!  The atmosphere was great; good music, good food...good vibe!!  I'll be back...real soon!  Get them oxtails ready!!

I had no idea this place was in a cute little shopping area. I have not had Southern food, so I was excited to try it. 

I had the Smothered pork chops, the brown gravy was very flavorful, the pork chops were nice and crunchy, and you got to pick two sides to accompany the food; I had mac and cheese and potato salad. 

Everything was perfect the service was excellent, I will be back to try more.

Food was sooo good and came out hot and fast!!! 
Service was quick and efficient and we were even greeted by a manger of some sort that stopped by to welcome us in and make sure we have a good time. In which we did with the great music selection and vibes. 10/10 would recommend and come back. Need to try and bring friends!!

We ordered this to go and brought it home tonight. SO SMART... and DELICIOUS! 
I'm so glad we did this. One - for leftovers and Two because it's fixin's!!! 
This was soooooo good as it always is. 


Make sure you check your order to go before because it was busy and workers are human and can only do so much! :) 

We ordered 
fried chicken 
Corn bread 
Red beans and rice (amazing) 
Okra side
So much food and flavor. The potato salad, fried chicken, oxtails, red beans & rice were my favorite

Food is legit.  Classic southern south food, prepared well and excellent technique on the fried chicken. The Mac and cheese was the best I've had and collards were prepared with a good amount of acid and smokiness, exactly how I grew up eating them. The gumbo had great depth of flavor but not enough shrimp to justify the extra cost. Oxtail is a must. I loved the nostalgia of kool aid as an option for drinks. Sweet tea wasn't overpoweringly sweet. The vibe is great and servers are always super sweet and responsive. This place gets busy so be sure to make a reservation online and expect to wait another 15min to be seated and another 15min for service. That being said, we will come back again and again. The food, kindness of the staff and the location are great.

A hometown gem! Their food is consistently amazing every time I visit. The service is outstanding, and everyone is incredibly friendly. The ambiance is perfect and feels so homey. I'll always recommend Fixin's to anyone visiting Sacramento!

Great ambiance and food. If you are looking to get your soul food fix while chilling at a great spot, this is your place. I love the collard greens!! The fried chicken can be hit or miss. A little too salty or doughy, but when they get it right it's delicious .
Been to this restaurant 3 times already. We try to come here whenever we are in the area and they never disappoint.

Let's start with the food!!!! I never tried fried deviled eggs before - and am likely to not ruin it but trying anyone else's - these were perfect.  
I had the smothered pork chops and the hubby had the oxtails - chef kiss. The portions are generous and well seasoned. The sides (mac&cheese, collard greens) felt home made like you'd have at a family reunion. We got the red velvet cake to go and it'll last for at least two days!! 
Only downside was the wait for the food, and honestly it was a modest inconvenience- especially since they were also accommodating an extremely large party of at least 30.  As for ambiance - there was great options for inside or outside dining. With great music. Overall it was my first trip to Oak Park and I'll definitely be back.

Went to this place for a birthday dinner and I definitely understand all the rave reviews! I had the fried catfish with Mac and potato salad ($20) and it was delicious. I finished every last bite! The drinks menu was also creative and range from $11-$16. The service was a little slow, so that's why 4 stars. 

While we ate inside, the outside area also looked really nice. I would definitely come back.

I was genuinely excited to bring my in-laws to this location. My family and I have been to the LA branch multiple times, and every visit has been excellent top-notch service and incredible food. So, when I found out there was a location near my in-laws, I couldn't stop raving about it and was eager for them to experience it.
While the food here did not disappoint--it was absolutely delicious, the service was, unfortunately, a major letdown. From the moment we checked in with Hayden at the front to our experience with our server Aimee, and even the manager, the overall service was chaotic and unprofessional.
My father-in-law waited nearly an hour for his meal. We were patient and understanding, but out of nowhere, our server Aimee snapped at us, yelling, "I already comped it, What do you want me to do? All I can do is comp it!" We were shocked, we hadn't even complained or asked for anything to be comped. When I tried to speak to the manager about the situation, he cut me off before I could say a word, saying, "I know, we already comped it. I'm doing three things at once," and then walked away, never to return.
It was a complete contrast to the warm, attentive service we always received in LA. The staff at this Sacramento location came off as off, very rude, and completely unprepared to handle a busy shift. It's unfortunate, because the food really is outstanding, but the service was enough to make us not want to return.
Also, the air conditioning was barely working. Being overheated in a restaurant, on top of dealing with unprofessional staff, made the entire experience that much worse.
In short, amazing food, but the service needs serious improvement. It's a shame, because this place has so much potential.

My middle sister, family friend, my older niece and I came to Fixins Soul Kitchen on 03/05/22 . Our friend highly recommended it and so glad he did!

We arrived around 1pm and there was about an hour wait. So we explored the surroundings . Once we got the notification that our table was ready we were seated immediately. 

The staff was very friendly but it did take a while for us to have our orders taken. But I will mention it was VERY busy so expect a wait time. Staff was seriously BUSY I never saw anyone standing around they were on the go! It was refreshing but also they were working so hard! So I made sure to tip generously.

Any who, the have a unique menu cratering to Soul Food which is not my go to unfortunately but I do like it periodically.  They have Koolaide that is VERY sweet. The family friend let me try his it was good but too sweet for my liking. They have a good drink menu and drinks were affordable too! My sister ended up with the Watermelon Margarita (delicious) and I got the Lavender Lemon drop 7/10 I would recommend it was tasty. We all enjoyed our drinks!

The family friend got appetizer of fried deviled eggs which was tasty but not for me. Glad I tried it but he finished them.  The family friend got Shrimp and Grits which was so delicious and plated beautifully. The sister got the Cat Fish Fillet with greens and red bean with rice, the niece got a kids meal and I got the Smothered Pork Chops with Mac and Cheese with greens. The smothered pork chop needed more salt the greens were alright not like moms :( but edible. I wouldn't get the greens again but the Mac and Cheese was my favorite!! I would recommend. The portion sizes are HUGE. I gave my left overs to the family friend he seriously had a full meal as I only sampled a little of everything on my plate and I was full!

However, a heated argument broke out near a table by us so there was some drama going on, people raising their voices, a lot of movement and people walking away to cool off. Our food got sent to that table :( so we had to walk over and let the people know and find our server. They apologized for the mistake and the family gave us our food with no problem. It seemed that family calmed down and appeared calm so maybe it was a blessing our food got sent over xD

Other than incident it was a pleasant experience. I would love to come back again when I am in the mood of Soul Food. I would recommend it. Friendly staff, nice music and decor.
Great chicken and waffles and the cake (red velvet) is so good. The service is amazing and the restaurant had great ambience.

I love to come to Fixins because of the ambiance, the drinks, and the fried chicken and waffles. When it comes to their waffle and biscuits with their house made butter, no other fried chicken spot in the area takes the cake. 

The chicken is perfectly fried with a juicy center. It is very well seasoned, and goes perfect with your choice of condiment if you choose.  For me, I like to ask for ketchup, and I mix it with the hot sauce they have in house. 

Their sides however, are pretty mediocre. The greens taste really healthy and I like my greens to have some fatty flavor. It's kind of on the bland side. The Mac and cheese is okay for me because I'm a creamy Mac and cheese person. The grits are delicious because who can mess up on plain grits? The red beans and rice were a little dry for me and needed more gravy/sauce. 

Pictured below is the family meal that comes with 8 pieces of chicken, 3 sides, and biscuits. We added waffles and grits as an additional side 

All in all it's a great place to visit, and it's located next to other great shops.

It was only 1 day earlier that I'd tried to go to Fixins Soul Kitchen but the wait - even at an odd, off-time - didn't work for me.  The hostess was so sincerely warm and apologetic that there was no way I wouldn't return 24 hours later, when heading back through town.  

Here's what I'll say about this casual, packed eatery:

*  They are, apparently, never not busy.  You'll wait, but the wait is probably shorter than you're told to expect.

*  The soul food here is delicious.  I sampled Fried Chicken (choose from white or dark meat), Fried Chicken Salad, Macaroni & Cheese, Cole Slaw, and the Pimento Cheese Dip.  In a nutshell, all were perfect.  

*  I saw a couple dine and dash here.  

Fixins doesn't need my help at getting folks through the door but based on my visit they do need help with their service levels.  Food took a very long time to be delivered to my table, eventually arriving after the food at two other nearby tables, both who had sat and ordered after me.  I wouldn't normally notice, but the wait for my food was long enough that it caused me to notice.  

When I was done with my meal things grew worse.  Where did my server go and why was nobody else paying any attention to my table?  I wanted both a box for my leftovers and the bill but nobody cleared my plates or checked on my table.  Annoyance grew into frustration.  Was this the reason another table dined and dashed?   Eventually I walked up to the hostess stand to request a box and my bill and the hostess made it happen but really?  Nobody should have to do that.  

The service I received here was a huge stain on the overall experience.  This was a special occasion meal marred by the very poor service.  I enjoyed the food enough that I'd happily return for another visit but if I had a similar service experience I'd really rethink my rating.  Annoyance and frustration were not things I wanted with my otherwise excellent meal.
The food was awesome   The service was horrible   The waitress could not handle the busy time kinda lost it a couple times

This place has a modern and classic interior that meshes perfectly together. Employees were helpful and definitely quick!

Ordered fried green tomatoes, fried catfish bites, smothered chicken and shrimp and grits. All the food was amazing! The tomatoes were delectable, the catfish was cooked perfect, the chicken was juicy and crunchy, and the shrimp and grits were creamed perfectly. Everything was delicious and this place is definitely worth a try!

For dessert, the peach cobbler is to die for!

P.S.
The Uncle Nearest 1856 Whiskey was smooth! And the New Orleans sweet tea had the perfect sweetness!

I came for lunch and had two chicken pieces which comes with 2 fixings (Mac & cheese & coleslaw and biscuit.  

The chicken was moist, juicy and so full of flavor.  The macaroni and cheese is a must for me and I enjoyed it here a lot.  The coleslaw wasn't bad either.  To drink I had sweet tea and it was so good.  It was so reminiscent of the south.  

I enjoyed the service here a lot.  There's street parking available.

must try if ur in the area! Cool vibes and perfect place to eat w friends and family. The service took a little long but that's okay because we ate lots of food. I really recommend this place, I loved the meal so much! 
Fried deviled eggs 5/5: crispy, creamy, savory all the things you would want 

Catfish nuggets 5/5: so so soft and crispy on the outside. Really good. 

Shrimp and grits 5/5: a little kick and creaminess. I really enjoyed the shrimps and the sauce. 

Family meal 5/5: fried chicken was so crispy and had a lot of flavor. The sides we got was Mac and cheese, collard greens, and the candied yams. Enough to feed 5 people. 

Banana pudding 6/5: so freaking good! Creamy and delicious. I really enjoyed the banana flavor with real pieces of banana and crispy chunks of wafers I believe? anyways you gotta try it!

This could be an isolated incident, but it's worth sharing for the sake of service improvement. Our family visited this restaurant for the first time on 7/25/24 around 2-3 pm. Our initial impression was positive due to the cleanliness, spaciousness, and pleasant music. We were promptly welcomed and shown to our table, and our waitress brought us water upon request (although I noticed lack of friendly face so thought maybe she had a long day!)

We ended up waiting a long time for her to return and take our order, which never happened. I asked another waitress if she could take our order, but she said she would inform our waitress. Despite this, our waitress never returned to our table, so we decided to leave the restaurant. Unfortunately, we didn't get to try the food. We are unlikely to return due to the staff's lack of professionalism and cooperation.

Wow, the food is impressive!  Came here Monday for lunch at 11am.  Prior to restaurant opening, waiting outside was another hungry group.  Arriving at opening also meant we didn't have to wait long to order and and the food came out quick.

Portions here are large and sharable, photos doesn't show size.

Chicken & Waffles are a 10/10!  Waffles are thin with perfect texture.  Chicken is extra crispy and juicy on the inside.  The strawberry butter was amazing.  Everything worked so well together, including their hot sauce.  Love savory and sweet.

The shrimp and grits was good, but I wasn't as excited for it as I was for the chicken and waffles.  Jumbo sized shrimp with buttered grits.  Still a very tasty dish.  Nice kick and spice in the gravy along with onion and bell peppers.

Sweet tea was very sweet.  Too sweet for me, great if you have a sweet tooth.  Had to let the ice melt.

Very clean inside with a good amount of tables.  Within an hour the restaurant filled up.  Friendly service. Definitely returning for more chicken and waffles and to try other menu items!

My husband took me here for our valentines dinner. I didn't want to go on valentines because I hate crowds; we went on Sunday. The service was excellent! Our server was Cheri, she was fun,bubbly and took care of us. The food was delicious, seasoned perfectly. I had the fried chicken; the brine really made the chicken outstanding. The sweet potatoes were so good I didn't want to run out so I was eating slow. The Mac and cheese was really good too. Back to the chicken: it was crispy, tender and had a little spice. The ambience was friendly. It was busy. I still felt taken care of in spite of the bustling flow. Biscuits and some type of butter and like the last time I went the koolaid is delish.
Great food, extensive menu and our server was outstanding! Would definitely come back when we're in Sacramento.

Visited Fixins today with G. We chose it since it's usually good and it was happy hour 3-6p, so that means 6$ appetizers. Or waitress was training a newbie, they were nice and professional. I didn't order a whole meal just some nuggets. Those and the Mac n cheese balls. They were ok. Towards the end of the meal, BBHMM by Rihanna came on unedited and it was inappropriate for a family restaurant even at happy hour. Lost a star for that. I am full though and the big lemonade in a mason jar was refreshing.

Definitely grateful to have found this restaurant. Had the oxtail over rice with collard greens and this is by far the best I have had in town. I have been on a search for the best soul food since I started working in Sacramento and I think these are the best oxtail. Great portion, wonderful taste, and great atmosphere! Would most definitely recommend. Will be returning soon to try some of the other deliciousness they have to offer.

This place is Legit, you can't eat everyone's soul food especially when it comes to potato salad, Mac n cheese, and collard greens. But let me tell you, "they put there foot in this food" it was bussin bussin!!! Highly recommend everything we ate including the fried chicken the catfish all the sides and the biscuits!!! Definitely coming back with friends when I'm in the area!! Well done fixins Well done!! I'm just sad I was too hungry to get pictures.

This place has some pretty awesome fried chicken & a very chill vibe! 
1. Must try: Fried Deviled eggs topped with bacon and hot sauce.
2. Smothered Fried Chicken!

The fixins we really love:
1. Fried Okra with Bacon 
2. Grits & Cheese

Their cocktail menu is very creative! Our favorites:
1. Chocolate Martini 
2. Watermelon Daiquiri
3. Lavender Lemon Drop

All in all, we LOVE everything about this place! Staff are really nice, service is very good too!

They do offer activity sheets for kids to keep busy, this is something we really appreciate! 
Patio seating available. 
Roadside parking.

we've been here a few times and look forward to many more fine meals here.  Their fried chicken is amazing.  Rich flavor and thick batter.  They use big chickens too.  Some places try to cook you with their spicy sauces.  Not here,  this chicken is featured in glory.  I worry about those hot spicy chickens.  Are they hiding spoiling meat?  Again not here it is so good.  it came with two sides.  My wife had Cole slaw and potato salad.  I had collard greens and potato salad.  We were too full for desert.
