We sat outside on their pleasant patio.  We keep meaning to try other things, but their chicken usually wins.  Last Winter I tried their gumbo.  Delicious!  Enjoy this Tasty Oak Park gem.

To start, our waitress, Cat was absolutely outstanding. 
Very pleasant, polite and a great sense of humor. 
As usual, the food was on point.
I've had the pleasure of eating several times at the Los Angeles restaurant and have never been disappointed. 
I strongly suggest if haven't visited Fixins, you must get there.
These places are getting hard to find. Tasty food great service reasonable price. Patio was nice and clean. Staff was great and friendly

Menu was great, service excellent, nice location and great vibe. But the salt. There was enough sodium in my fried chicken and sides to double the salinity of the Pacific Ocean. If I didn't have high blood pressure I do now. Easy to add salt to my plate, impossible to take it out.

We were there on a Friday evening without reservations. About a 30 min wait. We snagged some bar seats so it was quicker. I got an adult kool aid to drink. It was way too sweet for me so not my thing. A shout out to Christine the bartender who was so busy with the drink orders, But she still found time to check on our food for us. So we really appreciated that. This place Definitely needs two bartenders. The food took almost an hour. But boy was it worth the wait. 
I got the smothered chicken with a side of Mac n cheese and okra. The chicken was delicious and okra was so good. My bf got the shrimp and grits which was equally delicious. We were stuffed so no dessert but next time. 
Parking: found some street parking.

Omgoodness! We went this last Sunday after church and it was the best dining experience ever! The food was amazing, delicious and savory from the appetizers to the desert! I was craving Soul food and this place did NOT disappoint! The service was quick and the waitress was friendly and gave each of her tables attention. The ambiance itself was really warm and inviting. My husband enjoy his very first bowl of Gumbo w/extra Shrimp. I myself had the Fish, sides of Mac & Cheese and Candied Yams! We both shared Peach Cobbler which is by far my favorite desert! We will definitely be back to try the other dishes! Thank you so much for some good food!

Brought the family and got the 8 piece family meal, Mac n cheese and fries as our sides. Vanessa was our server, she was very friendly and attentive. Food tasted great, we had water as our drink and you can tell it was tap water. My husband bit into his chicken thigh and it was pretty bloody. He told Vanessa that if she could advise the cook about the chicken, she then took the plate away went to speak with cook and came back apologizing and said that she took something off our bill and even gave us a side of banana pudding (which is my favorite as this is Kevin's grandma's recipe) Even though his piece of chicken was bloody, everything else was good (not counting the tap water). Service was great and restaurant was very clean.

Good food, nice portions and great service.  We had the fish nuggets appetizer, shrimp and grits and fried chicken dinner.  The lemonade was very sweet but yummy. We were too full for dessert. We look forward to going back to try more.

The food was okay. The service from Eliza was EVERYTHING. 1000000/10, she was so attentive, kind, and overall great personality and energy. She made the night so amazing. We are so grateful (college tour group travelling through town).

Who knew I'd have some of the best fried chicken I've ever had in Sacramento of all places. Being from out of town I can honestly say that I'm craving for some fixins right now. Luckily our amazing server Austin told me that they have another location in my hometown. 

We ordered the fried chicken with fried okra and candied yams. All we're out of the park amazing. You can tell that they make all of their food with TLC. 

I also ordered the fried green tomatoes. It was so good. It had everything you can ask for in a dish. Crunch, sweet, spice, umami with the sauce. Soooo good

Now let's talk about our server Austin. He made our experience better with his kindness and attentiveness. We also had a chat about his hometown and that made my party feel at home with how much he loved Sacramento. 


Can't wait to come back!

The shrimp and grits are near orgasmic, have a nice kick to them. Add an extra side of grits to go with it, they give you a lot.
Smothered fried chicken was juicy and crunchy in all the right ways. Fried deviled eggs were a unique option paired with the zingy dressing that it came with. Can't wait to go back!

First time here- rec by my sister and some friends.
Ansar was awesome. Need some soul food?  Great vibe and just overall good music?  This is the spot. Dropped $90 but soo worth it.

Incredible food and incredible service. The staff are so nice and efficient and they give great recommendations if you're ever in need. The food comes in large portions and a great price too! U ordered the the Smothered Pork Chops with Potato Salad and Mac n Cheese as my two Fixins. The pork chops were cooked perfectly and the gravy covering it gave it the perfect seasoning. The two fixins were also really good, and I highly recommend the potato salad! In addition, I also got a Kool Aid pickle on the side which I wasn't sure I'd like but definitely knew I wanted to try. It had an interesting taste to it, but the combination actually worked together quite well! For dessert, I ordered the Banana Pudding which had the cutest presentation and also tasted amazing. I can't wait to come back here again when I'm in Sacramento!
Food is delicious, but is not worth the price. Very expensive meal. There is not any good views. Service is good.

The 1st time--- I loved everything! Best catfish, juicy yummy fried chicken. The rice and beans were not mixed. Kinda a dollop of rice on top. The greens were bland, but salt helped.

2nd time. I took clients and we ordered much of the menu. The catfish--still spectacular. They know how to do this. 

Fried chicken was over cooked too brown and you could taste it was old oil. Ugh...I was so embarrassed after raving about it. 

Gumbo?? It was so dark everyone was leary about putting a spoon in. It wasn't good.

The mac n cheese was really nice and creamy. The corn bread and biscuits with their yummy jellies were too.

So... I'm mixed on this place. Should I go for a 3rd time???

My cousin and I absolutely had to stop here before the Kings game. The whole menu looks scrumptious! We ordered the fried deviled eggs, but they were just okay. I ordered the smothered oxtails and candied yams. It was so good I kept eating even after I was full. The server was swamped but made sure to leave us smiling every time she touched the table. We also spoke with the chef who was nothing but a good vibe and humble. Lastly, we asked the manager for the Spotify playlist because we had karaoke moments between bites.

The moment I saw there was a Fixins in town I had to come! I was craving soul food! I came solo and unfortunately they weren't taking any additional walk ins BUT I lucked up and got the last seat at the bar. The service was GREAT and fast! Super attentive! The scene was great! Such good vibes, this location is a bit smaller than the location back home in SOCal but it's still good vibes. My food was great! I ordered 2 wings and a waffle and red beans and rice. Fire. 

My server was super dope too! Loved her! She was nothing but good vibes!

Second time visiting the Fixins because I wanted to bring my family this time. As before, all was great. My smothered chicken was on point. I'm thankful they had a vegan menu for my spouse. Her and my daughter ordered several sides to go.
I'm sure we will be back again.

Had the fried chicken Mac n cheese fried deviled eggs and potato salad. Everything was delicious. My favorite was the mac n cheese. Love a good Mac n Cheese.

The server was very much on point. Made sure my date and I received everything we needed and checked on us through out our time.

The overall vibe of the establishment was amazing. Lighting is perfect. Spacing between seats so it's almost impossible for someone to bump you while eating.
And just was able to focus on my time with
The food was very very good. Well seasoned and incredibly fresh. We came with a party of 8 and our waitress was extremely friendly and attentive to our needs.

Great staff and great food! I've been in a couple times so far and have been impressed each time. The location is great, parking is typically a non issue and the vibe in the restaurant is top tier. Both times I sat at the bar and had a drink while waiting for takeout and both times the bartender was friendly, efficient and totally pro. It's also a very friendly joint and conversation among folks at the bar was lively and enjoyable. I found the menu to be fairly broad, but still on-brand and everything I've had thus far has been fantastic. So far I've have the Gumbo, the Fried Chicken and the Red Beans and Rice.  My next visit will surely be for the Oxtails. And I'll be heading there soon!

Fixins has been on my list to check out since they opened! I finally was able to check them out while doing some Christmas shopping in Oak Park!

The inside and vibe is so cozy and welcoming. Our server was super friendly and had great recommendations.

I ordered the Red Kool-Aid and I feel like I could drink this by the gallon! It was so good, not too sweet, but so thirst quenching! My daughter ordered the Pink Kool-Aid which I believe might have been watermelon flavored and I loved that one too.

For my entree, I ordered the Smothered Chicken with Red Beans and Rice and Mac and Cheese for my sides. I loved the gravy and how comfort food feeling the chicken was! The Smothered Chicken comes with 2 giant pieces of chicken that you could definitely share. I enjoyed the Mac and Cheese, but the beans in the Red Beans and Rice were a tad bit undercooked for my liking - still had great flavor though!

We also tried the biscuits with the amazing butter! Is it mixed with grape jelly?! It's great and I'd come to Fixins just for that! Oh and the Kool-Aid of course.

The other little surprise of the night was the Kool-Aid pickled Pickle! What?! So good if you love pickles. They need to bottle and sell these!

Don't sleep on Fixins!

I know, the picture makes this sandwich looks amazing, but it was really quite the opposite. I had a not so great experience. It started off when I ordered online for pickup in 30-45 minutes. When I placed the order, I got a text literally 3 minutes later that it was ready. So it was sitting there for 40 minutes?? Not cool. By the time I got there I picked up my order which was easy and the staff was nice, but when I finally opened the box, it was clear that my order had been sitting out. The fries were soggy and the sandwich was like warm. 

I didn't want lettuce, onions, and tomatoes on it, but ordering online wouldn't let me change that so I had to take them off when I got it. The sandwich was super messy and the chicken was bland and kinda felt like it wasn't fully cooked. There were two sauces on it and coleslaw which was a bit excessive. Also, the "hot sauce" tasted more like a sweet bbq sauce. I will say that the one bright spot was the ranch. The ranch was awesome. 

I was super disappointed because this place was supposed to be great and I'd heard great things, but this is most certainly not a very good sandwich. I've had better fast food chicken sandwiches. There's even a post on here that says this is the best chicken sandwich ever, but the guy doesn't even eat chick sandwiches. So listen to me, a chicken sandwich connoisseur and don't waste your $17.
Food great. Love the ambience relaxing with class and the sweet tea brings back memories.

Service is always Great. Waitresses 
Goes beyond to accommodate one. As far as the food, it's a larger portion that  you would think. It's a great place for a family to go to.also the waitress is very friendly and on point.
Amazing food every time , I've been about 4-5 times food hit exactly the same. The vibe is always lit as well as the drinks.

Today's experience was Excellent, 
I had just left Church and was hungry. Our Sever was knowledgeable and helpful, The food came quickly and It was good. Justin R. Was very kind to us we appreciate his service. We will be back See you soon.

Absolutely fantastic from service to presentation to taste. Having lived in Arkansas & Texas I became accustomed to southern soul food and this is as authentic as any I've had. Clean and friendly environment in a vibrant restaurant. Thanks for reminding my tastebuds the pleasures of southern comfort food.

I came here after a looong day in the sun with my family. This place was really delicious. The portion size is a big smaller than what I used to but the quality of the food is great. The juicy, cripsy, crunch of the chicken is superior! The drinks are a bit too sweet for my liking but my sons and brother loved it. Our service was great. Everyone was friendly. The bathroom was clean. And more importantly, the food was delicious.
Food was great came very quickly and server was great and very nice place to check out

Best fried chicken ever!!! Gravy on the smothered chicken only makes it better!! 

It gets very busy just FYI

I had the Fish and mac and cheese with okra but no bacon on the okra.. my friend had the same thing but her side was greens.. super delicious and flavorful! The drink is a lavender lemon drop- also super delicious- the staff is very polite- there was a wait but i definitely can see why. The food is amazing.. 10/10..

The food reminded me of home and I've been away from home for a long time. The staff was engaging and inviting. The atmosphere was family oriented. We loved the food so much we bought one of their T-Shirts. Can't wait to go back and enjoy the bakery and bookstore on the same block.

Cool place, very chill. Chicken and waffles. Hot chicken sandwich was fire! Make sure to add avocado. Crunchy French fries, some deep fried bacon deviled eggs and candied yams. You can't go wrong. Excellent service. Servers gave us some good laughs. Enjoyed this place thoroughly.

First time visiting this place, it was such a cute place and our waitress was the sweetest. The food was delicious, and the drink menu was super cute. I would def go back again for brunch to try their other items. My chicken breast was a lot smaller then the 2 piece combo, so I was a bit disappointed lol. And another thing I say they should improve on was making sure they meet reservation times especially for people who have short lunch breaks and they book ahead of time so set them in a time manner not 10-20mins past their reserved times.
Have been here many times and it's always so wonderful! Service, atmosphere and food are consistently great. Fried chicken is drool worthy! I

Amazing! This place is seriously just amazing, from the staff to the food and aesthetics. I've here twice and both times I'm blown away! We ordered biscuit and gravy, side of Mac and cheese and chicken and waffles. 20/10 

P.S food was so good, I didn't have a chance to take a pic

Wanted to try this place on the way back from Tahoe and came around 3pm on a Tuesday and was seated right away. The menu is small but you'll want to try everything. We ended up with shrimp and grits, chicken and waffles and hot chicken sandwich. Their water glasses are huge 32oz mason jars.

The decor was cute! Before our food came, we were warmly greeted by Auntie Pearl who gave us a little history of the restaurant and the surrounding area. Loved her shoutouts for the neighboring establishments

The food was amazing. Shrimp and grits had a generous portion of large, jumbo shrimp. The gravy was pleasantly spicy and full of bell peppers and onions. The chicken was tender, the batter was crispy and crunchy. The waffle batter was something different, definitely tasty and had a touch of sweetness to it. The hot chicken sandwich had a spicy sauce that paired well with the chicken. We also had the fixins: collard greens and Mac n cheese, both were delicious.

Throughout our meal, the waitresses talked with us to check in on how we're doing, but honestly I didn't hate it. They were genuine and seemed like they actually wanted to converse. It was a good vibe.

Would come back to try other things on the menu. Parking wasn't too tricky, lots of street parking, even around the block.

Great fried chicken and biscuits. But I also ordered a Caesar salad. For $15. And it was delivered without dressing, and it was limp and otherwise wholly unimpressive. Like, not exaggerating when I say it was a half-assed bowl of chopped romain with no dressing and six actual FRITOS instead of croutons. 

I think this is more a negative review of myself. Lesson learned; don't order salad from a place that makes its name on heart attack food. Even a Caesar salad.  I feel robbed.

One of our friends suggested this restaurant so we decided to try it. 
We went on Saturday afternoon so parking was a bit challenging but we ended up finding a place down the street. 
Since we did not have reservations, there was a bit of a wait. It was in a nice neighborhood so we walked past the coffee shop to browse a nearby bookstore while we waited. 
We walked back in early so we could look at the menu while we waited, and we found that our table was ready.
The 4 of us ordered sweet tea. And wow. It was SWEET.  Unfortunately, it was so sweet that by the time I got my food, I was a bit queasy. Next time, I'll get water because I need 100% space for this good food. 
We got the deep fried deviled eggs. Very good. Just the outside is deep fried so you still have a soft egg white. 
Between us all, we had:
Fried chicken- perfect crisp and juicy and flavorful on the inside. 
Smothered chicken - my mother in law (RIP mom) made smothered chicken and it was my husband's favorite. He totally downed this. He said it was good and the gravy was a bit spicy. 
Hot spicy chicken sandwich- it was a big boy. My daughter circled it a bit to find out where to bite first. It looked pretty good. 
Hamburger- who goes to get soul food and gets a burger?? My son. He said it was good. He ate the whole thing in record time.
Fries- these were beautiful honestly. Perfect crunch.  
Yams- like mom used to make. Went perfect with the greens
Mac n cheese- homemade. A mix between bougie & traditional. Perfect mix
Greens- I kinda teared up here. My MIL just passed and she made excellent greens. These were similar: smoky flavor with a hint of sweet. 
Banana pudding- next time I'll come back for just dessert. SUPER GOOD. 
There's many little additions that really made the experience shine: the pickles were Kool-aid dyed, the biscuits were top tier, the butter was slightly sweet which paired perfectly with the biscuit, and the tiny salad that went with the Deviled eggs had a great vinaigrette. 
We'll be back!!
We sat outside and the ambience is relaxed, servers are prompt, and the manager checked on us twice, the food is 10/10.
Comfy vibes & delicious southern comfort/soul food. Perfect lunch or dinner spot & love that it's close to the law school

It was finally nice to be able to get to one of their locations to try out. The day before having dinner here we did make reservations online to make it a little bit easier and less of a weight. Definitely my first recommendation. Once we arrived, we only waited about five minutes and we were seated. I can say from the time we sat down until the time we finished our meal and got up the music vibe was fantastic! I really enjoyed their playlist that they had playing this particular Saturday night definitely reminded me of my high school days. The service was definitely mellow and on point here. Never a rude interaction with any of the crewmember here. I liked how everybody that was working even though they may have had a signed tables they were all Hands on deck and assisted each other. From the drinks that we had bartender did very good, they weren't too strong, nor not strong enough. We started with an appetizer had the fried fish. Definitely a great choice wasn't chewy or gummy or anything and the dipping sauces they had were really good. Moving onto our main course Mills everything was really good. I think the only thing that we had as far as our meal that was a little bit. Maybe undercooked was the peach cobbler the whole pieces inside were extra crunchy as if they could've maybe baked a little bit longer, but still having the ice cream on top of that, and the warmness to melt it from the cobblerwas still a good choice to choose. Overall, our first visit definitely speaks to us visiting again in the very near future.

Soul food without soul is just food & just a restaurant. And last night the soul was missing. In the south when these types of foods was served people was welcoming , happy, inviting, it was a party. You felt a part of family and culture. 
Fixins  Soul Kitchen felled short of that mark last night their service was less than one star  and after speaking with the manager it didn't get any better. 
We was seated and waited over 15 minutes before we complained to the manger in the blue shirt and then another 10 or so before somebody showed up. I had to go to the bar after waiting a little over 30 minutes to buy drinks for our table  twice during the night . I had to personally go to bar and get them myself . We had to order all our food at once appetizer and main course because we didn't know when we  would see our server again. Unfortunately do to poor service it killed vibe and the feeling soul food is supposed to bring. 
The ambiance was very nice the restaurant was clean. Great music from DJ played at the right volume. 
**** ****** Now the food **************
The food was 4 1/2 half to five stars .  Their was a lot wow factors, great smells and taste.
Staring with OXTAIL's great favor meat was tender cooked to perfection. Smoother Fried Chicken was excellent 
The smoother pork chops in point . We literally eat the whole menu , I should just tell you what was not on point only two sides . The Mac and Cheese was lacking favor and the Grits was dry. Everything else was good and excellent . We had the Fried Okra, deep fried devil eggs etc....

FIXINS is the 1, not the 2 or 3! Came here on Sunday around noon. The wait was approximately 20 minutes which wasn't bad because we were told 30 minutes when we initially arrived!

This restaurant is excellent. The staff is very friendly and so helpful. We were attended to throughout the dining experience and we were regularly checked on. We were brought waters upon seating. Then the waitress took our order. There was no appetizer menu but the waitress told us about them.

We started off with the catfish nuggets! BOMB!!! For entrees, I got the 2 piece chicken. The 2 piece chicken came with 2 sides. I got candied yams and Mac n cheese. Also, BOMB!! My friend got the shrimp and grits! 

 All the food was delicious! The catfish was fresh and so crispy. It is battered in cornmeal and it created for a nice crispy crunch with each bite. The chicken was so flavorful and crunchy. Every bite there was flavor and a crunch. The chicken was also very moist and all-around good! Honestly, this is the best chicken I've ever eaten out! Loved it. The mac n cheese is legit!!! It was packed with flavor. There are breadcrumbs in the mac n cheese. This was a first for me, but I enjoyed it. It added more texture and gave the mac a nice flavor. The candied yams!!! WHEW!!! Those were soooo good. The syrup they were in was full of butter and was so good! The sweet potatoes themself were cooked to perfection. They were not mushy but they were not hard. They were cooked just right! 

Also my food came with a biscuit! It was to die for. The biscuit melts in your mouth. The biscuit came with a strawberry butter and that butter + biscuit combo was unmatched!!! It was soooo yummy! 

Everything was soo good! I am looking forward to coming back. The next time I may get the shrimp and grits because that was soo tasty.

Fixins does soul food right and I highly recommend coming here! You won't be disappointed!

- The hostess/greeter was so friendly and welcoming 
- Great music and clean restaurant 

- The menu is mouth watering 

- catfish nuggets are really good. Seasoned well and fried well, not too hard. I wasn't a fan of the sauces on the side (chipotle Mayo & aioli) 
- The fixins were out of this world. The candied yams were tender and sweet and the collard greens were cooked perfectly with shredded Turkey neck and the right spiciness.
- the smothered pork chops were not my favorite, and I wish there was a bit more gravy on my plate. It seemed as though the pork chops didn't come directly from the pot/stove because it wasn't hot but rather warm.

So happy I got to try this place! I was only in Sacramento for a day and a half, and this was the one place I really sought out. 

The service here was great -- everyone was warm and friendly even though we walked in during the Saturday brunch rush. 

The place itself was also really lovely -- spacious and well-lit. The AC was great for the summer weather, but the open-air garden area was also pretty cool. 

The food was the standout, of course. The fried chicken was classic Southern delicious -- crispy and juice and salty all at once. It was the perfect foil against the sweet waffles. The shrimp and grits were definitely memorable. The grits were flavorful on their own, but with the shrimp cooked down in that rich PBR brown gravy, they were the perfect pairing. The sauce was rich and deep, the kind that you wish you could drink up. I kept making yummy sounds each time I stole a spoonful of sauce off my friend's plate. 

The servings were pretty big, such a good deal for the price. I wish it wasn't too full for dessert. Hopefully, I'm able to try those out next time I visit!

Okay so let's be honest here! I have been trying to come to this restaurant for the past year with my bestie finally went on a Sunday! Parking was horrible!! I had to wait for what felt like forever for someone to move then on top of that the parking was tight. When I arrived with my bestie and my kids the line was long and when we finally made it to the hostess she said it would be about an hour wait. I was reluctant due to the fact I was hungry but we decided to wait.

When we were finally able to get a table they said I would have to detach my babys car seat from her stroller in order to sit at a table. It sounded strange to me because my baby was sleeping and another lady didn't have to take her baby out of a stroller but mine had to. Now when we were finally at a table our waiter came over she was nice we ordered adult Kool aid and my kids got sprite and regular Kool aid. 

Those drinks took a while to come due to the fact they were busy. Now let's get to why I reviewed it two stars! The waiter was nice and attentive. The food is another story!! I ordered smothered pork chops, Mac n cheese , candied yams, and potato salad. The smothered pork chops were heavily breaded with barely any meat!! The gravy tasted old and a bit spicy the Mac and cheese was bland and the candied yams tasted like canned yams not acceptable. 

Now my besties order she ordered chicken and waffles. Her chicken was pink on the inside which shouldn't have been if you know how to cook chicken! Basically what I'm saying is this is not real soul food! Clearly people in the comments have never had real soul food because it shouldn't taste like that! The peach cobbler crust tasted watery there was no real heart put into the food I spent $250 on food for the food to be crappy!!!

Only thing about this restaurant that was great was adult Kool aid! The atmosphere was horrible black people really aren't for other black people there was women hating on other black women. I see why the black community is what it is today because they hate each other and when you come here you see it I know I saw it. I'm all for supporting black owned restaurants but this one isn't it honey from the food to the atmosphere its a no go for me.

I've been hungry for some good old soul food but my wife is not such a huge fan, so my birthday was a great excuse to get out and experience Fixins Soul Kitchen. She had the fried chicken (which I'm glad I did not because my grandma made it so much better, no offense to the folks in the kitchen). She was happy with the chicken her potato salad was fair but the coleslaw was too wet and soupy. I did the smothered pork chops with greens and black eyed peas. The chops were good the greens were spot on and the black eyed peas were a little bland. Service was really slow but we could tell they were very understaffed so can't complain too much about that. The restaurant is laid it pretty well but I could see it getting pretty packed when busier. Overall, a nice experience and I will be going back.

I had the pleasure of visiting Fixins Soul Kitchen this past weekend for a nice brunch with my sweet friend who's getting married this year and the rest of her bride tribe.

Parking was surprisingly easy for brunch hours. We parked in the lot around the corner and dodged the Girl Scouts slanging their cookies (which of course on our way out, we folded and purchased a few boxes).

Let me first say, that the vibe of this place is so upbeat and lively! The playlist alone slays! From Aaliyah to Mariah Carey, you're in for a good time! Our group was lucky enough to have a wonderful server such as Jazmine, taking care of us! One of our appetizer plates had some schmuck on it and she profusely apologized and quickly replaced it.

Now on to the food! Our group got two servings of the Fried Green Tomatoes and Oh Boy! They were delicious. Perfect crust and the sauce is such a lovely compliment! 

I, admittedly, ordered the Soul Caesar, thinking it would be a lighter fair and boy was I pleasantly surprised when it came out of the kitchen. The only way I could describe as to how large the size of the serving bowl, is to compare it to those extravagant Kentucky Derby hats women wear! It was LARGE. I wasn't mad though, I'm a sucker for a good Caesar Salad and this was up there. I loved that the croutons were sweet and not too difficult to chew into and anytime a salad has a generous amount of parmesan shavings  is a win in my book. I ate half and took the rest of the half home with me.

Bottom line; I'm looking forward to visiting again and trying the rest of the menu!

Good food and cute atmosphere. 
I didn't give a 5 star review due to the downfall of the waitress we had and then the fried green tomatoes. 

Appetizers:
We tried the fried green tomatoes, which unfortunately were soggy on the inside and the fried deviled eggs, which were really good. 

Entrees:
- omelet with grits - my husband enjoyed 
- Fried chicken w Mac and yams - I forgot to take a pic because I dove right in! I absolutely enjoyed this entire dish! The fried chicken reminded me of dinner after church AT the church and it took me back to my childhood. Very fond memories came about and the chicken was juicy and flavorful.  Mac and cheese had several different cheeses that made it good. And the yams were the best I've ever had at a restaurant! 

For whatever reason the waitress did good in the beginning then fell off and just took too long to start bringing things out. She really put a damper on our service. Overall I would return and just pass on the fried green tomatoes. 
As a southern girl, I was looking for a little more crisp in them and they just didn't do it for me.

The best fried chicken and collard greens I have ever had! The Mac and cheese, coleslaw, and potato salad were great, but oh them greens!  We sat at the bar and had an excellent hurricane cocktail. The service was good and for the quality of the food, the prices are fair. I highly recommend this place!

OK, I'm not a fan of chicken normally but I do love soul food and I was super hungry. This may explain why I ordered fried chicken today. This chicken, OMG, this chicken - flatly should not be called chicken. It is tastier, juicier, crunchier and plainly more amazing than anything ever called chicken. More could be said, but take my word for it. This was an exquisite lunch. 
On top of that, I had ordered through Yelp and then after I paid Yelp told me the food wouldn't be ready for an hour. Turned out, Nope! It was ready the moment I walked in. 
All in all fantastic food, great service and what looked like a cozy location. Can't wait to go back? Maybe dinner tonight?!

Excellent every time!! No problems. Always enjoy my food. Always consistent! 
Chicken is crispy and hot! Yams and Greens great portion size always hot and yummy!!

A friend suggested "Fixins Soul Kitchen" for lunch and a time to talk. I cringed inside because my concept of "soul" food was woefully lacking. We arrived, and we were immediately greeted by 4-5 "Welcome to Fixins" greetings. We were seated immediately, and although busy, our table still felt like we had some privacy. No more than 2 minutes later, our server, Maia J, greeted us with a big smile. She got our drinks and took our orders with little time wasted. My friend had the Chicken Tenders Basket, and I had the "Better Burger" (I was thinking, "It takes some work to mess up a burger").

In just a short time - just long enough for my friend and I to start talking about the issues that brought us together for lunch - our meals are set before us. One bite of the "Better Burger," and I was ready to call it the "Best Burger." Perfectly cooked with just the right hint of seasoning. The french fries were hot and crispy. When I finally flagged down Maia, I told her this was one of the best burgers I ever had and to please pass my kudos on to the kitchen staff.

When we were leaving (and, of course, another chorus of "Thanks for coming" from the staff), I said to my friend, "Well, we need another follow-up meeting, so let's meet here next week!" He replied, "I thought we covered everything!" I said, "We did. I just want to eat here again as soon as possible!!!"

Well done Fixins Soul Kitchen!!

Possibly the BEST southern food I've had outside the south in my life. Everything shown on the table here was delicious. This was the first time I've ever eaten oxtails in my life, and I was blown away at how I haven't tried this dish  sooner. The gravy mixed with the fatty & tender oxtail made one of the best meals I've had all year and is truly a dish I can't put in words to describe. The fried chicken was super crispy, and you can tell was made right as we ordered. 
Throw a little of their house hot sauce on there and it will make your week. All this along with the creamy Mac and cheese and collard greens, there is literally nothing to complain about with this place. Fixins truly embodies what it means to be soul food: food that makes your soul happy. If you're anywhere near the Sacramento area, you NEED to try this place at least once, you definitely won't regret it!

5 stars across the board for Fixins. Everything was really good & everyone was really nice and greeted us with smiles. I got the oxtails and okra, so tender & flavorful! My honey got the chicken wings and waffles & he enjoyed it as well! Anytime I'm in town I'm stopping by and I need those oxtails again!!

Delicious and always packed.
I'm glad I got reservations for Saturday brunch online! People are normally waiting forever!

Service was very good. Casual vibe without being too relaxed was the feel. My wife and I split a smothered pork chop and it was tasty with just the right amount of gravy. Our okra with bacon was a little salty but I think it was the bacon. Our red beans and rice were spicy but not hot and I really enjoyed them. The Mac and Cheese was creamy. One gripe the food prices were quite fair but the drinks were very overpriced. 5$ for an iced tea or kool aid is a bit much.

Music great, few minutes to be seat. Staff were amazing!!! I ordered fried green tomatoes absolutely the best!!! Black eyed peas were fabulous without meat!!! I'm a Pescatarian so the options were great! The catfish nuggets are tasty too! Give them a try!!!!

i'm visiting from Atlanta & wasn't impressed ; the ambiance is great & the hostess was very inviting & nice . 
the food was ok , to say the least . we ordered hennessy wings , oxtail , mac & cheese , black eyed peas , candied yams & a waffle & wing . 
hennessy wings were cooked too soft ; the oxtail had good flavor , but was chewy . the sides were ok , not the best 
we had a lavender lemon drop , blue raspberry margarita & a sidecar 
the margarita was NOT it AT ALL ; but i drink it because i don't waste liquor
service was slow ; we had to wait for a waitress & ended up getting another person from a different section & after we got the food & drinks , we didn't see the waiter again

I came back to try more items on the menu. During this visit, I was craving gumbo and opted to add shrimp. Overall the gumbo was underwhelming for me. It came with 3 medium-ish sized shrimp, 2 slices of sausage, 1 cube of chicken, and 1 scoop of rice. The sauce tasted like gravy to me as it lacked any taste of added spices or kick (I like my gumbo spicy). Nonetheless, the dish was filling. I would give this dish a solid 3.5/5. 

The service was great as usual and parking was easy on this particular Monday. I look forward to trying more from the menu.

This place is a must to get your Soul food fix. Everything was delicious from the deviled eggs to the fruit punch that was childhood reminiscent. The hot chicken sandwich was the bomb. Service was a bit on the slower side, but the food was amazing.

I wish there were more stars to give.. This restaurant is LEGIT!! My kids have all raved about it so for Mother's Day, we were fortunate enough to get a reservation! As PACKED as it was, I never had such fast and friendly service!! Our waitress ALIZAE was soooo sweet and fast! Mikey was great and helpful too! The food was spot on-- sausage gravy and biscuits, smoky collard greens, smothered pork chops, shrimp and creamy grits, and excellent fried chicken on a CRISPY waffle- which thrilled me to no end cos most places only serve Belgian waffles ‍. The drinks were great too- a Rose Martini, Soicy Bloody Mary and a great house Chardonnay.. And the prices are EXCELLENT and the servings are HUGE! I see why this place is so popular!! Gonna be my family's Mothers Day tradition from now on- but I'm not waiting til next May to go back either!!  My Grammie was from the South and she would have been so proud! Thanks again for such a wonderful meal!!
Hella good oxtails need more meat less fat but it was good will b bk even my daughter enjoyD
Food was just delicious.  Service was ok. Ambiance was good too. Definitely going back again

Delicious southern food with perfect texture and rich flavor. There's a reason why there's a line here! I had my food to-go, and it was fresh and hot when I received it. The catfish is crispy and flaky inside, a unique treat. Love that they come with many sides! They also gave a lot of hot sauce to go. Parking is easy in this part of Sacramento since there are parking lots and plenty of street parking. Just be prepared to wait if you're coming at a busy time!

Yes.  

All of it. 

Just YES!

Check it out.  The atmosphere, experience, flavor, and SOUL is to die for. Not just in this restaurant, but the community surrounding it.  The barbershop, coffee shop, and bookstore on the same block.  

They get busy but I promise you it's worth the wait.  I highly recommend a reservation. Especially evenings and weekends.  

The staff are extremely friendly and make you feel at home.  The youth they hire are extremely professional and have a beautiful and bright future ahead of them. 

The food.  THE FOOD!!!   Talk about love going into every dish.  You cannot go wrong with anything on the menu but I highly recommend the fried green tomatoes and the shrimp and grits!   It's okay to get a to go box.  Believe me, you'll likely need it. 

All in all, I want to say thank you to the amazing team. Front of house and back of house who put so much love into this place.  

I will be returning often.

Delicious food! We ordered catfish nuggets, shrimp & grits, greens, red beans & rice and black eyed peas. I wasn't a fan of the way the black eyed peas were prepared, but everything else was full of flavor & cooked perfectly. Love the house hot sauce!
Our server & everyone we encountered was very kind & welcoming, but the restaurant got busy & we were forgotten. The food took longer than expected since the restaurant was fairly empty when we ordered. Maybe our server forgot to put the order in.  In spite of that, we enjoyed everything about this place.
Love the interior, the artwork, cool T-shirts & the entire neighborhood with unique shops & a great vibe. We can't wait to go back!

Got a variety of food & it was very flavorful! The shrimp and grits was amazing! Their chicken and Mac n cheese - so good! Also a fan of their catfish bites. Our server, JC, was very attentive to our large party and had great lively energy!

Finally tried Fixins after letting the buzz die down. Let me qualify my review with the fact that I can cook but my mom was renowned for "putting her foot in it" and on top of it, my nephew/chef was in town visiting, so humbly stated - we have advanced tasting skills.

The vibe and music alone made me want to come back after dark for an adult beverage as we were all bobbing our heads to the 90s jams. I also enjoyed reading about the family/restaurant history. Consistent with other reviews, the fried chicken is a must, juicy with great crunch. The fried fish was just ok; beans and rice are worth a repeat, greens were a little salty and just slightly undercooked; Mac&cheese had great flavor but the cheese was a little grainy (per nephew); charred okra was cooked well and delicious. We finished off with a shared cobbler which was different than our family recipe but still good flavor.

AMAZING!!!! We came on a Saturday around 1pm and it was a 15-20 min wait, not bad! 

Service: great. The waitress came by the table a few times to check on us. We ordered us drinks, but she gave us water too within the first 5 mins of sitting down and food came out in 10 mins?? It was so fast. Plates were HOT so it kept the food warm throughout the meal. She was also proactive and got us boxes to pack leftovers before we had to ask. 

Food: wow. So so good! We don't get soul food too often so we ordered a lot to try. 
- Chicken and waffles were bomb - chicken thighs were a good size, crispy skin, juicy. Waffles were thinner, had alittle crunch but soft inside with a hint of cinnamon. Syrup was thick and rich. Butter was elite. A lot of food for 1.  
- fried catfish - liked this more than we thought. Crispy, light and different. There was tartar sauce and also an aioli and it had a hunt if spice. Went well together. 
- sweetened ice tea - 10/10, are you kidding??? I need this on tap. I like my drinks sweet and this was perfect and came in a large mason. 
- mac & cheese side - not a huge mac n cheese person normally but yum. There were two types of cheeses, tasted cheesey but not too much
- gritz - first timer and it was interesting. Kinda of like porridge/mashed potato texture. Was a good experience for my first time

I'm not normally in this area, but would make the 1.5 hr drive for this!

The food is delicious but the service leaves a lot of be desired. The music sets a great vibe but it took a long time to get our drink order, then we received our main course before our appetizer or drinks. We had to ask for things several times before we got them. Maybe they're just low on personnel. The manager seemed to be trying to take care of a lot and even assisted when I needed something, I just wish our server had the same enthusiasm. 
I'd definitely come back because of the quality of food, but I'd take into account the delay in service.

So. . .this was a random night out for our family. My husband and I have been wanting to try this place but because of life just hadn't got around to it until now. Someone said this was the most consistent soul food in Sacramento so we decided to make the effort to try it (however, I disagree).

One of the things that I did not like was being sat in the very back by the opening of the kitchen and where the servers use the machine to place orders and ring up the bill for their customers. I don't care what business it is, I don't think any tables should be put in those types of spaces. It takes away from the experience when servers congregate near your table while you're eating. The second thing that I think really affected my experience here was receiving dirty dishes with food still stuck to the bottom of them before we even received our food. This grossed me out. It affected me a lot more than I anticipated so I wasn't able to fully enjoy my meal because in the back of my mind, I'm wondering what other dishes are we eating off of or putting in our mouth that are actually still dirty. Additionally, we waited an extremely long time for our food to be served as well as to receive our check. 

The food in my opinion was not that great. There were five people in our party and no one was truly satisfied with their meal. The thing that we liked the most was our drinks that came in large mason jars. That's not enough to keep us coming back so I don't think we will be back to this establishment. We'll see.

This place has a nice ambiance with a full bar and delicious drinks. The staff were all very nice and helpful when it came to choosing menu items. We chose the Pimento cheese dip to start which was delicious! The fried green tomatoes were just as good. I was a bit indifferent when it came to the deep fried egg cups, but my GF loved them. They were a little too dry for me. The chicken (dark meat), grits and Mac & cheese were all tasty. We will definitely be back to try other dishes as I really wanted to try the shrimp and grits!
I love the fried chicken and greens.  Parking can be an issue but parking is always available across broadway.

This is combination review  April 2023 and January 2024.  Having tired fixins on a number of occasions from la and sac I have to say I love the vibe. I love that I can walk in fixins in la or in sac and I'm going to get the same tasting product  they have on hell of a standardized recipes and they stick to it. The service and the food here are both great.   The fried chicken is crispy  seasoned and fried well it's not the best I have had  but it's  good. The greens I love them here that Smokey flavor the pot licker  I could drink by the gallons. The Mac and cheese is cheesy creamy and delicious. The yams are soft rich buttery and sweet. And potato salad is def how I prefer it with mustard and not sweet but tangy. The fried catfish doesn't have the earthy taste that you get sometimes from other spots they do a great job in seasoning and preparing it.  The  fried devil eggs are my favor deviled eggs I don't eat pork so I give the bacon away but the. Crispness of the fried egg whites and the seasoned yolk filling with the hot Sauce is a must have for me. As much as I love the deviled eggs. For me the star of stars here is the oxtails.  Slow cooked. Fall off the bone seasoned  and soaked in that heavenly gravy. That's rich brownish red. It's great  I could eat this by the truck loads. Fixins for me is def the best soul food spot in the bay of u consider sac the bay lol

I don't come out to downtown too often, because of the whole parking situation dilemma almost every time. But, when I do, I always shoot for new places that look amazing. This place was no different, just a random find in the old neighborhood and stomping grounds. 
The food was pretty amazing! Their catfish was crispy on the outside, nice & juicy on the inside. The sandwich combination and the different flavors all balanced very well together. The potato salad was pretty bomb as well and very original. A different spin that took me a minute to put my finger on was their pickles that were pickled in cool aid. That was new, different, unexpected, but, I think I like it!
The service and overall experience were great. They were busy during a lunch rush hour. But, everyone was attentive, helpful, friendly and on top of the whole process. The location, layout and set up of this place is nice. We sat at the bar, which was comfortable and roomy. 
I definitely recommend this place, if you haven't been. Next time I'm in the area, will definitely stop here to try something else on their expansive menu!!

I have been hearing great things about this restaurant and everything I heard was true, since there is always a long wait I decided to go around happy hour time which was perfect and I was able to be seated right away.  The host and server were extremely nice and attentive and the ambiance was chill with 90's R&B playing was 10/10 . 
 
I had the deep dried deviled eggs that were 10/10 and the pork chops with Mac and cheese and yams also 10/10. 

My whole experience was great and  food was superb so I will definitely be back again.

Made an online order because they don't accept phone orders. It makes hella sense because it's poppin in Fixins on a Saturday evening! 

I ordered a 3 piece fried chicken meal with potato salad and red beans and rice. It also came with a biscuit and a sweet butter spread. I added a yella sauce for dipping my pollo. My boo got the shrimp and grits and a side of Mac n cheese. 

After pick up which was quick and simple we went home to indulge. Listen, if my car doesn't smell like the food I just bought I'm pissed! I had no worries with Fixins. My car was filled w delicious aromas! Almost pulled over and ate on the side of the damn road! That's what good food is supposed to do. 

Anywho, got home and plated our meals. My first bite into the chicken was, chefs kiss homie! Sooo good! The red beans and rice were also fire. The potato salad was good as well. The biscuit was biscuiting with that sweet creamy butter! My boo enjoyed every morsel of shrimp and grits! 

Oh, almost forgot. The only meh item was the banana pudding. I happen to love mad cookies layered in my banana pudding and theirs is mostly creamy. Decent flavor. 

All in all peeps. Goooooo. Ruuuun to Fixins. If you're missing your granny, your mom or anyone who would nourish you with good soul food. This is the place for you! And invite me!! 

Truly delish

Well I'll be dreaming of this place until I can't get back again. WOW!!!! Everything we ate absolutely incredible. Seasoned to perfection. Cooked to perfection. Plated to perfection. The fried chicken was so crispy on the outside and really juicy on the inside. Flavor packed through out. The potato salad was the perfect combination of vinegar, touch of sweet, a bit of crunch, nice and creamy. So good! The charred okra was the best okra I've ever eaten. But the STAR of the show was the BISCUIT AND FRUIT BUTTER.  Sweet, savory, flaky, crunchy, pilllowy. I have not one negative thing to say about our experience. The service from our waitress Aimee was above and beyond! She was attentive and so nice! The restaurant itself is tucked into the oak park neighborhood and was packed with locals. It's decorated with memorabilia from the neighborhood and the family that started it.

Delicious fried chicken, and it goes so well with their hot sauce. The biscuit and fixins were incredible. The servers are nice and the restaurant is in such a cute part of Oak Park. I want to try the brunch menu and peach cobbler next!

Came with a large party this past Memorial Day weekend. Most of us, it was our first time there so my only two issues were, the outdoor area not having fans. I was expecting it to be some because it gets real hot in Sac during the summers. We were sweating our butts off. The other issue was bugs flying around or food and drinks as we were eating.
Service was good. Food was good.

I left sorely disappointed my last two visits. 
I ordered shrimp and grits. The grits were literally scraped from the bottom of a pot that had been sitting for a while. The Grits were dry and even had that film they get when they have been sitting for a while. No- one tried to stir them and hide the fact that they were old.
The next and last time I went I ordered the fried eggs. The eggs were rubbery... and tasted sour. I left so disappointed I don't see myself coming back. 
Fixins has really fallen far in my book. 
The next time some suggests Fixins... you know what my answer will be

This place was recommended by a family member and a few others but this place menu caught my attention with all the sides and entree's! Service was great by Kronk and the environment and cleanliness was good! I am def telling all my followers on all platforms! We will be back for sure! I took some pics of what we ate I hope your eyes get full lol everything tasted great and the sauces I just know are homemade!

I'm visiting family in Sacramento and we had dinner here. Wow! What a treat! We started with fried green tomatoes (a family favorite! My grandfather made grew and fried them for me as a kid, great memories!) 

We had drinks hurricane and a daiquiri. Both strong and delicious! So amazing! 

For our entrees, I had shrimp & grits and my cousin had oxtails with rice & sweet potatoes. All of it was INCREDIBLE! So good!! We were so satisfied so we didn't have dessert but it was such a great vibe, our server was sweet and the bar and outdoor dining was real cool. 

This will be my spot always when I visit Sacramento!

I was in town for interviews and was hungry so I decided to look up some restaurants nearby. I found Fixin's soul kitchen and decided to check it out because I love soul food and miss my old go to spot that I don't live near anymore. Upon arrival, the energy in the building was great. The atmosphere was welcoming and comfortable. Everyone from the greeter to the bartender was fantastic with their service. Since I just walked in, I was told that the bar was open and I was by myself so I sat there. Mackenzie was quick and attentive to me. I ordered the oxtails as I wanted to try something new. 
My food came out quickly. I want to say no more than maybe seven minutes. The oxtails were super tender and delicious. Not only was my food delicious but I met the most wonderful woman. Aunt Pearl was greeting every single customer at the bar and she came to me next. She was the sweetest woman and genuinely cared about each of the customers. She was happy that I came in there and that I was trying something new and she also gave me some history about the owner of the restaurant, Oak Park, and a little about the area. Honestly, Aunt Pearl made me feel so welcomed that it made the visit extra special. I would definitely come back again. Thanks, Aunt Pearl!

I had no clue this place existed... I'm so glad we gave it a chance because their food is so good! 

I highly recommend you make a reservation because they are busy!!!! We made a reservation via Open Table and that saved us a 2-hour wait. The staff was great, they were so busy but still managed to take care of us. The place is super clean and super cute. Parking is not that great but walking a block is not bad at all. 

The food was great. I highly recommend the fried green tomatoes and deep fried doubles eggs. They both are delicious. The chicken and waffles... holly cow I mean holy chicken... you have to get them, this was the best fried chicken I've had. The shrimp and grits was good as well, the only thing I didn't like was they left the tails on the shrimp, but that might be a personal preference. 

Will I be back? Absolutely! Do I recommend this place? Absolutely!

Had a craving for some good 'ole soul food before the Kings game...so we popped into Fixins.  First, the customer service was exceptional, from the greeter to the wait staff.  Our waitress was Vanessa and she was soooo sweet and attentive.  She consistently checked on us despite having several other tables to wait on.  Her recommendations did not disappoint either:). The decor is best described as urban with a historical twist - artwork was a play on African american hx and black folk traditions - we loved it!  Even the wait staff's shirts were cute, you all should sell 'em, lots a folks would buy them!

To the food - So, my roots (historically and familial) are born and bred Lousiana and Mississippi...so soul food and Sunday dinners were the traditional gold standard in our family (read everything made from scratch, no boxed nothing, lard, fatback and all parts of pig, traditional gumbo, hot water cornbread, chowchow, red beans & rice...u get the picture, right?!).  So let me start with the fried chicken- seasoned to perfection, crispy, moist and hot!  Okay, Fixins, I see you!!!  (Ps- I did see one patron using a knife and fork to eat her fried chicken‍, baby girl, you gotta get your hands dirty wit fried chicken, dig in, it taste better that way lol)...

The fish was juicy, plump and the cornmeal breading was delicious!  While I am partial to Basa/catfish other snapper (yeah, I know, bottom feeders - but they make for the best fried fish) - Fixins choice for fish was good too:)

The greens and smoked turkey necks were well seasoned, tasty and tender cooked and a hint of sweetness (iykyk), but didn't need the vinegar, not a bad thing-i know alot of us do it to our greens but they dont really need it, just a personal preference:). 

The mac n cheese, I sadly had to pass on.  So the thing about mac n cheese is this, the star is the cheese - so it better come gooey, cheesy and flavorful.  It wasn't horrible, but it had a tangy taste to me (like sour cream or a super sharp cheese was used idk...) and I am not a fan of "cheesy sauce" - I grew up on hand shredded cheese, butta and binders (milk, egg) - so cheesy it bubbles when cooked, has that slight bit of browning on top (ya'll know what im saying lol) and is gooey and stringy when plated!  That is really all u need in a good mac n cheese- so while it wasn't horrible, was my least fav at Fixins. 

While I didn't try the gumbo, it looked good.  I don't eat everyone's gumbo (remember Louisiana roots strong over here), but I will say, the coloring (looked like they started with a good roux) was on point and folks seemed to be enjoying it...maybe next time!

Overall, I really liked Fixins and would have given it 5 stars, but for the macncheese .... so get on over to Fixins, order some fried chicken and sip on some sweet tea or better yet, your favorite flavor of kool aid in a large mason jar (and you betta say red)(and yes, for those of ya'll that don't know, red is a flavor- don't try to understand - just go with it- lol) - Check 'em out, you won't be disappointed!!!

I officially have a new favorite restaurant.  My son and I were in the Sacramento area and knew we wanted soul food. I'm beyond happy we chose Fixins. 

My son loved the catfish. It was a sizeable portion, crispy texture with tender, flaky fish. He couldn't stop raving about the Mac-n-cheese. I tasted it and decided to get extra to go.

The Hot Chicken Sandwich was a definite experience in flavor. Just layers of perfection. Soft bun, followed by a flavorful sauce, crunchy slaw and that bite of crispy, juicy, perfectly spiced chicken. I kid you not, it was the best chicken sandwich I've eaten in my life. I very rarely eat fried foods, but I am extremely happy I did today. I cannot say enough about this sandwich. Oh yes, yummy pickles within the sandwich as well.

The lemonade was just like I would've wanted, nice tangy lemon with a hint of sweetness, it was just right. 

The restaurant is just aesthetically pleasing, gorgeous. They are meticulously clean, which was very nice. The service was a 5-star experience as well, everyone was courteous. Our server was great!

We left Fixins very happy, with full bellies and a promise to return soon. As my son said, "I would drive out here just to have brunch." I agreed!

On the road starving and wanted something to eat on the drive back home .Very disappointed with their to go oxtails. At $25 per plate I was expecting a good portion. There was literally no rice underneath the oxtails. I get that the whole pandemic has been hard for a lot of businesses but people want consistency and rice is cheap so why skimp on rice especially when you're having oxtail ?! 

I couldn't even eat my food since there was barely any rice and it's pretty salty eating it alone. The oxtails was a bit underwhelming and didn't have the taste like it's been cooking for hours. It almost tasted like adobo. Wasn't bad but not exactly what I was expecting. I don't think I'll ever be ordering oxtails to go again from this place. 

Also if you're ordering to go and need utensils double check your bags since they don't put any utensils in there.

Just walking up to Fixin's I could feel this was going to be a great experience. We were greeted in the front by a friendly group of team members who greeted us right away. 

Chandra was our server, and she was kind, giving us great customer service. She recommended a few appetizers, and the fried deviled eggs really grabbed my attention. Perfectly fried and seasoned, topped with crispy bacon. 

For the entrees, we ordered the two piece chicken and waffles, topped with strawberry butter. The waffles were so delicious with the strawberry butter I didn't need syrup! The chicken was perfectly seasoned inside and out. Also added sides of candied yams and Mac n cheese, both were on point!

I get excited when I try a new place and they get everything right. Looking forward to coming back with the whole family soon!

This place is so good! The watermelon margarita is delicious! One of my favorites! The mixture of the tequila, kool aid and salt is perf! I ordered the fried chicken, cheesy grits and Mac and cheese. Bombbbbbbbb! I definitely recommend!!

Fixens is Fire!! Every time we step foot in the door. From the customer service to the food our mouth is on fire with the great southern taste of the best soul food in town. 
Whether it's the fried chicken; Mac and cheese; greens and yams or the brown oxtails nestled with the savory brown gravy. You're in for a treat of goodness!! 
Go get that plates! Cause it Fireplace!

I've never been more disappointed in my life. I ordered a gumbo with shrimp to-go and it came with just three pieces of shrimp, one slice of sausage, and a spoonful of rice, with no chicken in sight. And this cost $28. The soup also tasted like it came straight out of a Campbell's soup can.

Some good soul food never hurt nobody.
If you're ever in the neighborhood, FIXINS should be top of your list. This place is affordable and elite! I came here on a whim and I am so glad. It literally is so good. Too bad they open at 11am! I would love to come for breakfast.

I came on my day off on a Monday and it was still busy! Business is great!

I got a few items to share with friends and I also picked at their plates.
Sweet tea - top tier! 
Kool aid - oh you know it!

Fried Cat fish - $21 pricier than what I am used to, but the batter is good and seasoned.

Chicken and waffles - you can pick from 2 wings and a waddle - $9, to just chicken or lots of chicken with a waffle. Some meals come with fixins(sides)

I got the 2 piece Fried chicken - served with two fixins and a biscuit for $17. You can't get those prices elsewhere. I got mac n cheese and fries. Wow. Just yumm. I picked off my friends coleslaw (eh), candied yams(good but I was not in the mood. Reminds me of Thanksgiving), and Potato salad (the best, and I mean it, the best I've ever had) They need to send me the recipe. 

I also got to try the oxtails which were really good. Priced at $26, comes with gravy, rice and a fixin. 

The service is amazing. They have great attitude and check in very often. This place is def worth checking out.

I took my family here for the first time and we LOVED it. Why have we wasted so much of our lives by not coming here sooner? Not only was the food absolutely incredible and made with such love but the service was outstanding. Justin was our server and he was so knowledgeable about the food and offered excellent recommendations. Our food came out quickly and we never were without drinks. Justin was hilarious and very personable. We have other dinner plans this weekend and will ABSOLUTELY be coming back to eat with other family then. As well as requesting to sit in Justin's section. We love you Fixins!!!!.

First time here after having this recommended to me a few times. 
So I decided to check it out with the bestie and while the wait was a pinch long they sat us at a comfy booth and Aimee our waitress took care of us till we were out the door.
We started with the fried green tomatoes and mason jars of kool-aid 
For our entrees we went with the fried cat fish and chicken and waffles plus ms Ruth's plate of three sides(we did the Mac & cheese, red beans & rice and sweet potatoes).
And finished off our meal with the not too sweet peach cobbler a la mode. All in all a 10 across the board. Although the kool-aid was TAD overly sweet but really that was such a minor thing compared to everything else. 

Definitely will return again.

We wanted to get some Soul food and found this place highly recommended.  We came on a Friday afternoon and it was busy!  We managed to get a few dishes and all of them were excellent.  They were braised oxtail, fried green tomato, biscuits, Mac and cheese, coleslaw, and fish nuggets.  Out of all those, the oxtail was totally worth it.  It was not too salty and the rice soaked up the juice.  I managed to eat some oxtail cold the next day and was still great.  Too bad we don't live close enough to come more often!

This is for their take out: 

The wait was 1.5 hours so we just decided to get it to go. My fiance ordered the fried chicken, which I was leary about him doing because sometimes when you pack fried food it gets to the destination soggy due to the condensation and the restaurant not letting it cool down enough. However, we got home and the skin was still crispy and delish.

I ordered the oxtail and it was so good and gelatinous. Very tender and tasty. For sides we had collard greens, coleslaw and red beans/rice. All were good but I think I would pass on the collard greens. We were stuffed and were able to save some of our food for a snack/small lunch for the following day. We definitely want to try dine in next time. Nothing like fresh hot fried chicken from the fryer.

On another note, parking can be crummy and though the neighborhood is rehabilitated it still has some sketchy blocks so just don't leave your valuables in sight.

Came here before a lakers game with a reservation. Still had to wait about 5-10 mins for a table for two. My friend ordered the gumbo and I ordered the fried chicken. The fried chicken was nice and crispy on the outside and juicy on the inside. The fries were well seasoned and crispy too. I also ordered the Mac and cheese on the side which was a little bland. Food was excellent and service was friendly. They also have a good selection of cocktails.  We ordered a flight of mezcal. They have lots of tvs too for keeping tabs on the games.
Food was excellent, the server managed our rowdy group of nine with patience and attention to detail.  Great night!

my boy and I arrived in Sacramento at this beautiful charming little restaurant called Fixins. We were first met by the beautiful aroma of good southern soul food. When we entered a charming little atmosphere. We were greeted by a wonderful hostess and we're guided towards the good eats on the menu, including fried green tomatoes, cornbread, fried chicken, pork chops you name it, my boy, and I ordered a sampling of everything, including the greens, macaroni and cheese and sweet tea. It was a wonderful meal. I recommend this place highly and would suggest that you go in for a visit even if it's just to get the deviled eggs.

This place is definitely going to be one of my go to spots ! It is delicious! Normally it is super packed , like wait 2 hours packed but I guess if you go in the early week days they aren't ! Honestly it is a good ol southern hit your soul type in place. The menu is not huge but you don't need it ! You here for the fried chicken! 

What we decided on was the fried chicken smothered in gravy , with potato salad and Mac n cheese! And the 3 piece dark fried chicken with fries and Mac. The food came out pretty fast ! Man the flavors in everything was definitely made from the soul! Chicken was crispy and moist ! The gravy had tones of flavor. The Mac n cheese went crazy and so did the potato salad! 

Y'all need to try it for real !

Fixins Soul Kitchen: A Soulful Experience in Sacramento

Fixins Soul Kitchen in Sacramento is more than just a restaurant; it's a full-fledged experience that tantalizes the taste buds and warms the heart. Housed within a charming brick building with a distinctly urban vibe, Fixins offers an atmosphere as inviting as its incredible southern cuisine.

My recent visit was nothing short of phenomenal. From the moment we stepped inside, the friendly staff made us feel welcome and at ease. Their enthusiasm for the food and genuine hospitality elevated the entire food experience (we picked our order up but will definitely be returning to dine-in).

Now, onto the main course: the food. It's impossible to talk about Fixins without raving about their culinary creations. We started with the fried green tomatoes, a dish that perfectly balanced tangy and savory flavors. The crispy exterior gave way to juicy, perfectly ripe tomatoes, leaving us wanting more.

Next up were the cheesy grits, which should be considered a mandatory order for any first-time visitor. The creamy, decadent grits were infused with just the right amount of cheese, creating a dish that was both comforting and addictive.

For the main course, we opted for the chicken tenders, which were juicy and flavorful, boasting a perfectly seasoned golden-brown crust. The chicken and waffles, a true southern staple, did not disappoint. The fluffy, golden waffles provided the perfect platform for the tender, flavorful chicken, creating a textural and taste bud explosion.

No southern meal is complete without sweet tea, and Fixins' rendition was a perfect complement to the savory dishes. Refreshing and perfectly sweetened, it was the ideal way to cleanse the palate between bites and tie the entire meal together.

While everything about Fixins was exceptional, my only minor suggestion is for them to improve their online ordering system. We attempted to order through the Yelp app, but certain menu items, like the cheesy grits, were unavailable. This minor inconvenience led us to their website, where we were able to place our order successfully.

Despite this small hiccup, Fixins Soul Kitchen deserves a resounding 5-star review (and plenty of bonus points from a true southern native). From the welcoming atmosphere and friendly staff to the phenomenal food and reasonable prices, Fixins offers a dining experience that is truly unforgettable. If you're searching for authentic southern cuisine in Sacramento, look no further than Fixins Soul Kitchen. You won't be disappointed.

We have been to this restaurant a few times and had great experiences. However, this experience was terrible. Our server, Nadia I believe, was MIA the entire time and no help at all. We had to talk to multiple other servers in order to get any help at all. This is one of the worst experiences I have had from a server ever before.  She ruined the entire experience.

Wow. Why didn't I come here sooner? Service and food was AMAZING. We ordered the chicken and waffles with a side of candied yams and the oxtails with a side of Mac and cheese. For the price, they give you generous portions. Their drink menu has so much variety for alcoholic and non-alcoholic drinks. One of Sacramento's best restaurants easily.

Came here last weekend and had a pleasant experience overall.  The chicken was seasoned well.  The service was fast and friendly.  The cornbread was awesome.  The fries had a nice crisp to them and their signature hot sauce was great as well.  The ambiance was kind of loud and the prices were a little on the higher side in my opinion but I still enjoyed my visit.  I'll have to try their signature cocktails next time.
Great service and food!!!  Fried chicken is very juicy!  And the biscuits.....delicious!

1-Blacked eyed peas too salty, 
 2-Fried green tomato's small portions
3- Out of vegan greens 
4-Catfish was undercooked.
5-Fried okra was not prepared properly.

Been meaning to try this place and am so glad I finally did! Everything was tasty and full of flavor. We got the chicken and waffles, shrimp and grits, and mac and cheese. Highly recommend both! Also, tried their sweet tea-- it was extra sweet and reminded me of all the teas I devoured back when I lived in the south. Will definitely be back here to try more of their food. Service was great. The staff was all very welcoming. Lots of indoor and outdoor seating. Gets packed during peak hours.

Plenty of street parking available.

Just dropping off a quick review to say the food is still really good!
I have previous reviews that can be checked out as well. I got the fried chicken with potato salad and candied yams (which is starting to become a fav) and a sweet tea.
So definitely stop by here if you're thinking about it.

{Ig: Oohdearfoodie}

Great find! Scrumptious tastings I couldn't get enough! Fried green tomatoes are OUT OF THIS WORLD! Roasted okra = unbelievable! Whether you are resident or visitor to Sacramento area this is a must to your restaurant visits.  Customer service outstanding - feels like having a meal with close family and friends! Ms Pearl checks on you regularly - so much love and kindness in this place! Thank you for a great experience!

Man the Oxtails are divine, we also had fried green tomatoes,  fish nuggets , smothered pork chop with mac & cheese & yams everything was on point. The highlight was Auntie Pearl coming around checking on everyone.  10 of 10 recommend!

I rarely crave fried chicken, but whenever I do, I want the entire FIXINS. No pun intended ;)

What we ordered for lunch:
- Smothered Chicken Tender: chicken smothered in brown gravy with two sides of your choice. I chose mac and cheese and cheesy grits
- 2 pieces fried chicken (dark) and a waffle

I could not decide which one was tastier... they were both out of this world. The chicken was sooooo tender. Batter was light and crispy. The waffle was one of the BEST tasting waffles I have ever had. It tasted like it had brown sugar in it. A LOT of it, but I ain't complaining. All of the sides were rich and creamy; my favorite was the mac and cheese.

I wish we ordered more to try, but we were so happy and stuffed after the meal! Will be back for sure.

Definitely a great place to get an adult cool aide, some fried chicken and tasty Mac and Cheese! We started off with the fried deviled eggs and fried green tomatoes.  Then had our other dishes listed above with the delicious shrimp gumbo! Highly recommend checking out!

It's been so long since I've genuinely felt compelled to leave a review but this place needs to get its service together. Asked for the estimated wait, was told 30min for a table for two. An hour later, our name on the waitlist mysteriously disappeared and we had to show text proof to get seated. Unhelpful host who couldn't be bothered to make the experience any better.

Food is fine although pretty salty, but next time just order takeout.

I went there today, September 27, 2024. I will start with the ambience, great ambience, great atmosphere,  very comfortable. The food was fantastic. I was there with my sister and my niece. My sister had oxtails with rice and gravy and candied yams,. My niece had smothered chicken with rice and gravy, and I had catfish with potato salad and yams. Prior to ordering the potato salad I was a little leery because not everybody makes good potato salad, but this was actually very good and I was pleasantly surprised. The yams were great and not too sweet. My sister marveled over the oxtails and my niece enjoyed her meal totally l. We also had appetizers,which were deep fried mac & cheese balls, and I had beef sliders, ALL were delicious! 
Now for our waitress, Cat. She exuded great customer service. She was very patient with us and very helpful with us, in helping us choose our meals and our appetizers. I give her 5+ stars. She was phenomenal! She also hugged us when we were leaving. Thank you Cat for making our day so extra special..

With the high ratings, I came with high hopes, yet left a bit disappointed.  Fried chicken and oxtails are two of my faves, and since both of these were on the menu, Fixins was a no brainer for lunch.

The high point here is definitely their Fried Chicken.  The batter is really good and it's nicely deep fried.  Apparently, the chicken is brined, but I couldn't tell.  That's totally fine with me though because I don't typically like brined meats.  Originally, I got just a piece on the side, but it was so good that I got an extra couple of pieces to go.  It's fried to-order so it takes a while, but it's worth the wait.  It comes out really hot and juicy.  It's not inexpensive though at $15 for just two pieces.  I wish I ended it here.

The Oxtails though were a huge let down.  The "sweet garlic brown gravy" was overly sweet and overpowering.  Not my jam.  I was even more disappointed just a few minutes ago before writing this review when I saw a Yelp photo of the oxtails served as good sized chunks.  Mine were definitely much smaller pieces and some pieces were just shredded.  I took most of it home and then ended up tossing it.  I wouldn't at all mind paying $22.00 for a plate of amazing oxtails, but these weren't that.

Service was just ok.  It wasn't overly busy, yet I had to ask for everything. 

The fried chicken itself is excellent.  I'll come back for that.  I just wish everything else was as good.

Holy cow Batman!!!
First off had super service by Jasmine and then had the perfect meal .
Smothered chicken w/ red beans and rice and some mac n cheese , fried catfish and some grits ... oh if you do come in the PURPLE KOOL AID , turns you back to your childhood ... awesome and my new go to for some meals when in the area. THANK YOU JASMINE!!!!!!

There really aren't even words... let's just say I ate my meal with my eyes closed dancing! That's how I felt when I ate this food! I had the shrimp and grits and they were unbelievable!! My husband had the smothered chicken with Mac and cheese and the grilled okra.... Wooooowwwwww. It truly was the most amazing food we've had. We've travelled many places in California and had soul food in San Francisco many times... nothing comes close to the Fixins. I'm going to be talking about and thinking about this food for a looooong time. No questions about this place it's a 20/10.

Fixins got it all right from the ambiance and food to service. Family was craving some chicken and waffles which was sooo good at Fixins. Chicken was seasoned and fried well. Waffle wasn't too thick or soggy or too hard. And cornbread was delish too! Will definitely come back if I'm in the area. Highly recommend!

We made reservations because we knew they would be busy & glad we did cause it was packed! Service was quick & we got our food 10-15 mins later.
