They had the game on & the music was blasting, loced the environment, it just felt inviting! We ordered 2 piece (Dark), cornbread, biscuit, fries, mac & cheese & yams. SO GOOD! The chicken was crispy & very juicy! I look for that when finding the best fried chicken & they nailed it! I loved all their sides but mac & cheese was fire! We had their strawberry lemonade & kool aid this time it was sweet & refreshing!! Big jugs, that we ended up taking it home!

Yes, yes and yes! Excellent food...very flavorful, Excellent customer service and excellent atmosphere. This place never disappoints...ALL the appetizers are good. Portions will have you full with leftovers, kool-aid will bring you back to your childhood and its not too sweet. Mac n cheese is cheesy. The fried green tomatoes are my favorite along with fish nuggets and then artichoke dip..had a kick. Fav main dish is shrimp n grits...with sides..the grits aren't gritty,  any side is good. My fav is the Mac, Yams, greens and blk Eye peas. Desserts are good as well. Always lively...and there are always large groups. Luckily I've never had to wait. Parking seems limited.

I've come here twice now and both times the food was outstanding! We ordered for take-out, but the service was great for pick up. We had the shrimp & grits and beans & rice. The ingredients seemed fresh with lots of flavor. It's a good thing I don't live closer because I would spend all my money there. Needless to say I would highly recommend this place!

Such a great experience! Luckily we got there about 20 min before 11am, when the open, and there were already hella people outside waiting. Our waitress said it's never been that busy before. Also, while you're filling your stomachs with their good food, you soul is also being uplifted with their gospel music in the background. 

They have a full bar, inside booths, and outdoor tables. You can either request for a menu or scan the menu with the QR code. Everyone is polite and professional. It was hard not to get multiple things on the menu! My friend got the catfish with greens and mac and cheese while I ordered the oxtail w/ mac and cheese. Then deviled eggs to-go. Everything was beyond perfection! The catfish was extra crispy but the fish was still moist and not dried out. The greens had great flavor. The oxtail was fall off the bone and the sauce had a little sweetness to it. Mac and cheese was bomb too. The deviled eggs were lightly coated with some bread crumbs and fried just a little bit to add that cripsy texture to it. MAN! 

I wish they open more locations, tbh. But this is definitely a go to spot in Sac. I will not stop raving about this joint.

YES.

For a place that is only operating take-out and outside dining during a pandemic, they still managed to give you a warm and hospitable experience :)

GRAPE BUTTER.  Did I hear that right?  Yes!  Look it's what you didn't know you needed in your life.  A little splash of sweetness with a tint of lavender hue to your biscuit.  Speaking of!!!  Girl, boy, man, woman, human!  The biscuit is the one.  Thick, fluffy, dense, moist (I know we hate that word, but in this review, we most def appreciate it), and everything one might want in a perfect biscuit.  Whew.  Give me another.  I should've ordered an extra few on the side.  

The two pieces of chicken I ordered were dark meat - drumstick and a thigh, my faves.  Crispy, juicy, hot (temperature wise) and nicely seasoned.  Got my packets of Crystal hot sauce and BBQ sauce and I was set!  I ate all this in the car bc I'm still weary of dining in and I'm so damn glad I did.  It was the perfect comfort meal after a crazy week at work.  

The collard greens were good with pieces of turkey to assist in the flavoring.  The mac and cheese was nice and dense, just the way I like it and had a good cheese pull to them.

I can see why this place has such a high rating.  If I received such a wonderful experience taking out, I can only imagine dining in!

The banana pudding is a delight and was the perfect ending to my much needed comfort meal.

Can't wait to be back xo

Eat here! It's delicious. 
Southern comfort food. 

Experience: 
The lines get long after church gets out on Sunday so get there early so you don't have to wait in line!!!! 
Parking is not the easiest either once it starts getting busy so make sure to plan accordingly. 

FOOD: 
Deviled eggs - FRIED EGGS! never had deviled eggs before but they were delicious!! Garnished with bacon and a perfect portion for a bite. 

Fried Catfish - 
Crispy, juicy, comes with 2 sauces - ones like a thousand island-ish sauce and the other is like a tartar sauce.  Deliciousssss. 

OXTAIL dish
Comes with rice, comes with your choice of a side. 
Rice is cooked well, the oxtail is melt in your mouth. WARNING* this dish can feed 2 people for sure if you're having appetizers. After I was done eating it looked like I didn't touch my plate at all!! Great problem to have haha. 

My side: MAC n CHEESE
this is the best Mac and cheese I've had in a very long time. It warms up well also if you save any for left overs! 

Coffee: 
Coffee was yummy, good temperature and our server was awesome with the refills! Thank you Fixins! 

SURROUNDING AREA:
If you go for brunch there are some shops on the corner of the street if you walk towards bank of the west's direction. 
The area wasn't the safest neighborhood but has now been developed to look really nice.  definitely will come back during Sunday Brunch!
Excellent food (fried chicken) and service. It's a must stop if you're in Oak Park. Get the Mac and Cheese on the side, you will not be disappointed.

It was a blessing to find some good soul to pick up after our souls were filled up at a local church service. 

The chicken was probably one of the best I've ever had. The biscuits were homemade pillows of soft dough and the grape jelly I read about was worth trying. 

The hot chicken sandwich was oh so HOT. Fire  in your mouth but really huge and tasty. 

Sides ordered:
Greens 5 stars -loved the flavor
Mac n cheese 2.5 stars - too greasy and the cheese was curdling up in between the macaroni
Red beans and rice 1.5 stars - beans seemed hard and could have been cooked longer in my opinion. Not as tasty as some I've had at other places.
Pickle was a surprising sweet and salty add 

We opted to order takeout due to the wait. The only downside was the orders are managed through GrubHub and I didn't receive any notification of the food being ready. It had given me a 30-60 min wait time but when I came to pick up around 45 mins after I ordered, the food was on the old side.

Staff was nice. They a good pickup system.

Loved how they were playing gospel music in the restaurant. 

Will return to sample more items.

We had a party of four and it took about 20 minutes in the afternoon on a Sunday to get seated- which is not bad for such a busy and bustling place! Our server was so sweet and the food was INCREDIBLE. I ordered the fish plate and a Bloody Mary. The koolaid pickle was yummy. I lived in North Carolina and can say wholeheartedly this food was definitely authentic southern.

fantasitic food, service and ambiance for sure. Go here if you like your southern grandma's cooking. I had the smothered pork chops, collard greens and potato salad. yum yum. the fried chicken my wife had was delish as well. go here, walk away completely happy.

This restaurant is in a great location off of broadway, beautiful inside. Very friendly staff made us feel right at home when we walked in.  Our waitress was wonderful as well. aunt Pearl gave us a nice history of the restaurant.   We got the fried deviled eggs, fried chicken, macaroni and cheese, French fries and okra.  Everything was deeeeeelicious !!    Generous portions.  I also tried the adult kool and and really liked it.  We WILL be back.

Wow! I am not a fan of fried chicken. I went with a group after a show Sunday evening to Fixins. I really didn't want greasy chicken and almost ordered a burger. After seeing chicken and waffles I said I will try that since I like waffles. Wow! I order 3 breast 2 waffles! Way too much food! Oh but not to worry I gladly took home one piece of chicken. The waffles had cinnamon butter and they were huge! On top was the juiciest non greasy yet crispiest breast I have ever had! I wanna go back asap!  The service was 5/5 stars.
Amazing food.  I am definitely planning to go back!  Order in advance and sat at a table.  It was 10 of us and every single person was happy with  what they ordered!

The shrimp & grits were ok, the shrimp was a bit undercooked. My partner got the fried catfish, mac n cheese and red beans & rice. The mac n cheese had shredded cheese that wasn't fully melted on the top and had no flavor. Fried catfish and red beans and rice were bomb!

The food was tasty, but not exceptional. My review is relative to the service. 

This past Saturday (June 7), I joined my family at Fixins for brunch; it was my first visit. We were a large party requiring a booth and a table. I say that to possibly explain the inordinate wait for our food after ordering (although I've been to plenty of restaurants with large parties, and having far more complex menus than fried chicken and sides, that got food out relatively quickly). I was the first to order because of where I was seated. I ordered the veggie omelet and requested that chicken be added in. They offer a chicken omelet, but I preferred the add-ins in the veggie omelet. When our food finally arrived, I was the last person served (an omelet!) and after that long wait, the chicken hadn't been added. The server (who was very pleasant) assured me she told the kitchen to add chicken; she asked if she could bring it to me on the side or if I'd rather they remake the omelet. Obviously, something being cooked into a dish would taste different than if an ingredient was served separately (imagine the turkey necks being left out of the collards and then served on the side). I was a little miffed, but I didn't want to make a big deal about it nor wait for Heaven only knew how long when everyone else was already eating. It took about 15 minutes to get the chicken, while my omelet got cold. The person who brought it, who was behind me, sat the saucer on the corner of the table and said, "I'm very sorry." I turned around to acknowledge him and he had already walked away. His so-called "apology" was not at all sincere. Were it not for the company and it being a beautiful day, I would not have enjoyed myself. But everyone's food looked good and I told myself mistakes happen and I'd give the restaurant another try.

Today, (two days later) I decided to order takeout from Fixins for dinner. I ordered two pieces of chicken, the collards and fries. Since a biscuit was included, I also ordered the grape butter (I'd never heard of it and it intrigued me) and both the chipotle mayo and blue cheese dressing for dipping my fries. Each item was listed on the ticket. When I got home, there was no butter (which the dry biscuit needed) nor either of the dips (I did get two packets of hot sauce, which I neither wanted nor asked for). These are service issues; for me, great service is almost as important as the quality and taste of the food. If a restaurant provides great service, it can compensate for a mistake every once in a while. I wanted to speak to management about this rather than leave a negative review, but when I called, the phone just rang and rang--another service issue. BTW, the guy at the pickup counter when I arrived wasn't particularly friendly. There won't be a third strike for me.

Everything was great. The cornbread is  sooooo good 

Black Eyed peas super great
Candied Yams perfect

The service was impeccable

Soul food is alive and well in Sacramento!

We came here for brunch on a Sunday 30 mins after they opened at 11:30a. This was our first time here. During COVID-19 times there was no dining inside the restaurant. There were a lot of delivery and takeout orders and only the patio seating was available for dining in. We went to the hostess and she asked us if we were okay with bar seating out on the patio and we said yes so we were seated right away. 

The friendly bartender took our order. Hubby and I decided we'd share an order of the Smothered Pork Chop. The meal came with 2 fried pork chops smothered in gravy and pickled onions and choice of two Fixins in which we chose French fries and potato salad. That was it, one entree, we were good with that. 20 mins... 30 mins... the bartender was apologetic and gave us a complimentary serving of Mac & Cheese while we waited. 45 mins... 55 mins passed... by this time bartender said that he will give us dessert on the house. Finally, 58 mins later our food was served. From the time we ordered to the time we got our food -- one hour! The bartender was so nice about it though serving Mac & Cheese while we waited and the Peach Cobbler was free.

Anyway, the pork chops were tasty and delicious and made everything better. Yes, I completely forgot about the wait after a bite of that pork chop. When we arrived at the restaurant the patio was completely full... apparently, we were told that once in a while sometimes they would get a car club and also a motorcycle club dine in the restaurant. By the looks of the cars parked outside the club was a Corvette Club. By the time we finished and left there were a lot of people waiting outside to be seated. Super popular place.

Fixins is located in the Oak Park section of Sacramento. And, no, we did not meet owner, Kevin Johnson (nicknamed "KJ" and former NBA player as well as former Mayor of Sacramento). He takes his family to church on Sunday mornings.

Review #2140

Once we finally got seated, the service was great. Our waiter was attentive to the needs of our large party. As far as the food, it was ok. I previously said I wouldn't go back to Fixins, but it was where the birthday party was held. It's not "bad," it's just not worth the trip from the East Bay.

On Monday, 3/18/24, we came in for a family dinner with my Mom, sister, and me.  I decided that we'd go to Fixins based on my 3/9/20 visit.
I mentioned that it was a birthday when I made the reservation. 
Not one mention of the birthday.
We came in before another party. I don't know whether they had reservations, but they were seated before us. 
Kevin Johnson sat at the head of the long table facing the entry, watching all of this. 
The very pricey food was meh!!!
Four years later, you would think there would be an improvement, as I recall, it was very good 
I don't need to ever go there again
Food was great and greeter was very friendly and informing! He knew exactly how much oxtail comes with the plate! Great experience definitely will be back!!!

3rd time here and I swear it gets better and better every time. From the food to the service. Today, our server was Alizae. Super cute young lady, soft and sweet spoken, attentive, great customer service, engaging and knows her food! We had the dark meat fried chicken and braised oxtail with red beans & rice, collard greens with turkey necks, grilled seasoned okra with bacon sprinkles, catfish nuggets and buttery biscuit with strawberry whipped butter. 

INCREDIBLE!!!!!!!! AGAIN & AGAIN & AGAIN!!!! THANK YOU!

Fried Chicken: Crispy, crunchy and flavorful on the outside, juicy, moist and buttery on the inside. Very well seasoned!

Braised Oxtail: Abundant in portion, perfectly seasoned to eat with rice, melts in your mouth, so delicious I used my hands and licked my fingers!!! And Alizae, our waitress said, "Yes! That's how you're supposed to eat it! Suck out all the meat and juices!" Lol I agree

Catfish nuggets: Don't get me started, i've said multiple times already that I would literally even just come here for this dish! Super crunchy on the outside and pops in your mouth as you eat it because the fish inside is so soft white moist and buttery!!!

Just GO ALREADY!!!!

Oh my gosh, this is amazing. This maybe the best southern food we have ever had. I had the chicken and waffles, and although the waffles could have been crisper, the waffles had cinnamon in them, and I stop couldn't eating them. The chicken was crisp, and delicious. The greens were about the best I have ever had, including my own. My husband had the catfish, hush puppies, and red beans and rice. He ate everything as his eyes rolled back in delight. We also tried the Kool aid pickles that were surprisingly good. All on all, we will be back soon!!!

Food Immaculate - Service Unforgettable - Playlist has all the vibes

I've been to Fixins before and the food is always *chefs kiss* 

This visit our server just caught my attention. Nataya is just THAT girl who goes above and beyond. She made me feel like I was her family. She checked on our table so many times to make sure we were satisfied. She apologized for any wait (which was only about 2 minutes) and stood by the table to ask if there was anything else we need (which unfortunately we were that table that kept asking for things when she asked) but she was being a good sport about it. It just felt so good to have someone care about our experience as much as she has. Anyone in the food service industry can take notes or if she offers classes, just to learn how to make sure that your consumers are being treated like people.

Excited to look out for her the next time we visit.

Our table shared the family meal (8 pieces of chicken- 4 biscuits- two large sides: Mac and cheese + candied yams)

Vegan greens were great. The "catfish" that is listed on the menu was absolutely NOT catfish though! Very disappointed in discovering this as they used to serve catfish and hope that they update the menu. 

As soon as the breading falls off you can see the smooth white fish -- seems like frozen Swai or Tilapia.  Other reviews mention the same thing

Went here on a Friday night and there was a bit of a wait for our party of four. The wait was about 45 minutes so we walked around the surrounding area, but it overall felt like it was a safe and hip area. 

Once we were texted that our table was ready, we got seated in a booth. Each table was occupied, but no one was at the bar and the tables were adequately spaced out. Staff wore masks and were very helpful and attentive. I was even surprised when I asked the table what kind of butter was on my order and our waitress heard and answered despite facing away from us a few feet away with the music playing! 

I ordered the 3-pieces of fried chicken and two waffles. You get to pick what parts of the chicken you'd like for the pieces. My food came out a bit later than everyone else in my party but our waitress explained that my chicken was hot and it was indeed freshly fried. The chicken could have used a little bit more seasoning but it was crispy and hot. The butter was purple because from what I remember it was made with grape? But regardless it paired really well with the 3 packets of syrup they gave me (I only ended up using one). My partner got the hot chicken sandwich and he also enjoyed it. They included fries and a kool-aid pickle for his order. Water also came with ice in large mason jars so we didn't need water refills. A bit pricy but worth it for the taste, portion, atmosphere, and service. 

They also were willing to split the check in two. Bathrooms were clean, and we didn't feel rushed to finish our dinner and head out. Overall a fun environment with yummy foods!

Originally had plans to eat at Meet & Eat, but because of the long wait times my wife made a last minute change for us to eat here at Fixins Soul Kitchen.

I ordered the blue Breakfast Plate, it was supposed to be 3 eggs on this plate, but I only received 2. They were cooked wrong. The country potatoes were good. The bacon was over cooked. 

I ordered coffee, but didn't get it until half way through my visit. It was basic coffee.

Staff was friendly. You can tell they run a tight organized ship. 

Parking was lacking. If you see a spot take it and walk. Don't waste precious gas circling. 

I would only go back if my wife made me go.
Exceptional Southern Food! It is a quaint little restaurant off broadway with a really nice patio. The food is excellent. A great way to spend any weekend morning.

My favvvvorite place to eat at in sac. The lady checked in with us consistently & was very accommodating. When I say I could eat this ALL the time I mean it. Everything was a hit, seasoning was on point. My friend got the oxtails we could barely move after we left the restaurant lol. The portions are super generous & worth every penny. There is literally nothing that taste bad here. I also got a cocktail with Hennessy, super good as well. The chicken stayed consistently crispy even after being doused in gravy. They're always quick and the restaurant is always clean.

I went with some friends on a Wednesday around 2pm and it was pretty slow inside. They weren't busy at all and we were seated promptly and our orders were taken rather quickly. The ambiance inside is very homey and comfortable. I like the warm lighting which gives a more calming vibe. Our food took a while to come out, I would say around 20-30 minutes after placing our orders. So that's the only minor inconvenience I have to say. I ordered the spicy chicken sandwich and it was very good. The chicken breast itself is thick and perfectly seasoned. On the buns there were two sauces and I think it made the sandwich too moist, so after a few bites it just fell apart. The flavor was amazing though. My vegan friend really enjoyed her tofu and the fried okra (I think that's what they have). My other friend loved their fried chicken and took some home with her. I would come back whenever I'm craving authentic soul food.

This food is beyond good. Everything is seasoned perfectly. I mean everything ordered was DELICIOUS!! Oxtails. Amazing. Okra. Amazing. Red beans and rice. Amazing. Yams. Amazing. Catfish bites. Amazing. Greens. Amazing. Peach cobbler. ABSOLUTELY FREAKING AMAZING. Highly recommend. Good thing I don't live in Sacramento because I would be at this place multiple times a week. Please open a location in Dallas!!!

Came here with a friend after hitting the show. It was late. Chicken was a bit on the bland side and I had a better waffle. The fried green tomatoes were just okay. Could of used a bit more of the aoli that comes with it but I wasn't a fan of the batter they used. Overall even my friend agreed that this place is over hyped. Our server was great though but the food was giving very much mid. I'm Try for yourself but I don't have to come back.

The vibe of Fixins is dope.
On game days make a reservation or you might be late to the event.
Oxtail is dope, and the alcohol is a perfect mix of flavor and good stuff

The food here feels like a hug. It's the most comfy comfort food I have had! I really like the fried chicken, biscuit and Mac and cheese. I just wanted more by the end of it! This place is a little on the pricier side... dishes 14-20, but it's worth a splurge once in awhile. 

Outdoor dining in the tent, but the make the ambiance nice and inviting. 1 hour wait on Saturday night! Make sure to join the waitlist on Yelp before you go! Street parking but free.

If you want comfort food. Go here. 

From service to menu selection to ambience. 
This place has it all.
This is where southern tradition meets Soul food cookin!
Last time we went there, the line was out the door and down the block. Everyone waiting for a table. This lil place is just right if you want you some fried chicken, or some blackened okra. Or maybe you have a mind to taste some Jambalaya. Whatever you have a mind to get into, this is right where you want to go. And don't forget to order up some fried green tomatoes while you are at it. Yum!!
They even have a vegan menu now.

Also 4 different flavors of koolaid. Go on now, get some!

Good location in Sacramento but parking is horrible and that is if you can find a spot. We usually order online and pick up our food to eat at the hospital since we are there so much. The food is great and they get the order correct. They did only give me 1 syrup for my waffles

Came here with very high expectations and was craving some delicious food to fill my soul. We ordered a 2 piece combo and the chicken and waffles. The food took a while and came out lukewarm. The chicken was not very crispy at all and the waffles were very soggy already. The one bright spot however was the cornbread. It was fluffy, warm, and the perfect consistency. Hopefully this is just an off occurence and the food is not always like this.
Good food and fast delivery. Place is always crackin'. Wish they had more space to fit everyone bc it was a tight squeeze.

I finally got a chance to revisit Fixins. I've been missing out! 


After a very lackluster 1st experience here, I was a hesitant to return but I've only heard good things. It's been a long time coming but I got an oppourtunity to take my family there for dinner on a week night. We were able to be immediately seated outdoors on there patio area. All of the staff were so nice and happy to assist as my parents gazed over the menu. 

I ended up getting the 3 piece dark meet fried chicken plate with a side of red beans & rice and potato salad. The fried chicken is great! I feel like Sacramento has a pretty competitive fried chicken market and Fixins is definitely on the better half of options. Loved the red beans & rice and potato salad. Great flavor for both and can't go wrong with making those your sides. This meal comes with a nice sized buttermilk biscuit and its great compliment to the meal. 

Our server was so nice, she checked on us a couple of times and helped my very indecisive parents make their selections. During these troubling times, I know it can be tough time to earn a living, Kudos to her for making us all feel welcomed and delivering excellent customer service.

Excited to return back to Fixins for the great food and atmosphere.

Listen to the hype.. This place is the real deal ya'll..the bees' knees, the cat's meow, the sockdolager, the slap yo' mamma- damn that's good, now take me to church so I can say Hallelujah!

With a menu of oxtail, fried chicken two different ways, catfish, gumbo, sandwiches, bourbon selection, biscuits AND cornbread, they really DO have all the fixins'.

I know it's stereotypical, but I judge southern restaurants by their fried chicken, catfish, and cornbread.

SMOTHERED CHICKEN  5/5*
Once you've had fried chicken with gravy, you'll never go back to plain old naked chicken.  This was like Phillipino fried chicken and Southern fried chicken had a baby.  What impressed me the most was the fact that even with all that gravy on top, the fried chicken stayed crispy the entire time and you can still taste the seasoning of the batter.  You even get to choose dark/white meat or combo. Usually I'm not a fan of the breasts (said no guy ever).. but Fixins wasn't dry, and the gravy added extra flavor and moisture.  

FRIED CATFISH  5/5*
The cornmeal batter was a bit salty, but the SLAB of catfish was cooked perfectly - soft, juicy, fall apart flaky but still held together for a nice texture.  This was a big fish.

MAC AND CHEESE  4/5*
At first glance, I thought, ehh.. elbow macaroni, mushed into a lump.. it's probably cooked too soft like all BBQ/southern-style mac and cheese.  But no no no.  The flavor was on point!  Was that a creamy sauce I taste and not just some cheese mixed in?  Was that the bite of al dente noodles?  Oh yes it was!  This mac.. approved.

COLLARD GREENS w/Turkey Necks  3/5*
This was your typical collard greens.  Not too memorable.

CANDIED YAMS  2/5*
This wasn't for me.  There was too much sauce, too sweet and cinnamony.  Reminded me of eating dessert.

CORNBREAD  5/5*
Wow!  The size and height!  It looked almost like a pop-over.  With the sweet butter and grainy, soft, fluffy texture, this cornbread was delicious.  Definitely no Jiffy box.

KOOL-AID  5/5*
How can sugar water be so good you ask?  This one had a slice of lemon.  I don't know if that was what did it, but this Kool-aid was tart and tasty.  Not your typical diabetic hater drink where all you taste is artificial sugar.

The patio setup is cute with different style seating:  benches, tables, by trees.  They have heater lamps and the space is enclosed in a portable tent to block out the winter air.  Their menu is impressive and I'm waiting for the day I can dine there again to try out the oxtail.  Based on what I had so far, Fixins' is SO good it's got me going home singing salivatory praises in my lowest possible gospel voice. uh hum oh ~yeeeaaah.  That was a goooood meaaaal ya'llll~

Was so delicious!! 10/10 would eat again! 
The chicken was juicy and the sauce on top was just the right amount. The fries were crispy and paired really well.

Comfort food fix!!  Had the chicken and waffles. Outstanding. Service is excellent but we went at a slower time so get there early.  Great vibe for a relaxing meal.  Great spot everyone should be able to find something they want to eat!

This easily may be the best soul food I've had so far. I had the fried chicken with Mac & Cheese and Collard greens and everything was so good. Its so good I actually became angry because I'm not local and I'd have to travel across the state to get it. Highly recommend.

I happen to leave do a Book Fairy drop here the week of Halloween and scanned their menu, it sound delicious and it was busy so I figured I'd have to return.

Did just THAT straight from the airport! I had the catfish GOOOD LAAWWRRDDD it was fresh and the seasoning was on POINT! Lavender Lemon Drop was tasty, Sweet tea was refreshing and LARGE and Little Bit got the chicken and waffles.  The macaroni and cheese taste like my 'Patti Labelle' recipe and it's ALWAYS a winner, now let me tell you about their Candied Yams... it's definitely on the sweet side (which is how I like them) We enjoyed the yams so much I decided to order a Sweet Potato Pie for Thanksgiving from them. Met the Chef she was kind enough to come out when we returned to pick up the pie.

Ol skool provided superb service was friendly and patient with all my usual 3k questions LOL. They have outside seating with heaters. Stanchions to form a line and markers on the floor. Street parking on a one way street so keep this in mind if you are arriving via GPS. 

Respectfully Reviewed

Very good. The chicken and waffles are good. So is the macaroni and cheese and catfish. I usually am not impressed by soulfood restaurants, but this one is good. There tends to be a wait especially on Sundays, but the service is good. 

The family history theme ties in nice. It should definitely be on a must try list for visiting Sacramento.

First time here and might I add the food was smackin!!!
I had the smothered pork chops..... which I never eat I didn't give 5 stars cause the batter on the chops was a bit much for my palate but I jus scraped it off and kept digging in! 
The potato salad was perfectly to my liking, the right mixture of creaminess and texture combined in a dish and it had that slight sweet taste to it, that I enjoyed as I grew up, eating it as such.
 the Black Eyed Peas...... my Gawd today.... I could've just eaten a whole big  bowl  of those by myself Amazing.!!!! my dude had the fried chicken. I asked for an extra crispy fry on it and it was juicy when you bit  into it and I had a RED..... yes y'all RED flavor koolaid mimosa and everybody know what flavor RED  is when you get Kool-Aid...... tropical punch !!!!! The ambience was cool. The service was good. The mixologist KIM  was on point !!!! and the music gave it a whole at  home we chillin and eating gud vibe, I'll definitely return to try more items on the menu
Fast seating. Great servers. Food was fantastic. Great flavors and amazing choices. Menus don't have to be complicated do what you do good!  This place is it!

What a gem! Their food was amazing, the service was awesome and the atmosphere is chic and upbeat. It was easy to make a reservation through open table and we were seated a few minutes after arrival. This will be our favorite new spot! Highly recommend.
Bomb.com. Period T. We shall be back and can't wait. Best southern food you will get unless you are from the south and have a grandma you can visit.

We always have fabulous time at Fixins. The green tomatoes and fried fish are my favorite dishes . The food is always delicious, and the vibes and good music playing is a win. The lively atmosphere made our experience even better. Highly recommend for a fun night out!

Food was so good the kool-aid pickles and the friend chicken hamburger were muah. And the service, all came very fast and it only took 5 minutes to get the food. The ambiance was probably the star of the show though and the inside was beautiful.

My friends and I stopped by this place on our way up to Tahoe over the weekend. We got to the restaurant around 4pm. There was a bit of a wait -- probably 30 minutes or so. 

We were seated at the outside patio area because we had a dog with us (so this place is pet friendly for those wondering). 

The staff was extremely helpful! For context, there were 7 of us, it was our first meal of the day, we were all hangrily shouting our orders out, and all of us had annoying special requests. 

But again, the staff is great, and was making suggestions and helping us determine the best portion sizes for the sides we wanted to share. The food came out quick and fresh!

I highly recommend the fried chicken sandwich and the mac and cheese. And the watermelon margarita was perfect! 

Will definitely come back the next time I pass through Sac.
The chicken and Mac and cheese were delicious!!!! Will return again sometime in the near future.

Food was great as always, but the service was non existent. Our "waitress" did not take our order, another server had to since we were sitting there waiting.  She had the audacity to yell at Jae for "taking her table" after he took our order. The only times we saw our waitress was to bring the bill, then 30 minutes later when a busboy had to remind her there was a table waiting to pay. I would mention her name, but she didn't show up enough to tell it to us. Jae was great and helped us when others would not.
Service was amazing and food was close second. Had the gumbo and fried chicken. Both super tasty. They were able to accommodate our party of 16

Service was impeccable! Drinks were bomb!! 
Food portions are generous and taste was bomb.com! Wait time for a Saturday was like 45 mins but once we got seated ordering and good to table time was fairly quick. Definitely going back when I'm in town for a Kings Game! 

Light the BEAM!!!!

I got recommended to this place by my airbnb host, and I'm impressed!

First off, the service was awesome!! I got a seat right away at the bar and Q was bartending at the time. She was quick at taking my order, kept all pf us entertained at the bar, and also kept everything clean and organized. I know a superwoman when I see one!

Food was delicious- when I saw the portion of it, thought I wouldn't be able to finish it, but I did 'cause it was yummy sould food! I ordered the Smothered Chicken with a Fixin' of Charred Okra (a must try!!) and Collar Greens.

Great ambiance too - love the southern vibe!

Maaaaann Kevin Johnson went 100% from the field with this place!! Everything is good!! The catfish nuggets are next level, the shrimp & grits are outstanding, and the peach cobbler is phenomenal!! Also the candied yams are amazing!! The music in this place is the best!!

This review is for a takeout order. 

I finally made my way to this place after taking care of business in Sacramento. I didn't have time for a dine in experience, so I ordered take out. 

When I walked in, fly music was playing and oh my goodness, the prettiest man was sitting at their counter. But that's besides the point LOL. (A good indicator of the type of customer's this place attracts, maybe?) 

I'm not sure about some of the reviews saying the staff was rude but a takeout order doesn't get the same treatment like you're staying! Folks were plenty nice and helpful. 

I came home and placed my order on a plate and got to work. One of our mac n cheese was missing, but that's alright. The fries were perfect and great seasoning on the chicken. 

If you order pickup, there's a small space to pull over to walk in. 

I'm hoping to try again and sit down to enjoy myself.

I already came to this location twice and I cannot wait to come back again! The first time is with my colleagues and boss, I had the fried fish (catfish to be exact) with fries and okra! It was ah-may-ZING! The fish was seasoned perfectly, although I wish there was some dipping sauce on the side but still good! The okra was WOW, with the bacon *chef's kiss* and the fries, I know this sounds weird but I am a fries girl and this was YUM, perfectly crisp and crunch -- not some soggy, sad looking fries, she was fresh and delish! The hush puppies were a bit dried for me, it could definitely use something. Thankfully my colleague shared her strawberry butter (?) with me because that is exactly what I needed! Now for my second time, my hubby and I ordered the oxtails with mac and cheese, 3 piece fried chicken with candied yam and fries (yes ma'am again!) and chicken and waffle! The oxtails were very tendered and flavorful -- my hubby was very impressed! And the fried chicken was so juicy and perfectly seasoned! I was very surprised that they let you choose what kind of pieces you want (thighs, breast, legs, wings -- I chose all thighs!). Lastly, the peach cobbler! I just wish there was a bit more dough on it, just a little bit more and it would had been perfect! And of course, cannot forget the kool aid. And the customer service is phenomenal -- love that they always check on you, and if you ask for extra stuff, they respond with a smile, LOVE that! Thank you for this wonderful food and I cannot wait to come back!

Everything. The food the atmosphere and the drinks. I will surely go again.  It would be nice to get validated parking but it was $10 to park for 2 hours. 

Other than that it was a great experience.

The food is good. I had the fried chicken white meat, mac & cheese and greens. I should have gotten the fried okra because I tried some of my sisters and it was really good. The ambiance is nice and my sister told me the food is consistent, good flavors every time. She is a great cook so I know it must be good if she says it's good. This will be one of my go to spots when I come to town to visit. The only thing that left much to be desired is the waitress, we kind of felt like she forgot about us. The place wasn't that busy for a Friday night. She was nice but definitely not the best.
Simply amazing! Every time I eat here I leave so full I can barely walk. Always wonderful food and the service is so warm and friendly. Shaundra was our waitress and she was so nice.

I came here for brunch with a few friends. This is not my first time here. I noticed that on the menu the fish entree said "fried fish" and I remember it used to say "catfish". I remember hearing a while back that restaurants were serving swai fish instead of catfish but falsely advertising the fish as catfish. I asked my server what type of fish and she replied "It's swai fish but it's just "like" catfish and it I ordered fish before it's the same thing". I distinctly remember the last time I ordered fish from here it tasted dry and off but I couldn't put my finger on what was wrong. WELL WELL, WELL, come to find out Fixins is one of those restaurants who was LYING to their customers. So if this is swai fish, why are they not indicating that on the menu? They are still trying to deceive their customers by not including what type of fish they're serving on the menu. If the rest of my party had not come in specifically for chicken, I would've left, but I stayed and I ordered food. We ordered fried green tomatoes, chicken, red beans, and rice, greens and mac & cheese. The chicken was good, flavorful and crispy. Mac and cheese was not as good as it used to be. It was very saucy there was barely any cheese. 
The red beans and rice were very basic flavor was decent, but it was nothing to rave about. The greens were very basic and sweet tasting. I was not impressed this visit. The food seem to not be as good as what it used to be. I don't think it's worth it for the price. Fried Green tomatoes and chicken are the only things I'd come back for

Hubby & I tried it for the first time and it was AMAZING! The food was fresh and made correct, our server was friendly and checked us but didn't hound! The red beans & rice & Mac and cheese is fire love the vibe, it feels nice and cozy but still slaps amazing music

Excellent!!

Chicken was a little different. Spices were toned down. It was good however I miss the kick.

****Black owned restaurant ****

Okay, this is one of the best soul food I've ever sat down in. The service was amazing!!!!!

It was an hour and half wait for a seat. And it was worth it!!! We added our name to the list and walked around the neighborhood. 

I ordered the wings and waffle. The waffle is so delicious and the wings were so crispy and tender.

What's not photo is the red velvet cake and peach cobbler.  They both are delicious 

Go...

Amazing service, great food like grandma used to make (black grandmas lol) nice ambiance and friendly atmosphere. I love their creamy Mac and cheese and the candied yams !!! I'm more of a thanksgiving guy than I am a soul food guy.

Fixins has a friendly environment with good music playing in the background.   We sat at the bar and had excellent service.   The bartender was efficient, and the food was served timely.  We ordered the catfish nuggets and adult kool-ades for starters; I had the Catfish plate, which comes with corn fritter hushpuppies. My husband tried the smothered pork chop this time, which he liked, but his favorite is still the three-piece fried chicken and biscuit. As for our two side dishes, we had creamy mac and cheese and perfectly seasoned collard greens with just the right kick.  Everything was delicious.

The BEST hospitality and southern food I've ever had. The hostess and server were welcoming and peppy; love it. The Hot Chicken Sandwich was cooked to perfection; crispy, yet juicy. Their signature hot sauce is bomb, as well as the chipotle mayo to give it a nice kick. The bun was buttery, fresh, and soft. I'll definitely be back.

On a side note, we got there at 11:30 on a Saturday. There was a farmer's market nearby, but plenty of parking and we were sat immediately.

Lastly, I thoroughly enjoyed the music; a mix a 80's and 90's soul/R&B... took me back to a familiar, warm place.
Every time we come here is a delightful experience. Iselle at the bar continues to kill it with the drinks and her service

Great food. Love the Caesar salad with cornbread croutons. Fried chicken is the best. Great bar with exceptional craft cocktails. Friendly engaging servers. Good vibe with upscale comfort ambience. One of my favorite places in Sac.

Very bad experience! Ordered 2 PC Chicken with the sides of Greens and Yams and deviled eggs through doordash. We'll only chicken and a biscuit arrived. Oh, and the deviled eggs which were tasteless and sitting on whilted vegtables. When I called I was told there was nothing they could do and to call DD. $50 for basically 2 pieces of chicken. Furious!

Food was very good.  We had the artichoke dip for the appetizer and it was excellent.  We also had ox tail and fried chicken for our lunch with collard greens and potato salad as our sides. The ox tail was tender and very tasty but pricey.  The fried chicken was good and filling.
Love this place!  I get the same meal every time we go...catfish with coleslaw and potato salad with banana pudding to top it off!  Always yummy with great service!

I have heard nothing but great things about this restaurant. So I decided to drive up from
The Oakland to have brunch.  This was the best decision I have made today. The wait staff very professional and friendly. My waitress was amazing She recommended the charred Okra and macaroni and cheese.  I love Okra and never knew I loved it charred with bacon. I also had the fried chicken which was flavorful to the bone and it came with the fluffiest biscuit and strawberry butter. If people weren't watching me I would have licked the plate clean. As I'm eating I'm fighting tears because all of my favorite praise and worship songs are playing in the background. This set the tone for the rest of my day!  Thank you!

First time at Fixings Soul Kitchen per my friend's recommendation. For some reason I was not able to join the waitlist via Yelp app. I found parking on the street and walked over to join the waitlist in person with the host instead. The wait for a table for four was an hour and a half! Personally I would leave if the wait anywhere is over 40 min because I get hangry. My friend said it's really good and worth the wait so the waiting began...

I walked around the neighborhood to kill time and it got so hot I went back to the car and wait there instead. Our turn finally came and we were seated at the outside patio area. The waitress is friendly and I do like the atmosphere. I ordered 2 pieces of dark meat chicken and a waffle and at first I thought to myself, maybe I should have ordered 3 pieces instead but after finishing the whole plate, 2 pieces of chicken and a waffle was already plenty for me.

I don't know what they did to their butter. The butter on the waffle is pink? But it's delicious! I was a fan of that butter. I prefer my waffle to stay crispy so I did not spread butter everywhere right away or the waffle would get all soft. Personally I would like the chicken to be even more crispy and crunchy but that's a personal preference. 

We ordered alcohol beverages too and it came out pretty late in the meal. The waitress apologized and followed up with our drinks. 

At one point there was a bug on my shirt and I got out of my chair and started to freak out. If I have a chance to come back I would dine inside next time, lol.

Hats off to the chefs at this restaurant for everything we sampled was delicious I would order everything again. Let me break down what we had and how it all faired

deep fried catfish with side of grits - catfish was seasoned well with light batter, crispy on each bite, added some tartar sauce on some bites just to add more flavor haha, the grits were also good filling and not super grainy most other places I have tried the grits were very grainy this place surprisingly was not, I would order this catfish again and sample another side

bucket of chicken - deep fried, 24 hour brined chicken total of 8 pieces each piece was delicious, super crispy outside, super seasoned inside on each bite. This is a must try, they do have another restaurant in Yountville that claims to have the best fried chicken, I've had it and I don't think it was anything special, this spots 24 hour brined chicken you can actually taste the seasoning bleed through everything, again order this

smothered oxtail with side of collared greens - when it comes to oxtail if I see it on the menu, imma order it, had my share of spots that serves this dish, let just say anytime I am in the area I will definitely be coming back and ordering another plate. Heavily seasoned, almost fall off the bone, tender, delicous on every bite, also in the dish is the sauce and rice and beans all combined a perfect plate. The collared greens were made in my opinion perfectly, not sour, not slimy, clean flavored slightly crisp bite, made again perfect, Definitely order this!!!

Gumbo - pungent smell that is intoxicating, sauce is semi thick consistency, could be thicker to my liking but the flavor on this dish was very good, we added the shrimp to this dish to add more flavor, didn't disappoint, definitely order this 

there were also some added baked good, 3 hush puppies with the fried catfish, 4 biscuits that came with the chicken, and one cornbread here is the breakdown

hush puppies - browned outside flaky goodness inside, sweet

4 biscuits - darkened yellow tops, plush bite, add the butters offered, we had a blueberry spread that was perfect match, and the other I don't recall but it was more of the yellow color, it was semi sweet butter spread also good

1 cornbread - fairly small, actually bite size but you are going want to break this apart and eat it slowly, every bite is decadent, I will need to do a side order of nothing but this next time

In all everything was good, I do recommend sampling everything we had, none of it disappointed, can't wait til covid is done so we can eat inside, instead of togo

oh I also sampled 2 of their drinks the adult Kool Aid and Bourbon Sweet Tea, the Kool Aid had a strong punch of alcohol and was very sweet, the Bourbon Sweet Tea was sweet but not overpowering sweet, I recommend having both haha

food ***** hats off to the chefs
price $$
service ***** online order if togo, there was patio seating but during covid we opted with togo
ambiance ***** from what I can see from the pickup line, patio seating was well spaced out and looked inviting to sit, the inside that I was able to see was allot of dark hardwood with fairly large bar, can't wait til covid is done to come back and eat inside haha

This place SMACKS!

Everything we ordered was DELICIOUS!! The fried deviled eggs, grits and coleslaw I highly recommend. We got various drinks, all were equally good. There's really not a bad choice here!

We waited for a table, everything was clean and great. Our server was great and recommended several items. 

Parking is a little challenging. You might have to park around the corner and walk, depending on the day and time. 

I will definitely be back when I'm in the area: matter of fact this is a MUST STOP type of place.

Ambience is very nice. Our server was amazing and provided great customer service! We ordered the gumbo and the catfish fillet and a few sides. I was disappointed in the gumbo, it lacked flavor and the roux was very thick. The catfish fillet was delicious and flaky but it only came with one piece and two sides for $23. As far as the sides...delicious! I highly recommend the Mac and cheese, collard greens, red beans & rice, and candied yams ! I would love to come back and try a few other entrees on the menu.

This place was great! I would definitely come back. We both got shrimp and grits which were filling and delicious though there was only five shrimp. Very flavorful! We also got a mango lemonade drink with Hennessy that was really good.

This is my third time visiting Fixins and each time it's been good! Yesterday I tried the shrimp and grits and it was delicious. It was spicy, the shrimp were big and juicy. My only suggestion is to double the grits to go with the sauce. I had a lot of sauce leftover and had to order a cup of grits to go. 

Service is always pleasant and I love the ambiance on Sundays with the gospel music.
Best okra and catfish nuggets, service was very friendly, fast, and considerable for having a short school lunch break!
Food and service were excellent.  And I'm adding this to get to 85 characters.  And that was not quite enough.  How about now?

This is my Grandsons Favorite place and we went there after Graduation yesterday and it was the BEST FOOD I as well as all 16 of us have had. The Service was Outstanding, the BAR was Awesome, The Atmosphere was Great.   I had Fried Fish, Black eyed peas and Collard Greens (Turkey necks),  others had Oxtails , Rice, Smothered chicken, Smothered Pork Chops,  yams,  Mac n cheese,  cornbread muffin honey butter,  just to name a few plates we had and it was all so very YUMMY, seasoned very well just the very BEST!!  We highly enjoyed ourselves while dining and our Bellies were more than Satisfied when we left.   We will have to come back again and next time leave room for Dessert

This is my second time here as my love for soul food continues to grow. The catfish nuggets were a hit both times as the fish was hot, soft, melts in your mouth, and it's covered in a light crispy layer of batter. I like how they're bite size pieces which makes a perfect warm up snack before the main course! We opted for the fried green tomatoes today and they were delicious as well! I've had plenty of fried tomatoes from soul food places in NOLA and Miami and believe it's quite comparable. I thought the blue coolaid would have been a nice touch as it's been years since I've had, but unfortunately it came warm despite there being ice. It definitely do think it's a fun addition to your meal!

For our entree, we ordered the 3 piece fried chicken, which came with your choice of two sides. We chose to go with the collard greens, a biscuit, and grits. The chicken was cooked to perfection, so juicy, flavorful with a crispy skin. I was hesitant to get the grits, but ended up loving them. I didn't think the extra cheese was necessary as it was already quite creamy. 
I was seated in their outdoor seating which always has a great vibe and doesn't feel as cramped as their indoor seating. It also feels so nice especially in the late afternoon. There was a homeless man seated right outside the gate, but the staff respectfully asked him to move which was very considerate for restaurant guests. 
My waitress was very nice and constantly checked on us. Our waters were refilled and requests were handled very promptly. Before being seated, the wait time was about 45 minutes, and we were seated way before then.
 
All in all, you can never go wrong with Fixins when you got that soul food craving. You already know I'll be back!
Had a great experience here all around! Definitely recommend to make a reservation or you'll be waiting a while. My partner and I got lucky because we spotted open seats at the bar.

Super long wait and they stopped taking walk-ins at 6:30pm so get here early! Food is so good, they do take reservations. They have indoor dining as well as out door seating. Great place to accommodate large groups and  they have a lovely bar. Can't wait to come back for the shrimp and grits and the ox tail!
First time I ate here it was good.  Ate there two other times and didn't like it.  Change in kitchen?

Great food and atmosphere to match. 

Arrived on Saturday for lunch. It was a little crowded but we were seated right away. 

Staff were very kind, service was fast and food was delicious. You'll get all of your favorite staples of soul food here and everything is yummy! 

Restaurant is very clean and lots of screens to watch whatever current sports are playing. 

We ordered: 

Friend chicken 
Catfish 
Greens
Candied yams 
Black eyed peas 
Mac and cheese 
Dinner roll 

The fried chicken and black eyed peas were especially delicious. So well seasoned and perfectly cooked.

It's about time I found some southern cookin that is ACTUALLY REPRESENTATIVE of southern cookin!

LOCATION:
I'm the 40 Acres Complex in Oak Park next to World Class Faders. Street Parking is available.


DECOR:
Classic brick building with a family feel and a great outdoor patio with plenty of seating.


PRICE:
Worth every scent and on par with dine-in market prices. Fried Chicken sandwich is $14 and you will most likely have leftovers. I do every time.

They also have reasonably priced sides. Trust me on the 5 corn fritters for $3. TRUST ME! I have to stop myself from ordering up 6 orders of those and dipping out. Lol.


DRINKS:
Umm, did you say Kool-aid?! Yes, Please! It was just like I remember all those years back. And here's a hint. They make the best DIY popsicles.

Can't forget the lemonade. Freshly made and DELICIOUSLY REFRESHING! 


FOOD:
THEY BEST CHICKEN SANDWICH I'VE EVER HAD! If I were going to make a fried chicken sandwich, it would taste like this one! I mean, it's not an easy one to eat. The 3-finger rule is definitely broken, but I don't care because it's too damn Delicious to have any complaints about. and if you try it and don't like it, then go ahead and box it up for me. I'll take it off your hands!

The fried catfish is flavored as I I've always remembered it to be flavored down south. It doesn't even need hot sauce, but the hot sauce does still add that extra pop.

STAFF:
Real, approachable, sincere, and chill. I get a good vibe from every person I talked to that works here and I kind of love my life on vibes. They aren't too pushy or talkative. It's just the right amount of conversation and my drink never runs dry.


EXPERIENCE:
I probably ordered take out three or four times before I actually ate here. Then one day, I decided to stop in and experience the entire field. Well, at least from the patio since the inside was closed to due to regulations which day are abiding by beautifully. All in all, I'm proud to be able to recommend a place that represents the black community so well and I'm not afraid to say it.

My wife and I have been wanting to here for awhile and I'm happy we did. The food is amazing my wife had the fried catfish and I had the oxtails. The sides are just as delicious. Our server Kim was awesome, she called us family and we sure felt at home. The food and vibe for a Sunday in Oak Park was just the best way to start my day. This is a must place and I suggest getting a reservation through Open Table cause this place gets busy.

Chandra's service is amazing! First time visit because our favorite southern spot, Chez Soul, permanently closed this month and we need a good southern place in our rotation. The food is delicious. We arrived at noon and it wasn't terribly busy, however by 1 it was full. The vibe is good and we'll definitely return!

What can I say? The food is outstanding, taste like my auntie is in the kitchen. The service is awesome. The vibe is everything. Don't forget to look at the wall posters they're awesome. The food is so good. The plates are very hardy. The cornbread muffin is gigantic. It's like the size of a Costco muffin maybe a little bit bigger.

I've been on the fence about coming here for the past three years.  I've heard a lot of mixed reviews, so I decided to try it myself.  

So I bring my mom after a day at the hair salon.  There was supposed to be a 30 minute wait, but because it was just the two of us, we actually got seated within 15 minutes.  So that's one star.

Let's move on to the food and drink. I order the adult Kool-aid and mom had a coke.  Didn't like my drink at all. Too sweet and not too many levels to it. Just Kool-aid and vodka....and not enough of the later.

Moving on to the food. We ordered the catfish nuggets appetizers, and for our individual meals, I ordered the smothered porkchops with yams and mac 'n cheese, and my mom ordered the fried chicken with yams and potato salad.

The catfish nuggets were absolutely flavorless. I thought we were being tricked or something. You can't tell me that a place specializing in soul food can't get catfish right! No seasoning at all! Very SMALL pieces of fish. When you think of catfish nuggets, you think of at least a 2 inch piece of fish.  These were the size of a quarter, if that!

Getting to the main courses, my mom's fried chicken was dry and flavorless as well. The potato salad was extremely bland. The yams, although sweet, they fell flat on the palate. My pork chops were underseasoned and underwhelming. The mac 'n cheese was decent, and probably the only thing I could really eat. I hardly ate anything on my plate.

We asked for to-go boxed within minutes of tasting the food. I love supporting black businesses, but the quality should still be on point.

Now I've heard really good reviews and really bad ones. And unfortunately I have to cosign with the later. This place does not deliver. Come on, Sac!
You've gotta have better taste buds than this.  It's gentrified soul food!

Won't be back!

Not pictured was the catfish plate. They provide a big ol' slab of Catfish with a light,but crispy batter. Sooooo moist and tasty. Other two sides were collard Greens and yams. I loved the collard greens the most. The greens were cooked down perfectly and had just the right amount of tang from the vinegar. The bacon offered a nice saltines. The yams tasted like they could be a side OR a dessert. They practically melt in your mouth! These yams are next level.
Loved the food, service and location! It was a plus that it was near a lot of black owned businesses to support the locals in the area. Will be back again

Incredible meal. I started with a Bloody Mary which had a giant piece of bacon sticking out of it. I for my meal I had the fried catfish. The catfish was cooked perfectly. My meal came with hush puppies. It's been so long since I had them!

For my fixins I chose okra with bacon, seared beautifully. And collard greens with turkey neck. Yum yum!

I can't wait to come back.

This was my second visit to Fixins and I must say, the food is bomb! The staff is friendly and the drinks were amazing. I ordered the chicken and waffles which was delicious. FYI, I would order the chicken extra crispy/well done. I also ordered the oxtail and rice with Mac and cheese to go. I just wish it came with one more side. Other than that, the place is amazing and I will definitely be back before I leave the Bay Area.

This place is a gift from Heaven. Food so good, it will make you cry tears of pure JOY. 

And the drinks?! Delicious. 

The owners and team here just blessed my life with the catfish, macncheese, greens and yams. 

It will change your life.

After graduating.. again, I decided to come on down to oak park while I'm in town. The whole experience was amazing. I had shrimps and grits and it was good, adult kool aid was great, service great, catfish nuggets the best. It was so airy and fresh, I must say best I have ever experienced. Overall this place get my stamp of approval

What can I say that hasn't been said before. Best Fried Chicken in town (and we have tried pretty much all the others). We had  sides of Black Eye Peas (Good, but not my thing - texture issue), Mac n Cheese (really good) and Collard Greens (outstanding!!).  The DW wanted to try the Fried Green Tomatoes which are out of this world. The chipotle mayo adds the right accent and the red peppers help get your attention.  

Lots of room as it was a Monday night. Definitely a do-again for us!

This was the first time I visited Fixins Soul Food Kitchen.  Everything was great right from the start. The customer service was very good. My server, Kim, was very helpful and attentive to my needs. The food was excellent! There were 5 of us in my party and everyone ordered something different and everything was delicious ! I had the veggie plate. So many choices to choose from, I had to order two veggie plates.  Can't wait to go back for more. I love the fact there is  an outdoor seating option and it is family oriented. I just wish there were more meat options to choose from that are not fried or smothered in gravy.

The server John-Nia will provide you deferred service if her family or friends are dining. Not only did she not provide refills until the end of the dining experience; we waited 90 minutes to receive our food. When asked about the status of the order she stated because we ordered an appetizer we had to wait longer for our full course. Our doggy bags were placed on another table and she wasn't attentive. 

Finally we walked inside to pay for our order and found her having casual conversation instead of cashing us out. She didn't bring the receipt we had to ask the manager for their receipt. 

Normally I have good visits at this restaurant however today was an exception to the rule. Avoid this server.
Fixins is a vibe! Food was on point...amazing! The spiked kool-aid smacks too. Will be going again soon and can't wait until the new location comes to Oakland.

The artichoke dip was so good!!! I had the impossible burger and the fries were cooked to perfection, my best friend had the gumbo and she loved it. The service was great the only feedback I have is our waitress got really busy and if they had one more person helping with the tables instead of the front that would have made the experience 10/10. The decor was super cute and the vibe was really fun. The playlist was also super good!! Will be back for sure.

Went to FIXINS yesterday for the first time, and they were everything that I expected. We ordered the 3-piece fried chicken with  mac & cheese, the candy yams along with the fried catfish,  collar greens, and the red beans and rice. The food was excellent and the service was out of this world! We will definitely be going back to the Sacramento location... and when I return home to Southern California I will be taking both my brother and sister to the LA live location in downtown Los Angeles

The food is so good, you don't need a picture you've gotta go!!!!!

Can't believe I just found out about this place a week ago. First off, my friend and I were greeted and seated by an incredibly sweet woman who told us all about the place. Several times she made it clear that we should not hesitate asking her or anyone else for help with anything. Server arrived quickly and took order accurately and politely. We started off with the fish nuggets which were incredible. Scorching hot (temp), crunchy, but with incredible tender and flaky fish. They were served with a spiced chipotle mayo and some type of tartar but without much relish/pickling but lots of fresh dill. Both sauces were awesome! We ended up asking for some of the chipotle mayo to go with our fries. We both ordered fried chicken sandwiches, one "hot" and one (mine) plain. OMG, this sandwich was ridiculous. The size of the chicken breast was almost comical. Definite Flintstones vibe. Super crunchy, fresh lettuce, and to die for pickles. I left off the onions. Fries were hot, fresh, and crispy. Sandwich and fries were fantastic and are something I will definitely order again. My friend said the hot chicken sandwich was really good and on the spicy side. Dude literally started sweating about halfway through. So, if you cannot tolerate spicy you should probably opt for the plain. It took a few mins to get our check but they were pretty busy and we weren't in a rush. I have a feeling if we had asked someone to fetch it, we could have gotten it quicker. Given the state of food prices these days, coupled with the large portion and high quality, I feel their pricing is very fair. It's definitely not cheap but I regularly spend more and don't get as much for my money. 

All in all, this place checked all the boxes and I can't wait to go back to try the fish plate or fish sandwich. Highly recommend!!!

This has been a place that I've wanted to try for a while. After finally experiencing it, I will say that it was okay. The fried chicken was good but just that and not particularly memorable. Not too much to talk about the other items either. Unfortunately, this trip did not turn out as I'd hoped but perhaps it was an off day so I could be convinced to give it another try.

Visited last night service was not good the waitress wasn't great at connecting with both of us in the party.  I do believe she could be stellar with training / coaching. The food came out cold the first time and raw the second it's my 3 visit. 1st time pre COVID was phenomenal 2nd and 3rd horrible. I won't be back.  That's unfortunate since their aren't many soul food restaurants in the area.
Really great food.  It's a bit out of the way for us, but worth the trip.  Just awesome!  The prices are still very affordable too compared to other places.  We'll be back for sure!

Good food.. good customer service.. great portions 
Probably will return to try other items

Visited Fixins to celebrate a new job. I strongly recommend a reservation. The wait time can be up to an hour. The restaurant can get really busy. There is indoor and outside seating. The restaurant is cute and has some interesting artwork. The service was good with the amount of customers they have. You can tell they are hard at work and doing their best. Our server was kind and he checked in on us and so did other staff. The food was good. I really like potato salad and Mac and cheese so they were my go to sides. The fish is fluffy and crispy. It was not fried too hard and it was not too salty. I also got the kool aid with ciroc. The food is good, service is as well, and I recommend fixins when you are looking for some Soul Food.

Stopped in while on this side of town. This place did not disappoint. That hot chicken sandwich is money!! Nicely seasoned batter on the chicken, perfect spice and a whole mess of fries. I can't wait to come back! Telling the friends and fam about this spot! 
Desiree was on it, she's super sweet too!
nice ambience, good service, and great food!!! the shrimp and grits were amazing!! I would definitely revisit

author_location: Downtown San Jose, San Jose, CA
text: I had the smothered fried chicken, red beans and rice, and collared greens with turkey necks and it was an absolutely wonderful meal. Everything came out quickly. The staff were all super friendly and make you feel right at home.
Smothered chicken is so good. Mac n cheese is deliciousss. Biscuits are amazing too. All around good food and good service.

My first review since COVID started. Restaurants are opening back up for dine-in, and I'm so excited! Stopped by for takeout in support of Black-Owned businesses. Customer service was excellent, and they had very careful social distancing measures.

Ordered the chicken sandwich and two-piece chicken and waffle. Chicken was super crispy! I had the chicken sandwich and really enjoyed how light and crispy the chicken skin was. My boyfriend got the waffles and the butter that came with was delicious. I can't pinpoint what they were, but one was fruity and the other savory. Excellent pairings with syrup.

Will be back for the dine-in experience!

I just thought I post review of this place because it's been consistently a 10 out of 10 every time I've come. The Oxtails, Mac n Cheese, Corn bread, Yams cocktails, basically everything that I've tried on the menu has been consistently good. I can't stop coming back. Not to mentions the service and servers are superb .  This is one business that I fully stand behind and support.

Oh one last thing the ambiance is top of the line. I love hearing gospel when I come on Sundays too.

The food, service, and setting are amazing even when Uber busy at the Sac location. I wish LA could improve their business because it's horrible. Love the people working at this location. The food was amazing! My colleagues literally took it on the plane back to LA because we know the food, cleanliness, and service are horrible in LA.

Fried chicken & waffles (flavored well, cooked well, nice and juicy, waffles were light and not dense, so yummy with the flavored butter that sits on top of it. ) Catfish was fried to perfection with mac n cheese greens and fried okra and I'm pretty sure everything else on the menu is amazing. 

This heart attack food is worth the calories and all the sinful things that follows food organism. 

Staff was friendly, buss boy really got down and cleaned the tables nicely. I really do appreciate quality work...

This food is really good for my soul. My mouth had a party tonight and can't wait to go back!

This is the SPOT! 

The food was absolutely amazing. The chicken was so tender and delicious. I had their collard greens and it had so much flavor. 

Don't get me started with that alcoholic kool aid! It was amazing, definitely tasted the alcohol in it. The staff were super friendly as well. They were super helpful and attentive! 

The best soul food I've ever had!

This was a nice little vibe in Sacramento. Met up with a friend who lives in Natomas and she brought me here. I had the fried devil eggs and catfish bites for apps. I had the catfish, she has the oxtails and my other friend had shrimp and grits. The food was sooo good. Mac and Cheese was not all that but it hit the spot. The catfish was delicious! I wanna go back and try the other dishes on the menu.

We were there for Sunday brunch and the place was full. Our server was friendly but it took some time to get our food. The kitchen was likely a bit behind. We had mac & cheese, red beans & rice, the fried chicken omelette and cornbread with gravy. 
It was all delicious. The fried chicken was moist and tender but crispy on the outside, perfect. We will go back for sure!

This place right here is something else. I had my share of soul food at different places. This place has to be one of my favorites. My friends recommended this spot and took me today. 

The place was very clean and we were seated and taken care of right away. Everything on the menu looked delicious. I ended up ordering the oxtails with a side of collard greens. It was amazing! I wanna try other things on the menu, but the oxtails were so dang good that I might have to get that again! We also got a side of catfish nuggets. It came with 2 different sauces that tasted homemade. I also order an Arnold Palmer (half sweet tea/half lemonade) which was also amazing.

Our server was really nice and we were able to get our food quickly. Payment didn't take long and on the way out, all the employees acknowledged as just like when we all walked in.

5/5 recommend - you need to try this place now!

This place has the BEST food!! Everything seasoned well and not salty. Our food came out hot and delicious. We ordered fish nuggets for appetizers, pancake breakfast with a side of grits, and the shrimp n grits. OMGGGGGGG it was SMACKING! This is our 2nd visit on a Sunday, gospel music was playing while we enjoyed our breakfast. Thank you for another great experience. See you soon!

Try. Everything. Here. 
Our food was hot, delicious, and we have no complaints! Even the dessert was delicious, and we hardly ever order dessert. 
We will be back 
P.S. we made reservations, arrived 20 min early, and were seated within 5 minutes. Excellent service and environment.

COVID Review:

Thank you Baby Jesus this place is still open!!!  The food here is good!  This place is our go to for take out because the menu has options.  And did I tell you the food is good?  LOL!

Order online/pay online, head over to the restaurant and park your car, call in letting them know you're outside, and then someone masked and gloved comes out with your order.  If felt safe and when I got home, I was full and satisfied!

Our COVID go-to meals:
1.  The 8pc bucket of chicken family meal.  Its' a good size for 3 hungry adults. It comes with biscuits and 2 sides.  We still order a kids meal for the man child bc homie can eat.
2. The hubs and I are in LOVE with the Smothered Oxtail- hand to God, get this and you will not get disappointed.  They do not skimp out on the oxtail and they still give you a side that you don't really need.
3.  All the chicken and waffle entrees!

We are regulars and will continue to support places with good food :).  BC really, who can cook all day every day, 3 times a day?? - def not the wfh, now Pre-K teacher, mama.

Hell yes for SOUTHERN COMFORT FOOD! Smothered chicken brought back memories of visiting family in Texas... and our waitress was incredible! So friendly, knowledgeable, and smiley. 

Grateful for the quick turnaround on the food - since we were starving!

Came in for lunch midday. Parking was a little sketch.
Food was good, drinks were good, service was okay. We will be back to try more.

I am a very opinionated and hard critic when it comes to food. This place is AMAZING. Their seasoning on the chicken is to die for. Their collard greens are a perfect balance of heat and flavor without being overly salty like most places. Their Mac and cheese is to die for. Also - I don't eat biscuits from anywhere. Not my thing for whatever reason. However. This place has the best biscuits I've ever had. I actually eat them! lol. I would like to also add that they're EXTREMELY reasonably priced. I got three breasts, greens, Mac and cheese, biscuit, two sides of ranch, and a side of gravy for $26. This is my top 3 spots in Sacramento. MUST HAVE.

The food was absolutely amazing!!! I had fried chicken, Mac n cheese, yams and corn bread! My husband ordered the fried chicken with waffles. All amazing! Service was great! They were all so friendly! We went on a Sunday so I loved the gospel music selections they had on. The decorations and details of the restaurant was so nice. Will definitely head back!!

Me, my husband and a friend of ours decided to try this place out for the first time. I had no idea what to expect, but was super impressed with the whole experience. Walking in, the hostesses greeted us. There was a wait time but we were seated earlier than expected. 
I ordered the pork chop with Mac and cheese with collard greens, while my husband and friend ordered the ox tail. I kid you not, that ox tail was to die for! The meat just fell right off and was packed with so much flavor! The pork chop was just as yummy! It wasn't dry and the breading was just perfect! Mac and cheese was super flavorful as well. I love collared greens, so I can't complain how theirs turned out. The presentation of all the food came out beautifully as well. 
We also ordered the adult koolaid and a brown sugar drink that resembled an old fashioned (recommended by our server). Both drinks were sweet and savory. They are creepers through so be careful! lol
The only thing I wasn't impressed with was  the fried deviled eggs. It lacked salt and flavor. The salad it came with did have a nice vinaigrette flavor with a spicy kick to it. I wouldn't order again personally. 
The whole restaurant itself had a nice, chill vibe to it. Music was perfect and wasn't too loud or wasn't loud enough. It definitely meshed well with the crowed. Overall the experience was a success. From the food, to the great customer service we received, to the ambiance. Totally recommend! For weekends, I would recommend making a reservation on their website just to be safe! Happy eating!
The auntie here made it feel like home. She was very clear on KJ is her nephew and she runs the place. Whatever you need, she got you. Authentic soul food.
Fried chicken. Fire. Mac n cheese. Corn bread. Fire. Yams. Fire. Deviled eggs. Fire. Kool aid with ciroc. Fuego!
Best soul food around. Great service, too!! This time I had breakfast, but I love the fried catfish and fixins, too. Can't wait to go there again

I was visiting for work and decided to check this place out. The service was great. My server was super nice and offered top items off the menu. I decided to go with the fried chick with Mac n cheese and potato salad. The food was so delicious! The chicken was juicy and the breading was perfect! The potato salad was so good and the Mac n cheese was to die for!!! This place took me back to ATLANTA type feel and food. The vibe of the place was great it was busy which made this place even better! I would definitely recommend this place and I know I will definitely be back!

The breading on the fried chicken is amazing, I also got to try several of their sides and was thoroughly impressed, as well as sad that I never tried the Soul Kitchen. 

I hope to come back again when my partner is craving soul food!

The place was very crowded when I arrived.
