I was early and had a reservation.Lucky there was a seat at the bar ordered drinks and food but was unclear that you can't switch to a table when ordering food at the bar must eat at the bar only can order drinks lucky the bartender was able to switch to my table when it was ready I tried the fried chicken and oxtails that was great but today tried the smothered pork chops was disappointed wasn't to my likings (to each is own) even though the presentation was nice didn't satisfy my taste buds! Sides corn bread was great!

3.5 stars.

We have been here several times and have recommended many friends to come. During the beginning of pandemic, we ordered takeout once or twice and didn't think the quality was there, ex: the oxtail was very tough. 

Have returned 2-3 times since about a year ago and the recent experience still proved that the food just wasn't as impressive as back in 2018-2019...

Had gumbo and fried chicken this time. Maybe because I had many gumbos in New Orleans back in Feb and set my bar too high!?

We will still return and support the business but maybe will explore other menu items instead.

I cannot stop dreaming about their chicken  and adult Kool-Aid takes me back. The only thing that surpassed the food is the service. I've been for dinner and happy hour, but I recently learned they also do brunch, which was incredibly reasonable in cost. Highly recommend.

Good music & food are always a good combo along w/ friendly service from diverse staff & customers.  I felt like I was back the the Bay Area (diverse since childhood).  The catfish on point (no slime): crunchy, light & tasty.  Hubby liked his fried chicken; I stole a bite & agree.  The Fried Deviled Eggs have a great hot sauce dressing that I wouldn't tolerate on a 'normal" deviled egg, but it really made the dish.  Being fried the egg white wasn't too overcooked.  The only wish is that there was more mayo mixed with the egg yolk.  My southern bestie liked the gumbo (not love).  The Soul Cesar was great; really liked the cornbread croutons.  As for the fixins, potato salad my fave, I like the greens, Cole slaw was a bit dry, biscuit moist & light, mac n cheese pretty good.
10/10 EVERY TIME! The food, vibes, service, & music make me feel comfortable & right at home!

Wow! 

We got the deviled eggs, fried green tomatoes, beans and rice, Cheesy grits, collard greens and oxtails. We loved everything but the collard greens! 
I got the food to go, but the restaurant looked like a great place to dine in. Can't wait to go back and try more from that delicious menu! :)

The food and service here was amazing ate dinner with the family while I was in Sacramento. The portions were large and everything that I put it my mouth was packed with flavor. I had the gumbo and my brother had the oxtails which were both cooked well and had amazing flavors. The ambiance was nice it was crowded so it got a little loud but you could expect that when it's a full house...

We were there yesterday and were not impressed. No parking around granted it was a Saturday afternoon,lines were awful even with Reservations. From the young lady at the front door, Nayiasha, Server JC had a bad attitude. Now, i regret giving her $40 tip for our group.
Food was blended, and the serves were small.
Ambiance was the only thing that got a star.
It feels like just another Chilies or Applebee's now, I prefer a mom & pop soul food establishment. Sorry we won't be back.
Fixings is always a vibe.  Food is delicious! Justin was the icing on the cake.  His drinks were "all the way up. Made just the way it should be delicious!  Thank you for a good time.

We called ahead of time and reserve a table.  When we arrived it took over 45 minutes before we were seated.  Once seated it took another 35 minutes to get water!  Once we got our water another waitress took our order.   I completely understand that they were busy but our waitress was helping other people that arrived after us.  The food was okay, but not worth the wait...
My first time here, Ryan and Luc were so welcoming! Ryan recommended the mac & cheese and peach cobbler and omg it was so good! Overall great restaurant and great music! I'm excited to come back.

We like this place, but the shrimp and grits I had was missing the grits?  It was more a shrimp in a savory sauce, the grits were added at the end - I like my grits.  The oxtails were also a little light - only two bones? Flavor was good.  The biscuits were disappointing - two small flat biscuits - I guess I was hoping for some large fluffy solid biscuits.

I will come back again and again, but just wishing they would offer a little more of the food - especially with the higher prices nowdays.  Our simple lunch was $55 bucks plus add a tip for good service.

For starters l the service was excellent! The beautiful and spacious dinning area adds that extra element to your experience. I ordered the fried chicken with yams and Mac. The meal came with an amazing biscut that was buttery with crisp edges and tender innards. Everything was tasty my only 2 critiques would be less sugar in the candied yams and my chicken was over cooked. I will definitely be back in the future.

Definitely a must when in Sacramento. Close to CSUS for those parents dropping off and picking up. A Great clean spot for everyone! What can't I say about this place. The service was great, the location was great and the food was beyond great!  yes that great  We had the 2pc chicken and waffles and it was perfect. The grape butter was damn good, and a side of biscuits and corn bread. Delicious! To top it off we shared a banana pudding and trust I would have had it by myself but I was trying to control myself  a must go!

I meant to update this so long ago but since my initial review this is a go-to place for me and my family. There usually isn't a wait longer than 15 minutes and every server I've had has been so attentive, friendly, and helpful. It's perfect if you have a slightly larger group and there's something for everyone on the menu.

The food is amazing! I've tried almost everything on the menu and the food tastes like something you'd have at home. Not only does the food taste great but it has stayed consistent and you know that when you go you will get a good, hot meal and wonderful service every time you walk thru the door.

I was in town and my mom brought me here after I told her I was craving shrimp and grits. This place did not disappoint! They are very popular and I can see why. The food is excellent. I came for the shrimp and grits and I love that their grits are not already infused with cheese. The dish tasted exactly how I wanted, I had no complaints.  The cornbread muffin and fried green tomatoes are delicious appetizers. All the servers are welcoming and it's a lovely environment inside with outdoor seating available too. Overall, food was 5/5 across the board and I would recommend.

Music was a bit loud for my taste. The genre of music was the new stuff they call "music". The fried fish was salty. French fries should have been fresh cut since the restaurant has the appearance of serving what Black folk eat. Fried chicken had great flavor but could have been crispier. Red beans and rice were good. The waitress was great! Wouldn't eat here again. Rather give another soul food restaurant a shot!

Here visiting some friends in Sacramento and came here for the 2nd time. The food here is amazing! The fried chicken was hot and crispy I couldn't have been better. The flavor was on point. I had the candied yams and the Mac n cheese and these sides were just as good.  But the service was excellent. Our server was Cat and she provided excellent service. She was kind and friendly all which made our dining experience perfect. Everyone there was friendly which made the entire experience memorable.  I was watching one of the bidders there by the name of Martin, he cleaned the tables and even swept the floor under the tables and chairs which you don't see often. What a good job he did. Again added more great things about this wonderful restaurant. I can't wait to come back here next time I visit from San Diego. Thank you Fixins for providing excellent service and food that more than satisfied my appetite. 
Until next time!

This place is outstanding in menu selection, food and service. It has it all. It has a family friendly environment. I love the soul food. They have generous portions and very tasty. I highly recommend this house of tasty food and great service.
Food was gross , service could have been much better.   The smell alone in this place is disgusting!

Service was mediocre. Bar was super slow. Wouldn't order a drink there. Food was delicious. Except the Mac and Cheese no flavor and greasy. Okra was bomb!!! pork Chops was THE BEST. YAMS WAS AMAZING. CATFISH NUGGETS WAS FIRE!!!! we tried a lot everything was great except the Mac and Cheese unfortunately. 

We definitely will be back.

Great food! Great, not good! 
Tasty, beautiful & fresh.
Prices are reasonable, too.
Fried chicken, shrimp & grits, black eyed peas, greens, gumbo, etc.!

Holy Toledo, their food is so good!

I didn't expect to find a restaurant in Northern Cali with Southern comfort food as good as what I had here at Fixins Soul Kitchen.

Let me start off with our server. What a joy it was to have Cat as our server. This was my first time and she was extremely helpful in navigating me through the menu as well as making spot on suggestions. Cat was not only attentive to our needs, but such a joy to talk to throughout our meal. She is reason enough to come back.

Yes, there was food as well. We started off with the catfish bites followed by the 3 piece chicken with mac and cheese and okra. Let's not forget the red velvet cake for dessert. The catfish bites had a good flavor, but seemed to have more fluff than substance; Almost like they were over battered. The chicken? The chicken was perfectly seasoned and fried to a golden brown with juices flowing out of each bite. I have eaten what some considered the best chicken in the country and this ranks right up there with them. The okra wasn't your traditional breaded okra, but more grilled and seasoned whole. I wasn't as much of a fan of the mac and cheese as it was a little bland. The red velvet cake was a nice way to end a great meal. The cake was moist with an icing that wasn't overly rich, but more of a creamy extension of the cake itself.

Do your self a favor and stop in at Fixins. You won't be disappointed.

Service was great. The fresh orange juice, cornbread, and lemonade was delicious. It was a pretty and clean place. I definitely enjoyed the fried chicken. Husband got the fried chicken omelette and I got the chicken and waffle. I prefer my waffles to be fluffier and thicker, so if you are like me, then I'd go else where. Other than that, this place was pleasant. The menu does not have too many options like other brunch locations.

I have eaten here a few times and I really like this place s as bf so does my family.   I do like their fried chicken and their cheesy grits and collard greens are terrific.   The biscuits here slap.   Get the biscuits.  The blue koolaid is perfectly mixed.  The cornbread muffins?  Meh.   The Mac and cheese tasted decent but it's real oily/greasy and on the gritty side.    But overall I really like this place and I think it is easily the best restaurant in Oak Park.

Fun side story:   Was on a morning walk with the missus on Easter Sunday of 2020 (very beginning of the pandemic).   We were walking past Fixins and were taking a look at their Easter dinner menu out of curiosity.   A man greeted us at the front door very warmly (with mask on) and just struck up a convo with us.   My significant other had no idea who he was, but I did.  It was Kevin Johnson.  He wasn't putting on airs.  He was just sharing his love of his restaurant.  Him showing how hands on he is was impressive.

This place was so good & is definitely a go to spot if you're in the area. 
There was a little wait but we came right before dinner time & the wait wasn't too long but so worth it.
Our server, Talia, was so sweet. She gave me great recommendations for a margarita (peach mango) & it was delicious. Perfectly balanced. 
My partner ordered the smothered pork chops, collard greens & Mac n Cheese. Gravy on the pork chops was great. Not salty. Seasoned great. Collard greens were good & the Mac n cheese was perfectly creamy.
I had their spicy chicken sandwich & my only drawback was that it was so messy but so worth it. Their hot sauce doesn't hide the flavor, it really adds to it, so that was a plus since most places will hide their lack of seasoning under their sauce. I wasn't a fan of their Kool-aid pickel but that's just me. 
Instead of fries, I had red beans & rice & my partner & u shared a potato salad. 
Their potato salad was creamy & a little sweet & tart & it was perfect (i almost licked the bowl). Red beans & rice was also delicious. 
All in all, if you want some good soul food but want to eat out & want that home cooked taste, this is THE place!!

Great lively soul food place with fried chicken, tender oxtails, and must-try sides like mac & cheese, candied yams, crispy okra, and collard greens. The deep-fried deviled eggs are a standout! Weekends can mean long waits, but reservations help. Service can be quite slow when busy, but staff are friendly and hard working. The food comes out fast and is well worth it.
Never an off day. Peaches was an awesome waitress. The other staff was amazing as well. My go to place while visiting Sacramento!!!

I tried out Fixins after hearing so many people speak highly about the soul food that this restaurant served. From my siblings wanting me to try this place to even Kendrick Perkins on ESPN raving about this restaurant founded by former NBA player/Mayor Kevin Johnson and his wife Michelle. I had to give this place a try especially since I have been craving soul food and all I can say is wow.

This is the best soul food I've ever had. We got catfish nuggets as an appetizer and they were fried perfectly and the dipping sauces went great with the nuggets. I also got a sweet tea and it is one of the best out there. The fried chicken is incredibly juicy on the inside and crispy on the outside. The meat fell off the bone so easily. The biscuits were very moist and their strawberry butter was a must have with the biscuits. I'm not a huge collard green fan, but even I couldn't get enough of them. Lastly, the mac n cheese was also incredible and made perfectly.

The staff was also very friendly and the environment eating in the Sacramento location was also very comfortable especially in an area that has a lot of culture. I would highly recommend going here if you are ever in the area.
Jae did an awesome job serving my friend and I. He was attentive and accommodating, with a positive attitude. Thank you Jae!!

The worst experience....someone spent over $200 to have food delivered to my mom and family to help out with the passing of my sister. You guys gave uber eats the wrong order. Instead of remaking it for my mom you was more concerned on who was gonna pay for the new order....ummmm don't you think you need to make it right on you. My mom drove from North Highlands to go get the order and yall gave her a hard time on making shit right....this was her first time coming and I'm sure you lost a customer

We were here on July 26, 2020. They were open for outside dining. Tables spaced out pretty well. Staff wore masks and gloves and our server was very pleasant. Along with our entrees, we got their sweet tea and let me tell ya, boy it was shhhweet and DELISH!

Caesar Salad + shrimp was so, sooo good. My favorite part was the cornbread croutons. Only thing I knew I should've asked for was dressing on the side - it was a bit tooooo much for me.

Catfish and sides were yummy! My fave here were the greens - just a hint of that smoked turkey flavor.

Gumbo was OK in my opinion. Nothing too special.

Portions were HUGE and worth the try. If you're looking to satisfy your southern food craving w/a twist, go here!

The fried catfish was amazing! It was a bit saltier than I'd like, but the fish was so tender and the batter was perfectly crisp. The charred oakra was a great side, but I would go for the collared greens next time. The service was very slow, but I think the food was worth the wait.

Well I wish they would fix the check in feature on Yelp because the wait for a party of two on a Sunday was close to an hour.  Fortunately the courtyard on the side of Fixings is a nice place to wait.

Fixings is smaller than I had imagined so this probably contributed to our wait time.  InsIde is cozy and nicely appointed.  Music is sort of loud but I enjoyed it.

We tried several things today:
Deep Fried Deviled Eggs, must eat these with the small salad provided to get the right flavors.  Bland without the salad yummy with.
Ala carte fried chicken thigh, super crunchy, well seasoned juicy chicken, scrumptious.
Catfish with okra, black beans and hush puppies.  My friend said everything was delish.  I tried the funny looking okra and it was the best I had ever had by a mile.  I also tried the hush puppies which taste like deep fried corn bread another hit.
Smothered pork chops with mac n cheese and red beans and rIce.  My pork chops were to thin making them chewy.  It would be a better idea to serve one thick chop which woUld make it easier to not overcook them.  The red beans and rice and Mac n cheese were both super delicious.
Cherry Bomb and Adult Strawberry Lemonade both tasty drinks on the sweet side.

Service was astonishingly good and friendly considering how busy the place was.  In addition I really appreciated seeing a Kevin Johnson on the floor running his business like a good owner should.  Overall this place lives up to the hype for me.  I am definitely trying chicken and waffles next visit.

I gave fixins a try. The gravy was caked up on the chicken. I'm sorry, but it was terrible. The greens were gritty as if they weren't fully cleaned. Not a good experience. I should listen to Greg H. He told me it wasn't good, but noooooooo I had to ignore my good buddies advice.  May be I was supposed to have added water to the chicken. SMH!!!

Yo this place HIT. Their koolaid was so good. The chicken and waffles is a must get. The waffles were so fluffy and the chicken was so flavorful. This is a must get. Service was EPIC. Our waiter was very patient with us as this was our first time getting soul food. He explained every question we asked with the utmost respect and kindness

My boyfriend and I wanted to try a new brunch spot, so we finally visited Fixins on Sunday. We were lucky to find street parking right outside. 

We only had to wait 5 minutes for a table, but note that you can add yourself to the waitlist on Yelp. The hostess desk was often unattended as the hostess was taking guests to their table, so I think they could benefit from having two hostesses. 

The restaurant's decor is very modern and the table was clean. Our server Jena was very friendly. After ordering our food arrived in 15 minutes. 

I had the blue plate special ($13) and my boyfriend had the two piece dark-meat fried chicken and waffle ($14). I chose bacon to go with over easy eggs and potatoes. The bacon was thick and crispy. The potatoes had a little spicy kick, which was nice and different than expected, but a little on the mushy side. The eggs weren't crispy at all, which was weird to me.  The fried chicken was crispy and juicy, but the waffle tasted pretty standard to me. The portion sizes were enough to fill us without being stuffed, but I noticed that the lunch portions look significantly larger. 

The breakfast was good, but not amazing enough for me to drive out of the way for it. I'd recommend if you're in the area or in the mood for southern food. I would return to try their lunch.

Im thoroughly upset I don't live in Sac because this place is ahhhmazing. Did I order too much food? Absolutely. Do I have regrets? Absolutely not. I loved the flavored butters that came with my meal. I also ordered a side of gravy (not pictured) and it was scrumptious. The chicken was juicy and the waffle was perfectly cooked. Roscoes who? Our waitress was friendly, service was great and the vibes of the restaurant are lively with great music playing throughout the restaurant. I 100% will be back, there are more things I need to try & the adult kool aid cocktails are one of them. If I were you I'd definitely check out this restaurant you wont be disappointed

I ordered the smothered chicken and also tried the fried catfish. Overall, the service was great, but the food was too salty for my taste. They had some unique options thought that I've never had. The sides were decent, but also too salty. I am definitely looking to try more southern soul food, but probably won't come back to this one.

Hands down the best soul food in town!

We arrived on Thursday afternoon and we were seated right away. This restaurant has a great ambiance and atmosphere. Our server was attentive and the food is freaking delicious. We tried the fried chicken with mac salad and collard greens and oxtails with mac and cheese. Both options tasted great. If you haven't tried Fixins you need to go. Shout out to sweet Aunt Pauli for sharing the background story of the owner and the establishment.

Food selection was on point. It's a good variety but not too much. Potions were plenty, and appetizers were uniquely designed. Fried green tomatoes, deep-fried deviled eggs to name a few. We were a group of 12, so we ordered everything on the menu from smothered porkchops, fried chicken, catfish, oxtails, tofu, and the fixins were spectacular. Definitely a must try when in Sacramento.

The food here is heavenly! Elevated comfort food with incredible flavors. The service is friendly and generally prompt. The ambiance is cool and comfortable with a hip neighborhood vibe. They also have very tasty cocktails.

My favorite restaurant!!  I love fried chicken and they do it right. Juicy chicken and the crust is amazing. And the sides! My favorites are the okra, so smoky and flavorful, and perfect potato salad. 
Sometime there is a wait list for seating, but it has always gone fast for us. The servers have been friendly and attentive. 
It can get loud in there, cause there is always so many people. It's the loud of a good time. So don't bring you sleepy babies. But I think it's family friendly for everyone. 
Go and enjoy!

This was a great meal for being in Sacramento for the first time. All the dishes we got were delicious. My favorite is the oxtail and fried chicken. The oxtail is slow cooked and meat falls off the bone. It also has a great sauce and cooked to perfection. Fried chicken was crispy and not dry. They also have a great banana pudding dessert. Service was great. Everyone is so friendly. The whole experience was very enjoyable.
Great food! Nice clean restaurant! Upbeat friendly staff! Really recommend this place not many good soul food spots around.

Still a huge fan of Fixins! The food is consistently excellent and they manage well with how busy they've gotten. I had dessert here for the first time the other day (unbelievable I know, but the portions are very filling so I never got dessert), and the banana pudding and peach cobbler were delicious! Still recommend making reservations in advance if you plan to come.

My husband ordered the gumbo without shrimp. My husband said the gumbo was excellent. The roux was flavorful, it reminded him of Louisiana. My  fried chicken was cooked to perfect perfection. Crispy and flavorful skin, just the way I like it. The chard okra was good, but a little on the salty side.The red beans and rice, and the grits with cheese were good. I would definitely tell my friends and family about this place. My husband and I were immediately greeted and seated. Our server was excellent. She was attentive and friendly. The ambience was friendly and inviting. The place was pretty busy. People were enjoying their food, laughing and having a great time. Fixins soul food kitchen did not disappoint. I almost forgot. The bathrooms were extremely clean. As you guys know, before eating at an establishment, I always check the  women's restroom (I'm not able to go inside the men's restrooms).

Everything was great: the food, service, and outdoor seating. I ordered the fried catfish with sides of collards greens and red beans and rice. They won we over with the sides. The collards were cooked just right and had the amount of vinegar I like. In terms of the fish, I usually want mine fried until the outside turns into a really crisp shell. It wasn't like that here, but the fish was especially tender (outside was still crispy by normal standards). I had a few bites of the oxtail with rice. Super rich and tasty. The rice alone was great. Their fried chicken was well seasoned and had a light peppery flavor. The only thing I thought was just ok was the adult Kool-Aid. It was too sweet for me but I'm not sure what I was expecting. I often forgot to put sugar in mine when I was a kid. Go here, sit outside, and enjoy the food.

A couple years ago, I had ran into the owner, former Sacramento mayor and NBA All-Star, Kevin Johnson and briefly talked to him. He mentioned that he had a Soul Food restaurant and recommended I give it a try. 
Fast forward a couple of years and I finally came to Fixins with a few friends. It was clearly a popular place on a Saturday night and we waited for about 30 minutes to get our table...and after having eaten there, I can see why! We got the Deviled Eggs, Catfish Nuggets, Fried Chicken, Spicy Chicken Sandwich, and the Chicken & Waffles. We also got a couple peach cobblers for dessert. All of the food was absolutely delicious from start to finish. Everything was served hot and full of flavor. I had not had Kool-Aid in a long time, but both the plain Kool-Aid and the alcoholic Kool-Aid drinks were great as well. 
The service was very friendly too and the entire visit was a splendid time.
Fixins always has great food, especially the fried chicken and biscuits. The service is consistently attentive.

The food was delicious and impeccable, however, from the greeter who didn't greet to most of the staff  were dreadful.  No smiles, no hellos, not receptive at all. My server Mia and the Asian guy were the only people to say hello and made my group feel comfortable.  Not the best experience for an almost empty restaurant. Fire the staff and start over
Delicious food, great service and a very cool location.  Love all of the decorations inspired by the culture.

Location is nice in Oak Park and parking was easy in a Tuesday evening but the place was definitely busy but we got seated right away. 

Adult koolaid 9/10, really liked soft ice cubes! Easy to drink. 
Mac n cheese 10/10
Yams 10/10
 Chicken 8/10 -got a little soggy on one side
Biscuit with fruit butter 10/10
Pimento cheese -7/10 needed more salt on chips
Chicken sandwich -8/10 buffalo flavored hot sauce was good for a non-buffalo/non-spicy chicken sandwich fan but would try original next time!
 Fries that came with sandwich-7.5/10 very solid 
 Whiskey neat- 10/10 one of the best whiskeys I've had 
Purple kool aid-10/10 perfectly made
Blue kool-aid -10/10 sweet tart, made right 

Staff is super friendly and helpful! Got checked in on by manager, had Austin who was attentive and great. 

Easily come back to get the food again and try more! Will also recommend to friends and family. 

 Will update with banana pudding review but it looks bomb: it was good!! Solid 8/10. But nothing can probably beat my moms haha I really enjoyed it and would eat it again

They get very busy, so it's kind of hard to get the waiter/ waitress to check on you. Chicken and waffles was amazing! Love the strawberry butter very different. Gumbo was mid however. We ordered the blue cool aid super good. 2 orders of fried devil eggs bc they were that good! Banana pudding wasn't hitting tho would definitely skip that next time. Food was decent.
Their chicken is one of the best chicken that I have ever had specially their food are all affordable and has great price!

I had heard about this place from a friend so when we were finally in the area, we took advantage and had to make a stop to eat. Upon first arriving, I noticed the vibe... positive, uplifting and just happy people eating, serving and making drinks. We asked to sit outside because it was a nice day and were happily accommodated. Our server was nice, outgoing and attentive! My husband ordered a Hurricane and I ordered a Raspberry Fizz drink which was so good! We ordered deep fried bacon hard boiled eggs because we had never even heard of those and they did not disappoint! My husband ordered the fried catfish, fried okras and coleslaw and said it was fire. I ordered the fried chicken (which was perfectly seasoned on the outside as the inside), collard greens and mac and cheese. It was delicious! The food was reasonably priced, the music they played was 5 stars and went with the whole soul food vibe and the service was great, the mgr stopped by our table to check on our experience which is always nice. I would recommend ordering a few appetizers to start because the side dishes are pretty small and after dinner, we were still hungry so ended up ordering the pimento cheese dip and chips to fill up, still not worth taking a star off though. Highly recommend this spot for a date night or a group of friends!

Soooooo Goood! If you're having a large party definitely make reservations in advance they are awesome at accommodating your party. We had a few minute non-RSVP people decide to show up and rightly so the wait was two hours! Luckily you're allowed to sit at the bar and order food there so those non-RSVP yours were still able to enjoy time with us ;) The staff is super friendly and on it! Great location plenty of parking all around, and if you are waiting a couple extra hours pop next-door to old soul coffee ;) 

Leftovers, if there are any, can we reheat it in the air fryer and taste like they did at the restaurant. Find and follow directions online ;)

Fixins is our go-to spot when we are craving soul food. Everything on the menu is amazing, no complaints! But the Oxtail dish is to die for!! We have tried every item on the menu but this is worth every penny. Make sure you make reservations because they get packed, especially on the weekends.
Very delicious food and service was outstanding . Catfish, chicken and oxtails were delicious.. kool aid! Mmm good

I don't know why I gave them another chance after almost a year of not going & saying I wouldn't be back. They must have new management because the food was not top tier. Nothing like when they first opened... We ordered oxtails with rice , catfish nuggets, grits & koolaid. Why 6 minutes later, they notified us that our food was ready!?
 Now even a person who doesn't cook, knows that when food is instantly ready that means ITS NOT FRESHLY COOKED... sure enough, All the food was cold. WHY ARE YALL SERVING COLD FOOD THAT HAS BEEN SITTING OUT???? For $39 I shouldn't have reheat anything. The koolaid is good but it's basically diabetes in a cup. so hey drink at your own risk.

I'm fixin to give y'all the scoop on Fixins Soul Kitchen. 

I was super excited to try not only a new restaurant, but one that would sing to my soul...

The yelp app for call ahead doesn't work.  We arrived in hopes of having a short wait.  We were greeted by grumpy hostesses, who confirmed that they'd disabled the call ahead on yelp.  

We lucked out, and only had a 20 minute wait.  My mouth was watering as I watched dish after dish pass by.  I kept trying to breathe in the goodness that I saw. No scent filled the air.  That my friends, is a sign. 

It took a good 15 minutes before we were greeted by wait staff.  Another 10 minutes before our drinks arrived.  When we ordered, the waiter looked like he couldn't be bothered. 

We started the meal with fried green tomatoes.  I had spied a few dishes being served prior to being seated.  They looked good. 

The tomatoes arrived fairly quickly.  They were nicely presented on the plate, with a swish of sauce beneath them.  The tomatoes themselves lacked flavor.  Including the breading.  It was as plain as it comes. The sauce was super spicy. All this means is a complete imbalance of flavor blending.  

Our entrees arrived- the hot chicken sandwich was super huge. Hot wasn't the style, but again a spicy sauce layered with coleslaw, on a tasteless bun.  The only flavor that came through, was the sauce.  

I had ordered the fried chicken. This girl will break diet plans for good fried chicken.  This girl wishes she hadn't.  The chicken was moist, but where is the seasoning?? This is the make or break.  The skin was crispy, but that was it. Together, it didn't pass the taste test. Remember when I said earlier that you should be able to smell the food in the restaurant? I truly mean that. If you cannot smell the amazing flavors of the food being served, think twice about your dining options. Especially when it comes to fried chicken. You should be able to smell that goodness wafting out of the kitchen, if nothing else, at least get a hint of yum in the air from some of the tables.   

The mac & cheese was dry, and the strawberry butter that was served with the cold biscuit, I'm still trying to figure out. I get that it should've been like putting strawberry jam on a biscuit, but it to fell on its face.

I really wanted to like the chicken. No, I wanted to love the chicken. And I really wanted to love Fixin's. I just found the food to be overpriced, and not worth the long wait that others have commented about for the food. My chicken should've been flavorful both the skin as well as the meat.   I should not need a variety of sauces to eat fried chicken. I know plenty of cooks the pride themselves on their fried chicken. They would be appalled at the need for sauce just to eat the food.  

The table next to us had ordered dessert. For the $10 cost for dessert, I was embarrassed by the small serving.

If you are looking to get your Fixin of soul food, you may be better off making your own.

I loooove this spot. I'm really glad the location is very far from me or I be there to often. I've tried almost everything there with friends. Try the waffles with the butter. OMG amazing. I love the decor the wait staff the service is fast and quick. Just a nice spot to have a good lunch or dinner.

A fix for everything.

Wow, long review short, this place is a winner!

If you plan on coming here make sure to have practice some patience because the first time we tried to eat here the wait was 45+ minutes -- our second attempt on a Sunday evening was a little better but we still ended up waiting over 25 minutes.

The restaurant was PACKED but the staff did well to ensure the flow of everyone coming and going was smooth, but no onto the food.

A few months ago the craze all over town was to try those Chicken Sandwiches from that one fast food chain...the hype came and went and of course I wanted to see for myself how Fixins $15 Chicken Sandwich compares to the sub-$5 fast food version was. The answer? Worlds apart.

This visit I opted for the Hot Chicken Sandwich, it arrived doused in their hot sauce -- only way to properly have it. The chicken was nice and moist, other places I've tried the chicken was a little on the dry side but not here. I did ask for the chipotle sauce on the side which the correctly did, another plus!

To top off the trip, we got to also meet and talk to owner and former NBA Star/Mayor Kevin Johnson -- he came in, greeted everyone and went about his business.

It was great to see an establishment like this in the heart of the Oak Park area here in Sacramento -- great food, great staff, overall great experience.

I'll definitely be returning!

The food is great! The atmosphere is welcoming. My son took me there for brunch on Mother's Day! It was almost like eating my grandmother's cooking. Then the Kool- Aide options just made the whole meal better!

I could write a long review explaining how delicious this food was but you wouldn't truly feel me or understand. Go. If you crave a 10/10 soul food meal, go. After 3 tries this place has only made me fall more in love with each bite. The service is as quick as it can be for the volume of people. Cute place. Good cocktails and amazing food. Go!

The oxtail plate was a hit! I had a side of collards that were pretty good but not like my down south spots lol 
I did order the hush puppies for my bread they were okay. 
My plate portion was just right I had enough to take home for left overs which is always a plus in my book for soul food! I want to try their fish next! 
The vibes, music, and customer service was excellent. I love supporting black owned businesses and seeing them thrive in all areas! 
I will be back and bringing my colleagues.
Keep doing your thang Fixins!

We ordered gumbo with shrimp, my friends and I was really surprised because the food was amazing they'd served it as fast as they can. The service was commendable. And the fact the the ambiance was really cool and friendly. Overall, I love the entire experience.

Had the:
Appetizer: 
Fish Bits
Deviled Eggs

Entre: 
Fish sandwich w/ fixins of redbeans & rice and mac & cheese.

Customer service was great and food delicious. Fish had a light batter and although fried, did not feel heavy. Definitely, recommend.

This place has excellent food.  Cooked fresh.  I'm going back today
The staff is great and the atmosphere is down to earth.

I like the friendly customer service

Came to this place per another yelp elite recommendation. Shout out to Douglas Y. For the recommendations because the food hit the spot. 

Food is as good as many restaurant in Nashville. 

Oxtail: 5 stars. Pretty tender well seasoned, being very savory with a kick of heat (spiciness) at the end. Served over a bed of rice with your own choice of side - which I chose collard greens. 

Collard greens: 5 stars. I enjoyed this so much I wanted to make its own separate review. Salty with a hint of acidity and the texture of the collard green was just right still maintaining a slight crunch. If I had a bigger stomach, I could order a separate side of this and eat it by itself. 

Shrimp and grits: 5 stars. Large prawns with grits in the middle. I like the gReen onions on top of the grits because it changes the flavor slightly periodically, changing up the taste a bit. 

Fried chicken: kids enjoyed the chicken. Didn't have it so can't say how good it is. But well fried and not soggy. 

Mac and cheese: okay, I had my doubt but was it so cheesy and tasty ( tasted different type of cheese) and the pasta itself still had good texture and not over cooked.

Grilled okra: 5 star. No goey stuff since it was grilled and I don't know how they flavor it but I liked it. I would select this side again 

Overall very good food and I glad I came. I'll make it a point to stop by when I'm in the area.
Chandra was my waitress and i absolutely loved loved loved her energy. Food was good but couldn't eat too much due to drinking last night lol. But everything about the place is on point

My family traveled from New York for the week. And the 1st place my wife and I took the was here because we have a great decent experience everytime. Yet if something is not right, they are good about fixing it on the spot. However, yesterday was a bad experience. Very bad and it due to the server more than anything. (Maia) I believe her name was. 1st my father in-law order 2 sides with his dish. Mac and cheese, and red beans and rice. But received cold slaw. I order the same sides and received greens. Things happen so wept it under the rug. My wife order grilled chicken salad. But the meat was not fully cooked. Yes raw meat on a salad. I spent 15 year in General management in the food industry. I should caught this myself. So when I tried to explain to Maia that I spent 15 years in the food industry, she must have to offense to that because before I could say it's not her fault he cut me off and said well I spent 20 years in it.. I found this funny because after 20 years, you should get your sides right And caught the raw chicken. This place is nice but it was a bad time I guess..  I had a 137$ tap I only left 5 for the respect of the industry. I would left way more but I just couldn't.

5 years ago, I gave this restaurant a 1 star rating based off of all the events that prevented me from ever actually trying out their food or eating there in the first place.

Congratulations to them for getting another star putting them at a 2/5, I guess, now that I have had a chance to dine in. 

It was a Sunday late afternoon and all of my favorite brunch places were either closed or closing, so I finally decided it was time I keep an open-mind and give this place another try. A few months can change a lot of things much less a few years, right? So much improvements could have been made during that huge time span...But...Some things will always remain the same, and unfortunately for me that day, it was their horrible customer service. 

For a restaurant that was just moderately busy with half the tables inside and out still unoccupied, service was so slow to the point where I actually started taking it personal like it was intentional. It felt intentional because we literally sat there for over 15 minutes with NUMEROUS, I mean many, many different staff anywhere from hosts/hostess, servers, and busers who all walked back and forth next to us and around us, but completely ignoring our table. 

Now growing up Asian, you know it is going to take more than that to hurt my feelings like truly. I eat at hole in the wall Asian places all the time where I have to go get my own water and veggie plates (mhm, some of you probably know exactly which restaurant I'm thinking about too). But I don't mind. Why? Because the food is legit. They're not worried about customer service. They're too busy maintaining food quality. But here? What's their excuse? Food was totally mid. Fried chicken was cooked just right, but the flavor was nothing to rave about. Candied yams were bathe in butter. Mac & cheese was mediocre. The sweet "tea" was sugar water, no tea. Just...sweet water. When we sat at the booth that had a picture saying "A little bit of tea and a whole lotta sugar", I thought it was just a cute little joke. It wasn't. Waffle that came with the chicken and waffle meal? Disgustingly thin and limp. 

If you're going to be rude, at least have good food. Balance, you know? (Like how the sweet tea should've been).

Never again.

Everything about this place was on fire! From the deep fried  deviled eggs ( yep I said deep fried), to the fried chicken. Be prepared to chose the pieces you want. Was off the hook! Atmosphere had good vibes as well as the excellent service was awesome!!  Fixings was recommended to us by a guy waiting in line with us!! Will definitely be back!!
Been wanting to try this place for a while. Finally got to and the food was amazing and the service was great!

My boyfriend and I came here for dine-in and had an AWESOME experience.

We came in on a Sunday afternoon around 4:00 PM. We walked in and were immediately greeted by the hostess at the front. I wish I had gotten her name, she was so warm and inviting. After about 20-25 minutes, we were seated inside of the restaurant. Our server was Jenna and she was the best! She was attentive, helpful, and knew what we needed without us having to ask. Thank you for taking good care of us, Jenna!

For drinks, we ordered the Adult Kool-Aid and the Watermelon Margarita. They were both good, but in my personal opinion, could have used a little bit more alcohol. But still good, nonetheless.

For food, we ordered the artichoke dip, 2 wings & a waffle, side of gumbo, and a cornbread muffin. The chicken was fried to perfection and the cornbread muffin was bomb!

Overall, the food was great and the service was amazing! I will definitely be coming back here soon!

Hands down the best food I have had in a looooong time!! So much flavor and deliciousness. Our server Nataya was so kind and welcoming, definitely setting the bar high. 

We started with the deviled eggs appetizer- highly recommend. We had the fried chicken and cat fish entrees which were both so flavorful and cooked to perfection! The sides were all delicious too but our favorite was the collard greens and oohhh don't forget the biscuit. 

We will definitely be back!!

I have been wanting to try this place since it opened but somehow didn't get the chance until today. On weekends, you will need reservations for dinner which was no problem using Yelp.

Now to the Fixin of things

Decor is simple yet clean & modern
Service from the moment we walked in was excellent 

Food/menu is easy to choose and we chose Chicken Gumbo, Oxtail/Rice with Mac/cheese, Grits/cheese, Caesar salad
For appetizers:  Fried catfish, Okra/Bacon, Fried devils eggs/cheese
All were very tasty & delicious 
Needless to say, we were stuffed as the servings were good sizes

I would recommend this place for Soul food Fixing!

BTW: Our own Kevin Johnson was also present & I got a chance to greet him as We were leaving

This is a neighborhood favorite that's going nationwide for good reason. 

Don't skip out on the corn bread. 

Try the oxtail. 

You'll never be disappointed.

Went for brunch with a friend and everything was really good, with one exception their Mac n'cheese was way off, the server did exchange it for a fresh portion and it was better. We had chicken and waffles, the biscuits (so good), cornbread and another entree along with a few drinks. Was definitely curious about the adult kool-aid though I didn't try one, maybe next time!
Great soul food loved the potato salad and red beans. The mac and cheese was my kids favorite by far.

Came to Fixins today with a huge appetite. The chicken had authentic southern seasonings and crispness.  The waffle melted in my mouth. I could have eaten the  cinnamon butter straight out of the cup. The red, purple, and blue Kool Aid were delicious. It reminded me of when I was a kid and use to lick the Kool Aid packets. 

Our waitress, Chandra, had a wonderful personality and extremely courteous.  

I'm Fixin to be coming back soon?

Overall excellent experience. Tuesday night was buzzing but had plenty of seating. Keyshawn is still a new employee, he announced right away, but he did a good job overall keeping us happy customers. Our food came out really quickly (though it took a little longer to get our cocktails).

So much on the menu is enticing, and we definitely chose well. The fried green tomatoes appetizer was small but very pretty. Our mains were huge! The three piece chicken and waffles was very satisfying : moist chicken and flatter, cinnamon waffles, which were great with chicken bites. The charred okra and mac and cheese should not be missed! Okra included pieces of bacon, and I always love mac with pieces of cheese. Lastly, the rum raisin bread pudding was good - a tiny bit too sweet for me, but I loved the texture.

Had to dock a star because everything was just a little bit too salty. Also, the old fashioned was super watered down (love the bourbon bar concept, though).

Kevin gave us a solid handshake when we walked out, which is a nice show of appreciation.

I look forward to coming back!

I came here on a weekday morning and was surprised on the hours. We were seated immediately since there was no wait. There were many great options and it was a good idea to share.

I ordered the Fried Catfish with a side of Mac and Cheese and Creamed Corn. My friend ordered the Fried Chicken with a side of Green  Beans and Potato Salad. 

I was very impressed with the food and everything was delicious! The fried chicken was crispy and juicy and the sides were all delicious. I would highly recommend going with friends and ordering different entrees and sides to share. 

There's plenty of seating and a full bar to quench your thirst. They also have Kool-Aid! Our bartender/server was attentive and made sure we were taken care of. The banana pudding was delicious as well!

I would definitely recommend this place and I would come back to try the other menu items!

What can I say about this place besides its GOOD AF!!!! I literally drove from Oakland on several occasions to get some of that nice golden friend catfish. They cook almost like a southern person. The service is always amazing. Smiles and fast food to the table. The people are walkways a vibe. Everyone is in a good mood when the food good!

Where do I start so me and Bestie went to go eat today for my first time, the food is amazing. Drinks amazing whoever made the Kool-Aid. I need to know your recipe. Everything was so fresh, the service from our waiter was 10 out of 10 homegirl was on top of her service and kept coming back periodically to check on us. I love the ambience, the location is in a good area for service eye-catching, the fact that they have indoor and outdoor is amazing. I'm pretty sure it looks really nice at night. Also, the music is definitely giving what is supposed to be given it's a vibe 10 out of 10 five stars definitely will be back. Thank you

The food tasted good, the service was good but a little slow. But would it be a soul food joint with fast service? 

The greens had a bit of kick, the yams were a lil sweet for my tastes but I think everyone likes them sweeter than I do anyway. The catfish breading was too salty for me, but thy fish orator was fresh, tender and juicy! 

The Side Car was a recommended drink, and it lived up to expectations!

Had to wait an hour on a Saturday night to be seated but it was worth the wait for some legit soul food!

I ordered the fried chicken with Mac n cheese and collard greens. The fried chicken was super juicy and had some amazingly seasoned skin. Highly recommend. But even better than the chicken was the Mac n cheese. Holy smokes the cheesiness was intense! The collard greens were alright and so was the biscuit. The biscuit comes with a sweet strawberry like butter spread.

It's a pretty noisy ambiance but it's a nice casual spot. Servers were incredibly friendly.

Long waits while there are wide open tables. if you go up they tell you it's at least an hour wait yet if u walk back outside and go on an app and book a table before you walk in they seat you immediately, how does that make sense? for mediocre overpriced food, poor service, and servers often have a unwelcoming attitude unless they're trying to get a tip. Servers are extremely forgetful and slow despite there being a large amount of them and not that many people and orders to be fulfilled. i ordered a blue kool aid (never came), i asked for a kids menu (never came), and i asked for a plate (had to get myself), the service was unacceptable whether packed or not and it was not even packed. I saw so many workers just walking around the premises yet doing nothing. chit chatting with costumers they knew while i was still missing the extra plate i asked for 10 minutes ago. Once seated it took my server 20 minutes to take our order and another 45 minutes for a cheeseburger and fried chicken. this wouldn't be a problem if it was packed and workers weren't just walking around doing nothing. The hosts that greet you at the door are not welcoming at all. Matter of fact it's always the same guy and girl and they always look at you rude and speak to you without respect or service.  they laugh and have fun with other workers and treat you like you're the one ruining their day. I had to go up and request my extra plate after it not being brought to me and the host was incredibly rude and dismissive. I told him i was sharing a cheeseburger after he asked what size plate i needed and proceeded to bring me the smallest plate they had with an attitude. Even if i were a rude customer they had no right for this poor of service. I was polite and not rude at all even after putting up with them. Yet for some reason the staff thinks and acts like they're above you when they should be serving and doing the most they can as it is their simple JOB. unbelievable. i've been here multiple times and it's always the same, this most recent time was definitely my last and i would not recommend. You can go somewhere nicer, more affordable, with better food, and way better service. Fixins needs to seriously fix it.

The whole experience at Fixins was great. When you walk in you can't help but be impressed with the whole vibe. Then our server came over ready to help our party of 10 and she didn't a phenomenal job. Then sweet Auntie Pearl stop by the table. I can safely say we all would go back just for Auntie Pearl. Finally the food arrived and it was a beautiful site. There was nothing left on the plates hour later when they were picked up. Do yourself a favor add candied yams and corn muffins to your order. You will be grateful you did. Can't wait to go back.

Some of the best soul food around!!! Great Job!!! There has to be somebody's grandmother working in the kitchen.

LORT-HAV-MERCY!!!

Came here after I read reviews of them having a vegan menu but bad news is they had to downsize their menu at some point and got rid of it BUT good news is we were told they're bringing it back, soon! So we definitely will be checking back in here! We stayed for some fries and a salad, all which were good. Definitely liked the vibe of this place, friendly staff and will be back when they being back their vegan menu and update here!

My husband, cousin, and I went there for lunch on July 23rd. Luckily we had a reservation because the place was packed.  We were seated right away, which was nice, and our server, Alizae was very friendly and attentive. She took very good care of us. 
My husband ordered oxtails and rice, with a side of potato salad. My cousin ordered oxtails and rice, with a side of collard greens and yams. and I ordered smothered pork chops, with a side of mac n cheese and potato salad. The oxtails and rice were very tasty, the sides were decent, however, I was not fond of the smothered pork chops. I ordered banana pudding to go, but I had to taste it first to see what I was working with. It was pretty good. Despite the smothered pork chops not being my favorite, the food at Fixins was quite tasty, and I do plan on visiting again.
First time here and all I can say is...WOW! Friendly service, huge portions and fantastic food. Can't wait to come back and try more of their menu!!
Best souls food I've had in the Bay Area. Customer service was top notch! KJ's auntie put the frosting on top!
Sooooo good. Servers are so nice and food is always hot and never dry. Blue koolaid is fire

Food is mediocre. The catfish is "Swai" not real catfish. Be aware you are served one swai filet. They make it sound like multiple pieces on the menu. The seasoning was good. The wait was 40 minutes for a to-go. Just not impressed. The Mac side was good. The potato salad is made with sweet relish so if that's your thing you might like it. I have attached a pic tell me what you think?

So we made our first visit Sunday afternoon and were not disappointed.  About 30 minutes after we arrived we were seated in a booth and scanning our menus. Service was timely and enjoyed the atmosphere too. The food is definitely worth the wait and lived up to the hype I'd read this past week. Deep Fried Deviled Eggs were a tasty start. I loved the smothered pork chops too. Already made a reservation for next week, there are a lot of items still to try...

I ordered takeout on Grubhub.  This type of food is always better when it is eaten immediately after preparation and it's still piping hot and at its peak texture.  That said.  The fried catfish sandwich was my favorite of the sandwiches I ordered.  All of the flavors were paired nicely together.  The lettuce was the ingredient that wilted the most on this sandwich from the point of preparation until I was able to enjoy the sandwich.  I simply pulled the lettuce off but the pickles and onions and sauce still gave it the acid it needed to accompany the fried catfish filet.  The spicy chicken sandwich was the second sandwich I ordered and it was good.  The sauce had heat to it and reminded me of a buffalo sauce.  The spicy chicken sandwich had romaine lettuce on it and it held up better as this was a to go order if you remember.  The candied yams, a side item, I ordered were yummy.  French fries were served with both sandwiches.  They would have been at their best eaten at the restaurant.  I also ordered a side of grits.  The taste was there but the texture had become thick like paste.  For dessert, I ordered one banana cream pudding.  I split it with a friend because we were stuffed from our meal.  It was a good, classic banana cream pudding.  

Parking could be a challenge.  It is street parking out front.  I was fortunate to find a spot close to the restaurant.  There was a tent out front where patrons were lined up to pick up their takeout orders.  It was an efficient process.  

While standing in line for my order, I observed the restaurant had a nice, spacious patio area where a few customers were having dinner.

Baybee, when I tell you we slammed down that food...

I loved the environment. This was not my first time at this rodeo, however. I had visited before but only had the opportunity to dine outdoors on their patio. When I walked into the place, it was unrecognizable because I had never had the chance to see the restaurant's indoor seating area. I enjoyed the open-floor concept; it was airy and not too crowded. 

My friend and I were a little worried about the groups of people waiting to receive their food and thought the wait would be long. We were seated quickly since we had a 5pm reservation. 

We already knew what we wanted and glanced at the menu a bit. 

Our server came out of nowhere like lightning; she almost seemed out of breath. We asked questions and she answered with an informative attitude. I love that about servers! 

Also, PRO TIP: From the lips of our server, we came at the right time before the dinner rush (Wednesday). If you want to avoid the throngs of people, get there around 4pm/5pm. 

We ordered our food. My friend ordered the Catfish plate with yams and greens as their sides. I ordered the Shrimp and grits with a side of catfish nuggets because what those delicious catfish nuggets didn't know was that they were going into that delightful shrimp and grits gravy, OKKKURRR!!!

We also ordered those scrumptious appetizers: Chicken sliders and Mac and Cheese balls. Of course, you have to cut one of the Mac&Cheese balls in half and put it on that Chicken slider with some hot sauce and some of that gravy! I've died and resurrected myself!

Honey, that shit came out so fast, our heads were spinning. We didn't even have enough time to talk shit about the wait we thought we were going to have to live through. 

We ordered our drinks and food at the same time because like I said before, we already had a game plan. Our drinks came after 5 minutes, then our appetizers after another 5 minutes, and finally after a loooonnnnggg 3 minutes our entrees came out! Like damn, can we get some time to talk and chit chat!? I'm feeling rushed! (JK LOL).

Anyways, I'm rambling now! Let me go get my laundry out of the dryer now! Get yall asses down to Fixins ASAP! Enjoy the food and Black culture! And, tip these workers.

Their customer service/staff are just perfect ! Large generous portions, delicious comfort food and great atmosphere to enjoy your meal! Love their variety of sides their greens and okra are my fav One thing I was a bit down on was their corn bread muffin it's was more of a good light corn vanilla muffin then the classic cornbread I was hoping for Overall I'll still be back soon to try other dishes

What a great place!  We were in town for a conference, and this was maybe a 10-minute ride from the Hyatt near the Convention Center.  I recommend making a reservation, as this place was packed on a Sunday night.  
Aliyah was our waitress, and she was EXCELLENT!  One of my co-workers had never had soul food and Aliyah was patient in answering questions and had good recommendations. Everything was delicious: fried green tomatoes and catfish bites appetizers, fried chicken, with sides of mac and cheese and yams, and the rolls with some kind of berry cream cheese.  The yams were amazing, the best yams I've ever had!  And don't forget to try the sweet tea!  My friends really enjoyed their meals of shrimp and grits and oxtails too.  I am looking forward to eating at the Fixins' Soul Kitchen in Los Angeles.

Great food, good service. The place was packed even on a Sunday night. Half of us are vegan and they have a great vegan menu! Our daughter and husband raved about the fried chicken. Highly recommend, but make reservations.

Absolutely fantastic meal .  Came down from Portland, Oregon just to try Fixins and it did bit disappoint.  I had catfish meal with greens and potato salad.  Everything was perfectly cooked.  Staff was solid.  It was a perfect day.  I'm going to try the one in Detroit next!

Excellent food, great ambiance and friendliest of staff. We went in for Saturday brunch and I had chicken a waffles. Best fried chicken I've tasted in at least a decade. The strawberry butter on the waffle cut the sweetness with a perfect hit of tart. GO!!!

I have been wanting to try this place because it is Kevin Johnson's restaurant and everyone who has tried it recommended it. It excceed my expectations and more. Their chicken was mouthwatering along with the sides that came with it. Some of my favorites were the collared greens, mac and cheese, sweet potato and potato salad. 

Friendly service and a very family restaurant, perfect for any occasion or a night out. I would totally recommend this place if you in Sacramento!

My family and I wanted to try something new for my birthday and I came across Fixins Soul Kitchen and decided to go here. We were going to come in on a Sunday evening but after finding out there's no reservations or call ahead and that the wait was about an hour. We decided instead on go Monday evening and there was no wait when we showed up, so if you're going to stop in take that into account.

After arriving and being seated in the back area because we were a table to 5, we looked after the menus for a few minutes. The hostess was really friendly and before deciding on our meals we ordered drinks. For our meals my sister ordered the fried chicken meal, my girl friend ordered the fried catfish with mac & cheese & collard greens. I ordered the same meal but got the black eyed peas and the collard greens, while the youngest child got the fried chicken meal. And the older child got the fried chicken sandwich and fries.

The food was so good, the catfish was hot and crispy and the mac & cheese was amazing. We all also enjoyed the music as it 90's and 80's music we all enjoyed.

OVERALL: Great food, good establishment and vibe. And make sure you call ahead on the wait time, and possibly go in on a weekday when they're not as busy.

It was my first time going to this soul food restaurant here in Sacramento and I was not disappointed!! My friend Dakota invited me and my girls for dinner and I'm so glad we went! She made a reservation which made the wait process short and sweet! So I do recommend making a reservation if you plan on going as it does get very busy. We went at 7PM on a Sunday and it was a full house with more patrons still coming in. The food was amazing hit delicious  flavorful. There is a kids menu for the little ones. They keep the menus straight forward with great choices. They offer Kool laid in Red, Blue and purple flavors (Love the play on that tbh) our server was Kat and she was attentive and knowledgeable and very sweet  The Host Hayden was also kind attentive and got us set quickly and with no fuss ! The establishment was clean (Which in part had to do with Daivon who kept the tables clean and the dishes cleared) and the music was epic It took me back to the good ole days we danced, as we waited for our meals to arrive. I had the fried chicken plate with dark meat. (you do have a choice of dark or light.) My sides  were the candied yams, greens and Mac and cheese. The plate comes with 2 sides, but I ordered the Mac and cheese because I heard it was phenomenal. The food did not disappoint and I enjoyed every bite.

Delicious food with lively service!

I highly recommend trying the food here as everything was delicious and fresh. The food came out super fast, hot, and tasted great! The sweet tea was extra sweet (as expected), and the other drinks looked amazing! 

Our service was amazing, as she kept checking-in on us, and was super friendly. We were nervous as many reviews said wait service had attitudes, but we DID NOT experience this at all. Everyone was very friendly!

The restaurant seemed small, but it seemed like on better weather day there are some extra seating opportunities outside -- which would be nice!

When a colleague informed me that Kevin Johnson had just opened a soul food restaurant in Oak Park, I couldn't wait to find an excuse to visit his new establishment! There are far and few soul food (and BBQ) restaurants in Sacramento. Therefore, it is something like a big deal when a new soul food restaurant arrives in town. Ironically, a friend of mine wanted to catch up over food and drinks, so I quickly (and wisely) suggested that we visit "Fixins."
 
A friend of mine warned me that she waited nearly two hours to be seated, so I made sure that I was well-fed and packed a snack before making the trip to the restaurant. Upon arrival, we were told that there was a 45 minute to an hour wait. Since we were prepared for a longer wait, we were ecstatic to wait for an hour. A little after an hour later, we were being seated. Not happy about being seated at a table, my friend asked an employee if we could be seated in a booth. The employee said that we would have to be placed onto a waiting list for the booth because they were already accounted for. Unfortunately, the employee conveyed this message with an attitude. Therefore, another employee who was eavesdropping decided to bring the situation up to the owner himself, Mr. Kevin Johnson.
 
Kevin took the initiative of making multiple attempts to find us a booth, but was unsuccessful (it really wasn't that big of a deal). Meanwhile, Nickole (our waitress) brought us waters and took our orders. We shared the fried green tomatoes (appetizer) and I ordered the three piece fried chicken (dark meat) meal with a side of the mac & cheese and fries. This was my first time having fried green tomatoes and I was impressed (it made me regret not trying my grandmother's fried green tomatoes) by not only the texture of the tomatoes, but also the sauce paired with the tomatoes.
 
Fifteen minutes later our food arrived. Although the fried chicken had a dry appearance, I would later learn that things aren't always as they appear. The dark meat fried chicken was very flavorful and juicy. I found the mac & cheese to be delicious; however, I was disappointed by the portion size (I felt like I only had 3 bites before I was yearning for more). Although well-seasoned, the fries were a bit dry (as if they had been sitting too long after being removed from the fryer).
 
I was satisfied with my experience at Fixins. I'm really impressed by the cozy, family friendly vibe of the restaurant. There is plenty of seating for those who want to dine-in, and a roomy bar area for those looking to catch up over a couple of drinks. Everything was adequately priced and readily available to be ordered (not to be confused with the ill-prepared soul food restaurants that quickly run out of menu items).  Although Kevin Johnson was unsuccessful in securing a championship ring during his NBA career (thanks to Michael Jordan and company), he definitely secured a huge victory with this new restaurant. I will be back soon to treat my mom to a delicious birthday lunch!

I thought the food was excellent! We arrived ten minutes before opening and there was a line so you know it's popular. I would suggest showing up as they open or getting a reservation because the place filled up quickly on the weekend. 

Our server was friendly and they got our order in quickly. I got a bloody mary and it was spicy with tons of garnishes that were great. 

Definitely a place to try and you can go and get coffee next door afterward. There was plenty of street parking and the area was really walkable and cute. I would say the only off putting thing was the music playing and only because it was LOUD. I didn't expect it to be so loud but it was soulful and fit the vibe of the place. 

While the waffles were on the thin side (not a problem for me) I think it suited the chicken well. I'll be back for more!

Service was a little slow, the server would bring the food out, we ask for an extra spoon. No one comes back. We see them being out missing Fixen out, they turn around and then we had to ask 3 more people to bring it. We were almost done with the meal. I wish they had 2 catfish filets instead of one, but overall the food was great. We'll go back and hopefully they will be better staffed,

Still eating but bayyyyybeee .... From the time we walked in ...to our server Sierra.. then the appetizers and food! AMAZING. It's a whole vibe. Food came out quickly and was hot not luke warm... just the way it should be. We ended with two rounds of drinks and purchased food to go!

This place is phenomenal!!! Just amazing we had such a great time. We got there right before it start getting real busy and service was still great! We loved our food, everything was cooked to perfection and the serving size doesn't make you feel too big backed I like that. 10/10 !! We coming back.

I have been to Fixins since they opened in 2019...however, this time I took my granddaughter. She got the kids burger, and a waffle on the side. The waffle was bussin...but with the apple butter it was bussin' bussin. Then my honeylove tired the MAC N CHESSE and lost her mind. Needless to say, Fixins has taken the top spot from Captain Crab.

Service was excellent. 
I normally order to go....wait time was lengthy (Sunday),  but it was worth the wait.

This mimosa is the only thing keeping me from being so annoyed. The wait quoted is horrible. They told me 1hour it's been 1hour and 40 min and waiting and the host is rude. No apologies for quoting wrong... if the Restaurant is reservations only why even take walk ins. Could have gone somewhere else by now... waste of time
Was there for first time on Saturday food was great treated you as family atmosphere was great

Food is good, the cocktails are superb.
The service is great. JC is a major asset to the company! She gave excellent customer service. The ambiance is great! I love the music and the bathroom was clean. I give them an A+.

Food is consistently good. Best fried chicken. Service is excellent. Our server was very friendly and attentive. 
the decor, music, mood, and food all ties in together.

If you live in Sacramento I'm sure you have heard nothing but amazing reviews about the new hot spot in town!! I've honestly haven't heard anything bad about the food so it was a must try for me! First I want to point out that parking sucks! There is only a few in front of the restaurant.(you are a chosen on if you get one of these, lol). After driving around the block for 10 mins I got a parking spot! 

As soon as we got to the door I could tell the restaurant was packed! I thought how could this be! It's a Wednesday at 11am! The Hostess informed me the wait would be 30 mins, no problem. We received a text that our table was ready. As soon as we say we were greeted by our server. I've been eying the menu online so I was ready to order right away. I ordered the famous deep fried deviled eggs and the two chicken meal. After 10 mins we still hadn't received our appetizer. The server realized and went in the back to check. 5 mins later she came back and said sorry our order never was put in. Long story short we received our app and main meal at the same time.

Once we received our food I will say that everything was hot. The chicken was crispy, could have used more flavor for me. The Mac and cheese was just mac and cheese nothing special. The Greens I didn't like at all, didn't even finish them! The Greens has a sweetish taste and it just wasn't what I was used to. My two favorite things from this meal was the lemonade and the butter for the biscuit! Next time I will have to try something different from the menu. I have my eyes on the black eyed peas, hope they are as yum as the reviews say!

We finally got to try Fixins Soul Kitchen after hearing some many positive things about it!! We tried it on a Monday night.... knowing this is Kevin Johnson's spot, I was really excited! Fixins is located on 3rd avenue and parking is all street but when we came on a Monday, we found parking right away! 

The area honestly is not the best, but it's growing and developing with more business's and more attractions! 

When we walked into Fixins, it had a positive and uplifting atmosphere.... and it was pretty busy for a Monday evening! There was groups, families, and couples dining in! The bar area is pretty cool and it has plenty of TVs mounted for Monday Night Football!!! Decor is very modern, hip, and trendy!! 

The menu had plenty of options ranging from fried catfish, fried chicken, gumbo, shrimp and grits, burgers to desserts!!! Fixins has something for everyone! 

I ordered the 3 piece fried chicken with 2 sides! My sides were grits and green beans! Ah man, my first bite into my drumstick was amazing! It was crispy, hot, and juicy!! The batter was perfect and the chicken was hot and juicy and flavorful! Also, you can request dark or white meat! The grits was also amazing.... first time I had grits and I can't wait to eat it again! It was savory and slightly sweet.... went perfect with the saltiness is the chicken! The green beans was pretty good.... flavorful but i will try something else next time! 

My lady had the fried catfish, grilled okra, and fries!! The catfish was delicious... great batter and moist fish! The grilled okra was good but we always prefer it to be fried! The fries were excellent.... seasoned very well! 

For dessert, we had the banana pudding and it was delicious....  the whip cream and banana made it sweet, and the custard made it even better!!!

Overall, the food was delicious! I really look forward to coming back and trying another entree!!!

Erin was our waitress and she was awesome! She was friendly, professional and made our dining experience more fun!! She definitely should get some recognition!!! 

The only reason for knocking off 1 Star is because the music was so loud, it was hard to hear our groups conversation!! They play good music but it was just too loud! But other than that, great dining experience and I highly recommended!!!!

Peach cobbler was yummy. Fried chicken was seasoned great! Red beans and rice was good and Mac and cheese was ok. Gravy on smothered chicken was yummy. Chicken breast was as big as a whole chicken.

We ate here a few years ago I wasn't impressed.
Great chicken and waffles! My Bloody Mary was also delicious. The butter is what makes this place honestly.

Nice atmosphere. Beautiful location. Good service. A'ight Food. I must say I enjoyed the food at the Los Angeles location much better. The chicken and waffles I encountered  was not the same! Waaayy too much pepper in everyone at my tables chicken! Literally we had to take the skin off  and it was still spicy..and I like spicy food. This was just wrong. Like when you add too much pepper to the flour, but can't get it all out but use it anyway! Mac n cheese lacked flavor and cheese. Greens were OK. Red beans and rice were good. Catfish bites were delicious! I'd come back and give them another try.

Fixins restaurant is a literal godsend. Absolutely delicious, if you ever feel the urge to go eat there, then go because that was a message from the other side that you need some good food in your life. 

Our server was awesome and super attentive to us even though it was a super busy night. (awesome to see as the inverse happened to me and my gf the other night at a less exciting restaurant, where it was not busy at all and our server checked on us about 1 1/2 times) so shout out to our server Nataya for being the best! 

If you end up going to Fixins; get the oxtail, plus whatever else you want... you just gotta get the oxtail capeesh. Also the adult koolaid is exactly what it sounds like, bomb!

Came here for my nephew's birthday. We had the chicken and waffles, chicken sandwich and fried chicken. All
of the food was tasty, chicken, greens, and Mac and Cheese all were full of flavor. The staff was friendly. Looking forward to going back

The food was great!  The service was awful. Checked in to the host saying a open booth/table was for a large party so we had to wait... to only wait and having another hostess sit a couple at the same table while we were waiting. 
The waitress that came to our table said she wasn't our waitress our waitress would be coming soon. We placed our order with her after waiting so long to be seated.... The food came but another waitress never showed up.  I do however have to compliment Michael the busier.... He helped us get a box and our check.... Again the food was excellent..but the service is lacking.

I've always wanted to try this restaurant out and I'm so glad I finally did! I highly recommended that everyone tries the chicken sandwich. It was delicious! The mac and cheese is a must try as well! The service is good. There was a bit of a wait but it was worth it since the food was so yummy. It's a great place for families and friends to sit down and enjoy a good soul meal! I will be back again to try out different menu items

Food is rated at a A but the staff is always an A+! I love coming here with my family for lunch. The last time I was here I tried the fried catfish, candied yams, and Mac n cheese- the entree and two fixins (sides) were AMAZING. Everything was cooked very well and the crunch on the fried catfish was mesmerizing. The appetizers- fried catfish nuggets; the deviled eggs; and the artichoke dip were good but the artichoke dip was a little heavy on the grease. We told the server and another was prepared almost immediately. And of course you cannot go wrong with the red kool aid. We sat outside and while it was a little chilly, it was almost impossible to tell because of the heaters surrounding the patio. Will be back as soon as possible!

Delicious!!! The best Mac and cheese ever!!! And I'm a Mac and cheese snob. Kool aid is how we used to make it. The sweet tea is actually sweet. And the story behind the whole restaurant is beautiful. Love it!!!
Amazing food and service every time I go in! Aunt Pearl is ways a pleasure to speak with.

The fried tomatoes and the deviled eggs are amazing. 
The Oxtails are good, too, however, expect them to be slightly sweet. Their spiked sweet teas are delicious too. All the times I have gone, it was peak time so expecting slightly slower wait times. Definitely, a nice place to watch a game, casual date night, or if you are craving good southern comfort food!
