The food was A1, we ordered the Smothered Pork Chops and Fried Chicken with Macaroni and Cheese and Collard Greens. It was AMAZING & on top of that the service was impeccable. From the hostess to the waitresses they really know how to take care of their customers. 

Not to mention the environment had a really great vibe. We will be visiting again for sure.

my gf and I randomly found fixins one day looking for good soul food in the area, we have been about 4x in the last year and it's always has us coming back for more! We normally go for quick lunch or dinner dates. 

we normally get the chicken and waffles , greens and Mac and cheese off the fixins menu , and something added / new to try each time. 

we have never had bad service from this location , sometimes there's a wait but you can just do an open table reservation before you arrive to save time for waiting. 

They also do a happy hour Mondays- Friday 3-6pm
The yella dressing is awesome on the fried chicken sandwich. Friendly staff too! Have always had a great experience here.

Craving some southern soul food? If so, Fixins is a great place to go! One of my friends that lives in the area and recommended we try it and it was a great first experience. They have a cool space with seating both indoors/outdoors based on your preferences. The staff was great and quick to recommend their favorites and refill our cups whenever they were getting close to empty. 

| WHAT WE ORDERED | 

- Fried Catfish
- Mac & Cheese 
- Fried green tomatoes (can't remember if it was seasonal)
- Adult Kool-Aid

Yum! loved the fried green tomatoes as it added nice acidity to the meal and not something you find often. The first time I had them was in Atlanta and tend to order them every time I see it on a menu. As for the main entree I ended up getting the fried catfish with mac & cheese on the side. I enjoyed how the catfish was light and not oily and had a corneal batter coating (the only way!)

To pair with the main entree I ended up trying the adult kool aid to add a twist to a classic and it's nice if you enjoy sweet drinks.
My coworker recommended this place and it totally lives up to the hype. The fish and sides were delicious. Flavorful and fresh. Friendly service. Prices were a bit high but that's expected in 2023.
This is a great place to go in Sacramento! My family really enjoyed the ambiance and atmosphere! The food was amazing! My server provided great hospitality and customer service.

Went here for dinner and the food was amazing Collard greens ( remind me of mama) red beans and rice and the highlight was the catfish nuggets.  Friendly clean and I noticed the tables are sanitized after each guest. 
Highly recommend.

Oh, yeah! We love this place! Delicious! The barbecue sauce the ranch! We love the chicken! The patio! We have so much fun when we're there! We just absolutely love this place.
The food is good we really loved it and the DJ is a good touch 
Very nice place. Very good food absolutely love it.

Greeting=Excellent. Esa is amazing! She is a definite asset to this restaurant. She is friendly, personable and helpful. Esa, your smile lights up a room! Keep smiling :)

Service= 15 minute wait at table before anything happened. After that Erin kept everything moving great!

Food= Order put in at 1:35pm. It started arriving at 2:45pm. Everything arrived at different times. Thankfully, everything arrived hot and ready to eat.

Fried Chicken Omelet with Grits:
Tasted great, yet way too much cheese and not enough chicken. It said it was cheddar cheese, but it reminded me of govt. cheese back in the day. It was super thick. The grits were tasty and had a very nice consistency.

Oxtails in rice: very tender and tasty. It should have had a lot more rice 

Fried chicken, biscuit, greens and yams:
Chicken was tha bomb! That means it was yummylicious! Yams and greens were tasty. The portion of greens was too small in that tiny but cute skillet.

Potato Salad: Seasoned well. Too many onions for my taste.

Ambiance=Good. The music is quite loud, so we can't hear each other at the same table. Huh, what did you say???


Server=Erin. Matter of fact, yet knowledgeable of the menu and gives great suggestions.

In the end, we will return to try some other items. Be prepared to wait a while because a lot of other people like to come here as well. But, know this, once you get here you will be well taken care of.
Thanks Kevin, Michelle and staff!
Food was amazing!  Well seasoned, flavorful. We can't wait to go back the next time we visit Sacramento.

This was the best brunch I've had in Sac. The food was amazing, and our server Aimee was awesome.
Here's the details:
-Fried Chicken Salad 5 Stars.
Honey mustard dressing, delicious fried chicken, huge portion.
-Fried Catfish Nuggets 5 stars
So fluffy and fresh. Great tarter sauce.
Mac and Cheese5 stars
Out of this world. Creamy, a little crunch, just pure gooey goodness.
Roasted Okra 5 stars
The best okra I've ever had! Seriously.
Can't wait to come back to try the gumbo and chicken n waffles.
Looked like a great cocktail menu too.
Oh, and great music! It was a Sunday so really good gospel music playing!

Had dinner here last night. A friend of mine told me about this place.We had a 4:00 reservation. We got there about 345 and was seated about 415pm. Our waitress was nice and attentive. I ordered the catfish dinner and my husband ordered the wing dinner. I really enjoy my meal. Everything was good. However, my husband wasn't happy with his. They had these huge fried wings that looked bomb on the plate but just wasn't cooked long enough. The inside was really wet and tough he couldn't even pull the wing apart. So he didn't eat the wings just the leg. Neither one of us like to send food back because of the unknown. Lol He still ate his rice and gravy with yams. I would say look around at plates while you're waiting and if you see those big ass wings, get the chicken legs or strips. The wings are not being cooked long enough but I honestly they look awkward because they're so big. They look really good though super crispy. It's very crowded there and upbeat. The drink was just OK but it looked really pretty. It was ciroc, 7up Kool-Aid with kool aid ice cubes. It was super sweet and sticky. I hardly taste any alcohol so get a double shot if you order it. The yams were super delicious along with the hush puppies. Greens were good, but a little too spicy for my taste. I would definitely recommend this place for some good ole soul food.

This place is amazing! I've been numerous times and each time my food was fresh, hot and delicious!! And however is making the Mac and cheese can cook! 

Come try this restaurant out. I promise you won't be disappointed.

Great atmosphere and the staff is genuinely friendly. The brunch was good seeing g a living legend was even better. Kevin Johnson was in the house and it was just a cool vibe. Fried green tomatoes as weak as the rest of the food was great. Will definitely be back!

Less than stellar tonight.  Came into the restaurant for a table for two.  Girl wanted us to sit at the high top. I told her we were old and didn't want to fall off the stools. I asked if we could sit in one of the three open booths. She said no she couldn't and said maybe we can move later.  I guess that she needed us to balance out the sections so the servers didn't get overloaded.  So, we ended up at a table.  Sat down at 8:07pm and no one came to our table until 8:29pm when  Nicki G asked if we were ready to order.  Meanwhile, the two ladies that arrived at 8:20pm were seated next es to us had their order already taken.  I'm already pissed off and starving.  Funny thing is, there's currently five open tables as well. 
It's 8:53pm now and two tables after us have food already.  What is going on, Kev?

If you haven't been here and you're reading Yelp reviews trying to decide between here and somewhere else, I'll make it easier for you. Just come here!! Don't be like us where we had it on our list forever and only just now got around to it. We ordered online and when we picked up, they were really welcoming and friendly. Got the 3 piece chicken with cornbread+grape butter, and chose collard greens and mac as our sides and gravy as our sauce ($2 extra). Everything was so damn delicious. I'm a mac and cheese snob and have been burned so many times by so called great mac but, finally, this one gets it right! Can't wait to come back and try more.

It is nestled in the Oak Park area of Sac not far away from Sac High and the old KFC which was transformed to a Dominos pizza. 

Here we are at Fixins Soul Kitchen.  To my surprise, I'm eating at Kevin J's Restaurant.  I knew he went to the NBA playing for the Suns and also became mayor of Sac but I didn't know he had opened a restaurant in his old neighborhood.

Firstly, the host and servers were very friendly and helpful.  The restaurant is very clean with a cool decor.  

For a moment, I thought I was in New Orleans have some of the best fried chicken along with candied yams and mac & cheese.

The fried chicken was brined long enough to infused the right flavor.  The skin was crispy with the perfect crunchy texture.

The candied yams were sweet and also packed with lots of goodness.

The mac and cheese was made just right as well.

There was plenty of food and quite filling. We had to take some home. 

Equal important, Kevin & Michelle not only served really good soul comfort food, they are committed to the Oak Park community including Sac High. Very cool.

If you're looking for soul comfort food with real soul, look no further this is the place. 

Bravo to KJ & MJ

This local spot is excellent from the service to the food. I was in town and saw it on social media.  It is worth the visit. The food was fresh, hot, traditional soul food flavors and Vegan options which are a plus !!! The outdoor seating is cute as well. Cocktails we're awesome the bartenders
Are great group of ladies that are fabulous. If in the area definitely stop or make a trip you won't be disappointed.

Great food, great atmosphere, friendly staff! Check check check!!! Loved everything we got! We ordered catfish, pork chop, ocra, greens, Mac and cheese, red beans and rice and sweet tea! It was all delicious!!

BOMB! This place really exceeded my expectations. The food was amazing and the service great, also our food came very fast even though it was busy.
I had the 2 pieces of chicken with 2 sides. The chicken was delicious, juicy on the inside, crispy on the outside and had the perfect amount of seasoning on it. I had the dark meat, highly recommend!
My husband had the shrimp and grits and he loved it, he finished the whole thing and it was the best shrimp and grits he had yet!

Great atmosphere, even better food and the pricing was very reasonable. We enjoyed chicken and waffles + oxtail and okra and finished it it off with an awesome banana pudding dessert. Music was awesome too. Service was friendly and helpful with good recommendations. This fried chicken is off the chain!!!!

This is the best fried chicken I've had in Sacramento! What I loved about this spot immediately was the super kind host, Ebony! She made us feel welcomed right away and was so accommodating when we needed a booth because we had a toddler. 

We couldn't wait to order and we each ordered the fried chicken plate with two fixins and omg everything was so good! The chicken was fried perfectly! The mac and cheese was a smacking and the collard greens was out of this world! My friend got the coleslaw and it was perfect. 

I can't wait to come back here and eat up another plate and be in a food coma for the rest of the day.

Been meaning to come here for a while but every time we try, the line is out the door. Finally came on a weekday for lunch and there was no wait. We were greeted by Auntie Pearl who was super sweet. All of the staff and management were attentive and nice. Food came out fast.

The food is really good. We got the fried catfish and fried chicken. For fixins, we got collard greens, Mac and cheese, coleslaw, and candied yams. All were very tasty, though the apple cider vinegar or dressing on the coleslaw was a little more punchy than I was expecting. The fish and chicken came out piping hot and crispy, moist, and perfectly seasoned, not too salty. He cries was really good on both. Our boy liked the catfish so much, he couldn't stop talking about it. The grape flavored butter was interesting but a weird texture and didn't spread well. And the biscuits and hush puppies were a little dry. The mason jars for drinking water were a bit oversized and unnecessary given the drought we're in. But all in all, these little observations were offset by good on the rest os the meal.

And if you care about ambiance, the historic building red brick is a nice touch. We sat in a booth inside. The restrooms were simple and clean. I appreciate the little flipper trash can right at the door.

All in all, a worthwhile visit and extremely satisfying meal. Exceeded our expectations. We ate it all. Only left a few crumbs on our plate. You can see before and after. Would come here again.

Was in town so I had to stop by the original Fixins. The week before I visited to New LA location and the food was great. No change at this location, the food was excellent.  What's better here is the wait time. In LA, hood luck without a reservation but here we had perfect timing. Got in right before the crowd started to gather outside. I tried the oxtails and they were super tender and flavorful. Had a hint in sweetness to them, not sure it it was from the gravy but it was fine. For some reason they don't serve the cornbread warm but it would be so much tastier warned up with melted butter.
Their sweet tea was perfect not too sweet to hurt your teeth and not watered down.
Definitely recommend coming here when you're in the mood for soul food.
Me and my family had the gumbo, and it was a little too thick for our tasting. Customer service was great, and we didn't have to wait that long.

Fixins has won my heart! Talk about delicious!!! When I say we tore it up...I mean WE TORE IT UPPPP. Fried deviled eggs, pimento cheese, fish nuggets, friend chicken family meal, and banana pudding. Those biscuits and butter are to die for!!!!! Our server was sweet, but she was all over the place. I think she could've used some backup from support staff. The music was good too!
I got the shrimp and grits to go. They are so watery. The seasoning is very good though.

Very nice place. Professional staff. Friendly and efficient and cozy. Like an old school Soul Kitchen. Had the oxtail over rice. Excellent. Needed more rice to go with a delicious sauce. Must have been our lucky night because Mr K.J. stopped by to ask us how everything was. Very gracious as was Ms. Rhee, his wife, and his mom who we were blessed to have also met. Needless to say, lots of great synegy happening here. Reservations strongly suggested. We'll be back
P. S. The dessert was unbelievable (red velvet cake!!)

It has been a whole year since I've dined in at Fixins and did a lot change! First off I'd like to say I had the most amazing experience with our waitress. Her personality, her recommendations and her service to our table was wonderful. Her name was Sadirah and she has an amazing spirit about her. She really made a difference. A friend and I decided to come here for dinner around 7pm. There was one hour left for the restaurant to stay open. When we got there there was a line for to go pick up orders and a few people waiting to be seated. The place was packed ! Our door dining was closed. It was quite the ambiance, I miss places like these rich in food, diversity, culture and good music to go with good food. I had beer (modelo) and the catfish platter with two sides - my friend had oxtails with a glass of wine. We shared our food together and I can honestly say the food was amazing! Everything tasted fresh and hot. The crispy batter was amazing on my catfish and the oxtails were falling off the bone amazing. We enjoyed a peach cobbler desert and their deviled eggs as well. We were hungry and started eating before I could get all the photos but over all we spend $75 on our tab and it was worth every penny ! The service from Sadirah alone will make me return along with all the other benefits of this delicious place in the heart of oak park.

The service was attentive, but the food was even better. The chicken here is so moist and juicy and it really satisfied my fried chicken cravings! Highly recommend the mac n cheese if you're a cheese lover like me and the Kool-aid cocktail and bloody mary are a must have with the meal!

I have ordered Banana pudding waffles 6-8 different times ....wow! And.i domt even like bananas 
 Also got.2 chicken.wings at 8 am. Sooooo good. My friend  had Sweet potatoe waffles and loved it.  This is food that makes you smile when you take a bite.

I rated this restaurant two stars only because of Ms. Pearl. She makes the customers feel welcome and she feels familiar. I had no desire to go to Fixins until my cousin told me of his amazing experience at the LA location. He raved about the customer service e and the food so that I had to try it for myself. 

This review is mostly because of the bad customer service I experienced from the others even when I first walked in and was seated. The waiter was a little better when he was taking my order but that didn't last. I was going to order a couple of items to go but I decided against it because there was no way that I was going to spend another cent more there. All of the customers should be treated with respect and kindness, not just select customers. I wait for a long time to receive the check but when the waiter finally came over he didn't give me a check. He just told me the total. You only receive the check after paying? Kevin needs to fix this. That was weird. I did get a receipt after paying but this isn't common.

Came on a Saturday morning around and of course there was a wait. But it was definitely worth it. It's always that first bite you take, and you start to do a happy dance that you don't even realize you're doing, that's how you know it's good. Shrimp and grits was very sauced with gravy, and we love a saucy shrimp n grits! Very flavorful but very rich. The fried deviled eggs were new to me, but didn't surprise me. But the bacon on top was the icing on the cake. It just topped it off. Candied Yams, i have yet to have good candied yams but this was *chefs kiss*, definitely having that again when we come back. Mac and cheese was also really good. Our server was super pleasant, checked on us often, made sure we were good the entire time we were there. Decor was really cute. Loved all the artwork around the restaurant. And whoever playlist was playing, isssaa yes for mee! Chefs kiss all around. 10/10 definitely recommend :)

Wow! The food here is really on point. My friend and I ordered the catfish nuggets, charred okra, shrimp and grits and gumbo with shrimp. Had the sweet tea with bourbon. Finished it with banana puddling. The service was awesome fast friendly and helpful.  The food so good I cannot wait to go back to have more of the menu items.  If you have not gone you need to go. The Laos have patio seating.

We went to the spot on a Monday afternoon, the service was quick. We sat outside, the  weather was good and comfortable seating. We started off with the catfish and Nuggets. They were light meaty and very tasty. We have a few other options you could take a look they were all delicious.

Great food and great vibes. I've been wanting to try this place for a while and I cannot believe I waited this long.. We had chicken and waffles with a side of potatoes and pork chops with gravy, Mac n cheese, and greens. Everything was great but I wouldn't get the potatoes again.

This place deserves all the stars!! The hosts were so kind. We waiting for about an hour for a party of two. The ambiance is unlike no other. The music consists of hit after hit. We ordered two Adult Koolaids, chicken sandwich with a salad and a side of greens, smothered chicken plate with macaroni and cheese and potato salad. Next time, I will for sure try the grits and corn bread. Everything was delicious. I can not wait to go again!
Great food fantastic customer service good drinks, Kevin Johnson. The owner of fixing is doing a damn thing. Keep on keeping on Kevin Johnson Chef biggie.

The food is amazing. It's just like my mom made it. The greens have the perfect spice/hot ratio. The service both times I've been has been very good. It's a bit loud just be prepared. I don't know if the can support larger groups, I don't see why not. 

If you're curious -- don't be just go. 

Heads-up get a reservation to ensure you're not turned away.

This was our first time at Fixins Soul Kitchen. We arrived at 5:00pm on a Saturday evening. We only had to wait for 10-15 minutes for two of us. Although, I would recommend making a reservation because this place is popular for good reason. From the moment we walked in and greeted by the hostess to our friendly server who made recommendations and the restaurant manager who actually engages with his customers and truly cares about your experience, we were thoroughly impressed! We ordered the 3-piece chicken dinner with Mac n cheese and collard greens. We also ordered the Hot chicken sandwich with fries. Everything was delicious and flavorful! The portions were generous and we had to take home our leftovers. We were too stuffed to try dessert but that will be for the next visit. We will definitely be back here the next time that we're in Sac!

We just stumbled into this local southern food restaurant. Thankfully the other place we were looking for was closing so we decided to try Fixins Soul Kitchen located just down the street. 

We knew right away that this restaurant was special when we noticed a line waiting to get a table at 2:30pm on Saturday afternoon. 

The decor is modern, clean and vibrant. The hostess wasn't the most friendly but that's ok, maybe she was having a bad day. We sat outside at the bar and the outdoor patio is just as beautiful as the inside seating. Our waitress Alasia was wonderful and friendly. 

We ordered a 2 piece chicken and waffle, 2 piece fried chicken meal, deep fried egg appetizer, and several side "Fixins" like Mac & cheese, collared greens, red beans and rice, and potato salad. 

It's no exaggeration to say that everything we ordered tasted INCREDIBLE. The eggs appetizer is a must try served with a Franks Hot sauce/ honey sauce. The chicken had a great crunchy coating and was cooked perfectly. 
Collared greens may be the best I've ever had with a strong smoked Turkey flavor. Mac and cheese and Potato salad was flavored perfectly and the serving sizes are just right. 

I highly recommend that you visit this hidden gem in Sacramento. We will definitely be back soon.

If you love soul food I would recommend this place. I lived in the south for a while and it definitely taste like food from the south. I never had good soul food in Sacramento, but this place is my new go to spot for it. A bit pricey but definitely worth it.

my first time here !!!!! in love . i was super full n everything was delicious. I got the catfish with mac and cheese and greens (recommended by the server). I also go the mango hennesey drink. don't remember what it was called but it was so good.

This morning myself and my god mother wanted to get some good breakfast food. Upon entering Fixings the atmosphere  was calm and the music was settled. The waitress Chandra had good customer service skills and well as a pleasure attitude. As were eating the manager Brian came to ask if we has an complaints. The answer NO. Brian assured that if or order was wrong he would them fix it. But we had no complaints the servers were pleasant and very friendly.

After a long week it was finally Friday night and time to try a new restaurant! The waiting time online was 20 min but when we got to the door 5 min later it was updated to 45 min. We put our name on the list then walked up the street to try a local brewery. When we finished our beer we walked back to find they had wrote the wrong number and called it. The hostess apologized and was able to fit us into a table right away. 

I ordered the three piece chicken with potato salad and macaroni and cheese. The chicken was juicy and the breading was some of the best I've ever had. It was sweet yet had a little spice to it! The potato salad wasn't overdone with one ingredient and was well balanced. As for the macaroni and cheese it wasn't a sauce based cheese but actual cheese and lots of it melted. My date orders the catfish and was telling me how it was fresh and how they too liked the breading on that one. For dessert we got the peach cobbler! This is by far my favorite place and I will for sure be back when in the mood for soul food!

Great food great service family friendly they will see my family again had the games on the big screens loved it 5*
-RICK

My friend and I had reservations for this place, after discovering it online. We both are from out of town but couldn't pass up on the opportunity to support a black-owned soul food establishment! We went Valentines Day and we still had great service. I ordered the fried green tomatoes, & do yourself a favour and get an extra side of that sauce. I got the shrimp and grits. Amazing. But I reaaaaally liked my friend dish: catfish, Mac n cheese, and the collard greens. I could've had my meal and hers too it was so good. I am a fan of lemon drops so I ordered the lavender lemon drop. If you like lemon drops please order one! Very authentic and loved the vibe overall.
Although the lights went out Desiree made our experience unique and comfortable. She engages well with her costumers and made our experience fun and vibrant.

Wow wow wow the service here is amazing specially when you come on a slower day of  the week. Auntie pearl, Luke, and Isselle were amazing in every way possible. They were helpful when it came to helping us select a plate, they engaged in small talk with us, and made sure to check up up on us. The food matched the service! All of the food was delicious and the strawberry mimosas hit the spot, specially with the addition of the tequila shots wink wink. I will definitely be coming back here and recommend you come here as well. By the way it's blacked owned! So I gotta give this place a 10/10

First timer and it did not disappoint. I got the catfish dinner with red beans n rice and the Mac n cheese. I can't begin to say how amazing it was. The catfish was cooked perfectly with great flavor. Crunchy corn meal coating while tender and flakey on the inside. The red beans n rice was the best I've ever had. I could've eaten it as a meal by itself. Then the Mac n Cheese was so dope, it makes me want to stop making it for my Family functions. Mine is good but what I tasted blew my mind. 

My date had the Chicken n Waffles. I'm personally not a big fan of the the two together but it was the best tasting fried chicken I've had since I was in the South back in 2004. Crispy, flavorful, tender and juicy. The waffle was great as well. 

Our waiter Dom was very professional, nice, knowledgeable and a great human being. 

Can't wait to go back.
Let me tell you about this place, the food was EXCELLENT. the fried chicken and the fish nugget. So so good. And the service, everyone was so nice and made you feel at home. Highly recommend.

I came across this place by chance. I was reading the News and came across this article rating this restaurant as the Best Soul Food Restaurant in California and I had to try it. I was not disappointed at all. The service was great, very friendly. The food was AMAZING, I mean SOOO Delicious. Their drinks were well made and very good but I didn't take any photos  I highly recommend trying this place if you haven't already.

I went to Fixins with some friends for brunch and we sat at the bar. The bartender, Q, immediately got a rope full of drink orders but still took the time to take our orders! We ordered fried chicken and waffles. Not the first time I've been here, and the food is great every time. Q killed it on a busy Saturday morning and gave us excellent service! 5 stars!

I've been a fan. But they are a hit or miss. Especially when it comes to through delivery. I ordered oxtails w/ gravy and rice, side of Mac n cheese and a grape kool-aid. 
There was hardly any rice with my oxtails( and I've ordered them before I know what it should be) and there was NO gravy. The Mac n cheese was burnt. And the kool-aid was watered down. I've had all of these things before so I definitely know how it should have been. Very disappointed. I can't believe someone felt comfortable packaging this food up and sending it out. 
I ordered through their grub hub service. my whole reason for ordering food was so I didn't have to drive there... I would have brought it back and showed them but that defeats the purpose and waste of time I obviously was trying to save. But it was too close to closing anyway. 
I don't know if I'll be giving them another try.

Hi, Kevin 
I eat at the Fixin in Oklahoma last night I really enjoyed it, but the fried green tomatoes were a little costly for 5 slices, but overall great!!!!

This was a great place took my father for his birthday and the service was good and atmosphere. I ordered the gumbo with added shrimp and it was very tasty. The restaurant presentation was good and food came really quick to the table. Overall this is a nice place if you like soul food and in Sacramento area.

Came hungry, left full and happy.  Kiddo had the fish sandwich, spouse had chicken with collard greens and yams, I had the chicken and waffles.  The best bite of any day is juicy delicious fresh fried chicken wrapped in a waffle with butter, syrup, and hot sauce.  We used to come here intermittently during the era of pandemic take out and I always thought about the waffle but never got it because they probably don't travel well.  Definitely worth the wait!

Servers friendly, efficient.  Ambience great.  Music playlist management by the staff deserves 5 stars all on its own.
Great place for comfort food . It does get busy so expect a wait but worth it . Just taking off one star as service isn't as smooth as it could be. Definitely would come here again .

Was originally going to give Fixins a solid 4 stars solely due to the taste of the food (which was delicious btw), but what made this a 5 star experience was the hospitality of the staff. 

We had reservations for the indoor dining room, and we came with a newborn in a car seat and stroller. Not a lot of room to navigate inside, but not a huge deal at all. The hostess kindly informed us that the stroller was to be stored in one of the private rooms so it's not in the way. Totally understandable and the communication was clear. 

We were finally seated, and our friendly server introduced herself to us and took down our drink and appetizer orders. The food came at a timely manner, and it was flavorful - some of the best fried chicken and Mac n cheese I've had. We had peach cobbler and banana pudding for dessert, and they were both 10/10. 

It came time for our baby to nurse, but there wasn't really enough room in our booth to comfortably do that. This was when our server came in clutch by telling my wife to "Come follow me." She led her to the private dining room where my wife was able to conveniently nurse our baby. Huge thank you to our server and for being so accommodating. She didn't have to do that, but she did anyway. Glad that Fixins hires people that care!

Overall, enjoyable experience and the food was very tasty.

Ordered from this place yesterday after wanting to try them since they opened in Sacramento. I was expecting sub par food since it was delivery because that's mainly been my experience thus far. Restaurants don't seem to care as much for the quality of their food when they don't have to face you in person. I was wrong .....sooooo wrong. The food was 1000 percent on point. The chicken was crispy. The potato salad: I havent tasted potato salad as good as this since my Auntie died when I was a little girl! That is a compliment. The yams, the greens, the mac n cheese, the red beans and rice: I can't even sing their praises enough. Don't get me started on those biscuits and the butter. Needless to say, this is a place I regret not going to when it opened, that I didn't regret buying from and will be returning to point blank. They stood on business. I don't know if I caught them on a good day (looking at some of their reviews) or if some people are just being difficult on whatever day people got their food. All I know is I haven't felt this was about a food establishment in a long time. Thank you, Fixins!!!

I have come here everyone I visit sac and it really hits the spot as hard as the food coma you're going to get. The atmosphere is very lively and energetic and everyone has good vibes all around. The servers are really friendly and helpful too.
The food is amazing. The chicken is very juicy and thick and the batter has a strong flavor profile. The waffle is a little thin but usually crunchy. The biscuits are probably my favorite side because of the honey glaze with soft puffy layers of dough and the butter. The oxtails are tender and with some chicken really brings more to your taste buds. For drinks you should get any of the iced teas probably with bourbon or the kool aid. You generally cannot go wrong with any item.
The wait can be long as it is very very popular. It is good to get in during lunch time to avoid the dinner and brunch rush. I would absolutely recommend this place to everyone who comes.

We arrived to Fixins around 1:00 on a Wednesday and we had a 10 minute wait to be seated in side. 
We sat outside on a shade tree until they called us. 

Our server Maia greeted us and took our drink orders right away. She made several suggestions for us because it was our first time and  we did want alcohol beverages.

We had many option to choose from from a sidecar, a hurricane, lemon drop and several beers to choose from.

We went with the Hurricane, Lemon drop and a Red stripe beer! All awesome choices if you ask me. My drink was the hurricane and let me tell you. It hit me like a hurricane lol it was strong but also sweet. 

As for our meals. We went with the catfish and macaroni and cheese with fries.... We also went with the Spicy chicken sandwich along with a side of fries. Last but not least a kids meal with chicken strips, fries and a side of Mac and cheese . 

Our meals were all delicious. Only issue I had was my fish batter was not really stuck to the fish it literally crumbled once I picked it up so I had to eat it with a fork lol. Other than that it all was really good. The Mac and cheese was our favorite of all the dishes we tried. It was extremely cheesy and full of flavor. 

I would recommend Fixins if your looking for a good home cooked type of meal. I enjoyed the TVs and the bar they had as a option for those who did t want a table or eating on the patio. 

We will be back to try some other options for sure. 

@Starr_Got_Time Approved

If you haven't been you have to go!!!! absolutely delicious! we ordered the deviled eggs, chicken and waffles and the chicken sandwich. it was delicious! their sauces made everything 5 star, chicken was crispy and the salad Omg! i'm not big into salads but the sauce on the sasha's with the deviled eggs made me want to go back and get more!!! 

ordered to go and it was ready in no time. 

really cute restaurant, great for a date night

Great service with a smile. Excellent food and cool vibe!
Chicken waffles were on point!

Much potential to be had at Fixins but sadly much to improve. 

Oxtail entree - 1/5 stars Spicy and salty!  While I absolutely loved the savoriness, good meat portion, and generous gravy they provide, I wasn't expecting this entree to be spicy! It has more than a kick and today's cook must be heavy handed with the salt because this dish along with the collard greens was SUPER salty!  So much so it was almost inedible. 

Black eyed peas - 3/5 stars Little too mushy, either overcooked or sat too long. 

Collard greens - 1/5 Super salty! I read in the menu it's cooked with Turkey neck BUT I didn't expect it to have neck bones! Dangerous and they should definitely tell customers in the menu it has bones and as they serve the dish! 

Fried deviled eggs - 3/5 stars Panko was spot on BUT the sweet sauce drizzle on top was too tangy and sweet that it overwhelmed the appetizer.  Otherwise it was almost perfect. 

Fried green tomatoes - 3/5 stars cornmeal breading and thin sliced tomatoes doesnt let the tangy tomato shine in this recipe.  If they used the Panko they use with their deviled eggs and slice their tomatoes thicker I think it could be a real winner! 

Service - 3/5 
Atmosphere was bustling but my waitress was not.  I noticed I got different treatment as opposed to the obviously favored Black diners here.  I wasn't tended to as frequently and she was much friendlier to other specific diners.  You'd think the workers would keep this in mind that Kevin is married to an Asian woman, but our natural biases kick in first.  The business can only train servers so much but it's the individuals they hire that leave an impression as much as the food they serve.

Great food!... long wait. My husband and I wanted to try out some good old fashion Kool-Aid so of course we had to try some Fixins Soul Kitchen. The place was extremely busy and we had to wait during every part of the visit. We waited 40 minutes for a table which we didn't mind since we could see how popular the restaurant was, our server was constantly forgetting about us, our food took a while but we were patient but when our food finally came, she forgot my cornbread *big sigh, lol*. The place was high energy and had an amazing ambiance. The food was great but I think I will if we return, we will visit at non-peak hours.

Fixins came recommend by family in the area, and can say will be back! Atmosphere was great, food was excellent, and customer service was exceptional. The fried green tomatoes were good, but cut too thin so didn't get to enjoy much of the tomato and the catfish nuggets tasted a bit under cooked and were chewy. Other than this, the fried were excellent! The chicken and waffles were the best we've ever had! Will return specifically for that! And to try the peach cobbler! Plussss they sell kool-aid you can't beat that, we even grabbed a cup to go!

Pretty good food. Fish serving in the entrée was tiny. Sides are good especially the mac and cheese. Service can be slow depending on what's going on but everyone is pretty nice.
Really love the lemonade and the size of the jars in which the non-alcoholic drinks are served. Music is the best thing I experienced here.

I haven't lived in Sacramento long, but since moving here I have passed this spot multiple times knowing I need to stop by and try it. 

I'm going to attempt to describe how delicious our meals were. We got Shrimp & Grits, 3pc chicken and waffles, collard greens, mac n cheese, yams, and corn bread. We had outdoor seating, great music and excellent service all around! 

As soon as the plates arrived, the fragrance of the yams filled the air and I was hit with nostalgia. It reminded me of big holiday get togethers with family. Every single taste bud in my mouth was dancing out of pure happiness. The sweetness of the waffles and yams. The salted, crispy and juicy fried chicken. The tender greens, cheesy mac and cheese, and the soft corn bread that had just a bit of a crunch to it with a slight sweetness to that crust. I couldn't stop eating. 

Everyone we interacted with was such a pleasure to speak to as well. They made sure everyone was taken care of which made everything feel some homelike. Auntie Pearl? I believe was her name (sorry if I got your name wrong!) Was so kind speaking to us as it was our very first visit and she was genuinely happy to have us there to enjoy the ambiance, music and food that they offer. 

I've made a decision to try everything on the menu. So we will be back! You should definitely try it, and plan ahead to arrive early because the line does get long but definitely worth the wait. We arrived shortly after the rush, I think we were lucky on the time of arrival today.

Great atmosphere, The food was ok. The service was less than expected. Had to wait for Menus, 
Waited a while before someone took our order. The decor is pleasing. The portions were small good if you're on a diet. I didn't like it if you ordered greens. The cornbread didn't come with. It was an extra cost

Heard about this place by a friend of mine. They wanted me to try it. Omg it was amazing. I started with the deep fried deviled eggs, with a strawberry mimosa. Maybe I should I had a different flavor mimosa because it was a bit too sweet. The oxtail was my favorite. The fried chicken was tasty with a potato salad and okra. The fried catfish was good too. Cute spot. I was visiting so I ubered not much parking I saw. We had to take a Togo plate for a few people that knew about the spot. I'll be coming back!

Love their oxtails and fried chicken! Must try place in Sacramento. It gets crowded so we always order out for pick up. Waffles can get soggy if you order out with chicken being on top, so might want to have it on the side.

We got there at lunch time on a weekday and were able to be seated pretty quickly. I really liked the interior and the wait staff were friendly. Although they were quite busy, our waiter still had a great attitude and was very helpful.

I ordered the fried chicken and my bf ordered the shrimp and grits. Both were DELICIOUS! The chicken was perfectly seasoned with the most amazing crunch and I loved my bf's shrimp and grits so much I wish I had ordered another just for me  the only thing we didn't enjoy was the sweet tea because it was waaaayyy too sweet. The food is amazing though and I can't wait for my next visit
Food was delicious I had fried catfish and greens, my friend had ox tails and Mac n cheese. All was perfect

Let me just say that the food was banging! My catfish was hot out the grease and seasoned well. The sides (candied yams, macaroni and cheese) were delicious 
The smothered pork chops...OMGoodness and we won't even mention the gravy!! 
Oh wait, did I mention the kool-aid and lemonade in the huge mason jars 

If you're ever in Sacramento, add this place to your stop. Ammiie our beautiful waitress was the best. Thank you so much for making my visit memorable

My partner and I ordered food to go. I had everything pictured in the photos, plus catfish nuggets. My partner had 3 pieces of friend chicken and Mac n cheese. 

Food reviews:

Collard greens - good crunch, a little salty
Mac n cheese - tasty! 
Candied yams - not too sweet which I like 
Catfish - waaaaay too salty for me 
Fried chicken (dark meat) - delicious! Not too salty at all. Nice and crunchy. 

Customer Service:

My partner picked up the food. He says everyone was respectful and kind.

The food was really good as usual.  They give you generous sized portions of fried chicken and it tastes great too.  The prices are good too.  We had amazing service from a server named Aimee.  She made the whole experience a very pleasant one.  I highly recommend eating there and I highly recommend Aimee.
WAY too loud - ambience was a 3. Food was good - a 4. Service was excellent! A 5 with our server, Kronk.

This is a great place for a leisurely lunch. Great drinks and better food! The hot chicken sandwich is huge and the chicken is cooked perfectly - great crunch and great flavor with the hot sauce and chipotle Mayo on it. The coleslaw is bright and creamy, so it pairs super well with the spicy sauces. The Mac and cheese has good flavor, but slightly grainy texture as it is made with a roux. There are so many fun drink options to pick from! The 40 Acre is dangerously delicious! And the Po' Me Some Honey is a perfect sunny day beverage. Highly recommend both of them. The only down fall to this place is the wait time. The servers are super nice and helpful, but this place is incredibly busy so the servers have a hard time keeping up. If you go here, don't expect to get in and out very quickly. The atmosphere is very cool though so it's not a problem taking it slow to enjoy the surroundings. I will definitely be back!

Catfish Nuggets: Very well seasoned batter to a point where you dont even need any dipping sauce.  Crispy and not dry either

Chicken and waffle: I say that if they had same style of batter as catfish fries then chicken would've been better. There is no flavor to its deepfried skin and meat taste blend.. waffle..idk maybe its more just a preference but I prefer my waffle to be thicker and less like cinnamon toast.

Ox Tail Plate w/ Candied Yams: it was good. Meat was tender and flavorful with nice amount of salts and seasonings. Candided yam wasnt too sweet or sticky
Absolutely delish! The chicken, the biscuits and even the deep fried deviled eggs! We've yet to find anything we don't like.

This was my first actual time in the business , my mother had went before and loved it so much she brought left overs home . We decided to go in today and received the worst service ever from our waitress Maia J
She took 10 minutes after we were seated to take drink orders we than ordered apps and main on courses,which all came out together and before our drinks .we than sat with out any service the whole 30 minute lunch . I didn't even get offered a refil on my water ,and had to hound down which in this point seemed like I was bothering Maia J ,for some tarter sauce and lemon for my fish .the food was also really salty today compared to last time 
I will not recommend or return back thanks for taking my $120

My wife had been wanting to eat here for a while. We stopped in on a Thursday night and it was busy! Wait time was by bad 15-20 min, the staff was very nice and this was with them accommodating a lot of big groups. We said down and drinks were brought out quick and all questions were answered for us being new to this place. 
I ordered the catfish and my wife ordered the hot chicken sandwich. My catfish was good I ordered it with Mac n cheese and fries. My food was good, but the chicken sandwich was amazing!! Not to much of a fan of the kool aid pickle, but the sandwich and fries were delicious... 
we also ordered the deep fried devil eggs they were good , but next time I will try something else for an appetizer. 
We will be back to try other foods and see what's good.
Love it. Those scrambled eggs are crazy good. I don't know why but they taste so good. Love the bloody and the play on the expresso martini too. We love the chicken and all the gumbos and grits.

Excellent food and vibe. Packed on a Saturday afternoon at 2pm! Fried chickens with perfect batter, fixins of collard greens and okra, bread with yum biscuit and pink strawberry butter. Just scrumptious with a side of unsweetened  iced tea. Wish I could tasted the fried green tomatoes had more but that plate was filling!

The fried catfish sandwich with a side salad was huge and another winner. I need more of this delightful place.

Food was AMAZING like real shii it was so good, I have
never had soul food like that. The ox tails were juss wow probably the best i've had, my fav was definitely the deviled eggs and surprisingly their strawberry butter it went perfectly wit everything else to give in a little sweet taste. Their own hot sauce was also extremely good especially on the eggs and their chicken sliders!
Love this place. Their shrimp and grits is fire! Authentic soul food. Great service and ambiance. The place is always packed which is a sign of a great restaurant.

Chicken sandwich & burger were delicious!! 
This place is always packed so that's a great thing!!

What can I say about fixins nothing bad this is one of my kids favorite restaurants so we're there a lot if the food is great the staff is friendly and they look good too if you never been you need to go

I've been here several times and never had a bad meal. During my most recent visit, I came here early on a weekday and didn't have to wait. The food took a bit of time to come out. I ordered the oxtails. They were tender and flavorful. There was not enough rice that went with it though, so it was a bit salty. My mac and cheese was also yummy. It was a heavy meal. Parking can be tricky, but don't let that discourage you from coming here!
Real real good food and drinks. Had the oxtail's, greens and corn bread. The chicken and waffles looked good also. Fish nuggets are great, took home two orders  Drink menu

Food is always good when we come here after work. It's good any time of the day but I usually catch them on weekends and they are always so helpful and sanitary. Drinks are good and food is one to write home about.

HAVE AGAIN: Had the chicken again - just wonderful and fantastic! Mac n Cheese and biscuit also excellent. Service very, very good. Great place.
NOT HAVE AGAIN: The black eyed peas were good, but I'd rather try other fixins.

The name rang true after a long night of small bites and mediocre cocktails at stops from East Sac to South Sac. Fixins came about half way through the evening and thank gawd! EVERYTHING was on point. Still not sure if I enjoyed the drinks more than the food or vice versa. Either way, the experience was above and beyond the other haunts we visited. Staff, crowd, food, dranks..all superb.

Fixing is always great.  Consistent food quality, good drinks, and an amazing vibe.  I visit whenever I am in the area.  They are usually very busy, so make reservations, prepare to wait, or order to go.
The food and service are amazing, and the vibe and atmosphere are fantastic. Everything, from the food to the design, is taken very seriously.

The Mac n cheese here gives me the worst stomach aches cuz I'm not to supposed to eat cheese or gluten but whatever. It's so darn good that it's worth the pain. The fried chicken is also unbelievable. 
Actually, this is the first meal my dog and I ate after I adopted her. The fried chicken is special to both of us.
This was such a great experience. Food was exceptional. Service was amazing. Prices were reasonable

This place is AMAZING! The food was superb, the service was outstanding. Good vibes, good ambiance.  We had the fried chicken and waffles, the fried catfish, fried chicken. Sides of fried green tomatoes, Mac n cheese, red beans& rice,and corn bread.  Got a side of their gravy to dip the fried chicken in. I went into a food coma after eating here, woke up, and began eating again. Nothing negative to say. Our server (Cat) was great. Very hospitable and welcoming. This place reminded me of the glory days in the Baldwin heights/Ladera area eating at M&M's soul food in the early 2000's. What a joy to eat here and is highly recommended.

Just finished eating here and couldn't wait to write my review. This is some of the best southern food I've ever had in California and best fried chicken ever. Not too salty or greasy and heavy even though we ordered quite a bit. We also got the oxtail which was tender and fall apart and seasoned perfectly, almost tasted like it was braised in wine for days..

This place is reason enough to come back to Sacramento even though I live in SoCal. The service here is also amazing. Our server Don anticipated that our 1 year old would drop utensils so brought us extra and napkins. We also had a little mix up on our order which was fixed and even though we were rushing out trying to order dessert to go because our kid was melting, they packed the cornbread that didn't come out yet and we got out of there super fast. 

I love this place and will come back and tell everyone I know to go.

I love this place. Seating was quick even though there were so many people waiting. The Smothered Chicken was fantastic but I also enjoy the Fried Chicken. The Mac n Cheese was delicious. Fries were good (need a bit more seasoning). The biscuit was kinda dry and non flavorful. The Candied Yams were super yummy (I had it as my dessert) 

Overall, awesome place for awesome prices. Better than Roscoe's Chicken Waffles. Will definitely come here again and bring family :)

I've been here a few times now, but it is always best to have a reservation because they are always busy. The food has been consistently delicious. The restaurant is always loud because people are enjoying themselves. Good food, good people, good times!

Tried Fixin's for the first time because my friends recommended it. I never really eat soul food like that. But this place was pretty good. I like their Mac N Cheese especially and Fried Chicken was pretty tasty, along with some hot sauce on it. I had a taste of the Oxtail dish and that was delish. Overall cool restaurant and the service was great!

Excellent experience!! Took the fam there for dinner and it did not disappoint. My husband tried the smothered fried chicken, my son had the chicken and waffles with grape butter *YUM!! My daughter wanted the smothered pork chops but they were out which tells me they are most likely delicious. She decided to go with the catfish and I definitely had to go with the oxtail stew. I must say everything was excellent and service was on point. We will be back most definitely!!

Overpriced. Not worth the money. Been here twice maybe three times was not impressed.. The fried chicken is way too greasy. The food is not freshly made. So it's not for me. Maybe because I know how real soul food is suppose to look and taste..

I thought I would give this restaurant a try. It had great reviews my experience when I walked in it was very busy. I did have to wait an hour however it would've been longer. I don't think they added my name on the wait list when I finally got seated, I ordered two drinks. One of them wasn't good.(sangria) I ordered the catfish they said that was the best of the menu which it was good. I tried the banana pudding I won't order that again. I like to give restaurants a second chance unless it was just really bad to try something different on the menu however, I'll probably go back during the week when it's not so busy. The ambience was OK. Like I said it was really busy and I'm pretty sure it's like that a lot.

Food is better when dining in. Fried chicken wasn't crispy. The rue for the gumbo tastes like cream of mushroom soup. Oxtail was 2 bones, tough and all rice.

But i will still be back to support black business.
Authentic  Soul food in Sacramento Ca yummy food great atmosphere and service a must visit

Chicken sandwich was solid. Fries it came with- solid. 
Sides were ... okay. 
Bucket of chicken- It was OKAY. I wouldn't come back for it. Verry disappointed. 
The breading was crispy but under the breading it tasted very soggy which threw off the entire chicken.

Great food and great vibes 

Been here a couple of times and haven't been disappointed. Recommend the fried chicken and ox tails. 
Be sure to make a Yelp reservation or prepare to wait a bit during peak days/times as it's a popular spot in the OP

Tried this restaurant when I was in Sacramento as it was highly suggested. There was no wait on a weekday evening when I went. I got the 2 piece fried chicken and substituted biscuit for corn fritters for no extra charge! I got the collard greens and cheese for sides. Everything was delicious, and the waiter so nicely got me samples of many of their sauces which were all home made right in the restaurant. Would 100% suggest coming here if you're in the area, as everything was very affordable at 16 dollars for all of that, and I had leftovers as the portion was huge.

Chicken and waffles and grits w cheese.  Some of the best fried chicken I've tasted.  Wasn't greasy at all and golden brown   Good flavor.  Waffle had cinnamon on it and was great.  The grits w cheese was delicious.  Their hot sauce was a nice addition to the grits and chicken.  
I went around 830 before it got busy.  Old skool said that there is normally a 2 hour wait.  He is the bartender as I sat at the bar to watch some sports. 
They have an outdoor seating area that will be opening soon
Everyone was friendly and the place was buzzing w happy peeps.  Everything I saw looked amazing
Will be back to try more of their menu.  A must try in oak park.

Good food wonderful staff great service. 

I love the fried chicken, and the gumbo is to die for

Christen was our server and everything was amazing!!! Food was awesome, ambience was uniquely original, and the cocktails were delicious!! We had the Fried Chicken (3pc) with collard greens and cole slaw!  My husband had the shrimp and grits with a side of Okra! True the fried green tomatoes as an appetizer!! Sidecar and blueberry smash were excellent!!

Really glad we decided to come here. Went on a Sunday morning around 1130 and had about a 30 minute wait... was surprised to see a lot of people waiting outside that early but worth the wait. 
I really liked their menu options. 
Food was VERY GOOD we had chicken and waffles 
Smothered chicken with greens Mac and cheese and side of yams. 
Tried the catfish nuggets and the fried deviled eggs. Not my favorite but not bad at all. All the food was well seasoned/prepared and my husband loved the M&C (this taste like that REAL oven baked M&C) his words exactly..... loved the koolaid options lol. 
I've definitely recommended this to anyone coming to the area and wouldn't mind going again.

Love this spot with all my heart the food is always great but this review is more for more wonderful waitress Aeliza  (hope i spelled her name correct) (may 26) been blessed to have her as my waitress a couple times!

I was so excited for the food that I took no pictures and did a little dance when while I was eating. My only criticism was that it was a little cold, but cold and delicious is better than hot and nasty! I wanted everything on that menu, but unfortunately my eyes are bigger than my stomach. I will be going back!!!!

I saw this place on Yelp and knew I had to try it. Let me say in advance that I chose the wrong time to go! On Sunday, Father's Day, and right after church.... What was I thinking? 
We added our name to the waitlist and we're told an hour and a half.  It was closer to two, but I wasn't upset. Since it was so warm outside, I went to the bar to order a lemonade. The bartender did not even acknowledge me and it took another patron who got their after I did to tell her I was ahead of him. She was very rude when she came to get my order. 

We got seated and our server was very sweet. I ordered the Shrimp and Grits with a side of greens and my son got the Chicken and Waffle. The people sitting behind us got their food and it smelled divine!! Our food took a full hour to arrive. It was delicious, but by the time it got there my son was so anxious, we just got some boxes and took it home. 

I wish that I had known that there were appetizers available, but they were not on the menu and our server didn't mention anything about them  I would seriously recommend they either add them to the menu or on some table tents! I definitely would have ordered something to hold us over while we waited. 

We got home and the food was truly delicious, and I would definitely go again but I would try to choose a better day and time to go.

Pretty damn good.  Flavor 100 percent. Soul on point. Chicken omg lick your fingers good.  So freaking bomb. I will be back. Loved it all. Service quick friendly fast. The only down part is the food is really salty.   A little less salt and you might have five stars.  Place does get really crowded though.
One of the best Soul Foods in Sackanda.  Great service and ambiance was nice. I will definitely be here again.

The food was great. The service was fast and the overall ambiance was warm. We were pressed for time on way to concert. Although we didn't have a reservation we were seated within 15 minutes and served quickly. All food ordered was wonderful. We will be returning.
Great food service, it for sure has a lot of soul & a bar. The staff are great, friendly fun atmosphere, great hospitality!!!
Hands down the BEST soul food/southen/African American cuisine in the Sacramento area!

Good food.
Good drinks. 
Good staff. 
Good vibes. 
Good music. 
Shrimp n Grits. Catfish Nuggets. Lavender Martini. 
You're welcome.
Food was good and the customer service is top tier. Very nice & friendly . I would definitely go back .

We have been to both locations. I always have the fried chicken salad which I absolutely love. I had a chance to try what everyone else had, which is almost everything else on the menu. Everything is wonderful. The restaurants are clean and the decor is nice. We have always received great service.

Came here a few months ago and loved the food! Great quality! The food was very rich in flavor their cornbread was phenomenal. Service was a little slow especially the front. Everyone was just standing around watching our server work her butt off. But once we sat down Mackenzie I think is how she spells her name was working her butt off. We were watching her taking tables and running back and forth from the bar to the table. I asked her once our food came to the table if there were any sauces that would pair well with our dish and she brought out a few with great description of each one. She made the experience unforgettable.

The best brunch/restaurant we've been to in Sac to date! Everything was delicious, including the kool aid pickles (I was not a believer). The wait was not too bad - we had time to go grab a coffee from the cafe next door. 

The service was prompt and friendly. 

We love this place and definitely will be back!!

(Also, outdoor seating was well spaced! Big plus!)

author_location: Central San Jose, San Jose, CA
text: just overrated. The service is excellent minus the kitchen staff. I have been to the Sacramento location about four times and something is always wrong with the plates, this time my biscuits and gravy were cold, and I asked for a remake the server was very polite, but my food came back cold, again. it was also flavorless. In the past I have tried the shrimp and grits, and it was flavorless, shrimp was overcooked and small portions. the gumbo just taste like flavorless gravey. its just not authentic tasting soul food.
The food was delicious and the service was great. The food came out quick and hot. A lot of parking nearby

I placed an order and when I picked it up it had the wrong items. I was too far out of the area to take it back. Called and got no answer. This is actually the second time this has happened. I love this place but can't trust the orders. Make sure to check and check again!!!

Wait time was very short. Wonderful customer service. Employees very kind and welcoming. Food was absolutely exquisite. Everything was seasoned perfectly. Cocktails were amazing. They atmosphere was very family oriented. It really makes you feel your having Sunday dinner at grandmas after church. Love it here definitely recommend.

We came here after my dad's funeral, we had a group of about 20. Although they were only supposed to seat 12 of us, they accommodated us anyway. The service was AMAZING! The food was delicious (definitely try the Mac n cheese). There was not a long wait for food despite our large group. The waitresses were beyond nice and attentive. I will be going back and I definitely recommend. 

BTW, I had the catfish sandwich with fries and I ordered the Mac n cheese separately. Sooooo gooood!

Went here for the first time. We waited 1.5 hours. Once we finally got sat. We had THE BEST WAITRESS!!! Her name was JC  she was charming, funny, and personable. The food was amazing the catfish appetizer was top tier! And the oxtails were soo good! Either way I'm coming back for our server!!

My boyfriend and I tried this place for the first time on Monday, and we were NOT disappointed! We ordered several things just to be able to try as much as we can, and everyyyyything was delicious. I also want to mention that the service we received was so on point. I even made a comment that I thought they could read my mind. When I was thinking we needed water, they came with a water pitcher. When I was thinking we're gonna need some boxes, our waiter came asking if we thought we were gonna need boxes. When I was thinking that it was time to request the check, our waiter came with check in hand lol. If there was one thing I had to say was a negative (which isn't really a negative, I feel) it's that we thought we liked the catfish nuggets better than the actual fried catfish fillet, but that isn't to say we didn't like the fried catfish fillet. Honorable mention to that mac n' cheese. I remember saying to my boyfriend, "THAT was made with love forsure." We will most definitely be coming back to try more things on the menu!

First time checking out Fixins now with indoor and outdoor seating during COVID. Service was exceptional making sure we had everything we needed and were super attentive. Prices are more than reasonable for soul food in California so can't complain about that.

We got the Family Meal which is 8 Piece Bucket of chicken, 4 biscuits, and sides (we got yams, collards, and 2 orders of Mac/ cheese). I thought the fried chicken was very good but missing something on the breading of it. Only having Crystal hot sauce packets hurts it as I would have thought they would have some house made sauces or more sauce options for the chicken. Mac & Cheese was so good we had 2nds. Quality mixture of cheese, Mac, bubbles, and the crispiness I like in baked Mac. I didn't have as much of the yams or collards but everyone else at the table loved them. Biscuits with grape butter was an excellent side for the chicken.

Very good experience at Fixins. I would be interested in checking out some of their po boys next time we go.

Fabulous mission. Excellent service! Their biscuits are absolutely divine! I'd skip the mac & cheese, but if you love cheesy grits, go for the grits. As you see, I went full carb and left pretty satiated. Everyone else went with a combo of meats and greens and they loved it.

*This is a Pickup order review

I ordered through Grubhub (just call and place your order). Unfortunately, on Grubhub some menu items are missing. I ordered their spicy chicken sandwich, oxtails with mac-n-cheese, kids chicken tenders, and kids burger. 
When I arrived the restaurant was SUPER cute and trendy! There was a small crowd outside and their outside patio area was busy! Inside was clean and everyone was adhering to Covid procedures! There's a nice bar and a cute outdoor area with plenty of seating. I went to the "To Go" area to let them know I was there. I did have a wait but that's ok because our food was hot and well packaged for travel back home.

THE FOOD:
My boyfriend had the sandwich and he smashed it FAST. He said it was tasty but the piece of chicken was a bit too big. I ate the oxtails and mac-n-cheese. First off. I like their mac-n-cheese. I have NEVER tasted good mac-n-cheese since I've moved to Cali ten years ago. This mac-n-cheese tasted like my moms. There was super decent amount of oxtails but not too much rice and no gravy (you can get gravy as a condiment). The flavor was good but I know it would've been 10x better getting it fresh and eating it right away but I definitely wasn't disappointed! The kids liked their food as well. They have options to customize the burger for your picky kids lol! I wanted to try their Kool-Aid but their ToGo cups aren't worth $5 imo. I wanted to try their cobbler but $11 is too high to gamble with, maybe one day. 

Overall, hell yes to this restaurant and successful black owned business!!! We will be back to dine in when Covid is contained!
Good food, drinks, crowd and music. Patrons are friendly. Some servers are really nice and professional and others don't seem to be happy they have a job.

Poor quality.  Ordered Pork Chops, Greens and Gumbo. The pork chops were covered in batter to look large.  They were thin, tough and much too salty. The greens were okay. The gumbo was not up to standard with any I've had. Ordered via Doordash and my bag was not secured at all. Wide open with access to all food.  I was hoping for comfort food on a cold Fall day. Definitely was not what I hoped for. Too bad Chez Soul closed.

EVERYTHING we got was great. The service was outstanding. The ambiance of the place was great, the music, the dining are, the booths. A lot of cultural art on the walls. I really liked the cash fish nuggets. Those were probably my favorite. The biscuits come with some realllll good i think its apple butter and strawberry butter! The mac n cheese was too tier. Ive never had okra before and it was goooood. Almost like asparagus taste. Anyways really great. Highly recommend.

I have been wanting to come here so bad since all of my friends and family have raved about this place. The wait to be seated was around 15 minutes on a weekday evening. Not too bad. Dining was both inside and outside. I ordered the Smothered Chicken with Candied Yams and Mac & Cheese. The Candied Yams were my favorite and were so well flavored with that sweet syrup. The Smothered Chicken was okay and I wish it had more flavor but what made it amazing was that they give you hot sauce packets which I definitely used. The Mac & Cheese was okay. My sister loved her Hot Chicken Sandwich and I wish I had ordered that. It looked so good. Her fries were yummy too. We had Banana Pudding for dessert and that was delicious too! I want to try that sandwich next time I come in! Oh also the sweet tea is definitely sweet, refreshing, and a dessert itself!

I'm so glad I was able to finally try this place. Came for a birthday dinner for my grandma. A couple of family members came early so we could get a table since we had a large party of 10. There was no issue in us getting our table. My order was taken immediately.

I ordered THE BETTER BURGER well done (1/2 lb patty topped with melted pimento cheese, bacon, lettuce, tomato, onion, house pickles and burger sauce, $16 with an order of fries). It was delicious ! The burger was seasoned perfectly, not too heavy. I am now a big fan of the melted pimento cheese on it ! It gave it a kick, the flavor is something that can't be described.

The fries ? AWESOME ! I am a fry lover. The were cut, cooked, and seasoned just right.

The rest of my family said they enjoyed their meals. we will definitely be returning.

*********************************
PAY ATTENTION TO THE REVIEWS THAT ARE NOT 5 STARS 
*********************************
I'm going to get straight to it. I ordered 3 piece chicken with greens, yams, mac n cheese, black eyed peas.  The chicken and biscuit was alright the chicken was heavily seasoned. The sides were NOT good ..

DISCLAIMER:  I AM QUALIFIED TO TALK ABOUT SOULFOOD this is MY review there are enough reviews that rate this restaurant "5 STARS" my 1 star is not going to impact the business.  I write my reviews with honesty and sometimes its not a good look.  But my goal is to keep it 100 hunnit.   I have dined at plenty of 5 star restaurants and they have fallen short of just about 1 - 2 stars.  Fixins could be a five star establishment but it's not.  I don't think anyone invested in the business took the time to research how to provide fresh food in a timely manner. If food is not going to be made fresh to order due to time constraints. You can actually purchase canned foods that are really good when you tweak them a little bit.  The look of my greens, mac n cheese and black eyed peas were disgusting to even look at.   You can't cut corners when it comes to SOULFOOD.  My guest ordered smothered pork chops and they were super dark in color like where they get that gravy (mystery sauce)  from definitely not made in the kitchen.  If you notice they seem to use that gravy (mystery sauce) on almost everything including the grits and shrimp YUCK.   I think with a little bit of guidance from someone who knows soul food and can properly train or guide their staff how to prepare it fixins could be good good.  My mom, my aunt and a few big mommas could get this place straightened out.  It's apparent they chefs are not experienced in what they are doing.

GREENS: too, too much vinegar, soggy (overcooked) definitely not freshly cooked and tasted very much like they are from a can 

YAMS: definitely from a can with added flavorings  too much nutmeg, too much cinnamon and too much vanilla they left a very bad aftertaste 

BLACKEYED PEAS: not freshly made very salty and possibly from a can

MAC N CHEESE:  oh my , fully loaded with butter or some other type of oily substance, I have never had mac n cheese like this where the oily substance separates and floats to the bottom NOT GOOD 

GRAVY:  not sure what this mystery sauce is definitely not pleasing to the eye and not a good taste, YUCK the pork chops were very dried out and tough to cute

I came to celebrate with my family after finishing a big test annnnd after hearing all the hype. However, I don't think my experience lived up to the hype. The service was really just unfortunate to the point that when our food came, it was not even room temperature. Our server would also come out chewing so... yeah. Overall, the food was decent but I don't know that I'd hype it up to someone else based on service alone.

Everything was so delicious. Fried Chicken and catfish perfectly seasoned and cooked. The sides, oh the sides, each one was amazing. Collards are delicious, I've been craving them ever since I was there. Just go, you'll love it.
Food was amazing, service was a little slow but it was very busy , so I didn't mind the wait. Our waitress was very friendly.

Can't get enough of down south cuisine. Food for the soul. You had me at collard greens w turkey neck. The Bride and I go out of our way to feast here whenever we're in town. It's home to us. Check it out. You'll luv it. Guaranteed.

DRINK THE KOOL-AID.....cocktail. Even the ice cubes make you want more.

We came the day after Thanksgiving hoping to find a new go-to place for fried chicken. We found so much more than just fantastic chicken. 

Fixins is clean, well lit, easy to find, friendly and attentive staff, the decor is modern, colorful and fun. The neighborhood loves this place, based on our experience, with good reason.

Our party of four had lots of fried chicken, white and dark meat and tenders, Shrimp and Grits, House BBQ sauce, House Ranch, Mac & Cheese, Sweet Potatoes, Grits with cheese, Coleslaw, fries, Honey mustard, fresh butter whipped with GRAPE JELLY, Unsweet Tea, and lots of Kool aid cocktails. We ordered seconds on the coleslaw, pimentos and cheese grits, and biscuits. We took home some peach cobbler (I'm so full from dinner, I'll have to update the review after I taste it!)

The shrimp was cooked flawlessly. Chicken was well-breaded & seasoned, moist and tender. I have never had oxtail soup, but just based on the execution we sampled this evening, I'd go out on a limb and try it here. 

This place would hold its own for a night out for two, a family meal, or a party of 20. Fun would be had by all and everyone would still be talking about the food weeks later.

It was a good dinner tonight! Good fried chicken and sides.  Shrimp and grits were good! I wasn't sure about ordering the ox tail, I had it once and it was really good but the last couple time was overly salty and kind of chewy so decided to stick with shrimp and grits. The food took a little bit of time.  We'd like to come back and hopefully things will improve!

One visit and I now have a new favorite Soul spot. Smothered pork chops were excellent and great portions. Even tried the Cesar Saks with fried chicken which was amazing. Will do the fried chicken next time

Second time here....
Food is good!
Had to ask for our drinks twice (both visits)
Didn't get our drinks until after entrees were brought to the table (both visits)
Staff standing around by the bar, on their phones, and talking to each other (both visits)
Music too loud for Sunday brunch (second visit)

All aspects of poor management

Going to be tough to get there a third time

Awesome!  We had a large group and were visiting Sacramento and went to Fixins straight from the airport and it was just what we needed. Great service and they were able to accommodate our large group. Then we couldn't decide on dishes but most got the chicken and waffles, ox tail, shrimp and grits, gumbo and cat fish!  We will definitely go back!
Excellent food.  Expect to wait.   Service is excellent.  Ambiance is relaxing.   You have to try the black eyed peas and greens.

Tried to come here a few months ago on a Sunday afternoon, but the wait was too long. This time, on a Saturday afternoon, we were able to be seated almost right away in their lovely outdoor patio. The food was delicious and the service was friendly and efficient. The chicken and waffles was the best we've ever had and we also loved all the fixins! including the collard greens, rice and beans, cole slaw, and candied yams. Can't wait to come back and try more items!

Our waitress was friendly and helpful. The food came fast and it was hot. Love the okra side; It is a bit of heaven! The fried chicken delish! Mac and cheese creamy, greens yummy, peach cobbler was wonderful! The only reason I give it a 4 star is because of the wait. But worth every bite!
The food was delicious! My first time and the ambiance was very nice. You have to try the catfish nuggets and the Gumbo with shrimps! To die for.
Omg is all I have to say I enjoyed everything I had this evening Definitely a chilled place to go For evening drinks and dinner

I'm not sure where to start on this amazing place! AMAZING food. Beautiful location. Great Customer Service! What else can you ask for. 

Ordered:
Fried Deviled Eggs
Mimosas 
Shrimp & Grits
Hot Chicken
Kids cheeseburger 
Kids Chicken Tenders 

Side note: kids portions are big and come with a drink. My kids are young (2 &5) and they could have easily shared a kids meal. 

One of the reasons I love the food here is that they flavor everything and take it up a notch. The fries have special seasoning (if I had to guess it would be seasoning salt with a bit of paprika). ALSO!! They serve the kids chicken tenders with honey mustard and ITS AMAZING!! Their ranch is also AMAZING! It's definitely made fresh. I could taste the buttermilk and dill and everything else that's wonderful in ranch. 

My favorite thing was the Hot Chicken. Let me warn you it's BIG and it's MESSY but oh so delicious!! It's a beautiful combination of hot chicken (I mean temperature) sweet mustard and crunchy slaw. All the flavors are truly a perfect combination of flavors. 

I have to talk more about the chicken. The chicken itself is so crunchy. The batter is so flavorful. I loved it so much I stole some chicken tenders from my kids plate. Also the chicken is so moist. 

Before I finish the fired deviled eggs were really unique. They were hot. The egg white is the part that's fried and the filling is a traditional cold filling. It was topped with bacon and some hot sauce! I was so surprised by eating and enjoying a hot deviled egg!

Cool vibe, place is clean and tidy w/ great polite waiter/waitresses. 
Ordered deviled eggs and fried chicken w/ gravy.
