Devil eggs never came to our table, I asked the waitress about it after my main meal came out and she said it got sent to the wrong table so we never got it (which means she forgot I ordered it and never bothered to check). I just told her to take it out of the bill. 
Fried checked w/ gravy is meh, gravy sauce wasn't all that, I had ordered it with dark/white meat combo thinking I will get a Thigh or Drumstick with a chicken breast.... Instead I got a Thigh and a Wing..... I asked and she said it was extra for the breast. May not be coming back here anytime soon.

All I can say is delicious , delicious, delicious!!!!!!!!! Great service, and the food was so worth trying! We got seated right away. My husband tried ox tail, and I had a fried chicken plate and both were absolutely fantastic!
The Lil Fixins were delicious. The server was attentive. We are so lucky that there wasn't a wait to be seated.
The oxtails are so good, the fried chicken is perfect! Would recommend all the sides & don't forget your kool-aid!

Food should be flavorful and well seasoned. This is exactly what this place is about! Shoutout to the back of the house, you're the GOAT, keep making/prepping the food tasty and delicious. Visiting from San Diego, and I've had Fixins when I've traveled to Sacramento. It was 5/5 the first time and it was 5/5 during this stay as well. Best biscuits and gravy, shrimp and grits, fried chicken and waffles! My stomach and mouth  is screaming Oooh-wee!
Love it. My favorite is the oxtails though myself and others recommend a little more rice with this dish. The food is excellent here

Just went yesterday for lunch. Review is not for food, which was delicious, but rather our server. She forgot to put in our order. It was only after 2 other tables, which were seated after us,  got their order she figured it out. Then she tries to say we never gave her our order. I reminded her that she even gave us a recommendation, when she finally realized that she hadn't placed the order. Didn't apologize, didn't give us a comp for our inconvenience or anything. I will not post her name, but this server was forgetting which table she was bringing drinks to, food, and even forgot to-go boxes. Not a good service experience at all.

Craving the fried chicken salad.  Chicken was hot and crispy, coupled with cold crunchy lettuce, firm tomato ....
Grape kool aid

We've been meaning to try the Oak Park restaurant area.  Stopped at Fixin's before a show in midtown.  Super energetic vibe, great food and a real value.  The smothered pork chop was outstanding.  Same for the fried chicken.  I now understand why the wait.   Also, sides were excellent.  Wish I would have taken some pictures.  Food disappeared too fast.

First time trying this place and it was AMAZING . Place was nice and the music was great!. The fried chicken which I recommend besides everything else on the menu, the Mac and cheese is smacking! , over all the drinks are great and will have you lit !! Good customer service

We had a reservation and didn't wait long to be seated. It was a busy Friday night. I'm glad I made a reservation. We had a play to see just next door to the restaurant. We ordered two appetizers, green fried tomatoes and artichoke dip, both were delicious.  Our food came soon after. The kitchen was really on it. However,  they brought two of my order.  The server was having an issue with her hand held machine.  No worries, I told her and she already knew she placed two orders.  When the check came both orders were still on the tab. This delayed our check out and the show was starting soon. In the end she made it right but it definitely could have been avoided had she corrected it when she first aware of the mistake.
We ordered our food to go as we were in the area at an event. And everything was very good. The restaurant looks packed and would've been a great place to sit down and dine in.

The food, service and atmosphere are always amazing.  This is one of my wife's and I go to spot for lunch or dinner. I have not had a bad meal or received bad service.  It's worth the drive from wherever you live

One of my colleagues recommended this establishment to me. I decided to try it yesterday 09/24/24. The aesthetic of the place was nice. The staff was friendly and the server was very knowledgeable of the dishes. So I tried what the server recommended which was the brined chicken. It comes with two sides (fixins) and a biscuit. I chose greens and Mac and cheese. Let's start with the chicken, it was very salty for my taste. I didn't pick up much flavor over the salty brine. I give a 6/10. The greens were tender with some turkey meat added.  Also lacking flavor to my taste just salty.  This too is a 6/10 for me. Lastly, the Mac and cheese was botched. The roux had too much flour and the noodles were over cooked. This was a 5/10. I substituted the biscuit for a corn bread muffin and it also was missing flavor just bland no good butter or honey It tasted just a little sweet. 4/10. Not sure if I will go back.

Stopped here for lunch while on a business trip in Sacramento. Food was incredible. Elevated soul food is the best description. Great service and atmosphere. The owner came by our table to chat and tell us about the place. Will be going back next time I'm in town.

I love this spot the food is super good the service is friendly the restaurant looks good and the prices are great with good size portions of food love the corn bread and the kook-aid would recommend to anyone!

I was super excited to try Fixins soul kitchen. I was struggling to decide what I wanted to order between the gumbo or the catfish, so I ordered both.
Starting with the gumbo, I ordered a cup size and that was a meal in itself. It was absolutely delicious and the meat was so tender. I saw a lot of reviews saying that it was salty, but I did not feel that way. I highly recommend this dish.
The catfish had pretty good flavor but because I got my order to go by the time I arrive to my destination the breading wasn't as crunchy as I would like. But it was still pretty good.
The candied yams were one of my sides and I have to say it was one of the best yams I have ever had. The texture of the yam was soft but didn't fall apart in your mouth. It was perfect. The mac & cheese and cornbread to me were not bad, but they were definitely not some thing I would recommend that people must try. I have definitely had better. Overall I'm a happy camper and I am officially in a food coma after dinner. 
If I'm in the area I will definitely come back and try more dishes!
Fried chicken here is actually okay, but it's hella expensive and don't think it's quite worth it. It's fried nicely, but is missing some flavor.

Oh
My
Goodness 
JC took incredible care of us. 
The inside is very cool with the old brick walls. 
The food was absolutely amazing. 
We did the deep fried deviled eggs, catfish bites, chicken and waffles, chicken sandwich and one catfish sandwich. The best I've had!
It's definitely a place to put on repeat.

Fixins  Soul Kirchen has the most unique soul food that I've ever experienced. I've not had anything from their menu that I've not liked.

The food is authentic and makes you continue to come back for more. If you're looking for good soul food, I would suggest you try Fixin's.

It was mine and my husband's first time here today, and overall we were more than happy with everything! I ordered the gumbo (Mac & Cheese + fries for my side) and he ordered the catfish (black eyed peas + Mac & cheese for his side). The food was delicious and the portions were great! Only drawback was that the kitchen appeared to be backed up, so our food did take quite a while to come out.

Although our food did take longer than expected, our server was extremely attentive and continued to check up on our food and communicate that it shouldn't take much longer. She was clearly busy and had quite a few tables, but that didn't stop her from providing excellent service. 

The atmosphere overall was very cozy and inviting, perfect for a night out with your partner or friends! Even though it's December it did feel just a tad stuffy in there, which felt a bit more uncomfortable towards the end of the meal, but no biggie! 

Overall we enjoyed our meal and the service and would absolutely come back!
The food was awesome and so was the service. The ambiance had great energy. Their sides were a great compliment to the main entrees.

Do yourself a favor and bring friends so you can try all the food.  Generous portions is an understatement.  We went on a random Saturday and the place was packed, even outside.  Our server was friendly and on top of things.  The only thing that detracted from the meal was a group of obnoxious and drunk women screaming and yelling at the table next to us.  Thankfully they left before we were finished so we got to enjoy some of the meal in peace.  The fried chicken was moist and well seasoned.  It wasn't the best I have had but it was right up there.  The sides are what stole the show here.  Everything and I mean everything was perfection.  I got one bite of hubby's black eyed peas - he promptly told me that was it.  The yams were just sweet enough, the Mac and cheese was creamy and the coleslaw was not overly sauced.  Quite enjoyed the entire meal and took a lot home, of course.

The food was pretty good. I had the catfish, Mac and cheese, greens and corn bread. I enjoyed the catfish but I feel like they were a little heavy on the salt. The greens were sweet which is not to my liking but I still enjoyed them. The mac and cheese was my favorite - very flavorful. Corn bread was pretty good too - the butter is a nice add. The service was good from my waitress, the host desk were not very attentive when I first entered. Ambiance was a great touch - likely for me to return to the Fixins in my area Los Angeles since Sacramento was a good experience.

I had been looking forward to eating at this place after hearing so much about it and it did not disappoint! The building is a nice size with a good amount of tables inside and outside. The staff was very nice, friendly, and the service was fast! My friends and I ordered sweet tea and Kool-Aid to drink and the cups were huge for $4, and the drinks were deliciously sweet! We also all ordered the fried chicken plates with two sides and all of the food was so great!! The chicken was crispy, juicy, and flavorful, and the sides were also flavorful and tasty! The portions were very filling, so we all had to take leftovers home, but I am looking forward to coming back and trying more!
One of the best cornbreads I've had. Food has never disappointed me when I have dined in. Atmosphere is very chill!

Best soul food that I've ever had.

I stopped by Fixins on a recent trip to Sacramento and can easily say that it was the highlight. I came by around 11-12 PM on a Saturday afternoon for brunch and it was slightly busy, but I only had to wait around 20 minutes for a party of 2. There are some cool businesses around there, so my boyfriend and I walked around while we waited. The hostess and server were really nice and checked up on us throughout the meal. We ordered the fried chicken with candied yams and charred okra, and the fried catfish with mac and cheese and collard greens. It was hands down the best food I've ever had. The fried chicken and catfish were fried to perfection, and it was perfectly seasoned. The sides were just as good as the mains. The candied yams had just the right amount of sweetness, and the charred okra was beautifully crisp on the outside, with not much gooiness that okra normally has when you cook it. The mac and cheese was perfectly creamy, and the collard greens had a burst of flavor too. I am super glad that I got a chance to try out Fixins, and would recommend to everyone and anyone to check it out.

There was street parking available in the neighborhood and surrounding area.

We thoroughly enjoyed our afternoon here.
Returning from a special occasion at the military cemetery in Dixon, this place was recommended.
The place was packed.
We were seated outside where it was comfortable, clean, and quiet.
Our server was prompt courteous, sweet, attentive, and knowledgeable to all our needs.
Our meals were delivered hot all at one time.
Everyone was totally satisfied.
My only wish was to have red snapper on the menu. There must be a reason it's not there. 
Would love to know why it isn't. 
We will travel back here in the near future.
Thank you for a pleasant experience!!

This is the best soul food I've had since my Mom passed.
I know she'd approve!
The fried chicken is to die for! But, I haven't had anything less than amazing food here. I look for reasons to come to Sacramento just so I can pick something up.
If you want to eat at the restaurant you need to make a reservation. Take out is very easy though,

I can't find anything to complain about this spot. The food came out quickly,  the servers were super friendly and the music was on point. I ordered the fried chicken with candied yams and Mac & cheese. My soul left my body upon first bite! Cannot recommend this place enough.

10/10! I absolutely loved fixins. It wasn't too expensive and they give nice portions. It's very clean, nice ascetic and they played great music. Shoutout to the chefs and management for doing a great job, and Nataya. She was our server, very professional and really sweet. We drove from the Bay Area and really enjoyed this place. We even bought to go meals since we don't live close by. We will be back for sure. I recommend this place to everyone.
Love this spot. Right here in the heart of Oak Park. Delicious food and fantastic portions.

This place lives up to its hype!

The service is outstanding, very friendly staff and great ambience! Not only that, the food is worth going for. We ordered the catfish, chix and waffles, and chix sandwich, and none of them disappointed. Definitely come early on the weekends to avoid long wait times.

Overall, we had a great experience, and can't wait to come back!

Everything about this restaurant was on point for us me and my girlfriend popped up out the blue the music was a tan. The service was a nine the food was a nine the atmosphere was perfect. I will return.

chicken and waffles is my fave!
Their drink strengths vary and so does their customer service, but I would probably come once a year.
The best soul food in Sacramento! The service is excellent and so are the drinks. It's very nice and a fun atmosphere inside. Definitely must try, and we'll definitely be back soon!
Visited this spot a couple times now and it never disappoints. Best place for some chicken and waffles along with candies yams and a mason jar of sweet tea
Soul Food at its best. Customer Service at its best. OMG! That chicken sandwich was to die for. Now I went to DTLA. It's Black Business Month. You just gotta go to believe!

The best soul food in town. The mayor knew what he was doing when he set up this restaurant which revamped a special area in Sacramento. The food and customer service is the best. If you wish to feel welcomed with delicious food, well then you've come to the right place! 

Trust me, I'm nobody when it comes to critiquing foods. But I am considered a cook and am quite familiar with hospitality. And the food is all made by scratch! I love their mouth watering Oxtails! Those oxtail are marinated in well seasoned sauces. And the catfish is like homemade southern fried catfish! Wait! It is homemade! Those recipes whoever came up with them ought to write a cookbook with the story in how that food was made and developed. Serious! 

Their cornbread is so memorable. After dining in and after ordering so many selections, you can't help but get extra cornbread to take home! And not to say the least about their butter! The butter selection is impeccable! Seasoned with Cajun spices and the grape Jelly butter goes quite well with their Biscuits! Oh and don't forget about those greens! They always run out of them! Lol!! You have to try their greens! I must go back and try their fried chicken and gravy! The Mac and cheese is nice as well. And their cocktails are fantastic! I'm not sure if I'm a lightweight, but just one putme over the top! 

Finally, you simply can not leave without that delicious Banana Pudding! That nice smooth pudding made with Chantilly cream that pairs so nicely with the pastry cream made by scratch! I mean what restaurant makes their own pastry cream. Yep, if I'm craving a nice homemade meal, this is my go too place forever! Thanks Kevin for setting up a wonderful restaurant that was so needed in that area. You made it an Icon!

Very good soul food! I've dined here before and ordered takeout a few times during the COVID-19 pandemic. When I dined in, we didn't receive our fried deviled egg appetizers until our main entrees had already arrived, which was about 30 minutes after we ordered. However, our waitress apologized for the wait on our appetizer and took it off our bill free of charge, which I appreciated. Despite the wait, I will admit those fried deviled egg appetizers were probably my favorite food from Fixins! They were so unique and delicious. 

I've also had the catfish, mac and cheese, collard greens, fried chicken, and biscuits here. All were delicious! My only complaint is that it can take awhile until the food is ready - usually at least 30 minutes - whether you dine-in or get takeout. So I just make sure to order ahead of time for takeout rather than waiting until I'm super hungry haha the employees are friendly and the food tastes great though, so I will continue to come here. :)

I was in SF and had to shoot to Sac for the day with my brother, so we got up with some friends and checked out Fixins in order to give oneself a bit of a taste of the south in Sac.

We opted for the Family Meal Deal, which is 8 pieces of fried chicken and two large sides. The chicken was above average (but not spectacular). However, the sides were undoubtedly the stars of the meal.

We opted for some yams (super sweet with a very unique finish on them), greens with turkey neck (freshy, leafy goodness), and a killer baked mac and cheese. The Mac was so good that we had to get a second order for us to split and it didn't need any seasoning added whatsoever.

They top the Family Meal off with four biscuits and a unique grape butter. I'd recommend making a sandwich with everything you have, which was the way to go for me and blended spice, savor, creamy heaven, and veggies.

Service was on point, super friendly, and exeptionally accommodating. They were definitely killing it with everything that they were getting done with COVID, so support local and give this place a shot!

I just wanted to say... if you are ever in Sacramento this is the place to go. Fixins Soul Kitchen was by far the best soul food I have ever had in my life. Every piece of food that was set onto our table today was a 10/10. The hospitality was amazing and just the whole atmosphere was great. I have never witnessed such good customer service in my life. A lovely lady who worked there that went by Aunt Pearl went out of her way to talk with us, make sure we were good. She gave us back ground on the restaurant and just how much they put heart into their kitchen. My son enjoyed the food as well. Our waitress was extremely sweet. & overall I think this place is a place that everyone needs to experience  I loved it & cannot wait to go back!

I'm so glad they are open during Covid break out. They practice safety measures and keep everyone safe with mask. Food is always fast and hot fresh off the stove. 
The environment is my favorite great music in the background with heaters on patio.
Food taste  like your at your big mama house, grandma, nana,granny. Comfort food for sure Excellent soul Food. My favorite hot spot.

Wow 2 times here and it's freaking amazing food and service. Don't mind the little wait you'll be happy everytime. The catfish bomb! The grape jelly butter and other butter amazing!! The gumbo make sure to add shrimp also amazing.
Good customer service. Attentive and check on customers. The fry chicken and waffle is delicious. So good!!!!!!!!!!!

The environment is lively and energetic. The staff was very kind and efficient throughout the night. I got the smothered chicken with candied yams and mac and cheese. Each part of my meal was flavorful and complemented each other perfectly. I would highly recommend going to Fixins if you're ever in the area!

I stopped in while waiting on my vehicle to finish being serviced down the street and it was best way I could have spent the time. A stylish bar with tvs, outdoor dining, and spacious indoor table seating. 
The food was AMAZING and seasoned to perfection. Drinks were great also. 
Another thing that makes me a definite repeat customer is how CLEAN this place was. 
I will definitely be back!
Shrimp Gumbo + New Orleans sweet Tea was on point. Definitely be back whenever I'm in Sac

The two stars is strictly for the pimento cheese dip and our server. 

The cheese dip is interesting, however, it does seize up in the mini cast iron pan that is served at the table and over the course of the meal is quickly no long a dip but more of a curdled slice of pimento and cheese, extremely unappetizing. 

The tortilla chips that accompanied the dip tasted of refried chips.  They came stale, chewy, & flavorless.  Taste like they needed enough chips for service and took a few day old chips and refried them thinking it would save them? Nope.  

Our server wasn't very attentive and did lag quite a bit during our experience.  At one point we went some time wondering where our server had gone.  

It want a busy lunch as we say servers trying to find things to do and dancing.

5 stars for the food and the playlist though.

I have been wanting to try Fixins since forever and I finally came around to it. I was craving a chicken sandwich and saw they have Kool-Aid pickles on the menu so I figured I'd come here and try it out. They have street parking which wasn't too hard to find during the time I went (1.5 hours close to closing)

I love their protocol for Covid-19 with the social distancing procedures, having hand sanitizer at the front, and keeping your mask on until your food and drinks arrive. The utensils were even disposable as well, which I could understand why they would offer that instead. 

As I mentioned, I originally came for the chicken sando but saw oxtails so I immediately go for the oxtails instead (I wanted to be healthier). My sister stuck to the chicken sando so I figured I'd try a bite of hers. 

Oxtails were pretty good (4/5), it was sweet, salty, and savory. The taste almost, just almost reminded me of the Vietnamese dish Thit Kho (a braised caramelized pork with eggs dish). I loved how the oxtail pulled apart from the bones. The greens were decent (3.5/5), they gave a large portion of that, yay for greens! I loved the rice (4/5), it had some kind of good flavor added to it and worked well with the oxtails.

The chicken sandwich (5/5)... I made a mistake of taking a bite. Why? Because it was so darn good and as I was eating my Oxtail, I kept looking at my sister enjoy her sandwich. The perfect crunch, moist and juicy chicken, seasoned so well, melt in your mouth, and party of sauces on the sandwich.. mmmmm.  Don't get me wrong, the Oxtails were good but the Chicken sandwich would've hit the spot. 

Also got some iced teas, they came in a 32 or maybe 40 oz mason jar, nice and icy cold, and super refreshing.

Service was okay, It wasn't that busy when me and my sister went. Our server was fairly attentive but when he would ask us (along with some other workers) if we were okay and needed anything... they would forget and I wouldn't get what I had requested until maybe I've asked for the 3rd or 4th time.

This was one of the first restaurants I've dined at since COVID and they were definitely busy. They have seating outside and have 2 lines outside. One for take out and another to dine outside. 

I was excited to try this place out due to all the reviews and who can say no to fried chicken? We got the 2 pc fried chicken and it came with your choice of 2 sides. I got potato salad and candied yams. The chicken was soft and moist. It wasn't spectacular but it wasn't bad. Sides were ok. I think I like the fried chicken at South better. I definitely expected it to be super good but it was alright. 

Service was great tho. Everyone was nice and they all had masks on and they were quick.

Great southern style food! Their fried chicken is divine, crispy and juicy and although I thought I wanted  three pieces, I'm glad I had two. The wing and thigh were huge! I had greens and red beans and rice for my sides. I love good greens and these were just right. The red beans and rice was tasty and the biscuits were great! Our server was prompt, pleasant and knowledgeable. Fixins decor is modern-artistic-folksy with wood plank tables. Good bar drinks too. My spouse had the gumbo and loved it. Yet another winning restaurant for Oak Park!
The fried chicken was serendipitous, came out in less than 15 minutes a lovely waiter and a sensational atmosphere. 10/10 restaurant

Wow wow wow wow. I came here on a Saturday morning with a group of six and was lucky enough to get a table without a wait. The staff we interacted with were so friendly. I was blown away with portion sizes of everything. Heck, even the glasses of water were in large mason jars. I've had little soul food before but this place was amazing, I kept it simple and got a fried chicken omelet. The prices are solid for the amount of food you get.
If you crave soul food, this is your place. They have lots of tasty food and drink choices!

had brunch here with my family and the vibes were amazing. food was on point and loved the atmosphere 
in

I have come here a total of 5 times in the past three years since Fixins opened. Reason? Fried chicken is my guilty pleasure. Fried chicken is in its own food group. With that said, I've been to practically every business that serves fried chicken so this review is a reflection of my rating of this restaurant compared to others. 

I will start by saying the service is great here. It has been every time I've visited. 

The biggest disappointment is the food, however. I ordered the fried chicken 4 out of the 5 times coming here. One of those times which was 2 years ago, the chicken was moist and seasoned well. The other three times the chicken was too salty and unappetizing and not crispy and on the drier side (compared to other places) despite just being cooked. 

I also ordered oxtail two times. The first time the oxtail was tender at dinner time service and when I ordered it again today at brunch it was incredibly dry and salty. The sides are unappetizing and really you could get better food elsewhere. 

I really wanted to love this place because we live nearby but the inconsistencies make it a place not worth visiting again especially since they are only hitting the mark 20% of the time.

Love the food. Dislike the dessert. Red velvet cake was amazing when they first opened now dry, stale and just nasty. They need a new pastry chef and to not be selling pieces of cake that should have been thrown out a week ago.

This place is usually pretty packed so try to go during off hours or try your hand at the bar. There is also a bar outside. The adult Koolaid and deviled eggs is a must! I wish there was more turkey neck in the collard greens but everything is pretty tasty.
Amazing food and service. Vanessa was incredible and made our experience one to remember! The music and entire ambiance is wonderful!
Best soul food in Sacramento and customer service. Noah and BJ showed nothing but hospitality, I highly recommend this restaurant.

The food is edible and the service is so so depending on who is serving you. Shot out to the redhead he was fabulous. I didn't catch his name. But the true experience is the environment with all the beautiful people all the different people the atmosphere is what keeps me coming back!

WOW!!! Found this place on a whim and I'm so glad I went, the food is by far the best meal I have ever had. The staff was very professional and friendly to the point I felt like family. I love that it's black owned and that the owner is from oak park. The environment is awesome and I can't wait to take my children here to eat. Wish I could give a 10 star rating. I will definitely be coming back and would recommend this restaurant to anyone who is anyone.

If I am ever visiting Sac from the Bay Area, I eat at Fixins!! My go to is the smothered chicken. If I would fix one thing, the chicken should be served over rice with the gravy and the rice NOT considered a side dish. 
The lavender lemonade (lemon drop) is by far the best I have ever tasted. The deep fried deviled eggs are to die for and the catfish nuggets are amazing as well. If you plan to go on a Sunday, expect a long wait and plan accordingly. Highly recommended!!

Well, I'll keep it short, too much negativity to speak of, particularly the dirty drinking glass with something stuck on the inside.

Not impressed, started with the sub par customer service. Rude young waitress, seems she did not want to be at work to do her job.

GREAT CUSTOMER SERVICE IS KEY FOR SMALL BUSINESSES. Which was not evident, one person's actions effects the reputation of an entire business. 

Regarding the food, as Public Enemy expressed, don't believe the hype. At best, the food is mediocre.  Perhaps I could understand the positive reviews if we DID NOT have the skill set of cooking tasty Soul Food @ home & having many friends that cook tasty Soul Food very well @ home- PERHAPS I would have given 3stars.Nevertheless a big disappointment. 
Enough said. Will not return,  I do not/will not recommend. No one of our party of 6 was happy with our experience

Today, my boyfriend and I tried out a local soul spot that makes you feel right at home. With the staff being friendly as soon as you walk up, you may have to wait 15-30 minutes before you get seat but it is well worth the wait. They have a nice patio area and some seating in front of the restaurant doors since we can't go inside due to Covid. 

Covid hours 11-7pm, daily. 

We tried some favorites on the menu: 
- Smother Chicken 
- Fried Catfish 
- Peach Cobbler 

For our sides we had Mac n cheese and greens. The sides had a good proportion and taste was DELICIOUS! 

The entrees were the complete package. The smothered chicken has a kind of a spicy gravy on top. The fried catfish is covered in corn fritters. 

The cornbread was moist and comes with a butter mix that had me hooked. It's a sweet butter but has a orang-ish color. 

Peach cobbler wasn't served the way we expected and kinda lost our interest the more we ate it. The crust seems to be gone or soggy and only had 2 peaches and lots of filling to cover the steal iron plate. 

Drinks we had 2 alcoholic beverages: 
- Blue raspberry fizz 
- Hurricane 
The drinks were made smooth with a good amount of alcohol to taste. No complaints! 

We will be back to try some other items on the list.
Best soul food restaurant in California!! Forget just Sacramento!! First of all, Cat the server was super kind, attentive, funny and attentive. Secondly, the food a slap your mama good!!! Enjoy!

When this place first opened up, they were superb. Now I don't know what's going on with the kitchen staff the food is always salty or burnt or something scorched gravy. Maybe they need to send their Chefs back to culinary school.

Amazing food in a great, relaxed atmosphere.  Very popular, recommend going when you aren't on a tight schedule so you can enjoy it.  Kind staff.  Highly recommend the chicken and waffles.  The Mac and cheese looked amazing but we were too full to try it.

I WILL NEVER GO HERE AGAIN!!! 
Spent $60+ on food.. we live 20 minutes away. Got home and am MISSING HALF OUR ITEMS!! No sides. No biscuits. This is absolutely infuriating. I will never ever ever order here again. Do better and stop ripping people off.

We came here because of the good ratings, and to celebrate 3 birthdays. It was a big disappointment. There were nine of us. We waited 1 hour and 45 minutes on a Saturday. The hostess told us the wait time would be about 1-1:15 but it went way over. When we got to our table the waiter seems nice. She took our orders. Seems to know what she's doing, But man we were in it for a surprise! Kids got their drinks first, the adults drinks were late late. Then my friend had to ask the waiter for his lemonade four times. Everyone got their food finally but one entree and a side was missing. My kid never got his Mac n Cheese until he finished his plate. I also didn't get my entree until everyone was almost done with their dinner. The waiter keeps coming to our table to tell me they were making my plate and for 20 additional minutes I finally got my entree. So basically she just put my order in. No apology whatsoever. We spent $300 between 4 adults and 5 kids. Not only the service was horrific, but I hope it wasn't a Joke from this clown waiter... So instead of putting candles on a free dessert she came out with candles on a scoop of butter that you get for toasts in the morning  and handed to the Kids so we can sing Happy Birthday!? I hope the owner of this restaurant will read this and see how embarrassing this is for your restaurant. People came here to celebrate their kids birthdays, we had 3 Birthdays. Not only she messed up most of our orders but she tried to give us butter with candles on it?? You couldn't afford to give us a free piece of pie or a cookie with candles on it?? We just spent $300 plus the 18% mandatory tip! This restaurant was definitely a huge disappointment for all 9 of us! 

Some of the plates we ordered: 
Catfish plate: it was good (3 orders)
Oxtail: dry and no gravy or sauce came with it. The pieces were small and not much meat on it.
Gumbo: tasted good but there were nothing much in it?! And nope, is not a seafood gumbo! See pic. (2 orders)
Smothered pork chops: my friend said it tasted like there were too much chicken bouillon added.
Chicken Tenders: Good and crunchy and moist. (3 orders)
Burger: dry asf... looked burnt too.

Ratings:
One star for location and parking.
One star for some of the plates that were good.
**also they don't take reservations so this jacks up the wait time.

This used to be our go-to place for soul food. After I noticed something was off about a year ago, I gave it a solid 3 more tries before writing a review. Sadly, I have to now share that the fried chicken lacks flavor - nothing special like it used to be. I used to say the black eyed peas here are so good, I would come just for that -- my last two visits missed the mark completely. The mac and cheese used to also be our go-to but last night, it looked like we were served someone's leftovers. Overall, all the Fixins were overly salty. As much as we want this place to survive, I truly hope the management reads these reviews and improve. (I also informed the waiter - but I doubt they have the time to let their chefs know).

When I tell you, this is the best fried chicken I've had since moving out of Baltimore....good lordt. Fried chicken is  a tradition where I come from, present at everything from Sunday dinners to weekend cookouts. Heck, places in the south even have restaurants called "Maryland Fried Chicken," which I always thought was funny. When I moved to Cali I knew good soul food would be hard to find- I mean, it's not typically vegan or gluten free :P

I was skeptical, and then very pleasantly surprised to find the best soul food in Cali in Sacramento. 

We ordered the fried chicken (duh) and sides including collards, red beans and rice, Mac, and okra. The fried chicken was just- perfect. So perfectly crispy yet not overdone, you could eat just about every bit of the skin. Flavor was on point, meat for the light and dark pieces was perfectly moist. The collards were smoky and sweet, the type to write home about (literally texted my dad about them lol), something I just haven't been able to replicate. The Mac and cheese was THE BOMB- no filler in here! The red beans and rice were also awesome. I didn't try the okra but I typically only like mine fried, so knew better. The BISCUITS and THE LOVELY BUTTER omg I could eat a whole plate of them. 

What took this place even further over the top was the "service." It didn't have the stuffy, transactional feel of many good restaurants. The people working here were genuinely kind, and gave you the feeling of being "home." We were checked on multiple times by Miss Pearl, who just wanted to make sure we were fed and satisfied. It was so sweet and grounding. Not to mention they were super patient and welcoming with my 18 month old baby (she's a mess- takes after mom lol). It's clear that the people working in this restaurant are about building community, and welcoming you into it. 

Tbh, it's unlikely I will return to Sacramento, but if I ever pass through again, I will DEFINITELY come back, and would even go out of my way. I highly, highly recommend this place from the bottom of my country heart.

Been here twice now, and can confidently say that this is one of the best restaurants I have been to in Sacramento. I rave about this place to everyone I know. The service is great. The ambiance and the aesthetic of the restaurant is immaculate. Lastly, the food is so delicious. One thing I would recommend is not getting the smothered chicken with dark meat, only because there's not a lot of meat on those pieces. Also with the smothered pork, I wish it was boneless, but that's my personal preference. Aside from that, everything else was great!

came in to order. Service was okay. 
I ordered a basic fried catfish greens and yams.  I also got the hush puppies 

I have to say the greens were good 
Buuuttttt 

The cat fish, yams, and hush puppies were horrible.  I never tasted hush puppies with out something mixed in it. Literally one small piece of corn in the middle of the hush puppies Lol.  
Catfish was nasty the yams were nasty. 
I'm just thankful the collard greens was good 

I wouldn't recommend this place at all way over rated.

The food is really good. The service is really good. This place is really expensive for what you are getting though. The service is really good. 
Fried chicken should never ever be 20.00 for 3 pieces and two sides loaded with sugar. 
You'll be back though, and instead of a to-go box you'll be carrying your food in your hands above your head like ants do on their way to the queen. It's that sweet.

The food is amazing always great. The seating is horrible. This is my second time taking my infant and needing a high chair. I was denied a high chair both times because booths don't accommodate high chairs. I make reservations before going. They had a large enough table this last time but instead sat a family there with no reservation. I wouldn't not consider this a family friendly place.
Thank you for accommodating our large party today.  Kim was an amazing server! We can in after a celebration of life with about 30 people.  Excellent food and service!!

The restaurant is gorgeous. I love the artwork, make sure you take the time look at it.  I started this review preRona and needed to get this done!  Ok so I have eaten here AT LEAST 12 times not kidding.  

This restaurant is not hype at all.  PreRona the food was delicious and consistent. The fried green tomatoes an absolute favorite.  And PLEASE save room for dessert!  The chicken is consistently good.  I enjoy shrimp and grits and everything else I have ever tried.  The mac and cheese not my favorite, but that is because I am loyal to my family recipe.  

Please do not go in there asking for cherry or grape koolaid!  Do it like when we were kids, "Can I get some red or blue?"  Then wait for the jumbo mason jar to come in...

Again great preCovid and great during Da Rona.

We had friends in from out of town so we all met for brunch at Fixins. Our waitress was excellent, she was right there and took excellent care of us. At first she gave us the wrong bill but quickly corrected that. The food was excellent and I enjoyed it very much. The group had fried green tomatoes and I had biscuits and gravy. Others in our party chose the restaurant. I would recommend them and plan to return.

Since the beginning of our Pandemic, I have aimed to support local businesses. Fixins Soul Kitchen has not only made it so easy to order online, but they also put time and care into cooking and packing our takeout orders. Pick up has been well organized and customer service is superb. Hands down, Fixins serves one of the most delicious, perfectly crispy catfish I have ever eaten! Their hush puppies are fantastic and are a perfect pair with the catfish! My fave sides are cheese grits and collard greens. Their portions are super filling and keep me going back for more! Please support this outstanding local business and enjoy all of their food that satisfies your soul like they have for mine!

My sister, and I decided to try Fixins today... let me just tell you, we are satisfied. The ox tails mmmmm delicious. The Mac & cheese, on POINT!  The shrimp and grits, yummy everything was good. Down to the kool -aid and sweet tea. On top of the good food, the service Ms. Maia provided was A+.  The dishes, and mixed drink we chose were based on her recommendations. 
Ms. Maia was kind, welcoming, knowledgeable and just an over all great server. I hope we sit in her area the next time I visit.

Service was good and food was even better! We arrived around 630 on a Saturday party of 2 and seated within 15-20min. Waitress was very nice and attentive, the environment of the place was very chill as if you were hanging out at a friends place. Good music and lovely decor. I ordered the fried chicken sandwich (very tasty) my boyfriend had the smothered chicken combo plate he loved the roux! The Mac and cheese was very good and flavorful. I also got a order of the peach cobbler ala mode...mmm mmm mm! I would def recommend this hidden gem in the oak park area! Will def return

I was a bit disappointed in Fixins, tried the smothered pork chops, they weren't very good.  The pork chops are fried before being smothered, when you cook them that way you need to make sure that they are fried correctly so that when they are smothered, it doesn't turn into mush because they were not fried long enough to produce a harder crust.  It was very messy and mushy, and the gravy was not very good.  I may go back and try other items but the pork chops and the wait time have me somewhat reluctant.

Went here a few weeks ago. Loved it. Best Mac and Cheese I've had in a long time. My only recommendation is less gray on the shrimp and grits.
I would definitely recommend this place for good food.

Wonderful drinks/food with great service/bartenders. Their catfish nuggets and main course is one of the best.  Then you have the bartender Isselle who makes a bomb Manhattan with Uncle Nearest Rye Whiskey
Fixins never disappoints.

Food is always amazing! The server I had was so kind even though I came close to closing time. Most of their drinks are bourbon or Hennessey but I was able to switch it for tequila. Y'all it's called fixins for a reason, try the fuss!
Over-hyped.  So-so chicken with poor quality sides at ridiculous prices.  a very poor value.  very disappointing.

I didn't enjoy my food... the smallest oxtail I ever saw.
No I will not go back. Maybe because it was the end of the night but why alway serve your best

Being from Houston where there were many delicious southern style restaurants, this definitely delivers when I need some Southern/Cajun cooking. Chicken and waffles are great. The red beans and rice are also very good. I haven't been disappointed in anything I've had here.

The Kids took Us to this fairly New place in Oak Park called Fixins Soul Food...I am hear to tell anyone within 3 hours of this place to drive here for brunch or lunch or dinner! Absolutely Amazing food and service ! Probably the best wait staff I have seen in Sacramento, Honestly... attentive, cheerful and they know the food they are serving is top notch!  The 24 hour brined fried chicken, best I've had in California , the Mac and cheese I need the recipe, the grits and cheese , long cooked stone ground grits .... Somebody stop me.....appetizers Fried green tomatoes with a  special house sauce and Fresno fire Chile, the Deep fried Deviled egg is in one of my top appetizers ever! Maria had the Oxtails over rice and collard greens  and turkey necks! Blaine had smothered pork chops and of course Carl and Kirk joined in on the fried chicken they had fried okra ..... we will be frequenting this spot for sure! Incredible experience for a first time .... And honestly you all no my big Ass knows good food! #ozzfeast #soulfoodcooking #fixinssoulkitchen
Hmm, it's a little less exciting than expected. The chicken wasn't cooked all the way and no seasoning.  The second try was hot but still tasteless...simple like batter.

My wife, Lindsay and I stopped by Fixins Soul Kitchen on our way home (San Diego) from Lake Tahoe.

They had their patio area open for dining during this pandemic. After a brief 10-15 minute wait, we were seated. They played some damn good music. Everyone was spaced out as required and All the folks I saw working there had their face coverings on.

The most difficult part was deciding what to order because EVERYTHING on the menu sounded so good. Our server, Desiree (sorry if I spelled your name wrong) was great. Lindsay asked her for recommendations and Desiree said they're known for their fried chicken and waffles. We ended up ordering (1) Three Piece Fried Chicken with two fixins and a biscuit. We went with mac & cheese and collard green. Note, you get to pick your chicken pieces. We went with a breast, thigh, and drumstick. We also got a side of gravy so we could "smothered" our fried chicken if needed. (2) Chicken & Sausage Gumbo with cornbread and (3) a Waffle. Perks of being married, we share everything 

Our food came out quick (under 10 minutes). The food was BOMB! Everything was hot and fresh.

(1) The fried chicken is the BEST I have ever had (sorry Willie Mae's in New Orleans ‍). The chicken was fried perfectly. Great crunch and not greasy. The meat, white & dark was moist and tasty. The gravy was "icing on the cake". Not needed, but definitely good to dip the chicken in. Glad we went fried chicken vice smothered. Lindsay said the collard greens were pretty damn good. The Mac & Cheese was decent, nothing to really praise or complain about.

(2) The gumbo was on point. Great salty flavor. I don't recall seeing much chicken, but do recall the sausage. In hindsight, I should have asked for more rice to dumb in there 

(3) The waffle was yummy. It was perfect, not too thin and not too thick. The butter was pink and tasty. Desiree mentioned it had cinnamon in it. Wonder what else they put in it to make it so good 

Service was top notch. (1) The hostess was extremely welcoming. I couldn't tell if she was also the manager. I watched her fix a problem with a customer who sat near us. The poor lady had been seated before us and we somehow got our food before her. The lady was rightfully pissed off. She was promptly served her food (with 5 minutes of her complaint) and they added a third piece of chicken to make it right. They might have tossed in an additional 2 wings. (2) As I mentioned above, Desiree was great. She was friendly, answered all our questions, and provided recommendations. We asked to change one of our fixins from Red Beans & Rice to the Collard Greens after we had placed our order and she took care of it with a smile. Thank you for providing Lindsay and I superb service.

I took the biscuit and cornbread home with me. They will be Tuesday breakfast  I hope this is the "norm" for Fixins Soul Kitchen. Lindsay and I plan to make this a  standard stop every time we are in the Sacramento area.

The 1st visit was great (dined in) 5 stars. Staff made you feel comfortable and enjoyed the food (really delicious). Nice quaint location (inside and out). 

The 2nd visit (take out) 4 stars. Ordered two orders of the fried chicken one order was fine the other however the chicken seemed to be a bit overcooked. Sides were fine. But will need to remember to ask for the butter for the biscuits on to go orders.

The atmosphere at the bar is always phenomenal paired with delicious comfort food! I usually come to  Fixins on Wednesdays, when Q is bartending... Her personality is an entire vibe! Check Ms. Q out at the bar she will not disappoint. I recommend trying the catfish nuggets as an appetizer

Ok. So I talked about how good the food was to my wife and she said we had to go back the next day. We did and IT WAS AMAZING AGAIN! She ordered what I had the day before (fried chicken, mac&cheese and greens) plus the fried devil eggs and peach cobbler. SHE LOVED IT ALL! I had the oxtails, rice and greens. Like the Terminator - I'LL BE BACK!

My family and I came in on Saturday afternoon. There was a wait since our party was of 7 (their max party is 6, but called in to check if it was okay), but it was kind of them to be offering water to those who waited! I believe it was the manager who was handing out water, and to me, it spoke volumes.

While being seated, the server was so sweet! I wish I wrote her name down, I know it started with a S. She was able to answer all our questions with the food, drinks, and recommendations. Very patient and attentive.

The vibe was amazing! The music, the people. To the drinks and the food. We got a little of everything.

Drinks we had were Blue Raspberry Fizz, Lavender Lemon Drop, Adult Kool-Aid, Rosé Rebellion (beer), and It Is What It Is (beer). All the drinks were really good! I love how it wasn't overwhelming sweet - they were all refreshing.

Food was AMAZING! We had Catfish, Smothered Chicken, Hot Chicken Sandwich, Oxtails, and Smothered Porkchop. When I say they did not lack anything, I mean it! The sides were also really good!
Sadly, we did not get any desert since we were so full. But we will next time!

Our server and the manager were really good at checking in with us/everyone- making sure the food was good, if we needed anything, and taking away any empty cups or empty plates.

We've never left a restaurant so happy before! This place was def good to our souls. So genuine. We're most def coming back! We went to Oak Park Brewery and even seen Kevin Johnson! He was so kind and was happy to take a picture with us.

The hospitality of this place, words aren't enough. Thank you so much for all you guys do in the community!

Food is always on point here. Love the smothered fried chicken and I always get a kook aid lemonade, it's a must. Last time I went it took awhile to get served and we weren't sure if we were forgotten about. But still great food.
The best food anywhere, great ambiance and  fabulous service. Can't wait for our next opportunity to eat here.

Good food. Service could be better, it can take a while to get food/drinks. Love the vibe and cultural significance. The quotes on the wall and the mason jars of kooliad are so nostalgic. Truly warm and soulful place.

I've come here twice and ordered different items both times. It's a place I would bring friends whenever they visit Sacramento. The staff are nice and the food is good. The portions are pretty large for its price. I ordered the fried catfish that came with hush puppies and 2 sides (okra and collard greens were my choice). I also ordered an ape roll spritz and I did not regret it. Definitely a place I would come back to as a treat.
I had oxtails and greens my dinner was delicious! No waiting service was great! I will return.
Waited 1:45 hours and we had reservations.......can't tell you about the food yet...... maybe they're better at killing chicken.

Another take out experience done right.   We had 3 pieces of the fried chicken with 2 sides (potato salad, and black-eyed peas).  We also had fried catfish with hush puppies with a side of cole slaw.   We had sweet iced tea and 2 New Orleans iced tea.    The pictures tell it all.
Food and service is excellent.  Parking can be difficult but definitely worth the hassle.

This is my second time eating here and once again, had a great soul food experience. I had the fried chicken again but with different sides. Highly recommend the charred okra with bacon and the sweet candied yams. I had some gumbo to go which I had for dinner that night and that was also very tasty. Next time I'm going to eat the gumbo on site with added shrimp. Great service, ambience, a real treat when I'm  traveling through Sacramento.

I've been wanting to try this place ever since a good friend raved about it who knows a thing or 2 about good soul food. I ordered the red kool-aid to start for a drink and it was so sweet and delicious. We shared the artichoke dip and it was good, almost a jalapeño white cheese dip flavor...delicious. The pimento cheese dip was good too but, a more mild flavor. I had the 2 piece fried chicken plate with dark meat and for the sides mac n cheese and candied yams. The fried chicken was so good and cooked perfectly and not too greasy, so yummy. The candied yams were just as good a home made and sweet. The mac n cheese was rich and had the perfect combo of cheeses, not too sharp or too bland, it was great. The grape jelly butter that they make themselves is so good too. It tastes like berry flavored cream cheese and was great on the biscuit. Me and my friend shared the peach cobbler and it's served alamode with vanilla bean ice cream. It is very sweet but oh soooo good, we kept eating it even though we were stuffed. The service was great and timely as well. I'll definitely be returning to this gem sometime soon.

Food:
Fried deviled eggs (7/10): Came out hot, presented beautifully, looked tasty. Tasted like a warm deviled egg, nothing special to the taste, egg white had a nice crisp breading, great for novelty. 
Pimento cheese dip (3/10): Flavor was nothing special, Velveeta texture, no classic cheese pull. It came out of the kitchen warm (not hot) and somewhat coagulated already. Chips too thick in texture. Would pass.

Fried Chicken (9/10): Great chicken, very moist inside and the skin had a very good crisp and crunchiness to it. Probably better than Roscoe's.
Fried Catfish (8/10): I am a seafood fan, so you've really gotta screw it up for me to give a bad rating. My only gripe about their fish was that the breading was on the saltier side. Otherwise, the crunch and texture was amazing, fish was moist inside. 

Their side dishes ("Fixins") are what tie your dish together, so choose wisely.
Mac & Cheese (10/10): Delicious and flavorful, tasted fresh and made with real cheese. Different levels of texture, not too greasy either.
Coleslaw (8/10): Fresh slaw, not too heavy on the mayo.
Potato Salad (10/10): Great kick of flavor, potatoes had a bite to them, not too mushy.
Red Beans & Rice (8/10): Because I'm preferable to mushy beans, these were alright to me. But essentially it was flavorful, and not mushy.
Charred Okra (2/10): I love okra, so I was hopeful for this one. Okra was cooked to a good consistency (bit of a crunch, but not raw) and the bacon bits were a nice concept. However, they were served with a vinegary coating which made them almost unbearable to eat. Just now how I like my okra. I probably would've been fine with some cajun seasoning. 
Cornbread Muffin (8/10): Soft cornbread muffin with a light crust. Was served with a delicious grape jelly butter. Wish the inside was a little more moist. 

Service: We came on a Sunday around 1pm where the quoted wait time was about two hours to be seated. Ended up being about a 45min wait. Weren't greeted very quickly by our server, and when we were greeted the server seemed like she needed us to make haste. Our appetizer came out before our waters. After our food came out, she was very attentive. Her absence at the beginning was likely due to the busy nature of Sunday mornings and what seemed to be an issue in the kitchen. 
Ambiance: Great decor. Cool, afro-centric vibes.

I Recommend Johnnia due to how excellent her service is, her incredible memory, and her attractiveness .
She's so hot. I'm definitely​ coming back in again. Hopefully she'll be there when I go in again.​
This place was really good! I got the chicken and waffles. I didn't particularly love the grits. I wish I would've got the coleslaw or potato salad.

Cooking with love provides food for the soul...and it's called Soul Food. 

My friend brought me when I was visiting here. I recently became vegetarian and working on becoming vegan so that's why I didn't try their famous fried chicken. I couldn't resist the Macaroni and cheese. I'm only so strong! What we did order:

-Waffle 
-Cornbread 
-Vegan greens 
-Vegan Okra 
-Candied Yams 
-Red beans and Rice
-Macaroni and cheese 

I WAS IN HEAVEN! 
Not every day could be a cheat day but if it could be I would be here! But I was most impressed by their vegetarian menu. Yes, a soul food restaurant with a vegetarian menu, you heard me right! Now everyone can be happy. 

I would give you guys suggestions on what to order but I believe anything you order is probably a good choice. Quality food and quality service. Also, expect to wait, we waited for about 45 minutes. You can go across the street to the brewery to waste time if you need to! Anything good is usually worth the wait! 

Remember without Soul it's just food!

The worst service ive ever received. Ordered to go and went to pick up. The girl handed me the bag of my order..when I got to the car the ice cream was melted (like it was literally milk in the container.) My boyfriend had to walk back to get actual ice cream instead of a cup of melted ice cream..did they really expect us to pour melted ice cream on the peach cobbler we ordered?? Ridiculous!! Then when we got home..we were missing a significant amount of items (multiple sides and a drink).  We called the business and stated what was missing from our order and asked if we can get refunded...they told us "yes you will get refunded" 3 days later no refund in my bank account. So the person that answered the phone on that Saturday evening lied to us and told us we would be refunded but really wouldn't be (terrible work ethic) why would you lie to your customers? Since we didn't see the refund 3 days later we had to call again!! My boyfriend called again and told another manager that we needed a refund. They said it should be refunded. At this point the staff/managers lie and don't care about their customers. I have been to this establishment multiple times but I've dined in...customer service was decent then. So, I guess they really don't care about people that spend their hard earned money on togo orders?  What would be efficient is if the staff that is bagging the togo order looks at the receipt and makes sure every item that's on the order is going in the bag. An EFFICIENT and respectable business would do this and would value ALL customers and not just the ones that dine in..if you are ordering togo be prepared to have some items missing and then get lied to that they will issue you a refund. SMH.

Overpriced for what you get!! Small portions, food is not tasty, didn't seem fresh! I've eaten in the restaurant and ordered their food from Door dash. Significant difference! Tastes better in person, but it's still overpriced.

Some of the best soul food in town. Our waitress So yes SORAYA was amazing and if you ever come here request, Soraya as your waitress, she will take good care of you!!!. I ordered the oxtails and let me tell you I will order them every time. Everything comes in big portions just how I like it.

Very good soul food.  My favorite dish is the catfish plate with greens and yams.  All of the sides are excellent.  Very busy place so i def recommend making a reservation.  It can get very noisy.  The ambience is great and so is the service.  Highly recommend!  We've been a few times. I want to try everything in the menu so we'll go back!

Came yesterday for takeout and got the shrimp n grits and smothered chop. The food was terribly under seasoned. Felt like I was eating heart healthy unintentionally, which is a no for soul food. 

Yams were greasy and the Mac would've made my grandma cry- just noodles and cheese. The spices were great but hard to appreciate it when there's no salt to bring the flavor out. I was really disappointed after spending almost &100. 

Shrimp was nice and plump but the poor grits were so bland, like they used water instead of chicken stock. Gravy? Bland. 2/5 but have an extra star for the thicc shrimp.

OMG!!! Wow!!
My family had the opportunity to have brunch at Fixins and let me tell you this was one of the best culinary experiences we've had, ever! 

Fixins bring the fire when it comes to southern comfort food and is easily the best in Sacramento. The fried chicken was spectacular, super crispy, extremely moist and packed with flavor. The waffles paired perfectly with the fried chicken and came with strawberry butter, yum! The star dish was the Oxtail. They took this plate to the next level with some rich and delicious spices in the reduction sauce that smothered the Oxtail. The meat was super tender falling off the bone. This is one of the best food I've ever had period. 

Our side dishes were equally impressive. The Mac N cheese has abundant cheese within and was very creamy. The black eyed peas were cooked to perfection and had some great flavor. We decided to try both desert options and glad we did. The banana pudding was light, delicate and tasty, however, we also had the peach cobbler and that desert stole the show. Honestly, words cannot describe the amazing depth of flavors. The peach cobbler was hands down the best pie my family had ever experienced. 

The food was a 10/10 without a doubt. We also enjoyed the decor and service. Our server Alanna was attentive, friendly and gave wonderful recommendations. The kitchen staff had our food out in record time, literally 5-minutes after ordering! The hostesses were great as well. The staff really make you feel welcome and go above and beyond. Multiple staff thanked us as we were leaving. 

If you want truly epic food, great service and a warm environment look no further.
The food and service is always great, one of my favorite places to eat in Sacramento,CA

I 
I had fixins today day the customer service there is excellent the food is excellent management is excellent the best restaurant in the Sacramento throughout Vallejo area thank you for your good customer service on today

I was a big fam of Fixens but this was my last order of oxtails full of bones. The plate was $25 and this is unacceptable. To top it off, I called two days in a row to speak with a manger and have yet to receive a call back. This is bad business!

First time eating here but the food was fantastic. If you want a fulfilling meal and a great atmosphere for mingling and conversation, this is the place! I created my own sampler with my +1 and had the yams, fried catfish, oxtails, macaroni and cheese, greens, cornbread, and a few cocktails. I'm from the South and very familiar with soul food. Although this version was a bit updated from traditional soul food, it still had the classic taste. I will visit again when I'm in the area. Also, there's seating outdoors, inside and at the bar!

Very good food with nice size portions. The chicken, sweet potatoes and Mac and cheese were really tasty. I dunked my biscuit covered in strawberry butter in the sweet potato juice. I would suggest that give you a choice of biscuit or corn muffin. Our smiling server Aime was friendly, but she truly did nothing except take our order. Someone else brought our drinks, food and cleaned the table. I hope she shared the tip we left with the people that did the actual work. We met the manager Billy who was personality plus. He gave me a complimentary tee shirt and hat. I thought that was just sooooooo cool. He is the right person for that job. I am from Chicago and felt a warm welcome. My friend and her family eat at Fixins frequently. Great recommendation. I wish I lived in Sacramento cause I would eat here a lot. 2 thumbs up. Windy City Sister Girl Kim

You know how they say when restaurants first open they're at the top of their game making sure the food is just right well I thought I'd send a shout out to the cooks at Fixins Soul Kitchen!  A year later and the chicken is still the BEST!  Stopped to eat lunch with my brother (his first time there) around 2:00 pm on a weekday and there was no wait for the outdoor seating.  Didn't try an alcoholic beverage yet but soon!  My first time having the shrimp and grits and it was tasty but I would have rather had more grits and less of the gravy it was swimming in.  I may try ordering this one again with extra grits and 1/3 of the gravy.  5 stars because there are so many items that are so YUMMY!  Can't wait to eat here again with my brother, my Sacramento dining partner!

Fried Chicken - YES! YES!
Cornbread - YES! The sweet butter/honey I want to eat with a spoon!!!   :-)
Mac-N-Cheese - YES!
Blackeyed peas - YES!
Sweet tea - YES!

I've been here a couple of times and each time it wasn't my choice of place and each time I still haven't had that wow factor that makes me want to be eager to come back. This time I had the catfish nuggets and it was very salty, the greens had a little burnt taste to them, the Mac n cheese was just ok, I also ordered a peach sangria that tasted like orange juice and wine, was NOT good. I'm trying to understand what everyone see in this place.

I have had this place on my list for awhile now and I finally tried it! They are open for dine in and takeout currently. They have outside and inside seating available. The staff is very friendly. Perfect environment and really good soul music. I got the two piece chicken and waffles! It was so tasty!! The chicken was cooked perfectly and the waffles were sweet and delicious! Their sweet tea was really good! I haven't had really good sweet tea like that since my trip to North Carolina! True authentic soul food! They have the menu I have posted available. They don't have the vegan menu available currently! I will definitely be back to try more of their food in the future! If you want some yummy soul food I definitely recommend this spot!

Absolutely EVERYTHING was phenomenal! We got an order of every side as well as the ox tails and the catfish.  We had a couple of the specialty cocktails, sooo good! As a bonus my kiddos got to meet KJ!

I cannot recommend this place enough. Every time I come, the food, the service and the vibe is always great. From the catfish, to the chicken and waffles, you cannot go wrong with anything on their menu. I will always be back

I am from out of town and happened to be in Sacramento and I wanted to try a restaurant from a list of black owned businesses in the area. My boyfriend had been craving oxtails for awhile now and we don't have a place open right now that makes them. So he asked me to look for a place that has them. I came across Fixins. We came in around noon on a Wednesday and it was pretty busy, i was happy to see that. 
The atmosphere is very nice, not loud at all. You can sit inside or out on their beautiful patio area. I was amused by the growlers full of koolaid, I love a cold glass of koolaid on a hot day!
Service was great, the hostess promptly seated us and our waitress was very attentive. I really enjoyed her service especially since she had all of her tables full. 
I ordered the 2 wings and a waffle with a side of collard greens. The waffle was light and fluffy with just the right amount of sweetness i didnt even need syrup.  
The wings were huge and cooked perfectly, but like other reviews I read they just weren't really seasoned. But overall I was pleased with the dish. The greens were really good flavored well I was worried as I saw alot of stems in the dish but they were perfectly tender. 
My boyfriend ordered the oxtails with candied yams. I did not try yams, but he said they were really good. The oxtails were melt in your mouth tender and had great flavor. 
The atmosphere and service were excellent. Food is good, I will be back to try a few more dishes!

Such a gem, worth the wait if you have no reservations, the food speaks volumes, you can actually taste the LOVE . Hands down best chicken since South (RIP) 11th an T. 

Staff are attentive and sweet


Thank you Fixins for an absolute fantastic experience
I come here regularly and have to travel from Stockton to get here, but it's always worth it. Fried chicken is delicious and I'm always amazed at how good the greens are.

This spot beats our fave chicken and waffle shop in socal! The chicken is very flavorful and the waffle is perfect. Another thing is staff are very helpful and nice. Over all 10/10 experience! Would come back for sure!

Absolutely enjoyed Fixins Soul Kitchen! My wife and I tried the Hot chicken sandwich, the Oxtail, Deviled eggs, and the Peach cobbler--All very delicious, washed it down with a huge mason jar of Sweet tea. We can't wait to go back! Only have a picture of the deviled eggs because we wolfed down the rest.

This place is amazing!! They are always so friendly and the food is delicious. They usually get busy so make sure to put your name on Yelp or call ahead. 
My favorite is their fried chicken, and Kool Aid is so refreshing.

Absolutely worst customer service! I used to love this place, but I'll never go back. Was quoted with an hour and a half wait, and watched group and after group who arrived after me get seated! I asked the hostess Jocelyn how much time before I'm seated and she tells me "you've only waited 42 minutes" and turns around and walks away. About 20 minutes later I ask the hostess Terri how much time left and she responds in the same way " you've only waited an hour" as she seats a party of 7 that came in after me and without a reservation. I asked her how did they get seated before me and her answer was they are a larger group so they get seated first!! What a waste of time!

The second weekend in a row found us at Fixin's-  The place is slammed at any time of the day or night, which I think is a great sign- To say it's worth the wait is an understatement.  The fact that they are so busy and still deliver stellar customer service already gives them five stars in my book.  But now on to the food-  The chicken and all the sides are TOO DIE FOR... those yams thou!!!
The real standouts for me are the shrimp and grits and the oxtails.  Both give the other a run for their money.  Run, don't walk to Fixin's, and thank me later.  Plus, the people-watching is half the fun!

I met my friends here for a late Sunday lunch. It was busy with a short 20 minutes wait for a table of four. I ordered the chicken wings & waffles and it was delicious! I loved the whipped butter it came with, too. Our waitress was awesome! Despite being so busy, she gave her undivided attention and checked on us every few minutes, which was a huge PLUS! I wished I got her name (sorry).

Best food I've had in a very long time. My housemates loved it. One of them said it made him want to cry. The other said it made him feel black again. We all agreed someone grandma definitely put their foot in this cooking. 100% would recommend to eat here. Great welcoming atmosphere as well

My family and I are hoping to support more black owned businesses throughout the Sacramento area.  We ventured to Fixins for Sunday lunch with our kids.  No private parking available, I would recommend street parking for this establishment. 

We were able to get quick seating outside patio area without a wait or reservations.  The staff were all wearing protective gear including masks and gloves.  My husband is vegetarian so were were not aware that they had to revise their menu and could not offer the vegan menu.  We did find a salad for him to enjoy along with the candied yams (YUM).
I ordered the fried chicken salad with the honey mustard dressing. It was BOMB.  My kids ordered the mac and cheese as well as the fried chicken drumstick.  All food items were delicious! The kids menu was also impressive!  

I also ordered the unsweetened ice tea, very delicious and refreshing on a warm summer day! 

I had to take off a star because we did wait a pretty long time for our food which was hard with the kids.  I would definitely would come back and recommend the establishment to family and friends.

First time here, and saw they had Oxtail on the menu. It was one of the best , if not THE best, I've ever had. Tender, fall off the bone goodness. The sauce was delicious and and not overpowering. Usually comes served over rice but  I was able to swap for collard greens and cole slaw, which were both delicious. Their ice teas are huge and the service was friendly and on point. Definitely will go back next time I'm in town.
Very nice atmosphere and you must make reservations. Full bar and the food is very good. Staff was pleasant but they do get very busy

Great food and service every time.  They are local and I can walk, bike or drive right over.  They are an asset to the neighborhood.  Love me some grits. Fried chicken, catfish etc.  The brussel sprouts, wheat pie yum.  I just wish they had mashed potatoes.  But the candies yams.  I am making myself hungry.

Wow. Hands down the best fried chicken I've ever had. I ordered the 2 piece dark meat which came with two sides. I got the charred okra and the Mac and cheese. Both weregreat . The okra was so flavorful and was topped with some bacon. A group of us came here after an exam and got some cocktails. I'd recommend the cucumber one and the strawberry whiskey one! They have an extensive cocktail menu with cool unique drinks. They also have alcoholic kool aid  Make your way here if you can!

This place was absolutely amazing from the food to the customer service! Not only We were seated by Kevin Johnson, but our waiter who was also the team lead was even better! I honestly didn't even notice it was Kevin Johnson because he looked super younger for some reason, he looked light weight good but that's not the point of this review hehe  

The food was 5 stars the drinks was 5 stars as well. I love the precautions they took during covid 19 I felt super safe. I didn't catch the mixologist name but he was an attractive Asian guy, ans his drinks were BOMB! Please keep him!  My favorite drink is the lavender lemon drop I had 2 of those and so did my friend. I tried the adult Kool aid and it didn't live up to its expectations from the 1st time I tried it. When I had the adult kool laid the first time it was in a Mason jar I feel like they should keep that going because the presentation when you order this drink during dine in sucks! SAVE YOUR $. I also tried the sweet bourbon tea however that one was just ok to me because I'm not a huge fan of dark.  

Overall my experience was great I'm happy and relieved that the airconditioner was on blast lol)I hate going to soul food restaurants and its hot with a bunch of flies flying around and chaos. I mean who could enjoy their food that way? I'm super happy and relieved that Sacramento has a very good, chill, soul food place for all ages to enjoy, to have a good time, we need more of this in Sac! Because our black culture should be cherished and revealed to the masses! Thank you for a great experience! Now I can bring my Mom, Grandma, and Sis (They used me as a Guinea pig to try it 1st but they dont know its my 2nd time lol)

I forgot to take pictures before I did damage!  All I can say is I want this restaurant in Las Vegas.  Order the fried Chicken and so fried catfish.  Had red beans & rice, mac n cheese, collard greens and black eyed peas.  Bro I can honestly say I had one of the best home cooked meals of my life.  Everything tasted like it came from Granny's house.  Somebody in the kitchen needs to be slapped.  Food was perfect which is hard to do in a soul food restaurant.  Can't give them a 5star because I took it to go.  But the dine in vibe would definitely push me over.  Heck they even have blue koolaid.
Black owned will support. Foods pretty good, I think my gumbo is better but the vibes and service is top notch

Went with family on a Friday night. We had a reservation and were seated right away. The music was on point, our waitress (Alizae) was friendly & knowledgeable, and the food & drinks were delicious. We ordered 5-6 different dishes and couldn't find a single fault! I've been Jonesing for those grits since we left the restaurant, so I'll definitely be back.

Ambiance was great! Service, great! Music, great!! We had the Fried Chicken and Oxtails. Fried chicken was amazing. It was juicy, flavorful, and a small, nice little kick. The oxtails were amazing as well. Tender as all get out, the gravy was really good, and the rice was great also. I would give 4.5 stars but it's not available. I gave it a bit of a knock due to the sides. Greens were on point! Potato salad good, not great. Mac n' Cheese was good as well, but not great. Washed it all down with some Kool-Aid!! We will be back... Again and again!!
The food was great. Especially loved the fried green tomatoes, hot chicken sandwich, Mac n cheese and candied yams. So so fire!

I've heard really good things about Fixins so I was excited to try it today. I'll summarize by saying that it was good but not life changing. I got the fried chicken based on the other reviews and got a thigh and a breast. The crunch was really good. Probably one of the crunchiest fried chickens I've had before. I was a little underwhelmed by the seasoning. It certainly wasn't bad but it wasn't outstanding either. The chicken breast was a little on the dry side. That's the nature of chicken breasts: they dry out at higher heats. I'd hoped Fixins' 24 hour soaking might have changed that but it could only help so much. In the future I'd stick to dark meat. 

I got the Mac n cheese and the candied yams as my sides. The Mac and cheese, like the fried chicken, was good but not spectacular. The candied yams had some nice fall spices which complemented the sweetness. I was pleasantly surprised by the biscuit. Biscuits/rolls are often an afterthought but this one was really flaky with a great flavor as well. It was served with grape jam. 

The food takes about 20 minutes to prepare so I'd recommend ordering online ahead of time, especially since they're usually busy. Today was the first time I've been so I can't speak to the other things on the menu. Fixins was good and I'd probably go back but be realistic about your expectations. Again, it was good but not life-changing.

Love this restaurant! I have to go to Sacramento for work and my co-worker told me about Fixins. I have eaten here twice and had excellent food and service both times.  Oh yeah, they have great cocktails too!  
This time my friend and I got the fried deviled eggs which are awesome, and I got the Oxtails. The oxtails are excellent. They know how to make them. They are fall off the bone finger licking good. The Mac N Cheese is excellent too. I had the Lavender Lemon Drop and the Sidecar (I think that is what it was called).  Both drinks were great.  I can't wait to eat here again!
Everything we ordered was amazing. Fried chicken and ox tail dishes were Very delicious! The service was outstanding. Can't wait to go back!
