This place was sooooooo good! The food was delicious and the service was fantastic. We definitely will be back. The portions were huge and cooked to perfection.

Wow. This place EXCEEDED expectations. The service was slow at the bar (they were very busy and just opened dine-in back up) but the food is so good I didn't care. 

Their hot chicken sandwich is by far the best I've ever had. Our waiter said the hot sauce is ridiculously hot so I got in on the side, but I didn't find it to be unbearable and actually asked for extra. Next time I'll get it on the sandwich. 

My husband got fried dark meat chicken and Mac and cheese and loved it. We got the cornbread too... but it's just ok. Save your calories for the fried chicken. Couldn't recommend this place more and can't wait to go back!

The ambience was warm and inviting, the staff was very friendly and food was very good .
I was in Sacramento for a meeting and heard this was a good place for soul food and I was not disappointed.
I found a rare gem good food excellent service and great ambience.

Best fried chicken you will ever have . Juicey , flavorful , crispy and delicious.
Mac and cheese and sweet tea where bomb

Service and atmosphere was very good.  We had catfish and oxtail soup. Tried some sides and appetizers we enjoyed a tasty peach sangria with our meal was refreshing. The catfish filet was spot on! Spice choice and light crispy batter delectable.  Could not get enough of the oxtail soup!! I will ask for more of the sauce between a biscuit and the bed of  rice ; it's not enough. Enjoyed the coleslaw very tangy. The collards are OK, the grits too. Pimento cheese spread not so much and served with tortilla chips. Our Mac and cheese was tasty but seemed oily. The surprise of the meal was the charred okra! I enjoyed it!!

We stopped by to get coffee and we figured we were hungry so we went on Yelp and found this place! I wasn't really looking for anything in particular especially since it was a Sunday, I knew places would be busy so I was fine with anything. We got lucky finding a parking spot. We got to the restaurant and you can see their beautiful shaded patio! The inside was also very spacious, and the employees wear masks and gloves. I liked how the hostess asked us if we wanted to be inside or outside. I like how she provided options. Of course we chose outside because it was such a nice day.

They were pretty busy but we didn't mind waiting. They didn't mind us bringing our coffee with us. The waiter kindly approached us and he gave us huge mason jars of water. We ended up ordering the catfish and the oxtail. It was a little difficult to eat because they provide plastic utensils so it was hard for me to use it on a fried fish. The waiter constantly checked up on us, the music was good, and the atmosphere was just nice and peaceful.

Food: the oxtail was bomb! My bf loved it sooo much. It was so soft and was so easy to eat! He finished his entire plate lol. The Mac n cheese is to die for!! If you come here you HAVE to get the Mac n cheese. Ugh, it was soooo good I could eat it all day! My catfish was also delicious and my potato salad was also yummy. I just didn't like the rice and beans, it tasted like medicine to me (maybe it was just me since I'm picky with beans) but my bf said it reminded him of MRE food lol but he still liked it. 

Would I come here again? Yes yes yes! Awesome service, great food, great set-up/atmosphere. Might bring my pup next time :)

Every time I go here, the service is great and the food is better. True would good that would make my entire family proud from Georgia. The fried chicken and waffle is one of the best items. I love the sweet strawberry butter they give you. I also have had the catfish, and catfish po boy. Sweet tea. And the peach cobbler and banana pudding. Will I come back a 3rd round? YES. 

Only downside in my opinion is the Mac n cheese. But every person makes theirs their own way. I like more cheese, creamy and baked.

The food there is DELICIOUS!!! I had just about every Black reactive to having some good food! I had the fried fish & holy trinity of fixins (Mac & cheese, greens, and yams). Everything was so flavorful and soulful! My partner had the fried chicken with the holy trinity with hot sauce and ended up buying a hot sauce bottle because it was soooo good! Amy was very attentive and managed three brand new tables with ease and grace! The ambiance of the place was amazing! I loved the music it reminded me of dining in Atlanta. I was a little upset about the wait since we had a reservation at 8, however after eating there I get it! It was really hard to get up. It was so packed but soooo worth it!!!
this place is awesome! the food is well portioned, tasted amazing and the environment is super lively. friendly staff :) would definitely come again
My husband brought me here for my birthday and I was pleasantly surprised. The food was great and the atmosphere was awesome
Staff, establishment, and food beautiful! Everything was an experience. I enjoyed the vibe.

We came from Roseville and got in time before it starts getting crowded. I ordered the oxtail meal and it was delicious. My wife and son ordered the chicken waffles and it was delicious. My younger son had the burger kids meal and he said it was bussin. The waitress was very nice and the restaurant was nice as well and clean. We enjoyed the experience d and the delicious meals. Will see you soon.  

Why did I only get 2 pieces of Oxtail while all the photos on here has 3 pieces?
This restaurant is amazing. such good vibes and great service. Ryan and Luc are great servers. Had a wonderful experience. everyone should come here!!

Delicious food and great service! We've been here in a large group (pre-COVID), and ordered to go...each time Fixins did not disappoint. 

Some of our favorites: 
Catfish plate and the Nuggets, perfectly crispy, tender, and juicy. 
Chicken--drooling just thinking about it--same as above, but as a chicken lover, even better! Flavorful, perfectly crunchy, and so juicy. 
Candied Yams--I was only allowed to try one because my soul daughter loved them so much she didn't want to share. Only because I tried one and it was absolutely delicious, I agreed I would have called dibs too, just like I do with my fries!
Speaking of...Fried Okra...OMG...mouthwatering, delectable, savory. It's a must try!
I can go on and on, like how delicious the Chicken and Waffles plate was, as well as the Collard Greens and Bacon Deviled Eggs, but you'll just have to try for yourself, you just can't go wrong!

So, I have tried Fixin's several times but this was my first take out order. So disappointed. A $25 salad which arrived with no dressing, even though specified in the order, mac and cheese in a 4 oz cup with a teaspoon of grease at $6 and frozen french fries...not worth it at all. Total fee for meal and delivery $43.00. So wrong. So not worth it. Again, disappointed. Judge for yourself...the order image is attached. In summary, go South!

Fixins is a great soul food restaurant. So far I have been one time and I ordered the fried chicken meal (I chose wings) with two fixins (sides). The chicken is very well seasoned and I also really enjoyed the mac and cheese. I also had the candied yams that I thought were just ok (I pretty much only like the way my grandmother makes them so I saw that coming). It also came with a biscuit which was very good as well. I tried the banana pudding for dessert and was not really a fan, I would likely try a different dessert in the future.

I ordered online and received a message with the estimated time it would be done and it was done way faster which I appreciated. I parked and walked up and the individuals out front brought my food out right away (I realized as I was leaving that they have a drive up, pick up option where you can just wait in your car right out front and someone will bring your food out. I will be using this option next time. The parking seems like it can be a bit difficult depending on the time of day so this is a great option). The food was packaged well and was still hot when I got home.

I will definitely be back in the future to try something else!

With the "shelter in place" mandate, my fiancé and I wanted to try and support small local businesses during these trying times. When we saw that Fixins Soul Kitchen was offering take-out food orders, we decided to try them for the very first time. I had this place bookmarked forever, prior to all of this madness happening, and it was always too busy for us to go.

We placed our order online for a bucket of fried chicken which came with four biscuits. We also ordered a side of grits and a side of mac n' cheese.

When we got to the restaurant, they were following the fundamental health and safety precautions put in place, so we waited outside until they called our name to pick up our order. We had already paid for our order online so after making sure everything was in the bag we took our food home to enjoy.

The chicken was juicy and tasted phenomenal. But what really stole the show for me was the grits. I could have easily eaten an entire two or three orders by myself. And I am not a big fan of grits so that is saying a lot. The mac n' cheese was tasty as well but I felt it would have been even better if we had the opportunity to eat it while it was still fresh and hot.

Consider this review a placeholder - because I cannot wait to come back to Fixins Soul Kitchen when all of this madness has subsided and try their food at the restaurant itself.

4/10/20 

I tried Fixins for the first time back in October 2019 - when they opened. I was able to find parking around the corner. Service was friendly and food came out in a timely manner. The restaurant is spacious with lots of seating. The ambiance is warm and homey. It was a full house the night I went so it was a bit loud. 

I ordered the Fried Chicken with 2 sides ($14). I'm a sucker for mac & cheese and Fixins nailed their mac & cheese recipe. I also got their candied yams which were good but a little too sweet for my personal preference. I will try a different side next time - perhaps collard greens. Their fried chicken is delicious!! Their butter is delicious. My friend ordered their chicken & waffles, French fries, and grits w/ cheese. She loved everything! 

Their current promotion lured me in for lunch today. In light of the current pandemic, Fixins is offering a Stay at Home Box Lunch Special for $9.99. You get 2 pieces of chicken, 1 side of your choice, and either a biscuit or a cookie. Please note: this deal is only for a limited amount of time. 

I went online to order for curbside pickup today. My confirmation email said it would be ready in about 20-25 minutes. I called when I arrived and a nice employee brought my food out, along with to-go utensils and hot sauce. It was a smooth process. 

Thank you to everyone at Fixins for working hard to provide such appetizing food to everyone in Sacramento to enjoy.

A great stop with some truly amazing food! Show up hungry because you will want to order everything off the menu. They also have a great happy hour menu and the bar staff has always made amazing mocktails for myself! Great service, food, and location!

Food is great. 
Service can improve. 
Ambience is a vibe! 

My fave is the friend chicken, greens, corn bread, peach cobbler, Mac n cheese. All of it tbh

I always think the prices are high, but we always have a great time.  Eating at both the LA Live ( in Los Angeles) and the Oak Park (in Sacramento) locations. I have always ordered the gumbo, but the fried chicken is very good.  Peach cobbler and greens are also good.  Nobody ever complains about their meal.
I only purchased sides, mac and cheese, sweet potatoes and greens. The greens were a little on the sweet side, the sweet potatoes were oily, and the Mac and cheese was dry and over cooked noodles.

I will definitely come back here. I wanted some soul food for breakfast and was trying to find biscuits.  They had no biscuits ... but what they did have was shrimp and grits!

The grits were super buttery and cheesy. My favorite. They were definitely New Orleans style with a brown gravy too. 

I only got 3 shrimp. For $20 maybe shoot me 5 prawns? Ha. 

It was very good though. I would order it again.  

My husband settled with chicken and waffles always a good choice. The staff was great and everyone was distanced and masked during our visit

i'm giving this place three stars because it's definitely run down. It gives me the vibe of a Chili's or an Applebee's. The food was good at least initially I came home and got sick. The greens are too sweet as well as the potato salad. However, the biscuits in the cornbread were amazing fried green tomatoes amazing. Not sure if the chicken wasn't cooked all the way, but I normally don't eat me a lot so that could've been it however, I can't remember the servers name but she was amazing and kind if I find my receipt, I will update this review with her name. It just smelt dirty and we sat right next to the kitchen interest that could've contributed to it I've been here once before, and had a pleasant experience, I definitely think that the restaurant can use a makeover. However, if you keep up the good work with a good staff, it makes it worth it.
Good atmosphere, good food, and good service!!! I definitely recommend if you like soul food

Great place, nice e vibe, order to go, eat in and patio dining.  I had ox tail...really wanted to try a new thing.  It's great.  Service was good inside dining area.  

Outside "stand" was confusing and slow.  Recommend signage and more proactive staff.

The food was absolutely AMAZING!!! EVERYTHING WAS DELICIOUS!!! The service was okay but the waitress was a lil off, which is why I gave 4 stars!!! The ambiance was also great, music was great and atmosphere was relax and comfortable!!! I would definitely recommend this place!!!

Food is hella good and worth every minute of the wait. Portions are large, the drinks are bomb. 

Had the fried fish , collard greens, and Mac n cheese. I would go here anytime , anyway. 

Not to mention the food was served hot and I feel like things were fairly priced.
Great food about 99.99% of the tine.  The greens have been too salty the last couple of visits.

We came in with a party of 9 at around 6 PM, and had to wait about 40 minutes, but it was well worth the wait.

The place was very clean inside.   I ordered the smothered chicken, and it was very good.  The sides I chose were the greens and red beans and rice, and both had good flavor.  I substituted the biscuit with cornbread, and that was good as well.  Everyone at our table enjoyed their entrees and sides as well.

Our server, Nataya, was very friendly and accommodated all of our requests (and made sure all of the food was done correctly).  Portions were just right.

I think we'll be getting food again at Fixins, and need to try the other sides and entrees on the menu (as there was a good selection to choose from).
I thoroughly enjoyed our meal. I had the chicken and waffles, and they were excellent. The chicken was moist and flavorful. Our server was excellent I can't wait to to go back.

Used to be a big fan, but my last visit was heartbreaking and maybe even intentional. We were celebrating and ordered a bucket, sides the works on DoorDash. There's nowhere to add sauce so we called after ordering and asked and they promised every bag doesn't just come with ranch but a lot. Needless to say they forgot the ranch, all the other sauces and the dressing for the salad. Rendering a $90 plate of disappointment. 

I've dealt with some rudeness from Fixins employees before, but gave them the benefit of the doubt until now.
The service is good, staff is friendly and the food and the drinks are delicious! The ambiance is always inviting and happy!

Pros:
- great service
- great dining atmosphere, lovely outdoor patio
- portion size

Cons:
- very inauthentic
- a little pricey

I lived in Charleston, SC for about 8 years and New Orleans, LA for about 2. Charleston's famous for shrimp n grits, NOLA for gumbo (among other things). From reading Fixin's menu and what their shrimp n grits and gumbo tasted like...it seems that anything that's smothered or sauced has a brown gravy base. And I mean like a packaged brown gravy, not a roux.

Their shrimp and grits was overwhelmingly brown gravy and almost souplike. The grits were not creamy the way they should be, in shrimp n grits. However, the shrimp were not overcooked, I'll give em that one.

I only had a tiny bite of my dining companion's gumbo. Neither dish tasted bad, but neither were authentic enough to hit the spot for someone who's actually lived in the South.

Before we ordered, I'd asked the waiter if the collards were bitter, and he said yes. Collards done right are not bitter - - that should've tipped me off right there. (And they're usually cooked with smoked ham hocks or other pork product, not turkey necks.)

The waiter was very on top of things and he did also offer to give us a small portion of oxtail or a dessert when I voiced disappointment, so kudos on service.

If I were a Californian I'd likely be perfectly happy eating a meal here, if I didn't know what the dishes were supposed to taste like. This is absolutely not soul food.

I love love Fixins Soul Kitchen. I brought my family here back in February for my mother's birthday and everyone absolutely enjoyed it. When we got there it was a bit crowded so we had to wait obviously. After we were seated it did take time before we were served but the wait was worth it. 

We ordered chicken and waffles, shrimp and grits and a side of collard greens. OMGOSHH the chicken was absolutely amazing. It was crispy and crunchy on the outside and steamy and juicy on the inside. The shrimp and grits was very rich and creamy. 

I  recommend this place and will definitely be visit again soon. 

Oh yeah, forgot to mention that we also saw the owner Kevin J. He opened the door for my parents and thanked us for coming. The overall experience and food was great!!

This place is legit. I don'teat out often because my wife is an amazing cook and most restaurants can't compete.  I was here in SAC for business and decided to give them a try.  It was AMAZING.  All those good reviews are accurate. Fried chicken was perfect.  Mac n cheese, greens, yams, red beans and rice were very good.  Will definitely go back.
The collard greens were great. I liked the fried chicken and potato salad. My wife liked the okra. I'd go back when up in Sacramento.
Delicious food!  The greens, mac-n-cheese, fried chicken, and candied yams were so good.  The gumbo was tasty but I wanted more meat in my portion.  Leftovers today were great, reheated in the oven.

Good food. The menu could use a few more options. 

GOOD:
The peach cobbler is so so delicious, I had to order another one. 
Greens were flavor and delicious.
Everything meal had flavor and was tasty.
Smothered chicken was a very filling and tasty dish. 

OKAY:
Shrimp and grits could use more grits and a bit less gravy or sauce. 

Mac N Cheese was just okay, a bit of a dry macaroni, could use a bit more cheese sauce. Cheese just clumped up between the macaroni causing it to be dry. 

We ordered the gumbo and had to add shrimp. I guess there version of basic gumbo is chicken and sausage ONLY. Shrimp is an additional $7. I've never seen a gumbo made with shrimp. ALSO there were on two sausage pieces in the gumbo, I'm not sure if thats because we added shrimp or not but that was definitely a rip off for the price. 

All in all, PEACH COBBLER for the win!!

I lived in the south for 5 years. This is the first restaurant that I've been to in years where the food immediately brings you back! Cornmeal crusted catfish, peach cobbler, and typical southern sides. Yum! You really can't go wrong. My family also ordered the smothered chicken, chicken sandwich, kid's waffle and wing, and banana pudding. Everyone loved their meals, the portions were solid, and the drink options were fun. There are even monster glasses of refillable Kool-aid which will bring pretty much anyone back to their childhood.

In addition to that, the service was excellent. Our server was friendly, efficient, and went out of her way to make my 6 year old's day. Thanks for the relaxed family evening!!

I went for lunch and got the chicken sandwich with fries. We ended up being seated at the bar  so we could watch college football.  The food arrived fast & was hot & fresh. Service was okay. Im not sure if it was due to the restaurants being busy or the fact that's we were at the bar area. I will definitely be coming back.

Good is tasty and service is good. Love the artwork and sayings on the wall!
Fried Chicken
The food and drinks absolutely amazing!!! Great service as well. Definitely my new favorite place fotlr friend chicken and ox tails. Highly recommend

Good food and hit the southern food cravings. We loved the shrimp and grits the most..... and the fried green tomatoes wit catfish was really great. Of course the fried chicken was a bomb as well. 
Will definitely go back.
The best soul food in Oak Park. Great people, great service, beautiful atmosphere. This is a place you can take visitors for a soulful experience.

Since I'm really a fan of pork chops, I tried their smothered pork chops and have to say was really disappointed. The place seem noisy and busy, but the service was really slow. I might come back and try breakfast as it looked OK, but stay away from the pork chops.
Best soul food place in Sacramento kool-aid the bestSmothered Pork ChopFried Chicken Salad

I'm so happy to support local black owned businesses, and let me tell you this place has one of the best soul foods. I got to experience dine in back before covid happen, went during dinner; busy time for them. Our server was on it though, she gave us recommendations and was very helpful. There is so much on the menu I still want to try, but I've only had their chicken and waffles. It's amazing! By far my favorite in town. I SAID IT! The chicken is so juicy while the skin perfectly makes that crunch when you bite in. Waffles could be cooked a little longer but that's my preference (I don't like soggy/soft waffles) and I believe their butter spread is homemade but I can't recall if it was apple or something but it was good! Oh and you have to get their sweet tea, its sweet :P 

During covid, they did an excellent job with pick up orders. Providing umbrella for shade during the summer months. Still busy as usual even during crazy times... which I'm glad! Getting the food to go and dining in made no difference it was still amazing!

Aliyah was our server, she was the best! Dope service. She recommended the oxtails they tasted awesome 
Great conversation, respectful and nice. She made our experience really great

Went here when it first opened and food was soo good! Came in with some friends recently because my girl wanted to come out here and try it. We ordered the catfish bites, chicken sandwich, the 2 piece chicken, black eyed peas.... And waited for Mac and cheese. Chicken was dry, catfish bites were dry served not cold, but room temp. Mac and cheese took awhile to come out because "they were making it fresh" came out like it was, again, Room temp not hot. Black eyed peas were cold. Took forever to get a beer and the bar wasn't even busy. 

Service wasn't the best, I'm guessing they were short staff, but the quality took a nose dive after the pandemic. Not sure if I'll be coming back or making this a Soul food recommendation for friends who aren't from around here.

Hook, line, and sinker! This place will have you coming back for more without a doubt. My mom, boyfriend and I stopped in for brunch, and we're not prepared for the pure joy that was in store. 

I had the fried chicken, Mac and cheese and cheese grits. The chicken was flavorful, juicy and perfectly crispy. It was music to my ears with every bite. The Mac and cheese had several cheeses used in it, and was creamy and full of flavors. The grits were on point and the best I have had outside Louisiana. My boyfriend had the oz tail, and I stole a few bites, and I should have taken the entire plate. Hands down the best item on the menu. The meat was tender and fell off the bone. It was smothered in a mouth watering sauce that was very complex in flavor. We finished our meal with off with the peach cobbler which was the best pie/cobbler I have personally ever had. 

If you're ever in Sacramento, it's a MUST! It's the best Sacramento has to offer and I've lived here 41 years and tried it all.

I came to Fixins to get some down to earth comfort food as I was in need of a meal to put a smile on my heart and soul.  I ordered two eggs over easy, applewood smoked bacon, and fixins taters that are made with sweet potatoes, Yukon golds, crumbled bacon, diced onions, peppers, and herbs and spices. And I must say Fixin's potatoes are in one word ... yummylicious. The customer service is outstanding at Fixins as your coffe cup will never be empty.. This restaurant gets very busy so be sure to call ahead for booth or table reservations.

I wish I had stopped to take a picture of our delicious meals. It's Saturday night and I had plans to go to dinner but was just too tired. I've been wanting to try this place and placed a GrubHub order. I was worried that my Togo order wouldn't be as good as dining in. 

First bite of the fried fish and my husband's friend chicken and all my worries melted away! Literally the best fried fish and chicken either of us has had. I had the red beans and rice and fries. My husband had the greens and Mac and cheese. Both were delicious and we'll definitely order again. Can't wait to try their other dishes.
The food was great! I got the catfish with collard greens.

Food is good, but service is very poor. This happened on TWO separate occasions, with one time to myself, and the other time, to my girlfriend (we're Asian if that matters). We always order online for pick up. Each time, we were the first customer in line. No one attended to us. Only until a line started forming did they come up and asked if we needed help. We told them we were there to pick up an online order. They then proceeded to go bus tables and attended to everybody that arrived AFTER us. After waiting 20-30 min, they finally came up to us again and then proceeded to ask what we needed help with! We reiterated that we were there for a pick up order! The entire time, our food was just sitting there! Horrible service. The first time it happened, I gave them the benefit of the doubt, but after the same thing happened to my girlfriend, we'll take our business elsewhere.

This place was amazing! We should have gotten a reservation because it was a long wait but the food was some of the best I have ever had. The fried devilled eggs were to die for! The vibe was fun and lively and our server was fantastic.
I travel to sac. Just to have their food after trying one day! Soooo yummy! I would definitely recommend this spot!

While I've heard mixed reviews this is my 3rd time here personally. The place is packed with lots of participants from the MLK march today and I am just as pleased today as I was the first time we came for a meal! The catfish is SO good I have order the meal twice. Today I purchased the catfish nuggets with the shrimp and grits meal. Not only was the food piping hot and flavorful but the service was excellent! 

I knew if the grits were as banging as the catfish that it would be time for me to Yelp the experience! It's called "SOUL FOOD" for a reason! I'm leaving here today with and full stomach and spirit! Way to go Fixins a staff! I appreciate each and everyone of you for making our dining experience excellent from start to finish.

I highly recommend eating here
My current Sacramento food obsession. We go maybe too much (if that's even such a thing). Love everything I've had here but the standout is the okra. To die for.
The food was great and the kool aid was amazing. The service was a little slow but because the vibe was nice and chill. We didn't mind waiting.
This cobbler was more like a soup cobbler. Very thin and runny more peaches and juice than anything. The service was great wonderful atmosphere.

I have mixed feelings about Fixins Soul Kitchen. My first visit was on a quiet Wednesday night and there were only a few patrons. I was seated right away, but waited a bit for anyone to come by my table. I was in a bit of a hurry so I flagged down a server. I told him I was trying to get in and out of there within an hour and had 3 items which I'm considering but wanted to know which would be the fastest to make. I was thinking about the chicken and waffles, the oxtail and shrimp and grits. He said that all were pretty expedient to make and I decided on the chicken and waffles.

The food came out very quickly and I dug right in. The waffle was excellent! It came with a grape butter jelly which was addicting. I added that and just a hint of syrup to make my waffle perfect. I got two wings with my waffle and the wings were huge! The batter was nice and thick and the meat was juicy. However, there was something off about the texture of the meat. When I was pulling the meat off the bone, it didn't tear easily, as if it wasn't fully cooked. I checked to make sure there was no blood or any indications that it was still raw. There wasn't, but I felt uneasy about the texture and didn't really eat the chicken. If I'd had more time I might have said something, but I didn't. I wanted something sweet and ordered a buttermilk pie to go. The waiter brought it out in a plate with a to go box because he wanted to show me the presentation. (He noticed I'd been taking pictures of the food.) I thought that was very considerate. He was able to ring me up and I was out of there in the allotted time I asked for.  I had the pie later on in the evening and it okay, nothing spectacular. I'm not sure if I would get it again. For that evening, my server was good and very accommodating, but the food quality was a little lacking.

We came back on a busy Saturday night and the place was hoppin. We got seated right away, but it took over 10 minutes and 2 requests with the host for someone to come to our table. Staff were servicing the tables around us, but no one came by to check if someone had come to our table. When a server finally came by, our order was promptly taken and our food came out quickly. Although, our main course arrived before our appetizer. We got the deep fried deviled eggs for an appetizer and shrimp and grits for a main course. I liked the deviled eggs, but couldn't understand why they were deep fried. They seemed fine without the batter. My friend did not like the breading at all and took it off. The shrimp and grits were good. The grits were done well, but there wasn't enough of it. The shrimp were cooked to perfection, but were overpowered by the peppery gravy. This dish would have been better if there were more grits and less gravy. That way the shrimp and grits would have really anchored this dish. As it was both items were drowning in gravy.

Like with any new restaurant that's received so much hype, I really wanted to like Fixins, but I've had issues in each of my visits. I will most likely try it again, to give them the benefit of the doubt, plus I really want to try the oxtail.
This place was delicious. Potions were huge, we could have ordered one plate for the two of us. Chicken was crunchy and juicy. Highly recommend!

Wa so glad we got to sneak in for a table of three plus a kid on Mother's Day. Everything about Fixins was perfect. The drinks were so large & sweet, felt just like the south! They were great with my kiddo and I know this is random but the kids highchair was plastic, clean and new! Not the wooden nasty one. The food was five star all the way!!

Living in the era of "plant base" clean eating " this place is such a WONDERFUL time out to spoil your palette every once in a while . It's the BEST SOUL FOOD outside of your gramma's on Thanksgiving. I've been several times so I've had the pleasure of sampling most of the menu. If you haven't tried the DEEP FRIED BOILED EGG!!!! OMG they are to die for . Pork Chops are my fab fav , but my family members love the oxtails EVERY SIDE IS DELICIOUS!!! The peach cobbler taste like if was blessed by our southern ancestors !! If you LOVE African American Cuisine 


THIS PLACE IS AS CLOSE THE HAVEN AS YOU CAN GET
Had fried chicken and a waffle: both delicious! Service was fine too. Ambiance outdoor could be improved with more plants.

Yummm.. I'm a big fan of their smothered ox tail. I've been here several times and that seems to be my go to dish. They aren't shy when it comes to their portions. I never finish and I always have left overs for a complete second meal. Smothered pork chops were decent. Not my favorite on their menu but it was ok. I always order my dishes with a side of greens. The Mac and cheese is standard. And the potato salad is the typical yellow mustard kind. Everything is good. Everything I've ordered so far has been pretty tasty. The catfish nuggets I didn't get to eat but my friend seemed to enjoy it, so I'll take that as a plus. The okra was definitely hands down my favorite appetizer/side. It's unfortunate they don't offer it now with their limited menu due to covid. I'm hoping once things go back to a more normal state that they bring back the okra.
This is the best soul food. We just absolutely love this place. The food and the people are wonderful

We placed a reservation for 7:30 got sat at 7:25 with a great server named Sean he was super attentive, helpful, and polite. I had the catfish nuggets and the hot chicken sandwich with a peach cobbler for dessert my mother had the deep fried deviled eggs, chicken and waffles with a peach cobbler for dessert. While it was a little pricey coming to $100.05 before tip the food was great and I had enough for the next day (the hot chicken sandwich is HUGE imo) the lively environment was matched with great music, drinks, and even sports on the TV. We will definitely be back!

Love their oxtail. (Most of the time) occasionally it isn't falling off the bone but for the most part it is on point. I usually get the oxtail with a side of Mac and cheese my toddler loves their Mac and cheese, side of grits and cheese, okra and their fried catfish bites. Love their red kool aid. I have tried their gumbo which I tried a while ago but it was on the saltier side so I haven't tried again, but everything else has been amazing.
This place never disappoints! The wait can be long, but definitely worth it. I like to get the Oxtail smothered with rice and gravy and macaroni on the side.

Outdoor dining during COVID:

Came on a Saturday right at noon and got seating at the side patio right away, before the rush started. 

Food was really good. The chicken and waffles was something I would get again!! The waffles were crunchy but soft on the inside and the batter for the fried chicken was seasoned well. Mac and cheese was just okay; it came out a little too mushy. It was the first time I had collard greens so I don't have a reference for them, but I enjoyed them. For the price, portion size was pretty big for the main dishes but a lil small much for the sides. I couldn't even finish the 2 bit chicken pieces that came with the chicken and waffles.

Very friendly servers too who came and checked on us if we needed anything and of course, wore masks the whole time! (Shoutout to Key)

Seriously recommend trying this place out if you're in town.

Save your money go to Stagecoach on Florin for real soul. Chicken was fair at best, good crust but little seasoning. Fried green tomatoes were HARD...How are they hard, you just fried them? Took a lot of work with a knife to cut through. Deep fried deviled eggs...ehhh. Best appetizer  was the catfish nuggets. As a side I ordered grits with cheese and black eyed peas...grits came very COLD! I've eaten lots of cheese grits...everywhere else they are hot enough to melt the cheese...who wants to eat pasty grits and cold cheese....black eyed peas.....what a disappointment....served in brown gravy....something I have never seen, and I have eaten A LOT of black eyed peas. No trinity, no hocks, bland and tasteless. Hot chicken sandwich was a miss also. I've eaten hot chicken at Hatty B's in Nashville, and World Famous Hotboys locally so those are my gauges....this was simply a breaded piece of chicken with Franks Red Hot sprinkled on it....not even dredged in the sauce. I have nothing against Franks Red Hot, we keep it in the cupboard but for a "fancy" soul restaurant to not make their own sauce...epic fail. The best part of the meal was the biscuit and the epic size mason jar of kool-aid.  
The wait staff was very courteous and bussers quick to clear plates, or I would have given it a one star rating.

The food was delicious. I ordered the fried fish with potato salad and greens. They didn't have the potato salad. I picked another side. Then they ended up giving me a side while we waited for our meal. After we got done eating they gave us potato salad. That was so nice! My love got smothered pork chops with red beans and rice and yams. Yummy! Our server Cheri was very nice and she seemed very genuine. This is the best service we've had in awhile. It was very refreshing to see and feel staff who were smiling and who were respectful. From the hostess to our server, to the young woman Neysha who brought our sides, to Davon who was taking our plates away. Everyone was very kind. We haven't eaten at this restaurant in awhile, but we will be back. We left happy with full tummies and extra food for dinner tomorrow. Thank you!!!!

I expected a lot more from this restaurant since it's rated almost 5 stars.

We ordered 2 Old Fashions. I asked the server what do they use for their house whiskey and she said she wasn't sure and mentioned Maker's Mark was good, so I said ok that sounds good. She never disclosed to us that their was an upcharge for it. That's something she should mention or else I would've just gone with the house whiskey. 

I ordered the fried chicken and it didn't have a lot of meat on it. It was literally breading/skin/bones. If I'm paying $18 for a 3 piece chicken and sides it should have meat on the chicken. It also did not have much flavor to it. I had to put ranch and hot sauce to make it taste better.

My friend ordered the smothered chicken and it came out cold. 

My husband ordered the HOT chicken sandwich and it was really messy to the point where he had to eat it with a fork and knife. He also mentioned it had minimal meat and mostly bread. 

Overall our experience was not good.

Absolutely Enjoyable. I brought three old school women here for their mothers day treat and they all said 5 stars across the board including cleanliness, timeliness, quality and service. The are not stingy with their portions and you don't have to wait too long. 

The Mac and cheese was smackin, collards were full of flavor making me and drink the juice because I was so disappointed it was gone. That too, was delicious. I heard the yams were delicious but  I didn't taste them personally .  The potatoe salad was made with sweet relish. 

We ordered oxtails, catfish, burgers and and shrimp and grits. The oxtails are life changing. The catfish is fried to perfection with the perfect crispy crunch that you look for in fried fish. My neice was quiet in the corner with her burger and no complaints . Now the shrimp and grits don't got nothin on Roben's in Oakland but they tasted delicious. 

We went on Sunday we are head back this week for more.

Fresh meals, fried chicken was crispy and juicy!! The fried catfish was fried to a golden crisp, hush puppies tasted like donuts (very soft), collard greens with smoked turkey necks!!
Braised oxtails with rice was tasteful, seasoned great and potato salad which was nice and cold!! Love cold potato salad!! 
TREAT YOURSELF DON'T CHEAT YOURSELF!! Come to FIXINS!!

Maia got great customer service really knowledgeable about the menu, great at recommending. Sweetest server great personality. Auntie pearl is so proud of this restaurant and it shows through her caring personality. Always checking to make sure the table is satisfied. Great addition to the community. Get it while you can before this location ends up like the LA location. Packed!
Some of the food is good I just wish they had a bigger menu and more items I tried shrimp and grits for the first time and it was bland
Fixings was delicious everything on my plate was good and my whole party had a good time and delicious food service was good as well I would go back

I have to subtract a star because of the shrimp and grease- I mean,  shrimp and grits. I wish I could've took a picture but I was so disgusted by it that I threw it away.  I ate the shrimp and grits out of it but the remainder was,  grease so thick,  it sat on top of the sauce or whatever it was supposed to be.  And it gave me acid reflux so strong that I had to take 4 Tums. My husband's sandwich wasn't good either.  He didn't like whatever sauce they had put on it.  I understand that it was crowded that day but,  wow.  Just wow.
Food was good & the service was perfect. Food came right out they had a great playlist!
Q was absolutely delightful and attentive. Great food, awesome service, and just an all around nice place to hang out. Outdoor seating and bar area is spacious as well. Look forward to coming back.

From the service to the food this is a 10 out of 10! It has a real vibe going on as well. The service has been some of the best I've had in years. Definitely a go to spot! No matter what you order you can't go wrong, been several times and have always had something new. You will leave full and happy!
Pricing was reasonable. The gumbo was a little too thick for my taste. I like my gumbo a little more running. The fried chicken, yams, and macaroni and cheese was pretty tasty.

MAIN DISHES: I have been to Fixins 3 times and haven't been disappointed with their dishes. I've had their fried chicken 2 times (has always been consistently seasoned/cooked) and oxtail once. 

The fried chicken is crispy and not greasy it has good seasoning and batter. I always have leftovers because there's so much food (not complaining, I'm a happy customer). The chicken is so crispy I warmed up my leftovers for lunch the next day in the microwave and it was still crispy/crunchy. I like wings so I usually request for wings instead of leg/thigh/breast. That's the good thing about this place they let you choose your pieces.  

The oxtail is perfectly seasoned with spice and cooked to perfection, falling off the bone good-ness. They do not skimp on the oxtail either, you get at least 4 pieces with lots of meat unlike some places that serve oxtail.

The catfish was a friend's order and I tried a piece of it and wasn't impressed it was bland and needed some extra dipping sauce/hot sauce or something. 

SIDES: Charred okra was the best side, it had some blackened seasoning (I can't even begin to explain the taste) but was so amazing...I never thought I'd be a fan of okra but I am now. It was topped with little bits of bacon.

Biscuit and buttery berry spread was really good too. The biscuit is nice and crisp on the outside and moist and fluffy on the inside. The berry in the spread gives it a sweetness.

The red beans and rice was so yummy and perfect amount of spice, yum creamy beans and rice. 

Mac and cheese I wasn't too impressed by it the first time I had it, it tasted bland. I tried it again this time and really enjoyed it. I don't know if my taste buds were off the first time but I'm glad I got a second chance to try it and confirm I like the mac and cheese. 

I can't recall how the hush puppies were so can't review it but I'm not a fan of those to begin with. I'm sure I would've remembered it if I liked it.

LEMONADE: The lemonade was wayyyy too sweet and not enough lemon. I asked for a few slices of lemon and squeezed all four slices into my drink and tossed the slices in as well, was still too sweet and not lemony enough. I won't order again.

PRICE: I think the price is on point because it's quality food and you get big quantity of food and the drinks are huge too. I always leave with a to go box and cup. 

SERVICE: I've always had great service every time I went. They're friendly and fast when not busy. They tend to stay on the busy side so I recommend early lunch on the weekdays between 11-1130. I haven't been there on a weekend.

If you're looking for a foodgasm look no more. Talk about comfort food but it makes ya feel "fancy" lol I love this spot. It's comfy cozy yet new age and "cool". Staff is super helpful and actually share what they like when you ask for an opinion vs the cliche answer, "well this dish is popular, or people like.." lol can't wait to come back

Fixins was absolutely delicious as all of the food was full of flavor. The catfish nuggets were perfectly seasoned; the only thing that would make them better would be fresh/homemade tartar sauce with a bit more bite. I am not a huge fan of gumbo, but it was good and arguably even better as leftovers reheated the next day. If I ever find myself in Sacramento again, I would definitely return.
So yummy! The wait wasnt long for the weekend. Service was 4/5, had a couple minor order hiccups. All in all we will be back and would suggest if your in the area!

I had to come see what the hype was all
About. And the hype was real ! Kool aid was perfect i got red kool aid. I ordered the Fried Catfish it came with two sides and hush puppies. I ordered red beans and rice and Mac and cheese for my fixins. My picky boyfriend came with me and got fried chicken with candied yams and corn bread and he fell in love at first bite! I think everybody should try it especially if they have something on the menu you know you like it's twice better here the flavors are incredible!

I cannot get enough of Fixins'! The servers are always friendly and the food is great. I go usually for either the fried chicken, or the fried chicken omelet, but also the biscuits are baked insanity along with the berry-butter they use. My wife especially likes the collard greens, while I tend to order the grits or the beans and rice. Delicious.
Yummy ..................................................... that's all I gotta say . Very tasty can't go wrong with this place it hits every time

The fried chicken was juicy, crunchy and seasoned perfectly! The oxtail was falling off the bone BEST oxtail ever.!!! The collard greens, yams and grits was a 10 also!!! Ambiance was perfect went with my daughter and grand daughter. We got our food in about 15 mins everything hot and DELICIOUS!!!!

Great food! I had the catfish with mac n cheese and potato salad and my husband had the shrimp and grits. We agreed everything was cooked to perfect. The catfish was crispy and tender. The grits almost didn't even need the shrimp. We will definitely be back!

The staff were also very nice and friendly.
Some of the best soul food. So tasty everything was seasoned to perfection. The ambiance was very nice. And a great music selection. If you haven't been you need to go ASAP. REAL authentic soul food.

Food was delicious, service was gast and on the ball. In the last 2 years restaurant service has gone downhill across the board but than we came here and our minds were blown by how good the wait staff was.  We had fried chicken, smothered pork chops, greens, grits,  mac& cheese and some coleslaw. Everything was good. Coleslaw was the worst out of the bunch but it wasnt bad. Def a must try place

We had brunch at Fixins today for the first time; I'm new to Oak Park.  Fixins deserves a 5+ star rating on all counts.  Amazing service, amazing ambiance, and amazing food!  We had the fried catfish with corn fritters, and chose braised okra and greens for our sides.  Everything was super flavorful and perfectly cooked.  The portions were generous.  Our son had the waffle and fried chicken which he loved and I want to try that next time, as we live nearby and can't wait to return to keep trying new dishes.  Sharing the homemade peach cobbler, topped with a scoop of vanilla icecream was the perfect finish to a perfect meal.

We came here on a Saturday morning around 9 and didn't have much of a wait at all. They were super accommodating with our baby's stroller, letting us park next to the table, but then again there were open tables nearby.

We ordered the chicken and waffle, as well as the fried catfish. Portions were pretty good, we were definitely full by the end of the meal, but as far as price wise, I've had bigger portions for the same price and were able to take some food home. Not that it's a bad thing! We all kind of need to eat a little less, I guess!

Onto the actual taste. Leave it up to southern cooking to make a garbage fish like catfish taste good. It was moist and flaky, the batter wasn't too heavy at all. I opted for a side of mac and cheese (which was really just a few tablespoons full) and the candied yams. The corn fritters were just ... meh... but the other sides were so good. I could have gone for more mac and cheese, though.

Chicken and waffle (just one!) were good, the chicken was breaded perfectly and cooked perfectly too, and the whole thing didn't take too long to come out. Usually chicken and waffles take a little while to make, but it was probably around 10 mins for us.

The service was exceptional, everyone seemed so happy but without being annoyingly so. Maybe it was because we came first hting in the morning, I can picture it getting busier and probably more difficult to wear a smile on your face.

Regardless, a good new spot in Oak Park.

I've heard a lot of good things about this place. So I had to find out for myself and I must say, everything was true about how good the food was. I went there for the first time, and the manager randomly took care of my tab. Shout out to John(the manager) I had the three piece chicken(was juicy, crispy skin & well seasoned, yams(sweet enough & rich) and mac&cheese(oh so cheesy & creamy). Everything was on point.  I'll definitely be back! If you're looking for real authentic soulfood in Sacramento, this is the SPOT!

I was in town for business and a friend of mine recommended this place! It-was-delicious!! OMG! Everything was good! The catfish nuggets were well seasoned as were the collards. The cornbread was light and fluffy and the perfect mix between sweet and cornmeal. Oh, and the sauces!!! Sooooo good! I kept alternating between them! Might I add the people and Playlist in the restaurant were a nice vibe (mine was to go though). I definitely plan to be back next time I'm in town.

I've been a customer of fixins since they opened and for the first time in a long time I can actually  say the food was outright bad!!! Not the soul food they once served created in the name of " black excellence." Something has changed drastically  from the food to the ambiance....Something  is off . My advice is to teach the cooks to cook with love and remind them why it's called soul food in the first place.

Well sadly I ordered food to go but wish I would have stayed and got to enjoy the meal in the restaurant.  Felt like I was walking into someone's house, welcomed with open arms and couldn't have enjoyed the atmosphere any more!

The bartender was making drinks and had some fantastic recommendations on the menu as this was my first time here.

Auntie Pearl came and hung out with me telling me the history of the place and about the passion they bring to giving back and building the community up around them.

I've finished my meal and while I'm so full and need a nap, I already want to go back!!! Just YUM!!

Go to this place!!! You won't regret it!!

I stumbled upon this restaurant and I am glad to find another bougie (as in date-appropriate and $$, not suit and tie, $$$$) soul food place to visit. I have tried their dark fried chicken plate, smothered chicken, grits, coleslaw, rice and beans, Mac and cheese, biscuit, fried Devil's eggs and sweet tea. I'm going to review them in detail because I love this place and I want them to get noticed.

Fried chicken plate with two sides: I got to choose the kind of meat, light or dark, and 2 or 3 pieces on this combo. It also came with a non-traditional biscuit that was slightly sweet. The biscuit tasted great with the strawberry cream cheese spread, an interesting turn from plain biscuits. I loved the coleslaw; I had grits without cheese so it was ok. I will try it with cheese next time to see if it's super cheesy as I like.

Smothered chicken with two sides: the crunchy, scrumptious chicken is dressed in a rich gravy sauce. It was an intriguing mix of textures, and it was very well seasoned. Many places I tried have drier rice bean and rice than I would like but Fixins got it right IMO. The Mac and cheese was good as well.

Fried Devil's eggs: they tasted great, but I found it to be expensive since they're are only two eggs with some bacon bits.

Sweet tea: there was nothing special about this tea. Just a regular sweet tea

Parking: no designated parking, only street parking. It was relatively easy for me to find parking in the residential area at the time I went.

Service: the server was courteous but she forgot what we asked for so I had to come find her. It could be because they were busy so no stars taken off yet. I have to come back to see.

Suggestion: bigger portion

Visited for the first time on my Birthday with my family. Food was outstanding, we had a wait time of about 15 mins which wasn't bad. Our server was excellent extremely personable and on point. Our only issue they don't sing happy birthday lol otherwise we will definitely be back for the food!! I had  pork chops and my wife had the spicy dipped chicken sandwich. The muffins almost made us shout!!! So freaking good. And the atmosphere was cool as well!
The food was amazing! Our server Desiree was super attentive to our needs all the while we were there. Must go back and try some other items on menu. I had the oxtail and collard greens. Amazing!!!
Love this place, cozy & inviting. Food was bomb! Service was alright, they were pretty busy so I can't complain!

The food was great! 
The service was great! 
The portions were so huge! 
Flavor was awesome!
Price is decent for the quality of flavor and quantity of food that I got.
I ate here last night for the first time and am still thinking yum! I wanted to order so much more off the menu like the gumbo, or the shrimp and grits. And my mom says the fried green tomatoes are good ... so I definitely will go back to try some different menu items. Last night I had the pork chops with red beans and rice and mac n cheese. My friend had the fried chicken and her chicken breast looked so golden and crispy and it was HUGE! She also had the charred okra and greens for her Fixins. 
The setup is nice. . . 
Casual. 
Relaxed. 
Location is good but options for parking not so great. However I would not mind parking 3 blocks away to eat here again because you can't get food like this just anywhere here in Sacramento.

First time here and it was a good experience. It wasn't busy during noon. If you like Southern-style food, I definitely recommend trying this spot. The smothered chicken was yummy but really messy. Get ready to get your fingers dirty. The oxtail was very delicious and soft. The deep fried deviled eggs are a must-try! They are very interesting and delicious. The biscuits are very good with the strawberry-flavored butter. The Koolaid drinks are sweet and comes in a very cool looking mason jar! Overall, service was great and food was delicious. The only complaint for me is the price tag. It's a bit pricey, but the food portions are small. Each plate is roughly $20 and portions aren't that amazing.
Great food, desserts and service. Weekend are not the best time to visit but worst the wait. Did I say that have kool-aid...... To the waiter Dom you are the best and well verse with the menu.

Be prepared to wait at any given time , but esp on weekends ! We've been to here a few times and didn't have the patience to wait for over an hour so ended up leaving until our random Sunday 330 pm drop by where the wait was shorter , but still ended up being about 40 minutes  . So is it worth the wait ?! It's okayyyyy!

After looking over the menu for 40 minutes waiting , without hesitation we decided on the 3 piece chicken dinner with three sides to share, a large sweet tea, and a peach cobbler a la mode . We were thankful that our food came out rather quickly and looked really delicious. Highlights were definitely the fried chicken and the charred okra side and the flavored butter ! Chicken was very well made, seasoned well, and served warm.  The okra had great flavor and again it was served at the  expected temperature.  The flavored butter (it think grape?) was bomb.com ! I just wished the biscuit was better made to enjoy the butter on . I found our biscuit to be really small , flat,  and cold . But we still ate it with the excellent butter !

The other sides were just okay. I think if they were served warmer it would have tasted better. The green beans were actually kind of cold , and a bit mushy.  Their side portions are also not huge. Don't get me wrong , it's not like we need to be eating ginormous portions of anything , but we order a separate side of the green beans from the menu and the price you pay versus what you get ... is a bit   disappointing. 

The biggest disappointment was dessert, unfortunately. The peach cobbler , took its time getting to our table , because the ice cream was nearly all melted !! I was a bit surprised that the server even served us the poor looking plate. The peach cobbler looked like broken mush and white creamy stuff all over it . Like it was the last piece of some leftovers that was sitting around for days. Seriously ?  If we weren't already so full and kind of over just waiting in general, certainly I would have said something to the server and asked them to take the plate back or ask for the bill to adjusted. But really ... we were just ready to leave already since we can see the growing crowds outside the window and the noise in the restaurant getting uncomfortably loud.  So I'm waiving judgement on dessert. Maybe it was just a bad day for peach cobbler.   We ended up just shifting thru the cobbler. It was extremely sweet  and mushy. The consistency of peaches is like canned peaches, the  little bits of crust was complete mush and , it was not warm , just room temperature . Total failure on dessert on our visit. 

Anyway, main items were good. I'm not sure I would come back if I had to wait again as long as we did. If we were in the mood for some good fried chicken and had lots of time to kill , I would again visit . 

Service overall was friendly . The server who served us the mushy peach cobbler was not the same lady who served us initially. Initial server friendly and warm. She was great and  came by to let us know that someone else was going to take over helping us because she was finished with her shift .  Very professional . 

Space is pleasant, but very loud so be prepared.  Overall a good experience .  Definitely check out Fixins if you're in the neighborhood and cool with wait time to get a table !

When i heard this place opened i was pretty excited to have it near. First time here the wait was a bit long but they take numbers and text you get to see how fast or slow they are moving along with updates. That was a plus so instead of waiting around we went over to target and got items we needed by the time we got back wait was only 10mins and we were seated. 
The service was decent. Everyone friendly, energetic and they seemed to be working as a team. The menu has a good variety. 
I ordered chicken with greens and potato salad. I requested all dark. As the food came out i immediately noticed it was white meat. I simply ate my side dishes as i waited for someone to finally come ask how it was going so i can let them know. When i finally got attention and let them know the waitress was happy to bring out the dark meat. It was odd that they requested for the chicken back after it has been sitting on my plate. She even took the actual plate leaving me with two small bowls of sides. When she brought the dark meat or it was on a saucer. I didn't expect to keep the chicken but i would expect to return the whole plate including sides to ensure the whole meal was enjoyed at the same time and temp.
He ordered chicken and waffles! It was good! 

The second time i orders shrimp and grits. They were tasty but barely had grits i basically had mouths full of gravy most of the time. If there were more grits it would have been satisfying. 
I probably wouldn't go back any time work

I went to Fixins on Sunday after church and this was my first time eating in the restaurant. I ordered fried chicken, greens, and macaroni and cheese and everything was excellent. The portions were good and the prices were reasonable. The Peach Cobbler came out hot in a skillet with Vanilla Ice Cream and it was to die for!

As the Terminator says, I'll be back (even though I live in the East Bay)!  :-)

OMG!  You have to try this place!!  They have koolaid!!  Took me right back to my childhood. They also have adult (alcoholic) koolaid. Loved the cherry bomb and the grape koolaid. 

The food is delicious and the staff is awesome. We had to wait about 45 minutes but the service was fast despite the crowd. The food came out hot and delicious and our waitress was very nice and efficient. 

We all had some form of chicken (wings, two piece dark and tenders) and it was all delicious. The sides were good as well. The candied yams, red beans and rice and creamed corn were my favorites.  The potato salad was good too.

Ok. So we have wanted to visit Fixins for a couple of months but the wait was always super long on a weekends. We finally made it in midweek for lunch with no wait.
The chicken- super juicy with nice crispy skin. However, the batter was way WAAAAAYYYY too sweet for us. It would have been perfect without the sugar in the batter. Kind of ruined it for us. Fried chicken doesn't need to be this sweet.
The Mac and cheese- baked perfectly and on point.
Collards- REALLY good
Candied Yams- Pretty tasty 
Potato salad- completely worth your $$$. Delicious!
Banana Pudding- Best part of the meal. Do yourself a favor and order it.

Overpriced for the quality of food.
The food is overkill in seasoning and the chicken is overcooked and hard.

Met the family for lunch. Everyone enjoyed their meals. Catfish, gumbo, smothered chicken, fried chicken, potato salad, greens, black eyed peas. Everything was spectacular. Service was super friendly too! Indeed a nice day!
Pretty good southern food, fire chicken and fillling food. Yams are great, and service is good

The food is good and the prices are reasonable! Very refreshing! The parking was free and easy on the street. Nice neighborhood around the restaurant. Our server, Aimee, was friendly, informative and accommodating! A real sweetheart! A very nice experience overall! We will definitely be back!

My first time here and I was excited, the atmosphere was on point! The food was ok...Pork chops were overwhelmed by too much thick gravy, the greens and black eyed peas were salty (good flavor tho), Mac n cheese and corn muffins w/cinnamon butter were absolutely delicious! The teas were really good also. Our waitress (can't remember her name) was very attentive and knowledgeable. Parking wasn't too bad at the time we arrived which was around 3:00 on a Saturday.

Best soul food I've had in California and I'm visiting from Sandy Eggo!! Everything from the service to the food the ambiance, everything was on point!! Next time I'm in Sacramento we will definitely be back!! Hope to try South too!!

I found a portal in Northern California Saturday morning. It led us to a  Southern Kitchen that serves biscuits better than my Grandpa. Kudos to the kitchen for bringing Louisiana to me.
Nicki G. and Marlon bring southern hospitality to Sacramento. Great Food with Awesome Friendly Service. Modern look with Southern Charm.

author_location: Valley Hi / North Laguna, Sacramento, CA
text: Food is always good and served hot and fresh! Mac and cheese is bomb and fried chicken is well seasoned!

Well as I have been 3xs now i can confidently write a review and stand by it! 

Atmosphere-
Cool , slightly urban hipster vibe that can easily accommodate everyone to families, grandparents,  Birthday celebrations,  date night,  to girls outings. Parking lot close by, as well as unmetered street parking . 90s r&b normally playing to flashback you to the day.  
Food & Drink- 
Now we all know that Sac was way overdue for an authentic soul food restaurant with delicious food, full bar, with a pleasant atmosphere where all are welcomed.  So far I have had the shrimp & grits , creamy,  rich grits in a delicious gravy with 5 generous sized prawns. The fried cat fish filet is flaky, crispy and not too fishy tasting.  The fried chicken was probably my least favorite but I loved the greens that I ordered as a side. Somebody put their foot into cooking those greens!! The corn bread muffin is so sweet and buttery, no need for dessert! The koolaid is seriously sweet that takes you back to your childhood,  while the adult specialty drinks are good as well. 
Service- 
All 3xs I have been service has been friendly and professional.  Yes it is popular and new so expect a wait, and yes the food times can be a little long,  but worth it as when the food arrives you will be ready to smash it!! 
Highly recommend Fixins!! I will definitely continue to support it and plan to try it for brunch!!

I am sure I am echoing what almost everyone has said about Fixins. I went with my friends on our monthly dinner.  I didn't want to get my hopes too high and I wasn't disappointed.

I had the Fried Chicken with Potato Salad and Mac and Cheese. In my humble estimation this is the best Fried Chicken in Sacramento. My friends all gave me a pat on the back for picking this place.

Great atmosphere and a bit loud, but an exciting and eclectic group, kids, adults a great melting pot in Sacramento.


Not to be out done we were greeted by hizonner, the estimable Kevin Johnson one of the owners.

This is a place not to be missed.

I've been wanting to try this place out for a while and had the opportunity to eat here twice in the last 2 weeks LOL. 

First off, ambience on point. Both times I came here it was on a Sunday. The football games were on and the restaurant was playing gospel music. I really like the feel of the restaurant. It's modern. There's a private room in the back of the restaurant, however, it didn't seem like they were seating people back there. It may have been reserved for a private event. There is seating inside, outside, and at the bar.

We shared an order of fried green tomatoes and deviled eggs. The tomatoes were really good, not super-oily. However, the portion is pretty small and not really worth it for the price tbh. I had the 3 piece fried chicken with the cornbread, charred okra, and mac and cheese. The fried chicken was on point. The meat was moist and the skin had a nice crisp. The charred okra with bacon was definitely my favorite side. I wasn't a huge fan of the mac and cheese. The cornbread was really good because it wasn't dry. The plate comes with a biscuit by default, but you can sub for the cornbread--which I would definitely recommend. 

I tried a little bit of my friend's catfish and it was bomb. It had that nice crispy texture that you would want in fried catfish and it was seasoned well. The second time that I came here, I ordered the oxtail. I'm a huge fan of it! It fell right off of the bone and the gravy/sauce wasn't overwhelming.  

The only thing keeping me from giving this place a 5 is the service. Both times I came here, the service was okay. We had a different server the second time, but for both visits, we had to bother another server to get us refills. 

Overall:
Food: 5/5 
Service: 4/5

A soul kitchen in Sacramento.....yay!  My friends brought me here for an early lunch and we enjoyed it.   I liked the atmosphere.  It's urban and there is a bit of nostalgia that it brings.  I haven't had kool-aid in years!  They have some cool menu items and cocktails.  The servers were nice and the service was prompt.   I had fried chicken, greens, and red beans.  The greens were bomb.   I also had corn bread and there was this delicious strawberry butter that came with it - yum!  

If you're in the area and like a little bit of grease or gravy every now and again, I recommend a trip to Fixins.  

Also, the restrooms were very clean and they had at least 4-5 stalls.  This shouldn't seem like a big deal, but I've been visiting the area for the past 4 days and many places had tiny bathrooms with only 1-2 stalls available, so always having to wait in line.
Fixins never lets us down! Every time we've gone we enjoy the food, the atmosphere, the service and the drinks. It's definitely a family favorite for us.

In town visiting my college student & brought her to Fixins last night. We live in LA & I have enjoyed the Fixins @ LA Live/DTLA several times in past few years. 'This' was not that! SO, SO DISAPPOINTED. My oxtails were overly sweet, had A LOT of fat, very little (very chewy) meat; greens were not tender. My daughter ordered smothered pork chops : heavily seasoned with too much black pepper, gravy was thick & flavorless; mac n cheese was over cooked; she then tried shrimp & grits, but it was 'mashed potatoes with spicy gravy & hard shrimp! She ended up eating only the muffin with butter. 

The server was not great; he was tired, a little rude & clearly not interested in whether we were satisfied.

We will not be back.

Wow!   10 star food!  Our table ordered practically everything on the main course menu.  Everything was delicious!  Fried chicken, chard okra , and the Red beans and rice were my favorite!  But everything else we had was delicious!!! -including the drinks!  Mother Rose martini , lavender lemondrop, and New Orleans Tea did not disappoint!  
Very friendly service!!
Fantastic food and service. Enjoyed in the shade on the patio. The chicken was crispy on the outside and moist on the inside. It paired perfectly with the waffle and syrup

Food was excellent. Table had wings and waffles and catfish. Breading good on both as well as both being moist. Had potato salad, coleslaw and mac and cheese fore sides ... all good. Will be coming back.

Covid-19
Beautiful job doing pickup and and outdoor dining.

Indoor was distanced and extra care was taken for distancing, masks, single use menus and utensils. 

The food portions were generous and the decor was fanciful and imaginative. 

Delicious shrimp and grits and gracious service.

The food here is SO good!! I had been craving soul food for the longest time and I was 100% satisfied. The portions are big so I was full and I had enough leftovers for a light dinner as well :) The service was okay, we had to flag down our waitress multiple times and it took foreverrrr to get our check & pay but they were also pretty busy considering it was a Monday morning (but then again it was July 3rd so i'm sure lots of people were off). The music is pretty loud in the restaurant so don't expect to have deep conversations, just enjoy the food and the music lol.
I tried this place 3x already. All I have to say is their chicken & waffle is way better than Roscoe Chicken & Waffles. The fried chicken is very juicy. The adult Kool-Aid drinks are good.
As always food was terrific. We got our order to go but the restaurant looks amazing inside and out. Servers were super efficient and friendly.  Highly recommend!

I went to this with my girlfriend's family in Sacramento. And I must say Kevin Johnson really has something great with this place! I got the shrimp and grits and it was so flavorful and filling! My girlfriend got the catfish dinner and her mom got the ox tails. Both of them had rave reviews for their dishes. The drink menu is also very extensive and has some great options. The combination of the amount of food you get and great flavor definitely makes this one of my favorite soul food restaurants.

We just finished dining at this restaurant for the first time. I am going to give direct comments about our experience 
*hostess extremely helpful and nice
* bartender extremely nice and  helpful 
* service slow sparse , she was nice but was not attentive enough . Took a long time to be checked up on saw all of three times the whole time. One of which was when we were paying . Again nice but service was NOT attentive enough . 
* Food- oxtails delicious! Fried chicken delicious! Biscuits so good! Cornbread so good! Grits best I have ever had. Red beans and rice best I have ever had.

I've been trying to come here for months and was very excited about it but the results were just ok. The collard greens might be some of the best I've ever had, and the Mac cheese was traditional southern style (also very good). However, the fried chicken wasn't anything amazing. I enjoyed the meal but maybe I over hyped the chicken in my head too much. The worst was after the meal 30minutes later myself and two friends sprinted to that bathroom because of dumping syndrome.  If you don't know what that is, it's exactly as it sounds....

I finally made my way into Fixins Soul Kitchen.  Coming from my own kitchen frying my own family style southern comfort- I found Fixins to feel like home. We ordered take out. The aroma to the first bite into that very juicy tender crispy chicken not to mention the Mac n cheesiness to the charred okra and the tasty collard greens which had that smoky flavor -amazing. The fried green tomatoes were truly authentic although lacking the sauce. The grits were tasty. And lastly the biscuits came through! I would like to say there were slight hiccup in the to go order but this establishment greatly made up for that. And that's what you call customer oriented service and the kind of service that keeps an establishment moving. I found the staff and atmosphere to be positive and friendly but professional as well. They show they care about the experience. I would recommend dining in.
The chicken is always perfect and amazing with a waffle! Make sure to get a reservation otherwise wait can be long.

I've been coming here for years. And today didn't disappoint. My friend and I had excellent food. Our server was Chandra and she's the bomb. 
We had a coffee cocktail to accompany our huge lunch. 
After we decided to go to the bar & ordered the same coffee cocktail. It was made by a different person who lessened the pours of the two liquors and filled with coffee. We saw him and commented on it. Luc was rude and decided to argue with us. He may been in a bad mood but lose the attitude when you come to work Luc. 
I didn't drink mine (after tasting) and gave it back. 
Again the food was excellent as was our food server.

Came here once during pandemic times and got an order to go which was pretty good - so the next time I came up I had to drop in...

The thing I really appreciate about this place is the general approach to flavor - whether or not the dishes are to your liking, everyone can agree that the flavors are always going for it - whether it's the potato salad with the punchy tartness, or the shrimp and grits with it's peppery flavor, or a Mac and cheese that puts you to asleep with it's rich goodness.

Took us 20 minutes on a busy Saturday to get seated - if this place were in sf, I guarantee you'd be waiting over an hour.

First time here on a date brunch and it was great.   If you haven't been to this area of town you've been missing out.   I think this place is rather new and I honestly didn't hear about it until I looked up a brunch spot to go to with my girlfriend.  It's not exactly like how we have food in the south where I've been for the past seven years (North Carolina) but the food was delicious.  I ordered the oxtail and it was done very well.  She ordered the fried chicken with a side of candies yams and charred okra.  Our beverages included a Bloody Mary for me and mimosa for her.  Overall I'd say the ambience and vibe of the place was wonderful for a little Sunday brunch date.  
Our waitress was pretty good.  Water was always filled.  Our spirit animals are canes.  

I'd come here again and next time, with some of our friends.
Great food, it's seasoned well and presented beautifully. Great service. Large portions. I will definitely be returning to Fixins.

I applaud Kevin Johnson for making big strides in realizing his vision for a revitalized Oak Park, and putting a soul food restaurant in the heart of it.  The interior is clean and modern, so well put together, and the patio is nice and spacious.  I love that Kevin is usually there going table to table to check on everyone.  "Fixins" has been a long time coming, not only to this area of town, but to all Sacramentans craving authentic and familiar comfort food.  From a business standpoint, Fixins has exceeded all of its initial and projected expectations in the short time that they have been open (per Mr. Johnson, himself).  That shows that this community is more than willing and excited to support local and black-owned businesses.  My hope is that any short-comings in food or service will be remedied during this grand opening period and Fixins will continue to exceed expectations and become a successful model to present and future businesses in Oak Park.  

Now...onto the food!  First time we came for breakfast, and I had the 2 piece chicken and waffle and my man ordered the "blue plate breakfast" (chose fried chicken wings, grits, and a side of eggs).  We also had the fried deviled eggs that were like flavor bombs going off in my mouth (definitely order these!).  I think the chicken needed more seasoning but was nicely fried and not greasy.  Their house hot sauce (not really spicy) was delicious and complemented the chicken (I keep forgetting to ask if they sell the hot sauce).  Waffle was thin but dense and had great flavor, especially with the homemade butter and warm syrup on top.  Do not expect a fluffy pale Belgian waffle!  My bf loved all the food that came with his meal.  For dinner we had the fried catfish, greens, black eyed peas, mac n cheese, and corn fritters.  The catfish was breaded with cornmeal and seasoned right, but we both prefer the fish to be fried a little harder.  So far, all the sides I've tried were very tasty.  My favorites were the mac n cheese and the greens.

Recently, Fixins hosted the reception for their resident artist, Milton Bowens' art talk.  His amazing and creative work is on the walls in the restaurant and in the Guild Theater next door.  Aside from the passed appetizers, we decided to dine in, so we could try more on the menu (I have a personal goal to try everything!).  We got the catfish nuggets which were fried harder than the filet, but I was disappointed in the portion for the price point ($15).  Ours did look like they skimped a bit compared to other reviewers' pictures...maybe because it was a busy night with the reception going on? I feel that an appetizer should not cost the same as an entrée, especially if I would've gotten more food with an entree instead.
