Both of us ordered the "better burger," a juicy pimento cheese-stuffed beef patty with bacon, lettuce, onion, tomato, and some type of sauce,  that required a ton of napkins.  It was a good burger, but the pimento cheese didn't really elevate the flavor like I thought it would.  Fries were thin and crispy, and served hot.

Once they work out all the kinks in service and consistency in the kitchen, I know Fixins will become the go-to for soul food and a staple in Oak Park.  And I'll be right there to support them, as I work my way through that menu!

Here's how my visit went: it was a casual Tuesday and it wasn't busy around the time that my friend and I dined in so service was quick and attentive. I ordered the gumbo with shrimp and my friend ordered the plate with 2 piece friend chicken with cheesy mac and black eyed peas. We also ordered the adult kool aid to start off which was great, I recommend but it's pricey for $12. When the gumbo arrived, it was lukewarm which threw it off because I believed that if it was piping hot it would've tasted great but the veggies seemed to be over cooked especially the okra which okay. I had assumed that this was just reheated repeatedly so it had gotten soft over time but there wasn't much to be impressed by - the sausage was alright and there was a corn muffin right in the middle of my rice as for looks. The rice wasn't good in my opinion because of what I had expected from a soul restaurant, it tasted like instant rice that I would eat in school. The quality of the gumbo was a 5.5/10 and I don't think that they're known for their gumbo because everyone else around me ordered fried chicken but I wanted to see if this was the place for gumbo if I ever had the craving yet it didn't hit the spot. My friend said that the chicken was very dry and wasn't crispy, it tasted like it had been sitting out for a good minute and his sides weren't good either however we didn't want to make a bad rep for a place that was new to us so we just stuck with what we ordered.

Solid 4/5. Came here for a little family date. Immediately greeted and sat down quickly. The service and the staff were genuinely nice 10/10. The ambiance was ok that day. We were seated next to the kitchen door and for me I just don't like that too much 7/10. We ordered their oxtails with two sides (Mac,yams) amazing, the shrimp and grits were phenomenal. The kids ordered their fried chicken sandwich which was ok. The portion maybe was just a little overboard. The other ordered their fried catfish and he enjoyed it. The fries were very delicious. The kool-aid was fire and very nostalgic. We also ordered two of their adult drinks which were ok 7/10. All in all this is a great spot for date nights or lunch. Would recommend coming here, but idk if I would come back after 2 times. I respect the staff very much and blessings to y'all
Service was GREAT, food was WONDERFUL..I appreciated everything I experienced there & we will be back. Thank you because a good soul food place is definitely hard to find, you won't be disappointed.

This place is the business !  Well thought out menu, reasonably priced, experienced staff and authentically comfortable ambience. 

Each of our party of six was especially pleased with our choice of entree, sharing things with each other so we could taste as many different things as we could. We will definitely be back!

Maia is the best!
Fantastic food and atmosphere. It's now our new place for family gatherings such as birthday parties graduation events etc . The is comforting and authentic and the staff is amazing.

author_location: Central San Jose, San Jose, CA
text: First time coming here and it was delicious !!!! The service, the vibes, the food, everything was awesome. I definitely would come here again with my friends and order the same thing! Oh! Also the butter, goddamn, that was the best butter I have had!!!

Upon arrival we were told our wait would be about 45 minutes to an hour. In reality our wait was only 30 minutes, but it was well worth it. Come follow me on this journey...

For starters we ordered the fried green tomato's, they were simply delicious! Great menu choices but I settled for the chicken wings & waffles w/ a side of mac'n'chesse and collard greens. My husband ordered the chicken dinner which comes with two sides and a biscuit. For his sides he chose the mac'n'chesse and candied yams. Here is our take on the food: 

Chicken - BOMB! 
Waffle - BOMB! 
Mac'n'cheese - very good 
Collard Greens - needs some work, I personally thought they were a little bitter. 
Candied yams - very good!
Biscuit - very good! 

To wash down all the goodness we went with the red kool-aid. Man oh man, the kool-aid was so good, it brought back childhood memories (I can't tell you the last time I had some kool-aid). Next time I'm going with the purple kool-aid. 

Very nice establishment in the heart of Oak Park. Food is reasonably priced and the customer service was top-notch, very friendly and personable! Kevin Johnson stopped by our table several times to check-in on us. No doubt we will return, most likely sooner than later

Service always wonderful food is always giving flashbacks of home cooking, I've tried oxtails, catfish, smothered chicken and I'll be back again the kool aid is busssssssin any color but my favorite is pink

The chicken was juicy, even the chicken breast which typically would be dry and most locations, but they do a proprietary Brime  for 24 hours before they fry it, the some other pork chops were seasoned perfectly with a little kick to it. The mac and cheese is always a nice surprise. Not too aggressive on the cheese. Not too subtle on the flavor that cheesy grits were slightly disappointing. They were a bit dry with a cold clump of cheese on top of it. It would have been nice if It was melted into the dish. The gumbo was well seasoned, nice and thick and flavorful and the rice wasn't overcooked

The food is out of this world. I got the oxtails that were everything!! The fish sandwich was good too and the fried chicken wasn't bad. Parking is a little challenging but they provide a lovely patio.

Great food, service and atmosphere. However avoid peak time or holidays. The service is slow and quality of the food slips a notch. Small chicken wings too, so chose wisely if you want to take leftovers home. Overall the place still gets 4 stars. Bigger wings and better service during peak times would get a 5 star rating in my review.

First timer 
 
Food selection; Fried catfish cream corn collard  greens yams smothered chicken hush  puppies cocktail:  lavender lemon drop

 Review: so I've had soul food alota places and being honest this was not up to par no flavor , no seasoning  The catfish was slimy the greens were bland the cream corn wasn't bad Had  a little spice to it; the chicken wings  are huge and had a weird taste the greens were bland had lots of stems no flavor the lavender cocktail was pretty decent 

 Service : are server was nice asked us how everything was the ambience is nice clean and modern the outside patio and bar is nice 

Conclusion: not sure t this  place is community driven ; maybe for some it may be ok but not for me it was for the experience and to support and try it out but I'm sorry to say not worth the money at all it just wasn't it! This was the worst version of SoulFood ever! 

Disclaimer: my reviews are solely based on my opinion and experience but u should try things out for yourself!

Thank goodness for Yelp waitlist because this place is super popular already! Luckily we didn't need to use the waitlist option since we came Sunday morning around 9:30, so we beat the brunch rush and post-Sunday church crowd. The restaurant is pretty big though so there is plenty of seating! It's a lot bigger inside than it looks from the outside. 

The menu isn't a novel but they do have everything you would expect from a soul food restaurant, which is more important! I already had trouble decided what I wanted to drink, before I could even take a look at the food! I was stuck between sweet tea and kool aid, but I just had to get the kool aid, because where else can you order that with your meal?! They are totally making a huge margin off of the kool aid, which is genius on their part. But in return, you get a huge mason jar full of that sweet sugary goodness. 

Okay, on to the food. We started off with their deep fried deviled eggs. Have you ever eaten such a thing?! It was a game changer. The crunchy texture on the outside was so tasty with the creamy egg filling. Just needed a few extra drips of their house hot sauce and we were in heaven. 

Fried chicken, sides, gravy... I wanted EVERYTHING! But I think I got the best of all worlds, by ordering the 2 piece fried chicken plate, which comes with two sides and a biscuit. I really wanted to have their biscuits and gravy so I asked if I could add gravy on the side and they didn't hesitate one bit! For my sides I chose the collard greens and macaroni and cheese. Every single item was so delicious! We were taking a bite of everything and every single time we were just raving after each bite! The chicken itself was tender and moist (even when I took the breast home for leftovers, it was still moist!). The biscuit was tasty, and even better with the gravy which had bits of chicken inside! It even comes with a scoop of their purple butter, yes purple!! It tasted like blueberry. It was such a struggle trying to decide if I wanted to take my next bite of biscuit with the butter or the gravy. The macaroni and cheese was perfectly cheesy and not too dry or too saucy. The collard greens were the perfect addition to cut the heavy flavors of the cheese and the gravy, and had a nice hint of vinegar. And everything was equally delicious when I ate my leftovers at home. I also had a bite of the catfish and the creamed corn. The catfish portion was very generous and it was nicely cooked. The creamed corn was a dream and it was a great pair with the crispy catfish! 

There are a half dozen other things I want to try. Not to mention those amazing desserts! I'm tempted to come back this weekend already! We plan on taking people here whenever we need to suggest a place for breakfast, lunch or dinner because more people need to know about this place!!
Great Place service and food is spectacular Smothered Pork Chops  and Black Eyed are both delicious items I will definitely visit again

The first time I went to soul kitchen I didn't like it 

This time it was fabulous! I ordered the fried chicken salad! Delicious and the cocktails were divine

The food has gone completely downhill since the first opened. I wouldn't call this soul food and there is absolutely no love or umph to it at all smh very bland and subpar. I beg of you to hire some better cooks that are more knowledgeable about flavor profiles and soul food , this was not it
Great food for the Sac area. Nice decor. Busy during lunch - so be patient if the servers are occupied.

Amazing soul food. Love went into everything. My first time at a soul food restaurant feeling like this taste like my grandmama made this!

Fried chicken, oxtails, collard greens, Mac and cheese were all spectacular!

Nice ambiance mixed with typical soul food slow service. I got the fish, which was delicious. It was crispy and moist. My sides were mac and cheese and greens. The mac was good, not great. The greens were a little sweet for my taste.   For dessert I got the banana pudding, which was straight trash. My sister got the peach cobbler, which was good.

We were in town for the weekend for a concert, had them doordashed to the hotel while we were getting ready.  The food was very hot and super fresh, the chicken was crunchy, juicy and flavorful, the collard greens were sweet and sour and salty and spicy perfection.... and the grits were amazing...(btw my bf may have danced around the room while eating the candied yams)
Now, the fried green tomatoes... let's just say they were an absolute spiritual experience!!! We will definitely be back!

Good food all around. 
Service was good
Liked aesthetic of restaurant 
Over all would definitely come again

lately theres hasnt been any new restaurants in sacramento that gotten me "omg i gotta go back!"

finally i think fixins well fixed that. (ha).

-oxtails plate was so tender and the greens i gotten with was delicious. 

-fried chicken plate (light meat) was crispy and juicy. it definetly taste like its been seasoned overnight.  mac nad cheese was so good as well.

Dined at Fixins with 20 friends. About 12 more of our friends ended up at the restaurant as well, but the private
room accommodate a max of 25 which is still nice. As soon as we arrived, there were a number of people waiting outside to be seated. We were seated about 80 minutes later, due to our large party size and the restaurant was full when we arrived. I saw former Sacramento mayor Kevin Johnson greeting patrons throughout the restaurant. When we were seated, our party excited ordered rounds of kool-aid, sweet tea, and Arnold Palmer's. I don't think anyone had enjoyed kool-aid in the past 30 years so we all got a kick out of enjoying a childhood favorite. Starving after our long wait, the tables ordered fried green tomatoes and fried deviled eggs. The tomatoes were delicious albeit small portion to be a shareable app. The deviled eggs were a hit with everyone (to the point they ordered more). The only complaint was the amount: two eggs. On to dinner: chicken and waffles, fried catfish, smothered chicken, shrimp and grits, yams, collard greens, mac n cheese, potato salad, red beans and rice, and corn fritters. The smothered chicken was salty, and had too much onion powder or onion salt seasoning. The only flavors I tasted were onion and salt. The chickens was well, not dry, and nice crispiness under the gravy. Today was the first time I'd ever had smothered chicken that wasn't served with rice. The mac n cheese was righteous, several people in our party ordered extra mac. The red beans had instant/par cooked rice which isn't traditional red beans n rice. The sweet potatoes were not candied and just barely sweet. The entire party agreed (everyone didn't order sweet potatoes but we all taste tested when two people said they weren't sweet), I also found the sweet potatoes undercooked, the texture was too firm so I don't recommend ordering them (or the smothered chicken). One person enjoyed the catfish, another said it was not seasoned. Many people enjoyed the buttermilk pie. The red velvet cake slice is huge, like share with 3 people huge. The peach cobbler is the best I have had in my whole life, period. Fixins doesn't sell ice cream which is a bummer, cobbler without ice cream is like peanut butter without jelly! Our wait staff, Erin, and I cannot remember the sweet young lady with the blue dreads (Esa?), were amazing and took very good care of us. One person ordered oxtails and it's a dish that I'm very particular about but she enjoyed them and said they were cooked well. Oh! Almost forgot to shout out the "yellow" potato salad! Yes y'all! I'm talking backyard BBQ, annual church picnic potato salad, honey. Right amount of mustard, texture from the onions and relish, the potato salad was on point all the way. We will be back to try the oxtails.

Came here for my birthday with a friend for the first time the other day. The grits were so creamy and flavorful!!!! I ordered the smothered chicken with the gravy on the side--so not really smothered. The fried chicken had so much flavor on its own!! The gravy was little salty however, so I am glad that I got it on the side, it may have been too much salt with the seasoned chicken. The creamed corn was AMAZING!! I will be coming back to try the rest of the menu for sure!!!! The fried catfish was perfectly cooked & delicious. Wish it came with a dipping sauce however.

Friendly atmosphere, prompt and courteous service. A little long in food being served after ordering. About 30 minutes? Food was salty, black eyed peas alittle undercooked, and the oxtails could have been cooked alittle longer also!? Overall taste of the food was good, just that salt amounts a bit too much

Add my 5-stars to the constant stream of praise this much-anticipated, much-needed Oak Park soul food spot has received. I came in two weeks ago, before they were even officially open, and they were already firing on all cylinders. It's amazing what a combination of great food, service and atmosphere can bring when done right! 

The menu was simple and straightforward - a refreshing change from the Bible-sized menus I get at other places these days. I went with the fried catfish along with a side of collared greens and baked beans. The fish was crisp but light and fluffy with plenty of flavor, the greens and beans cooked to a savory perfection. It came with corn fritters which I'm not a big fan of, so I passed those on to my little guy. 

The place was busy as busy can be on a Wednesday night and I fully expected a long wait or some sort of service hiccups. Nope. Our sweet server got our orders right and got them out fast. We even joined in with the staff as they rallied the whole restaurant behind singing "Happy Birthday" to a woman at a neighboring table. 

I applaud them for not only opening up in Oak Park, where businesses like this are sorely needed, but being so on top of their game from moment one. You can bet that I'll be back - with friends and family - to try out their other offerings.
Loveddd. The fish was seasoned well but didn't like how the bread fell from the fish. The corn fritters were so sweet and soft. The Mac and cheese taste like my grandma made it. 10/10

Great food and drinks! A really nice place to sit back and enjoy the environment, staff, and food! Went back twice for the food.  Loved the oxtail!
Look forward to the next meal there!

First time at Fixins for a birthday. It was grad week so wait times for a table were an hour so we sat at the bar. Q served us and was amazing, she was making drinks left and right for all the large parties while still giving us attentive service. Food was delicious, had the fried chicken and fried green tomatoes, everything was excellent. Highly recommend-- even when they're slammed you still get great service!
Food is always on point. Staff is always friendly and accommodating. Outdoor patio is nice when it's not 100+ degrees.
Great atmosphere and service! Chicken is tasty and delicious! Will definitely be back for more!
Lord bless the cooks heart everything was so wonderful to good to get a picture sorry guys
Fried chicken crispy, moist and flavorful. Oxtail dinner is also one of my fav. Very tender and flavorful.
Great food & service!!! Get oxtails!! Yum isn't even the word! They even have koolaid

Located in Oak Park with street parking
Indoor and Outdoor Seating
Full bar and TVs
Booths and tables

I thought they had a secret vegan menu but when I asked I was told they had one vegan item-- Blackened Tofu. So, that's what I had. The tofu was good, the succotash was oily on a bed of rice. The hot sauce on the table is a Fixin's Hot Sauce and I assume they refill the bottles because the one I had was filled to the very top. When it's filled to the very top, it's hard to get out of the hole on top unless you pop that plastic piece off. I'm all for refilling the bottle, it's less waste but give it some room to move.. 

There was a lot of staff and I had good service. The restaurant isn't too big or too small. It's a good space.

I love Fixins!
The staff is so welcoming, helpful, & friendly.  I've always observed the restaurant to be clean and in excellent condition. And the food? BOMB.
I tend to go for the catfish or dark meat chicken.  The macaroni & cheese is a must, as are the biscuits and strawberry butter.  Be sure to try the sweet potato pie.
I always dilute the lemonade with water--it's just too sweet for my liking, but I'm sure others enjoy it.  Just a personal preference.
Overall, this is one of my favorite spots in Sac.

Can we talk about how this restaurant can do no wrong in my eyes?! My boyfriend and I stumbled upon it by accident-which I now know was no accident but a kind act from God herself!

Our server (I forgot his name, and we didn't request a receipt-Friday night, 9/25 around 9ish, young Black man with locs) deserves high praise from management as he was absolutely wonderful. Friendly, professional, and knowledgeable about the menu and excellent customer service.

The food: fantastic! We ordered the oxtails (first restaurant EVER that wasn't sold out, lol!) and shrimp and grits, Mac and cheese, greens, and candied yams. I was in soul food heaven. I'm positive my grandmother who passed decades ago, was happy to see me eating so well (fresh carrot/beet/ginger juice is healthy, but doesn't stick to my ribs like this meal!) I have no doubt that everything on the menu is delicious. Go. Today if you can. 

Soul food this good is hard to come by outside of a Black woman's kitchen filled with love and B.B King (or gospel, depending on the day) loudly playing in the background and kids running in and out through the screen door. 

I'll definitely return again and tell friends.

The server was amazing, quick, and so sweet. The inside is good for groups or bar seating and the outdoor patio was very great. The food was so good compared to so many similar soul food restaurants. Okra is a must! The chicken and gravy was perfectly smothered, not too much and not too little. The chicken was juicy and perfectly crunchy. Mac and cheese was not overpowering and had the perfect texture and moisture. Sweet tea is spot on. Lots of flavor without tasting like you're drinking sugar. Candied yams were delicious. Can't go wrong with anything on the menu. Music was fire  Looking forward to coming back when I visit Sac again, soon

Our second time visiting Fixins and once again it met all expectations.  Food, staff, cleanliness, ambiance get 5 out of 5.  Great place to eat and have a few cocktails! Thank you JC 2.0 for the great service!!

The food was delicious and the restaurant was clean and nice. Our server wasn't my favorite. We had to track him down to order our food after waiting a long time, he tried to get us to order more appetizers than we needed, and without us being aware, convinced my son that he was a growing boy and the kids meal wouldn't be enough for him and he should order off the adult menu. Spoiler alert! The kids meal would have been plenty and he hardly ate any of his food. We were a larger group and he was sitting on the other side of the table so I didn't notice. He also tried to convince my husband he needed something besides water to drink. I'm sure he saw the 20% automatic gratuity and wanted to get as much as he could. Anyways, I'd still recommend going if you're in the area as the food really was wonderful, just didn't appreciate the pushy salesman vibe our server gave.

I was recommended this restaurant to try, and it had great reviews, however, I was disappointed.

First off , the good dishes were the Fried Catfish, Mac and Cheese, and Rice and Beans. However, my main entree, Smothered Fried Chicken, came out cold! My chicken and gravy were both cold. When I asked the waitress if I could get a new plate of chicken since it was cold, her response was "you were outside so long it got cold". I was outside less than 10 seconds after my friend texted our food was ready. 

When I received my food again, no more than 1 minute later, my sides were unbelievably hot, like burn your mouth hot and my chicken gravy was incredibly salty. It's like they stuck it all in the microwave. I do not recommend this dish.

Lastly, my friend and I ordered 2 Old Fashioned drinks, which were a sitting at the bar for 5 minutes completed, without our server  noticing when we were sitting right next to it. And when my  other friend ordered one as well a few minutes later, the bartender just added whatever was left in the shaker and that was it. Also, get clarification what is their house alcohol they use, because they will try and tell you one thing and up charge you, then say, "no I didn't say that."

I spent $50 and to me, just not worth it.

I walked into grandma's kitchen and ate authentic soul food. The entire team was professional, but I didn't feel like a customer; I felt more like family. Comfortable upscale, and wonderful place to eat, drink, laugh, and enjoy life.
First time at Fixins! The food is delicious and the servings are large. Our waiter Willey was so friendly, polite and professional.

Came here on a Monday night after missing my chance to try it on their grand opening. I'm so glad I decided to try again because the food was amazing. We ordered the 2 piece fried chicken with Mac n cheese, fries, candies yams, and corn bread muffin. Everything we ate was delicious. Chicken was perfectly fried. Mac n cheese wasn't chalky. Cornbread muffin was moist and crumbled perfectly. Candied yam tasted like thanksgiving. Nothing disappointed. Their mixed drinks were also great. I got the tea and bourbon one. Highly recommend. The bourbon doesn't over power the tea and it's a perfect mix. Really wanted the try the blueberry fizz with the kool aid. Can't wait to be back to try more of their menu.

Went to Fixins for the first time today as a family 4th of July celebration. The food, ambience and acoustics were all great. What really stood out was the superlative service. Kronk stopped to talk with me and my family multiple times. He had a great sense of humor. We never felt rushed. Truly a fun, community atmosphere. We'll definitely be back .

The service was amazing whatever we needed at the table they make sure that we got it kept my table clean beautiful personalities
The food so delicious I had the chicken with the roux gravy, collard greens, yams the bomb.com
The advertisers deviled eggs and fried green tomatoes very good
I love the way they have the seating inside and out It just feels like a family restaurant

We finally have a bomb soul food spot in Sac! I got to try the smothered chicken, gumbo, and fried chicken. Red beans and rice, Mac and cheese, collards greens, and biscuits. EVERYTHING WAS DELICIOUS! I also tried their Up in Smoke beverage. 

Nate was our server and we was super nice and attentive.
I can't wait to go back!

Had been following them on Instagram for quite a while, and finally checked them out. Good ambience and service but the food was just OK.  We had a party of 5 & the sentiment was similar between all of us.

This was my first time eating at this establishment and for my first time I have to say I was not disappointed.  I ordered the smothered prock chops with mac-n-cheese and the greens this food reminds me of my  mom's cooking. I will be returning soon
Delicious!! I would recommend to anybody who loves chicken. The mac and cheese is a must aswell.
The food is delicious! I live the service! The ambiance is incredible. You can sit inside or outside for the same experience!

The food and service were brilliantly done. Definitely a foodie version of Southern Soul food. The Kool-aid pickle was the winner for all of us. The best fried food Eva! Light and not greasy. Cocktails are creative and delicious. (Pricey... Worth it!) I look forward to going back. #BlackdomRenaissance

Fried chicken and sweet potato pie was ok,   but I think definitely  over priced.
Appreciated ambiance. Service just ok as well.

So this was our second time here and was just as good as our first time a few months ago. I ordered the chicken and waffles and my wife and son ordered the catfish plate with Mac and cheese and collard greens. Both plates were really good and had lots of flavor. I've been to Roscoes Chicken and waffles in L. A a few times and I'm my opinion the chicken and waffles from here are better.. Definitely recommend this place if your looking for a home cooked type southern meal. Oh yea the kool aid must try also along with the corn bread that is really good and not dry like other places I've tried.

My fiancé has eaten here several time while on lunch break. He raves about it. And FINALLY we were able to come together. Wow! The BEST chicken ever! I had the chicken and waffles (order this!) and my fiancé had the two piece chicken with collard greens and red beans and rice. If I could eat the collard greens every day I would! This place was busy but worth it. So fun and the service was awesome! Such a nice place with good drinks as well.

I ordered the smothered chicken from here a few times and I highly recommend it. Sometimes it was on the saltier side, but it was still good. I also recommend the gumbo. Their sides are a must to order with your meal.

We went for breakfast. The 4 stars was for the food. We both had the blue plate eggs. I had mine with house potatoes and hot links and Mr B the grits with bacon. I ordered coffee and he ordered a Mimosa. 

The Good: eggs were cooked good, house potatoes were excellent with a great seasoning. The hot links too were great. Some places overlook them and they are dry but these were great! The BACON was PERFECT! I don't know how so many breakfast places mess up bacon but these were perfect. 

The hostess and wait staff were super nice. The ambiance is busy and loud but fun. The decor is really awesome. 

Great neighborhood being revitalized. Parking was busy but not a long walk. 

The Ok: coffee was good. When the server offered hazelnut I thought it was the coffee but it was the creamer packs. 

The could be better: it appears that the waiter / waitress is also responsible for taking drink orders and bringing them. It took a long time for my coffee to arrive (they were busy) and Mr B's Mimosa came just as he was finished with his food. We asked several times about the Mimosa but it looked like every time the bar tender did it someone else thought it was there order. Note: when we ordered a second one it came within 6 minutes. The first one took about 45 mins. 

The grits, this is hard as the food was good but it appeared they sat a bit before coming to our table. 
So they were a bit cool. Grits are so yummy when hot but they were just not that great. I suspect it was the temperature. 

We will definitely go back maybe during weekday and see if the service is a bit faster.  We'd also like to try the lunch/dinner options.
Aimee was our server. She is very friendly and attentive. Love their shrimp and grits.
All I have to say is that this is probably the best Chicken and Waffles I have ever had. The waffle just melts in your mouth and chicken has so much flavor. Can't wait to go back.
Super friendly staff, welcoming managers, and solid fried chicken sandwiches. Also good value for the price!

SUPER AMAZING GREAT PLACE! I absolutely enjoyed trying food and drinks here. It has an amazing taste of different spices and good vibes. Staff is very friendly and very helpful. They pay close and special attention to each group of people/party dining. My friends and I got the artichoke dip to start which came with pita chips and slices of toasted bread, which was BOMB! I tried the chicken n' waffles and it was SO DELICIOUS! Instead of butter, it was like strawberry butter that balanced all of the flavors of the plate. In addition, I got a side of Mac n' cheese and it came in a cute little red pot-careful though the place is very heavy, so allow your server to place it on the table- and a side of fries which had a rich taste of spices. 

The drinks, the kool-aid was very sweet, but I personally love that! I got the red one that took me back to to my upbringing. 

Definitely will go back with my family because it's such a cute spot in Oak Park and it is a soulful place to be!

The grape koolaide was perfectly a sweet, served in a large mason jar... I definitely didn't need a refill. The cat fish was incredibly crispy out the outside and super moist on the inside. Seasoned to perfection! I was surprised at the large portions. Absolutely loved the food!

Amazing food and amazing service! Chandra was attentive, funny , informative, and helpful. We had the fried chicken since it was our first time here and it came highly recommended. The coating was crispy and flavorful and the chicken itself was juicy tender and cooked to perfection. The sides were plentiful and delicious. This was the best Mac and cheese and coleslaw I have ever had besides my homemade. Their red beans and rice were delicious and not too smoky as we loved the subtle spices. Their biscuits and cornbread were so fluffy and the flavored butters were delectable. Besides the food the staff and servers were very friendly and helpful and attentive. They really need to clone Auntie Pearl for all the other locations. She's lovely!! She made us feel so welcome. We're definitely coming back!

First visit here. The vibes and ambiance were amazing. Absolutely great customer service. 
The catfish was perfection as was the Mac and cheese. Unfortunately, greens were not that great. This is because they had a sweet taste to them. I was a little disappointed with the greens. Hopefully they'll change up the recipe just to get rid of the sweetness. 
I will be returning though for sure
Ok, the catfish is pretty good, but most of the other menu items are extremely salty and not very special. I cannot recommend and sadly would not return.
Blue Kool aid was too sweet. Oxtails we're great. We had fired green tomatoes, fried deviled eggs and I had some of my daughters chicken strips. Everything was good. Great service!

Food was better than decent, but you leave broke and still hungry... the complete opposite of how I was raised around soul food. 

If you're craving it, and you don't make it at home already, then I won't steer you away. 

For the price vs quality/quantity it's just ok. Especially in comparison to other local options.

Came here for the first time and really enjoyed the atmosphere the second we came in and was seated. It's a mixture of different ethic backgrounds  soul music playing while you sit and enjoy the delicious food. 
  
My order consisted of 2 piece chicken which was juicy on the first bite a side of Mac n cheese which tasted very homemade delish n cheesy along with red beans n rice which I loved with there own hot sauce LAST BUT NOT LEAST THE DAAAAANNNG KOOL AID AND BANANA PUDDING I HAD THE BLUE KOOL AID AND IT WAS JUST TOUCHING ALL MY INSIDES LOL

Well let me tell you what. This place is the real deal. Maybe the best fried chicken I've ever had ( including Willie Maes Scotch house in New Orleans). 
The creamed corn was  sweet and spicy. Neat twist. Black eyed peas were perfect. 
My wife had the fried catfish, red beans and rice and  collard greens. All perfect. 
Neat casual atmosphere. I'll let you know on the buttermilk pie. Had to take it to go as i was full. 

Our new go to.

Jan 7th was my second time visiting Fixins! Had dinner with out of towner friends! There's always a vibe as soon as you walk in. Friendly and welcoming staff at the door. Our waitress MadMoney Aaliyah was DOPE and of course the food was on point! Had the 2 piece fried chicken with Mac & cheese and collard greens #slapyour*****good #delectable
Only thing that needs a slight improvement is how long it takes to get drinks. My company and I got there first and waited for my sister and her husband to arrive! It took a long while for us to even get water. Once they  arrived we ordered food and drinks from the bar. We assumed our drinks would be served first as we waited for the food. Minutes went by and our food arrived first before the drinks . Later we were hesitant to order a second round as we didn't know how long it would take. A second recommendation is to keep a pitcher of ice water at the table. It took several times of flagging down different waiters to get one glass of water!
Otherwise Fixins is the place where I get my FIX of soul food! Looking forward to visiting again soon

One of my favorite places to take friends when they come to visit from out of town. Do not skip out on the sides - I tried a few and highly recommend the collard greens, charred okra, and the grits! Some other dishes I'd recommend are of course the fried chicken, waffles (some of the best waffles I've ever had), and the hot chicken sandwich. And last but not least, get the peach cobbler, trust. You can easily make reservations online. Do not miss out!

This place is amazing!!  The staff here are very friendly and nice.  The day we decided to go, Kevin Johnson was there and he took the time to meet and greet every table.  The staff there had the same attitude...friendly took the time to answer any questions you had.  Every time they would walk by and check to see how you were doing, if you needed anything else...and always did it with a friendly smile and positive attitude.

The food here was awesome!  We ordered the catfish and the oxtail...so yummy!!  they both had so much flavor...the gravy over rice was awesome on the oxtail dish.  the sides that came with the food were amazing!!  the grits with cheese...bomb!!  couldnt really tell what kind of cheese was with the grits...but it tasted good!!  The okra was probably my favorite side dish...the mac n cheese is usually my go to at any restaurant that serves mac n cheese...but honestly...i think i might like the okra more here!!  i'm a little surprised i said that...but the okra was that tasty!

The drinks i had were nice and strong...i ordered the Bourbon sweet tea...that was good!!

dessert i had the red velvet cake...it was good!!  i glanced at the table next to me and saw they ordered banana pudding...so i might try that next time...EVERYTHING LOOKED SO GOOD!! LOL

Honestly, i don't think you can go wrong with any of the dishes here...i will come back to try the other dishes.  i saw a lot of the people there order the chicken and waffle...so i think i will try that next time.
The food was marvelous. The menu had a variety of options. The waitress and customer service were stellar, and the decor reminded me of good times. I highly recommend this place

bomb.com in & out from the food,vibe,as well as the cleanliness literally, me and younger daughter went,whom told me bout the ""FIXINGS" and everything from the food as well as the people and the "GENTLEMAN"who greeted us he was very professional, and i plan to go back asap!!!!!!btw reasonable prices  as well i know i'm from oakland but i was shocked i had never even heard of it and actually was mad at my daughter's for never telling me about the place.

Came here for brunch Sunday and must come back to try everything else ASAP. We had the 2 piece chicken & waffle (when in Rome) and the French toast with a side of biscuits and a coffee. Each was the perfect balance of sweet, spicy, and delicious.

DO NOT MISS ordering the side of biscuits.

Love the vibe of this place.  We were there on a rainy Sunday morning and it's so homey and welcoming.  Menu has something for everyone and service was great.  Everyone in our party enjoyed their selection.  I'll be back.

So I am updating my review because of the amazing amazing customer service. I left my new tumbler after dining here on a busy Friday night. I reached out and they couldn't find the tumbler, it was probably thrown away. The owner reached out and said that she would replace the tumbler for me! Wow just wow! This is the best customer service I've ever received in my life!! Oh and btw, the food is delicious and very reasonably priced. I'm sad that I was too full for dessert, but I will definitely be back to try other items on the menu.

Came here after a day out and called in to reserve a table since the waitlist curated through the Yelp app wasn't working. It was frustrating to see a low wait time of 25 minutes and see that there was an error. So, I called and was told that it would be a 60-90 minute wait and my party was fine with that as we weren't too hungry. 

There are so many options and all sound so great and I would love to come back again to try some other items. I settled with the catfish that came with some corn fritters, mac n' cheese, and grits (my first time having them!) and we ordered a side of biscuits as well. 

Portions depends on which dish. The catfish I ordered was a pretty good size for what you're paying and as someone ordered the blue plate breakfast and it was a decent amount of food. When our food came out we realized we received grits instead of house potatoes, so our waitress kindly told us not to worry about it and gave us an additional serving of house potatoes.

Their fried chicken is mediocre. The portion was really small even for those with a small appetite, so I was a bit disappointed since it didn't live up to my expectations. The size of the chicken looked like small drummettes and chicken thighs that weren't any bigger than the average palm. However, we reasoned that it was maybe the busyness of Labor Day weekend coupled with the newness of the restaurant that caused them to choose really small chicken pieces. But if you're solely coming here for fried chicken, there are better places that give you much larger portions and tastier chicken.

Grits were good, I guess, but it was my first time having them, so I don't have anything else to compare them too. And OH! The corn fritters were amazing!! I really liked the crunchiness of the exterior and how fluffy and tender and slightly sweet the batter was. It wasn't muffin-like and I could taste the texture of the corn meal itself. Their biscuits are the flaky-kind and while those are my preferred type of biscuit, Fixins biscuits were tender and cake-like.

We ordered the peach cobbler which was pretty meh. The flaky top was good, but ultimately got really hard to chew at some points. There were barely any peaches AND the whole cobbler portion was sitting in a pretty tall pool of butter. It had a lot of nutmeg, so if you're averse to that, stray away. I wouldn't order it again as it was just okay.

BUT. Fixins has the best hot links I have ever tried. I always look for a snappy skin in a hot dog or a hot link and Fixins lived up to it well. It was also a good amount of heat, whereas other restaurants or packaged hot links lack in the heat aspect.

Overall, Fixins is a pretty good restaurant and Sacramento has just gotten another solid soul food place! It was hard to hear conversations at your own table, but along with good music and just good vibes from people eating delicious food (and waiters and waitresses really enjoying good music and having a good time working) it's worth trying.

The grilled okra was amazing. The fried chicken and mac and cheese were aight. The service was good and the ambiance and music were nice. A bit pricey for the location and quality. It's no Roscoe's, but it'll do for Sacramento.

This restaurant is new and looks amazing.  The staff were friendly and prompt.  The chicken was fantastic with just the right amount of crispy exterior and juicy interior.  We all had the two piece chicken which cones with bread and two fixins (I had the Mac and cheese along with creamed corn).  We also had the soul Caesar which was very good and had corn bread croutons (they were very good!).  Couldn't pass on the kool aid they had so I got a purple kool aid adult beverage.  Didn't get to try their dessert but definitely looked like they are with trying.
First time eating here and all I can say is that the food was delicious...and the service was on point. I'll come back on my next visit tie Sac Town.

Expensive, but worth it! Those smothered pork chops were too die for, and the sweet potatoes almost made me cry! I wish I had gotten the peach cobbler! I live in San Francisco, but when I return to Sacramento I'll be back!

First time here and I have to say it's delicious  banana pudding and Mac and cheese was to die for!  The chicken was great don't get me wrong but it almost felt as if there was more skin then the actual chicken itself. Also the coleslaw was meh :/ like I would not order it again, thank god it was a side that came with the plate already. I didn't like it that if I had to eat coleslaw from KFC instead, I wouldn't mind.

The fried chicken is amazing. Hands down one of the best I have had. The seasoning is just right, the fry was perfectly crispy. I recommend to add a menu item called "bucket of chicken", coz I can easily eat 10 pieces and just roll out of the door. 

The collard green is good because it not mushy. It had some texture and not overly salted like collard green tends to be. The creamed corn is very tasty with some vegetables added to it. Biscuit taste like love. I am not joking. 

Only complaint is that the music is a little too hyper for 8AM.  Maybe just turn the base down a bit until 9AM?  

Will definitely be back.

Sacramento has long been in need of a good sit down soul food spot! Fixins did not disappoint. The fried green tomatoes, catfish filet and fried chicken were seasoned and cooked perfectly; tasted fresh and arrived to the table hot. The mac n' cheese, yams and greens were equally good... even the hush puppies left me wanting more. 

Place was packed on a Saturday night at 7 p.m., we waited about 45 minutes. Kevin Johnson actually seated us, which was pretty cool. The service was good and the food came out quick (maybe 10 minutes). Overall, the atmosphere was nice, good mix of people and some great oldies playing in the background.

My husband and I are already planning our next visit to try more food (no lie, it may be tomorrow).

Went back for a second visit in less than two weeks. Had the gumbo this time and those dreamy creamy grits again. Delicious. My companion loved her catfish and greens. Our servers were so nice, we decided it was just a characteristic of everyone who works there! Other than the noise level (it's a little high), I cannot say enough good things about this restaurant!
Really good Southern dishes. The fact that it's ALWAYS crowded tells you something about how good it is, but keep that in mind if you're on a tight schedule and looking to get a quick bite.

If you want some down home soul food this is the place to go. The service and atmosphere is great and the food is excellent. I highly recommend the fried chicken, waffles, and the fried catfish recipe is delicious! The Mac n' cheese and candied yams taste just like my Granny's. I've tried most of the cocktails on the menu, my favorite being the Side Car;  they are all tasty  (however, I do think they are a bit pricey, hence the four stars). All in all, I love going there.

This review might be premature because I have not tasted the food yet but, this is the FIRST TIME my macaroni and cheese wasn't full of butter or hard edges. I swore off fixins but decided to give them another chance with the Ruth's plate and by visual inspection......I'm pretty happy
Always a enjoyable experience.  The menu is tight and everything is spot on.  Excellent service as well, with a fully premium stocked bar

My boyfriend and I have been wanting to come here since it opened.  We had to wait about 30 minutes for a table, so we ordered drinks at the bar while we waited.  As other reviews have stated, the service is amazing!  Seriously. From the bar to the table side servers, everyone is friendly and attentive.   

We ordered the fried deviled eggs as an appetizer.  My boyfriend had the 3 piece fried chicken with collard greens and red beans and rice for dinner. He really liked the food except the collard greens.  He felt the greens were a little too salty for his taste. I had the smothered chicken with the mac and cheese and red beans and rice.  I really liked how the chicken skin stayed crunchy even though it was covered with gravy.  The red beans and rice had a nice kick to it.  

Definitely a great spot, and we will definitely come back.

Food, service and atmosphere were all great. The place was a vibe. Very much soulful. The cool aid drinks pleasant  welcome treat to the table for the inner kid. The fried green tomatoes, fried catfish nuggets, pimento cheese and bacon burger with the cool aid pickle, and candied yams were all tasty.

Ox tail is a is amazing
Drinks are good. Just kind of hot sitting outside. Chicken is delicious to better KFC.

OMG this place blew me away. The okra and greens are a must have. The food looked, smelled and tasted amazing. There were 12 in my party with kids and our server was so awesome the whole time taking care of us. So pleasant with her beautiful smile. I also loved the display of art on the walls. A very nice place. Will definitely visit again.
Always so super amazing. With everything you order it's hot delicious and my God the Sweet Tea reminds me of living down south. Always make sure you get the cornbread with that amazing butter.

Daaaamn. This place was delish. Everything we tried was great! Fried chicken, flavorful and juicy. Mac and cheese, yum! Biscuit, I'll take two! But let me tell you about the candied yams. They were ridiculous. Ridiculously delicious! Tasted like a mixture of yams and applesauce, with the perfect amount of spices. I'll be dreaming about those yams until I can get them again!  Everyone was super friendly. Also, it's in a cool, hip, up and coming location! 

We really wanted to try a couple desserts (peach cobbler!) but we were so stuffed, we had to pass. Will definitely be back soon, and try to pace ourselves so we can try the desserts!
Mackenzie was amazing!!! Great bartender, great vibe, and good food! I will definitely go again!!!

I came here today for my first time with a bunch of family to celebrate two birthdays so we needed two large tables outside and Cat our amazing, sweet funny waitress handled all our demands effortlessly by herself! The food was delicious, the service excellent and the ambience was wonderful. I can't wait to come back to try more items on the menu.
 I will be booking my bday party here in November!
Food and staff was very good.  It was very crowded and very loud though.  Tough to have a conversation.

Awesome vibe, food, presentation and service!  So excited and happy to have this tastefully modern,  vibrant Soul Food Restuarant in Sacramento.  We no longer have to drive to Fairfield. Thank you Kevin ~ it's a slam dunk!  

Dinned twice this week.  My husband had oxtails & collards. I had chicken wings & waffle. 2nd visit had catfish, mac n' cheese & black eyed peas.  Fish was crispy/flaky & fresh.  Mac n' cheese was creamy & cheesy.  Everything was perfectly seasoned ... my husband and girlfriend were in oxtail heaven.  We also enjoyed the buttermilk pie for dessert.  Bloody Mary's and mimosas were on point too. 

Additionally, Major Johnson was there on both occasions visiting each table/guest inquiring if everything was okay. 

Have I said enough?  Prices are good and there's choices other than soul food on the menu. This will definitely be one of our favorite spots ~ same menu breakfast, lunch or dinner. Highly recommended!

Came in during grad weekend so the floor was busy but we found a spot at the bar. Q was the bartender and WAS FANTASTIC! She had great recommendations, the drinks were made to perfection, and she did it all with the most upbeat attitude! Would never have believed she had only been bartending here for six months - she was hustling like a pro. I always love coming to Fixins, and she's has given me another reason. Will definitely be back soon!

Chicken was well seasoned, gravy was amazing, macaroni was decent, yams were good, however the greens and the oxtail were salty. I would eat there again however I wouldn't order the greens of the oxtail
Again. Peach cobbler was pretty good
As well.

Best soul food in the area. 
I had gotten the Waffle with chicken & its so good! Very professional fancy restaurant. Employee were also very helpful. Will be coming back soon .

Went in for some great food and ran into a great bartender. Mackenzie was wonderful with the drinks and conversation. Definitely made my wait for a table worth it. Great service, my wife and I loved her whole ambiance.

Phenomenal. This time around shared oxtails, gumbo and shrimp & grits. Of the three the shrimp & grits got the highest rating, but in truth you can't find anything bad here. Great atmosphere, fantastic wait staff and there was no line (of course, we went for lunch on a Wednesday, place was about half-full but started to get more busy as we were nearing the end of the meal). And they do a pretty good takeout business as well. Can't say enough about them and can't wait to go back!

Best Soul Food in Sacramento 10/10 definitely recommend the food was really good Koolaid was perfect wasn't to sweet or watered down corn bread was soft and good peach cobbler was good but a little to sweet for me
AMAZING. The fried chicken reminds me of my dad's cooking god rest his soul! Very nice customer service and highly recommend

Cat was a very nice server. Smothered chicken was very good. Crunchy and still covered in gravy. 
The oxtails with gravy, really good. 
Totally worth finding in Oak Park.

My family and I have been here a total of 3 times and I feel that it is safe to say that the customer service is phenomenal! We've had a different waitress each time and each provided excellent customer service regardless if it was busy or not (we've come at both peak and down times). Additionally, I believe it's the manager or supervisor, who comes to greet us during every visit which makes us feel we are well taken care of. This place definitely has that "home" feel to it. I don't venture far from my likes, so I have always ordered the same dish: chicken & waffles with a blue kool aid. The chicken has just the right amount of juiciness to it and the waffle is thin yet dense. I will definitely be back and I will for sure be ordering the same thing!

I heard great things sbout this place and every single one of them was true! Amazing food, great ambiance and the staff was awesome! 

We sat at the bar where a friendly bartender with a great personality by the name of T'LA TEQUILA took exceptional care of us. Also a very nice gentleman by the name of OL'SKOOL dropped off a special Birthday Drink to me. 

I ordered the chicken and waffles with a side of Mac n cheese. The chicken was crispy AND juicy, the waffles were flavorful and the Mac n cheese was the REAL DEAL.

South used to be my go to for fried chicken but now Fixins is definitely my #1.

Thank you for not disappointing Fixins. Will definitely be coming back...again and again.
Great food and atmosphere! I'd heard about it from relatives in LA, and this one definitely met the expectation.

Oh my!  What a find.  I was looking for something other than a typical lunch and I followed my nose to a real treat.  They don't try to do everything but my guess is everything the do is good if not plain great.

Don't miss the grits and if you get a chance say hello to Ms. Pearl.

Easily found parking right to the side of the restaurant on 3rd street.  Came about 445pm, hostess was welcoming, no wait and was quickly seated :)  Restaurant was clean and very nice.  Our server Jazmine was attentive, pleasant, and very responsive.  Atmosphere was culturally diverse which is what I like to see here in Sac.  
We started with the deep fried deviled eggs accompanied with a salad garnish, this is a must try!  We got the fried chicken wings & waffles and the fried chicken with collard greens and beans and rice sides.  Everything was tasty & delicious and serving portions were perfect.  Also finished off with the peach cobbler..excellent as well!  Definitely want to come back and bring the rest of my family for an excuse to eat here again.

Better make a reservation- even on a Wednesday night. This place is hot! Our group of 5 each ordered something different, including sides, just so we could all try each other's plate. We each went in having some dish we didn't care for. After sampling from each other's plates, we all agreed this place could change our minds. Everything was superb. Not just good or great, truly superb. Definitely worth it. 
The environment is still casual yet elevated from the other 2 Southern style restaurants in the greater Sacramento area. Gotta say, the drink service took way longer than I've ever experienced and when I sipped my friend's Mai Tai, it was a little disappointing. Perhaps they were short a bartender that evening. All in all, I will definitely be returning and bringing as many friends as I can to share this new local favorite.

this was the worst food I've ever purchased. Really disappointed in the quality of food and the price. I placed 2 orders for takeout! 3 pc chicken $21.00 (2 legs &thigh) collard green 6 bites in container,  sw potatoes, 8 bites
catfish meal $23.00 turned out to swahi ( I asked what kind and was told this) 1 fillet , potato salad 7 bites $6.00 ,  I ordered greens as my side and girb2 horrible Mac n cheese , which was inedible! (velvets  and macaroni?) I also ordered cobbler, it was runny, no crust and filled with gooey dough) I did get 3 sm peach slices!  I was told this was $6.00 but Ipoh receipt $10.00! banana pudding , was told $6.00 old on receipt $10.00 . My bill was$76.13 including tax for a meal that was not only tasteless but expensive! I will NEVER go here again! shame on the owner snd the cooks for selling this!   You better start tasting your dishes before selling! really horrible
I highly recommend this place. Alize was out hostess, Everything was fresh! Staff was very friendly as well. I recommend making reservations this place gets lit!

Hands down the best plate of fork tender flavor I've ever had.  Oxtail with collards. 
What a treat!!  Can't wait to go back!

I'm going to start by saying this..... I spent a lot of time in the "south", from North Carolina down to Florida and this is by far the closest you'll get to real soul food in the Sacramento area. I'm mean this food is GOOD! 

Second I ordered fixing again today and I noticed the last couple times I ordered the biscuits, they were a little iffy. But today ... MADE TO PERFECTION. So i am doing sit this picture here and let y'all know that whoever made the biscuits today 1/21/2021..... There needs to be a meeting so they always come out like this. Lol anyway! Love your food!

The staff were all very friendly and welcoming upon arrival and throughout the whole lunch. Ordered the Po'Me some Honey and it was delicious. Not a drop of it was wasted . I was lit  after two sips just the way I like it. For lunch I ordered the chicken smothered in gravy with sweet potatoes and Mac& cheese . The Mac and cheese was my favorite. I could have eaten a whole plate of the mac and cheese and been perfectly happy but with my drink of course .

Their menu options are fantastic and the staff is extremely friendly.  A very nice experience in this establishment with very delicious food.  However, the parking space is terrible!  May have to drive around waiting for a parking spot.
Ambiance was immaculate as well as the food. I wish I could eat here every day! Reminds me of back home

Wow. This place was incredible!! Came here for lunch with a group of friends. We were seated quickly, and our server was friendly and really took care of us! Be sure to ask for Cat! I ordered the smothered chicken with greens and Mac and cheese and a side of cornbread. Oh my word, everything was delicious. Delicious!! The rest of my friends enjoyed their food as well. So happy to find this gem, and I am looking forward to coming back! If you are in Sacramento, be sure to check this place out!!

Disappointed in the fact that we were asked to vacate the table to accommodate other patrons. We were there for 58 minutes. Each reservation is loaded for 90 minutes. So we still have 32 to go. Because it was my husband's birthday. I did not want to cause the scene. We finished our beverages the table next to us was also asked to vacate they refused.  I obliged, but it left a overall bad taste in my mouth. To enjoy your food and not your experience.
Second time we went, food was good. They were busy. The only thing they need to work on is the seating.

Terrible experience with their to-go tonight.
The chicken sandwich I ordered came with little fires and was extremely over cooked and tough. I also ordered a side of coleslaw and it was mixed with potato salad. You spend $26 on a sandwich and a side you should get better quality food.
Exellent service and delicious food. The peach cobbler is to die for.
Best restaurant in Sacramento hands down. Quenyaunta took real good care of me at the bar. She was so professional and polite. I'll be back before I head back to LA.

I wanted to try the best chicken sandwich in town, so I made my way over to Fixins. Not sure what was going on,  maybe it was just a busy Saturday,  but after ordering, I waited 20 minutes outside to receive a text letting me know my order was ready. I then waited another 5 minutes at the outdoor counter to be told my food wasn't ready and I needed to wait 15 more minutes. Not having any of that, I went inside and spoke to another staff member. Sure enough my order was sitting at the bar, the outside staff just genuinely didn't care to look. 

By the time I got back to my hotel, the food was cold. The food itself was a 3.5 stars. Nothing too special. Not with the $17 I paid to sit outside and deal with the horrible customer service. 

I'm sure it's a great restaurant, but my experience was the opposite of what I was expecting.
Love coming here on the when I can You guys make my day better when I'm otw home  thank youuu
I love Fixins. I always have a good time here. The food is amazing! Q is such a sweetheart and gave us amazing service!!

We just had brunch here on Father's Day. It was fantastic!  Service was great!  Food was wonderful!  The inside was clean and has a great vibe. 

Thank you Fixins for such a wonderful Father's Day brunch!

After a tough week at work, we needed sometime alone, where we could just tall and relax. I'm not really  sure how my wife found  this place but I'm  glad she did! There was a line out the door and I can now understand why, this place wasn't  just good eatin, it was an EXCELLENT EXPERIENCE!  The hostesses greeted us with friendly smiles and walked us to our table with was already set up for us, the waiter promptly came to us with these Mason jars full of ice cold water, the Mason jar drinks took me back to being a kid. The food selection  was on point and when our food came out not only was it hot, but the servings were plentiful. And it was delicious! The chicken was tender and moist. The beans and salad were amazing, the mac n cheese was phenomenal   the biscuit and cornbread were out of this world! I wish I would've tried the adult kool-aid.  We're definitely  coming back and we're  bringing the family and  friends.  I'd  give extra stars if I could. Shout out to Kevin Johnson for bringing such a  great  establishment to a upcoming part of town.

Man let me tell ya, this place is the bomb for real soul food. Those Ox Tails and Rice, Collards Greens is good. I've been there several times and never disappointed. Hurry and get there. Yes I recommend all soul food enthusiast try this place out, you won't be disappointed.

Always on point. Tried the catfish with collards, red beans & rice to go. So good! Tasty, with enough spice that my eyes watered and nose ran  but not enough to leave my mouth burning. Served with adorable, amazing round corn fritters. Planning a redux for my birthday in a month, as I can't think of anything I'd rather eat!

This restaurant sets the benchmark for soul food!!

My wife and I showed up for this restaurant's opening day. We were told the wait was a little over an hour, but we didn't mind. We spent some time speaking with some of the patrons leaving the restaurant after their meal and was impressed with hearing their rave reviews on the food.

We started with the catfish nuggets for the appetizer. The fish was cooked perfectly and had a pleasant flavor. The dipping sauces provided were a wonderful compliment to the fish. So far, a great start.

My wife ordered the fried chicken with candied yams and mac & cheese for the sides.  The chicken was cooked perfectly with a light, crunchy crust.  Both side dishes were to absolutely die for.  The candied yams were scrumptious and the mac & cheese was creamy and so flavorful.

I ordered the oxtails with collard greens for the side dish. The oxtail was moist, tender, and tasty. The gravy had a very nice flavor. Collard greens were absolutely perfect.

We finished the evening with peach cobbler for myself and buttermilk pie for the wife.  Both desserts were expertly prepared and were nothing short of wonderful.

Atmosphere was a little noisy, but fully expected for opening day. Decor was pleasant. Service was really good for the restaurant being very busy.

If you are looking for a restaurant with seriously good food, look no further.  THIS is the place to go.  My wife and I will be looking forward to many more dining experiences at this wonderful restaurant!

The food was definitely a hit. My husband is usually not a fan of restaurant soul food. We were trying to be cautious in our choices but there was no need, everything was so good. We came on a weekday to avoid the crowds and wait. Definitely would recommend!

Ended up getting the Fried Catfish with yams and mac as sides - flaky and flavorful fish. Also tried the blue raspberry fizz drink which tasted like an alcoholic kool aid! They seem to have plenty of other options that I would go back and try.

I was a solo diner in this busy lunch hour. I sat quietly at the bar. The customers let me squeeze in, and the bartender was very busy! Even with all the hustle and bustle- I was treated as though my meal mattered just as much as everyone. The food was so good. Thanks!
This food was amazing! The catfish could be cooked a little longer but everything else was good.

Arrived for lunch and with 1 table mate ordered fried green tomatoes (crunchy batter but no flavor, sauce redeemed order) oxtails were tender with lots of gravy though expensive. Final dish, gumbo was over salted.

We were so glad that we finally decided to give this a try! From the service to the food, everything was very good.

I am vegetarian, so it's good to know that most sides are vegetarian. I ordered the mac & cheese, candied yams, cole slaw, and cornbread. The flavor in the mac & cheese and coleslaw were perfect, and I would certainly get these again, though I want to try the grits next time. The coleslaw had a tiny little kick to it and had just the right amount of sweetness. Mac & cheese was very flavorful but not overdone, and the top of it was covered in cheese crustees.. mmm. The cornbread was fine and I'd say a little underwhelming on the flavor.

My boyfriend got the chicken and waffles, and that waffle was quite tasty! He said the chicken was very moist while the skin was the right amount of crunch. I sure was having flashbacks of my omnivore days while watching him eat it!

They have a covered outdoor patio, and good thing the weather was decent today. Hopefully they can get some misters for the summer. We'll definitely be back!

07/24/21-Ordered to go/pick up via grub hub at 6p. Drove 20 minutes to the restaurant. Waited/double parked in the "to go line area". As you know per the grub hub app it'll let you know when your order is ready for pick up. So at 644p my order was ready for pick up. Went up to the to go/pick up line, which is located to the right of the entrance. Waited in line for 10 min (as there were people in front of me). Got to the front of the line and was told that "there were three orders ahead of mine and it would be another 20 minutes." As I sauntered back to my car, I overheard a party of four mention they've been waiting for "TWO HOURS" for their to go/pick up. My first thought was, hell no, not I. 21 minutes have passed. It is now 705p went back to wait in the to go/pick up line. Waited in line for 2 minutes (again waiting behind other customers). Food still not ready. This time I'm told my wait is "10-15 minutes." Now I'm confused, borderline hangry. It's 715p back at my spot-the pick up line. Server went inside to pick up my order. It was ready. Wow. A whole THIRTY minutes later (after my pick up time per grub hub of 644p)  my prepaid to go Grubhub order was ready. Even though I noted in my order I wanted hot sauce as a side. I had to remind the server to put hot sauce in the bag.  OVERALL,  this is definitely a restaurant timing issue.  As the owner or General manager needs to rectify the miscommunication with grub hub, the cooks, SOMEBODY! Oh, and not to mention you can't even get a live human when you call the restaurants number-it's literally an automated recording. SHAME. Food is good- but NOT that dang good to have customers wait up to 30 minutes, heck two hours (for some) for a to go order. I understand there's a pandemic -but adapt, revise, and figure it out. Efficiency is key. And IF the owner happens to read this review- I'm available (for a fee) to provide a consulting services on how to make the aforementioned happen.

This is the best gosh dang meal I have ever had in my entire life. I wish I lived in this neighborhood so I could eat here every single day. 
I ordered the "Smothered Chicken" with some Okra, and the yummiest mac n cheeses. I added a cornbread muffin and an amazing cup of Blue Kool Aid. Would not change a single thing about this place at all. It is in an awesome location, and the place itself has such an hip-n-homey vibe to it. 

Honestly, I had goosebumps all over my skin after each incredible bite. 

Finally, my server was super down to earth, helpful, quick, and really friendly. Tierra, you rocked! Keep up the good work! (Also, thanks for not judging me for think okra was some type of beef. Really appreciate that.. hahah!)

Thanks for this insanely epic meal!

Sacramento NEEDED this restaurant! My family and I had the best experience from the sweet greeter (can't remember her name) , bartender, Old School and our waiter. As expected, it was a long wait but the outside patio and bar was helped the time pass by fine. 

The vibes are incredible. Feels so good to be around all ages, races... just good company. Feels like a team of "Big Mamas" ,with the big elbows were in the back making all our meals 

We had the following and HIGHLY recommended: 

oxtails & rice with greens 
Catfish with Mac n cheese & yams
Fried chicken with Mac & greens 
Chicken wings and waffles 

Very delicious!
Delicious meal with excellent service. No issues with finding a parking space and no wait to be seated. Will definitely return when in Sacramento.

I love this place. I'm not supposed to eat fried food anymore so, when I go over the edge, I want the best. Their fried chicken is the s#*t.  I mean, sometimes,  I just gotta have it...and the sides ! MmmmMmmm. The staff are always friendly and inviting and the atmosphere has good vibes. If you haven't been here you are missing out.
Food and customer service has been very good at this location. Whenever we are in Sacramento we stop here to eat. They can get busy at times. So have a little patience.

I've had good experiences here and bad experiences here.  Overall the food is pretty good.  I am a big fan of red beans and rice as well as greens.  My favorite dish so far here has been the fried catfish.  I had a bit of the oxtail and was pretty pleased.

Room for improvement could be in the service.  Each time I've come here, the service and speed of your meal to the table has been inconsistent.  Overall the service is very friendly but drink refills or asking for a to go container can be hit or miss.

Wait times can be kind of long so I definitely recommend coming early!

Saw this place out the corner of my eye and was hungry so....
I walked into the wrong business at first. Place on the corner said it happens often and directed me to the right entrance. Walked in and was greeted by another customer waiting to pick up his order. A nice bubbly waitress came by and asked if I was there to pick up. No sorry just lonely middle aged me trying a new place. I was seated and quickly asked if I wanted a drink. Corona please. Bottle or cup? Bottle all the way. I was given a menu and had to wait longer than I expected to place my order. I just glanced over the items and this is my kinda good so I already had a good idea of what I wanted. The blackened catfish was delicious. Soft tender juicy breaded and fried to perfection. The gave me 2 sauces to dip into both were delicious as well. The side of greens I ordered was so good I put my main dish to the side so I could finish that first.  The food definitely did not disappoint. Lots of nostalgia vibes while eating. Some guy sitting with his girl said something about the only better soul food she's eaten is in Chicago. It caught my ear cause that's where I was born and raised. I nodded in silence saying in my head, me too. After about 30 mins it was time to leave. Left a fat tip and told the waitress everything was amazing. She didn't really need the encouragement she was so bubbly and happy from the start.  Definitely coming back here with my wife soon.

So far so great. This place opened last WK according to Mum and Anie. We're here for a Monday lunch. Yep, we ate A LOT and it was good. 

To be clear we are a family of cooks, so we loved dissecting the flavor profiles as much as we enjoyed eating the food. 

The wait staff was a bit slow, but they just opened. Every person was super nice and extremely welcoming. Mayor Johnson along with the other owners were on site to make this place even more special. The music was on point. The space is lovely too!!

Please tell chef Boots the Leanna's say hello!

Win win win. 

Been here multiple times now and have loved every meal - brunch or dinner! Always welcomed by who I presume is the manager (super buff dude) who's super friendly. 

Get their fried chicken, biscuits, and their gravy. Honestly the best southern meal you can ask for. Nourishes your soul. Didn't know chicken breast could be so tender! Well seasoned and crispy. 

The biscuits come with a strawberry cream (or some similar berry) that's perfectly buttery and sweet. Must try. 

Lastly, get the Mac and cheese. And the beans and rice. To be honest, the food's great and worth the price so I can keep going.

First time coming here and it won't be the last! I was recommended by a friend to try this place and I'm so glad I went. First, the interior is really clean and has a industrial rustic vibe to it. I came with my boyfriend in the afternoon on a weekday and it wasn't too busy. We were given the option of seating inside or outside but thankfully inside was available because it was hot out. 

I ordered the 3pc chicken (dark meat) with coleslaw and Mac and cheese. I also ordered 2 extra sides, the collard greens and candied yams. 

Immediately as the plates were set in front of us, I could smell so much flavor and seasonings. The chicken is BOMB! Perfect texture! So crunchy, flavorful, and juicy! The Mac and cheese obviously has so much love put into it. It actually reminds me of Mac and cheese I've made a couple times for Thanksgiving but was never able to master. This is nothing like the Kraft Mac or the drive thru Mac. This is REAL Mac and cheese. The coleslaw is yummy too! I prefer this over other drive thru coleslaws (duh) and even places like Raisin Canes or Angry Chickz. This isn't toooo creamy, and it has a nice tang of acidity. Not terribly sweet either and has a nice crunch from the cabbage. It hits different because other coleslaws just makes you feel like you're eating sugary mayonnaise. The candied yams were divine, soft but still a bite to it that just melts in your mouth. It's also my first time having collard greens and they're really tender! A biiit too salty for me but I'm still glad I got to try it. Their biscuit is also pretty soft and puffy! I usually hate biscuits because it's so dry and it's like dust in my mouth, but this I got a good taste of buttermilk coming through. 

Banana pudding was good, a little too sweet for me but it was a nice treat to finish everything. Unfortunately they didn't bring new/small utensils with it like other restaurants did so we used our old forks. They also carries paper straws if you're environmentally conscious. Parking can be a struggle during busy hours such as the weekend or during dinner in general. Our server was attentive and I appreciate that when I asked for extra things such as separate checks/boxes/to go orders, she did so quickly and graciously. I did feel a little self conscious asking because her demeanor seemed pretty reserved than what I'm used to, but she was definitely on top of it. Altogether, I definitely recommend you try out this place if you're looking for quality soul food.
Food and services is bad . The food isn't good. The sides were ok. Not the greatest soul food place

The food was good, a little heavy handed on the salt level, the shrimp and grits my wife
Loved it. I had the smothered chicken, it was tasty. The Mac and cheese was good. The fried catfish app was good. The restaurant was nice. The reason for the 3 stars is the staff, we walk in and they say it is a 15 minute wait but that table that we sat at was empty and they had enough staff so I was confused why we had to wait. The waitress was in over her head, constantly fixing issues she or the patrons created. We waited a good 40 minutes for the food and we saw people who had come in after us getting served way before us. Confusing. The waitress never came and checked on us, again she was not prepared for lunch service. At the end she did apologize but my wife was not happy at all with the service at this point. The people who came in to eat one family was wrestling around, literally, and no one told them to calm down this is a place of business. I would order take out next time. The food was good enough, but be ready for poor service. Hopefully they get better with that part.
Food was delicious! So far I have tried their fried chicken and waffles, catfish, gumbo and almost all the appetizers.  Omg forgot to mention their amazing cornbread!!!!

Awesome! Fried Chicken tastes homemade (even though of course we know it's not), sides are generous, it's on the border between reasonable / pricier side of things, service was fast, good, place was clean and good ambiance inside the place. Would come back

The food was extremely delicious. The ambiance was excellent, and the service was great. However, the banana pudding looked good, but it may have had 3 cookies and hardly any bananas. And the peach cobbler was not worth eating.  The 2 desserts were a waste of $20

AMAZING food service and ambiance! The absolute best and most authentic soul food I've had since I left the south. 

Wirth the long search for a parking space.

I knew it wasn't going to be a good experience when on a Sunday morning by 12:00 noon, they were OUT OF COFFEE AND HOT TEA but I took a chance and ordered food anyway!  It was so disappointing!  I ordered smothered chicken. What is the point of having gravy without rice or mashed potatoes? The gravy was soo thick that I don't see how it could be called gravy. It appeared to be made with cornflakes instead of flour!  I felt bad for people who we're trying soul food for the first time because it was the worst example of soul food I have ever had. EVER!  And the staff ?  Well let's just say that they didn't appear to be excited about working there.

Hit the spot! Tucked in the newly oak park/broadway with a huge patio and full bar. Wait was about 25 mins so make sure you get there early or after 8pm. All the fixins was delicious!
