The biscuits was my favorite! Thick cut piece with sugary, cinnamon butter! Collards green just cooked down nicely with that smoky flavor. Mac and cheese was cheesy and tasty. The rice and beans is just how I would imagine it in the south. Now to the chicken!!! I ordered the chicken wings and waffle. Omg so delicious! Only wished for my chicken to be a little more crisper, we also got the hot chicken sandwich'. You can choose between white or dark meat and we got one of each. Really huge pieces cooked so well! And we also got the smothered chicken.... the gravy was on point! I will deff be coming back! Oh and to drink I got the New Orleans sweet tea, best sweet tea!!!! I'm in heaven!

Fixes is great I love the chicken the oxtails amazing the food is so amazing. I love this place. I would go every weekend if I have to the service is great. The waiting time is only like 10-15 minutes, which is when it comes. I am already just ready to eat they're so quick to come out the place looks so nice and clean. They're always the nicest greatest music. Also if you get to eat outside you're in for a special treat. It's real warm because they have the heaters. I love this place. I definitely recommend 100%.

LOVE me some Fixins. Always good energy and good service when I come in. The atmosphere makes you feel at home and just enough options on the menu. I normally go with the fried chicken with Mac and cheese and greens. I have tried the oxtails which were also good and the chicken sandwich. Love the drink menu also if you are treating yourself. I ordered the gumbo for the first time after many visits and that's probably the only think I wouldn't recommend. Gave more of a gravy feel than a roux, couldn't find any chicken in my serving,  and I thought $7 for 4 shrimps was a bit much. BUT I will definitely be back. Check them out.

Just moved to the area, and this was the first place where I actually had good food. Everything was amazing. I had the 2 piece chicken meal with Mac n cheese and okra. It was fire! I had a sweet tea as well and it was so good. I wish I would have saved room for dessert. I'll definitely be back. 

The service was amazing. The staff was so kind and welcoming! Literally made you feel at home. They clearly care about customer satisfaction. I will definitely become a regular at this place !
Never disappoints! Great food and atmosphere... food comes out hot and fast. Friendly staff... positive vibes

Hitting up a new restaurant during its first few weeks is always a gamble. However, hitting up a soul food restaurant during its first few weeks is a serious crap shoot, but this visit paid off! 

Arriving around 7pm on a Friday night, we were greeted by a line at the door. After waiting about 7 minutes we were added 14th to Yelp waitlist and told it would be about an hour. We considered that was pretty good for every single seat being taken in the place and no one looking ready to bounce.

We decided to grab some drinks from the bar and hang out on the patio which currently serves as a holding area for diners. There are plenty of tables and chairs outside, and I have a feeling they will eventually start using that space for seating once everything gets worked out. Former Sac Mayor Kevin Johnson was walking around outside to make sure everyone had water or a drink while they waited. An older woman thought he might have forgotten her and called out, "I want one (water), Kevin" to which he replied, "I come for you!" Everyone seemed quite familiar and he made sure people felt welcomed. 

We ordered the Lavender Lemon Drop and it was perfect. The lavender was not overwhelming and mixed well with vodka. There was only one bartender and he was keeping up with the rush. 

Roughly an hour later, our time had come and we had plenty of time to review the menu. As soon as our waitress hit the table, we ordered! Fried chicken, catfish, fried deviled eggs, sweet potatoes, mac & cheese, red beans & rice, black eyed peas, and okra. The chicken came with a biscuit and the fish had hush puppies. 

The chicken and fish were seasoned for the gods! They were just right and not too salty. My friends ate every bit of it and one was tempted to order another 3 piece! My fish was good, but I would have liked a dipping sauce to come with it. I asked for ketchup, but it never arrived. It's their first couple of weeks so it's nothing to downgrade them about.

All the sides were delicious with the red beans being a table favorite. The Mac & cheese was good, but a bit dry. Probably baked a tad too long, but I'll order it again before making final judgements. The okra was ranked last , but we appreciated the effort. I mean, you throw bacon on something you're bound to make somebody happy.

Overall, this place is wonderful. Kevin Johnson did a wonderful job with his beautifully decorated Oak Park Restaurant. It resembles any new, trendy neighborhood spot, but it still incorporates elements from the culture. The restroom sign states, "If you gotta go, go here!" Along with other thoughtful design elements. I'm glad we got to check it out in the early stages and we can't wait to get back after the official grand opening on Aug 28th!

UPDATE: the owner and manager reached out to me within 10 minutes after posting my review to make the situation right. I absolutely appreciate the speedy service as well as sending me a gift card to make it up to them. 




Day 34 of quarantine: so my fiancé and I decided to order food last night, and we had such a pleasant experience last time we ate there so we decided to have it again. Aside from DoorDash taking almost 25+ minutes to deliver, and we don't live that far from Fixins, the food came back cold as expected due to taking long to deliver. The only real complaint we have is that the first bite I took into my chicken sandwich I realized I had a full wing. So not only am I having to deal with the food being cold, now I have to deal with bones

Good service, clean, good soul food. I always order the deviled eggs and fried green tomatoes.  I'm a sucker for fried chicken sandwiches. I get that anywhere they sell them.  It's good but I've had better. Just keeping it 100. Def get the peach cobbler and banana pudding.

I came here with my family and we started with the fried green tomatoes and fried deviled eggs. Both were really good and I highly recommend. We also ordered the fried chicken and waffles which were great. We also had their biscuit with grape jelly butter which was amazing! The butter was to die for paired with the biscuit. The fried chicken lives up to the hype. I ordered the fried catfish which was really good. I love the breading they use, it's so crispy. We also tried the shrimp gumbo which was okay. The sides we tried were red beans and rice, collard greens, & Mac and cheese. All were pretty good, my favorite was the beans & rice. Their drinks are also good we ordered the adult kool aid and hurricane, both great drinks and they were on the stronger side.

We traveled 200 miles to visit and it was well worth it.  Alejandra, our server was bubbly, helpful and attentive. 

We tied the fried chicken- it was crispy and very juicy. As crispy as the outside was, the inside was moist. 

Mac n cheese was cheesy with large noodles- perfect for absorbing flavor. 

Now, for the good stuff. The gumbo was better than E. Legassie. I've almost perfected my own Gumbo trying to imitate his recipe- Fixins is better . Emirel. please copy Fixins recipe- it is better than yours.  

We will be back!

This was our first time here. We came at about 11am on a Sunday. There was no wait. Clean and cute place. Our waiter was Nate. He was super nice and attentive! The food was really good. 

We ordered the deep fried deviled eggs and cat fish nuggets for appetizers. Both were good, but the fish nuggets were bomb. The dipping sauces were really good. I don't know how, but there tarter is different then other places. It was bomb.

Hubby ordered the oxtails which came with one fixin (side) and she chose mac and cheese. The Mac and cheese was really good (though I always prefer homemade-however I would order this again). The oxtails were tender and tasty and came on a bed of rice.

I order chicken tan waffles. The 3 peice came with 2 waffles w/grape butter and 3 chicken pieces (we chose all  thighs).  The waffles were delish. I loved the grape butter and the syrup was pre warmed. The chicken was so crispy and the inside was super moist and tender. 

They left a water carafe for us so we never ran out of water. 

I will definitely come here again!

Authentic handmade soul food? I doubt it. I don't know if it's Sacramento's tastebuds but the only thing I can fully eat here are the sides and the drinks. 
I've ordered smothered pork chops which was hard to chew, a chicken sandwich which I assumed was a breast I don't know what part of the chicken it was but it was giant and undercooked. My yams I feel like I ate a bowl of oil I only received one or two potatoes. I don't know I returned several times hoping maybe it was just me or the day I came but it's every time so it's a no for me.

Good for the soul 
A little pricy but the catfish is a great dish to share and to split the cost. 
Not a fan of sugary drinks like their homemade koolaids but they do have a huge selection at their bar. I love their collard greens, wish their okra is on the sweet and savory versus pickled in vinegar. 
Fried chicken is ok, for the price I rather get it at popeyes. Hush puppies are great. Corn bread is a bit dry. Oxtail is a bit too sweet, very decadent yet heavy. Service is great, we always get seated right away and some of their servers are always nice, to the point and approachable, hence why I rated them 4 stars, mainly for their excellent service. Food can be a bit cleaner as far as ingredients and pricing. Other then that this is my go-to when we have friends or family in town looking for some soul food and in our neighborhood.

Ok so let me be honest. This is my second time going and service was great drinks great!! The food was AWESOME...... but I got the oxtails to go because I live in Oakland and didn't want to cook later. We went for brunch. Sat at the bar outside and Ansara was our bartender and server and she was EXCELLENT!! Food for brunch was wonderful. But when I got home and wanted to eat the oxtails it was a no for me . They were to sweet and the rice was over cooked. I did also get a order or catfish nuggets to go and they were good even the next day. I should've just got that. That's only reason why I gave a 4. Would've been a 5 if not for the oxtails. I will definitely be back and WILL ALWAYS SUPPORT.

Disappointed.  The first time we visited our food was delicious but today it is disgusting.  The fried catfish is greasy, mushy and undercooked.  The hush puppies are burned beyond edible and the sides (candied yams, greens and Mac n cheese) taste & appear several days old with no flavor in the yams. And nothing but salt in the greens. Spending $50 for 2 inedible plates- We will not return.

This is hands down my favorite restaurant in the Sacramento area. Everything I've ever eaten here is amazing. Today, we went as a family of 4 (including a rambunctious toddler) and had the best experience we've ever had dining out with kids. The kid's meals were reasonably priced and portioned. Our food came out quickly, and our waitress was friendly and attentive. 

Their greens are amazing, and I love their extensive list of cocktails! My husband demolished his chicken sandwich, and we were able to take leftovers from both kids' plates home to feed them for dinner. We'll definitely be back again soon!

Food was super good! I love their Catfish but service I have to give them a 5/10 the waitress was nice but there was not enough attentiveness! Asked her and another employee for a to go cup never got the cup.  Just felt like we had to wait for ever to get the check  and  we ended up asking another employee for the check. What's the best visit!

Oh wow what a treat! I've eaten in a few soul food place all around the bay but this place tops them all! Juiceeey chicken! Really really good chicken and everything else too!l! . Coming back for sure! Please try the chicken , catfish and the oxtail. So good. Out door dining available. No reservations. First come first serve. Weekend wait time around 15-30 min currently on a Saturday evening. Servers and staff are thoughtful and attentive.
Amazing service, great food and it came out fast and hot! Rare for a soul food spot. Great location. And they have Uncle Nearest (if you know you know)
Good food, service and ambiance. Catfish, chicken,  and smothered pork chops are my favorite dishes. Upgrade biscuit to corn bread if you get greens! Bomb!

Everyone has raves about this restaurant, so it was about time to try it out. We got the catfish sandwich and fried chicken entrees, and couldn't have been more pleased. Everything tasted so good! Even the biscuits they served us on the side were fluffy and paired with this amazing sweet butter. The chicken was well seasoned, crispy, and tender. We also ordered the lemonade in this giant mason jar which topped it all off being just the right amount of sweet. We'll be coming back soon to try out other menu items

Sunday fun day morning brunch. 
.
So I was early in the area for once. So I was ready for some fixins. Here I come ordered me some smothered chicken. Which comes with 2 fixins. 
.
I got me potatoe salad fixin - which was so refreshing, and I loved the red onion in the salad which complimented the chicken. There was a crunch in some bites which had a great texture. 
.
Mac and cheese - clumps of melted cheese on the macaroni. This was a little cheesy which is great for cheesy lovers. Flavor was great and goes with crispy fried chicken smother with gravy any time. 
.
So the smothered chicken I had a choice of dark or white meat. I chose dark. But then when the meal came I recieved a breast and a leg. Luckily I'm not picky so either worked. But the breast was a little dry and the dark meat on the hand more moist. The batter was crispy. The gravy was great and needs a little more flavor. I did add lots of black pepper and sprinkled fixins hot sauce which reminds me of crystal hot sauce. 
.
Service a friendly server who brought me what I need quick. As well coming back and check up. 
.
So I will come back with a great experience. Cant wait to try the waffles and some other fixins. Come here early before 930 or 10 because this place was packed after 930am. I was shook and maybe it is because it's new. I am glad we have some great soul food for sure ;). Thanks for reading and let's get fixins! 
.

.
Great food Great location. Amazing service. Everything was so delicious. Cocktails were perfect.

Decided to try our new neighborhood restaurant and oh my god was I not disappointed. My girlfriend and I came on one of their first soft opening days and the place was absolutely slammed, but the staff handled everything like they'd been doing it for years. Really great service. Sat at the bar and the food took a bit to come out, but our bartenders made sure we were well taken care of. 

Started with a Bloody Mary that was garnished with everything under the sun. Very tasty with just enough spice. Then had the fried green tomatoes - also really good, but maybe nothing to write home about. 

Then the show stopper showed up. The grits. Oh my god, the grits. The grits, the grits, the grits. Without a doubt, hands down, the best and most ridiculously good grits I've ever had. Absolutely perfect consistency and a taste that is second to none. I had them accompany the oxtail, which was also outstanding. Excellent flavor, just a bit of spice, and fall off the bone tender. 

Will undoubtedly come here many more times. Seriously though - give those grits a try.

Best fried catfish!!! Oxtail was good too!
Quick service, Covid clean, plenty of outdoor seating, good prices and friendly.
I'll definitely be back!

The bartender was a little disgruntled but she just seemed to have a lot in her mind, so she gets a pass.  I ordered the oxtails, my husband got the catfish. Both were delicious. The deviled eggs were rather flavorless. The drinks were weak but it was a nice atmosphere. I would go again
There are no words. Just come here and delight your tastebuds. The biscuits and butters (honey, strawberry) are worth the visit on their own!
Great place, great atmosphere, great food. great service. One of my favorite places to eat.

Hands down the best oxtail and fried chicken I've had at a sit down restaurant. Oxtail was so tender and fried chicken was seasoned so well and crispy. Can't wait to go back and try the rest of the menu!

I like the location of Fixins. There's plenty of street parking around the restaurant that it shouldn't be a problem if you don't mind walking a block. 

The looks are very modern with an outdoor patio area. The noise though can get loud. The sound just bounces off the walls in there and it feels like you have to raise your voice just to have a conversation with someone across the table. So, no, don't go in there expecting to have a private conversation without someone overhearing it. 

We got the fried chicken, oxtail, deviled eggs and fried green tomatoes. In all I think that it was average. Not worth the 40 minute wait that we had. Would I come back? Maybe.

The staff was really friendly so that was a plus! It's busy so please be patient with them.

Good old fashioned American barbeque. Excellent service, great food, the atmosphere is old school and comfortable. This restaurant has so much class. 
I believe it's Sacramento's best hidden secret. 
I don't know why it took me picking up an order to hear about this place. 
I went back after I delivered the customers order because the food smelled so good. 
Recommended 1000 percent. 
Great place to eat!

Great flavor, great temperature, great crunch, great portion sizes. I thoroughly enjoyed the catfish bites and pimento cheese dip. Grateful for friendly service as this was my first visit here. Looking forward to visiting again soon.
Food tastes like my granny made it. Service was so professional that it made me for get about our long wait.  Ambiance is clean and fresh
Great food. Chicken is like your grandma made. The greens are just right and the gumbo and biscuits are excellent

The chicken was amazing, I recommend the dark meat.
Make sure you get a order of wings and ask for the sauce on the side, the sauce goes with everything.
Lastly the grits was on point, make sure you add cheese !!

Definitely will be back

Great atmosphere and vibe. The staff is customer focused! The food is very tasty. They offer a nice selection of custom drinks.

I had the chicken and waffles with a side of macaroni and cheese.  It was good.

I recommend this restaurant.

2/6/2021 What a disappointment this evening was! I finished a round of golf and only had a sandwich all day. I was hungry. I ordered an 8-pc fried chicken dinner while still at the golf course about 7 minutes away. I arrived about 40 minutes after I ordered (5pm) and my order was ready and handed to me within a minute. I couldn't wait to get home where white rice and black-eyed peas were already prepared. The chicken was packed  in a medium size aluminum tray and still very warm. They still looked crispy and were. But the disappointment came in the lack of flavor and dry. There was no way the chicken was brined for 24hrs as they claim. I honestly don't think they were brined at all. There wasn't a hint of salt nowhere in the meat, skin, or breading for that matter. The breasts were so dry, it was difficult to swallow. We had their fried chicken about three weeks ago and it was so good. You could taste the salt brine throughout the meat; so I don't know what happened this time. What's more disappointing is the fact I spent over $33.00 on plain fried chicken and not on what they advertised. I think I'm being generous with the three stars!

This is by far the best restaurant in Sacramento. Their food is to die for. I literally am speechless because of how overwhelmingly good everything was. The hosts and servers were super nice too. They give you a giant mason jar of crisp ice water. The butter was this like whipped blueberry spread that was so good. The chicken was moist and fried to perfection. The coleslaw was citrusy and wonderful. The Mac and cheese was *chef's kiss*. I can't wait to go back and try more of the menu and some of their adult kool-aid drinks.

Zero stars. Made a reservation with confirmation number and they didnt have it, and even with photo of reservation from and confirmation number they told us two other people booked at the same time who came in after us would be seated first. We came from out of town and looked forward to trying it but decided to leave.

I am glad that I found this place. I LOVE soul food. I live for the holidays when the gumbo pot would be busted out. But, I digress. 

The ambiance of the place is fantastic. It reads good ol Southern vibes with the brick walls and rustic lighting. Our waitress, Toni, was a sweetie pie! She was a wee absent-minded and forgot to give us plates and utensils but overall gave good service. 

The food was so flavorful. Not too sweet and not too bland. Everything was seasoned appropriately. I had a great experience and recommend that those who are new to soul food try this place.

I was there yesterday food was so good for my first time I tried fish sandwich with salad it was delicious plus fill my stomach plus I like their server they are nice and friendly person also I will love to come back again and try some good delicious food there and it was my first time there. I will tell my friends and family about this restaurant.

Today was first time eating at Fixins Soul Kitchen. Quality food and customer service. If you are in Sacramento and want fried chicken, this is the place to go. The staff was very friendly and was able to seat us earlier than expected despite the long waiting list. Our server had such a great personality and even did so well with getting us everything we needed on top of having to wait on multiple other tables. The owner (Mayor Johnson) was there today and he was greeting all of the customers, checking up on the tables and even assisting with food service. He even thanked us on the way out.
The mission statement is really evident in the way he and his employees treat their customers. What this restaurant does for the community is so beautiful and I'm so happy that my family and I were able to support this local business.

I've always wanted to go here because the pictures looked so yummy on Yelp. I'm happy to report that this place is worth all the hype it gets. From the second we walked in, the staff were sooo inviting and friendly. They offer indoor & patio/outdoor seating. Side note--the music that they play is my type of music, so that's a plus. I ordered the 2 piece fried chicken. It was sooo crispy and juicy; and it's so perfect with hot sauce. One of the best fried chickens I've ever had. I also got collard greens and Mac n cheese. The collard greens were definitely my favorite thing there, but still everything tasted so good. I also loved that they have Kool Aid on the drink menu. Brought me back to my childhood, and I forgot how great & refreshing they taste. The prices are fair for the amount and quality of food you get. The service was quick & we didn't come across any issues. I would totally come back to try more items.

This place was wonderful the first time I went on the weekend. But when I went during the week, the food wasn't as tasty, but still good. The koolaid idea was bomb. Fixins Soul Kitchen is my place to go every time I'm in Sacramento.
Great location. Absolutely delicious. Beautiful presentation. The friendliest staff. Will most definitely be back!

Sean was a great server on top of everything! Attentive friendly and empathetic. Good can't put fresh and quickly. Tried ox tails for the first time bomb af plus lots to take home the martini was perfect as usual love  it. My daughter cleared her plate at love her chocolate chip cookie dessert. Definitely try this place food is amazing

Supporting our neighborhood, my sweetheart & I rode our bikes to Fixins on a Friday night. There was a line out the door already, but there's an app via Yelp that updates you on your place in line. #13 on Friday the 13th! We walked down to OPBrewing while we waited. 
Fixins texted us that our table was ready, and we ambled over. It was LOUD, with so many people, but a beautiful space with bar seating, tables, and some booths. We sat down and chit-chatted about our evening plans. Fifteen minutes passed, and I noted that two other tables had been seated and received drinks while we were still wondering who our waitstaff was. 
We flagged down Jenna, who was very sweet and distressed our person hadn't materialized. She took our order and looked after us the rest of the time we were there. Here's the break down. 
Note: My date is a culinary-institute trained chef; I'm just a picky eater. 
Fried Chicken: delightful, great crispy skin and juicy meat
Potato Salad: solid but not a stand-out
Black-Eyed Peas: good side dish, great flavor
Gumbo: a bit salty, not enough heat
Fried Deviled Eggs (not pictured): absolute artistry, a stand-out for presentation and novelty, perfect execution and delicious
Fixins also serves breakfast, and so we will definitely be back to try that as well. 
Overall a solid 4/5.
We never did find out who our assigned waitstaff was supposed to be...but they made it right.

This is my go to brunch place for chicken & waffles! Bombbb. I also love their Sactown Mango Lemonade.
Service is great and they play good music while you are eating out door on the patio :)

Fixins is goooood! The fried chicken, the catfish, the sides (all of them), the deviled eggs, the corn muffins -  all tasty. The service is typically on point too!  You get decent portions to say the least. 

I love what Kevin Johnson has done in Oak Park as far as bringing jobs to the community and making that corner super cute and appealing to the eye. 

I'm hoping Fixins will add some VEGAN options soon! A lot of people are trying to eat healthy so need establishments like this to open up their perspective to this and come through with some options!
If you want good southern dishes this is the place!  The fried chicken is so crispy and moist!!   The greens were spot on!!   I highly recommend reservations because they do get very busy!!!

I absolutely love this place. I went when it wasn't busy so that was nice to get to be catered to like that. Our server really gave us her honest opinion about different things on the menu. I am giving this place 5 starts because the flavors in their food remind me of New Orleans which you never get in California so this was definitely exciting. I love the drinks. So far I think my favorite on the menu is the gumbo with cornbread plus 2 biscuits on the side

One of the best restaurants we have eaten at Sac. We had a hard time deciding which item to order, but ended up with the catfish, pork chop, and chicken and waffles. Everything we ordered was delicious, including all the sides. The place itself is very modern and clean. Staff was super friendly. We went in a Saturday at around noon and had a 20 minute wait. Will definitely return!

I Had been hearing alot of mixed reviews about this place so I had decided to come and make my own assessment..I was highly disappointed to say the least. The macaroni and cheese was warm and dry, the hot link was dry as if it had been sitting in the fridge overnight and to add insult to it you have purchase a piece of bread separately I mean who serves a hot Link with no bread. Also we had tried the chicken which was overcooked the only positive was the bland rice and gravy..They dont ever have to worry about me coming back here.
Delicious food! My order arrived early and the chicken was perfect, tender and juicy. The Mac and cheese tasted homemade as did the biscuit. I can't wait to try the oxtails next!

Fried catfish was seasoned well, Mac was pretty good, beans &rice was good, and the cornbread was perfect just how it's made in the south. 

We will return next time we visit SAC.
My first time at Fixins Soul Kitchen....loved it! A lot of love put into that food! It's a must have!

good service 
are server was amazing kind and great 
food was great I suggest the mac very rich and creamy are server was -kronk and was very nice the ambiance was good aswell

An overall beautifully delicious experience, absolutely amazing food, seriously I never knew okra could taste so flavorful.  Seriously EVERYTHING was next level good.  Best service ever, from the host (shout out to Domonic who perfectly managed the Saturday night crowd) to the waiter who had the perfect balance of fun and professionalism.  Oh and did I mention the atmosphere?  Live dj, spinning great tunes, honestly the visit had this trifecta effect leaving my stomach, heart and soul all full of joy, gratitude and a strong desire to return.   

I honestly wish I would have taken a picture of my plate, but truthfully I was so shocked by how fast my food arrived (and by how appetizing it looked), that I honestly forgot all about my phone until it was too late.  The final shocker was the price point, I searched the bill twice to make sure nothing was accidentally omitted.  The perfectly portioned plates (be prepared to take home leftovers), come with a price tag that will warm your soul.  Thank you Fixins for doing Soul Food right...prioritizing incredibly delicious food with quality customer service!
First time, food was Super Bomb! Q was very helpful and hecka cool! The vibe was casual/social with good music playing. We left there feeling good and over full!

This is a great place in Oak Park. I normally take my out-of-town friends here as a way to impress them lol.

I usually get the chicken with mac and cheese and veggie sides. Sweet tea is my drink and if I'm ambitious I will end the meal with banana pudding.

The wait staff is really nice and attentive. The vibe is pretty nice. Not too quiet but not too loud. It's perfect.  It's also really cool to see KJ walking around and saying "hi" to the customers.
I'm never disappointed! I tried the fried green tomatoes for the 1st time and they were super delicious! Whenever I'm in town and want some oxtails I go here! Their catfish is bomb too!

Best soul food I've had in a while.  Just happened to be in Sac visiting some friend and we decided to eat here because most places were closed Christmas Eve.  Let me tell you, I'm glad we did because the food was amazing.  I haven't had fried fish that good in a really long time.  The yams were excellent and not to sweet like most places.  The greens were delicious and also had a vegetarian option which I appreciated because my wife and kids are vegetarian.  I had to order more catfish nuggets because My kids devoured them.  And lastly the Mac and cheese was perfect balanced flavor with the right amount of cheesiness.  We I pass through Sac again I'm definitely stopping by.

Lunch was catered at my work place South Sacramento Cancer center by a patient who's family owns this business. I was instructed to call Brian the manager and he would take care of everything! Brian was a delight to talk with he was very knowledgeable and very accommodating! He personally delivered lunch for the entire office everything was hot and delicious! He brought fried chicken, catfish nuggets, collard greens, sweet candied yams, macaroni and cheese and cornbread muffins! Everything was fresh it was hot it was appetizing it was some of the best soul food I have ever had! I highly recommend this restaurant for their customer service,  their friendliness, and  their  appetizing , delicious and perfectly prepared food!! I look forward to actually experiencing the entire atmosphere inside of the restaurant! Thank you again Brian Rhee and the entire family!!

I had the oxtail plate. It was so good, falling off the bone. Sauce was flavorful. The entire plate was great. They were not stingy with the meat. The greens were also flavorful. Can't wait to go back again and try another dish.

Ever since they have opened, they have exceeded expectations with food and service. Fried Green Tomatoes are my all time favorite for starters. They're perfectly cooked to a crisp and the flavor is immaculate. Please give it a try! My brother doesn't even like tomatoes but he loves the fried green tomatoes and to add to that... I've been sleeping on the Catfish Sammy with Fries!!!!! It was so delicious, I ate the whole thing and this was a BIG sandwich. Mmmm mm MMM! Also you can never go wrong with the kool aid! Shout out to the chefs for really throwin it down each time!

After a long anticipated wait of wanting to try this place out, My 5 year old son and I came here on Friday night. With outdoor seating only available were  quoted a 30 min wait ( we got sat in about 20, not bad for a Friday night ) the hostess was so nice ! She brought my little guy over some crayons and paper to keep himself occupied while we waited on Our table. We ordered the catfish plate to share ( that comes with 1 fillet of fish, 2 sides and a hush puppy like balls) and some extra sides and corn bread ! It was sooooo yummy! My 5 year old ( who can be a picky eater ate almost all of my catfish ) my favorites were definitely the catfish and the Mac n cheese was to die for ! The greens were sooo tasty as well!  It most definitely fed my soul ! I also ordered a lavender lemon drop that was amazing !  We will definitely be back to try out some other dishes. Can't wait

I don't give out a lot of 5 stars but this is a no doubt about it. First thing we noticed was the great (not just good) service. We were seated quickly, took our drink order and made sure we didn't have any questions. Excellent fried chicken. The greens were good. Mac and cheese was as good as home. You will not leave thirsty - the sweet tea and kool aid serving is HUGE. Easily the best friend chicken I've had that wasn't cooked by my mom...
Amazing food, amazing service, and great prices.  26$ for the best oxtails, meat fell right off the bone!

Only giving three stars because of the service. The good was great! Our server was really nice at first but then took 20-30 minutes to bring water to the table. She stopped by 2 times to remind us she would be bringing the waters. I understand they were a little busy, but not busy enough to not bring water by. Great food and ambiance, but I am disappointed in the service. I had been one before and was impressed, but not so much this time.
I got takeout and it was absolutely delicious! Fried chicken was on point! The macaroni and cheese is perfection! I can't wait to try the other things on the menu. Yum
Excellent customer service, excellent food, clean, friendly staff, very reasonably priced. My new favorite soul food spot!

Great service and ambiance.  Food is not cooked to order.  Plate came out in 30 seconds and chicken was dry and hard.  How do you ruin dark meat?  Sides hit on flavor.  Greens could have been more tender.  Even for downtown LA, not a good value if food isn't on point; which it wasn't!  I'm probably spoiled and setting the bar too high by comparing to my wife's cooking.

Best restaurant in Sacramento. We came for the first time yesterday and are back again today. 
The chicken plate and sides are the way to go... get to try a lot of everything. I also recommend the sweet tea. Half sweet half unsweetened and it is perfection. 
Don't miss out on this spot!!!

Gumbo was so bad I couldnt believe it was from fixins. 
Definitely did not taste like a gumbo at all more of a thick gravy over chicken pieces and rice , I definitely wouldn't recommend. I've had the ox tail and smoothered pork chops in the past that was really good to the point  I'm surprised the gumbo was so bad. It's not really a gumbo it's more of a chicken and rice smoothered in Gravy, I thought gumbo was more of a soup.

Today was my first ever visit to Fixins Soul Food, and I was not disappointed! Everything was amazing from the hospitality to the food (not to mention the entire ambiance of the establishment)! Incredible service 10/10. I left more than satisfied and I will be back very soon!!!

The service was ok the Host was a teenager who was very rude advised the manager of her unprofessionalism. He responded that they hire neighborhood youth/young adults. But we need to train the youth/young adults to be courteous and professional at all times or if they are having a bad day need to less with the public.
The food was not so flavorful. My guests felt their fried chicken was not cooked all the way but had more fried crust then chicken meat.
Dessert are very minimal. Like a flute glass I was disappointed with my banana pudding sample no banana or vanilla cookie just custard. Kool Aid was so good  
The ambiance is very nice  clean and modern. Overall it's not worth the wait or price. I may go 1-2 a year. Very disappointed

Every time I go here the food and service are amazing!!! This last time Kevin Johnson's aunt stopped by our table!  What a treat, she was so funny and fun!  The mac and cheese is the best!  The menu offers a lot so come prepared to eat hearty!!!

So me and my family decided to try this restaurant today. My daughter had a taste for soul food. I ordered the fried catfish with the side of greens and red beans and rice. One of my daughters had the shrimp and grits. He other had the oxtail with a side of greens. And my guy had the smothered pork chops with a side of greens, and okra. With a side of cornbread. So starting with my food. The greens were ok, better when you added hot sauce. The red beans and rice had no flavor whatsoever. He catfish breading was falling off the fish. My daughter's shrimp and grits were really runny. Had lots of oil on the side of the bowl. The pork chops had no flavor. Even with the gravy. The oxtail was mostly bone very little meat. Was really tough not tender at all. I thought I'd like this place. But I probably won't be returning. The waitress was nice. The restaurant is decorated nice.

When the place first opened the food was so good, now that they've been open for awhile the quality to me has just gone down tremendously. The chicken tasted old, & greasy! The oxtails tasted like they were frozen and de thawed. I give 4 stars because the waiters & waitresses are so welcoming and super kind! They were attentive the whole time and tried there best to make sure you enjoyed your visit. If the quality of food matched the service the restaurant would be grade A

I went there for the first time today and it was absolutely delightful. The staff was VERY nice, the food came quickly and it was hot and delicious. Hat's off to the chef. They know how to cook well and know how to properly use spices. I could not finish my food while there, so I took the rest home. The food was even good cold. I HIGHLY recommend this restaurant.

I have only gotten food here to go so I can't really say much about the staff or service really. But, if you haven't tried it, then you cook with only salt and even that is too spicy for you sometimes. But everyone else... Do not order to share. You're not going to want to.

I am never one to write a review, but the experience I had was ridiculous. Wanted to try something new for a date night, ended up coming here and instantly greeted with a sad faced hostess with a little attitude. Once we were seated, service from waitress wasnt too bad, I ordered a Caeser salad adding shrimp (only 3 small pieces for $7) and my boyfriend got the chicken and waffles, the waffle was mushy and didn't look good presentation wise (mushy and small) after eating that chicken he knew something was wrong and he went home and immediately threw it all up. That wasnt the main issue, the main issue is that Fixins added a FORTY DOLLAR TIP to my boyfriend's card when we left TEN DOLLAR CASH TIP. When we called them back trying to figure out what happened, they said they will leave a message for the manager..... Honestly very very disappointed in the service and quality of the food, maybe we caught you guys on a bad day, but we love trying out new places but definitely will not be back or recommend this place to anyone.
I had two adult koolaid drinks they were good . My food was so delicious ! When my family comes to visit I will bring them here. I was so full after too it was great.
Fixins is hands down the best restaurant in Sacramento for good vibes, great atmosphere and delicious mouth watering food!! Ask for Maia, she is amazing an amazing server
The cool aid slush is good that's about it for me !! I wish I could say more but unfortunately I can't the food is not great

FANTASTIC!! The food here is out of this world! I had the smothered pork chops, red beans and rice and mac&cheese. My friend had the fried chicken and said it was awesome. The staff were very professional and courteous. Food came out at a surprisingly good pace. We can't wait to go back and try some of the other items. Thanks

The food was seasoned well and just delicious  
It is worth the wait!!! The Mac and cheese was my favorite.
The wait was super long due to COVID regulations (1.5 hours) but the food was amazing so it was well worth it. Everything was so flavorful and tasty. The service was great as well.

been wanting to try this spot for a while & i finally did today 10/30/23. i can see parking being an issue, but it was fine for me. ambiance cool. seems like it's more seating outside than inside though. service trash honestly & i even sat at the bar so idk what that was about. the fried green tomatoes i enjoyed. i also the catfish meal with whatever them balls were & mac & cheese & greens. catfish was very good. greens were an absolute no for me. mac & cheese just okay, seemed a lil off. & them balls if dint even try. seemed a bit overpriced for the meal versus the portions. overall, i would try this place again, but wouldn't go out the way for it.

First time here and all I can say is absolutely delicious,   so flavorful the ox tails were so tasty,  so many delicious ingredients in this dish..
This restaurant is definitely going on one of my top selections...

Good atmosphere.  Sat at the bar on back to back nights.  Great place to hang out. But the food another story.  Got shrimp and grits and it was extremely loaded with brown gravy and tasted awful. Previous night had catfish and Mac and cheese balls.  Fish was so so and Mac and cheese was ok. Being from the south , this soul food is not the best at all.  Needs a lot more southern soul to it.

Ordered well done burger got in done burger ,I'm no vampire ,hadron wait 15 extra minutes because of order back up ,please check your Togo orders because this is unacceptable.The other food was ok they cheated us on fries with sandwiches.

Have been to two locations for this restaurant.  I enjoyed the one in Sacramento better than the LA location.  It could have been the crowd.   At the LA location, I had to sit outside.  Not good with homeless people walking by.  Nonetheless, best shrimp and grits I have ever had. The gravy on the grits set it apart from other shrimp and grits meals.  Biscuits and strawberry butter was excellent and the corn bread was very good.  Others items I have had were either a NO or just OK.  People have different taste, so try it, you may like it !  It gets 4 stars for the shrimp and grits alone!

I am a true southerner from Louisiana. I went to Fixins on May 19 with family from Louisiana. The food was absolutely horrible. I am not sure how anyone would think that food was true southern tradition. I wanted to actually show the chef how to make smothered pork chops with a roux and not packaged gravy. The customer service was nice however I will not be returning.
Good so bomb highly recommend everything was delicious. The chicken was perfectly seasoned and was not greasy and the fish was too bomb

So glad we decided to try this place! everything we ordered was sooo good and the service was amazing. Candied yams was a showstopper, portions were generous and quick too! 10/10 will definitely be coming back

Very rude staff, no customer service, no smile, bad attitude, bland food, people stare at you an don't smile an say welcome or nothing, I came all the way from Vallejo to eat their food with a 4.6 when it really was a 1.6... people lined up outside crowded waiting for their food when it wasn't all that great, the catfish could've been more seasoned an fried, yams was garbage slimy no flavor an no sugar

I literally love this place. The atmosphere is so fun.... a real chill vibe. The food is amazing. This time I had the fried chicken, Mac. And cheese and the greens. The chicken was seasoned to perfection. Mac and cheese I like soft cheesy pillows in your mouth. Soooo good. The greens were...,meh. The banana pudding was bomb but it's super small for the price. I had a 5 year old with me who ordered chicken and waffles and she ate everything on her plate. My absolute favorite thing is the lavender lemon drop. It is better than at any fine dining restaurant that I've ever had one at. I continue to recommend this place to anyone that will listen.

The wait for this place is unreal. 

Tables are TINY. We literally have a 6 people in our party including 2 kids. 

We waited for 1 hour almost, and an additional 30 minutes just waiting in front. We put ourselves on the wait list. 
Food is amazing, staff is great. 
My son got the waffle and he wasn't a fan because of the cornmeal. But i honestly loved it. 
I just wish they can accommodate with bigger tables. We sat outside no biggie for us, but we sat under trees that were poking us the whole time.

I Love the food and the Biscuits with that Jelly is to die for lol
Area was cool never a problem finding parking !!

Super happy to come to Fixins for our first time!  Great food. We had the catfish, gumbo, chicken, and a bunch of sides. The peach cobbler was amazing!!!  And, they played some pretty cool jams (Lisa Lisa and the Cult Jam, Michael Jackson, 112.... play some Prince and I'll come back!) Happy Holidays!!!

Update:  JC, our waitress, told us that they  play Prince quite a bit during the week, so we will be back. I think they were taking a Prince break...  Thanks for being so kind, JC!!!

Chicken here is amazing! The other items I tried were not quite as amazing, but still good! I ordered online and ended up having to wait an extra ten or so minutes past my pick up time and the food is a bit pricey, but it's well worth the time and money! 

The fried chicken is unlike any other fried chicken I've had. I had to drive a little over twenty minutes to get home but the chicken was still very crunchy, juicy, and loaded with flavors. The waffle was pretty soggy since it was under the chicken and definitely not on par with the chicken, but it still worked with the chicken. The menu does note that the waffle is best consumed hot and doesn't travel too well. 

I was not a huge fan of the fried catfish. I personally didn't like the cornmeal crust, but my friend seemed to really enjoy it so it just depends on what you like! 

The corn fritters were a nice sweet side to have with all of the food! I highly recommend trying the corn fritters if you're already getting food!!

Great authentic southern style food. My dad can't get enough of the collard greens. Reminds him of when he was younger. My husband loves the grits and biscuits. Great service, friendly staff. Love to support local.

I was shocked at how good everything was. I ordered the fried chicken with sides of mac and cheese and potato salad. It was all perfect. It comes with a biscuit, but for $2 I switched it for cornbread w/honey chipotle butter. Superb. The lemonade--served in large mason jars--was spot on. I tried my dining companion's cheesy grits and they were so smooth and cheesy. Huge portions! I brought home enough for dinner tomorrow. Can't wait to go back!

Great southern style cooking. The fried chicken is crispy and moist. The catfish is large enough to take leftovers home. The service ok when you can wave one down. Seems like they are assigned a few too many tables to be able to be attentive to all. Decor is nice. Keep Oak Park viable. Give them a try you will love the food. Great meeting place.

A bit of a wait but worth it. Delicious food, sides were all great.  We got the "better bugger", 3 piece fried chicken, smothered chicken, kids bugger, rice and beans, collard greens, and black peas for sides.  Sides where all wonderful, the collards greens in particular where super tasty, springy, a bit of spice and a little sweet.  Seasoned fries for the kids meal was a good extra touch.  Kool-aid pickles were interesting, but not my favorite.

Soul food at its finest we tried pork chops gravy w yams & black eyed peas, oxtails rice & greens we were not disappointed thank you to Aaliyah for excellent customer service and and Kevin Johnson for the picture.

Had the chicken and waffles the waffles were light soft and full of flavor, delicious I tell you the chicken was very good and we were welcomed at our table by Auntie and felt like home please support great business and great food

I don't write many reviews but this has to be said.  I ordered the gumbo with shrimp for about $25.00.  It looked like gravy.  I tasted it and it tasted like gravy.  It was very salty.  I looked through a couple of pictures on Yelp and found that the Shrimp and Grits gravy was very similar.  There were three shrimp and three small pieces of chicken thigh in the Gumbo served to me.   There was no visible andouille sausage, it was very sparse in meat and flavor.  What I had was not gumbo.  I came from out of town so I did not complain but I hope the owners rethink the gumbo option on the menu and actually serve gumbo and not gravy.  I really feel cheated.
When you need good, comforting, down home food, there is no better place.  Great drinks, amazing staff.

The drinks are great, Mac and cheese is awesome. First time trying their fried green tomatoes and fried deviled eggs. Soooo good.  I always get their fried chicken, yams, and greens.  Such good food!  Probably the best would food spot in Sacramento, definitely the most consistent.
The food was great and our server was very nice and helpful! The restaurant had a great ambiance.
I have been coming here for about 3 years now. The quality of the food is really good. But I will say the quantity of food is a lot smaller.

The service is SAAAAAAALOW! We did go on a Sunday. Why does it take an hr to get some SALTY eggs, SALTY grits, and SALTY potatoes? I might sound SALTY, but this ain't it! I'm going to try it one more time on a week day, but it won't be soon! The music is so loud that you can't hear the waiters, but it might be the building. TURN DOWN THE MUSIC!

Warning! Long review coming up...

Got here on a Sunday evening and the wait was about 30mins. Not too bad. Checked in the front and gave my number to get a text when table was ready. While my boyfriend and I was waiting outside.. these ladies walked out from the restaurant and was like "Yall!! Dont eat here! Food is naaasstty!" We were like whhaaattt?!! Forreals? The lady bust out laughing and was like "I'm playing! The food here is hella bomb! LOL man... she got us good. We believed her for a min. She and her friends started telling us what they got and told us how bomb the kool aid was that she had to get one to go. And funny thing is, while we were waiting, we were looking at the menu.. my boyfriend and I both agreed we wasnt gonna get a kool aid for $4! Nahhh we can make it at home. But after she said that.... shoot, we had to reconsider! 

Finally got a text message to let us know we were ready to be seated. Walked in and shortly, our server Damiano greeted us and took our order for 2 kool aids  he got the purple with lemonade and I got pink with lemonade. Dude.... ya need to get that shit! Lol it was good and it was on point!

My bf ordered the catfish platter with collard greens and mac and cheese. I got 2pc chicken plate with mac and cheese with potatoe salad. Our food came out pretty quick! The catfish was super crispy and hot. It stayed hot for a very long time too after getting our food. And my chicken! It was hot, juicy, tender and crispy! I got a drum and a breast. My bf enjoyed the collard greens & hes pretty picky about his greens.  The mac and cheese was good. I'd give it 4/5. The potatoe salad.. if you dont like onions, DONT GET IT!!! it was a lot of onions. Every bite, youd taste onions. I dont mind onions so it was cool. It wasnt bad but I just wouldnt get it again next time. I almost forgot to mention how bomb the cinnamon butter is that comes with the biscuit!!! It reminds me of the butter at texas roadhouse and that shit is BOMB.COM 

Damiano was very attentive and great! All the staff here seem super friendly and helpful. Kevin Johnson came by our table and asked how everything was - it was wonderful! We will definitely be coming back and trying some other things!

This hit the spot! Excellent staff and amazing comfort food. Loved everything; tender juicy fried chicken, the oxtail was delish!  Will definitely come back to try other things on the menu. I recommend the bourbon sweet tea.
The food is fire. You'd think the price is too much, but the portions and the taste are worth it.

This is our "go to" when we want to grub. The portions are generous and the food is delicious.  I always get the Ox tails while my wife and son get the fried chicken. The fried deviled eggs, Mac n' cheese, cornbread, red beas and rice, and the greens are staples when we partake.  Sweet tea, blue, and red Kool-aid are our libations of choice.  We never leave disappointed.  Sleepy maybe but never disappointed.

First time here after seeing a review on Instagram. Learned its black found and owned. Highly suggest getting a reservation because it stayed full the entire time we were there. The waitress Vanessa was AMAZING! She came at the right times to see how were doing and if everything was ok. Didn't have to ask for a spoon for my boyfriend's gumbo or extra napkins. She was very knowledgeable of the menu and asked if we had an allergy for shrimp as the gumbo was made with shrimp stock. She even helped us with merchandise we purchased (shirts and hot sauce). Now on to the food! The chicken has great flavor and moist with crunchy skin. Biscuit was sooo good! Gumbo has great flavor. Boyfriend thought the charred okra was good. Drinks were great. Loved that they played old-school R&B jams (90s - 2000s). Well lit inside; seating in booths, tables and at the bar. Very clean and the other workers were friendly. Will be back again!
Food was fresh and delicious. My order was to go so it was a little bit of a wait but worth it.

My friend from Sacramento introduced me to this place outside of the downtown area. Living in the Midwest and having family in the South, my expectations were low. But oh, boy! Fixins has been the closest thing to southern food since I started my Yelp career. The fried chicken is at the right wavelength of crispy, and this characteristic did not compromise the flavor. Once you finish up, throw in that delicious biscuit, and your body will thank you. The southern comfort did not stop there; the service was impeccable, with staff attending to our every need and adding genuine personality to the mix.

From starters to dessert. Everything on the menus are AMAZING! We tried the Tulsa location off a recommendation from a colleague. We even had the pleasure of meeting Mr. Kevin Johnson. The food at the Sacramento location we hit up everytime we are at the Capital for meetings.

Food was amazing. We had the fried green tomatoes, fried chicken (with gravy,) okra, collard greens, fries, and biscuits. Everything we ate was so good. The service here was attentive and friendly. We came in of course to eat, but also to watch the 49ers game and hopefully charge our phones since our power was out. We charged our phones and when we were paying our server made it a point to let us know that we were fine hanging out if we wanted and that they were no longer seating anyone in the area we were in. She didn't have to go out of her way to tell us that staying a while was an option, but really made sure we knew we could which was really kind. Definitely not a "bully" in my book!

Clean good service nice atmosphere , But lets talk about the food.
OX tail and I also got a kids meal with one piece of chicken and mac and cheese we also had the spicy chicken sandwich everything we ordered was great and the portion was good on all the meals we ordered.
I had a good time here. The food tasted good. Drinks were okay. I can't say I got an amazing experience here, but all in all, I left happy. Will be back.

Fix n my soul and my tummy! Love their fried catfish! Fried to perfection, light crispy and airy. Great choices of side fixes too. Love that it's in our new neighborhood of oak park. Us females coming from San Francisco. So that is a big comparison! We also tried their sister spot across, Oak park brewery! Both amazing and fun! Love this place! Only thing is we're not big drinkers anymore but they have a nice huge selections of beers and nice big bar with fun cocktails. 

*The LOCAL femme lesbOS Frm THE bay.

My favorite soul food place in sac. Staff always remembers me when I come in I always order the same meal the chicken with mac and cheese and greens so yummy I especially love the butter that comes with the biscuits. Restaurant is always clean and the employees are always super sweet

I'm so glad I found this place. I lived in Mississippi for a few years, and other southern states and LET ME TELL YOU.. this is the real deal. Their yams. The Mac and cheese. The chicken. 
This is literally my happy place in Sacramento when it comes to food. Love them!
I can't stop going back. My family and I love the food the people and ambience. God bless you!

I ordered 2 legs with mac and cheese and greens and roll. The chicken was crispy & juicy. The mac & cheese is so cheesy and tasty. The greens were good too. The roll had like a purple cream cheese side and crispy at the bottom. It was warm and sweet. This is a true soul food restaurant.
Everything was great! The chicken was just super wet and a bit to much for me but personal the sides and lemonade was excellent!

Ambience (5/5): Went to get dinner on Mon night as I was craving fried chicken. Really cool space in Oak Park and they do have an outside patio but that space wasn't open yet. We put our name on the waitlist and while they said 30 min wait, we were able to just sit directly at the bar right away. Glad to see new eateries in Oak Park!

Food (3.5/5): Ordered fried tomatoes, 3 piece fried chicken and for the sides mac+cheese as well as fries. There was no option for spicy/hot style friend chicken, so taking .5 star off for that. The serving size of the appetizer was small but the dinner plate was plenty. Chicken was well fried but could've had more seasoning.

Service (4/5): Bartended took our order but we were served for the kitchen. Took a little bit of time for the food to come out.

Value (3.5/5): Appetizer wasn't quite worth the price but the dinner plate was.

Overall Rating: 3.5/5

Super disappointed in this place...I have been here a few times, but today was horrible...the host started off by lying stating that the booths were only for groups of 5 or more and sat my group of 4 at a table.  As we sat, we saw several groups of two seated one after the other at a booth. When my group asked to be sat in the next open booth, we were told we could not because they were booked for people with reservations. We explained that we did have a reservation, they then changed it and said that we couldn't be moved at that point for reasons that made no sense. The manager semi-apologized for his staff that lied but did not fix the issue. I had to ask how we would be compensated and he reluctantly said we could get 15% off our bill. ...This was very poor and disappointing management and hosting...I decided not to spend any money there that day....and probably won't in the future...
Good food, just could have been a little hotter. Service was ok. Recommend making a reservation.
Great fried chicken! Great vibes, a little pricey but worth. The biscuit and butter is a must get.

Dine in was 2 hours so long wait however, we did go on a Sunday after church.
after for the take-out order was 40 minutes quick considering in dine wait time. We were surprised about the portion sizes too small for price. we ordered fish we had one large piece of fish.  Food was not well season. just OK but I am an old-school cook. so SALT was missing too much HOT taste food and everyone's sides were the same.  For example, the greens cut well cooked well hot and taste not in temperature need more onion badly. The Black Eyed Peas needed garlic and onion and salt but the hot taste as well.  seasoning very very important. I do not like my macaroni and cheese hot to the taste I think that's one area where it should taste more buttery and cheesy then hot. You can NOT put the same flavors on every single food, and expect that all of the food is going to taste differently.
 it is going to start tasting the same black pepper, cayenne pepper, hot sauce, or something like that and every side that we ate. this was pretty good food. Not sure if I could say I'll go back, but if I do go back. not sure how long they've been in business. Maybe I'll try them again in six months and update my review.

I really want to give the restaurant more than 4-stars but I just can't. The food was great and they had some of the best greens I've ever had, tender, flavorful and still intact (not at all mushy). The oxtail dish was flavorful and melt in your mouth but the portion of meat and rice was super small for a dish that is over $20. I'll be back to try some other dishes but not sure how long they can stay in businesses with their prices/portions in Oak Park.

As a vegetarian going to a BBQ place, I did not have high expectations. We had two orders of (2 eggs each) deviled eggs, a side of mac and cheese and a caesar salad. It was nearly $40 and most of it was not edible. The mac and cheese was cold and greasy, and the $13 salad was literally a cup of lettuce with some stale croutons. To say I am disappointed would be an understatement. And, I am still hungry. 

(The women running the front of house were fantastic, and they are the sole reason for the stars I gave)
Love the food and the service is great! I especially loved the fried chicken and the banana pudding!!

Went for the soft opening and had an AHHMAZING experience! Not only was the food delicious, the service, ambience, and music was excellent too! I ordered the catfish, yams, green beans and potato salad. Soo tasty and flavorful! My daughter had the chicken & waffles with strawberry butter. She didn't leave a single crumb on her plate! The homemade lemonade was perfect, simply refreshing & sweet. My daughter got the blue kool-aid and said it was THE best ever! We also had fried deviled eggs for our appetizer and devoured those in seconds! We have definitely found a new fave in the city! A 10 out of 10! 

Update: Went back to Fixins tonight. Had fried green tomatoes, fried chicken, greens, and banana pudding that were D-E-L-I-C-I-O-U-S!!

So, I guess timing is everything; especially when it comes down to dining at a popular eatery. In fact, we've been wanting and waiting to eat at this super busy  restaurant owned by Sacramento denizen, our former Mayor and Phoenix Sun - Mr. Kevin Johnson for some time now but could never get in. Today was different, as we were able to get in at 11 am on Veteran's Day (yes, they honor vets) and we got to sit inside to dine in (everything is shutting down again very soon). 

Greeters, Servers and Kitchen staff are all on point, professional and courteous. Our server Chandra did an excellent job - knowing when to visit our table and when to hang back and allow  us to eat. 

Restaurant itself was extraordinarily clean, modern and polished. Great atmosphere and positive vibes. 

Now, onto the food. 100 year old recipes - clearly curated in a purposeful and meaningful way. The food was prepared with intelligence, passion and care, honoring the roots of its origins. 

The Fried chicken, Mac & cheese, grits, potato salad, the waffles, red beans & rice ... wow. Top to bottom - Excellent. Well seasoned, perfectly cooked and balanced for the palette. I recommend the unsweetened iced tea as a beverage because the sweet iced tea ... is ... well ... sweet. LOL

To the staff: Don't change - Stay consistent - it works!

To everyone else: Go here - Order a variety of foods and eat well. You will not regret the experience nor will your stomach. 

Peace!

The food far exceeded my expectations. I ordered the 2 piece fried chicken (white meat) & I could taste the seasoning in the breading. It was crispy without being dry. The yams were sweet & had the perfect amount of cinnamon (it added flavor without overwhelming the dish). The mac n cheese was impeccable. I was impressed because usually places have good main dishes while the sides lack but everything we tried was good. Even the dessert (peach cobbler & buttermilk pie). The kool aid comes in a big ass mason jar & tastes like childhood nostalgia. My husband ordered the chicken sandwich with collard greens & red beans + rice. He was equally as impressed as I was (so much so he went back the next day). 

The service was great as well. The hostess was warm in demeanor & the wait time is tracked through yelp so I had an approximation of when we would be seated. Our server, Nate, was jovial & attentive. (I appreciate that he brought out two glasses of water for me as the mason jars were small. Efficiency was definitely noted).

The table service was great. The food was good. Only complaint is the extremely rude bartender. We put a name on the waiting list and were told we could sit at the bar until we were called. I tried to order a drink but was cut off and asked if we were eating at the bar. I replied that the hostess said we could sit there until our table was available. Again she asked if we were ordering food. I felt pressured so I said I wouldn't mind eating at the bar. She replied that I had to go take my name off the waitlist for a table before she would serve us. I got up and started to leave but wanted to give the restaurant a try. Most likely I will not be returning. Service plays a large part in a dining out experience.

Nice ambience. Good music. Warm employees.  Fixins Soul Kitchen is worthy of the trip and of all its clout.  We ordered the smothered fried chicken, smothered pork chops, oxtails, and chicken and waffles.  No complaints at all.  Every bite was a delectable and exquisite experience.  Sides included the Mac n Cheese, collard greens, candied yams, potato salad, corn bread and biscuits.  Again, a home run worth every single dollar.  I wish there was a location in Los Angeles since all that's really there is Roscoe's.  Highly recommended.

Re: Manager _ discourteous_ curt

We have always been huge fans of the Fixins brand and love the spirit of the Oak Park location. It's the people that make it special. We have never had a bad experience...until yesterday . 

The food was great, the servers were great (Desiree), the hostess, the bussers, etc - they all represent the spirit of Fixins on a Sunday afternoon. However, the manager, Brian, soured the day and was a stark contrast to all the other employees. He did NOT represent the brand well. 

Our Bay Area family chose to celebrate Nona's 78th bday here in Sacramento and Nona wanted to return to Fixins - her favorite spot in Sacramento. We made reservations for 8. We were initially seated outside, but due to the populous flies, we asked for seating indoors. Desiree, the waitress, was accommodating and pleasant and identified space inside. However, 8 of us were squished pretty tight at a table and it would've been a challenge to eat and have space for all the plates. There was a small table next to ours that would allow us to eat w/o bumping elbows. The waitress allowed us to add this table to our existing table; it was an easy and common sense solution. Desiree expeditiously handled the situation with poise and grace. We added the table and sat down. Unnecessarily, Brian (the manager) inserted himself and without any customer dialog started to move the table back! When we inquired why, he stated that it "may" already be reserved. We shared w/ him that we worked this out with our waitress and suggested that perhaps he should verify that first before requiring us to again move about while he moved the table back. Our suggestion was ignored and he proceeded to move the table back! This caused chaos not only with our group, but other guests sitting nearby. Trying not to add to the bad vide/negative energy Brian created, we 're squished' back to our original seating. Shortly after we 're-squished' Brian returned and indicated that we "could now" add the table. Note - not only was this something the server (Desiree) already established, we asked this of Brian BEFORE he decided to move the table back. This was a classic over-reach and felt very authoritative. His communication with us was rude and curt. Now ,while Brian is hurriedly moving the table back, multiple items from the table fall to the ground and a bottle of hot sauce explodes at our feet (yes - hot sauce splattered on our shoes and pants). We are fully aware that accidents happen, but this was totally unnecessary - again...all was fine until he inserted himself. Brian's desire to be in charge tainted the energy of the celebration as well. 

Instead of celebrating our Nona, we have shifted the conversation and now talking about the unprofessional way in which he handled the situation and soured the mood. 

We move on, eat (the food was great as usual) laugh and pay the $350 bill. We sing happy birthday. I ask the waitress for forks and plates for those of us wanting to have a taste of the 3 bundtinis (mini-sample size cakes) I brought. As we're packing up and saying goodbyes, in walks Brian again now stating, "that will be a $15 charge"! We inquire about the reason, and share with him that we didn't know there would be a charge. He justifies this with saying "the waitress told me she told you"; I said, "no, no she didn't". 

At some point, common sense has got to prevail. After the flies, the table incident, the hot sauce explosion and a $350 bill, is it really logical to press a celebration for our 78 year old Nona sharing mini-Bundt cakes over $15? Brian answering with "This is our policy..." and ignoring all that happened during this exchange is a clear indication that he is not capable of managing - much less leading! Then, his weak attempt to blame the server further indicates his inadequacies as a manager - that's not what leaders do. I want to stress, Desiree and every other worker on the shift that day represented Fixins well. This was 100% on Brian. 

A manager is in a role to be a decision maker. Based on our experience, he was the worst decision maker in the building. There are no excuses for this behavior. This was totally an ego thing and is not good for the business of Fixins. Brian's practice is text book "How to get a negative review"! Restaurants are about making it a great experience for customers. Brian is an outlier to every experience we've had at Fixins. Please address this. We love Fixins - but if Brian is the standard, I guarantee you will lose customers.
Amazing!!!  Will definitely be back.  Staff was super friendly and check in process was well organized.  Can't wait to bring my husband here.
The Smothered pork chop dinner was divine! And the service was great!. We had a great memorial day lunch

This place is amazing!! Service was great, food was phenomenal and atmosphere was a plus! I will be going back. Not many places can match southern food but this place is spot on. I will add this place does get busy and packed fast.
The food is good, I usually get the fried fish and the corn bread I love them both. The kool aid is delicious it's the only time I would drink it but is a good spot to get a quick soul fix!

Service was very good. The food was flavorful. The only thing I didn't care for was the cornbread. It was a bit drier than I'm used to but I know that's how some people make it. I can't wait to go back!

My family and I always eat at this place. Our fave is the oxtails rice with their flavorful gravy side of mac & cheese and yams .I also love the Shrimp and grits and the fried chicken as well. Collard Greens are also delicious. This restaurant never disappoints, I recommend this place highly

Thank you auntie pearl for the most amazing experience I have had in a restaurant in a long time. She came and welcomed us in, explained changes in the area and even checked in with us as she was leaving. Can't wait to see you again soon! Thank you for the wonderful hospitality and wonderful food.

This food was a 100/10!!! We had ordered the fried chicken and ox tail. The ox tail melts in your mouth , and the fried chicken had so much flavor . If I could leave a six star review I would. We will definitely be coming back!
The greens and delicious, gumbo is pretty good has a thick roux and the fried chicken is crispy.

So I just assumed this place was all hype because the former mayor, Kevin Johnson, is one of the owners. Boy was I wrong! The food here was amazing and I can't believe the service matched what the kitchen is putting out. All facets of my family's dining experience were A1. 
I finally made it here about two weeks back. This is during the Covid pandemic so we were seated outside. The wait was about 20 minutes, which isn't bad at all, once seated the waitress quickly introduced herself and answered our questions. I got a beer and my lady ordered a kool aid. Their kool aid came in a large mason jar and was so sweet but in a good way.  My lady said it tasted just how it did when she was a little girl at the dinner table sharing a jug with her siblings. I can attest how good it was and for $5 you really get a lot. 
I ordered the ox tails. It was my first time ever trying ox tails and I was nervous. I've seen pics and sometimes they're small and the meat can be scarce. This was not the case at Fixin's! The ox tails were full of juicy flavorful meat. That perfect combo of fat and muscle that made me fall in love. 4 very large pieces laid on a bed of rice. My side was the Mac and cheese. Those two together made me feel like I was at my great grandmother on my dad's side whom I've never even met. I would order this every time if it not for me wanting to try more of their food! The cornbread was spectacular and came with some butter to spread the lovin with. I enjoyed that. 
My 10 year old son had their chicken tenders and fries. He was in one of his moods and had told us he would not even be eating yet once the food came he was intrigued and proceeded to eat everything in sight haha. My girlfriend had the fried chicken and the little bit I had made me wish I had told her to get us some to go lol. 
I really enjoyed the food, service and ambience at Fixin's and can't wait to go back again.

Giving a three star because of the over an hour and half wait. We learned the hard way that making a reservation via open table is the best\only way to get a seat. Unaware of the reservations, we had to wait for what seemed like an eternity and watching other people get seated was hard but once we were finally given a table, every thing was great. Food came out not long after we ordered, was hot and delicious. Keyshon was an excellent server. Music was great, don't think I'll come back on a Friday without a reservation but I will be back.
Very nice and clean restaurant. The service was great, however, the food came out very slow. I guess thats what happens when you dine on a Saturday!
Was in Sacramento and stopped by Fixins for an early dinner. Arrived by 5pm and there was no wait! Everything was delicious!!
Excellent service and excellent food. Try the lemonade, they make it in house! We got the fried chicken and the catfish. No complaints at our table.

I had a reservation at 0830 pm for 2. Did not get seated until 8:50 pm. The waiter came over took our order drinks order and I ordered the friend deviled eggs. About 10 minutes after the drink order the waiter came back because she forgot, which drink one of us ordered. it then took almost 25 minutes for our appetizer to come.  I had to ask the waiter several times where it was. Her response was they gotta cook them.  she would also disappear and we would have to hunt her down. I think she had more tables than she could handle and didn't seem like she had any help. We don't know the restaurant closed at 9pm when we made the reservation. About 930 the waiter came back for our food order. The food came pretty quick in comparison to the appetizers. When we were finished with the meal I tried to order a dessert and was told the kitchen was closed. At this time the restaurant is still pretty full and at no time did our waiter notify us the kitchen was closing or that we needed to order everything up front. The food was amazing the greens were the best. Perfect amount of seasoning. I would visit again for the food and not the service.

This is hands down of the of the best restaurants, not just soul food restaurants...in Northern California. Despite it being busy for lunch, COVID precautions, and hella hot they were on they A-game. The red Kool Aid was fire (and refillable), the sweet tea was actually sweet and everything on the plate was great. Drew was on it; checked in on us, gave us recommendations, and was overall a cool dude. If you in the area and ain't been here...get there! And if you in the area but ain't tell me about it...you wrong! Lol

Everything I've tried has been good except the fried chicken and the oxtails. Both of those are not bad, just not good. The fried chicken is overcooked.
The fish is wonderful. The sides are all tasty.
Delicious! No wonder why there is always a wait to to be seated. The chicken was perfect! I'm a sucker for Mac and cheese so I had to get two orders.

Omg what took us so long to go here. The food is amazing, and the service was incredible. We had Des as our server and from beginning to end she was incredible. Had watermelon margarita and my husband has adult kool aid. Their drinks are spectacular. The greens and fried chicken with a side of gravy (suggested by Des) was sooo good. What a great way to spend my birthday with friends. The music, vibe and everything was perfect. Thanks!!
Oxtail was delicious. Fried chicken was finger licking good. Collard greens were morsels of healthy goodness!

Okay just prefacing this place is next level. I went here on a date and omg this place is worth falling in love with. The atmosphere is amazing, the staff is friendly and the food- OH MY GOD THE FOOD. That being said, I took both my leftovers and my date home with me later that day. MUST COME HERE.

I had this restaurant for the first time and I came back today. The chicken was crispy and seasoned to perfection. The waffle was perfect and the butter for the waffle was amazing. All the waiter have amazing smiles and very good energy. Not only that I felt like I had food from a elder family member. I felt warm, full and ready to tackle the day.
