Thank you to the chefs and the waiters for this amazing experience.

This was such as amazing time! Our food was delicious and our server Desiree made our experience even better!  This was our first time in Sacramento and I could not have asked for a more enjoyable time.   

I recommend you go for the food AND the Desiree experience!

Hands down best fried chicken I've ever had.  I always get the hot chicken sandwich w a side a Mac and cheese, and of course a sweet tea.  My wife gets the chicken and waffles and it's amazing.  I love my Koolaid soaked pickle too, it's a game changer.  Definitely visit this place if you haven't yet.

The first time I visited this place the food was prepared to perfection. I went back maybe like a month later and it was the same as the first visit. 2weeks ago this place crossed my mind and I decided to take the 1 hour drive thinking I was going to have a nice pleasant meal as I did the 2 times I visited there. Man........ was I wrong, the drive wasn't worth it. I went on a Sunday which was extremely busy. The food wasn't prepared as amazingly as it was the 2 times as before. I had the oxtails which looked like a big hunk of fatty meat with a side of macaroni and collard greens. All am going to say is Fixins, am going to need y'all to improve not only the food but the service as well.

#worththewait overall the food is !!! But overall service would give 41/2 stars. I wanted to order some alcoholic beverages but was never offered a menu. Also, it took our server too long to check up on us before giving us the bill and even after, we waited at least 20 minutes for her to come back with my card and we understand it was very busy! But I can tell they are still trying to figure it out. 

I'd still go back 100%!!!
Excellent food, a bit of a wait , difficult to find a parking spot. Best to pick up and eat elsewhere. Room out front for 3 cars to wait
My favorite soul food restaurant. The food is delicious and the staff members are friendly and professional.

One of the best southern restaurants I've been to outside of Louisiana! The fried chicken was perfection, gumbo was amazing and the Mac and cheese was perfectly cheesy (I miiiight judge restaurants on their Mac and cheese ). Our waitress, JC, was a doll, super sweet and all smiles. I highly recommend Fixins when you want to get down on on some grub.

We came here last Friday to pick up our takeout order that we placed via the Toast app.  (Delivery isn't an option, as we live in the Elk Grove area.). Being in the high risk group of serious illness from the dreaded virus, my family and I have been staying home, not even going to grocery stores (we only order online for delivery). But we had heard such good things about Fixins from friends and coworkers, and five months of quarantine has us missing restaurant food, so we decided to do what is risky for us: pick up an order in person.  Unfortunately, Fixins does not do curbside pickup, and they don't do contactless pickup.  I really wish they did.  When we were notified that our order was ready, my son masked up and walked up to the outside podium to let the staff person know he was there to pick up his order. There were no other customers there at the time (it was about 2:30pm and not at all busy), yet it still took at least 10-15 minutes for them to bring out our order. During that time, people were walking by, without masks, and three new customers walked up wanting to be seated, also without masks. I understand that the restaurant has no control over others, but this is why it's so important to offer curbside, contactless pickup. My son was like a sitting duck out there.  When the staff finally brought out his order, they just handed him the bag, and we drove the 25 minutes home.  When we got home, we opened the bag, wiped everything down with sanitizing wipes, and discovered one of our meals was missing, and one of our sides was missing (replaced with collard greens, which we didn't order).  We called the restaurant and explained the situation, and they were very courteous, apologetic, and said they would issue a refund for those two items.  They also said the staff should have gone through the items in the bag with us, so he would make sure to do follow up training with them.  

Now onto the food:  it was delicious!  I had the 2-piece fried chicken, and it was the best fried chicken I've had in Sacramento (even beating out South, which I also love). I split it with my son (whose meal was the one left out), and there was plenty for both of us with the sides.  Seriously, everything was fantastic.  My spousal unit loved his fried catfish.  The Mac and cheese, red beans and rice, and candied yams are definitely going to be on our next order (probably post-pandemic), as they were all scrumptious. 

I'm reluctant to order again during the pandemic because they don't have curbside or contactless pickup, and that means it's so risky for us because we really didn't like having to stand out there for so long. And the parking situation wasn't conducive, either, since there were cars parked in the marked "Take Out" area that were clearly not there for take out.  But the food here is delicious, and if you're not high risk like us, I would recommend coming here for excellent take out!

Jeezus Louizus... This place was so worth the wait. I put myself on the waitlist at 6pm and they said it would be an hour wait but I didn't get seated until 7:45pm. But I don't care because it was so worth it. 
My family and I got a variety of stuff. I had the chicken and waffles, my boyfriend the chicken sandwich, my mom the oxtails, and my dad the 2 piece with sides. The fries were so crispy. The butter made my waffles sooo good. The chicken was hot, crispy, and juicy and the oxtails were cooked to perfection. We also got the red kool-aid and a lemonade. They were both really sweet, but I prefer that over sour lemonade and watered down kool-aid. We had to get the sides of mac and cheese because of all the reviews we read and it was so yummy. The biscuit with the butter alone was so good, too!
Our server was Liya and she took such good care of us! 
I'm from Reno but will gladly be returning again even if it's only for a day trip.
Food was not good and service was quite slow, would reconsider but I found a better soul food location

I've been eyeing this place for sometime now finally was able to get over to try this spot out.wife and I were seated right away inside plenty of open tables to choose from.I ordered 3 pieces of chicken along with 2 waffles,wife ordered a chicken salad.Probably the best chicken and waffles I've had in a long time,maybe ever!Chicken is moist and tender on inside with a crispy crunchy outside.Waffles were just straight up delicious!I washed all down with sweet tea,wife loved here salad she had a (mimosa done right)easy on OJ heavy on champagne.I was stuffed but couldn't pass up the opportunity to have banana pudding.This place nailed it in terms of fresh food done right,pudding is made to order doesn't sit in a fridge for several days before someone orders it.staff here was friendly great feel to this spot,I would highly recommend for all

The chicken was wonderful!  Paired with macaroni and cheese and Cole slaw.

Highly recommend- you won't be disappointed

Even if you don't wanna come here for the food which is amazingly delicious you have to come here to see auntie Pearl!  She is a beautiful soul and makes your day 1 million times better!  We would've taken pictures but honestly we were starving and the food was so good that we annihilated it. My daughter and I started with the fish nugget appetizers with chipotle sauce!  For the main we split a three-piece chicken meal with candied yams and french fries, it was it was too much food for the both of us but we loved every bite!!!  Plus how could you not love a restaurant that has Kool-Aid!  We really wanted cobbler but we were too full. I can't wait to bring my husband back for the deep fried deviled days!!!!!!!!

Omg omg omg thee absolute Best food in town!
If you love soul food and comfort food this is the place for you!!!! 
I suggest the oxtails mac n cheeses corn bread yams and greens trust me you wont be let down!!!!

Browsing yelp looking for food, tacos actually since it's Tuesday.  Saw the reviews for this little gem and decided to get off the freeway and head in the opposite direction.  Only in town for the day.  Sat down and asked for a drink recommendation, Lavender Lemondrop YUM!
He wanted chicken and waffles and was sent to the moon, outa this world good!
I ordered the Chicken Sandwich wasn't really wanting fries so asked for more recommendations and also ordered the Mac N Chz and the Greens O M G never had better!!  Sorry Dad (rip) your greens aren't the best anymore.  
I can only say that we get so disappointed eating out that if I have a craving for something I just make it at home it's always better that way BUT today we were so surprised at the quality, flavor and service that we want to make a 2+hour drive just to come back.  Thank You Fixins, everything was awesome!

The food here is great. It's some of the better soul food you can get in this area. The service is friendly, and the aesthetic of the building is elevated soul Food. It feels like a treat to be here, but also is still good homestyle cooking. I enjoyed the sweet tea very much. I haven't had tea that good without feeling overly brewed or therapysince my trip to the south.
Had lunch here with family from out of town. Service was A+and food as well. I had the shrimp and grits and it was delicious. Environment was lively and clean. Will be back soon.

We ordered the oxtails, smothered chicken, fried green tomatoes and a blue
Kool aid.....hands down the oxtails were amazing, tender (no fork needed)....fried green tomatoes were fried perfectly( not to greasy or mushy) smothered chicken was a little on the salty side but the manager was very accommodating. I would try it again maybe when it's not so many people (we came during brunch time ) over all I was pretty pleased with my experience-I would def come back on a weekday and I believe my experience would be even better then today....

The food is AMAZING as usual. Fantastic vibe, good music and Ansar was an amazing waitress. Constantly checked up on us and made sure everything was going well.

I highly recommend Fixins to anyone living in the Sacramento area or visiting!

The place was packed when we went. Good vibe, good service food okay until I saw macaroni mixed with my collard greens leasing me to believe they cross contaminate their food by using one serving utensil for all items. It must be pretty crusty by the end of the day. Small portions
Came into town, read the reviews and Fixins didn't disappoint. Ox tails, collard greens and black eyed peas were on point and the peach cobbler was bussin! Definitely a come-back spot.

This place is awesome!!!!! I went to eat dinner TWICE in one week on my trip to Sacramento.
The 1st time I had the smothered chicken with 
rice n gravy, greens and yams. 

The chicken maintained its crispness while covered with the gravy - it was perfect! The greens were so good and seasoned to perfection and the yams the same!!!

The 2nd time had the oxtails with rice n gravy, greens (again) , yams (again), and added extra 
side of Mac n Cheese I shared with friend - I could only finish one oxtail .. they were so big and meaty! The gravy wasn't that usual thick, brown gravy normally served with oxtails. I loved it with the rice by itself. And again the sides fantastic.

My friend had the oxtails both times we went with the same sides, lol .. we both had the bourbon sweet tea which is so delicious!  We did try two of the desserts: the banana pudding, it was good! Also the peach cobbler, unfortunately it was too sweet to eat  .

I love the way the place is decorated and the service was good too - we will be back next time in Sacramento!! I love this place!!!
Their food is good but I would go just for the chicken. I recommend getting the sweet tea and going for the vibe. It's a sac classic and the staff are phenomenal.

I didn't know Kevin Johnson is an investor before coming in, he greeted me while I was waiting for our table. We arrived at around 1130a on a Sunday. We were told the wait was 45 mins but it was actually 30. 

The bloody mary had really good bacon on it but only that was the best part of it. The drink itself wasnt cold enough, I had to ask for more ice. The spices weren't mixed well enough, they all sank to to the bottom, so it was hard to sip. It tasted better when I mixed it myself with my water glass. Other than that, everything else was amazing. 

The smothered chicken was moist and crispy, the collard greens were delicious, and the mac n cheese was amazing. I ended my meal with the rum raisin bread pudding, which was perfect. We are definitely returning!

I had a very pleasant experience here! The service was top tier. Incredibly efficient, warm, welcoming, and attentive. The food had amazing flavor and texture. I ordered the shrimp and grits!!! SO GOOD!! And the banana pudding.... Geesh. It was BOMB. I also really enjoyed the look of the place. They had modern and fun decore. I would definitely return and I highly recommend!

Food was great, waitress (JC) was the best. Very attentive, made sure we were happy. All was well until after we paid for our food and were conversing when I guess some guy who was one of the managers came to our table and said we had extended our 1.5 hr visit and they needed our booth. Thought that he was joking at first but No, he was actually serious. He stated that the time we had been there was over the 1.5 hrs they give to customer visit. Have never been told that there was a set time to be seated at their restaurant. NOT COOL!!!!!

Fixins is a gem of Oak Park. I'm a lover of soul food and Fixins definitely hits my soul. My favorite dishes are the shrimp and grits, oxtail, and any of their fried chicken combos. Their side dishes are also very delicious and the appetizers are oh so flavorful. The restaurant is always packed and crowded whenever I drive passed it. Fixins is definitely a place I will always find myself going back to!

First time at Fixins and they did not disappoint!
We waited about 20 min which is nothing on a Saturday night...restaurant was clean,staff was friendly and the place had a great vibe! Between the chicken being crispy & tasty and the Mac n cheese being cheesy along with the good selection of music in the background...My son and I were definitely happy we took the drive from out of town. My son had the chicken & waffles with a Hurricane and I had the chicken plate ...everything came out delicious & hot. #supportOAKPARK
I absolutely love coming here. The service is always good, music is such a vibe and the food is just the cherry on TOP.

We came here to celebrate Juneteenth. Now that the country is open...(kinda) what better way to celebrate being free from the confinement of our homes than on a day that celebrates the freedom of Slaves, and just like slaves to this day are "kinda free" so are we right (from.COVID-19)?
On doing my part to support and shop Black Owned Business we came here.
There was a half hour wait (to be expected) but they did NOT disappoint. 
The set up is cool the servers are SO FRIENDLY.  They take COVID-19 serious here. Tables are appropriately spaced so patrons can still enjoy their food and be properly spaced apart but it not feel like you're 20 million feet away from the next patron. 
Now onto the food.
You know I got that fried chicken. Hellooooo
I got the wings...Why? Because fried chicken wings are my favorite part of fried chicken of course. I got the special 2 wings and a side.  I got grits with cheese. But the cheese is different, it's not shredded, nacho, or any of that it was this pimento cheese and O M F G. I first looked at it like wtf is this? My friend tells me girl its pimento cheese trust me. Just eat it. 
What in the faaaacckk?? It was soooooooo good. I mean soooooo good. 
They have all kinds of soul food. So I am pretty sure everything on the menu is delectable.  I will def be back here. Hello someone say Shrimp and Grits? That's on my next quest of a dish to try. 
For drinks they sell KOOL-AID. Yes as in that big fat pitcher coming thru breaking thru that wall. Yall know what it is...Sooooo good. I had red Kool-aid. The alcoholic kind. They also have regular Kool-aid for the kiddos.
So tasty.
Thank you Fixins!! You have a new fan...
You know how in Toy Story he sing "you gotta friend in me" well take that tune to..."you gotta fan in me"
South used to be our go-to restaurant for fried chicken, but.... after eating at Fixins, our allegiance changed. Great choices of sides and spectacular fried chicken.

Just picked up my order I came from Pittsburg because everyone said this place was great I order chicken yam and greens, the chicken didn't have no seasoning only thing was good was the greens.. I also order the chicken tenders and chicken and waffles was not good I give 2 starz because the customer service was good

It was my first time at Fixins. The Vibe was everything.
I ordered the smothered chicken, mac and cheese,yams, a side of rice, a side of gravy with a cornbread muffin.
You know how good the food will be from the first bite. It was absolutely FIRE (delicious...lol).
Hug from me to the chef...the flavor is on POINT.
The customer service was the best. Our waitress was always right on time when I needed something.
Shoutout to Jazz...thank you for your great service and great personality.
I'm a customer for life...much love and God bless.
Best soul food I've ever had. I'm so glad I tried this spot. Customer service was excellent. And the food was extremely fresh and mouth watering. I would defintley come here again!

Chicken cravings satisfied. 

Ordered two pieces of fried chicken, fries and coleslaw. No pictures cause I was too eager to dig in and now that I'm finished I can't wait to go back for more.

The chicken is out of this world. I usually get dark meat because the white meat is too dry but I ordered 1 piece white/1 piece dark and this was the juiciest piece of chicken I had ever tasted. The outer fried crunch complemented the juicy chicken perfectly. 

The sides were on point. Coleslaw is a beautiful amalgamation of sweet, tangy, crunchy and savory. With added greens and bell peppers for a pop of color. The fries were slightly sweet and savory, thicker cut and Cajun style. The biscuit was divine. Crispy outer edge with a soft fluffy center. I don't know what's in the flavored butter but I could literally just eat the butter plain. 

My take out order was quick and easy and came with sides of ketchup and crystal hot sauce. Thanks fixins for fixin me a perfect weekday lunch!

If you're looking for a good soul food spot or support a black-owned local business, I'd recommend stopping by. I've been twice now and both times did not disappoint. I'd recommend the oxtails, fried chicken plate and the shrimp and grits (my favorite). Our last visit the owner stopped in and greeted all of his employees and customers with a smile, you can tell he really takes pride in his restaurant and I'm glad a great soul food spot was brought to Sacramento.
Super bomb. One of the best restaurants in sac. The Mac and cheese is to die for! Staff are awesome
It is one of my favorite places to eat for comfort food. I get fried chicken, oxtail, smothered chicken, gumbo, and, of course, the Peach cobbler.

So good I had to come back the very next night just to see if I was puttin too much on it or not...happy to say that the hype is real! 

Don't let the long waits turn you away, it's worth it trust! I highly recommend keeping an eye on seating at the bar and having your meal there, both of my visits I was able to find a spot after a few minutes of waiting! 

What made me come back the very next night you might ask....the Catfish...Mac n Cheese and Greens!!!! Next visit I'll try something else but I just had to try it again just to make sure it was real! Lol 

My brotha from anotha was with me both nights and ordered his same as well...fried chicken...Mac n Chesse...Red Beans n Rice with the "Red" Kool Aid (colors not flavors just like home)...he swore by his as well !

Do yourself a favor and check em out, give KJ some love when you see him as he's frontline most nights!!

Next up BreakFried Chicken Omelette!!!

The catfish and mac & cheese were good.  Corn fritters were hushpuppies, black eyed peas, meh.  When you pay $27 for a salad it should come with more than 2 shrimp and no dressing!!  Lost someone who really wanted to be a loyal customer.

Fixins was fantastic! 

We picked up our order from their outdoor takeout stand (great idea!). We got the fried chicken and biscuits. We also wanted to try all the fixins: greens, mac and cheese, red beans and rice, coleslaw, and yams.

First the fried chicken is awesome - crispy on the outside and juicy on the inside! The Biscuits were also super fresh! I think my favorite fixins were the greens and red beans and rice but everything was very good! We will be back!

Great service, fun atmosphere and delicious food. Every person we interacted with was kind and attentive and we left feeling excited to go back. Our food was so well seasoned and prepared as well. I ate my sandwich like a salad because it wouldn't stay together - too much goodness for the buns to handle. Highly recommend!

Best soul food around I love it 
Come here for birthday and just dinner 
Ox Tail Plate is my favorite and the green kool-aid
Smothered Chicken is hella good 
Candied Yams must get all the time
Sandria the waitress was Amazing. She very friendly and super professional.. value this employee, their rare.

I loved the catfish I got. The greens were good. It could be because I'm used to my mother's spicy greens they felt bland to me. But the serving size was decent. Idk if it's 20$ worth a plate. 

But the whole atmosphere was wonderful I would come back again

I was feeling like experimenting with a dine-in experience during the covid era..  no one that's high danger in my immediate circles, and this was one of the few restaurants available this week.  And wow, it had a lot of nice things going for it...

Unbelievable patio space, very upscale feeling, nice cocktails, food was also memorable (fried catfish + gumbo)... service was quick and friendly.  Normally, I would be sure to return. 

So why only 3 stars?  

Because both waitresses we dealt with weren't wearing masks properly.  The first one really didn't cover her nostrils, and then (I kid you not) a second waitress came up to say she was taking over because the original waitress "wasn't feeling well".  Great!  And to compound it, the second waitress was actually walking around with her mask down around her chin roughly half the time.  

Boy... that made me feel really uncomfortable.  I don't think I'll be back until after covid is gone.

Our first  time at 
Fixins 
Man what a treat
The food was delicious 
The music was bumping and Vibe was fantastic 
We will return

I just wanna start with their service, 10/10 Amie was our server and she was such a sweetheart told us recommendations but we knew what we wanted also constantly checked on us if we needed anything. Auntie Pearl also came and talked to us science she recognized that we were new faces. Auntie Pearl was us feel soo welcomed in their restaurant told us the history about oak park and how it came to be currently, the sweetest little lady ever!

For food we had
-Fried chicken with Mac and cheese and collard greens, fried chicken was nicely seasoned and super crispy, Mac and cheese was delicious but a little dry for my liking and the collard green was definitely a favorite 
-gumbo with shrimp and corn bread, definitely a favorite of our that we'll be ordering next time it has a little kick to it and is one of the best gumbo I've had in town 
-smothered oxtail(the start of the show), if you've been to stage coach you know they use to sell oxtail and gravy but the owners stopped making it since the cost in oxtail rose up, this dish made me soo happy I cried  I wish I was joking, it's been a while since I found good oxtail and gravy so I was excited when it tasted just as good if not better than what I remembered from stage coach, more than excited to come back soon just to eat this.
-kool aid originally we weren't going to get this but it was as just so good I had to get another one 

Overall experience was excellent so was the ambiance. I've noticed that they one of the busser cleaned the table they had little plaque that let you know the table was cleaned and sanitized.
Delicious and the service was awesome! Great vibe, the music was so good and the place is beautiful! The portions were perfect.

The food is fresh and yummy!! Service is warm and friendly!! Wait time was reasonable.  Extremely satisfied. Loved the vibe and the area surrounding the restaurant is nice as well. Took a short stroll.

The service was great and the food was even BETTER!!! 
10/10 recommend shit is fireeeee

I wasn't gone say nothing but... wait a minute!!!! That red gravy on them oxtails and I ate it ALL. That macaroni and Chuh-Eeeeeszzzeeeeee... Will turn you from Buddhist to Baptist! Ate it every bit. I tried the Yams.. ate 'em as I should. Appetizers? Yes please.. the Fried Deviled Eggs ate them and all the garnish that came with it thank you for asking. Took a picture of it all. .. ate that too.
Food is not good! We got multiple items jambalaya  chicken etc nothing had flavor.  Drinks where weird. Staff was rushed and rude

This place was absolutely amazing! I had the shrimp and grits. My husband and son had the chicken and waffles and biscuits and gravy. All of it was so delicious! The staff was super friendly and the atmosphere was great! We will definitely come back!!
Love the food, but the service was a little rushed, they were under staff. The waiter was too busy to check if I needed anything else. Next time, I will do take-out.

I found this place to be amazing! We must of arrived at the perfect time it wasn't too busy, we were seated right away. The servers were professional and super nice. Our food was delicious, hot, a healthy portion, and satisfying. Nothing makes me happier than to go to a soul food restaurant and not be told we ain't got that, or that just sold out. They had everything that was offered on the menu. It's was delightful to see other races and ethnic groups sitting down eating as well. I never once had to look for a waiter or waitress. We were always checked in in a reasonable manner. The only complaint I have is the peach cobbler didn't come with enough crust but if you're a person who don't like crust you in the game.

At Fixin's ordered gumbo with no rice they brought me a bowl of gravy that shook like jello, no flavor, with some chicken and sausage thrown in to make it look like gumbo. If you don't have something just say you're out. Yes I sent it back!!!

It took a while to be seated, even on a cold, rainy night at least 1.5 hrs. But, it was great seeing the crowd and even more waiting to get in by 9pm. Very courteous staff, server was very nice and attentive. My favorite is fried fish , always my go-to. Sides were black eyed peas and collard greens, just rightly seasoned w/the tasty hush puppies. Quality food at a great price.

*Chefs kiss* everything just felt like a I was being hugged with amazing food. And the banana pudding was outstanding,it was light, creamy and that subtle salty taste from the cookie just balanced so well.

Ordered online delivery for Father's Day dinner for my elderly parents. Missing several items in my $160 dollar dinner. Needless to say how disappointed I am with the lack of due diligence from the restaurant not making sure all the items were in the bag. Will never order online from here again.

Dining at Fixins Soul Kitchen was  a perfect way to top off a beautiful day celebrating our graduate, Jahlaina!  Oh MY GOODNESS! The food was delicious, and truly made my soul rejoice! The Fried Chicken and Cat Fish were seasoned just right! We also enjoyed Red Beans and Rice, Yams, Greens, Mac and Cheese, homemade biscuits and cornbread, creamy fruit flavored butter, Peach Cobbler, Vanilla Wafer Banana Pudding, refreshing drinks and great service. Everything was on point! Thank you Kevin for being such a wonderful host. It was a pleasure meeting you! I also wanted to note that I greatly appreciated the ease of Parking, because of the garage. Awesome!
We will certainly be back!

Slid up from the bay to check this place out for a friends bday lunch. Food was pretty good. Drinks were decent. Booked a reservation, got right in and was seated. Food came fairly quick. Check them out if your in the area and want soul food.
Ordered gumbo, adult kool aid, and a michelada. Favorite part of the meal was the corn bread and the michelada. I kinda wish the gumbo had okra but still great.
Overal decent place, I really like the outdoor seating. Nothing mind blowing but a good place to eat in the area. I like that there is kool-aid on the menu lol
Outstanding soul food. You gotta try this place out! I didn't even know I loved soul food until I ate here.
Was Awesome food loved the chicken and waffles. A little slow but the atmosphere is nice.
Sacramento's best soul food restaurant.   You have to come.  Their meals are outstanding.

Food? Delicious. Wait? Takes a minute, but worth it because the food is so good. Servers? Nice, but one problem. My mom and I called in our orders for pick up, when we got there to pay, my mom paid her bill first using a credit card, the young man working outside automatically added a 20% gratuity to her bill, my mom was just going to pay not seeing that the young man had hit the 20% gratuity button before turning the card reader in her direction. I said to my mom, "hold on, do you want to pay the 20% gratuity the young man just added?" Looking at him to let him know I seen what he did. My mom said she was going to tip anyway because of the workers standing in the heat, but that was up to her to decide, not him. 

 Point being, it makes the establishment look a little shady, I eat here often and I know that's not the case, but had he done something like that to an astute first timer, there would have been no return visit. Pick employees that will make your establishment a plapeople want to return to, not just people who need jobs. Other than that, wonderful restaurant.

Update: The owner got back with me immediately and advised me that this would be a teachable moment for their employees. She was prompt, kind and apologetic. I appreciated the quick response and the kindness. It makes all of the difference.

First off the place was extremely clean, the server was very pleasant. The fried chicken was cooked perfectly not greasy, not overly seasoned and the breading in the chicken was spot on. The sweet tea was perfect, I also ordered the Mac and cheese and the red beans and rice as a side very delicious as well. I will be back, very reasonable pricing as well. You would be a fool for not stopping here for a good soul food restaurant.

I've been twice and the food was consistent and good both times. The first time was a walk in. We were told it would be a 45 minute wait but were seated in about 20 minutes. The second time I had reservations and was seated immediately. I enjoyed the food and would return when in the area.
Food is ok. Prices are too over the top. Need to come down at prices or serve better quality of food.
Fried chicken and oxtail on point! Also thank you to the lady at the front. It was a very busy night and she was very patient with me figuring out my order.
The food is pretty good unless you like your food extremely seasoned. This was just right for me

Great food!
Great environment 
Staff is courteous 
Where we sat was near the patio door, we inside but felt like we were outside. It felt nice and open like we sitting outside.

This place is the BEST!! The food is FRESH, SEASONED and ok portions. I'm HOOKED! Only thing I didn't like is the banana pudding... too much whip cream and pudding. Very little bananas and no VANILLA WAFERS... you gotta have vanilla wafers...the Peach Cobbler is BOMB!!! Love this place 20 stars
Outstanding southern waffles and chicken. Great relaxed atmosphere and mouth watering food.

Sub par food for a 5 star price. 
I ordered for the first time food from this restaurant through DoorDash. Was exited to try it and glad that I didn't have to drive down there and wait for it myself. 
Fast forward to the food delivery- Paid $88 for the food and it all arrived in one bag. I open it up and in it are these tiny containers. 
I ordered the smothered chicken with red beans and rice with greens. Two sides of Mac and cheese, chicken and sausage Gumbo and then peach cobbler for dessert. 
In the container the biscuits that are about 1"  (shaped like cubes) were two for $7.  The peach cobbler was in a small cup container, so I paid $10 for a "cup" which contained a small piece?? Talk about over priced. 
Taste wise there was nothing spectacular. Being black and raised around soul food I can tell you their greens were hard, Gumbo- my husband said could've had more flavor. Mac N cheese was basic. Rice and beans was just rice and beans. Biscuits were soft which is good and the peach cobbler was ok. I just wish I hadn't wasted almost $100 to be disappointed by not only the portion size but the taste. If you haven't tasted better you'd think this was good. But I've tasted better and will tell you this was very basic. Who ever cooked the greens definitely needs to review their method. The portion I received was more crisp than soft. Greens should melt in your mouth. 
Try at your own risk. Make sure your wallet can handle the disappointment!
Hands down the best burger I have ever ate in my life. Perfection in every single bite!

Fried chicken is now a thing in my house.  We will never cook it but we love take out.  Popeye's, South, Bawk, we have tried em all. 
But this experience took it all to another level
The friendliness of this establishment was awesome, from the people at take out to the hostess, to our waitress,  who helped us through the menu, to every person that came to our table.  Never intrusive, but always eager to help. 
The food was fresh.  Like super fresh.  The coleslaw was not a mix of leftovers from the bargain section but instead a crunchy flavorful ensemble of garden veggies that complimented the dense Mac and Cheese and the flavorful black eyed peas!(my first ever!) 
The chicken was clearly brined as when you get past the delicious covering, you can taste the seasoning well into the meat. 
And then there's red and purple drink.  
Whoa. Me and Suzy felt like kids playing on a summer day with water balloons and hoses!  This was the perfect accessory to a perfect meal!
Glad you came out of covid  ok Fixins!
We will be back!
The food was very authentic and tasty. Parking was kind of trying but that's how we knew it would be good it was packed.
The food was awesome and the service was even better! Came with a large party and they accommodated with no issue!

Food is amazing.  Don't use the grub hub link to order online.  Use their website.  We know grub hub adds as much as 30% to the items you order!  I ordered the catfish nuggets $11 menu price $14 grubhub.   Beware,  just use their website to order!
Great service,  food was okay, I would go back.I also l learned the history of the community.

All I have to say is... wow!!! Went in today for the first time. 
Was craving gumbo, but ended up going with the catfish based on what the sweet girl who helped me said. Thank you! The catfish was the best I have ever had. Moist on the inside but crunchy on the outside. Amazing. Tried quite a few sides. All absolutely delicious. Coleslaw was on point, potato salad was yummy, all my kiddos loved the mac and cheese. Then on to dessert. Something had to be just meeeeh, right?  Wrong!!. We had the banana pudding and peach cobbler. BOTH SOOOOO good!!! Let's just say I already want to go back and I barely left and finished my food. Had to write them a review and give them the credit they deserve.

 Everyone working was great as well! The girl helping me was awesome and double checked my order with me when done. Every time she walked away I was asked if I was being helped by someone new to make sure I wasn't just standing there. Great great service. I will be seeing you guys again really soon

We went to Fixins on a friends recommendation.  I was glad we made reservations as they are really busy.  We both ordered the fried chicken meal.  I usually take the skin off my chicken but I had to eat theirs.  The skin was perfectly seasoned and fried to perfection. My husband ordered the 3 piece meal with 2 sides.  They are very generous with their portions.  I wasn't fond of the biscuit as it was dry and hard but the other dishes were great.
Love this place, I personally would highly recommend, warm atmosphere great environment

This food here is delicious! I've had the fried chicken and catfish, both are so good! The whole vibe here is fun and they play good music as well! One of my favorite places to go eat at! My entire family grubs here.

Came to this restaurant as a first timer on 10/4/2020 and it was wait. Not bad as the weather was great. They were pretty organized with having the checkers outside to get your name and put you on the text message list to let you know when your table was available. 

Food was actually good. My friend and i ordered oxtail meal and the catfish meal. They were well season and delicioso. Our waitress was super bomb. She was such a sweet heart. I can't remember her name but she is short, curly black hair with blonde highlights.  She made our visit worth it!! 

Would i come back yes!
Y'all might have to change the recipe to the greens... too much vinegar in it. But for the most part it was cooked all the way just awkward taste.

As a teen who lives in the area, me and my siblings went to try them and I guess being young and black with money disturbed them because a 15min wait turned into a 1hr wait for us leaving us to go down the street to settle for domino's pizza. Service was alright but I feel as though they don't really serve the black community as they should. We were very respectful and waited patiently, tables were empty and we were never called. Maybe it was a sign to not try them lol!

I don't come here often but they never disappoint. I like to bring my out of town family to get a taste of this delicious soul food. The outdoor area is a nice touch and the wait staff is very knowledgeable.

Tried this place for the first time a few weeks ago. Ordered the fried green tomatoes as an appetizer, very good! We had the fried chicken (crispy on the outside, juicy in the middle) along with the following fixins: greens, red beans & rice, and mac & cheese- my favorite out of the 3 were the greens. The plates came with a fresh biscuit served with a really yummy strawberry butter!
In addition to the good food, the waiters were very friendly. We enjoyed the ambiance and music with the outside seating.  Additionally, a very nice lady, who I believe to possibly be the Owner/Cook, came out to check on us to make sure we were enjoying our food and visit. The next time we are in the area craving some soul food, we will definitely give this place another go round!

Let me start by saying I have visited this restaurant multiple times and after today I will NOT be back! I had a reservation for my son birthday and  let's just say it was ruined! 3 people in our party didn't get there food cooked right and it took over 20 minutes to get 2 pieces of fish. The waiter we had Sean was horrible! my husbands beer sat at the bar until it was hot and had to be poured Out and given another one. We kept asking to ask him and other staff members for refills of water. We asked for the manager and he was very disrespectful! He REFUSED to give me his name and I can't believe a business will treat their paying customers this way. I will be telling any and everyone as well and posting on every platform I can because I was so sad that my sons birthday was ruined by poor staff and management

I love a good chicken sandwich and this has to be the best I've ever had.  The hot chicken sandwich is definitely hot, which was perfect.    Ordered for dinner last night and just might order again today for lunch.  YUM.
Loved everything I have ordered on the menu! Fried chicken, Mac & cheese, and the collard greens are delicious options!

Look I'm here right now on Day 8 of the Quarantine and this is some of thee best customer service I evah had in life. I mean yeah the hostess had us order off iPads but 6 feet distance right? 

Right. Well that still ain't stop us from getting her to make us a fresh batch of Red Kool Aid. This jawn come in a huge reusable glass jug. On some real moonshine ish.

We ain't tasted the food yet, cuz ya know Take Out Orders only. But the car smellin hella good right now, and my homegirl is a thick girl and she da one who recommended this spot so...

Update: Food devoured. Everything was above average and held up on the 15 minute drive home. D f###ing licious. Highly recommend. Goin back right now matter fact
The food was so good! And it was a huge serving :) and the sweet tea is bomb! I recommend coming here. The staff was very nice and the food came out in a quick fashion.

This place is soooo good! We moved from the bay area to sacramento so our standard for soul food was based on some of the small joints in oakland and the east bay. This place definitely is on the same level if not better! The flavored butters are bonuses that we definitely kept to use for homemade pancakes the next day :) 

I got the catfish and my husband got the chicken. Both were amazing and the biscuits were extremely flavorful and moist as well. We will definitely be coming back.

I've been meaning to come here for a while but I have been craving gumbo for the longest. We came here today with my husband and baby, the staff were so nice right away. Super friendly, we got the best waitress there , (the reciept name has it as John-Nia as our server )but omg she was the sweetest person and has the biggest heart! Super attentive while it being busy as well. I would love to have her be recognized because she was just awesome. Besides that the food was awesome we ordered sweet tea and green koolaid it was super delicious. Gumbo was how I imagined to taste like for it being my first time trying it. My husband got smothered chicken with Mac and cheese and coleslaw. It was to die for! Definetly when we're back in town we would hit that spot again !

Had to give this place a try as I love southern food.  Not sure why southern food is so expensive!! It's supposed to be comfort food.  Ordered online due to covid.  They were not ready, took almost 45 minutes more than the estimated time!

Good to support a local business, but probably won't be back here.

Wow if you need a sign to go to this restaurant over another I hope you see this review!! My best friend and I could go on forever about there corn bread and berry butter. The Mac and cheese was better than homemade even. The chicken fell right off the bone too! The owner was so sweet and gave off the happiness most genuine vibes. Please come and support this business they deserve it!!

We started with the fried green tomatoes and an order of deep fried bacon deviled eggs, both of which were very tasty. I had the fried chicken salad and it was large and fresh. My companions ordered fried fish and gumbo and they were quite happy with their selections.
Food was very good.  Well seasoned and really good portions.  Prices were also reasonable.  Choices rather limited....

Ordered takeout and was underwhelmed. Fish was seasoned well. Macaroni and cheese was just nasty, I was very surprised at that. Banana pudding was $13 half filled small, cup wasn't good. All sides were extremely small for the price. If the food was great I wouldn't mind prices at All. Macaroni and cheese and banana pudding are my favorites and neither was impressive, lackluster at best. I won't be back
Great food and excellent service ! Came for brunch for my son's birthday brunch . Kids enjoyed themselves , food was so flavorful !
Nice place, service was great! Food was what I expected, amazing! I had so many recommendations from friends and they definitely proved to be awesome:)

Not going to lie, I prefer the fried chicken here over the ones at South.

Once you've had the fried chicken here, you'll keep coming back for it. Crispy, crackling skin. Moist and rich chicken meat. Okra and collard greens are great. Bloody mary's are on point. 

Plenty of street parking all around the area. Quaint niche stores across the street to browse as you wait for your table. 

Protip: use the yelp waitlist to shorten your wait.

Do I need to even say anything? Costumer service was through the ROOF. The atmosphere felt like home, but the food... THE FOOD HAS SOUL. This is what soul food should feel like. It should feel warm, it should feel like home, it should feel like being in your grandparents kitchen during a family function. That's exactly what this place is, a family function. I'd go every week if I could.
Soul food is a really hard restaurant to duplicate when you have southern roots you need experience on your own and formulate your own opinion too me just alright.

Chicken Waffles is better than Roscoes IMO.  I will always order the chicken and waffles from this establishment.  The service we received was also A++.  Its very popular so I recommend to get a reservation for larger parties if needed.
long wait for some nasty food would not recommend.the only thing that was good was the fish fried deviled eggs and the green tomatoes

Great food! I love the patio seating, on Sunday they were playing gospel music, it filled my soul. Fried chicken was perfect, the fish sandwich was amazing. 
The sweet tea was very good not tooo sweet just right. My favorite the cornbread YUMMMMMMM

Visiting a friend who is going to Sac State and after a night of shots and clubs, we HAD to recover. Fixins came across my Yelp and I am soooo glad it did. The food is AMAZING. Mac and cheese is so cheesy and hearty, great comfort food. I had the Caesar salad with chicken and it got me full! Totally ready for a nap after getting fed well.
Not only was the food great but the service and staff are so sweet. I look forward to my next  visit
The LA one is good, figured Id try sacramento. Saturday 7:30pm reservations only. Pretty inconveniencing
Food at the Sac location is not consistent. More than likely will not be returning anytime soon

My wife, myself and a friend ordered takeout from here. All of the food was great. Between us we ordered catfish, smothered pork chops, fried chicken, greens, red beans and rice, yams, fries. Everything was great, will definitely eat here again when I'm back in Sac.

Love coming early on Saturday for brunch as they're opening before the rush comes in around noon. The portion for dining in is great however please do not order takeout. They will literally underserve you and cut portions out. On average, I've gotten about 5-6 pieces of oxtail for a plate however when I ordered takeout I got literally 2 1/2 pieces with a few spoonfuls of rice. Don't be fooled by the picture, the portion is smaller than it looks. Paid $20+ for half a meal, never again ordering out from here.

The fried chicken was incredible. The fried catfish amazing. I'd never had full size okra, and was a bit apprehensive when I saw the charred okra I ordered, but wow. Amazing. This place is a guilty pleasure.

Amazing! Delicious! Large servings! Kool-Aid! Best Sweet Tea! No other way to describe this place other than perfect! They even had a DJ last night (Saturday) playing great music! Great vibe! We will be back! Oh, they even serve brunch on Sat-Sun!

Great food and very friendly awesome staff. I had the Gumbo which was delicious and my friends had the catfish that they all loved. Also, the booking system is smooth and efficient, wait time was only 15 minutes on a Sunday. Recommended!

I ordered takeout and I got home and my main entree wasn't in the bag... I called and I asked if I could drive back to pick up and said it was too busy and it would be another 30 mins. I kindly asked for a refund and I never received one. Not the best experience to a place I had been wanting to try for a while.

My friend and I placed an order for pickup, and it was hot and ready in about 30 minutes. We got the 2 piece fried chicken meal with Mac & cheese and coleslaw, their Caesar salad, and another side of Mac & cheese, yams and potato salad. 

The fried chicken was juicy and had a bit of a kick to it, the Mac and cheese was well seasoned with just the right amount of cheese, and the coleslaw was reminiscent of salsa. The Caesar salad had cornbread croutons that I thought were super neat. The yams were sweet and swimming in a sweetened butter and the potato salad was mustard/vinegar heavy (just how I like it)

But the biscuits, THE BISCUITS! My friend and I took a bite of the biscuits and our first reactions were "oh my god!" They were the best biscuits that we've ever had in our lives. Period. So treat yourself and go for the biscuits at least. Your mouth will love you for it.
great spot! mac and cheese was really good so was the fried chicken and the fish! staff was all really nice. good vibes.
So great and quite an experience. Go with friends. Also, enjoy a walk around the neighborhood. A rare find.

I seen this place off of tik tok and decided to bring my family. The restaurant was super cute... during this pandemic they are offering outside seating, Staff are masked and gloved up And require everyone to wear a mask. We had the same idea as everyone else is Sacramento on a Thursday night because we arrived around 6:30pm and there was an hour wait. It also didn't help that we had 13 people in our party. When we were finally sat the atmosphere was so cute. The chairs were a little uncomfortable and no where to place my purse nor my diaper bag..We were greeted right away and had our order taken since we already knew what we wanted. I ordered the 2 piece fried chicken that came with 2 sides (I chose Mac n cheese and potato salad) with the blue kool aid. Food was decent; the fried chicken was fried perfectly but didn't seem seasoned. The potato salad was commercial tasting and Mac n cheese was again good but not a lot of flavor... I'm use to my mother in law southern cooking. I wasn't a fan of the waitress bringing our drinks after the food came. And due to us arriving late and the long wait the kitchen closed soon after we received our plate so we didn't have the option to order dessert. I was really looking towards the banana pudding... I did taste my sisters ceasar salad and their homemade dressing was delicious.

I thoroughly enjoyed my fish Sammy and fries with a watermelon margarita.  Everyone was very hospitable and welcoming. The music and overall vibe were good- it felt like home/family.  
I started at the bar for a quick meal after work and ended buying more to take home for my family. 
I'm a hard critic on Mac N Cheese and Greens- we enjoyed both.   Along with the catfish nuggets and chicken.  No complaints. Whenever I'm in the area I will stop by for SOMETHING.
Ox tails are amazing!  Great service , don't let me forget the house drink ole school

Today was my partner and I'd first time here. We walked in and didn't receive much service until a waitress let us know the host would be with us shortly. We ended up waiting for 10 minutes while others orders who had gotten there after us were taken. Most of the employees were unfriendly and unhelpful, they were continuously staring at us and didn't not smile or say anything at all while we were just waiting to be seated. It was a feeling of purposely being ignored.

This was the first place I visited once the stay at home order was lifted. I must say I loved the environment and the staff were very professional. My full course meal was okay. I thought the smothered chicken was breaded too much, and the gravy reminded me of the packets of gravy they sell in the stores. The greens and Mac and cheese was delicious. I ordered my husbands shrimp and grits to go. When I got home I looked at his food and it looked as if they put their gumbo over the grits. Not what I was expecting...however my husband said it was good. I will try this place again because of the customer service and cleanliness.

Yesterday, my family and I celebrated an important family event in the Dunlap room at Fixins Soul Kitchen in Sacramento. The room and patio were perfect for our family of 35 people. The service is unmatched. The staff truly cares about your experience. The soul food is fantastic - everything we tasted melted in our mouths, and we enjoyed every bite, from the catfish nuggets to the fried chicken, mac and cheese, collard greens, and peach cobbler for dessert. Absolutely the best meal we had while in Sacramento. Considering the quality, the price is reasonable--ideal for large family events.

Look fixins has great food. The issue is they have no staff and most of the time restaurant is empty but you have to wait an hour to be seated. We waited 1 hr to sit down yet there was only 4 tables occupied in the restaurant. Completely unacceptable and is not ok. We saw a few parties being sat and no one helped so they left. It ruined the experience and unfortunately it will be awhile if we even go back.

Ordered take out for Christmas & was disappointed . I was aware the size portion for $6.00 is 8oz. I had to triple up on the portions due to the size of the container. My suggestion instead of packaging an increase quantity in smaller containers, use a larger container instead of the customer picking up a dozen little boxes . 2nd, the quality of the Black Eyed Peas has gone done .. very watery . The Collard Greens may have been mixed with mustard greens. Charred okra was very salty . Based on my recent experience with take out, I'll go elsewhere next time or make myself . A couple of years ago when I ordered take out for Thanksgiving sides, the boxes were larger ; price may have been more .. i got Collards; not a mixture & the Black Eyed Peas had more substance .

First time here. Quickly seated, but right after me the lunch rush came. I just chose a table top. My server suggested the oxtails and they were fabulous! Of course, very fatty and messy, but amazing. I asked for a sample (small bite) of the Mac and cheese and greens but the cook or manager said no. That's why not a five star review. The Mac is basic. Wonder how the greens are. The chicken was hot and fresh and juicy!
My go to for fried chicken in Sacramento. The Family meal of 8 pieces 4 biscuits and 2 large sides is a great deal! The Adult Kool Aid is a must try.

Absolutely yes, all I can say is yes, yes, yes!!  The food is packed with flavors that will make your mouth drop and your heart do a little happy dance. The wait was supper long but honestly it's worth it.

Yum! The Mac and cheese flavor is on point - delicious. The peach cobbler is so delicious I almost cried. Lol but seriously it was that kind of tasty that turns a bad around. Definitely try it! Atmosphere and wait staff were really pleasant too! Highly recommend.

All around wonderful place to dine. The service was exceptional and everyone was more than helpful. The drinks were really great especially the Adult Koolaid. The sides, shrimp and grits were really delicious. I did however have to give it 4 stars because it was not disclosed that the gravy on the pork chops was sweet brown gravy and I personally am not a fan of that. The chops were cooked perfectly though. I would definitely still return and I do recommend.
best spot in sac! the food will put you to sleep, love the customers service and food was off the charts!

Been hearing  about this place for quite sometime now.  Finally got the chance to make it down from Rocklin and it was well worth the drive.  I had the Chicken and Waffles, Collard Greens, Candied Yams, and Peach Cobbler.  You guys did not disappoint at all.  I wish you guys sold the entire peach cobbler.

It was so good I forgot to take a picture when the food first arrived! But I ordered the fried cat fish with Mac & cheese with yams as my sides .... the yams were really good and the Mac & cheese was great too .... the fish I liked it was just a little bit to salty but other than that it was so good the look aid was super sweet which I love brought me back to childhood lol.... I definitely recommend and the customer service was great! Our server he was so sweet
This place was delicious and hopping with people. The neighborhood is neat and there's a great bookstore around the corner where you can wait to be texted that your table us ready.

The food was amazing, so delicious, highly recommend the catfish, the hush puppies, cornbread, biscuit, and their fried chicken! It was all so good. The atmosphere was fantastic too, it was really nice and chill. No loud noises. Music was good too. Highly recommend this place

For a soul food restaurant, the prices are pretty nice on the wallet, which is so great when you just want comfort food at comfortable prices. I'm really glad that my friend recommended this place; my party of 5 was seated quickly and greeted by a really friendly waitress.

The two piece fried chicken plate is a pretty good deal for $14. Though, I did opt for dark meat and I got two drumsticks (they were good! Very crispy), but if you want a larger portion, I did get a little jealous of my friends HUGE thigh and breast pieces. They got significantly more meat, which I felt a little cheated out of, but it's a compromise. The biscuit was so good, didn't even need the strawberry butter they provide. I got candied yams which were a little too sweet and syrupy, but still nice. I spread it over the biscuit and it was great. The black eyed peas are a nice way to fill you up too if you think that two pieces of fried chicken won't cut it. 

I'd definitely recommend this location to other friends!

The best soul food restaurant I have ever been to. Great food! Great atmosphere! Great cocktails! I just wish it was bigger. The fried chicken is wonderful and the mac 'n cheese is smackin' cheesy! I love this place.

I really enjoyed the brunch. Oxtails we're so tender. It didn't fully commit to jerk seasoning and seemed a little off in the flavor, but I still enjoyed them. The fried green tomatoes are also good. Just a solid place, but nothing amazing! I would give it 3.5 stars. Really happy it is in the community!
This place was wonderful the first time I went on the weekend. But when I went during the week, the food wasn't as tasty, but still good. The koolaid idea was bomb.

The staff park in front to where we have to find parking around the back of the building. When I walked in all three ladies had their backs turned to the door so they didn't realize that I was standing there for like five minutes. My ox tails are very hard. I had barely any gravy in my bowl. each time I got here if it is getting worse and worse.
Ohhh what can I say... The Grits are great... The fried chicken is always perfect and the customer service is always great for me!

The place is nice! I would recommend it! Great hosting staff! You need to get there early if you are hungry. The kool-aid is a great addition. We will be going back. The chicken is great, the sides are great also, the gravy over the chicken is slamming!!! Great family atmosphere!!!

Man.... I'm really trying to support this place because 1) the food is pretty good.  It's nice to have a solid soul food option in Sac, 2) I love Oak Park and am happy to see a place that reminds me of why I love it so much and 3) because a place like this is LONG overdue!  But cmon, you MUST get your service situation together a quick!  I don't understand WHY there is a long wait for everything!!! Even in times where it isn't super busy, there still seems to be a wait.  And the lack of professionalism in the staff is only going to be tolerated so much.  PLEASE get your collective act together before your patrons start losing patience and this wonderful place goes the same way as it's predecessors in the neighborhood.  

Mr. Johnson, if you're listening, please do something.  Good food will only get you so far.  The customer experience counts more than you realize!

Honestly, I don't eat soul food a lot, but this spot hit the spot for me. From the sides to the mains everything was delicious. I felt at home here. Very rustic southern vibe. Server was extremely nice and inviting . This place is very welcoming. The food HIT. The fried catfish went crazyyyy, the chicken and waffles are a must too. The rice and beans and max and cheese go stupid!! You need to try this spot seriously

This restaurant is my #1 place to go to for the chicken. I always get the same sides which r mac & cheese & yams(bomb)..The cornbread muffin is my fav & the biscuits r yummy too. I like to eat the biscuit in the morn with my coffee Now the ox tails were sweet, that was differentthey were still good & tender..So I decided to try them 1 more time & they were not as sweet, they were on pointSo if u come to sac I will say go try The Fixings I think u would be pleased

I absolutely love this restaurant! It's worth the drive from Hayward, Ca. The food is delicious, the service is great and the atmosphere is awesome. It's very clean! They really care about their customers.
Anything you order is yummy! They know how to cook real soul food! May God continue to bless Fixins Soulfood during this pandemic!

Food is good. Service is trash. I base this on dining here 5x, weekend, weekdays and a doordash order. Service declined after every visit. The drinks (bar and fountain) are slow to arrive to table, today it took over an hr and requesting from waitress 3x to even get a plain koolaid. Spoke with on shift manager( spanish guy) about it and still took 20 more minutes to get water!!!! Then the waitress decided that she was butt hurt by this and I had to give my card to another waitress to pay the bill... Absolutely atrocious, I have a degree in Hospitality Management and this place needs FOH training badly... Won't be back

Fun kool-aid themed bar drinks and food that will make you want to come back and try more! On top of that, the atmosphere is amazing and everyone is really friendly and welcoming. We brought our parents and little kids here after Disney On Ice one evening, adding to the diversity of this popularly packed restaurant--families, couples, singles, young, old, we were all there enjoying the food and fun!

I don't frequent Fixins but I just might after being served by Carmen! She's so lively and upbeat, it makes for a great experience. The food was tasty as well, I just wish the banana pudding had more nilla wafers!
I've been there 3 or 4 times over past couple of years. Food is always good. They also make a great Bloody Marys. If you like soul food this is for you.
Friendly and casual place to enjoy a meal. The chicken and waffles were delicious. I will be returning.
Has a really good catfish entree and the chicken was amazing! Loved the potato's salad and the Fries were great!

Omg!!! Excellent food and great service. Ordered fried deviled eggs, pimento cheese dip, fried chicken plate and fried chicken salad.  So excited to know they have another location in LA!  Def coming back for the fried green tomatoes.
Excellent food and service. I've attempted to go a few times but the wait was too long. As long as you plan ahead and don't show up ready to eat ASAP then you'll be satisfied the wait is worth it.
Chicken and waffles are superb! Veggie scramble and strawberry mimosas were also on point.
I've been many times there on and off but to me most of the time on when I bring people I always hope it's a on day lol

I've been hearing about this spot for a bit and finally got a chance to visit.  It had a great atmosphere, and the servers were very friendly.  The menu was on point with the traditional soul food entrees.  I ordered the fried chicken with greens and yams.  The chicken was fried nicely, however there was an unusual sweetness to the batter which was not my cup of tea.  My friends complained as well on their dishes about the usage of sugar.  They need to lighten it on the sugar as it should not go on everything.  Eliminate it on the chicken and the greens and it would make the entrees better.  Otherwise it was a nice and intriguing meal.
Service was good especially for how busy is was. Great southern food and modern ambiance
Everything was very yummy. Our waiter was the absolute best! I gave 4 stars only because it was a little pricey and spicy. But very yummy.

Had to update my review on this based on my last experience here the other day...completely different experience with the wait staff this time...our waitress was so friendly and attentive even her colleague was cracking jokes with us everytime she passed our table...i feel bad i forgot both their names so can't give them the recognition they deserve...this time around i had the deep fried devils egg, fried catfish, and shrimp and grits...I'm not a big fan of devils egg so the fried version really didn't do anything for me...the catfish pieces were just melting in my mouth and i usually hate catfish...but the shrimp and grits was the best of all 3 in my opinion...I've never seen shrimp and grits that soupy before...i thought they gave me gumbo by mistake at first until I saw that scoop of grits in the center...the grits was creamy as expected but that PBR brown gravy sauce really made it stand apart from all the other shrimp and grits I've had before...I'm glad I came back to give it another try and especially happy with my experience with the wait staff this time around

Fixins is legit even though I didn't get to try their Kool-Aid drink because I did take out. I'm a foodie so I'm extremely critical that's why Fixins received a four star. I loved the catfish nuggets and would recommend ordering them. The deep fried deviled eggs on the other hand, nothing special. I ordered the two piece fried chicken and I loved the chicken but the Mac n cheese wasn't the best but the candy yams I'm sure was full of sugar so it was delicious. The biscuit that comes with the chicken meal was a rock. Overall, I'm glad we tried it and if we end up going back I'll look forward to trying different sides and the grape Kool-Aid drink.
One of my favorite chicken spots in Sacramento! Ordered take out through the height of the pandemic and now we can enjoy dining inside or outside!
Excellent service, location,, ambiance. Mac & Cheese balls delicious!!! I shall return.

This place is definitely a must try here in Sacramento. Food is very traditional southern with a nice modern twist but not enough to take away from the roots of delicious soul food cooking. Prices are decent but definitely worth it. Some must try recommendations would definitely be the chicken and waffles (chicken is cooked beautifully and waffle is very tasty), fried catfish (packed with flavor), Mac n' cheese. Just to name a few. If you're ever looking for some authentic southern food...Fixins Soul Kitchen is the place to be. Great atmosphere! Great staff! Great music!! Parking is limited but shouldn't have too much trouble finding some near by.

I visited this establishment two weeks ago on a Sunday. This would be my second time patronizing Fixins. My friends and I noticed that the Gospel music was really loud. It was to the point that we could not hear each other talk. I was a little taken aback because the first time I came the music was not that loud and the genre was R and B, and hip hop. We asked the waiter if she could have the music turned down a little and she said she would ask the manager. We also asked about gospel music-and she said it was always played on Sundays. Now, as a foodie, and restaurant critic, ambiance plays a great deal in your dining experience. If you cannot enjoy good conversation, drink libations, enjoy your food, and service with audible conversation your experience will more than likely be lackluster. Hence my point. 

Then the waitress came back, she informed us that the manager was not going to turn down the music and that he said we should come back on Monday.  WOW.  So, I am going to go ahead and ask the question...Why is it when black soul food restaurants get a following from the community, they do not feel like they need to treat their patrons with consideration when it comes to their business???? I'll wait. Ok, tired of waiting. Two things... when a manager/owner has this kind of mindset it speaks to their lack of emotional intelligence and how he views the consumers opinion.  So, in other words, if the manager does not care, that is a problem. A BIG PROBLEM.

Is FIXINS catered to a variety of individuals, Christians, Muslims, Buddhist all that could be of African descent, and the regular curious "allies"? Hmmmm. Lasty, from a Public Health perspective, blasting gospel music or any music at uncomfortable and unsafe decibels (dBA, a unit of sound proportion) can damage the hearing of those exposed on a long-term basis (like the employees), and people that bring their newborn babies, and toddlers. Sounds at or below 70 dBA are considered generally safe. But again, come back on Monday, right? The manager could put on headphones or go to church on Sunday to repent if needed. No pun intended-just an alternative-why expose the masses for one?

In closing as a Public Health professional and a proud member of the Black Diaspora, I want our people to exceed and showcase our best because we are capable and worthy of being the recipients of having Michelin rated chefs in our restaurants and having a safe and fun atmosphere where families, homies, girlfriends, and the like can come and enjoy.  I feel sorry and concerned for the employees most of all. Get your ears checked. As far as the food....my fried chicken was under cooked. Do better, I would like for you to succeed.

This was my first time trying this restaurant they open at 11 we got there at 10:30 and there was already a line...good sign. Our waitress Kat was an absolute doll! The food was great. I don't know what they do to that grape butter but it makes everything taste good. The banana pudding is to die for and the koolaid brought me back to my childhood. I will def be going back.

The fried chicken is so good. My sides were mac n cheese and charred okra, okra had bacon which I don't eat and that was a disappointment but the Mac n cheese is one of the best I've ever had. Definitely recommend trying those spot out. We went on a Monday at 1230 and had a 30 minute wait. Place was packed the entire time.

Customer Service Excellent! 
Music / Decor/ Bar Excellent!
We got there on a Wednesday around 11ish.....I was soooo disappointed with my Ox Tails that I was sooooo looking forward to. 
The SAUCE that tastes like Worcestershire it ruined it for me, along with the half way cooked rice underneath. I asked for side of GRAVY that's used for your Smothered Chicken and Pork Chops...MUCH  BETTER!!! Portion was little for the price. 

I will be back to try other dishes....and appetizers. 

I do hope that you decide to  change the way you serve your oxtails....Photo you have on your website is deceiving and nothing like what I received. Definitely noo fluffy rice in the middle of the oxtails as shown.

First, my sincere apologies for the late review. The STELLER service I received from BJ , the manager, the young lady who was our server and the young man outside who speaks Arabic MUST be acknowledged.  I was in Sacramento for my family reunion and the rave reviews I was told about the food at FIXINS, DID NOT DISSAPOINT! The food was DELICIOUS!   Second, We had an extremely large group, and BJ was able to accommodate us showing a great measure of patience and care for the elders ensuring they had comfortable seating.  Third, It's hard to choose a favorite.  We kicked off with fruit adorned mimosas, and the BEAST, SEASONED artichoke dip and fried boiled eggs. (Please add a non-pork option of Turkey bacon and Turkey sausage to garish the fried, boiled eggs).  I never had a fried chicken omelet before and Fixins did NOT DISSAPOINT!  The flavored butter was so good with my waffle.  I washed down my beautiful brunch with my cool glass of water with ample lemon wedges.  I can't wait to try the Oxtails my cousin raved about on my next visit.  Lastly, Now I must try the Los Angeles location. Bravo BJ.  You run a tight ship.  Keep up the good work.  Your chefs are AMAZING! P.S

Omg the food was delicious! And for the price you received huge portions! A few cons was the chicken was too crunchy but seasoned and cooked well. I'm used to home chicken and home chicken is not super crispy. The real con was the drinks for the price the drinks were extremely small and light on the alcohol they should revamp the drinks immediately! But the food was A1!  The service was great as well! We also loved the area and the decor of the restaurant!

I wish I could rate this place higher than 5 stars. I left a previous 4 star review a month or so ago on Google. The review would have been 5 stars but I was a little disappointed with the service during that visit. I wasn't able to write them another one there so I came here to give them their much deserved kudos. I have been a repeat customer here and hands down, today's experience was the best yet. I ordered what has become my regular. The fried deviled eggs, shrimp grits, unsweetened iced tea, and the buttermilk pie. The food was excellent as always. Consistent, good portion size, value for the price, and very satisfying. What separated today's experience from all the others was my waiter. I believe his name is Michael, based on the receipt, but his name tag said Ol School. Old School is EXACTLY what he delivered. Good old fashioned customer service. I could not have asked for a better waiter. He was observant but not intrusive. He predicted and anticipated my needs. This guy single handedly made the experience. My thoughts about patronizing a restaurant may differ from others but I enjoy quality food and I have a certain level of expectation when it comes to the servers that are hired to represent a company. Especially when I drive 20 plus miles to eat at a specific place. The behaviors that are tolerated from the staff says a lot about the ownership. Ol School is what I would consider to be a necessary part of a successful business model. My hat is off to him, the management, and the ownership. I will definitely try the Oxtails next time as he suggests.

*One small note I would like to add should the ownership read this. During 2 separate visits, I have been fortunate to be in the company of some of Sacramento's finest. One visit, Chief Hahn was dining with some of the HR staff for the City of Sacramento and today, the former Mayor, Mr. Johnson, was there on 2 separate occasions during my meal. I know both of these gentlemen are extremely busy and I can imagine interacting with the public and fans can be tiring, but, observing Mr. Johnson chat with some of the diners and not others could result in making some guests feel less important than others. Even just a brief hello can go a long way.

Great job Fixins. Looking forward to my next visit.
The food was delicious and the service was excellent. I would recommend this spot to anyone.

Sacramento is usually on my list to pass through and leave, this time, however, was different.
Couple or three persons within our circle says, "Hey Mania, you feel like some Soul Food?" I says, "you mean like SOULLLLLLLLLL TRAINNNNN?" They all laughed and called me the "H" word. That done, we found Fixins with SOULLLLLLLLLLLLLLLLLLLLL! The fried chicken was outstanding, matter a fact, we fought for the leftovers later in the car. One guy said he had neve had such GREAT FIXINGS in his entire life, so I says, "IT'S PRONOUNCED FIXIN'S...FOOL!!" Sucka' called me the "H" word again so I tossed his chicken wing out the car, stupid fool! All seriousness, GOTTA' LOVE FIXINS' (w/)SOULLLLLLLLLLLLL! (R.I.P. Don Cornelius, sorry had ta' throw that in there). 

Hello Becca P. (below). Why you would bring up your past has little or nothing to do with the FINE FIXIN'S here. Take your delusional self and go across the street to the help center. Your problems ain't got nothing to do with this GREAT RESTAURANT with SOULLLLLLLLLLLLLLLLLL! Nobody gonna' miss yo' "unfixable soul" at FIXINS'!

Back again yesterday, STILL de' BEST in SACTO, despite all you knuckleheads nay-sayers on here.
SOULLLLLLLLLLLLLLLLLLLLLLLLLLLLLLLLLLL!!!!!!!!!!

The best! Loved it. Had the gumbo it was so good. My daughter had the chicken and waffles another item so good my niece had the Mac cheese with fried chicken and yams all were really good and fresh. The banana pudding bomb! Will be back for sure!

First timers here and have to say we loved it!! The chicken and waffles were everything I expected and more.  So much flavor, juicy, & serving is plentiful.  We tried the greens and they were really good enough spice to them.  The staff helpful and nice.  Our server Chloé was a delight super sweet and very attentive. We will be going back for sure.  Too many dishes to choose from.  You will not be disappointed.

Have you ever been to a place that had a lot of food items you wanted to try but of course can't try them all in one sitting? This is one of those places. The food was great.. the options were attractive..and the food came out fast. I want to give this a 4.5 but that's not an option..I'm def going back.

This place has delicious food! Every single thing the table ordered was phenomenal. Went with a large group of girls on a busy Saturday night and still got good service. We weren't in a hurry and ordered drinks/sides/entrees and desserts. The grape butter was something special along with the shrimp and grits, Mac and cheese and greens. The area is questionable, but places like this will hopefully bring more business to help it grow to a place where people will want to frequent.

If you come here on a Sunday, expect a really long wait. With that being said, the wait is totally worth it! Best fried chicken I've ever had. Perfectly crispy outside and juicy seasoned meat on the inside. Delicious side dishes, and fluffy pancakes. Def a place to check out.

If you're in the soul food mood, this place will be hard to beat. My family tried catfish, fried chicken, and the fried chicken omlette, all of which were great. Now Fixins is the name so it's important to know their fixins (sides) we're all top notch too. The greens, fritters, cole slaw, biscuit, fries, and candied yams were all good to great. This is the perfect comfort food after doing on a 4.5 mile march!

Personally, I'm hankering to hit them up for a breakfast in the near future and get me the omlette or the old standby of chicken and waffles. This was our first visit, but definitely not our last. Only down side I could dream up is that I was craving some ribs, but maybe they have it as a special sometimes, I'm not sure. Lastly, all the staff was very aware and quick to ask if we needed anything, and our server Toni was a gem. When I ran out of water, she brought me another glass 3x bigger, problem solved, lol.
