Okay this place was good!! I ordered the fried green tomatoes, chicken and waffles and collard greens. The waffle wasn't the best, but everything else made up for it. My only wish is that they offered fried chicken livers (and that they were in my town)!

I'm so glad me and the family tried this place the food is so tasty I got the shrimp and grits my hubs got chicken sandwich with fries and my daughter got the mac n cheese with a drumstick boy was the mac n cheese delicious we all got Kool aid which was amazing the inside was clean and the service was top notch they all work together this place is a MUST TRY!
Good food and fast recommend for a game day! A good drink and just fun ambience. Dark Meat Chicken

We ordered our food to go online at 6:48 and were emailed that our food would be ready at 7:23. When we arrived at 7:23 we were told it would be an additional 30 minutes, making it ready by 7:53. 

Our food was actually ready at 8:21

Came here a few weeks ago with my nephew. We dined insidThe waitress was busy and took a while. Then once she took our order it took 15 minutes for the drinks to come and 45 minutes for our meal to come. I ordered the 3 piece meal with waffle which was okay. The blueberry butter and waffle were super sweet without syrup. The waitress too a long time even check up on us after we finally received our food. It was as if all staff went and remained in the kitchen. I had to get up and ask the bartender for hot sauce. Basically, customer service could be better.
It was busy, so we sat at the bar. The bartender was friendly and made suggestions. The service was excellent and the food was fantastic. Will be back for sure.

This place is great especially during Covid. Open patio seating. I thought it would be a long wait but we got seated fast for 7 people at 6:30. Jasmine, our waitress was bomb, she gave us 2 sweet tea to go when we received the check.

The food was great. The staff was very friendly. Good size portions of food. I wish there was more than one location but I definitely will be coming back whenever I am in Sacramento... 

The peach cobbler could use more crust and less cinnamon.

This was one of those visits that was made possible by the Yelp community! I was looking for a dinner spot and came across Fixins and saw its rating and reviews. I stopped by with a friend who is local to Sacramento before catching my flight home. There was a wait for a Sunday night but we were able to get a table at the bar. It was fairly busy and it took a while for the bartender to take our order. I also understand that we were occupying a space with no intention of ordering alcohol but it saved us from waiting. After we ordered we waited no more than 20 minutes before our food got to us. The hot chicken sandwich was delicious, juicy, and had a kick to it the bartender warned me about. The fries were delicious and used as a way to pick up any fallen coleslaw.
Amazing.  Delicious food, good selection of drinks and great service.  Can't ask for more than that.

Food was good but not sure if the wait was worth it. It wasn't just the wait to sit down but the wait for the food and check was just as long. You cannot join the waitlist before arriving or call to save your seat. Only allowed to put your name down upon arrival. 

Kool-aid was the best part! If you come, make sure to order the Kool-aid. Catfish nuggets were really good. I got chicken and waffle and it was ok. My waffle got soggy while waiting for the server to bring me syrup. My boyfriend ordered the spicy chicken sandwich and it was drenched in hot sauce. It was almost ineligible due to how spicy it was. Overall the food was okay but again not sure if it was worth the wait. Really wanted to love this place but sadly it was a miss for me.

This places got swag and hospitality. Charred okra is bomb. Of course you cannot go wrong with chicken and waffles. Portions are large. They also serve adult koolaids. Parking on broadway is ok. Just don't park at the limited time zone. They will tow!

It's so hard to find good soul food, especially soul food without pork added into greens and beans for flavor. Greens with smoked turkey and pork-less black eye peas are some of my favorites! I often go meatless and love that I can make a meal out of their sides and it is delicious!

Brought my fiance here for his bday, we both loved it! We ordered a bunch of items of the menu to try out. Everything was amazing! 

Pros: The food here is straight fire!!  --soooo good. Fried chicken is crispy, juicy and fresh. Candied yams are sweet to perfection. Oxtails over rice do not disappoint. Mac and cheese is made with real cheese (they do not cut corners). Cornbread is just right. SWEET TEA and KOOL AID are beverages options (both made with real sugar). The ambiance is great. Speedy and professional service. Clean space. Parking can be found even during peak hours! 

Cons: NONE.

Overall, I really recommend this place. 5/5 in my book!

We had a work party at this place when they first opened. The food was pretty great. The service was pretty good as well. I think they still had a few kinks to work out but overall I think this place is going to be amazing. 

Their passable apps for a standing party style event were fancy for soul food and quite delicious. The only reason I give a four is because the bar setup for the outdoor area was a little frustrating. Probably one of the kinks and most likely figured out by now, as this was in September. 

I'll definitely be going back for a proper meal. The food was too good not to get a full portion of.

I didn't expect much when we decided to give Fixins a try, considering most of the items on the menu either myself or someone else in my family can make and make very well. I was very surprised by the great flavors and depth of the flavors in the foods I tried! Everything was delicious. I started out with the adult kool aid. Which was great. Then the fried deviled eggs. They were bomb. I've only had cold or room temp deviled eggs. These fried deviled eggs were very satisfying. You have the crunch from the perfectly fried egg portion, then the tasty filling and the great texture and chew. A great starter. My weakness is soda, that being said my sprite came is a big ass 24 oz jar and was carbonated just right. The shrimp and grit were the best I e ever had from any restaurant and from home or any family member. The gravy was on point the green and red bell peppers were not mushy and the onions were perfect. The prawns were perfection plump, sweet and savory at the same time. The fried chicken was perfectly breaded , seasoned, fried and still juicy. The greens were the best I've had a a restaurant ever. Still can't beat my uncle Artists' smoked greens. My wife said the Mac n Cheese was the best she's had at a restaurant. Service was quick and friendly. We will certainly be back to try more items.

Bummer, quality was not as good as my 1st visit. Busy night. Ran out of yams, and all gravy. Took 20 minutes to get drinks at the table. Over an hour for food. Red velvet cake was wonderful. Catfish was cooked perfectly but nothing special. Kids fried chicken was stupid spicy and she couldn't eat it. Fyi, we should have silverware before the food... oh, and should not have to wait 5 minutes after you bring the kid waffles for syrup. Boo

Great ambiance, southern food and service. We loved the Deep Fried Deviled Eggs, Fried Chicken and Fried Green Tomatoes.  It was fun to meet the owner who apparently is there frequently talking to his guests which is a sign of a terrific restaurant.

If you are looking for a fun time and great southern food choices, you have to try "Fixins"

The BEST fried chicken I've ever had and the BEST in California for sure! Everything I ate from the chicken, waffles, fried deviled eggs (A MUST), to the slaw, etc. It was all so delicious!! The only challenge is that it gets really busy, so the wait time is less than ideal and the service goes down a little because it's jam packed. Worth it though!  

And if you're a basketball fan, the cherry on top is that you might get to see Kevin Johnson there.

Whew Chile. Let me tell you about how good the Lord is. This place took me back to my grandmas kitchen. Everything on my plate had so much flavor. I gave up all my diet. The two chicken plates comes with two fixins that are nothing but tasty. I was smack in' the entire meal. The Lord is good all the time. I had to loosen my belt strap after this meal. The bartender Asia was amazing. must go. Bring your kids. Bring your grandmas. But don't come on Sunday because I'm coming back then. Okurrrrrrr

First of all, I love the ambiance and aesthetic of the place. The staff, however, was a bit lacking; mainly due to their lack of enthusiasm. But our server gave good recommendation and explained some items kn the menu well.

I got the gumbo and I really love it -- this is the reason for the 4 star review. However, my girlfriend got the chicken sandwich and it seemed like the chicken had been sitting for a while. We also got some fixins: the collard greens and mac and cheese. Both were pretty good in my opinion. 

Overall, it's a 4 star rating due to the gumbo. My points of improvement would be on having a more welcoming staff and bringing out freshly friend chicken.

What a beautiful space to eat soul food with friends and family! 
Great dining experience indoors or out. Great ambiance! 

The food was awesome! We ordered catfish, fried chicken, waffles, oxtails, banana pudding, peach cobbler. Everything was delicious and to our liking!
 
I would highly recommend this restaurant for excellent food and service.

My first review was removed.  So sad.  

I did eat here.  I found the food ok and the service was moderately bad.  The decor left much to be desired. 

The owner of this establishment has a very complicated past.   He sat in the corner the entire time we were there.   It made me very uncomfortable.   

Supporting this restaurant means that you do not support survivors of sexual assault.  Avoid avoid avoid!

I'm sure Yelp will remove this post because KJ doesn't like being reminded he's worse than Trump.  

Believe survivors.   AVOID!
I really enjoyed my experience and service here at Fixins. The fried tomatoes were my favorite! I had the amazing shrimp & grits and a chicken wing on the side. Coffee & water was great.
Pa POW  soooooo good!! Chicken  and waffles are life. They do it right!! Yes you better go. Like now!

This place is good. I had the fried chicken and my friend had the gumbo. I had one of their kool-aid cocktail drinks which was delicious. Service was good. Wonderful lighting. All good things. No complaints.

We finally got to try Fixins this past weekend after seeing all of our friends posting their Food on Social Media. We went on Saturday and had to wait for about 50 minutes, but it was WELL WORTH THE WAIT!  Everything from the Service and MIST Importantly the Food was AWESOME. The only negative was that they ran out of the Charred Okra right when we were about to order at 6:00.. Since we didn't get to try the Okra, WE HAD to come back Sunday and try the Rest of the menu and of course the Okra which they Thankfully had that day..  THIS PLACE IS A MUST and I can't wait to come back for the Chicken Sandwich and Chicken n Waffles..

Finally came to Fixins to get my fried chicken fix! Definitely worth checking out. Great prices and a nice patio with social distancing. Thanks to the nice staff for the experience, will definitely come back!

I've been seeing this place all over my Facebook timeline with raving reviews.  One day after visiting the Plant Foundry I decided to eat lunch at Fixins.  It wasn't at capacity but it was definitely JUMPING!  When I entered the establishment I wasn't greeted so I walked up to a lady with a name tag on that read Myako. I approached her and said that I was dining alone. She immediately offered me a seat at the bar. I told her that I would much prefer a table. She then told me it would be a half hour wait.  I found that to be outrageous due to the several empty tables in sight. Nonetheless I said I would wait. Several guests came in and was placed on a list. It dawned on me that the host never placed me on the list. I alerted the host and she reassured me that I was on the list and once again offered me a seat at the bar. I quizzed her on my name and she was clueless.   I then asked if I could be seated at the small 2 person table by the front entrance that  had been unoccupied for the past 15 minutes.  The host looked annoyed and offered me the table with a slight attitude.  I didn't mean to upset her but I didn't want to sit at the bar nor did I want to sit for a half hour NEXT to a vacant table.  She definitely does not need to be the first person of contact in that establishment.  Once seated I actually contemplated leaving.  

Ok enough of Myako.  

Once seated I was greeted and given a menu by a tall young lady.  The menu was had an assortment of southern delicacies. I wish I could've tried them all but I would definitely need a pay raise because I found the food to be on the pricy side especially for lunch. Also they didn't have any small bites. The average entree started at $15-$17 and a beverage was $4-$5.  Definitely should look into lunch pricing. Especially if you're looking for customers to stop by for lunch several times a week.  I heard the father of the family seated next to me humorously gasp at the bill he received for 2 adults and 2 children. 
I ordered the chicken and waffles which to my amazement was very reasonably priced at $9.99.  And I also ordered a side of their baked Mac and cheese. The Mac and cheese had cheddar on top that was no where near melted. Kinda like carrot  shavings. After saying that, it was actually delicious if you disregarded the appearance and lowered your expectations of what mac and cheese is supposed to look like. 
Ok on to the main course. The waffles were really really delicious. And there was this purple butter on top. I looked over the menu and could not find a description of what it was because it was absolutely AMAZING!  I late was told that it was grape jelly and butter .  I just knew it was something extravagant. 
The chicken was Uber-AMAZING!  Something in that breading as addicting.  Once I finished eating everything of those bones, I reopened the menu so I could order more.  I then realized that would cost me another $15 and that would just be ridiculous. 
Other than the price and the attitude I received upon entering the establishment, this place is pretty  could actually become a fixture in this community.

The food was not good at all, we had smothered pork chops, no season and most all the batter became part of the gravy which the gravy was gross! We also ordered catfish nuggets and the portion for $11 looked like they cut one nugget in mini bits. Again no seasoning fish was very hot where you could not chew without blowing on it. And how can they mess up artichoke dip! This restaurant is over rated if you just like ambience and so so service go. One last thing we asked the waitress if this was real soul food she didn't commitment but her facial expression should have made us run! We wanted dessert but passed and figured there was no Soul in this food. We would have complained but figured why because we should have known when they put pickled onions on smoothed pork chops we were not in a real Black Soul food restaurant. If I could rated it starless I would have. Don't believe the good reviews!
Amazing service and always a fun vibe when in town. It is always hard to choose what I want when I go so I end up ordering a lot of sides and a meal to go!

Visiting Sacramento decided to try this new establishment before heading out to a show. The interior is new, clean and modern. Wait staff was very pleasant, willing to help and on top of their job. I ordered the chicken waffles with a side of Mac n cheese. My mother had the gumbo. We were both happy with our choices. I think the restaurant has a few kinks to work out but overall great experience I will return to try other items.

Ate here for the first time with my family and it was okay. There was a bit of wait to be seated and the food took a while to come out. The fried green tomatoes with the sauce was by far my favorite! Super crunchy and just absolutely delicious! I also tried a piece of my husband's catfish (not a huge fan of catfish) but it was really good, well seasoned and crispy! The potato salad was also really good. I appreciate the portion sizes as my meal came with three pieces of chicken and you get to pick! I would challenge them to use a little more seasoning but the chicken was juicy- breast. The red beans and rice was my least favorite with no flavor at all! My family did say that the macaroni and cheese and candied yams were very good!
Everything we had was delicious. Kool aid, fried green tomatoes, chicken and waffles... yummy! The service was excellent and quick! The fried green tomatoes were our favorite. We still crave them!

FIXIN'S SOUL FOOD WITH A TWIST!
This is surely the next new spot to get your grub on when you need a soul food fix.  This spot surely delivers the traditional but with a twist.  For example, the hot peach cobbler was served up warm with sweet thick cream instead of vanilla ice cream and the strong beans was dressed up with tomato and chunks of thick bacon. The difference was very pleasing.   My meal was supurb.  There were only happy" satisfied people. The ambiance was like a family gathering.  There us also a full bar featuring all the southern style drinks you can think of.  Very friendly atmosphere and multicultural staff and clientele.
Greatest food I ever tasted in a local restaurant .I adore the employers with much respect....Great Service..

Fixins never disappoints. If you're craving down to earth- home made soul food, this is your place! Fried chicken is hot, fresh and juicy as hell. Plus, they throw down on making great cocktails! If you're cruising thru Oak Park, stop by and eat this bomb food.
Delicious! We all got something different, chicken sandwich, fried chicken, pork chops, fried green tomatoes, fried eggs, lol, we couldn't finish all of it

This place was so delicious. Upon arriving we did have to wait about 15 minutes for a table of two during lunch time. The pimento cheese deep was delicious! So cheesy. The fried chicken was so crispy on the outside and tender on the inside. The hot chicken does not mess around, it is hot! But so good if you like hot stuff. The chicken was so tender and crispy on the outside. The sides, mac and cheese and potato salad did not disappoint. They were delicious.
The staff has great personality!  Kat, you crazy girl! The food was good and their Proverb Pinot Grigio was delicious.

Huge shoutout to Stefani Cortes for going above and beyond trying to navigate me through the online website; on how to make a reservation for the Dunlap online. I'm not the best at technology, but she definitely helped me out by walking me through the steps. Thank you for helping me out and being a professional worker.

I gave this place two tries and both they came up short in delivering good soulfood. Gravy on my pork chops was sweet not sure where they do that at, sides were below average not only that but I didn't get my complete order on my pickup order. I will not be trying again.
Great food and customer service.. we love the ambiance is is inciting and the vibe was excellent we will definitely be back again!!!
Had catfish nuggets while waiting for my orders Togo.  The catfish was delicious. When I got home and tasted the chicken it was just ok . The yams was wayyy too sweet and the whole catfish was thin .

While checking out the cute shops in Oak Park we decided to stop for a bite. This place did not disappoint at all! We sat on the patio because #covid and it was just lovely. Extremely clean, awesome service and the food was ah-maz-ing! New stop on our way back to Reno from now on. Thanks Fixins!!!

Traveling once to Hawaii we met Kevin Johnson and had a nice chat with him and he mentioned his restaurant, Fixins Soul Kitchen.  There are many great places to eat in the mid-town area but this is truly authentic and serious comfort food.  I wish we would have tried it sooner.  This is the best place to eat in Oak Park and surrounding areas.  Thanks Kevin.

Everything was so good... the drinks , food , service!!! Very nice to children as well and that's tuff in a busy restaurant!! Can't wait till our next visit! We had a table of 12 plus 3 kids at A separate table, seems like they can only seat 12 inside we just had a separate table on the side for the kids still worked out  perfect  thanks again!

Great ambience, friendly staff, reasonably priced menu. Plus it's Black owned. So far everything I've ordered has been on point and accommodations met. However, all the bartenders should be on the same page when drink making...no matter who pours it, it should taste the same. Right or wrong? It's all good in the neighborhood though, and we love to support local!

Went here the other day with my girlfriend around 11 am, we were seated within a minute, and greeted within 3. The pink and blue kool-aid was really good. The only problem was my girlfriend had to pay $14 for her 2 chicken strips and waffle while I paid $9 for 2 chicken wings and a waffle. The server did not tell us it was that much of an up charge, that's where his tip went. Other than that, the food was great.

I went on  a Tuesday evening ordered  oxtails an Mac n cheese with sweet potatoes on thE side. Food came out quick didn't have to waiit long however the oxtails didn't seem fresh it was steaming hot but more like warm looked as if it had been leftovers an put in a bowl over rice heated up. I most likely will not be returning.

Very good soul food in Sacramento. Moms small inside and we have a bit of a wait, but it was worth it! Try the cherry bomb. It's made with red cherry kool-aid. Our server was excellent and very personable. Her name was Chloe(?), I think. Best addition to the place.

Not sure if we caught them on an off day but food and service was horrible. Our Party of 15 came in for a birthday dinner. Took our server 20 mins to get our drinks and another 20 to get our food orders. When the food finally came out, 3 meals had uncooked rice and came out cold to touch. It was 2 orders of the smothered ox tail ($30) and gumbo with uncooked rice and cold. Our server came back apologized and asked if there was another food dish we wanted instead since their pot of rice was no good. They brought out another round and the food was still cold. They ended up taking it off our tab. I was really bummed because i heard so many good things about this place but it was just a horrible experience all together. My fried chicken was over fried and our buns we paid $6 extra for was cold. I probably won't even bother coming back to try it again just because the experience and food was a 1/10.
So delicious! Food is always on point and service is always amazing!! My family and I travel from Oakland just to get our Fixins fix!!

food was great however service was HORRIBLE!!! this was my first time at Fixins, and i was very excited to try their chicken. service was excellent at first but we waited AN HOUR just to get the check closed, to get to go boxes, and to have the correct receipt. the bussers/hosts did not help the server at all which was frustrating to see. they just stood there in front of our table doing NOTHING. not checking in on our table and others at all, and would ignore looking at customers if they needed anything

Again, I loved the food and the vibes but I hope the manager really retrains the staff on how to work effectively together and provide decency in good customer service.

Unfortunately, customer service was a fail here.  Entered Fixins at 8:50P,  we stood at the entrance and waited to be greeted/seated.  At 9:07 we were finally approached by a male  staff member and he stated that they closed a 9P but he will ask if we can be accommodated/ seated.  He proceeds to ask a female staff member, she looks bewildered and tells him to ask someone else.  They all go to the back behind closed doors.  Long story short, a different staff member now approaches and basically states that they stopped seating guests at, 8P due to the wait!  So that means new guests that entered were told to leave... for an entire hour... hmmm.  We left disappointed and feeling misinformed.  

That's unfortunate for business and guests.  I realize that this is a new and popular restaurant but regardless how delicious the food may be, customer service has to be on point as well.  I really wish we been not been left standing at the entrance for so long and informed right away that you stopped seating guests at 8P.

I can't review the food or service,because I didn't experience neither one.

I've heard good things about the restaurant and food so I wanted to try it out. It was the day after labor day(9/3) and I give them 2 calls after they have opened...no one answers so later in the day my mom and I decide to make the 20 minute drive to the restaurant. We had already decided what we wanted off the menu. It's around 2pm and the restaurant is packed and there is also a wait. Immediately when you walk through the door you are greeted with the faces of the people waiting to be seated. Waiting area is very small.So I go to the hostess stand and she asks me how many people,I let her know I wanted to do a Togo. She informed me that they currently aren't doing Togo's  so I ask if it was just for right now or in general she stated that they are not set up for Togo orders and that they are waiting for the manager to set everything upI said ok and walked out!

Now, I understand you are a new restaurant...HOWEVER how are you not set up for Togo orders? Not everyone wants to sit at a restaurant to eat not only that parking is scarce in the area.So what do you guys do if someone sits down to eat and they have left overs??? I guess I'm just a little confused as to how this came about when it's not like you woke up yesterday and decided to open a restaurant. Togo orders should have been part of the planning. No where does it state you don't do Togo orders and you guys don't even answer the phone for customers to even ask!

Amazing food ! Just how brunch with family vans friends. Great decor and amazing staff. They were super sensitive to my son with Autism. Made a quit spot for him during a very busy time. The fried deviled eggs were the best thing I have had on a long time. Kool Aid on the menu is genius .  Recommend it to everyone!

Food was delicious staff is friendly price is good.  Menu is not overwhelming.   Location is in a upcoming neighborhood will return in the near future.   The wait time was short less than 30 minutes and I had a party of 5

First timer here at Fixins. The wait time wasn't bad at at all. The layout of the restaurant is really cool and welcoming. Now let get to the food and drinks. They really serve kool-aid! I feel like a kid again and had to get some blue kool-aid just for fun! They do have a lot of other drinks. I definitely recommend getting the fried deviled eggs (amazing)! We had to get another order. And the rest of the food is amazing as well! We will be back again!

I actually got to enjoy some yummy Soul food here at Fixins during their soft opening with a bunch of my gal pals from work. I was the first to arrive and I seen a huge crowd already so I went right in to place our group on the waiting list. 

The restaurant is modern, clean but a bit small for this already popular place. There is a full bar, private area for intimate events and an outdoor patio. The seating is a mix of sitting tables, bar tables and booths. The "waiting" area consists of two small cushioned benches at the entrance but if weather permits you can sit outside.

Now down to the gritty aka the food!! I ordered the fried catfish that comes with two sides so I got greens and mac & cheese. My mouth is already salivating just thinking about how damn good it all was! The catfish was juicy inside with a light and flaky outside, the greens was meh and the Mac & cheese was fire! This meal made me think Kevin Johnson was holding someone's granny hostage in the kitchen because it was that good! Oh and to wash it all down I had the "red" koolaid to top it all off! I mean I was sold on the koolaid when it had colors not the actual flavors on the menu! Haha. 

If you haven't already, you need to eat here! For the price and the quality of the food, you'll leave satisfied and already making plans to return! Just beware, the wait can be long but worth it so plan accordingly!
Always delicious... great service. Fried catfish, greens, blackeyed peas, cheesey grits, cornbread, soul Caesar, Oak Park IPA and peach sangria ... all on point.
Everything is amazing. The only reason I'm giving it 4 stars is because I truly disliked their coffee.
Great ambience and the food was delicious ! We had oxtails, rice n gravy, Mac n cheese, greens and smothered chicken! All was an A+
Fried chicken! Mac n Cheese! The cornbread and biscuits !The Ox Tail!  The service ! The vibe! Everything at Fixins was amazing! Most def coming back here!
Food was delicious!! First time in Sac town and happy I came across this restaurant!!

My first time and I'm sure I'll be visiting again when in the area. New establishment and it's definitely odd to a good start. First, I'll start with my drink choice Sweet Tea and it was "SWEET". I don't get sweet tea vary often so it struck me by surprise but if you don't like it that sweet go with the Arnold Palmer. My appetizer or as they called it LiL' Bits was Fried Deviled Eggs and they were delicious the filling to the touch of mayo, bacon and hot sauce. I wouldn't have guessed they would be that good. Lastly , I chose a breakfast order 2 wings and a waffle and after those deviled eggs I knew it would satisfy my taste buds as well. I would have  preferred a boneless chicken option and I dont think that was an option. Overall, nice atmosphere on the loud side but what's to expect when your enjoying good food. I didn't have room for dessert but on another occasion I will likely try the banana pudding. I did place a to go order of Gumbo for my daughter and she said it was good. 
SN: parking can be a challenge as most parking spaces near I'm sure fill fast and the others are 1hr and 2hr parking. I found a 2hr a short distance away so not bad but I was there around 1pm.

I've been wanting to try this restaurant for awhile. I love soul food so finally we went. The fried chicken had a sweet batter so I didn't like it. The cat fish nuggets was bomb but portions were small. The oxtails were cooked to perfection. More rice would be better. I didn't like their lemonade Or Appletini, it was hella strong. The also do not carry coke so how do you make a jack n coke? We saw Kevin Johnson and his wife but never did they once at look at us or come and greet his customers. The manager Rhee looks like a relative of Michelle since they have the same last name. I noticed he was on his phone the whole time. He could of been helping out his employees because the service was a bit slow. The red velvet cake was cut small and dry so I am disappointed because I paid $10 The food is expensive so I'm not sure if I will be back.

So let me start off by saying I was so excited to read all the 5 star reviews and even my coworkers went here on grand opening night and gassed this place up, so I was totally hype when I decided to go the other day. Waited 2.5 hrs for a table and an additional hour for food. I heard good things about everything food wise so we ordered almost everything ox tails, Mac and cheese, red beans and rice, fried chicken omelet, cheese grits, chicken strips, fried chicken, French fries, fried deviled eggs (which was the BEST thing here), collard greens, waffles, spinach artichoke dip, and peach cobbler. EVERYTHING was mediocre at best but a few items I will highlight. The cheese grits, served with a dollop of Mac n cheese as the 'cheese' on top-disgusting. Fried chicken/chicken strips had NO seasoning bland!. Spinach and artichoke dip was served with RITZ CRACKERS when the menu stated it was served with toasted bread. Peach cobbler I was so upset with, the crust or topping was like a edible paper that wouldn't tear and served cold. Definitely not worth the 4 hrs of my day or the 50+ bucks I spent here... I would probably try this place once the thrill dies down in 6 months to 2 years. Highly disappointed!

I honestly almost cried reading the menu. Lol. I thought it was just TOO good to be true. But I was wrong! It was true!! Everything we had was good! And the ambiance was great! I loved the style # the are, the dishes. The service was great too. I will definitely revisit this location.

Went here tonight got two plates of fried chicken with fixin's. 
1. Chicken: needed salt. Had lots of flavor, but very little salt.  And while the thighs were over crunchy the drums were under crunchy. 3 stars
2. Ocra: Great. 5 stars
3. Collard Greens: Excellent. 5 stars
4. Coleslaw: not too sweet and perfectly acidic: 5 stars. 
5. Biscuits: these could have been 5 stars had they not been over brown and burned tasting on the bottom. 4 stars
6. Grape Jelly Butter. 5 stars.  
7. Mac and cheese: these had potential. But they were served to me barely warm and congealed. I ate them cause I was hungry. But... 3 stars is as high as I can rate them. 

Overall, the places is aptly named. There was a line so we sat at the bar. The bar service was good. The kitchen was on the slow side. And the left out the grape butter I requested on the biscuits to go. 

The people sitting next to us got the smothered chicken and the braised ox tail, and I felt like maybe we should have tried one of those. Maybe next time. If there is a next time. There are a lot of Southern places to try in Sacramento. And I really do want to see any of them make better fried chicken.

Upon arrival I couldn't help but notice the crowd of people waiting outside which I thought was a good sign. It was Saturday around 7-8pm so they were pretty poppin. Wasn't able to check in on the wait list beforehand on Yelp so the wait was 45 min to an hour. 
We only waited about 40 minutes or so but it was definitely worth it. The fried chicken is really tasty with the right amount of crunch to the batter and tenderness and juiciness of the meat. The gravy used on the smothered chicken is delicious and not too savory. Their mac and cheese is bomb with the right consistency. 
Their candied yams were perfect too. And their biscuits are really yummy with a hint of sweetness like their specialty butter. The butter is pinkish with a fruity hint, it's amazing, truly one of a kind. 
Although, I wasn't a big fan of their collard greens, mainly because I didn't know they were cooked with (what I'm assuming) pork until I got it. I wouldn't order it again. 
I also ordered the fried green tomatoes as an appetizer but they took extremely long to come out. Our main dishes arrived just moments after. The chef apologized and was polite enough to comp us for the tomatoes because of the wait time. 
The tomatoes were really good although the slices were small in my opinion (4 pcs). I enjoyed the chipotle mayo so I wish there was either a bit more or they gave you some on the side for dipping instead. The garnishes used such as sprouts, red peppers, and watermelon radish were great compliments. 
Overall, I truly did enjoy my experience here and the food here is delicious! It shows in the product that the person in charge cares a lot about their work. Will be returning!

I'm so glad I tried this restaurant I'm not disappointed at alll. It's black owed. I had the cat fish yams and black eyed peas. Everything was superb ratings is over the top very very pleased. I w be back for sure. I can't stop thinking about the food and flavor. My waitress was alena she was great.
Clean restaurant. Food was delicious and flavorful. We got the oxtails, greens, & catfish.

Checked this out last week and it was great. I have the catfish and I loved it everything was very fresh. Much more on the menu I want to try need to go back to. 

I have now been here three times and thrilled every time. Old School was our waiter and he was fantastic. If you haven't tried the rum raisin pudding yet it is to die for almost better than sex  Will definitely be going back.

Edit: Kevin the owner reached out to me and informed me that unfortunately there has been an error made on my meal cost for the upgraded pieces of white meat chicken. He graciously offered me an eight dollar credit for next visit, talk about customer service! Everybody was wonderful from the manager, to the hostess and servers, the bartenders and now the owner. This is how you run a business! Very impressed.

I have to say I was pleasantly surprised by this restaurant. When I was told the restaurant was in Oak Park, I was very curious, I know they are fixing up a portion of that area, as it does have a reputation for being on the rough side, But it was quite charming. We got there around 2:30 on Sunday for brunch, there was already a line out the door, took a look around and saw all the diversity of the clientele so we knew we had to eat there. We were seated in about 15 minutes, then we waited an additional 15 minutes before anyone was even over to our table. We didn't fault them for that though, they were packed both inside and outside on the patio area. Granted, they are a bit on the pricey your side, but I don't think you're going to find better soul food in Sacramento to be fair. Now the food didn't quite taste like soul food you'd find in the heart of Louisiana made by someone who's been using recipes handed down from generation to generation or anything, but it was still pretty good. My husband and I got the fried chicken plates and those came with your choice of two sides and a biscuit. I got the mac & cheese and the fried okra, both were amazing, my husband got the red beans and rice and the grits with cheese. Neither of us particularly cared for his choices. One of our friends got the braised oxtail dish, mouthwatering and amazing!, The other friend got fried chicken on a waffle. She said that the waffle was yummy but a basic waffle. Fried Green tomatoes were very good, and the peach cobbler at the end of our meal was the perfect way to tie the meal together. I will admit, my chicken was a tiny bit dry, and when ordering I ordered three pieces of white meat thinking I would be getting three chicken breasts. I was definitely incorrect. Found out that chicken wings are actually considered white meat, so I paid an additional eight dollars for the two extra pieces of white meat chicken, I was planning to share with the table after all, and received two wings for that eight dollars. Sort of wished I had done the half-and-half, half white meat half dark. Four dollars a wing seems pretty steep. We got Moscow Mule's for three and mimosas for three, alcohol is definitely not cheap but the hardest part about this meal was easily the tab. It was $160 for the table, not including tip. I'm all about quality over quantity, but they are definitely a costly soul food restaurant..FYI. Still- Can't wait to go back to try the other options on the menu that we didn't get to sample, so many great options!
Went here s couple of weeks ago and I am hooked. Had the catfish with mach and cheese and greens. And side piece of a chicken wing. (First time here had to try it). Everything is delicious.

Oh HELL NO!!  That's not soul food. That must be SOLE FOOD. 
The food is bland and without flavor. I'm positive they got Becky back there in the kitchen!
The food is a total joke and worse than 'Olivia's' in Old Sac  back in the day.
When this novelty wears off. This place will close so quick the name will forever be a "remember when".
Wont see a three year anniversary.

hands down the BEST soul food in CA!! 
from collard greens to waffles to fried chicken to fried mac n cheese...EVERYTHING is PERFECT! they fry like nobody

FAVES:
blood mary - get it spicy!! great addition to any entree.
presentation was phenomenal. the bacon was CRISY!
chicken and waffles - best waffles ever. it's not the belgian thick kind. it's on the thinner side but so dang good. it comes with syrup that's not super gooey or sweet and strawberry butter. i think it was $10-11. good price for a good drink!

collard greens - any chance u have to order a side option GET THE COLLARD GREENS. THIS IS MANDATORY! i kid u not. these greens are life changing. even if i don't like greens, u will LOVE these!!  juicy and not bitter in any sense. come see me if u don't like it.  

fried catfish - crispy but lighter than a cloud. loved the  tartar sauce that it comes with.  

smoother fried chicken - i didn't care for the garvey smothered as named bc i feel like i want my fried chicken CRISPY, so we got it i the side. it worked out better i ate all the skin and left the meat for the others  

cornbread - $4, but worth $20!  it's has a light crunchy on the edges but with buttery goodness. 

some will say maybe i was hungry bc everything was good but ya know what? i've been back every time i'm in sac and it hits right EVERY SINGLE TIME.

ON THE FENCE:
fried deviled eggs - yes, they are fried. we ordered one to fry and my boy got a second order just for himself even after he was full. i thought it was a bit salty but wash it down with a beer and i'm sure it'll be oooookay.  

let's just say, you will be spending some $$ here, but it's worth it. i prob wouldn't do the deviled eggs tho bc it's only 4 pieces for $11(which is actually only 2 eggs. 

best part..they opened up another location at LA LIVE! let's gooooo!

Their chicken sandwich is my fave..and I've had a lot of chicken sandwiches. Their shrimp and grits are the only reason I'm knit picking and giving 4 stars - it's delicious, but there's not a good ratio of grits to gravy...very little grits with lots of gravy..but the good news is the gravy is hella bomb, so you can dip whatever you have in the gravy overage. If you don't want shrimp, get the side grits with cheese and a side of gravy. A small ramekin of gravy is $3, but you really taste the layers of flavor. This place is the real deal. Oh and the peach cobbler. It's pricey, $10 for a serving with ice cream that, size wise, seemed it should be less..but it's got so much flavor. Just do it. It's a place that we def want to take out of towners to impress.

Stopped in on a Friday night at 5:45pm during their soft opening for an early dinner & it was PACKED! We waited about 15 mins for a table and the line kept growing and growing after us. With lines like these, I was thinking the food better be good. And it did not disappoint!

Once we got a table we were greeted by our friendly server right away. I ordered a 2 piece fried-chicken meal that comes with a biscuit & 2 fixins, so I chose collard greens & mac & cheese. I requested one dark meat & one white meat chicken pieces (since you have the option for both). The hubby got the hot chicken sandwich which came with fries. Food came out within 10-15 mins &  everything we ate was very hearty & absolutely delicious! The portions were big & everything was nice and hot. 

We had a little confusion with the white vs. dark meat chicken when my plate came out. It was plated with a thigh & a wing, both of which I would consider dark meat. So I asked the waitress to see if they messed up my order, and she returned and told me the kitchen says chicken wings are considered white meat. I was under the impression that only the breast was white meat, so I thought I was going to get a piece of breast meat. She kindly switched out my wing for a piece of breast, the gracious server that she was. However, next time I would check with my waiter to see what pieces of meat are classified as what. Because if I ordered a plate with all white meat & got two wings I would be pretty disappointed.

Regardless of our little snafu, the waitress handled everything courteously and quickly so it did Not disrupt our dinner too much. I actually LOVED the fried chicken breast the most, it was not dry in the slightest (that 24 hour brine works magic!), & the batter was so crispy and peppery. The Hot chicken sandwich appeared to be very similar to the chicken sandwich on the menu, except it has hot sauce drizzled on top & spicy mayo for a $1 more. They do leave a bottle of hot sauce on the table that I suppose you can turn all meals into hot versions!

Overall, this restaurant is an amazing addition to the neighborhood. The atmosphere here is awesome, people are lively and smiling all the time. The food is fantastic, not the kind of meal my waistline could handle every day, but certainly to fulfill my fried chicken cravings. I love that they serve breakfast all day & the wide variety of fixins they have on the menu, no 2 meals will be the same here if you mix & match! The waiting area is pretty small, but you can order a a giant mason jr of Kool-Aid, a fun cocktail, or other drink at the bar while you wait! People started hanging out on the patio once the weather got cooler, so outdoor seating seems to be an option. And there's even a secondary room in the back (towards the bathrooms) with tables for overflow seating. Or, maybe it could be used as an event space? Hmm, a birthday party here would probably be super fun & I know the food would be excellent!
The food here is amazing. I come all the way from the Bay Area just to get to them. It's definitely worth the drive.
Delicious and teal sweet tea... friendly staff... No waiting... Patio dining option. I will be back.

Went on a Saturday, party of 5, around 1pm. My mom and I got there before the rest of the group so we checked in around 12:40p as the yelp checkin feature did not work. We were quoted at 30-45 minutes, the restaurant was very busy. 

Once seated we were given menus and then left to review them (with a 45 minute wait we all already knew what we wanted to get.) we ordered the following: I got the 2 piece fried chicken (light meat) with Mac and cheese and green beans, two other of the guests also got the fried chicken wirh sides, shrimp and grits were ordered, and fried catfish. The food took forever to come out. The fried catfish side choices were potato salad and Mac and cheese, the plate came without potato salad. The 2 piece fried chicken also came wirh just 2 wings... once we were checked on again we asked about the potato salad to be told they had ran out and asked what we would like instead, fries were chosen and didn't arrived until  after we had finished our meals! About half way through our meals we were also brought chicken breasts to accompany our wings, so that was nice. 

All the food we had was good! The portions are large and I think match the price tag. However, there are no small plates and I think that would be an option to look into. 

Once we got our meal we again waited forever to pay, we waited a lot. The service was nice, but slow and inattentive.

If I could give this place six damn starts I would !!!! 
And I honestly don't say that about most place's I very very picky as a customer and I do a lot of food reviews throughout the nation and so far for my local Sacramento area this place is what's happening frfr 
So far I have had the smothered chicken and fried chicken and sides I have tired greens , Mac and cheese red beans and rice and potatoes salad...I kid you not is the only place so damn far that makes Mac and cheese like I make Mac and cheese which is fire and that tells alot about a place especially a soul food place noodles were cooked just right and the cheese and flavor was showcased amazing...hats off to the chef frfr everything is always hot and fresh to order never waited over 15 mins for my food and don't get me started on that peach cobbler I man when I tell you I wanted to go slap somebody after eating that .. that's how damn good it was I mean not even my peach cobbler is that damn good who the hell is cooking in that kitchen is the real reason damn!!! Prices are super reasonable especially for some food this place is on the up and up and I am here for it ...side note : The potatoe salad was too sweet for my liking however I do know a lot of folks who like it that way me personally I tend to like very little relish but they had all the other ingredients on point tho

Wow. Food, service, atmosphere were perfect. Could not be happier with this place and how much we enjoyed it. It blows Lucille's and the Porch out of the water. Even the Ice Tea, the honey biscuits, Mac and Cheese, hush puppies, all of it was fresh, showed that care was taken in everything we had, and on a Monday night, when a lot of restaurants were closed, I got the best of the best. Great Job!!!
Both times I've eaten here it has been delicious. I love the sides as much as the mains. Great cocktails and friendly service

I absolutely loved the catfish platter. Super fresh with amazing crispy cornmeal crust and a couple of fantastic sides. Collard greens were perfect texture and seasoning. Potato salad had a great zesty pickled thing going on that I really liked. 

Other items to note, good selection of beers (including Abita!), nice simple cocktails that aren't a pretentious ripoff, and endless great service. I cannot wait to try them for breakfast.
Amazing customer service & amazing food! My family and I are from Los Angeles and will be visiting the LA location as well. So comforting to eat well while traveling.

I had the 2 piece chicken and waffle when I came here.  It was better than the chicken and waffles I had at another restaurant I had gone to recently, though I still find fast food (and my dad's!) fried chicken to be tastier.  Also, I prefer my waffles to be crispier than theirs were.  I'm giving 4 stars though because they really seem to aim to please with their service, and that Kool-Aid drink, wow!  It comes in a huge glass (you can ask for a to-go cup because it seems like two glasses' worth) and they offer watermelon flavor :)  I love all things pink and watermelon and it tasted just like a watermelon Jolly Rancher.  The restaurant is in what seems to be an up-and-coming neighborhood with lots of fun little businesses to check out and parking usually isn't too bad here.
The hot chicken sandwich I just had was bonkers. Wow, keep up the good work. Will be back again!

My boyfriend and I came in for brunch and it was absolutely amazing, he got the oxtails and I got the chicken and waffles - both were incredibly flavourful. A family member of the owner came by to check on us and was so kind, everything from the service, food, drinks and atmosphere was great. We will definitely come back.
I was disappointed yesterday when I went to Fixins. I really like this place and come here often but yesterday the fried chicken was extremely dry.

The restaurant looks nice, parking is tight and you will need to just find parking a block or two away from the restaurant,  but it's a nice walk. We waited about an hour to be seated. When we got seated we were great probably  another 10minutes by our server and she didn't come back for a while. She took our orders and never came back to really check on us. When our orders came, we were missing one child and one adult entree, but our server never checked in on us so we waited a while until we can let someone know that was missing. When we finally go our server she finally told us that the kids meal took a bit because the burger needed to be cooked well done and she was surprised that the adult meal never came out, so we just  canceled that because we didn't want to wait another 20-30minutes. We were all basically done eating our entrees and she had already brought us Togo boxes, without asking, which we didn't need, but I was bummed the service took too long because she also didn't even ask if any of us wanted dessert, I really wanted to try the banana pudding. The only reason I gave this restaurant 2 stars was because their food was real good. We ordered the catfish nuggets as appetizers and those were fried to perfection. I ordered the 2 piece fried chicken, dark meat with Mac and cheese and collard greens,  and let me tell you, it is the best I've tasted. My husband got the catfish , black eyed peas, and red beans and rice and he had already started eating before I could take a photo. He also got the kool-aid adult beverage which was delicious  as well, I tried it haha. If you don't care about service, then by all means yes, this is great for soul food, but if service is big then you might want to skip out if you don't want to ruin your lunch/dinner. Overall, if you want to be ignored and wait a while for your food, don't come Herr super hungry or you'll get HANGRY.

Stopped in for a quick breakfast due to the reviews...again not steered wrong Yelpers!  Had to try the deep fried deviled eggs just "because"...Bomb!  Way to go chef.

I had the 2 piece chicken and waffle..excellent.  Syrup served warm and the butter was somehow different,  couldn't tell how or what and forgot to ask..but it was delish.

Hip place with bomb ass patios as well as a conference/meeting room in the back.  I cant wait to be back for a happy hour at this joint.

Them deviled eggs were beautifully prepared. The environment is really nice. Talking but not too much chatter to where you can not think. I got this server named ol skool on his name tag. He made the experience one where he showed concern for how my time/money was being spent. Asking about food taste, water, just being real cognizant of my presence. The Mac I will say was tasty but the special request was to add any veggies to it. Ol skool said they may had green beans and I told him ya whatever you guys have if they can "mix it in" id appreciate it. Not only did they not mix it in and throw it on top but the mac was hot like it was supposed to be but the green beans felt like they took out of a can put it in microwave for 30 sec and through it on mac and cheese balancing out the temp to a WARM dish instead of hot mac and cheese once mixing up myself. So I was a little disappointed on the way they executed that. The peach cobbler was real tasty. Just could use a little more crust and texture on it to make it feel like a cobbler and not a pudding lol. Anyways I did enjoy myself for most part loathing with a full course.  Appreciate the service :)

Lunch here was amazing.. Saturday afternoons could get a little busy however it's worth the wait. Excellent customer service and honest answers when asked about the dishes that's being served and recommendations. Not shown in pictures however I also got to try the ox tails over rice, yams,  red beans and rice, fried cat fish, and asparagus all delicious choices.

The review is based on the Take Out food ordered - arriving at Fixins we weren't greeted friendly at all, but heard a "you have to talk to him" without us even asking a question yet. Now to the food - I wish I could tell you about the cobbler, but it was missed altogether. The smothered chicken had good taste but looked really bland. The chicken burger looked like half a chicken was breaded and slapped on two buns. Again, it's not the taste but overall experience.
The is place is so good, I don't think I'll ever forget it, and I'll be back over and over again! THIS THAT SORT OF SO-GOOD-IT'S-EMOTIONAL kind of experience.

The host situation was a bit off; we waited a cool while to put our name on the list and get a table. Looked understaffed. 

That said, 1000000% worth it. Food was absolutely amazing. The different colored butters were bomb! Isselle, the bartender / server / superhero, hustled and made an incredible lavender lemon drop. The woman was honestly doing 10 jobs at once and doing them so so well. Nicki G. was super sweet and attentive. Totally worth the wait.
Food was on point I tried a few dishes everything great give the cook a raise!  Waiter too

Nice atmosphere and friendly service. Had the smothered chicken with black eye peas and greens. A bit of nostalgia  in their coolaid drinks!! Enjoyed the trip back down memory Lane! Did not enjoy the food. I was visiting From Atlanta Georgia and I just haven't found any one that can come close to the cooks in Georgia. Please revisit the soul food cook books. With special attention to the seasonings. The gravy on the smothered chicken is not good. Incorrect for smothered chicken. Again please check the cook books or give that southern cousin a call. Peas and greens seasoning just didn't do it for me either. If you get this right the lines will wrap around the building. And I will stop by next time I am in town.
Great food cooked to  Perfection  I don't recommend getting the shrimp and grits it wasn't all that good and it does not taste good

Way overrated soul restaurant. KJ needs to try the restaurant called "South" in midtown Sac. 
The fried deviled eggs were meh. The Artichoke jalapeño dip was meh. The hot chicken sandwich was dipped in a wing sauce and just a hot mess. Way to much sauce used because it took over the flavor. The Gumbo is just ok and the biscuit that came with it was meh. Now, the Mac n cheese was decent and so was the smothered chicken. Other than that we won't go back.

The chicken tenders and ranch are . The best I've ever had and delicious every time I go there. Tip: Mix the red and blue koolaid. Our family has our own favorite dishes and we mix it up and share each time. Such a chill vibe inside. Highly recommend!
Fantastic grilled okra! Saucy gumbo, flaky catfish, scrumptious fried green tomatoes and Bourbon Sweet Tea! Great passionate owners Kevin and Michelle Johnson! A great addition to Oak Park!

I've been to Fixins soul kitchen a few times. I think the food is pretty good. I'm pretty picky about my soul food and I will say it's not the best I have ever had, but still very good. I love their grits! I also love that their waffles have a cinnamon flavor. The chicken is delicious. I will say their service is not great. Each time Ive gone it's been the same thing. We got our seat right away but waited 15-20 minutes for a server to ask what we wanted to drink/order. Even receiving the check and waiting for them to make the transaction took a very long time. I saw a long wait outside and I think it would help if they get their customers who are ready to pay out of there much faster.

Came for dinner during a soccer tournament with our teenage son. Wonderful mix of people--some very casual, some dressed to kill--but everyone enjoying the atmosphere. GREAT fried chicken! I had some with the chicken and waffles and my son had the chicken sandwich. My wife had the shrimp and grits, which she proclaimed some of the best she's had. The specialty drinks are very sweet. However, I tried the Uncle Nearest Bourbon neat and have since bought a bottle. Staff were friendly and the outdoor seating was comfortable.
Awesome chicken and waffles with a great drink menu and excellent service. Come just to try the kool aid pickles. Good outdoor seating as well.
the food was outstanding the chicken was cooked perfectly the service was fast and everyone was really nice

Overall the food was good, It didn't take long at all for our meals to come, However my sisters and I had a table about 8-9 of us and when it came time for the bill, We were under the impression that we can split the bills into 3 (it's clearly stated on the bottom of their menus) but our waitress said that they don't do that and they can only take 3 different cards for payments. Someone please make it make sense. If something is printed clearly on your stuff, When a customer brings it to you, Than you should honor it, Correct?. Idk if any of us will be back. The food was good but wasn't stop the car good.

Fixins soul kitchen is probably one of the best soul food restaurant I've been to! The food is as soulful as it gets! The chicken and waffles is delicious and a must here. The chicken is flavorful, cooked to perfection with the crispy flakes on the outside and the juiciness on the inside. The okra is also delicious and different as it is cooked long enough for it to have the perfect texture with a lemony zest to it. I also tried the catfish, mac and cheese, corn bread, and fried deviled eggs. Everything was very tasty except for the deviled eggs that were okay to me. I thought it would be different because it was fried, but it was okay. Thirsty? Fixins also has really great cocktails here. I treied the bourbon sweet tea and it was delicious as it paired very well with each other. Overall, the food was delicious, they had great and friendly customer service, clean place, and great atmosphere. I truly got my soul food fix at Fixins and will definitely be back!

Service was great and everything was delicious!  We had the 2 piece chicken and waffle (dark meat), fried green tomatoes and hot chicken sandwich with Mac n' cheese.  We really enjoyed everything but the Mac n cheese and waffle with cinnamon butter were highlights!

I have now gone back 5 more times and the Mac and cheese is cheesier and crispier then before. The latest time I wanted to try a dish outside of my waffle and chicken and got the smothered pork chop. The gravy is so flavorful and left my mouth watering. Service is always great and helpful and there is honestly not one item that I have tried that I have to like soo far. Fixins is an establishment that I want to dine at on a weekly basis

In the food industry training staff is so important 
Seating was difficult for hostess to many tables available but I couldn't sit there.Soon as you walk in the door there is one those garbage cans you find in your bathroom at home full..really Hostess greeting was dry like she didn't wanna be there I ordered oxtails salty green beans overcooked  food luke warm halfway into my meal they bring my beverage kool-aid..was good server was pleasant but in food industry your hair should not be all over the place like Diana Ross singing in central park.

Awesome food, drinks and service! I would give them an extra star if I could. Love their beverage menu selection & decided on the bourbon sweet tea, Bloody Mary & the adult kool aid. (Don't worry, I didn't drink them all at once even though I could've!) The fried chicken is so crunchy on the outside yet juicy and tender on the inside. All your traditional southern side dishes like collard greens, Mac n cheese & corn bread! Save the candied yam for dessert, it's really really sweet and I have a sweet tooth. Get it smothered in gravy, you won't regret it. The fried catfish was a great starter to share. Service was out of this world friendly, she was funny to talk to and even got us a refill on my daughter's kool aid to go. (I forgot her name but know she loves America Horror Story like me!) Outdoor patio seating is dog friendly but super hot in the sacramento heat. Full bar service inside. Street parking so get there early as they got really packed after we left.
The best oxtails outside your mama's kitchen. I love the bar but the outside space is lovely.
So good I love their food. This is my second time eating their food. The first time I got their hot chicken sandwich and fries . Their ranch is so good.
Very good friendly customer service. Family atmosphere with great southern vibe and food

South is head and shoulders better for food, drinks and ambiance. If I could give 0 stars I would! The food was average at best but no one in our party of 13 was impressed by their order. The manager was extremely rude and unprofessional as well. Sad to think this place had so much potential and is just a complete fail.

Wowzers.... food was on point. For the price the portion was solid. Mac and cheese had good flavor (wish it was a little cheesier though), catfish was fried perfectly, corn fritters were delicious. Candied yams were a little too much for me but maybe that's just my personal preference. Really enjoyed the coleslaw and cornbread, too. We sat on the patio and it was so nice! Our server was super friendly too. One of the managers came out and asked how everything was and I super appreciated that little gesture. I'll be back. 

Food 4.5/5 
Service 5/5

I had never been to Fixins before yesterday, and up to now, I am not a dine-in customer. But I needed something special. A dear friend passed away and her family needed main dishes for the small reception they were planning. Fixins is in the neighborhood of the reception. I called to ask whether they could prepare a few trays of fried chicken that I could take to the reception. "We don't cater,' I was told "but let me se what we can do."
The next morning, I picked up three hot trays of some of the tastiest, most succulent fried chicken I've ever eaten. The staff were polite and caring. More that simply making sure I was accommodated; they wanted to be sure that the food would get there hot and delicious and that the family would be pleased.
Today, I looked at their menu. There's gumbo. I was going back with my family for chicken dinners, but now . . .

Was really hoping to leave a positive review. Door dashed a family meal 8 piece chicken. Must of got a bad batch, batter was not flavorful and you could taste the old oil that was used for this fry. 2 star for decent red beans and rice.

The food is awesome !!!! I can't express this enough. The wait time is usually an hour or more but it's totally worth the wait. The owner's aunt was sweet enough to come around and chat us up while we waited for our food. She gave us a little history on the area and encouraged us to visit other businesses close by. I would definitely recommend the chicken and waffles, the catfish and the beans and rice. They have a real nice twist on Kool-Aid and liquor. So come with a designated driver. I live in San Francisco but I will definitely make the drive back to Sacramento just for the food.

EXCELLENT!!! This is hands down the best soul food hands down in the bay besides my moms house!! Lol we drove 40 miles just to try it and let me tell you... from the service to the food to the mimosas... WONDERFUl! Do yourself a favor and make this place a destination! Can not wait to come back!

Great staff. Not a long wait very nice and clean. Indoor and outdoor dining available. Great food great waitress I enjoyed everything about my visit and it was my 1st visit. I heard about this place from one of my friends who visits here at least twice a week drives all the way from Stockton as I did and it was worth it!! My husband and I enjoyed our meals and the atmosphere we will definitely be returning!! Thank you for the great experience and service!!
Great dinner! Collard Greens over the top! Okra is a great side.breading on pork chops a bit heavy.

Love the Blackeye Peas, Collard Greens, Catfish, and cornbread muffins...DELICIOUS! The Fried Green Tomatoes are to live for and my daughter's fav is the Asparagus Dip N chips. Kevin Johnson has been there 2 out 3 times I was there...Very tentative and pays attention to his staff and his delicious products. It would be nice if he opened a store in Stockton:)

I'm giving them a 1 star and that's only because of the grape koolaid if it wasn't for that I'd definitely give them a 0 star. Today 9/18/2022 me and my wife decided to sit and eat and enjoy our Sunday instead it turned into a decision made to never go back. The customer service was so terrible, the people checking you in had complete attitudes and wasn't really communicative secondly three people who came after us were seated before us also our server "Cat R" is what the receipt said her name was. She was awful and very monotone as if she really didn't want to be there. She came and checked on us once and we were there for a good 30mins. Our experience today made us decide to never come here again my bill was $48 and I hate I had to pay that because the service was that bad. Save your money if your reading this and go elsewhere

Always a good time with good food when you come. This time I ordered the jambalaya and it didn't disappoint. I love rice so I ordered an extra side. Ordered the fried deviled eggs and they were just as good as the last time I was there. 

All of the food is fresh, has good flavoring and perfect comfort food for this cold weather. The service is always good and that makes me always sure I want to come back.
New to the area...love the food/drinks and ambiance everything I've had has been really good, but the catfish nuggets and greens are my favorite
The staff is always nice and the food is  great. I like the ambiance. The prices are fair.

This was our second time at Fixin's.
Great food and great drinks.
The service is great and the people are super friendly. Best fried chicken in town and the shrimp and grits are the bomb. Never go away hungry.

The food never disappoints at this Fixins location.  We come from the Bay Area to partake.  We came to Sac for my birthday yesterday.  Although there are many spots in Sac that we enjoy, I chose Fixins to have my birthday celebration meal.  What a disappointment and a sad way to end an otherwise lovely birthday celebration.  From the moment we walked in service was lacking.  The host forgot to tell the server we were waiting on the patio for 10 minutes before being greeted.  The server took our order, brought us water and never revisited our table again.  We sat and watched one of the other 2 occupied tables get 5 visits from their server. "How was your food?"  "Can I get you anything else?"  "Let me get this out of your way?"  "How did you like that? I'm glad you enjoyed it."  "Would you like a carry out box?"  Meanwhile, our plates stacked up, flies converged, leaving us no option but to leave our leftover food.  I finally called over the other server and told them we need our check, a carry out box and where in the hell is our server?  They go inside and within a minute our server emerges with our check and a box.  I don't believe in not giving tips even when the service isn't the best BUT we LITERALLY got no service and her response to our complaints where nothing but excuses.  It's so true that poor service can make you forget how good the food was entirely.
First time here. What have I been missing? Food is great, service is great! Portions are large. You're full by the time you finish dinner. So happy to come to Fixins!
Tried this place a year ago. Fried catfish is great here. I cant wait to try more of their menu. Cornflake crust!

Great Southern food in a nice location. Was able to snag a patio seat and order. Got the chicken sandwich which was sooo good- Chik-Fil-A can keep their nasty homophobia wiches. The chicken was flavorful and juicy. It's designed so that you're likely to wearing at least some of it before you're done. The black eyed peas were a little forgettable but the collard greens with turkey neck were packed with flavorful and vitamins. Due to Corona, they weren't serving okra or the fried deviled eggs. It was nice to wash it down with grape Kool-Aid. Service was friendly and price was a little up there but worth it.

Okay, there are not many places around Sacramento where you can get good ass soul food, that has now ended with Fixins. If you want to go back in time to the smells, flavor and love of those southern grandmama, aunties and uncles whose cooking came from the soul, this will take you there. I went with family and we decided ahead of time we'd share whatever we ordered and it was the best idea! We had a blast trying each others food. When we asked for a table it was a busy Sunday and were told it would be a 30 to 40 minute wait. We sat in the patio area to wait which is not open yet. Once called we each swore it was not even 30 minutes and we were already seated. 
We started our adventure with the fried deviled eggs. I've never had fried boiled eggs before and these were so yummy! The bacon and mustard along with a small bed of greens was very good. Next, I ordered the oxtails n gravy with a side of black eyed peas (I knew what I wanted before getting there)I have not had oxtails since my dad was alive and these did not disappoint. They were delicious, The meat on the tail fell off the bone and was drenched in gravy. The black eyes were cooked to perfection. The other two in my party ordered catfish and smothered chicken. The catfish was ordered with yams and green beans. The smothered chicken was ordered with bake mac n cheese and collard greens. 
We ate off each others plates! Everything tasted divine. The catfish was fried with cornmeal and I put the in house hot sauce on it. Wow! Green beans were fully cooked too.If we had to complain about anything it would be the collard greens had a bit too much vinegar but it wasn't horrible. The bake mac n cheese had plenty of cheese! We got to meet and speak to chef, Melvin "Boots" Johnson who informed us that he made the gravy for the smothered chicken with a darker rue to bring out a bit of bitterness which it did and was delicious!! 
We topped off our meal with the yummy banana pudding! The atmosphere of everyone just out of church, talking and laughing just made you recall your childhood or living in the South! 
The food is so good and cooked just as you'll remember that you feel like grandmama, auntie or  grandaddy is going to walk out that kitchen and give you a hug! Service was good, everyone nice but not sure if that was because it has not opened yet or that the former mayor and owner was walking around but we had a great time. We will definitely be back!!! Loved it!!
Great service, food was amazing, and the place looked beautiful! Would definitely come back!

Wow! We are not coming back here unless Cat is working specifically! She was such an amazing server and she really took care of us and made sure we enjoyed our time here at Fixins. She was very sincere and prompt, we never had to wait for anything she was on it! We love Cat! + the food was amazing! She offered great recommendations and knew the menu very well!
Went on a date here, good food and good service. Really enjoyed myself. Great decor with a comfortable vibe. A great place to to eat with friends, family or on a relaxed date.

The chicken wings and waffles were delicious; however, the waitress (B/F with a Heavy-set frame) had a terrible attitude...and it was SUNDAY! Gospel Sunday had an awesome spiritual vibe the gospel music playing.

I am stoked to have a good soul food joint so close to home. Fixins really knows what they're doing and they have a great selection to choose from. The reset of Sacramento also clearly recognizes this because this place gets packed during peak (and non peak!) hours. 

The first time I came I had the shrimp and grits with a side of greens and okra. I was diggin both sides, perfectly cooked and seasoned. The shrimp and grits was exactly how I prefer to prepare it with a rich cajun sauce and it was fantastic. 

The second time was somehow better than the first! My husband had chicken and waffles and their chicken was mmm *chefs kiss*. I had battered fish and devoured it. We also tried the fried deviled eggs which are weird but really good lol. I had the same server both times, I can't remember his name but he did a great job of taking care of us. Price-wise you really won't find better. Urban Roots has comparable food but way more expensive. 

They've expanded out to their patio which is awesome because it adds more seating and it's really tastefully taken care of. I can't wait to get my fix of fixins again!!

I came on a busy Friday night with a party of 8. It was packed. We had a wait time of about 1 hour but we had good company so it wasn't too bad. Once we were seated, service was great from start to finish. Food didn't take too long to come out and we didn't have to ask for more water or anything. They were on it. Our waitress Chloe was the sweetest thing and she had great energy. She really made our first experience there a great one. All 8 of us were more than satisfied with our meals and we all are already talking about coming back to try more items!

So I went there to pick up my food waiting in line my wife got the text that the food was ready then they took forever to give me my food. They were to busy talking to co-workers taking there orders. Then after there we're done they still was talking to each other like they haven't seen them in months. Customer service was horrible and I didn't her one full order we spent over 100$ for food and didn't get all of it.
First time eating here today and I have to say the food was incredible! I got the Gumbo with shrimp, my family got chicken and waffles, and everything was *chefs kiss* will definitely be back.

The food was SO GOOD and exceeded expectations - Chef Boots and his crew won't disappoint! Some of my favorites among the things I tried, and I'd probably recommend sharing everything so you can try as many things as possible: 
+ Oxtail
+ Fried chicken & waffles 
+ Mac n cheese (with the hot sauce, it's FIRE)
+ Collard greens
+ Fried green tomatoes
+ Biscuits
+ Lemon bars

Not only does it have good food, but the staff is also great and the restaurant looks amazing. Will definitely be making the drive from the bay area to go here as often as I can!
Great food remind me of down home southern goodness. Service was great. The sweet ice tea was truly sweet. My family was satisfied.

Great food. Placed a big To Go order for extended fam. Entire order was received as ordered. No mistakes. Even a warning was provided letting people know that certain items do not travel well. Food was tasty despite the warning. Chicken and fish not over salted. Waffles and all other items great and very much enjoyed by all. Thanks.

My friend and I planned to go to South but it was their last day and we weren't able to get in. So we ended up at Fixins. The hostess wasn't very friendly but our waiter was attentive and friendly. The food was okay - we order fried chicken dinner and fried pork chops. We got four sides - greens, grits, mac and cheese and sweet potatoes. The sides were tasty. Loved the grits and sweet potatoes. The fried chicken was good but didn't come close to
South. Fried pork chops were okay.

Loved everything about this place! The vibe, the decorations, the music, & the food! I have  absolutely no complaints. We ordered the fried chicken with a side of collared greens and okra, shrimp & grits, and the oxtail with a side of candied yams. The chicken was perfectly fried with not too much batter and was juicy. The okra was bombbbbbb & tender, the greens were flavorful with a little  kick to it. We devoured the shrimp & grits! It had good flavor!! My favorite had to be the oxtail! The meat was so tender and juicy! All portions were good! I also ordered the lavender lemon drop. First sip reminded me of drinking a massage lol but that's just cuz of the lavender smell but I quickly got over it and loved it cuz I ordered two! Oh! And we also ordered banana pudding and it was the perfect treat to finish off the meal! It wasn't too sweet!  The server was nice too and the music topped of the whole experience! Can't wait to come back!

I have been hearing about this place since it opened and I've been excited to have soul food in Sacramento. Really wanted to like this place, however my dining experience was interesting...

The wait: 1hr 10mins 
That's to be expected as we went on a Sunday after church so it was pretty busy.
