The food: This is where the 3 stars comes in. The food was decent soul food. It was hot and flavorful. I ordered an apple juice which was a little different, it was carbonated. Never had carbonated apple juice and I sent it back for water instead. Water didn't come while I had my food. My nana had to ask two more times for the water. 

The service: The server wasn't very attentive. I hear orders being wrong is not a rare occurrence. The silverware where we were seated was dirty and had to be replaced. 

The seating: We sat outside and the seating area was nice. There happened to be a mini staff meeting behind us which I thought was a little odd. They were talking loudly with team members across the room about clocking in, the hours they could work and other business related issues that I shouldn't be privy to as a customer and it kind of interrupted our conversation/meal. I've never seen anything like that before. 

All in all if you're looking for decent soul food in Sacramento and don't mind the wait you could get it here. Would I return? Probably not.

Just ok.  The sauce on the pork chops was nasty (sweet and very dark). Once I removed the sauce off the pork chops, they were pretty good.  The greens were overcooked and made with vinegar (yuck). The mac & cheese was ok.  Don't think I'll return.  I'll stick to making my own.
Good ambience and food.  Happy they installed a reservation system and now can avoid long wait times.

Here's one of the newest additions to offer out soul, comfort food. You can find fried chicken, catfish, Mac and cheese, gumbo, oxtail, and many other southern, style dishes. I do like the atmosphere, ambiance, being surrounded by televisions to watch sports, and settle down with a refreshing beer or other alcoholic beverage. Former Sacramento mayor, Kevin Johnson opened up this joint in the heart of oak park. Anyways onto the food. Oxtail and deep fried deviled eggs were on point. I really like the idea of having kool-aid on the menu making me wonder why other soul food restaurants don't have that. Catfish nuggets were seasoned well but didn't have that crisp to it. I'll have to let the cooks know to fry it even longer. The fried chicken was expected to be juicy and tender but really flavorless and dry. Tried a little bit of all the fixins from collard greens, Mac and cheese, candied yams, coleslaw and creamed corn (stood out the most). It kind of reminded me of el pollo locos. Oh and the butter served with cornbread and the biscuit was quite tasty! I'd love to give this restaurant out another time for different menu options! Give them a visit if you're in the oak park area!

Best fried chicken joint in Sac (alongside South)! I always get the family deal- 8 pieces of chicken, 4 biscuits, and 2 sides for $55. It's a pretty decent deal and always leaves us with some leftovers.
ABSOLUTELY DELICIOUS! Service was great as well! So worth the 2 hour wait on mothers day!

Fixins Soul Kitchen is your southern comfort food with a modern vibe.

The food quality is rich in flavor and is more of a fusion from old and new food techniques.  They still stick with staple southern food items but apply their twist to it.  My wife and i loved the atmosphere and whole energy of this place.  It was cool to chat with Kevin Johnson himself as well :).

The food is 'heavy' but not.  Not sure if that makes sense but for example their Chicken & Waffles entree is perfect yet not too dense.  The chicken is thoroughly fried yet we noticed the meat was still juicy.  So there was a balance of crunch everywhere.  The seasoning was just the right amount to match the sweetness of the waffle/syrup.  The waffle wasn't thick or dense.  It was light and sponge-y enough to soak up the syrup.  That soft waffle soaked with some syrup contrasted the crunchy chicken so well and they got this dish right.

Note they are VERY new and expect some delay or minor hiccups but overall they are hitting the ground running.  We had no issues with the food or service and can already see the potential of Fixins.  This place will DEFINITELY become a hit. Enjoy!
The oxtails, the burger, the chicken strips are all recommended the sides that we had were bomb as well! Wish I would've tried the deviled eggs but that's just another reason for me to come back!

OMGGG BEST DAMN SOUL FOOD IVE EVER HAD. @fixinssoulkitchen Is A Black Family Owned Biz, Check Them Out in #Sacramento & #LA  They Opened The Sac Location 2 Yrs Ago, Thank God They Survived The Pandemic If Not I Wouldn't Have Gotten To Experience It! (IMO They're Better Than What I Had In NY, Which Was MAD GOOD! Mannn, I Demolished Those Chicken n Waffles   , I Wasn't "Full Full" Afterwards Lol So I Also Got #PeachCobbler  That  Cobbler Was Waaaaay Better Than The Ones I Had In ATL (Which I Thought Were Heaven In A Cup Hahaha) & That #SweetTea Was Ginormous Jug! My Soul Feels Happy I So Needed Some #SoulFood On My Last Day Of #TreatYoSelf Bday Month. Time To Get Back On Track W/ My Nutrition, Gonna Try To Make It Thru Nov W/O Any Sugar #WishMeLuck  Now I Should Prolly Get Another Workout In  Or #NapTime 1st Lmao  #HappyHalloween2021 #FoodComa #FixinsSoulKitchen #SacramentoEats #HappyFood #HappyLife #HappySteph #SoulFoodSunday #SupportBlackOwnedBusinesses #SupportLocalBiz

lets just say fire! ox tails. fire! fried chicken. fire!. sides. fire!

vibes are dope too. must try oh yea. corn bread. fire!

The best Mac & cheese I've ever had!!! Delicious! Great food! Expect a long wait for a weekend but honestly it is well worth the wait! The food is just amazing! The service isn't bad either. This spot also has a full bar & serves mimosas and bloody Mary's for brunches. A must try!
The lax tails , chicken, and Mac were on point. I ordered out & the food was still great. I'll definitely be stopping in to dine in in the future. The service was great & professional.

Amazing!!!! A few of my bfs friends came from out of town and magically found this place online. I'm soooo glad they did! Definitely will be coming back amazing food and service! And our amazing server had the same name as me! Aimee! I don't see that many other Aimee's so that was awesome! Amazing food highly recommended.
Great  customer from hostess/take out person Ebony she suggested the oxtails and it was great.

I am a very particular person about meat and how it is prepared, I do not like fat or tendons etc, I run into this at most places, it's rare I do not end up with a napkin on the table with gristle, tendon, cartilage and fat.
I am very picky about meats texture, I hesitate to order anything with chicken that is not whole chicken lately I do not like, it seems like factory parts assembled ?
At Fixin's we had a very friendly engaging & efficient waitress who was very knowledgeable of the menu and described the items that sounded delicious, her exuberance gave me confidence,, I felt like the Gumbo on rice was a safe choice and hoped it was not  less than expected

To my pleasant surprise my food was beyond my expectation, every bite was perfection as was my girlfriend's.
Fixing's is my #1 favorite,Nicky our waitress is a fantastic person and someone we both look forward to seeing soon!

Fixins did not disappointed. I ordered for take out and it was a longer wait but expected for a Friday night. The food was made on time and easy pick up. We got the smothered chicken and the gumbo. Both were amazing. The gumbo doesn't look too appealing but the taste is all there!!! The chicken was amazing and that smothered sauce was everything! Savory with a kick but not too rich, and the chicken alone was seasoned very well. The chicken skin was a little soggy but it was take out so again expected. I'm sure it would have been even better if I had eaten there and fresh with a crunch. We will definitely be back.

I've been her twice and goin back with to of my friends food is very delicious and I'll Recommend this restaurant to other friends the food is awesome I found me a new spot it's very neat and clean and the waiters are very nice so yeah I will be seeing them soon

Chicken is ridiculously good here.
Not 5 stars because the wait was so long on a Sunday afternoon. Otherwise, great place!
Delicious! Best soul food in Sacramento. We tried the catfish bites, Oxtail, chicken and waffles... all delicious. We're excited to go back soon.

Exactly what oak park needs to boost the rejuvenation.  This place is really good, the food is delicious I had the chicken and waffles and the candied yams.  The only knock is the wait time and sometimes the service can be a bit slow. My advice is to sit at the bar !! You'll get your food fast.
Best soul food in Sacromento great service. I'll definitely come back for there drinks as well !

Shall we begin?  Being a Vegan in 2023 isn't that special so I never expect any special treatment beyond keeping it as adverstised, truly vegan.  But a line must be drawn and/or added depending on your public school geometry recall skillages, as after the first criteria is met, flavor is the next culprit on my "not special' vegan check list for return dining status.

Being I've tried the "Blackened Tofu w/corn succotash" before and was not floored by the flavor the first time.  I figured I'd give it a 2nd chance to either reel me in or throw me back.  Well they definitely didn't hook this Big Mouth Vegan Bass, so I'll be wading in the water albeit, I'm in no hurry to get hooked unless its a mircale menu recovery which I'll be waiting with "baited" breathe to catch that line. 

I say all that to kindly address the "let's make it spicy and they'll have to love it" approach to the Blackened Tofu sidekick, the corn succotash. When I say all I tasted was hot ass peppery spices sans any flavor, I wouldn't be lying.  Next up the tofu, which any vegan knows can be a hit or miss depending on how it's prepped and cooked.  (Side Bar with counsel says) Prep includes pressing as much water out of the tofu as feasible within the kitchen's time constraints to get the meal out.  Being it didn't fly out to my table, the soft--also they shoud be using an ORGANIC firm or super firm--tofu had the typical moist not pressed feel and taste which meant any spice except the peppery heavy handed tear inducing flavor transfer from the aforementioned corn succotash wouldn't stand a chance infusing the tofu with any type of tasty flavor.  End results was the consuming party was hit with the flashback from the first time they tried badly cooked tofu.  And that ain't the magic any  kitchen should be whipping up amongst native vegans.  Especially when I heard from non-vegans their other items are on point, hence the long line to dine!!  See most vegans who consume tofu have either found their spot that prepares it to their liking or many like me own a few tofu presses and won't hesitate to Glad Bag that block in Coconut Aminos and/or any other non-soy marinate out there to get it where it needs to be flavor wise to ensure we never re-visit "our first bad tofu moment" again!!   And 10mins minimum of marination ain't asking to much if the meal recipient is gonna be blown away by the infused flavors and not the peppery heat!!  And since I'm already 10 toes in on the tofu lemme address the (Almost) meal savers, the un-dynamic duo.  Yes those vegan black eyed peas and collard greens.  Both are prime canditates for a lil liquid smoke to bring 'em up to above par as current status was somewhere between, "My moma ain't gotta worried about being slapped" to "who raised (the chef) you?"  See I've tasted and whipped up some vegan soul food and know what can and can't be in regards to flavor boosting the un-dynamic duo!!

I'll end with what has to be FSK's secret go to vegan "Crack On The Menu" item, that goddamn "What you talking about Willis" Arnold slash "Keke" Palmer drank that needs a pre-diabetic warning label on the jar cuz I may have a pending charge for slapping somebody mama sitting across from us in the booth adjacent booth cuz it beez like that sometimes when you get it right.  And who eva mixed that sweet @$$ tea and lemonade needs to be the one who oversees revamping your vegan offerings!!  Cuz they can't do it no harm.

I digress.

Food was on point portions a little on the small side but for the most we where pleased. Catfish and chicken with fried deviled eggs as an app is what we had . The lack of app options , cost  and the suspect service issues  at times held this from five star . They where busy as all get out so that may have played a part as well as fact that they kinda of cover for all it appears well there was point we had to hunt down some one to get a late drink and the check and boxes .

All in all positive experience we shall be back probably at a less busy time of the day and week to explore other things on the menu .

Amazing southern food and a full bar. I really enjoyed the oxtails with a side of grits and cheese. My boyfriend ordered the fried chicken (dark meat) with collard greens and creamed corn. It was also delicious. Drinks were sweet tea and lavender lemon drop. We sat at the bar and had really nice service.
Excellent food! Good people! Good atmosphere! Soul food at its BEST!!! So much variety, I'll be returning for sure!

Anytime I'm in sac I wanna stop by this place. Deviled eggs, fried green tomatoes and grown up Kool aid is all I have to say. A place I can eat fried chicken cold and still makes me feel some type of way is well worth it!.

Staff was very friendly. Wait time wasn't bad.  Drinks were expensive but good. Now for the food.   We were a party of 6.  Wait staff was great.  Didn't have the pork chops due to meat supply.  The fried chicken was good. Crunchy skin but not over done. Smothered chicken was good. Gravy was good. Collard greens I have had better. Red beans and rice Popeyes are better. Mac and cheese I have had better. Cornbread was good. Biscuits are better at Kentucky Fried Chicken.  Time with friends priceless.

What happens when quality "black entrepreneurship and quality food meet?" You get "Fixins Soul Kitchen!" My party of six (6)  from Boise in town visiting friends and to see a concert at Thunder Mountain.
Yes unfortunately it was Bobby "garbage" Brown, attempted to go on Saturday night (that was a mistake) everybody in town had the same idea (hour and a half wait times).

We were greeted by former NBA great Kevin Johnson (The Owner) who greeted us and sheepishly apologized for the long wait. He invited us back the following day, and recommended what was the best time.  
The atmosphere is a potpourri of multicultural, multi age, multi fashioned, birthday celebrations, churchgoers and hungry folks, smiling and "getting their grub on."

The menu of "oxtails, collard greens, mac & cheese, fried chicken, smothered chicken, chicken and waffles, cheesy grits, red beans and rice, kool-aide,  BBQ wings, sweet tea, and fried deviled eggs" did not disappoint. 

I could describe each item separately, but as one of my party members said, "this food is so good it reminds me of church, thanksgiving, butt whippings, family, afternoon naps, church music on the radio, the front porch and LOVE" all in one.
 
Friendly staff, awesome décor and reasonably priced menu .

To show this review is not just hype and I'm not related to anyone at Fixins nor have I been paid, here is my complaints, (1) No Order out service (yet), (2) No reservations and (3) Yall are sooooo wrong after eating all of the above to bring out the "Chess Pie??"   STOP IT!!!

I overheard someone say Fixins replaced "Sandra Dee's?" Uhhhh no, Fixins put Sandra Dee's out of its misery!!

Thanks again to KJ and his staff, for launching this eatery along with the revitalizing of a great old neighborhood. This is just what Sacramento needed. Put this place on your "must visit" list.

BLee

This place had good reviews, so I decided to check it out. It was alright maybe because u was hungry. I got a mixed drink..way too strong. I got fried chicken with side of red beans & rice & candied yams. In my opinion Popeyes red beans and rice taste way better. The candied yams was alright. The fried chicken was ok...I've had better! The best thing on my plate was the biscuit!

I went to Fixins about two weeks ago,  after waiting 45 min I was happy to try the food. I had chicken and waffles it was ok and blackeyed peas(needed seasoning). My bestfriend had shrimp and grits, which again was ok at best. 
The artichoke dip was DISGUSTING.  Now I could not believe it was served with... Ritz crackers, like where they do that at. I just couldn't believe "toasted bread " was Ritz crackers like its still unbelievable. 
The only thing I enjoy was the Red Kool-Aid. It took me back to my childhood.  I myself wont be back, its truly not worth the wait.

After hearing so much about Fixins thought I would try it out. The wait was about 20 mins which isn't bad, only thing I didn't like was the fact you couldn't go to bar and sit and have a drink without losing your spot on the list. I must say for starters the deep fried deviled egg were delicious!!!! I had smothered chicken and my bf had fried chicken everything was delicious, only thing my bf didn't like was the okra it was just bland. But overall I liked it a lot and will be back!

This review is based purely on takeout. I've heard absolutely amazing things from here so I ordered it twice this week for my office. Service and speed of overall service amazing. Food was very ok. I personally ordered corn bread, Mac and cheese and gumbo. The Mac has a thick layer of oil it was sitting in, the corn bread had zero butter or honey butter and the gumbo was just good. Maybe I'm just ordering the wrong things...

I haven't eaten but the fact that I can't take my food with me is enough to dislike this place.
Because I'm being paid to sit here...I have to stay but...I'm already not impressed.
Will update

The food okay. Kevin was there walking around. Honestly that probably ruined it for me.
After complaining I was able to get a To go box.
I spoke with several others that were also disappointed

From the music feeding the soul, the speciality drinks, kind southern hospitality, to the homemade style fried chicken, you will be transported to true southern cooking. I went there in a recommendation while in a short visit to Sacramento. I will be back!  The entire staff was so accommodating and friendly. Their unique Mint Julep, amazingly depth in flavor collard greens, and truly the best ever fried chicken. Back home now, I am still craving it.  A reason to return to Sacramento.
Loved it all....service...food...okra yum....fried chicken yum....In Arnolds famous words "I'll be back!"

So I had to leave a review because I was totally impressed!! Food was amazing. We had a larger party and sometimes things get a little confusing with a bigger party and our server was kinda struggling to keep up with us. But she still did great but my main thing with the owner Kevin Johnson assisted he brought out our corn bread muffins and gave us extra. It means a lot when owners take initiative and make sure their guest are taken care of. My first time and I will be back  oh and the fried green tomatoes are a must try !!
Amazing server, food was good, on a Saturday late afternoon we didn't have a long wait.
When I tell you this restaurant is amazing.... The service was great!! The food was even better, my family and I didn't wait long and the sweet tea had the perfect amount of sweetness.

I love this place the location the dude the drinks the atmosphere is always so upbeat pleasant and fun the location is so family friendly and joyous I would go right now if I had some  money. It's a beautiful day to be there to.
The food was delicious. This was my 3rd  time eating here. So far I loved everything I've had there, even the lemonade is delicious. Staff is super cool.

Delicious food & drinks! Large portions .
Service was also good. Definitely a repeat place!

Decided to check this place out after eyeballing it on Yelp for a few months. As we were waiting for our food, I peeped a dude sporting an ankle monitor--and I thought, damn, this food must be good if people are willing to violate their house arrest. I kid, I kid. But anyways, Y'all... This was the best chicken and waffles I've EVER had. I used to frequent the Home of Chicken and Waffles in Oakland, but Fixins blew them out of the water. The chicken just slid off the bone and the butter had a hint of strawberry. Had to pair it with a Side Car.  And don't let me forget about the bomb AF deviled eggs we had as our appetizer.

Go. Now. You won't be disappointed... But be prepared for a food coma. We both passed out when we got home.

I never take the time to post a review, but this has got to be a top 3 meal of my whole life. I got the smothered chicken with Mac and cheese and grits. It was my first time having grits. I had absolutely nothing I could possibly complain about except not being hungry enough to have another serving. Great taste, good service, nice ambiance.
Amazing food and nice patio. Collard greens are fantastic. This place is a must visit in Sacramento.
Great venue for a night out or business lunch. Service is consistent, food is flavorful and the bar is awesome. May be a bit of a wait, but worth it.

It's about time we got another soul food restaurant in Sacramento. The official opening is not until the 28 but I've already been three times. The staff is super nice and always check on you. The food is so good. 

The fried devil's egg tastes like that one grandma who always makes the devils eggs decided to "take it up a notch" and coat it in bread crumbs and fry it. The artichoke dip is cooked in a mini cast iron skillet. It was hot and gooey when it got to the table. We could have used more chips. The catfish dinner is probably the best catfish I've ever had. I ordered the charred okra as a side and BABY let me tell you; they were the amazing. I spent the rest of the meal trying to figure out how the chef made it. I believe they were tossed in lime juice. I've also had the chicken and waffles. I love how tender the meat is in contrast to how crunchy the skin is. The seasoning is on point. The waffle comes with this berry butter and it is just yummy. I believe it is a cinnamon waffle. The kool-aid has the right amount of diabetes (aka it's sweet). The lavender lemonade alcoholic beverage is good, you don't feel like you're drinking a flower. The peach cobbler comes in a cast iron skillet it has the right amount of crust vs peaches. The lemon bar was tart and I was seriously mad I only ordered one.

Overall I haven't been disappointed once. Go try it.

Delicious food always.  Very consistent!
We have tried pretty much everything on the menu.  First time we tried the Gumbo.  Our opinion is that all the flavors of a Gumbo was perfect, however it lacked substance.  It was only a few small pieces of chicken and I think 2-3 sausages.  Plenty of the sauce/soup like that we had to ask for another bowl of rice.
The service is always professional and nice.  The other observation is that the walls against the booth tables need to be cleaned.  Well at least the booth we were seated at needed to be cleaned.  Not a good site while eating and trying enjoy our food.
But this is our families favorite to go spot for Soul food!

One of the best new restaurants I've been to in recent memory. The food was fabulous and the service very good, while keeping it fun. I started with a glass of prosecco, while my dining companion had a bloody Mary. My drink was yummy, but hers was spectacular with pickled okra and orange cauliflower, a celery stick, and a slice of crisp bacon as garnishes (she did note that the okra was under pickled and still a bit slimy, which was slightly offputting). I had fried catfish with the standard hush puppies, and cheesy grits and collard greens as sides. My meal was delicious and I especially liked the pimento cheese on the grits. My husband enjoyed the hush puppies I brought home to share with him. My companion had chicken tenders and substituted a nice, mustardy potato salad for the side French fries. She said the tenders were better than those offered at one of the more popular fried chicken places in town. We finished with a peach cobbler topped with a tiny scoop of ice cream, which we agreed was the best we've had in a restaurant. Our waiter was kind enough to offer to snap a photo of us and he stopped by to make sure we were okay just often enough to not seem intrusive. We can't wait to go back and try the oxtail and check out the vegan menu once they get it fully operational. I foresee good things ahead for Fixins.

UPDATED REVIEW:
I went back for a 2nd time.  I had these oxtails......I mean the oxtail... the charcoal okra was good,  however,  I don't know what happened to the Mac and Cheese!  I was not a fan this time around, it was not good to me at all! The first time during the soft opening, I really enjoyed it.  I finally tasted the waffle which had a good flavor to it if you're a fan of cinnamon.  We asked for more gravy for the oxtail, but they didn't bring the "oxtail gravy", they brought out the wrong one....#1 guest tasted it and she was not a fan,  she described it as "disgusting", #2 guest tasted it, her face said it all, therefore I was cool on not tasting it. So because #1 guest had already poured the mystery gravy onto her oxtail, she requested to send it back and replace it with the catfish dinner which they honored. The one oxtail on my plate had a good flavor but was VERY chewy!  So far the only consistencies from this experience between the first and second time that I can speak upon are the waffles and catfish. I will be back to try the breakfast/brunch and the burger, but sorry Fixins, I have to bump you down some stars, since the grand opening has been a 180 from the soft opening.

My wife and I were craving mac n cheese and Fixins came up. We each got the Ms. Ruth's Plate (3 sides). We both got mac n cheese and sweet potatoes. Then I had the coleslaw and my wife had the collard greens. Everything was amazing. We split an order of fried green tomatoes as well. I could have eaten the whole order myself. The staff were very friendly. Looking forward to our next meal from there

I've been here a few times, also gotten takeout, & have never been disappointed... I've always gotten the fried catfish...5 stars...but I will be broadening my experience! Faves are the Deep fried deviled eggs & the Lavender lemon drops...omg..bomb!!! Staff are super friendly, atmosphere is on point whether u eat in or outdoor patio...def recommend for after work chill spot or family get together...

So so good. Always skeptical of fried fish and Mac and cheese anywhere that isn't home but it was so good. Fried chicken was amazing, Mac was good, fried catfish was bomb! Waffles was good too. Yams were good as well. Will be back.

I really hate to give this review given I reviewed before a year ago with 5 stars, But fixin's isn't the same. I ordered to go  7/18 was my first time trying the smothered oxtails .. absolutely no flavor/seasoning everything was just ok even had my boyfriend try who's had oxtails before and agreed needed seasoning. I should have taken a picture but it honestly looked like they had been reheated because the gravy was a little geletized ; also got Mac n cheese which was different from the other times, cornbread was hard as a rock. Not a happy customer after this last order. Last time I ordered I had to drive back to get my order fixed. Idk if they have different cooks but I feel like food should be consistent. Fixin's was our go to spot for a nice southern Sunday meal but this was our last time coming.

I ordered fried chicken and two sides (red beans and rice plus creamed corn).  The fried chicken was very good !!!!  I didn't like my sides but I liked my sister's sides (collard greens and ?).  The biscuit was delicious.

It was good soul food just too crowded and not enough servers to accommodate all the customers that come to enjoy to delicious fried chicken greens macaroni biscuits. I have had the smothered chicken delicious as the pork chops
The fried green tomatoes and the bacon devil eggs. The service was impeccable I will go

Good service. Food pretty weak.

Oxtails were dried out n chewy. As for the gravy should i say don't expect any unless oily fat drippings is considered gravy.  Coleslaw tasted spoiled. Barely had any rice in the dish.

Artichoke cheese dip was luke warm...almost as if they had it prepared n just microwaved it prior to bringing out.

Best thing was the cornbread n grandpa's whiskey.

One customer even came up to me and said the oxtails were bad and I should try it at another place.

FABULOUS FOOD! FABULOUS STAFF! We were up in SAC yesterday from the Bay Area and had to stop by Fixins Soul Kitchen. My nieces had raved about the place and she was right. The food was so fresh and delicious.  The drinks were wonderful and the Kool-Aid containers made me smile. There is only one thing wrong with place, THEY NEED TO OPEN ANOTHER RESTAURANT IN THE EASY BAY.
I absolutely love this place! The staff is super friendly. My favorite drink is the lavender lemon drop, and food is delicious.
Great and food. Love the chicken and the fried okra was to die for. Went twice in one weekend :)  will go again!

Comfy eats! Get the fried chicken it's heavenly. We also ordered dessert despite the portions being large. The peach cobbler was like a warm hug from grandma on a summer evening in the south. 

We will definitely return.

We
Had the gumbo and it was not bad at all just not what we're used to as a gumbo. More like a Gulasch  great staff, good vibes. Good food, just not as authentic as we expected.

I Loved The Service and The Food at This Spot. You Can Even See The Former Mayor and Athlete KJ. The Ox~Tails Were Bomb as F. And The Service Is Oh So On The One !!! I Mean You Cannot Get Much Better. KJ Keep Doing Yo Thang Bru. I Can't Stop Coming to Yo Spot.

GOURMET KFC!!! 

After a round of golf at Haggin Oaks the fellas and I developed quite the appetite that only some of the best bbq in California could satisfy. Due to the shelter in place, we had to order take out and decided to park nearby to have our meals curbside. It was a bit of a wait while we got our food (50 minutes) but in their defense we ordered during the dinner rush on a Saturday night so I understand. 

I ordered the two piece led and thigh basket with fries, Mac n cheese, and a biscuit. The chicken was super moist and packed with flavor. The French fries had plenty of delicious seasoning that paired beautifully with a mac and cheese that was as gooey as it was creamy - just amazing! I'm very glad they also had syrup packets which I poured on my chicken and biscuit to get next level sweet/savory heaven! 

A fair price at $15 for my meal that left me incredibly satisfied and full. 

Would definitely come back!!

Food, excellent. Service, not so much. Had a passive aggressive server who forgot our drinks and had the audacity to give attitude about it instead of apologizing. Did not feel very welcoming. Wish I could tip those cooks though, best fried chicken I've had by far.
I've gone a few times with friends and family, nice atmosphere, the food is I feel more Caribbean rather than southern, due to the sweet savory taste. But it's truly sufficient!!

Came here about 10 am. First thing that struck me was the hosts seemed to be unsure of what they were doing. 
Got seated after a 5 min wait and got our waters. 
After ordering our food we waited about 35 minutes to receive our food. But even then we didn't receive one of the items. Never got a refill of water.
Both of us had chicken and waffles. Waffles 2/5, not crispy enough on the outside. Chicken 4/5, but I like the hot sauce. 
Peach cobbler 1/5, was so sweet you couldn't even taste the peaches. But the flakey top is the only thing preventing me from giving it a 0. 
Definitely won't be coming back
I'm a fan.  Went here for brunch, and the food and service were great.  The only nit is that it's a little pricey and it does get busy.

I've been here a few times. Each time I go different experience. I'm the beginning food was very delicious and atmosphere was comfortable. Last couple of times my catfish appeared as a decent portion, but in fact, had a lot of air pockets and was smaller than half the size of my petite hand. The Mac & cheese and yams were cold. I specifically requested to sit inside because of the squirrels that tend to run rapid in the outside dining area. Choosing inside dining was the worst due to the annoying flies & gnats and made for an unpleasant dining experience.  I will not be returning unless it is in the Los Angeles area.

They just moved to the top of list of soul food.  Went in for lunch, great decision.  We started with the deep fried deviled eggs. A very nice flavor and texture i loved it. Guess we'll try the fried green tomatoes next time.  I had the fried chicken omelette it was a winner. The taste was great and with grits I left satisfied.  My daughter had the catfish and okra, potato salad. She gave two thumbs. We will both try the fried chicken next time. This place has a bright future with a great location

Delicious and very big portions of food we always have yummy leftovers. The staff are friendly. The ambiance is nice. Very amazing addition to my Oak Park neighborhood:) would definitely recommend this place for some good soul food.

I just ate at the best restaurant in Sac!!!  The smothered fried chicken was amazing! But the Ox Tails were even better!! The cheesy grits were Home made and creamy. The cream corn, the yams, the corn bread, the mmmm Mac and cheese, the greens were all fantastic and so flavorful!! And the deserts were to die for!  We tried them all!!! The Banana Pudding was my fav. But every bite of the Peach Cobbler and Bread pudding was gobbled up! 
The service was first rate!!!  Victoria, greeted us with the best smile. Our server Jenna was on it!  Chef Nicki stopped by our table to make sure we were all happy. We were more than happy!!! It was a great great evening in the very beautiful city of Oak Park!!

Hello all
Well the new restaurant that the ex NBA player ex mayor of Sacramento put together is nice and cozy place to eat and have libations at. The server was nice pleasant and she was informative I asked about oxtails She said that had a sweet taste in the back ground Ok I'm game!! What disappointment yes it had sweet background a tad bit too much The thing that got me I got one large bone and one little bone Yes it was tender nice flavor with the sweetness But for $20 bucks 
Yes it's a new and cozy place to eat but tad bit expensive more than I'd pay again
So enjoy but be prepared for the cost
Cheers
good food. just a lot of talking about how genuine and authentic they are. i would eat there again

After seeing so many reviews both for and against the food I decided to try, and with everything going on I was scared of how the quality of food would be, but let me say I was not disappointed!! I got 3 pieces of chicken in case the portion was small but no I could not finish the thigh only the wing and breast and it tasted so good!! 
I was not mad at the money I was spending after tasting the quality of food I was getting, I was impressed and full

This restaurant was delicious! There was a line of people waiting to try it out. Our wait was about 45 mins and once inside the employees were professional, the ambiance was great, the decor was industrial & tasteful, the drinks were refreshing and the food was absolutely amazing! The wait for the food and drink wasn't long either. I wish there were more in the Sac area!
Enjoyed my Memorial Day weekend having a few drinks made my christen the bay manager and might i say she knows what she's doing.

Came here for a Birthday Brunch with my friends a few weeks ago, it was a nice experience. The waitress was very kind and helped a lot of us through the menu being it was most of our first time there. The food was really yummy, seasoned well, and big portions! The only thing that I didn't like is that they can only split the check in half... meaning 16 people had to figure out how to split there portion off.. pretty inconvenient considering they get an automatic tip... they can't make that a little easier on the guest? Anyway I know it was a big party, but that kinda put a damper on the end of our experience. I'll be back for the yummy food tho.

They just opened in Oak Park and everyone has been talking about them so me and Bae went to go check it out. First of all I loved that they had koolaid and that the flavors were the colors, brought us both back to our childhood. 

We started off with the New Orleans sweet tea and the lavender lemon drop which were both bomb af. For our apps we did the deep fried devil eggs, fried green tomatoes and wings. All three the flavor was spot on, however there were a few missteps. The eggs were a little rubbery, I would have preferred them just make the bacon crispy to give it that crunch or to undercook the egg before they deep fried it. Even still the creamy filling mixed with the crispy coating and the spicy vinegar of the hot sauce was fire. The wings were whole wings dipped in sauce, which while the sauce was delicious I would have preferred already separated wings just because it was messy and they didn't have wet wipes. The fried green tomatoes were the best in my opinion. They could have cut them just a tad bit thicker, but they were nice and crisp and juicy with that creamy spicy chipotle sauce and the greens. Omg yassss. 

For dinner we got the fried chicken wings, leg and thigh with the two Fixins (sides), greens and Mac and cheese and a biscuit. The biscuit was a hot water biscuit with this berry cinnamon butter, I was in heaven. The greens were cool, they needed a little kick so I put their hot sauce on them and yeeeeeeah schmoney. The Mac was bomb. My girl was iffy on it, but it was just how I like it. The chicken thooooo, lemme tell yall. Juicy on the inside, crispy skin and just yum. The food did come out hella quick so I wonder if the pressure fry it or if they just steady back there frying. 

The service was excellent. Our server was attentive, cool to chop it up with and made sure we were taken care of. The music took us back and had us both singing along to all the old school classics we grew up to. The food came out quick, was hot, and delicious. We can't wait to go back and try the oxtails, gumbo, waffles and their desserts. Since we live right around the block we will def be repeat customers.

Food and service was amazing. Staff were super friendly, helpful with figuring out how to address my food allergies. Enjoyed my purple koolaid, catfish and fried deviled eggs.  Music playing was a good mix of old school and new school.  I heard the mac n cheese was nom nom nom.  Serving sizes were on point, had left overs for late lunch.  Highly recommended. I will definitely be back when I am in town.

Guys if your looking for great soul food look no further!  This place has it all!  Super clean and vibey atmosphere!  I recommend the oxtail with collard greens!  So flavorful!  Also, Shout out to our server Ansar for making our first time experience the best!
Everything exceeded our expectations. We had a party of 8 people so we were able to try alittle bit of everything. You definitely will be leaving full and satisfy

We got there and they told us the wait time was 1 hour to 1.5 hours but we ended up only waiting 35 minutes!! So yay for that! Our waitress Chloe was one of the best waitresses we have ever had anywhere! Tipped her over 20%. So I couldn't taste everything but... Fried chicken was good, with their hot sauce was really good!, greens were really good considering they don't have meat, the MAC n Cheese is a must get and the Red Beans & Rice were bland and not good at all, My husband liked the candied yams but felt they were missing the marshmallow. Overall we will be back!

My new go-to for soul food in Sacramento! We ordered everything with friend chicken- salad, omelette, waffles, and sandwich! Sides included creamed corn and mac 'n cheese, which were delicious, but we could barely touch them. Pimiento cheese appetizer was gooey and hot . All the chicken was juicy and crispy. Be back soon!

Went here in a Sunday morning and the place was packed. Waited approximately 30 minutes to be seated. The waitress was very kind and helpful. We ordered the fried deviled eggs, 2 wings and waffle and and the 3 piece and waffle with the Sweet tea.  The food was cooked to perfection !! The chicken was crispy and moist , the waffles were good, didn't care for the butter that came with it, though it was still good. The fried deviled eggs were tasty and the Sweet Tea was really good!! I will be back to try other menu items soon
I absolutely love this place. The food is good, the vibe is amazing, great music, full drinks. And did I mention the food is good lol
First time doing a takeout order and I was very satisfied with the packaging an the portion size! The staff was very friendly and I will definitely be back for more!

Bringing classic soul food with a modern face. We heard around that the oxtail was very well liked, but two of us tried he chicken and waffles on our own.

Getting our initial orders in took some time, but the speed getting the food out and the quality of the food itself made up for it. The waffles came with a pink molasses butter. The chicken was a little small, but if high quality. The breading was excellent and tastes like it was made for the syrup!

Overall a wonderful addition to the community and I would totally recommend that everyone check this place out!

amazing!!!! Great service and great food! The catfish was so juicy and fresh and the Mac and cheese was cheesy and I finished d dry last bite! The yams were also really good, everything was delicious and the server was friendly and helpful! Definitely recommended it to all of my friends and family for some good soul food!

We made a rushed stop at Fixins Soul Kitchen, and out waiter, Kim, was incredibly accommodating. Not only was the service efficient, but the food surpassed our expectations. We got the gumbo, and it was one of the best I've had in a while. 5 stars because the restaurant was chic and clean, pet-friendly, the food was delicious, and the service was excellent (Thanks Kim)!
loved the sides, fried chicken and the feel of the place. Service was excellent.
This place is amazing. The food is delicious, drinks are good and the staff is awesome, they walk around greeting customers and it's just a really good vibe. It's worth every penny

Listen. I'm literally in the middle of my meal. My partner and I got the smothered chicken with Mac and cheese and  red bean and rice. Then we did the side combo with yams, greens, and cornbread. I legit just called them because I was borderline in tears. This food is obviously cooked with so much love. I've been to many soul food restaurants, but this was the first one that made me miss my Gran. If you're on the fence about this place, don't be. You can't go wrong.
Some of th best soul food I have had in Sacramento!  They also serve Excellent whiskey!  It was a little pricey but still worth it.  Ch KC them out on your next date night but bring a c note!

Possibly one of the best meals I've had in Sacramento and for sure the best southern food. We tried the fried deviled eggs, smothered chicken (gravy is to die for), the shrimp and grits, two piece fried chicken, Mac and cheese, red beans and rice, collard greens, candied yams and peach cobbler for dessert. Even the biscuit was freaking amazing and it came with some sort of berry(?) butter. All were the best I've ever had except the Mac and cheese which was still delicious. I lived in the south for 4 years and this is some great food. The service was also really great. Took a little bit longer to get drinks but other than that zero complaints.

I had the fried chicken and collard greens. The order was ready early and the staff were super sweet. The chicken was basic and not as unique as I anticipated. The collard greens were bomb! Loved them! Plus, the restaurant is super cute.

Let me start off by saying me and my entire family LOVE Fixins. My younger child was born medically fragile and gets most of her nutrients through a g-tube in her stomach so it has been extremely difficult trying to find foods she is willing to eat by mouth now that she is 6. Throughout this pandemic we have been ordering takeout from Fixins because she LOVES the biscuits and waffles here! That is a BIG deal for us because it is very difficult finding a spot we can all order food from that includes her. My son is a big fan of the fried chicken, Mac n cheese, and the waffles. Me and my man have loved everything we've ever ordered here ESPECIALLY the collard greens. Today me and the kids needed to pick up my daughter's monthly medicine from UC Davis so I decided to loosen up our little bubble and do our first dine-in experience here with the outdoor patio. All the staff were very friendly and our server was warm and welcoming. The food came out fast and fresh. The food from here is always bomb, but it definitely is even better straight from the kitchen. We will definitely be back for more. Thank you for a great lunch!

I came in here with high expectations and they were not met at all. I came in one day around 5pm. I was greeted nice and sat at the bar area. There I was given a menu and browsed their selection. I decided I was going to do the Hot Chicken Sandwich (Chicken burger) and I got a side of Mac & Cheese. Once the food came it all looked so good except the Mac and cheese. The chicken had a really dried taste to it. If it weren't for the chipotle mayo sauce I don't think I would've finished it. Some of my fries tastes really old and hard and only about a quarter of them tasted fresh. As far as the Mac and cheese (what I was looking forward to the most) was anything but good! I seen other pictures on here where the Mac and cheese has creamy cheese in it. Mine was another story, it had old dried cheese. I could compare it to the one from Golden Corral. It was super dry and had no taste at all. The burger was okay and the Mac an cheese I left it all. The staff is really welcoming.

My new go to soul food spot. 
Everything was on point.

Now in my top 3 hot chicken sandwich spots in California.

The fried deviled eggs are a must eat, and the green tomatoes are excellent too.

Don't skip the Bloody Mary if you like pickled veggies. House mix doesn't disappoint.

The owner was at the restaurant walking around talking to everyone and there wasn't a frown in sight. Perfect food and perfect vibes.

So I will update this review once I actually get a chance to eat, but this review is for the wait time. I came on Saturday afternoon with sisters. I was 100% expecting a wait since I know this restaurant has a lot of buzz. We were quoted 30-45 mins for a table, but ended up waiting for 2+ hours. Ultimately had to leave for an appointment right when our table was ready. Now I don't mind waiting for good food...but I need accurate information to determine whether I even have enough time to wait. 
Oh and also...about 1.5 hours into our wait another patron, who had just pulled up, came out and told us that there's no waiting at the bar and he had already ordered. We were not given this option when we checked in but...I guess that's a tip.

Ok its time to review this place. Four of us went one evening for dinner about two months ago. Two ordered the fried chicken and two got the chicken n waffles. The only thing that was even remotely good was the chicken n waffles. Service was slow (maybe 15 other diners) and the drinks were horrible! I was going to give this place some time to work out new restaurant growing pains and try  it again. 

The reason I decided to write now is some friends just tried it. One of them ordered a martini. When it came he asked for an onion  for his drink. The server came back with a slice of onion. A SLICE OF ONION??? Are you kidding me right now? 

Hey Kevin Johnson, maybe it's time to replace the high schoolers who work there with some real bartenders and wait staff. Just sayin.

I can go on for days about this restaurant so forgive me in advance for the length..

I would definitely recommend this restaurant to anyone that is looking for some good soul food that tastes pretty darn close to the food grandma would serve for Sunday dinner. 

For starters, the fried green tomatoes was a hit at our table (slightly salty but still worth it). Never had deviled eggs fried so you know we had to try that. It was "different/unexpected" but for sure addictive so be careful with those. The catfish was crispy yet juicy and you get to choose 2 sides/Fixins to go along with it. I chose Mac & cheese accompanied by the candies yams (cuz of course if you know anything about southern food, it would be a sin if those sides weren't served together). Lastly, THEY SERVE KOOL-AID fam. Not the "just add water" kind; they do it right and it taste like they're for SURE adding 1-cup of sugar to packet ratio just like I was raised to do. 

DOWNSIDE/improvements: The restaurant is still brand-spanking new and has yet to have they're official opening. I was in a 14-person party and had to wait 2hrs. I'm not stressing about though since we also came for Sunday dinner just like the rest of the black folks and families not wanting to cook after church. We were also separated in 2 different booths and a table on the side. The are not yet accepting reservations (so first come-first seated) and you cannot order food to-go. The kitchen also ran out of a few items (ox tail is really popular and chicken breast) but everything is made from scratch which is a HUGE plus. The menu is a bit pricy but once you taste the food you know it's worth it. If you are vegan/vegetarian/or just watching your weight, you are in the wrong establishment. Although you can make a meal from sides that are vegetarian and there is a veggie omelette (which is served with collard greens and is AMAZING), soul food is not meant to spare the calories or save the thighs. Just being honest here. Again, the restaurant is still new so I expect that they're still getting a feel for their operation and how they want to run the business. I'm sure some processes will be streamlined in the future and fingers crossed for to-go orders becoming a thing.

Final thoughts: You can spot former Mayor Kevin Johnson and his mother working the floors serving food and greeting guests (which is great to see from any execs in any business). The atmosphere is very nice, clean, and positive. Lots of families and friendly faces. You can tell this establishment will be a great addition to the oak park community and is being operated by hard working professionals serving up dishes to warm the soul. Wish them the best of luck for I will definitely be returning (I've got my eye on that banana pudding I didn't get to try).

This wait is absolutely ridiculous!! 2 hours and didn't even get seated. Y'all gotta do better than that. 

Update: I ended up revisiting Fixins about 2 weeks after this experience and have since become a loyal customer. I can tell they definitely made improvements and really are a great neighborhood restaurant. Plus, I literally haven't had ANYTHING on the menu I don't like. It's like being in your moms kitchen

The service takes my rating from a 4 to a 3. They are a little slow even when not busy, especially , for take out too.the food is good and is worth the wait if you are expecting it. The location is good parking a little tight. The place is cute with outside patio seating too. I look forward to them working out the kinks because they have the makings of 5 stsrs.
Fantastic as always! Great food and customer service. We had Cat as our server again... she's wonderful, always with her cheerful and beautiful smile. Can't wait to come back again...

Get the fish plate. Lavender lemon drop. You're welcome! Service is amazing. From the time you walk to when they are clearing things out. Very polite and profesional. The music is awesome. Really good vibes!!

The food was really good and tasty. I tried something new the charred Okra and it was delicious. The rum cake for desert was creamy and sweet. The atmosphere was lively and looks like everyone was having a good time. Reminds me of being in Grandma's kitchen. The smells, the tase and just the love that goes into preparing the food.

Amazing food and service! We had Maia as our server and she was very funny and attentive to us. I ordered the fried chicken with Mac n cheese and potato salad with the side of corn bread. BEST friend chicken i ever had. It was so juicy and crispy. Auntie Pearl also came to check on us and she was very sweet and made sure we had an enjoyable time there. Thank you will definitely be making the drive to Sac to come again :)

Great food , great flavors , KoolAid is the best. Loved the flavored butter they had and all the variety of sauces. Chicken wasn't the best I've had but it was worth another try. A little pricy but worth it if you like soul food. Red velvet cake wasn't the best.
Prepare to wait but it's worth every minute. The biscuits, butter, kool-aid, & chicken is to die for. Matter of fact, lemme place an order. Y'all making me hungry again!

The food is excellent, but the service is not. Hence 2 starts. I placed a pickup order and did not receive butter for the cornbread muffins or dressing for the salad. The staff needs to be retrained in basic phone etiquette such as letting the customer know they will be placed on hold before they do so. It was confusing to order, and all of the condiments were not given. I spent a lot of $.

First time here, and WOW! The chicken and waffles was delicious, as was the grits and omelette. 
Toni served our table with grace, efficiency, and a smile!
Please cone and enjoy the food, it is fairly priced for the excellent taste and serving sizes.

I am picky when it comes to soul food but this always hits the spot. 

The yams remind me of my Mom's. You can't go wrong with anything on the menu but I always get the catfish and grits. 

Yes, there maybe a wait when you go but to me its always worth the wait. So happy they offered to go during COVID but I do enjoying taking in the experience of eating there.

It only took once for me to go there and discover the items I ordered were extremely salty and I like salt but couldn't finish the meal after one mouthful.  I  spend a lot of money for food we couldn't eat.

I had the pleasure of coming here with my family for a baby shower. Even though our group was quite big, our needs were still met, and we had a good time. It is a lovely restaurant with great food and service. I'll definitely be coming back again.

Love this place! The Mac n cheese is always on point. The cat fish nuggets are so delightful! I'm not much of a fried food eater, but it's worth it to have their fried chicken once in a while. Sooooo Yummy!

My son ordered the Oxtails, rice & collard greens . What a great choice! Had never had this meal before, and now it's one of his and my favorites. This place is worth stopping by when in Sacramento! The only negative was I thought the Lemonade was way to sugary for my tastes. But don't let that stop you from visiting!
Great choice for soul food in Sacramento. Great drinks and nice atmosphere as well. I don't have any pictures unfortunately.

I've been to Fixins twice since it opened in Oak Park. I went for brunch and dinner in the same day because it was so delicious! The artwork is really cool because it represents the culture and was relatable to me. The restaurant wasn't packed and the food quality was amazing both times. The wings were huge and the Mac n cheese was amazing. The second time I had the chicken sandwich and I couldn't finish it. The chicken was so tender and crispy at the same time. I hope that they maintain their recipes because this is the best soul food I've had in a long time.

It's been a while since I went, but I visited the Sacramento location during COVID and although the wait was long, the food was worth the wait!! I had the Oxtails, Rice & Gravy and Collard Greens...delicious!! I wish I could find the pictures but this is my place of choice for my upcoming Birthday Dinner!!

At first I was a little skeptical another soul food restaurant. However I was pleasantly surprised on how good the food was its real down-home soul food.  The service was epic for a black restaurant I give this establishment 10 gold stars keep it coming guys I'll be back duces.
Not very many soul food restaurants in Sacramento but this one definitely is comparable to a real SOUTHERN restaurant.

Great brunch at Oak Park's newest spot.  Our table enjoyed fried green tomatoes as well as fried deviled eggs as starters.  Both were excellent.  The chicken with waffles was delicious, and the shrimp and grits was a standout. The gumbo had lots of okra and was well flavored.  The fried Chicken was a little greasy, but very flavorful.  Sides of red beans and rice and collard greens were tasty and well-prepared.  Looking forward to my next visit.

Food is great. My poor review is based on sanitation. I noticed the men's room soap was out for both dispensers. Notified the bartender since I was dining on the outside bar. She notified other gentleman. After dinner, went to wash my hands still noticed the soap was not refilled. Ok, maybe they didn't have time to or none of the employees used it. Then I saw a male employee went in there. I waited. By law, all employee must wash hands with soap and water. Went back in, nope. Still no soap. You get one extra star for great food. They do great job spraying chairs and table tho.

So we finally made it. This place has been the buzz lately but I was a little disappointed . We were there on a Thursday and it was pretty busy. We got seated within a reasonable amount of time. Waiter was good too. In between she was a little MIA but there were other people helping her tables out. We started off with the deviled eggs but with no bacon, and to my surprise it was really delicious. Then we had the wings, neither one of us enjoyed them they were a little soggy and I didn't like the Cajun coating. The ranch sauce however was everything . Then for our entrees we ordered the shrimp and grits and 3 pc chicken and waffle. The grits were so bland, tasted like hospital food. No seasoning it could of just been tonight and maybe they are really good but I've had a lot of shrimp and grits and I find them very satisfying but this was far from satisfying. However the chicken and waffles were a home run  the chicken had amazing crunch and seasoned well. The waffles were nice and cinnamonny it was served with grape  butter and hot syrup. We were so full so no dessert maybe just maybe, next time.

I am overjoyed to have a soul food restaurant in my neighborhood Oak Park! I would like to start off by saying I am a soul food connoisseur lol.... The collard greens were very good, I love my collards tinder! If you like stewed collards then these are not for you. The shrimp and grits were the absolute best shrimp and grit I've ever had! The fact that they serve the shrimp and grits with prawns is amazing! The catfish was delicious! The black eye peas were good. I must admit the candied yams were okay, I like my yams a little sweeter. Let's talk about drinks, I really like the lavender lemon drop! If you are spontaneous like my sister (who invented this concoction) please try the red Kool-aid mimosa! Overall my first experience at Fixins was a great one I plan on becoming a regular...

I lived in the Bay Area for 20 years and hands down this is my favorite restaurant here. Absolutely everything is good. The drinks, everything, is just spot on. This place deserves all the good word of mouth it has.

One of the best spots for soul food in Sacramento. The chicken and waffles was spectacular and I'd most definitely order it again. The hot sauce from Fixins is also a must try. Also tried the collard greens, Mac & cheese and biscuits with gravy. Everything was tasty. There seems to always be a wait but it's worth it!

The food was ok. I had the oxtails, greens, and an adult kool-aid. The oxtails were very good. The greens weren't like my grannies but still tasty.  The adult kool aid was not worth the price. We sat outside. I am a bigger woman and the seats outside were very uncomfortable. I had to ask to be moved so I can enjoy my meal without sitting on the tip of the chair. I highly recommend different chairs.

Food was top notch! Mac/Cheese a bit bland, nonetheless I would give this place a perfect 5.....BUT, the service was horrible. We sat at the high top table near the bar and finally had to go to the bar for drinks. Not just alcohol, but water as well. After 15 minutes, we had to start asking employees who our server was and no one knew, even the front of the house manager which baffled me. Finally, at one point a server said, oh, you may be mine, let me go see. After another 5 minutes we had to ask him again and he said he was and will be right back. 5 minutes later he stopped by asking if we want water. THAT WAS THE FIRST INQUIRY FOR SERVICE. After ordering, we had other servers bring out our food and it came in time and was delicious. Again, if it wasn't for the service, I would give them a 5. Oxtails, chicken, pork chop smothered, fried deviled eggs and tomatoes.....all were excellent. Kudos to the cooks. Honestly, the manager saw the issue and he did nothing. When I say nothing, he wasn't doing anything, a good manager would've assisted if the rest of the team was too busy.

Fixins has been on my list of restaurants to try for a couple years now and I finally got my soul food fix at fixins! There was a long line of people and it was an hour wait when we got there on a Saturday afternoon around 4pm. But let me tell you, the wait is WORTH IT! It's got a cool atmosphere, with good drinks (alcoholic and non-alcoholic). The outdoor patio seating was perfect on a warm afternoon. I ordered the two-piece chicken and waffles. The chicken was perfectly crisp on the outside, while juicy on the inside, 5/5. My partner ordered the catfish with hush puppies and collard greens, which he loved. Although the place was busy, service was quick and attentive. Already looking forward to my next visit!

sat at the bar with some friends and had a blast... The Bartender (Mackenzie) was amazing.. one of the best Old Fashions I've had in a minute.. Great Conversation, very polite and funny. Will definitely be going back for the experience.. 5 out of 5
Always my go-to place for soul food! Portions could be a little bigger, especially for the sides but other than that everything tastes great!
Them oxtails are smacking. Shout out to Mackenzie for top notch service.  Definitely will b back
Best bartender ever is MacKenzie!!!.... She made the greatest Old fashion I've had.... And she's cute

I came to this WONDERFUL restaurant with my family visiting from North Carolina last week. From the second we walked in we were welcomed and felt so at home. We were greeted kindly, complimented, the staff are very attentive to our needs. I would give them 20/10 amazing customer service. The smothered pork is a MUST! I am so grateful for Fixins as it was also my first return to dine-in after lockdown and the blue Kool aid. OMG! Love you Fixins! You have a life long patron
Fixin's delivers a great plate of some of the greatest selections of the southern meals entrees, sides & desserts to your appetite.

This was our 4th time visiting. Everytime we come we have noticed it doesn't matter of its a weekend or a weekday there is at least a 30 min wait for a table and the waitresses' service is just as bad. Also this time a table of 6 was seated before us as a table for 2 and we were given it was a bigger group by the hostesses as the reason. We have ordered the fried chicken, oxtail, gumbo, smothered pork chops, and smothered chicken. The fried chicken has been the best but it never seems to be the same when we visit. They need training ASAP. Everytime it's 60-70 per check. However we, love the bartender who has only been there 3 months.
Super good food! Fried chicken, yams, potato salad, and corn bread on point!!!  Hubby had the oxtails and black eye peas. Did not disappoint.
This restaurant is quite good. Some staffs are very friendly, but some are not. Chicken is okay but coleslaw and potato are very hard to eat.

We went twice in a span of 5 days, I think that says enough. Just go, stop reading this! The 5-star review should say it all! But I guess if you "need more convincing": It's clean, friendly, busy (so be patient and don't cope an attitude with the employees who are literally running around 'cause it's so busy). 

The chicken is juicy, tasty, and the coating is done so perfectly that I no longer make fried chicken at home. I leave it to the experts and am happy to pay for it! 

The mac n cheese is homestyle, not a creamy sauce but a savory taste that makes you think someone's auntie just dropped off a batch at a bbq.

The collard greens are fantastic, not too vinegary and NO HIPSTER KALE IN IT! It's real collard greens and I'm so thankful for it. 

If you're still reading this and still are unsure then I can't help you. In fact don't go, it'll shorten my wait time for a table!

Heard so much about this place so I had to give it a try. I ordered the fried chicken potato salad and collard greens as my 2 fixins and a biscuit. I will say I enjoyed the biscuit the most I think it was one of the best biscuits I've had it was soft flaky buttery just wonderful. The potato salad and greens were also good its hard to find these foods done well at most restaurants so I was impressed with how good they were. The fried chicken was pretty good but not exceptional it was crispy but lacked a bit of seasoning (I do not mean salt) the skin was still crispy (which is surprising since I had it delivered) but again overall a good plate for the cost, I would definitely order again. I think I will try the oxtails next time.

First time visit was all I needed to become a forever visitor...food exceeded expectations, JC our waiter served with humor made the wait worth it. Management is exceptional...if I could give it a 10 I would...amazing all around!

I would love love to give Fixin's a 5 star but I cannot due to the interaction around 4pm yesterday 2/20 as the day bartender Elise, literally screamed at my friend! Literally screamed! First and foremost,  RESPECT. There is a way to deliver your point that you are getting across. Even after that I observed her interact with her co workers. Horrible! See should not be a representation of Fixin's as customers are patiently waiting over an hour and a half to get screamed at for standing in a certain area of the bar. All she had to do was kindly ask my friend to move over to the side. Remy and Christen on the other hand, total 180. Amazing customer service as they apologized for Elise's actions, but they were giving us great customer service before that  anyway. I ordered tropical Kool aid and my friend ordered tequila with a squeeze of lime. We also ordered the cat fish nugget appetizer as the kind hostess informed us it would be an additional 20 mins we were quoted when we first arrived. Since my friend was yelled at, she felt uncomfortable and sat down in front of the hostess stand and I stayed at the bar and chatted with Christen as she was very pleasant to speak with. Very nice, we talked about her recommendations on kool aid, about me having type 1 diabetes, at the same time she was making all the drinks so smoothly and multi tasking so well. That's the main reason why I was at the bar, trying to get my sugar up as I already taken my insulin but had to wait longer to get sat, I didn't want to pass out in the restaurant, oh and my friend just had shoulder and hand surgery and had her arm in a sling, was literally at the bar for 45 sec when Elise yelled at her, Anywho, after conversing with Christen she made me feel at ease, like yes they do have great customer service. She was awesome and so was Remy. Then the hostess let us know we were next in line and there was bit of a confusion so we had to wait an extra 10 mins but it was okay bc they were very kind about it and I had my kool aid so my sugar was up and didn't mind waiting. We were seated and our catfish nuggets came right on time! Delish! We scarfed those down in literally 3 mins. I ordered the oxtails, greens and yams, my friend ordered fried chicken, Mac and cheese, black eyed peas and delivered eggs. I've been here about 15 times since they opened a few years ago and it's always been "good" but yesterday, that food was fire! Best flavors! My fav are the oxtails which are so tender, juicy and delish. The black eyed peas are fire. Yams, nom nom! I went into work informing all my employees that they need to try it out. Shout out to the chef! You are doing your thing back there! Chelsea was our server and she too was AMAZING! When she greeted us as busy as they were, we never felt rushed, she was very kind with a great personality. Love her, great service! Overall, the food and interacting with Christen and Chelsea out weighed the long wait and horrible representation of the bartender Elise. If it wasn't for Christen and Chelsea we would not I probably wouldn't be returning. Thank you so much. I am very big on customer service, as I used to serve and bartend and my friend is a chef, I'm no longer in the restaurant industry but manage 2 great medical practices and I would be sooooooo disappointed if one of my employee's treated one of my patient's that way. We did inform the manager of the situation. She apologized and checked on us during our meal. I highly suggest doing employee reviews or under cover diners, Kevin that bartender needs to go or go through training one more time. To the rest of the employees thank you so much! To the chef once again! Food was fire! Oh and my favorite kool aid is tropical and watermelon!

Everything was delicious! I ordered fried chicken with red beans and rice, greens, peach cobbler and of course the red koolaid!! It was so good I took home three more orders for my family. Auntie Pearl makes your visit complete with her sweet personality. I will definitely be back for some more delicious authentic soulfood!!
Amazing food, amazing atmosphere, amazing drinks! Had chicken and waffles and haven't had such a delicious meal for a while! Just didn't want to leave that place of how cozy it is inside!!

This place is exceptional. The service is excellent and all staff accomidating making you feel at home. David at the bar makes amazing old fashioned with Buffalo Trace worth upcharge. Fried chicken was mouthwatering, greens best I ever had and the gumbo perfect. Thank you for accommodating Hans Gunther and bringing him chilled ice water and scotch.

First off, The atmosphere is good. The margarita was delicious, and the food is the best I have had in Sacramento and I have been here 12 years. I am so thankful to the cook, the bartender and the staff. Good job. Please open one in the Citrus Heights area. I would appreciate it. Loved it. To anyone that wants some good ass soul food please go and try it. I guarantee you will go back.

My family and I were in the Sacramento area to drop off my son to Sac State. We were looking for a fun spot for a group dinner and came across Fixins on Yelp. It far exceeded our expectations. The food was absolutely delicious, the ambiance was fun and the service was warm and friendly. We ordered collard greens, mac and cheese, fried okra, grits as sides to go with our fried chicken. We ordered the fried green tomatoes as a starter plate. Our server Nathan was great and helped us think through our meal and drink orders.

Soul food atmosphere  staff Sadirha was my server- she rocks!!! Patio dining very nice
I've never had Oxtail before- to die for!!! 
I'm so happy to have found this place
I certainly hope I have the opportunity to return.

I loved everything! The fried catfish is to die for! And the Mac &cheese is pure heaven! The drinks are delicious too! I loved the lavender lemon drop...omg ! Highly recommend Fixins for some good ole comfort food!!
The food is here delicious the best soul food I had in a while. Customer service is great , very fast service I will definitely be back soon.
This place is so good, All the food was on point, I will definitely be back. I ordered the smothered chicken with collard greens and mac n cheese and it was Heaven on a plate.

First time here this past Good Friday and I must say it was well worth it! We waited about 30 minutes to be seated, there were people waiting when we got there (good sign in my opinion). We were seated in a booth and greeted by Maia who was friendly and helpful, they seemed a bit understaffed but I guess with the new regulations it's to be expected. I had the oxtails which were different than I expected a bit sweet but still very delicious and tender, the catfish nuggets were seasoned to perfection, the Mac n cheese Lawd! The greens were ok, overall everything was tasty and very generous portions. I highly recommend!

This place is so good!!! Friend chicken and waffles are to die for. Their mixed alcoholic drinks were good too. Real home cooking. Great atmosphere, great staff that make you feel at home. I can't wait to come back truly

My son and I were here tonight ...this is one of the best restaurants I have been to in quite a while . We had the Oxtails and Shrimp with grits . My son had a whisky drink and I had a Lavender Lemon drop , Peach Cobbler for dessert . It was all very delicious . Sean our server was the best; professional , friendly and knowledgeable. We were seated outside and it was just lovely . Beautiful ferns and comfortable seating with great R&B music . The DJ was on point ! I would highly recommend this restaurant to anyone wanting an exceptional dining experience . We will definitely go back and I will tell everyone I know of the experience .

Kool-Aid was amazing, the country fried chicken was great.
Flavorful, & satisfying.
Great customer service.
The food was absolutely amazing!!! I would definitely recommend this restaurant to anyone looking for amazing cuisine.

The fried chicken here was good! The pieces you get are huge. There was good flavor and the skin was crispy. This is also one of the rare fried chicken restaurants that don't use buttermilk in their batter (big bonus for a dairy free person like me!)
The food is Amazing!!!! The staff are extremely nice and welcoming. It has become one of most favorite places to go and enjoy some delicious Soul Food! KUDOS!!!!

The food was excellent and the service was great. We waited an hour to be seated and it was worth the wait. After being seated inside, we had to ask to be moved to the outside do to a family member needing to be in a warmer area. The staff moved us without any hassle. The prices were fair, considering the portions and the great taste. We definitely will be going back.

We enjoyed everything on our plates! Food was seasoned well and the portion sizes were filling.  Kevin Johnson was on site visiting tables and taking pictures with patrons.  We went this past Sat at about 4pm and there was a 45min wait, not bad for a Saturday at a  highly recommended place.  I encourage you to taste for yourself.

Our visit started out a little shaky. We had been waiting in line for 15 minutes and the another group walked in. The hostess ignored us and sat them immediately. After pointing out that we had been waiting, the hostess corrected her mistake. 

Our waitress was Jena. She was extremely attentive and apologized profusely for the mishap. She made a shaky meal much so better. We'll be sure to request her in the future. She's a prize!
My first time coming here and the food was really amazing, the collared greenz were delicious the service was okay

Food is great! Wife ordered shrimp and grits and i ordered smothered chicken.  Ordered the gravy on the side and dipped my chicken!  Also had the Louisiana sweet tea w/whiskey! Waitress was very friendly i believe her name is Nataya? Definitely be there again soon!

It was my first time here and I left feeling full of great food! The staff was very nice and helpful and the wait was only 20 min on a packed Saturday morning. I got the chicken sandwich with a side of mac&cheese and it was delicious! My fiancé got chicken and waffles with a side of cornbread and she loved her food. I'll definitely recommend this llace  to everyone for the great environment and great food. Parking was a little hard to find but once we drove a little down the block we were able to find a spot.

I would give it a 3 star except my meal was $25 and my 1 drink was $4, $26 total (after my military discount) was a bit too much. 

I arrived at 11:01 am on Monday (Columbus Day) and was excited to see my first soul food restaurant in Sacramento. Mind you (yes that is a warning...I'm a cook! But I feel that I'm lenient in other's cooking! I ordered Ox Tails. But instead of rice I chose yams and collard greens.

Out of the 5 bones, only 3 of them had any meat on them whatsoever. You know it's bad when you get a box of bones instead of meat! The gravy was super salty, like cooked all night and which made it dark, dry salty gravy. You know what I'm talking about. Moving on...
The yams: also cooked all night and dried sauce. Waaaay too much cinnamon in my opinion. 
Lastly, the poor collard greens! They did have a Turkey neck bone with no meat.  Again, it seemed that they were cooked on low all night, so served without mixing the flavors from the bottom. I feel that if the cook were to taste the food before serving it first thing, they would liven up the flavors. One last thing, the cornbread was extra! What the what? 
It seems this restaurant is in it just for the money and not to be very impressive. It's a shame bc people who enjoy soul food would drive from all around to enjoy good home cooking. In my opinion, this place was not it. 
Iced tea was not too sweet. It was very good actually.

Incredible food, great service, and beautiful location! We were lucky enough to have Q as a server and had the best experience in a space thats clearly community centered. go here if you're looking for a delicious meal in a family oriented, relaxing environment.

Came here for brunch on Saturday around 1130am. We had a party of 6. It was super packed and busy inside. We waited about 40 minutes for our table - worth the wait! We ordered chicken and waffles (w/ dark meat) and shrimp and grits. Both dishes were delicious! The chicken was so juicy inside #drool. We also ordered sweet tea and an Arnold palmer - both drinks were veryyyy sweet. We had one child in our party and they provided crayons and a kids menu that she could color. That was awesome! Overall, great experience! We will be back!

The food was great, but Christen was such a pleasure to have as a waitress I would recommend you come in and ask for her to help you out! She made sure we were well taken care of and her personality was amazing ! Give her a raise

I wanted to update this review due to all the issues I have experienced with my orders. The past 5 orders all had missing sides or incorrect items. The first order, I ordered 2 biscuits and got 1 cornbread and no grape butter was provided even at my request for it.
