Wasn't at all what I expected. Very small portions. Flavors not the best I've had. I can make better grits. Really wanted to love this place.
Oxtails were delicious. Sides and cornbread were on point!! Only drawback was how pricey the sides were for the size.
Food was so amazing. Chicken very crispy and flavorful. Waffle . Everything was super good can't wait to come back!!
The food is really good the server is very attentive.  Me and bf love this place we will coming back.
Loved this place! Chicken was super juicy! Okra was so yummy! Can't go wrong with anything on the menu. Great drinks as well. Will be coming back & telling friends.

My hubby loves fried chicken but rarely has it. For his birthday we were doing dinner at our son & daugher-in-law's home. I surprised him with a family dinner from Fixins. Everything was delicious! Perfectly crispy and tender chicken, good mac & cheese (though the Rind still wins that category in my opinion). Other sides were good as well. Expect a bit of a wait to pick up your order, at least on a weekend, they were very busy (which is great, hopefully they'll survive the pandemic). Highly recommended.
Amazing food and awesome service. The shrimp and grits was fantastic, love the atmosphere

author_location: North San Jose, San Jose, CA
text: My boyfriend and I came here on our way home from Reno. There was a super long wait, but it was definitely worth it! Luckily there's a nursery down the street that we were able to pop into and kill some time. The food was amazing and definitely checked all of my soul food boxes. Toward the end of our meal our server was less than attentive, but it was busy and the food was so good that 5 stars is still totally deserved. Free refills on the Koolaid is a definite plus too!
A decent little spot, however the collard greens were tough (not boiled long enough) and full of large stems. The oxtails were tender but very salty.

My family and I visited this eatery for the first time today.  We were very pleased from the welcoming at the front door to the staff. The food was absolutely amazing.  I had the chicken and waffles I must say it was simply delicious.  The chicken was well seasoned and the waffle was cooked to perfection. My wife and daughter had the shrimp and grits which they were very pleased.  That red koolaid was just perfect . Kevin Johnson was very accommodating and greeted each customer with kindness. We look forward to our next to our next visit.

This is one of my favorite soul food restaurant. The food, receptionist, waiters and Kevin Johnson (former Mayor) who owns the restaurant....was GREAT I will be back with family and friends...and you will too!

3.5 stars ox tails is pretty good, gumbo and catfish nuggets are just so so, I didn't like the okras because it's extremely hard to chew and the collard greens is bland, dining room were clean and nice, service was just okay
The service was fast, great and the food was delicious, fresh off the stove, grill or oven! The ambience was warm and comfortable for all ages!

Had a wonderful time at Fixin's. 
Kevin Johnson came out and shook me and my wife's hand. 
FOOD was great. 
The best soul food in Sacramento CA. 
Me and wifey of 20 years had a great little date night. 
Kissing' & huggin' at the bar like newlyweds. 
We will be back soon, and the bartender, STEVEN, had the drinks comin strong
"Thanks bro"
We'll definitely recommend to family and friends.
We traveled from the Bay Area and I can't wait to go back everything was delicious except the Mac n cheese. The Sac town mango lemonade YES!!! So good  pssst.... it has Hennessy in it!

I usually get chicken n' waffles in Natomas but I thought I'd try this place based off the pictures and reviews. I'll just cut to the chase.....First off my chicken was so undercooked and pink that it was inedible, the Mac n cheese had almost a half inch of grease in the container and my sons waffle was so soggy you'd think they poured water on it, just nasty.

Soul food in Oak Park, delicious! I had the smothered chicken with greens and mac and cheese. The food was flavorful and the portion was good for the price. Biscuits are made from scratch. Modern decor with a bar and three televisions to watch sports. The food was not overly salty like most soul food places. I will be back with friends and family.

The wait time was about 20mins. We sat at the outdoor bar while we waited - the bartender was great! She explained the drinks' ingredients precisely! We had the oxtails, fried chicken (3pc), charred okra (yummy) and hot chicken sandwiches. The only issue was the the hot sauce - it was just hot and too much. No flavor to the hot   I love spicy flavorful food. Scotch Bonnet is my favorite, so I wasn't too amused with the hot sauce they used. Other than that, service was great and our other food choices as well.

Was here Sunday afternoon at 3pm with a friend and waited a little over an hour which was fine. We knew what we were getting ourselves into. We were sat down by a very friendly female host. After about maybe 10 15 mins we were finally greeted by our waitress who seemed to be having a bad day. She asked what can she start us off with and I told her we already know what we wanted so we'll just order everything right now. She replied with an attitude "yeah I know that's what I meant/ said".... Okay? Me and my friend kinda laughed it off and decided to notice but ignore it. After she got our order (we were sitting by the register and kitchen door) she wanted to make it known that she was clearly upset by slamming cabinets and kitchen doors. Basically just making a ruckus CONSTANTLY. Honestly I get it, it's busy and things happen, but you're working and there are customers EVERYWHERE. Such a bad look for the restaurant. Unacceptable. On top of that she would occasionally storm back and fourth flipping her colorful braids aggressively  with her hands like it's the thing to do. Probably not the best time to be doing that when you're working at a restaurant during a pandemic. 

Thankfully, and not surprisingly we got a new waitress who was very pleasant and professional. I didn't get her name but my friend and I appreciated her so much especially after dealing with the one before. 

The shrimp and grits were good but you definitely have to add hot sauce to make it better. My friend got the smothered pork chop with gravy on the side. The gravy was definitely the star of the show. She said if she ever comes back to get it again then she'll leave the gravy on it. I got the 3 piece chicken and honestly the crunch was perfect but seasoning wise, could've been more to it. I had to dip it in the gravy and add hot sauce constantly to get some kind of flavor. 

Not sure if I would come back to this place. Our first waitress really left a bad taste in our mouths and to get just an okay lunch at the end of the day  after waiting an hour to be seated kind of makes you not want to go back. That day was just not their day.

This was the first time going to Fixins. The service was very nice our waitress was very attentive to all of our needs. The macaroni and cheese was delicious  I didn't order it with my meal, but I did order some to go. The red koolaid reminded me of my dad's. I would definitely go back to try other entrees.

The waffle was really good as well as the chicken. The Mac and cheese was clunky and not good at all. The waitress was super rude and we had to keep asking her for ketchup/hot sauce multiple times and she would walk by and ignore us.

I heard about Fixins from different people who said this restaurant was off the chart delicious. Well, it was off the chart alright but in a bad way. The chicken was flavorless and my chicken breast had a brunt taste, the collard greens had a sweetness to them, my red beans and rice were dry and the biscuits were cool...  I was going to give up on ever going back but maybe the chef was having a really really bad day. I can say that I will not have the chicken again. The staff was amazing and the restaurant is clean and beautiful...
Such a great experience. The chef is incredible, food was to die for! Atmosphere was upbeat, friendly knowledgeable staff, great music, can't wait to come back.
Very good and excellent take out service during covid. $4 for kool aid is gentrification gone wild but other than that i think I'll be coming back for more.

Love the location in Oak Park...aesthetically pleasing inside & out, the young lady serving us was fantastic, food was delicious, and the kool-aid was the cherry on top. Place was packed so I knew that was a good sign.

The food was decent, but definitely not the best soul food I've had by a mile. Service was non existent. Was going to give it a second try for the food, but the service was that bad to where I can't step foot in there again. Good luck with the business though and hopefully you have some talks with your staff.
Great food, ambiance and drinks. The people who come here also make this place a great place.
The food and service is delicious! This is the best soul food outside of grandma's cooking! I cannot wait to go back!

I understand that with covid and take out and craziness is happening, but our hopes for this place were shattered. We have had good reviews from here, so we took it for a spin. We ordered online as the restaurant employee on the phone told us so. We did and about 15-20mins later we got a notification the order was ready. We live 4blocks away so we got there about 2mins. My husband waited in line whilst the outside girl who ran the front, struggled with no help from ANY other staff mate. The manager was seen inside, by many, just looking and wandering around lost, with no urgency to help the host out front. Short story long, we got home to tepid chicken tenders, soggy fries, and a hot chicken sandwich unable to resurrect from the dead..... the poor girl running ship have ZERO to do with ANY problems tonight....she KILLED it as much she could. Restaurant manager failed.

Finishing up a draft review from January 2020. Charming, hip place with good food and service. Five of us had dinner. The fried chicken looked amazing. I had shrimp and grits, which was delicious. Also a good salad. Deviled eggs were good. Loved the fried green tomatoes.

I've been in search for some good fried chicken for a long time. Of course, South and Iron Horse's fried chicken are amazing. But I needed a place a little bit closer to home and easier to get to...and park. I'm glad I found Fixins. Everything was good. More than just your average fried chicken, biscuits, coleslaw, mac and cheese, and chocolate chip cookies. All of which I had and were AMAZING. I immediately recommended this place to my friends who haven't tried it. 
I ordered for pick up and the hostess was really nice and the process was easy and organized. I had high expectations and they were fulfilled. Definitely coming back here again!

What a great find! Fixins Soul Kitchen was everything I hoped it would be and more. Our server, Cat(herine) was great, super friendly and attentive to our needs and requests. We opted for the 3 pieces of chicken and 2 waffles and also ordered a side of Mac and cheese. Holy smoke, there was enough food for an army! There was a huge breast and two similarly large thighs. Bonus, you get to choose your own meat (white, dark, mixed). The waffle was served with grape jelly butter (who knew there was such a thing) and it was good and packaged maple syrup. The waffle was big and on the thin side but delicious! The chicken had a wonderful, crisp, but not greasy, coating and the chicken was very moist. The Mac and cheese was good, too! I had to pace myself so I wouldn't explode. We also requested one of their Kool-aid pickles (I saw it was served on one of their sandwiches and had to try one), which I thought was delicious. Very crisp with a little sweet and salty going on. We also got a side of gravy, brown not sausage (which is not yet back in the menu) and it had a bit of a kick to it. A little salty for my taste but my eating companion loved it. If you get a chance, head over to Oak Park and get some good southern cooking! You won't regret it.

The Food and the Service was phenomenal.  This was my first visit but it will not be my last. I met and took a picture with Kevin Johnson who's the owner and he was totally approachable, friendly and down to earth. I'm going to tell all my of family and friends
Never have been here before until yesterday, food was great the service was great. I would definitely come back here and recommend to anyone looking for good tasting food!
The wait was longer than anticipated but it was very much worth the wait!  everything I ordered was phenomenal!
Soooo good. Staff. Food. Everything about this place is great. Expect a long wait, but it's worth it.

By far the best soul food is Sacramento, none other comes close!!!  First off the grease was not old so The chicken was good, fish was over fried & dried out, but flavor was there. Cornbread & huspuppys were amazing!! Greens & Mac was really good too!! Red beans and rice definitely skip not worth the calories... Kool aid was good but they only gave 3/4 way full for 5$

You've probably heard people all hype this place up, and it's all true. Everything I've had here has been awesome. I like the vibe here too (before and after Covid, they somehow manage to make it fairly relaxed and laid back to customers. It probably helps they play good music inside and outside.) You will need to invest a little more time here than the average restaurant because they put quality over speed, and it isn't cheap, but I think it pays off. I'll keep going back.

I am giving this restaurant 4 out of 5. The staff were friendly, and the place was generally clean and organized. My party and I waited for about 30 minutes to be seated inside the restaurant. Given the crowd and the day and time we ate (Saturday at 6:30), we expected a long wait. Patience is key!

Our server seemed overwhelmed, but she was very gracious, professional, and warm. She gave suggestions about food and drink options. Although inattentive at times (especially at the beginning), she made a good effort to serve us while managing other tables and tasks.

Why 4 stars and not 5? The food. 

In general, the food was delicious. (Ask about the appetizers, which are not listed on the menu. The devil eggs were really scrumptious!) I ordered the chicken-and-waffle meal. The chicken was hot and fresh and was appropriately seasoned. 

But the waffles were delivered cold. The server apologized and offered to bring me a second patch without my prompting. 

Unfortunately, the second patch was lukewarm. The server asked if the second patch was better, to which I said, "Yes, they are." They were better, admittedly, but they were on not as hot and fresh as I normally like my waffles.

The food was a little pricey but prices are up everywhere. 

I would return again.

I went to FIXINS for the first time, and while I was in line I asked other customers there what was the best food to order. So I ordered smothered pork chops, fried catfish with his puppies, (2) peach cobbler with  ice cream, the side were greens, Mac and cheese corn bread, and blue kool aid drink... after waiting for 1 hour for my order and almost every one was gone I finally got my food bag. I got home, and as I started putting food on my plate, I noticed the so called smothered pork chops was over cooked and dry, the catfish batter fell off as if it had nothing wet to hold on to,  OMG!! The Mac and cheese is still a mystery (Dry with no taste), corn bread dry, greens had no juice and was crunchy. IN THE END OF IT ALL I DEFINITELY WOULD NOT RECOMMEND THIS ESTABLISHMENT TO ANYONE!!

Take out order confusion led to a delayed lunch with a complely soggy waffle with no crispiness but great chicken.  I ordered at the counter and waited 30 mins at outside tables while other names were called.  Got frustrated waiting this long, and after I asked, they just told someone to go get my order from the back, as if if I had just arrived for pick up.  After a 4 min drive home, the waffle was in almost inedible soggy deflated condition and tasted like holiday spice.  The chicken tasted fantastic though considering it had been sitting.  I won't do takeout again from here but will eventually come back to dine in for the chicken.
Insanely good catfish. Seasoned so well. Everything else was bomb too, but Imma be thinking about the catfish forever
Poor service! Waiter very unfriendly! Waited 2hours for table when told 1 hour won't visit or recommend this restaurant.

Came here on a Wednesday. Not too crowded. Was greeted by a friendly woman who seated my mom , daughter and I. Food came out fast! I ordered the catfish, Mac n cheese and potato salad. Catfish was SO DELICIOUS! it was seasoned just right. The batter was good. It had a nice crunch to it and the fish was moist. Mac n cheese taste like my NANA's. So cheesy. I didn't like the potato salad that much but that's only because I don't like potato salad with a bunch of mustard. My daughter had the chicken &waffles. She loved it. My mom has the catfish , greens and yams. Loved it! I will definitely be back! Only gave 4 rating because of potato salad

I think I've found my new favorite place. I ordered the two piece chicken with mac and cheese and red beans and rice as my sides. No complaint here, the entire meal was satisfying and good service too!

Second visit here.  The first time was simply spectacular all the way around.   This time the food was on point again.  The greens are to die for, shrimp and grits are for real, catfish bites super delicious and clean tasting.  The fried green tomatoes were tangy and tasty in the right away.  The atmosphere is a bit loud but fun.  Service is good.  The first visit service was world class.  On my second visit I server was running an extremely large section.  She was efficient and super pleasant.  However, I did not receive either my pre dinner cocktail ( Bombay sapphire on the rocks) or the glass of wine I ordered when my cocktail never arrived.  Evidently the bartender was completely overwhelmed and just didn't make some drinks.   That was a pretty bad point of service for me.   My review would be 5 stars but the inability to put a basic beverage in front of me is very disappointing.  That being said the food and service otherwise well worth the price.
Today I went and tried the fried deviled eggs. They were yummy! And the coffee was tasty. I usually put sugar but it didn't need any! I will be back for the fried chicken!

I fasted for one day... so I knew when my fast was over I had to have fried chicken. Run, don't walk, to Fixins. A quick glance of the menu will make narrowing one dish nearly impossible. But if you had one choice, choose the chicken  and waffles .  Gud lawd, the heavens parted with one bite. Crispy and seasoned right, people who work at chicken joints buy their chicken here when they get off work! Not overly breaded or greasy. Two pieces lay on top of a crisp waffle. Oh, and it comes with Mrs. Butterworth syrup, and the only hot sauce worthy of good fried chicken, Crystal! My husband and I ate in silence, neither one of us wanting to break the bliss of the fried chicken joy.

While visiting some friends in California we decided to go to Fixins since we heard so much about the food.  Let me tell you....there was no LIE!!! The food was delicious.  I order and highly recommend the ox tails, greens, mac and cheese, and anything else on the menu.  The ambiance is great and the staff is amazing.  I am heading back to California literally in a week so best believe I am going back to this place a couple of times while I'm in the city. I'm excited just thinking about it. GO HERE!!!

I had the catfish meal and the fish was cooked perfectly - not over cooked. It was crisp on the outside and cooked softly on the inside, along with a side of red wand and rice and tenderly cooked collard greens. During this pandemic, it has been challenging to find good food and service. Everything was perfect!

This is a really great addition to Sacramento cuisine. Been wanting to try it and went tonight with two friends--got oxtails, chicken and fish and Fixins of collard greens, yams and beans and rice. All were delicious. Loved having Oak Park brews available and huge canning jars of beverages. The vibe was friendly--the service just the right mix of interactive and hands off. Loved seeing so many people there in appropriately distanced fashion. Definitely be back soon

Really enjoyed our dinner at Fixins. We were greeted and seated promptly. Loved the decor of the restaurant. Nice menu selections. The fried chicken was served hot and had good flavor. For our sides we tried the Mac and cheese which was cheesy and creamy, the potato salad was pickle relish forward, the grits and cheese had a nice texture and the biscuit and cornbread were also very good. The grape jelly butter was not overly sweet. We loved the banana pudding but the peach cobbler was super sweet with not enough peaches. Our server was very nice. Parking is mostly on the street although I was told there was a small parking lot. The only downside for us is the noise level, so loud it made conversations difficult. Probably nothing they can do about it; all about the acoustics. All in all, a nice night with good food at a fair price. We'll be back.
The smothered chicken is wow!  Biscuits are wow!  Grab some extra gravy and throw it on everything. The spicy chicken sandwich was pretty good too

Fixins is simply awesome!!!!  Award winning customer service and tasty food!!!  Thank U Esa and Brittini for providing such wonderful service to our party tonight.  You guys were super busy but yet you maintained your beautiful smiles and simply Extraordinary service!!!

The Food was FIRE!!!! Hats off to the Culinary Team.  Keep maintaining great quality in your food along with your great customer service and Fixins will be around for a good while!!  See you the next time we are in town!!!!

First time here was on a busy Day, the wait was not too bad especially since the food was served minutes after ordering. The Chicken was really good and so is the sweet tea. The Waffle was okay and the Deviled eggs were good. I also had collard greens which were a little too spicy for me. I will be retuning time and time again to try more menu items since I live in the neighborhood.  

UPDATE: went here 2 more times since the first visit and the 2nd visit was better minus the fact no one greeted me at the door and I felt rushed at the bar. Now my 3rd visit was HORRIBLE all due to the waitress MAIA J! Now, this visit was on 10/16/19, (I saved the receipt to remember her name for this review). Not only did she not ever check on me, but I was ready to go for a good 20 minutes before I finally got her attention. And in the moment I got her attention she was walking behind me and talking to me while walking away. Rude! I then told her I needed items to pack my food to go, (she started walking away) as I was talking. I told her I also wanted a drink to go and the check. She brought me an empty cup to pour my drink into. Last I was there I received a fresh drink to go not a half glass of what i was already drinking. The customer service here can be way better. The food is okay but not like I will crave it. I hope with time it gets better. But you can tell the staff is undertrained and careless.
I love their shrimp and grits.. I only wish when u order to go.. that they separate the gravy from the grits. Otherwise... this is the perfect Soul food kitchen.. Thanks Kevin Johnson

This place is so good! The chicken and waffles is what i always get because the waffle is amazing but the chicken is SO good too. The sweet tea was also amazing. Most of the staff is super friendly and sweet too. Definitely a new regular here.

Took me forever to try them. I've only had the Mac and cheese and greens... if the greens are good then I'm good... they were fire. Pretty sure I drank the juice. If you're drinking the greens juice, it's a go.

Service was awesome! Food was absolutely delicious. Its nice to finally find a REAL soul food restaurant in Sac! When I tell you the collard greens was fire! Mac and cheese is so creamy. Chicken so moist. I promise I'm not exaggerating, Lol. Definitely worth the price, which was exceptionally reasonable. I got a Martini and it was only 7 bucks. I pay $15 for the same margarita anywhere else. And the one from here was stronger. I'll definitely be back to try multiple things on the menu.

The chef stopped by our table for a chat and was very pleasant. I was very satisfied with the food here and the service! Melanin excellence.

This could have been a 5-star review except for one very important omission. It also could have been a 2-star review or even a 1-star because of that omission. I decided to split the difference. Maybe in a non COVID world I would have chosen 2 or 1 because I know everyone is trying hard. Here's why it's a 3...I ordered two chicken dinners for delivery. Ordered 4 different sauces. (I have proof in my order that I did.) I got ONE small sauce. Someone didn't pack THREE sauces, including the gravy that one person wanted and didn't want the other sauces. One small mistake can ruin an entire order. We ended up paying way too much for not getting the correct order because we really wanted sauces with our chicken as we ordered.

Starting off to say Service was Spot on I mean right on the money the vibe an staff attitude +100 with plenty of energy I love it  I ordered the Fried Chicken had Greens, Mac an cheese, French Fries an Red Beans an Rice also ordered some Catfish the menu looked to good to be true so I wanted to dab a little bit into everything 

I have to be honest I was really disappointed in the Fried Chicken it didn't come out as expected honestly was very dark an crispy an over greasy kinda tasted like it had been cooked sat under a heater an dunked in hot grease again to reheat I heard that the taste in Food control depends on what line of cook is back there cookin I think that day I got the short end of the stick with the fried chicken lol 

Greens an Mac An Cheese was tasty French Fries we're perfect impossible to mess up on fries so no complaints the Red Beans an Rice Were very disappointing no flavor what so ever just a whole bunch of steamed rice over some flavorless beans. 

Catfish was perfection at its finest seasoning an flavor was all there HIGHLY RECOMMENDED!!!

I love this place! Literally drove from the Bay Area just to come here and eat. It was my boyfriends first time and before we left he already planned a date to come back! Staff was extremely nice and even during happy hour they were attentive!
The food was decent but I expected a bit more soul taste and I didn't get it. The wait can be long to be seated but the customer service is pretty good.
Excellent fied chicken and catfish. And the sides are great too. Also, if you aren't a fan of eating chicken on the bone, the chicken strips are essential boneless versions of the fried chicken.

Really delicious! We got the family dinner including a bucket of fried chicken, biscuits and two sides. We got the Mac n cheese and coleslaw. Everything was beautifully prepared and delicious. We've been been enjoying fried chicken from several local restaurants and this rates among the best.
Delicious flavor, crispy skin and the chicken inside tasty and juicy. Mac n cheese was the best I've had locally and the coleslaw was also delicious with hints of peppers and I think kale. We'll be back for sure!
Pre-covid review: Best chicken and waffles I've ever had. Much better than Roscoes in SoCal and worth the visit. Desserts were on point as well.
I had the grits and shrimp loved loved it, I tried the mac and cheese awesome my husband had the smothered chicken and mac and cheese along with the greens the food was really good excellent

If you have a hankering for fried chicken, look no further. Their fried chicken is fantastic and the sides are yummy too, especially the Mac n Cheese. Nice outdoor seating area. Service is attentive and friendly.

Wow Wow Wow! What an amazing place.  Food was so incredibly good!  Felt that the food was very reasonable priced for the portions and quality.  The only thing I felt kind of cheated on was the fried devil eggs was $10 for 2 eggs, there should at least be 4 eggs. I see skip the appetizers and spend the money on those oxtails!  Tender tender gravy sauced meat.  yummy!  I also highly recommend the hot chicken sandwich, spice level 7 out of 10, but this is the kind of sandwich I would crave. Wish there were other choices than fries though for that sandwich.  The cornbread was also a mist get.  1 can be split between two people.  Wish we had room for dessert, but that just means when I'm back in Sac, I need to go back!
Excellent! Nice atmosphere and great food and drinks.  Service is friendly and efficient .

I'm so glad we stopped here.  We travel a lot and I refer to Yelp as my go to for everything and when I have an experience an extreme experience good or bad I write a review.  This was a very good experience.  We ordered a three piece (two drummies and a thigh) and a chicken sandwich.  The skin on the chicken was super light, crispy and not greasy.  Both my Wife and I thought it was on the safer side of seasoning but I couldn't have asked for anything crispier.  We also ordered a side of fries (don't know if they were frozen but they were meaty and crispy), a side of baked Mac n Cheese which was so delicious!  straight out of the south.. rich mouthfeel.. buttery cheesy eggy goodness.. a biscuit.. thick and soft and coleslaw which was a little out of balance for me.. too vinegary despite it being somewhat mayo based.  Lastly the peach cobbler.. oh my goodness.. if you have any history with peach cobbler just order it.. you wi t forget it.  I would for sure recommend this place and eat here again... and again.

HOLLY COW!!!! Yumm yumm yummy SO GoOd!!!!!! I had the best greens of my life!!! The shrimp and grits are so delicious and season so well the fried chicken was juicy and crispy the yams were perfection the mac & cheese delicious!!!!! I can't complain about nothing everything was so good the service was excellent!!! Definitely coming back and I'm coming all the way from Vallejo
I'm so glad this place is walking distance from my house! The patio area is so cute and the Koolaide pickles are so good haha!
Amazing everything! Best cornbread muffin in my entire life! Fried chicken, greens, Mac n cheese! Thank God it's far enough from where I live that I can't go every day!
Fixins Soul Kitchen in Sacramento is an excellent destination for soul food foodies who need their southern fare "fix"! This restaurant gets 5 stars from me for food, service and location!

Not great service. The people were really nice but the order was messed up twice and didn't get our drinks until after we got our dinner. A side of that the food was absolutely amazing and the patio area was super cool. I will definitely go back. Catfish was to die for However portions could be a little bit bigger
I have been told the food is delicious. I am actually going to try it out in January. The menu looks fantastic.

I don't like giving negative reviews but not much nice to say. We ordered 2pc chicken and waffle, ox tail with rice and black eyed peas and peach cobbler. Nice part: chicken was pretty good and waffle ok. Ox tail nothing spectacular. Black eyed peas were vinegary for me. Could be how they make it in the south but i never had thar much vinegar. My wife got the peach cobbler. Not very good and size of a small peach and it was 10 bucks. This place is so overpriced. 2 pieces of chicken 2 waffles, small bowl of rice and ox tail and not good peas with small peach cobbler. 56 DOLLARS. Will not he back.
The food here was awesome! I heard nothing but good things about their food but the taste was by far better than what I expected. I'm definitely coming back!

Been coming here since they opened and the food and service never disappoints. During COVID they've gone an extra mile to keep employees and customers safe. Also it's a bonus to be able to support a black owned business. Highly recommend. Oxtail is the best but haven't had anything I don't like yet.
I'll keep it short and sweet: the smothered oxtail should not be missed. We love fixins!

Save the plane fair for a trip to NOLA and stop in to a hidden gem in Sac!  For forever... I've been curious about oxtail but would never try it until this spot. Start with fried deviled eggs, but get a few orders because after your first bite they're difficult to share.  Their oxtail is "mic drop!" good - fall off the bone decadence and if you have someone to share their fried catfish with you're in heaven!  Their okra is good enough that a fussy kid would eat it (along with their purple Kool-Aid) but for those 21 and over ask for a "Beautiful" or an "Adult-Kool-Aid".  You can't go wrong here with any of their dishes but if you live more than 30 minutes away you better Uber because you'll be in a food coma once you're down eating!!

I went here for the first time 2 weeks ago with my mom and son. I had been hearing about this place and was finally in the area so we wanted to try. When we got there, it was 4 in the afternoon on a Saturday. The place was small but nice and clean. There was a bit of a wait. We were told it would be 20 minutes, but it ended up being about 45. We had looked at the menu while we were waiting for our table so by the time we sat down, we already knew what we wanted to eat. I ordered the oxtails and rice w/mac & cheese. We ordered an appetizer to eat while we wait for our food. We were surprised at how fast the food came. We ended up cancelling our appetizer. I have never received my food that fast from any soul food restaurant before. The food was hot and fresh. It didn't taste like it had been cooked hours ago and just kept hot all day. The oxtails were very good to me. They were very big. I wasn't crazy about the gravy on the rice. It had a sweet taste to it. The mac was just okay as well. I also ordered the cherry koolaid. It was way too sweet. I sent it back and got soda instead. Overall, we had a good experience. I would like to go again and try other things on the menu.

Myself and two lady friends met there for lunch the service was great the food was excellent I recommended I'm going to go back and try everything on the menu the drinks was pretty awesome not water down
This was our first visit to Fixins and it won't be our last, good variety of menu items great food and atmosphere. If you're a sports fan they also have multiple TV's for your viewing pleasure.
Went on a weekend evening, it was like a 45 minute wait. Food was really good, waiter checked in often to make sure everything was going well.

Amazing!!!!! Came here on my birthday the energy, the service, and the whole experience is worth it!! I ordered their chicken and waffles ,and mimosas is delicious! I will be back to try their menu out  worth it I promise

I did not have a good experience with the hostess. Without going into details, the hostess gave my table away even though she knew I was sitting at the outdoor side of the bar. I didn't hear the ding on my cell phone alerting me that my table was ready as a result of the music that was playing, and the conversation I was in. The worst part about it was she could care less and was more than happy to deliver the bad news that we could not sit at the alternate table we chose. We decided to sit where she would not be our server... we were instead served by "Cat". She was very nice and attentive. Unfortunately, the food was a disappointment for me. My dish came with two med/large oxtail. Just two for $25.00, and one of them was 80% fat. Sigh  I left hungry and buzzed. My boyfriend enjoyed his fried chicken meal, but he initially had to send his Mac and cheese back because it was room temperature. Anyway, I do make a mean Mac & cheese, oxtails, smothered pork chops, collard greens, sweet potato souffle, etc., and etc., so next time we want soul food it will be in the kitchen I go!‍
What a good family restaurant!  The food was fantastic, we didn't get to sit and eat but we ordered to take home.  So fresh and tasty.  Will definitely be back and I Highly recommend this place!!!

I went to Fixins just yesterday... initially I thought they would be closed because of the COVID-19 stuff but they were Open and ready to serve indoors and outdoors... I sat outside on the patio area and the wait was only about 10 minutes... The servers were very pleasant, helpful and attentive... The food was amazing and reasonably priced... I have the catfish mac & cheese red beans and rice with lemonade...bomb dot com!!!!! It was so good and fresh!! I will definitely be returning soon!
Absolutely delicious authentic SOUL FOOD!! You will not be disappointed. I truly enjoyed Sunday dinner!
The food and atmosphere are great! Having to wait 75 minutes on a late Sunday afternoon was a bummer, but worth the wait. Everyone I bring loves it!

Des Hollister was an excellent host... she was amazing. She took care of our order perfectly. Personable she was attentive, personable, and seem like she enjoyed her job. She gave us great service overall. Thanks Des! We hope you graduate with honors!
We had the chicken and waffles. The waffle was amazing and the chicken was ok. Food came in less than 5 minutes and the service was good. Very trendy place.

My first time there.  I took my wife.  First impression was from the outside I thought it was small, much more roomier inside.  Was greeted by warm friendly faces.  Our waitress (Jasmine) was professional and friendly.  She listened carefully and became aware and subsequently helpful with my wife's many food allergies.  The food was great but what I like about "Fixins" was the observable sense of home.  People were talking, eating, laughing, and fellowshipping like it was at home.
Lord allowing, I will be coming back to dine at Fixins.

Out standing!!!!! Talk about good food and great service. Fried chicken and collard greens and Mac an cheese BOMB..  Sat outside nice patio. Great place.. Everyone was nice. We will be back again so many things to choose from.

I'm giving Fixins 2 stars solely because I like the ambience and the Kool Aid. Our server Brittani was in a hurry, rude and seemed irritated the entire time. I ordered a glass of wine and it came after my dinner had already arrived. I had the hot chicken sandwich and I wouldn't order it again. It has way too much sauce and wasn't flavorful. My husband ordered fried chicken and it was just ok. The potato salad was good and the greens, red beans and rice and Mac n cheese we're also just ok. Nothing was great and coupled with the poor service unfortunately, I likely won't be back.
We got take out here during the pandemic, food was really good. Our baby really likes the mac and cheese. Very likely to go back
So bomb. I love chicken. Adult koolaid was spectacular. Other restaurants take notes.

What a great addition to our wonderful city! When we walked in the ladies at front Ja'Cassia and the other young lady were warm and inviting.   We were told that wait was 35 minutes or so and it was precise. Glad to see our former mayor Kevin Johnson with a warm friendly smile greeting every person tableside!

 Ordered the omelet with chicken and vegetables. Seasoned wonderfully nice fluffy eggs with a side of house potatoes. Delicious!  My son ordered the 3 pieces of chicken with waffles . He has a  hearty appetite (15 eating like he's 22) so he had to get a side of potatoes to go along with that . My niece and her husband  had been before  And they were equally as satisfied this round. They only lamented that during the opening week the tea was a lot sweeter the lemonade was more fresh and had a zing to it , and the portions were a bit larger.  I should have visited then because I'm a lean woman, with a grown man  APPETITE..  I was still a bit hungry when I walked out... heyyy bet on me in an eating competition..

 My only slight complaint would be our server got " lost " during our visit .  Did not see  our friendly honorary soul brother for nearly 15 minutes.. no refills for water had to flag down a different serve . Hence we had to wait to get a to go Box  and waited an additional 10 minutes  for our check.

 I totally recommend this spot. Will be back to try out the bar .. had picture worthy drinks.. great artwork.   Line wrapped around the entire front area with people clamoring to get in. SUPPORT THIS BUSINESS YOU WON'T BE SORRY!

Sooooooo good! 

Great menu! Fast service! Everything is on point! 

The deep fried deviled eggs, Mac n cheese, greens- all so good! 

We will be back!
It was just ok I didn't like the butter with strawberry jam mixed in so it turn me off, should always ask customers if they would like syrup, would I try them for something else yes I would

This place is racist! I thought I was tripping but nope, the 1st time,they said 2 hr wait ,but than told black people 15 mins , the time eating there ,we have to ask for water , ask for straws twice , that missed up my order and charged me for the item they got wrong , they messed up my sons order , both request that we specifically asked for both not done, and for a long time my spouse said this was happening but I didn't want to believe it but I started watching how they treat other people and it's crazy because there was another Asian couple there for about the same time as we were and they didn't even get their water neither and as soon as another couple sat down they got their water, I didn't wanna believe it but now I realize it is that way and I'm not coming back here and even try to tell the manager about it and he was just like oh well. I'm done, for long time I didn't want to believe it but too many time this happened.
Very good food. This one is definitely worth checking out in Sacramento being that there are NO good Soul Food spots worthy of spending your hard money at.
I got pickup. Exceptional food and service! I'm very happy with my orders of gumbo, 2 wings & a waffle, mac & cheese, black-eyed peas, and collard greens. I will be back!

The chicken is fine. 
The mac & cheese is fine. 
The beans and rice are fine. 
What isn't even fine was the $10 peach cobbler soup. It was liquid when in a plastic container. There were bits of pie crust dumped in it. I didn't want deconstructed peach cobbler soup. $10!

I'm in town on business looked around for a good restaurant to have dinner after a VERY long day of meetings and driving, Saw this listing on Yelp thought I'd give it a shot for more of a home cooked meal. That is the EXACT OPPOSITE of what I got. It was ABSOLUTELY the worst meal I've gotten from any restaurant in quite sometime I wonderd if I offended someone but I ordered online, even the soda was flat which considering COVID-19 should have been in a can being it was delivered; but that's a whole different issue. I ordered smootherd pork chops they must have found the oldest ones they had put about 2 really small spoon fulls of gravy and sent them out. I know that having a business is though but in this case this was straight neglect of pride for your product, the food rarely looks like the photo on the menu but in this case wow... Hopefully this was just a bad night... Blessings for you business and prayers it gets better.
Tasty food and excellent service. I look forward to visiting again. The prices were appropriate and portions were satisfying.
So. Incredibly. Good. I got the chicken and waffles and a grape Kool Aide and can't wait to go back again for more.
Nice atmosphere. Vibrant! Good music playing in the comfortable patio while we waited. And the food definitely entertained my palette! I was quite satisfied! Good job Mr. Johnson!

author_location: Presidio of San Francisco, San Francisco, CA
text:
Fabulous fried chicken
Delicious coleslaw and mac and cheese
You gotta get here
It's great! Great service and v friendly staff

I was in town on business and yelped nearby eateries. Fixins came up and this place was hopping... I had the chicken , collard greens , and Mac and cheese ... all were delicious as were the cocktails ...I realized I showed up during the opening but they were organized , festive and handled the crowd well. I sat at the bar which is first come first serve but the wait was well worth it!  I will be back .., soul food done right.  Flavorful and thoughtful , seasoned well and the biscuit was also delicious. Once you order you are served promptly and everything looked delicious ... stop in ..You won't regret it!

Haven't had A1 soul food like this one is a LOOOONG time. I've had some good soul food but this place right here is LEGIT! They had the best EVERYTHING! Their fried chicken was PERFECT! I can't wait to go back to Sac from the Bay just to eat here again!

If you're looking for a place with some top tier fried chicken then this place is one of the best.  With all their options they have you'll be coming back for more.  The chicken burger I had was absolutely delicious with it being cooked to perfection and even though there were juices flowing from the sandwich the chicken still managed to keep its crunch factor.  The fries were a standard set of fries which was nice.  There kool aid pickel that they offer was very interesting but not for me.  There was something kind of cool of seeing a purple pickle biting into it and tasting grape but like I said it's for some people just not for me.  Overall very enjoyable experience will definitely be coming back.
We had the fried catfish and shrimp n grits dinners, they were spot-on and delicious. Will definitely go back.

Fixins Soul Kitchen! The name says it all. The food is delicious, and combined with a "meal at home" atmosphere, you really feel like the food came straight from big mamas kitchen. Hands down the best grits I've ever had!!!! And the biscuits will leave you wanting more! The menu has something for everyone and the staff is friendly and inviting. This restaurant is something Sacramento has been needing. There is also a huge patio and private area. Excited to try many more menu items and see how the community embraces this newest edition! Come get your Fix! You will not be disappointed.

This was our first time, but we will most certainly be back. Our server Jasmine is a treasure. She was attentive, friendly and generally made our lunch a real pleasure. I had shrimp and grits, which was delicious! A little spicy so had a side of mac and cheese lol. My husband had the waffle and chicken wings. He lived his too, and had a side of grits to set it off. Great place, clean, delicious and you should give it a try!!

Yum yum yum is what I'm saying about this place. The food was great, the service is good. If you want to come for some good southern food, you've hit the right spot :). They Definitely paid attention to detail to make the food delicious.  I would always recommend coming here for dinner or brunch on the weekends
The cornbread is worth going out of your way to try. I'm sure it's loaded with butter and stuff, but I try to ignore those facts since it tastes so good

Super disappointed with my takeout order. Containers were only half full and almost room temperature. I've eaten inside twice and gotten takeout once and I've never been this dissatisfied. Food is good, but today was a very off day. I didn't have the energy to take it back. $20 for half filled containers for sides is not good. This place really is hit or miss...never consistent.

Table for 5 on a Sunday night. Loved the interior design and overall ambiance. Very comfortable feel with great lighting and perfect room temperature. nothing fancy but very clean for the seating area and bar. Bonus star for excellent customer service and loved the Gospel music playing! Waitress was very knowledgeable of menu and providing her favorites and offering other suggestions.  The food came out hot and in a timely manner. My favorite was the biscuit and the flavored butter/cream cheese mix!  The fried deviled eggs were different but surprisingly pretty tasty. The fried tomatoes were fresh but just okay, not much flavor in the batter which tasted the same as the hush puppies. Greens: too sweet.  Yams: great texture and flavor.  Mac n cheese: Hit and creamy and good. My daughter had shrimp n grits with a brown gravy which was unusual and different, not bad just different from what we are used too. My husband and other daughter had catfish and loved it. I ordered a Hurricane which was wayyyy to strong and not sweet at all, I added kool aid to give flavor but did not help.  Overall, nice place to meet with friends or family because of the atmosphere, the food was just okay for me.  I would like to see a beef option or even smothered pork chops on the menu! I will try breakfast next visit to see if something blows my mind.

Visited this restaurant on a Saturday so it was super busy. We were told it would be a 30 min wait so we put our name down. Closer to the 40 mins wait time I asked the hostess if we were going to be next soon and she said there were 3 people ahead of us. She was very cold and nonchalant. We finally got seated and we put our order in after about ten mins. We waited another 30 mins for our food to arrive. My mom has the audacity (sarcasm) to ask if our food was coming out soon and the waitress said "well it's gone take a while sweetie, but if you want to leave you can and I cancel your order".  Not sure why she felt the need to say all that after simply asking if our food would be ready soon. It was bizarre that there were three people in a group that got seated after we three did, and received their food way before we did. Those folks were literally almost done with their whole meal before we even got ours. 

On to the food: the catfish nuggets were good which were ordered as an appetizer. I ordered the chicken and waffles and the waffle was yummy and fresh. The chicken thighs had so much breading on them that they looked like chicken breasts. I had to peel off like 50% to finally get to the actual meat. The taste of the chicken was just ok. It had an odd sweeter taste to it. I guess I expect fried chicken to be on the more savory side. My mom was not impressed by the food at all but hey she's from the south and can cook better food than what we ate so her standards are high, plus she was turned off by the semi rude server who made the condescending comment. My husband enjoyed his food but he eats anything. 

I may try this place again but honestly not sure if it's worth the rudeness and long wait time.

Jasmine was the best server , customer service A+, food was a solid 10 outta 10, vibe was awesome.  Overall I recommend fixins 100 percent, I had oxtail, Mac and cheese, corn bread and peach cobbler and I tell you it was worth every penny I mean it.  Fixins never disappoints and I recommend you give it a try.
Excellent meals prepared. Great staff and ambiance.  The outdoor seating made for an awesome experience with views of the neighborhood.

Now I am all about supporting local businesses. The gumbo I ordered for my dad's birthday dinner was a major bust.  The gumbo was watery and there was barely any sausage in any our dishes.   I ordered my gumbo with shrimp and there was only 3 shrimp for an additional 7 dollars. The service was quick less than 10 minute, ambiance was nice.  Food is not impressive .
Great place, good food.. I will come back here anytime any day.. God customer service

Well, I hate to leave a bad review, but I feel that I must.  First let me say that this was not my first time here, actually my 4th time, and I have always enjoyed the food.  Some more than others, but overall enjoyed the food.  Not on this trip.  My family, husband and 2 kids, ate here last week.  My daughter and I had the Fried Chicken, my husband had the fried catfish, and my other daughter had the Gumbo.  Sides were Black Eyed Peas, Mac & Cheese, Greens and Grits.  First let me say that I have ALWAYS enjoyed the grits, whoever is making them is doing it right.  The Peas and Greens, not up to my standard, but I have HIGH standards when it comes to those two items, can't nobody make them better than my mom.  Sorry, just say'n.  
The only thing that I can say about the protein that we all had was that there was something not right with it.  Not sure if the oil was bad, or the chicken or fish, but lets just say that the minute we all got home, 3 of the 4 of us were in restroom.  
Now, I get it, stuff happens, I notify the establishment within moments of getting home, and I will say that I did get a response back saying that management was out and would get back to me. They did, saying that they were out of town, would have poor reception due the fact that they were in Tahoe, but I could call back, but that they would be reaching out to me because they did get my message and it was important to them.  Well, I did not call due to the message, stating they were out of vacation, so I presumed that someone who have reached out to me either Monday or Tuesday, but that has not happened.  There is nothing worse than poor customer service to me, and that is what I experienced.  I should not have to contact your establishment, you should be reaching out to me, but clearly the management team handles things differently there.  Now just so ya'll understand, I was not reaching out to them to get anything, just to let them know, but clearly I was the only one who gave a $hit!  No pun intended.  
With that being said, I will NOT be coming back to this establishment again, and Mr. Farmer, no need to reach out, for I will not be returning to Mr. Johnson establishment.

It was my first time coming to this place and I was excited to see what the hype was. 45 minute wait to get seated outside, then we order our food and wait another 30 minutes just to be told our food was not being prepared and it would be another 45 minutes. Never again.

The wait was worth it! Once we were sat, we ordered and had our food within 10-15 min. We had to get the gumbo (added shrimp), mac n cheese (had real cheese crispy and gooey), cat fish nuggets, red beans with rice and collard greens w/turkey. ALL items were sooosatisfying! The buzz of the diverse community was awesome! To see this Oak  Park gem thriving was awesome! The parking wasn't bad as the short walk allowed us to appreciate the other businesses that were around. The experience has us looking forward to returning!

WOWOW! Consummate amazing experience! In addition to the outstanding food (Fried Chicken, catfish, greens, Mac and cheese, etc.), the BEST service ever! From the hostess that greeted us on the phone and in person to DESIREE - the most outstanding server - hospitable, helpful, smiling and attentive.  We will definitely be back!! Thank you!!

Ordered online at 6:55
I got an email notification that our order will be ready 7:25.
Got here 7:20.
Waited in line for 10 mins and was told it will be another 30 mins.
We came back at 7:56 and was told it will be ANOTHER 40 mins.
We canceled the order.

Me and my husband came to Fixins later in the evening on Sunday night. We chose to sit at the Bar area and received great customer service from Kristen. She was very fast, tentative and pleasant. She made our drinks and took our orders timely. Everyone at the restaurant seemed to be on top of things. The food was excellent. We love the fried green tomatoes,
Catfish and sweet potato's. We absolutely love the sidecar drink as well. One of our favorite places to eat!

My daughter had lunch here last week and couldn't stop talking about it.  Fast forward, we had lunch at Fixins today. My son was also with us.   We were all very impressed and could tell a lot of thought and hard work went into making this an outstanding place to go.  

The staff were very nice, personable and the food was excellent.  I read through the Fixins website wanting to learn more about the history.  It was good to find out the mission is to give back to the Oak Park community by providing employment opportunities for local youths.  Very kind - We will be back.  

Thank you.  
John
This place had fast service and the food was great. The wait is always a little bit longer than stated but the quick serving of the food made up for it.

Best macaroni n cheese I have (ever) had better than my mommas and that's saying something everything we ordered was good. service was excellent the owner came over and personally greeted us. Overall good experience.

Always consistent!! Good food nice host and waiters. Professional. Enjoyed the gospel music this Sunday! You have a good thing going here . Nice tent WARM I was able to sit down and enjoy my food without the cold temps. Thank you for a wonderful experience
This place is 10/10. Service, food, aesthetic...all 10/10. I'm from the Bay Area and I will be recommending this BLACK OWNED restaurant to any and everyone. I STAN!
The food here is really good and so is the service. I recommend getting here early so you don't have to wait long but the wait is definitely worth it.

So ordered Togo today and very upset for the size of the sides that case with combo. We got two catfish combos for 18$ each and they had small containers for the sides that were barely half full. Very expensive for such a small portion. Also they charge 5$ for the same side

My first visit here. Went for lunch. The person I was with ordered the fish I ordered chicken and we split. The chicken was good just a tad bit too salty. The fish had this odd after taste which gave me indigestion causing me to burp that weird taste the remainder of the afternoon. The greens were tender but the Mac n cheese was really greasy, like made the bottom of my chicken soggy greasy....yuck! I put a napkin in my plate to sop up all the oil. the atmosphere is good but I wasn't overly impressed good wise. Not sure I'll go back.

I've been to Fixins pre-covid and was satisfied with the food and the general atmosphere of the restaurant. Through the pandemic I have to say they're still the same! The food and service was good, if you're in the mood for soul food the quality is usually a 10/10, flavor is a 8/10, and pricing is as per usual a bit overpriced on some options. The drink options are great and even though that kool aid was amazingggg, I don't think I'd pay $12 for it again. Overall an amazing little spot that gives you a taste of culture that's hard to find. I highly recommend picking up some food, and then walking into the bookshop next door.
Terrible food. Will never recommend nor will I ever go back! Service was subpar and I can do better at home with my own soul food.
Everything here is amazing. I usually get the catfish with Mac n cheese, and banana pudding of course. Best I've ever had

Overall, the food and the drinks were excellent. We ordered the chicken plate, chicken tenders, gumbo, and koolaid based drinks. Everything was good but there were a few issues (w/ the seasonings). The gumbo was a bit salty but had a wonderfully excited rout and flavor profile. The fries that came with the tenders used some sort of sweet/salty seasoning that was reminiscent of Wing Stop. The tenders are to die for and HUGE! The portions to price ratio is spot on perfect. We didn't leave with togo boxes but no one left hungry. I highly recommend adding Fixins to your personal rotation of restaurants.

The food was pretty good. Party of 6 and we had the oxtails, chicken, shrimp, burger, & various sides. Everyone enjoyed their main dish and adult beverages. The server was slow but was very nice, well mannered. None of us were fans of the peach cobbler--doesn't have the flaky dough layer & came with a very small scoop of ice cream. Overall, the restaurant is unique and I would go again.

Just finish eating our takeout food and OH MA GAH! I'm big on collard greens and the way they make it was PERFECT! Not overly salted like I've had in places in the bay area. The oxtails were so delicious. My 5 year old almost finished the shrimp and grits! The peach cobbler was  Man talk about killing me softly food! The drive from Rocklin was so worth it! I wonder if they cater.

Love this place. Been here a few times already and have tried something different each time, and have never left disappointed. The food is one thing, but Kool Aid? Ugh... my heart. Plus, their customer service is fantastic.
Babbbyyyyyy !! This food is amazing ! Great service , long wait time but it is worth it !

They got ah good lil breakfast an I needed a pick me up one of the waitresses made me a hazelnut ice coffee,an when I tell you that helped my day,went the next day they didn't know so I stayed until I say the lady,she is the best I find out her name
Had the oxtails, first time I've had them in a sauce and was not disappointed.  The adult koolaid was refreshing and my daughter also enjoyed her meal.

Wooooooo if you're looking for the full bang on soul food. Comeeeeee hereeeeee for sureeee. Food tastes just like your auntie would make
It. 
This is soooo yummy honestly  but don't eat outside because there are too many flys.
Great food and a good atmosphere. Get one of their signature drinks or dishes and you'll love it. Top notch customer service as well.

Food and customer service is great!! Though very  packed all the time, it's a given because their food is absolutely delicious and not too expensive! Even if you're waiting for a table the staff is always checking in on you!! And their playlist!!!! Overall just great!!!

This is the best Soul Food I've had in Sac and among the best every anywhere. And I would know. I drove to Graceland and once we hit East Texas we did Soul Food or BBQ every night. The collards have just the right spicy kick and the mac n cheese are the perfect texture. Of the fried chicken, my Mom said "Boy, do they know how to batter a bird." And she would know. She's from East Texas. Their attention to detail in the time of to-go and Covid in en pointe. Literally almost a dozen sauces and sides, all which have to be kept up with. We will be back. Can't wait ti try the desserts! Thank you!

Chris Weber once said there is no soul food restaurant in Sacramento. Well maybe if comes back to town he find some of the soul food in town at Fixin Soul Kitchen! The food was great, and the southern style menu reminds you of the food your momma use to make for dinner after Sunday church service.  The customer service was excellent and former Sacramento Mayor Kevin Johnson is always around to greet guess and help with what he can. All I can say is the place is the BOMB, when it comes to really good soul food. They also have a really pretty outside padio, and a smaller dinning room that holds about 25 folks.

This place was so good! I found this restaurant by accident. I am so happy that we stopped off to try it. By far the best soul food I have had in a long time. You won't be disappointed. Try it!!! Also, super clean and friendly staff. Thank you

Great food, wonderful staff and the to go service was smooth! Would definitely recommend and we came all the from Fairfield. We will be going again very soon! Everyone was following Covid19 procedures.

Simple the food was horrible! My friend got smothered chicken NASTY! I got wings and waffles NASTY! Fried Devil eggs NASTY!  Kool Aid and Mimosa was good though. Not good enough to return. The chicken was disgusting, seasoned poorly and tasted burnt. I'm truly disappointed and waste of $50 bucks

Wish I could be writing a review based on the food, but this is solely on the customer service. I entered their restaurant today ready to eat and now am a very unhappy customer and most likely wont be coming here to even try anything at all. First of all if you're going to be racist about an interracial relationship don't make it so noticeable and screen your greater before you have that kind of person the face of your company.
I love the food, the best soul food I've eaten since I been in Sacramento the place nice and clean the service awesome

Fixing is pretty tasty service was great for takeout but fried Chicken was bland and sides were also blend for this to be a soul food spot so I'll keep up with mind next time I go maybe I'll get breakfast
This is the best place to eat! Everything is tasty here. The servers and bartender are the BEST!! That include the hostess.
Food and service was really great. A lot of choice's on the menu and you can't beat adult koolaid...

I was really surprised to find soul food this good in Sacramento. It was delicious and exceeded my expectations. I look forward to having some more of that delicious fried chicken the next time I visit.

Greeted with a genuine smile = yes. Service was genuinely friendly and quick = yes. Food was yummy = yes. Loved the fried chicken. Location = close to work and home. Parking = difficult during lunch hour. Owner (Kevin Johnson) thanked us for our patronage = priceless. Price = reasonable. Return = Absolutely
Food was so delicious. Good vibe. Will be returning to support this Black  business. Will go back again. Also heard great reviews from the Los Angeles
7/10 taste wise. We got chicken with coleslaw, mac and cheese, and biscuits. Think KFC but slightly better. Honestly the best best part of the meal was the butter for the biscuits.

First time here and we are definitely coming back! Everything we ordered was good! We started out with deep fried deviled eggs then I got fried chicken with Mac n cheese, greens, candied yams. The fried chicken was the moistest fried chicken I've ever had. The batter was so crispy and to have that perfect combo of flavorful chicken inside paired with the perfect crisp outside was fried chicken perfection! The Mac n cheese is homemade from scratch. No velveeta there or can  cheese! Yum! The greens were so good and are served in their very own mini cast iron pan to prevent the rest of your food from getting soaked. Great touch! The vibe was very laid back and I felt welcomed! Service was great even though it was super busy! We are coming back very soon! Bomb dot com!
It was good food fresh got we had the fish, I'm glad we tried it!, Goodluck to Kevin, glad he has this in oak park,

My wife and I love Fixins. Everytime we have been there the service and the food were exceptional. However this last time we eat at Fixins our server was very rude to me. They would speak to my wife and they were more proffsional. However when ever I said anything the server had a very negative attitude and she was very rude with me. It got to the point where I just stopped talking to the server and we got boxes for our food and left. My wife and I have decided to never return to Fixins due to this experince. It is unfortunate because Fixins was one of the places we really enjoyed eating at.

Good soul food and price. This restaurant is own by no one other then Kevin Johnson of the Phoenix Suns. Mr. Johnson has deep roots in Sacramento. Weekends are normally packed from opening to afternoon.
Y'all complain about the wait, but it proves how good their food is, period! The best soul food yet just a lil pricey but well worth it
The oxtails are good.  The kool aid is amazing  love seeing the employment opportunities the owner is bringing to the area. Keep up the good work
So good! I loved the food here. I did get some chicken that hadnt been well plucked but I don't believe they do that in house. Anyways everything other than that was super tasty!
So fye!!! Undefeated better than roscoes. Everything was delicious and I'll be making sure I stop by every time I'm in the area!!!
Great food and drinks, excellent service, and over all fun atmosphere. Went on a Saturday they had a DJ playing a variety of music that both the kids and I enjoyed.
Best soul food I've had in a very long time!! You must try them! I eat there every time I drive through Sac! Thanks KJ!!

I love coming here just ordered my food to go and the girl who checked me out was so sweet had great customer service  I think her name is Aaliyah I always recommend this place to my fiends best soul food hands down

First time i ate the ox tail was ordered to go. Was exited cause i love ox tails but it was bitter, and salty as hell... and just a weird all around taste plus they forgot my greens on the side. 2 years later its the wifes anny so we decided to go there and dine in giving it a 2nd chance. Got my ox tail plate again and i swear it was so salty i couldn't eat my plate anymore except for the greens. For the cook thats making the ox tails please give it up! Never again.

Bomb AF. Fried chicken, Mac & cheese, corn bread, biscuit & the different flavored butters are all so good. Haven't had the chance to try to fried green tomatoes yet but am looking forward to them being back in season. They serve Uncle Nearest whiskey & it's delicious. Been there 2-3 times & it's always packed. Wait time for food is a little long but worth the wait.

My experience here was horrible. 
I was seated question quickly however it took over 15 minutes for a server to come to the table and say hello or even ask about drinks. Mind you there where only 4 other parties upon my son and I being seated. There was not a shortage of staff but a shortage of training. The food once I was able to order to forever! Then I hear a server telling someone the chef is on a smoke break gross! 
The food comes out while I'm taking my son to the restroom and just sitting in the table when I get back. No one ever refilled our water. The food was nasty underwhelming. Unseasoned chicken and the burger my son said was nasty and spit out. 
I asked for the check and had to wait for at least 15 minutes for them to bring the check over. Needless to say I don't see what the hype is about. I would pass on this restaurant! Food is nasty and the service is bad. You gotta have at least one right.

We tried this place on a whim. We are from Texas, which has great food.   I come to Sacramento several times a year and have yet to find good tasting food.  We were both pleasantly surprised by how great the food tasted.  It was definitely soul food.  Loved it. Will definitely come back again.  We ate the fried chicken, Mac n cheese, grits, turnip greens, biscuit with butter, and catfish nuggets with homemade tarter sauce.  AWESOME!!!   The customer service was really good too.
I love the Kool aid to the oxtails and don't let me tell you bout they smothered chicken  I definitely will be eating here again

Had breakfast at fixins today. Our server was great. But it took long for our food to come. The grits were extremely thick. Not hot at all. Bacon was not crispy at all. Pancakes were very thin. Good but thin. Don't think I'll be coming back.
Best soul food I've had in a long time. Every time I'm in Sacramento I go here. So worth every penny!!

First I must say the wait is long we waited for an 1 hour and 30 mins but it is worth the wait.  I had black eyed peas that were so good.  Fried chicken which did not disappoint it was juicy and well seasoned.  The collard greens were divine and you must eat them with the cornbread.  The last of my meal was the Mac n cheese that just blessed my soul.  Creamy just the right amount of cheese, well season and far from being dry even after warming it up later.  I hope you enjoy as much as I did.

This is at most (right now) a 3 star restaurant. I totally don't get how this place has 5 stars?!?!  No way in the world!  It has to be the novelty of a new and black owned soul food resturant in the recently gentrified area of Oak Park. That's it. The wait was three hours. Three hours for just average food. Nothing that we ordered (our order included the 2 piece dark meat fried chicken with greens and yams, oxtails with Mac and cheese, chicken strips with Mac and cheese and French fries, fired chicken omelet with cheese grits and a side of biscuits, chicken and waffles, fried deviled eggs, and artichoke dip which was served to us with ritz crackers instead of bread like advertised on their menu-we sent that back) justified the wait. NOTHING. We were not wowed!  It's a soul food restaurant with under seasoned chicken you guys. Come on!! Everything we ordered was just fine. Not bad. Just ok. But totally not the best we've ever had. Not 5 stars. Folks are on here gassing this place for no good reason at all. I promise. Check it out if you have 3 to 4 hours of your life to waste on just average, (3 stars at the most) food. 
I'll give this place another shot IN A YEAR when the thrill is a new restaurant in sac dies downand maybe they (the owners) take some constructive criticism to their cooks. ‍
4 stars because the ox tails were really good ! And it's hard to make oxtails . Mac n cheese was not that great. Bf liked the fried chicken. Probably will only be back for the oxtails !
We had a 45 min wait, the food was ok I got the smothered pork chops they were tough af but the flavor was definitely there. I also had yams and collard greens which was delicious  Drinks was good

My friends and I stopped here for breakfast on our way home from Tahoe. It was delicious!!! We had the chicken and waffles, fried deviled eggs, and the hot chicken sandwich! The sides we ordered were the Mac and cheese and red beans and rice. All choices were so good! We also tried one of each kind of Kool aid, which is on tap btw! Service was also outstanding! Definitely will be back when we're in the area!

Great food! 

Black eyed peas were just too spicy, kool-aid is a bit overpriced (I can make a whole jug at home for a quarter of the price they charge). 

PRO: They did exchange my black eyed peas for something else and the music they play brings great vibes.

CON: Either they are short staffed or just extremely busy because I had to ask 5x for a to go box, 3x for my tab and nobody ever offered to refill our overpriced kool-aid.

I do highly recommend the mac and cheese.

I had the Blue Plate Breakfast with a hot link and grits. My eggs were perfectly over easy, hot link was on point, and the grits were a sweet corn flavor and buttery -- great fluffiness to density texture. 

Other orders at the table included shrimp and grits, fried chicken sandwich (very spicy), smothered fried chicken (I"m going to try this next time). The potato salad was a little sweet, some people like this, I guess. I prefer a more turmeric and smoked paprika spiced potato salad, but hey, that's just me. The green beans were very good.

And we got to pal around with KJ! Daytime vibe was great for the whole intergenerational family.  Good music blasting loud.  Kind of a funk and rhythm and blue schema going. 

Definitely coming back. I want to try everything on the menu.

I always get coffee pretty much everywhere I go, which was good. Next time, though I will get the Kool-Aid.

I hope that it was just my mistake in ordering the hot spicy chicken sandwich.  It was a piece of fried chicken that was drenched in a spicy hot buffalo wings hot sauce.  It was overpowering and I couldn't even eat half of it.  Since it was Sunday morning, the others were having regular breakfast items and the Chicken and Waffles seemed to be the best choice.  The chicken strips were filets of breast meat.  They were dry and tasteless.  Service was excellent but the food was very average.

I visited this place today with my family for an early Mother's Day brunch. The place is really cute and they have a patio you can sit at. The bar is nice and not crowded. Our food was delicious and we got the hot chicken sandwhich, chicken and waffles and side of the potato salad. Service was disappointing. We waited 30 mins before someone came to bring us just water. Another 10 mins passed before she came back to get our orders. Fortunately, food came out quickly. We each ordered drinks, and only one of us got our drink. We asked the waitress three times and we saw her walking and servicing everyone around us except us. When she did bring out our drinks, she forgot my husbands coffee but by this time it was our fourth time asking and so we didn't ask for it. Other than two times, our waitress didn't come. Again, we felt like it was on purpose because everyone around us was getting their food and drinks and asked if they needed anything. Regardless, we left a really good tip because of Mother's Day. We most likely WON'T be back.

This was our first time here. Many people recommended this restaurant and we finally came to try. It did not disappoint!! Every thing from the service to the food was amazing!! We had the fried deviled eggs, fried chicken and my husband had the shrimp and grits. We will definitely be back many more times!!

If you have a big party of 7 or more dont bother coming here. We came for my sisters bday and they split us up to 2 different tables. Didn't even feel like we were there together and charged 20% . Kevin Johnson was there and didn't bother coming to our table to greet us. Why because we were a different race? It took 40 mins to get our food. Terrible experience and wont be going back. They told us 2 hr wait and scared everyone away with that. We had originally left and after checking the wait time it went fast so we went back. Alot people left.
Back again for the second time this week. I was very impressed. Not sure what I will try next.  Maybe some oxtails or smothered chicken.

Was disappointed on the food quality. 1. We order gumbo and chicken sandwich. The gumbo came out lukewarm and the portion is very small. For the chicken sandwich we requested no pickle and ask the server if there were pickles in the sandwich and he stated it is on the side. Sandwich came out and the pickle was in the sandwich, so we requested a new sandwich, the server brought the same sandwich out with the pickle on the side. We ask is this the same sandwich and he said yes. At least he was honest and told the kitchen to make a new sandwich. 30 minutes later sandwich never came out and they told us they didn't make it and will give us a gift card. Upset that we wasted time waiting.

First time trying this place and I had to give it 3 stars. Smothered chicken NOT smothered chicken. Yams unique not sweet. Seemed more prepared from a can. The greens were good and right amount of texture. I definitely wont be back to this place.

Really great quality soul food! We ordered a variety off of the menu so we can get a little taste of everything:
chicken & waffles: chicken nicely battered and fried perfectly and came out HOT. Waffle was good but not impressive. Could have been crispier.
Ox tail & greens: can I just say YUM! In my opinion the best dish we ordered!
Biscuits & gravy: love that it's chicken gravy it was pretty herby which we liked but may want to keep that in mind if you're ordering it
Side of:
Mac: not really my fav Mac but it was good
candid yams: not overly cooked lots of cinnamon!

I live in SF and most of the authentic soul food restaurants have shut down... this place reminds me of Farmer Browns or Gussies.

Service: Excellent!
Waiting time: 20 min around 7pm.
Food: My smothered chicken was delicious. Mac N cheese and collard greens were good.
Bathroom: Clean.
Parking: We parked around the corner. It is a pay parking from 7am to 6pm.
