Food is amazing and all the servers were super friendly and helpful. They have spacious patio area as well.
I had the catfish nuggets + chicken and waffles with sweet tea. The service was good and the food was very good. I will definitely be back.
I love the food at Fixins. It's delicious. I do think the side portions are small. Like can I get more than a spoon full of greens? IJS.

Came here last weekend because we wanted to see the new restaurant that was opened by NBA fame and past Sacramento Mayor Kevin Johnson. We did not see Kevin there....so sad. 

Well, we came there during lunch and the hostess said it will be like 30 min wait. Have you guys noticed, when they give you a time frame of the wait, they are merely guessing? Like how do they determine how long you will really wait? Before you start running off because your patience is short, maybe just stick around...you'll possibly be surprised you were lied to and the wait will be like less than half that.  We waited like 10 mins, no biggie. Got a table, sat down and ordered our food and drink at same time as we already knew what we wanted to order - the fried chicken and the oxtail. Kid you not, we got our drinks fairly quick but literally in a matter of a few minutes (meaning - no more than 2-4 mins), we both got our food. That was the fastest we've gotten our food like ever from a restaurant such as this. Maybe they pre-made it or what (probably), but the food tasted fresh and very hot still. Either way, speed was top notch there! 

The food was great! The fried chicken was cooked all the way through, it was juicy, crispy. Great flavor on batter. Biscuit was good, corn medley thing was good too. The oxtail was soft, but not too soft. It was seasoned really well. The mac and cheese was great. No disappointments in any of the food that we ate. We'll be coming back...next time we'll try shrimp n grits and possibly the chicken and waffles. Yumm!!!
Disappointed. Unseasoned food, horrible service and over all just a terrible dining experience. I will not be returning. Growing up  with southern cooking this was far from "soul food".

I went here on my birthday and it was everything I expected. Good home cooking, large portions, great drinks and excellent service. I even got a chance to personally meet owner and previous NBA player and mayor of Sacramento, Kevin Johnson. I definitely loved the ambiance, the wall decor and being in the presence of people that look like me. It was a beautiful sight to see, an enjoyable time and I will definitely be back.

I drove all the way from the bay area just to try this place as everyone was saying good things about it. I am very very disappointing for them to be displaying so food is a slap in the face to the African-American community. Their oxtails were disgusting they tasted like brown sugar with gravy and they were undercooked the fried green tomatoes were like giving me slices of cheese gooey is hell the prawns were not cooked the greens tasted like shredded paper they gave you Kool-Aid drinks and cups that had holes in it the macaroni and cheese was hard my four-year-old granddaughter even spit it out and that's her favorite meal in the world . I will never return here you guys need new cooks in someone who can be constant with the same taste one thing I do hear is people come here one day a taste good the next day Idot to be in business you need to keep up with the flavors and keep it going far as I'm concerned I will never return this place is disgusted
The first restaurant in Sac I've been too that the food has flavor in it. Sac's food seen sucks but if more restaurants like this open up, then their might be some hope for it. I'll be back
From the south and this food was great! Our server Des was awesome tonight! Definitely recommend!

Let's start by saying how long the wait was even at 11:20am. Our quoted wait time was 45 mins but when it was almost 1 hour long and we haven't been called yet, I went in to ask the hostess how much longer, she stated we cancelled our name off of waitlist through text but the odd thing was, we didn't even get a text to begin with so not sure how that happened. I was a bit disappointed because it looks like we could've been seated at least 15-20 mins prior, but even with the screw up, the hostess was very nice and seated us within 7 mins after the confusion. Now let's begin with the food, I got the smothered chicken with creamed corn and Mac and cheese. Smothered chicken was way too salty, I ended up throwing it away cause I felt like I was eating a teaspoon of table salt per bite, Mac and cheese was pretty good but was way too greasy for me, and finally creamed corn was ehh. My boyfriend however got the fried chicken plate with the greens and charred okra. He stated the fried chicken was crispy on the outside but juicy on the inside which was true. His sides were ehh as well, nothing special. Honestly I think everyone overhyped this place because I've eaten at better soul food places. I wouldn't wait in the long hour wait just to come back. If there was no wait then I would consider it. Overall service was great.

I attended the soft opening for Fixin's and the food was delicious.  The atmosphere was wonderful and the staff was very friendly.  I will definitely be back.  I pray that the restaurant gets the community support so it will remain open.  Price points were great.

I'm in love with this restaurant, the staff are all super sweet and the food is amazing! My favorite dish to order is the fried catfish. It's always super tender and excellently seasoned. I often get the collard greens and I also really love the candies yams. I really enjoy their sweet tea as well! I'd highly recommend it on a hot day. Overall Fixins is a amazing edition to the Sacramento food scene and is an amazing local business to support!
The waitress, Des, was the sweetest ever! The food was incredible and very short waiting period. All around amazing experience.

This place was amazing! I love it here and brought my friends and they also loved it. The service is really great and they take care of their guests. They are working extra hard to take precautions during Covid as well. The food was hot, fresh and so delicious!

I love this restaurant and the food, but last weekend was a miss. The food was so salty we had to throw it out. The red beans, catfish, and greens were inedible due to the level of salt in them. At least the chicken was delicious so we had something to eat. We're hoping it was a bad batch and that the next time will be better!
Food was amazing! Service from start to finish was excellent. Drew took great care of us.

Went once for brunch pre-pandemic and have picked up to go three times since.  Love the fried chicken and great sides.  Unfortunately what you get is not consistent.  I got the three piece light-dark combo each time. First time nice breast piece, drum stick and thigh.  Perfect!  Second time lower (small half) of the breast, drum stick and small wing.  Not the same experience.  Hoping that was just a fluke, but tonight I got small drum stick, wing and back.  I am sure they mistook the back for a breast, but what a let down.  Not what I paid for. Now not sure if I want to try again.

Got my chicken fix @ Fixins! It's a new restaurant (~6 mo) and it shows. The wait is long and there are open tables all over. The food was slow to come out and the lettuce and top bun of the hot chicken sandwich was thoroughly cooked by the heat lamp. The bun was inedible and the lettuce got an unappetizing wilted brown rim. The chicken is WORTH IT! Can't wait for the owner(s) and staff to troubleshoot and get the flow going here. Their product is good but the restaurant itself has potential.
Absolutely delicious. It is amazing every time I go. Recently ordered breakfast to go and it was ready when I arrived. Great staff and service.

This place was great. It was super busy at the time me and my boyfriend sat down to eat but our server still did her best to cater to us which I appreciated. The food here is amazing. The chicken waffles with the biscuits and gravy was THE BEST I have ever had. The biscuits with gravy was straight FIRE! I highly suggest it! Thank you for making our first experience here a great one!

mannnnnnnnnnnn lets me tellllllllllll you about this place here its a bit of heaven in sacramento me and my family absolutely loved the cooking.  mr. ol sokol was an amazing food advisor.  if you want a adult drink made let him do it.   also they have koool aid yooo im overly excited.
Food was absolutely delish. Real soul food everything from scratch. Going back again now after having it last night.

The staff is very friendly. Kevin Johnson was in attendance providing us with great hospitality.  The former Major, seems to be very proud of his new restuarant.  I enjoyed the Catfish and potato salad. In addition the mac and cheese is very tasty. My experience over all I enjoyed the food.

I don't know here to even start with this- between the food, drinks, atmosphere. The game was also on. The wait was insane for a Sunday brunch about an hour. We just walked around Sac until our name was called. The online check wasn't working at the time - but the food and customer service made up for it!! Will definitely be back

Great food and amazing service. The staff is friendly and food is delicious. The food is priced fairly as well. They do a good job of turning tables over quickly as well. It can be loud inside, but that is a minor inconvenience for everything else you get.

On 8.28.20, my husband and I called ahead and were told that they weren't taking reservations but we would be able to walk in for patio service till 8 pm. We arrived on line at 6:28 pm. At 6:29 pm we were told by one of their workers that they would not be able to seat us because they are taking last call for patio service at 6:30, so the couple in front of us were the last to be seated. Even though it was now 6:30 when he was speaking to us and we arrived before that he refused to seat us. We explained to him that we called 3 hours earlier and were told by another employee if we walked in at this time we would be able to be seated, he still refused to seat us. We explained to him that after getting that information we drove 30 minutes from Folsom and he still refused to seat us. When asked why he said because they were closing early and celebrating and anniversary. I've eaten here before and the food was good but not good enough to wash out the disgusted taste in my mouth left by poor customer service.
We came here last week and picked up a to go order. The place was packed and for a good reason. The food is amazing and it's the best fried chicken we have ever had

So live in LA but came to Sacramento for the day to visit a friend who was had a medical emergency earlier in the week.  I took an early flight and by the time I landed was STARVING.  I looked on YELP saw the Fixins Soul Kitchen menu and I really didn't want to go anywhere else.  I could see there was a wait so I added us to the waitlist and made the drive. We were greeted by manager Brittany and Victoria.  Mia was our server.  We got the Blue Plate Breakfast and it was delicious.  We ended up ordering fried chicken to go (to have for dinner) it was a lovely experience. I will return next time I'm in town.

Staying home doesn't stop me from eating good foods! I am craving for fried chicken,  and I found fixins Soul kitchen from DoorDash! I order the 8 pieces chicken basket and a side order of greens. I have to say that I love the chickens! So favorable and juicy. definitely will visit/order again!!
My smothered chicken was cold and the food came out in 10 minutes. I was really disappointed because I couldn't wait to try this place. Maybe we will try again next time.

author_location: Travis Air Force Base, Fairfield, CA
text: Food is absolutely amazing and it reminds me of east coast home!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!! Definitely will be make a drive back here for the food!!!!!!!!

I come to this establishment often, today was my first day eating here during the covid. The wait wasnt bad at all but my server wasnt to helpful...and i ordered buttermilk biscuits...my food came out but my biscuits didnt, i asked a server walking by and they said it would be 60 seconds or less..i waited and started eating my food after i was done with my meal my biscuits still werent served i asked the table boy if he could find my server i needed my biscuits and a to go box my server took her happy time and flies were starting to swarm....by this time im irritated and over my meal, i wanted to speak to a manager but my child asked me to let it go so i decided to not cause a scene. My server brought my bill and forgot to take the biscuits i wasnt served off so i asked her to do so. She seemed not concerned with the fact that i never received  my whole meal. Im very dissatisfied with the service today and i believe i have experienced an unpleasant visit with that female older african american server with braids before. So hopefully a manager will see this and speak with her.

First time I went to this place it was really good. So good in fact, that I knew I was going to bring my wife. I brought her and I was extremely disappointed. It is obvious and profoundly obvious that the food isCooked by different chefs who carry different philosophies and visions of what the food should taste like. One chef is way better than the other in short! I gave this restaurant 6 chances, spent 670 dollars just on Fixins, and I am not happy! The potato salad does not taste like soil food and has little deviation from a store bought potato salad. Being from the south, I am extremely disappointed. I also afforded so many chances as I became aware that the ownership was black. Welp, thats enough of my money! Also watch out when you're paying, they are adding 18% tip and not telling you and it pissed me off when I saw my itemized recept day seventy but I ended up paying 84 as the total I saw was 84 when they handed me the payment device. I felt deceived. Over this place needs work. The most consistent product has been the deep fried deviled eggs and even that changes EVERYTIME! I blame the problems on management and ownership!

Horrible Customer Service : particularly from the manager on duty today BRIAN RHEE. I ate there last night and had some serious food poisoning from the oxtail. I called first thing in the morning and left my number for a call back. I called again in the evening to make sure it got to the management and the Manager was very rude and refused to refund my particular meal. I am 100% sure it was the oxtail because I did not eat anything else all day and after that as I had bad diahorea all night a few hours after consuming this meal. Instead of being empathetic and maybe even inviting me to try them out again, he asked for a doctors note, AND SCIENTIfIC PROOF that it was indeed the oxtail. This level of not believing customers is unbecoming and I expected more from a manager. I spent way more than the oxtail and instead of trying to regain a customers confidence, he just lost it. What a shame that this promising restaurant in a vibrant part of the City, would hire key staff that are untrained and not sensitive to customer complaints hence creating an unsafe space for a customer like me. I really hope the store owner sees and addresses my review. He also refused to give me the direct email to the owner. Oh well- just sad

We got take out chicken. It was amazing. The sides- red beans and rice, yum, and collard greens. I really loved the biscuits with the whipped chili butter. The cornbread was also very good. When our crisis is over I look forward to sitting in the restaurant- which was spotless and very pretty. We loved this place!

The food here was fantastic and the service was wonderful. Des was such a sweetheart and made our night great. She was such a kind soul and was very patient. We came in with a party of 10 and we were in and out within an hour

We had the best service and food! Nicki was amazing! Helped us right away and gave us great suggestions! Our whole party was happy and we all got exactly what we wanted. The place was very busy but we still felt like we were taken care of exceptionally well. I will recommend this to everyone I know! Such a great addition to Sacramento! Thank you Nicki and Fixens! Can't wait to come back.
Excellent food and polite welcoming staff. Surprised we haven't been here before definitely a hidden gem on broadway.

*Before I start my review I want to mention that if you're picking up food from here do so directly through their website and not through Postmates since they charge you more even if they are delivering the food to you. This information is NOT on Fixins website* Now my review this is my second time here. First time me and my boyfriend came for breakfast I ordered the chicken/waffles bacon. The waffles were super skinny it seems like they used cinnamon in the batter and in the butter overly sweet...and the chicken tasted like it was sprinkled with sugar so no salty savory taste just overly sweetness and the bacon had more fat than meat...So recently we went did curbside I got the hot chicken sandwich, fries, Mac-n-cheese and their gumbo. As soon as I picked up my food (which is when I got a text telling me it's ready) my fries were already cold. We get home and the gumbo had no flavor no seasoning just nothing..the sandwich was more breading than meat (the sauce itself wasn't too bad) again fries were cold so I didn't eat them the only thing that was good was the Mac-n-cheese...so needless to say I will not be trying Fixins again. Great staff great owner just not great or good food
Not only is the food amazing. The restaurant really went all in on making it an experience. From the interior design, music, menu. Did I mention the food was amazing. Can't wait to go back!

I'm so glad Fixins has managed to stay open during this time and offer such great food and service through it all. I'm always so pleased with my meal and this last visit was a treat because one of our favorite servers has returned! We hadn't seen Jena for a while, and we were overjoyed when we saw she was our server! She's so sweet and attentive, she even remembered us from months ago! We will definitely keep returning for the great food and familiarity. It truly feels like a family vibe. Keep up the awesome work Fixins!

I am very disappointed by the food. My first time purchasing any food from this establishment "Fixins Soul Kitchen. All the food purchased was poor quality...the chicken was so greasy, I could take the oil, and oil my body for the next month. It was on October 3, 2020. The Red Beans and Rice, flavor, yet not done, the Macaroni and Cheese dry, the greens the flavor was there, it was lacking the Pot Liquor from the greens. The one little piece of cornbread, honestly, I don't know what is was. Waffle's and Chicken that was also ordered. I would never serve to no person. Waffle was just a flat dark round disc, and a big piece of dark chicken lying in the middle. It couldn't be eaten! I am mad as hell!
Now, I've eaten at many Soul Food Restaurants, this is on the bottom of the list. Knowing how to cook, I take my cooking seriously, and I serve the best to my family and friends. This food is too costly, for it to be unsatisfactory. Whoever, their cook is, I would give him or her, their walking papers. KoolAid to expensive, just ridiculous! I do believe in supporting Black Businesses, this business is the worst!

This place is absolutely steller! I am a Sous Chef at the UC Davis med center. I tried them when they donated food to the workers at the hospital.  It was delicious!! Went back today and spoke to the 3 girls that were taking orders. They were super chill and we just started talking food. They were telling me about the dishes and I ended up dropping like 70$! Well worth it the chicken was crispy and hot. The sweet butter and the biscuits were off the chain. We got the mac n cheese and grits fuego!  Even the kool-aid was lip smacking and it took me straight back to my childhood! You know them hot summer days when u use up all the sugar and all ice. Great spot great service we will back!
Wasn't that impressed with the food at all. It was just okay. Service was about the same. Nothing about it makes me feel like I'd come back.
Love this place and the waiter we had I'd say is in the top 10 of my 46 years. Always a pleasure coming here.

Alright where do I start. Everything is on point. From the service to every single item on the menu, well seasoned home made sauces, even the to-go napkin and utensils are high quality. They don't miss a beat here everything is exceptional.
Terrible service. Food was good. The bad service makes me not want to come back. We'll see if we give it another try.

OMG!!!!! Quite the experience! Everything was phenomenal - from the drinks to the entree to the dessert.  The waitstaff was courteous and inviting.  Nikki G. was our "Fixins Guide".  I appreciated her knowledge of not just the menu but also the history.  The restaurant was clean.  The ambiance was great.  It had a really nice vibe.  Hats off to the chefs because without the chefs there would be no Fixins.  We took friends who are not soul food connoisseurs and they could not stop raving about the food.  Although the menu was limited due to the restrictions, there was still something for everyone.  Mr. Johnson got this right.
Excellent Food and Excellent Service. Im definitely coming back to try more of this deliciousness.

I really really LOVE this place. The food is delicious. The seasoning is a perfect balance, not too salty, with a perfect hint of spice. Fried items have a light flaky crunchy texture. Catfish is amazing. Chicken and waffles are incredible- similar to the famous Roscoe's Chicken n' Waffles in Los Angeles. Drinks are good. They have a Rodney Strong as their house Cabernet. The peach cobbler was a little too sweet for me, but everything else was amazing!!! Great service as well. I'm definitely coming back.

Been here twice and really enjoyed my food.  I've had the shrimp and grits which is unlike I've ever had-can you say delicious??   First time I tasted it I said "hmmm-someone's grandma or big momma" is in the kitchen running thangs lol!  I love the atmosphere too.   After eating such comforting food you just want to hang around but that's hard to do without looking at some poor soul who just walked up and hoping for a table as you chow down.  The place is always full because the food is good.  Somehow they are pretty good at quickly seating folks. Staff very attentive.  Also, it's a nice surprise to see  Kevin Johnson.  He's definitely a business man.  If he's  there he is like the  host of the year, mingling and making sure patrons are being treated well and are happy with their experience.

The first time I came to this restaurant, I ordered takeout. The server asked what 3 pieces of chicken would I like. I stated a breast, wing and thigh. However, when I got home I had 2 thighs and wing.  I came back this past Tuesday and ordered my 3 piece chicken meal again to go. ( Forgot to mention that I'm traveling from Antioch, over an hour.)  When the sever gave me my order, something told me to check my order. This time neither one of my sides were in the bag. The sever was insisting on seeing my receipt!! I ordered on Yelp via Grubhub. Why did I have to tell her that the chicken meals come with 2 sides!!! She finally went in the back and obtained my 2 sides. Wait it gets better. Why when I got home I had a breast, leg , and and half of a back. At first it looked like a thigh.The 3 piece was batter and bones. I'm done!!! The chicken is just ok!!! Definitely not worth the hour drive!!! The collard greens are good and the peach cobbler!! The Mac and cheese was just mediocre!!
Love the food and service. I've eaten here probably ten times, and I've never been disappointed.
Love everything about to the location, dining experience, staff & OMG Tha FOOD!!! THE BEST SOUL FOOD IN SACRAMENTO!!!
Fun atmosphere, good food and drinks. Crowded so plan to wait unless you call in. Greens had good flavor and catfish was light and not greasy. I recommend the fried green tomatoes - so good!
My first time trying this place! The greens and macaroni were soooooo good  But my chicken was so salty I couldn't eat it. I will definitely give another try.

My family tried to visit 01/31 around 5pm. We expected the restaurant to be business and it was which we were fine with. My wife, son and myself were seated at the high table right in front of the bar, again we were fine with that. After the hostess left we sat there for 15 minutes, no one came up at all!!! No drink order, no acknowledgement nothing at all. We saw workers walk by and no one asked us if we were okay or even said Hi. Another hostess walked by got a drink and went back to front, again nothing. We have been there numerous times and this was our first negative experience.

The food at this restaurant is amazing.  Although you will more than likely have to wait to be seated,  it is truly worth the wait.  I found a very nice bookstore around the corner to browse in while waiting.  Happened to be a book signing going on for a local author.  I spoke with the owner who informed me that they frequently have book signing with local, national and international authors as well as workshops and seminars.  What a lovely community

Let's start with the good stuff:
-awesome location and great decor-not to mention the cool waitlist text feature which let's you know your place in line. 
‐food was really good authentic soul food, and for those of you like me who don't eat pork, the sides were made with chicken stock. I had: red beans, blck eye peas, potatoes, candied yams and greens.
Now the problems:
1st- the prices are a little on the high end- I for one skipped the $6 biscuit as intriguing as it sounded.
2nd- food came out about 7 mins after ordering (awesome right! wrong) it was luke warm "not hot"
3rd - portions weren't huge for the price
4th- I ordered eggs scrambled very soft and somehow, even after the second try, they were never really soft.
5th- there was a mix up with my place in line even after letting the hostess know to add one to my party -and while waiting in the court yard I was taken off the list.  I had to add myself back on- but no worries I had time.
Last, but certainly not least was the service.  As nice as the servers were, they were clearly overwhelmed and lacked training.  In fact, the servers seemed to be so nice that they would begin conversations with other patrons and completely forget what I had asked for leaving me to wait for long periods for things that disjointed the dining experience.
Let me reiterate"I really liked the food". So much so, that since I was visiting from out of town I went back the next day for breakfast -with a side of peach cobbler for later since they do not do to-go orders. In the future I will come only if I want good soul food without cooking, and I have a lot of time to kill.
The Mac and the yams are enough to keep you coming back but everything was great. Nice atmosphere as well. This would make a great date night or a place to have some drinks.
Amazing, high-quality food!! The chicken is extremely tender, juicy, and tasty. The takeout experience was easy and super organized. Will definitely be getting food from here soon!

We love Fixins!  We ordered take-out using their online system on Friday evening.  The food was ready in 40 minutes, and during the pandemic they have a great system for curbside pickup.  The service was friendly and all the food was delicious.  The fried chicken is amazing.
Had the catfish, mac 'n' cheese, and collard greens. Everything was perfectly seasoned. Will definitely be back!
The charred okra is my favorite!!  Best staff and best food. Went with a large party and the service was outstanding. Would totally recommend!

I kindly asked for them to take my chicken away because it was undercooked...The manager I believe  who was brunette told me that it was cooked "perfectly fine" and that he could not give me another chicken with attitude. I always come here and have never had a bad experience until I spoke to this man. I am livid and NEVER coming here AGAIN. It is not hard to fix food that I didn't pay for!!!

Ate here a few times. LOVE the fried chicken!! So crunchy and flavored very well. The Mac & Cheese and greens are really delicious as well. I really love the biscuits with the white gravy too! Jena was the best server and had so much knowledge about all of the dishes. She had amazing hospitality and was always checking in. So sweet and helpful, she definitely made our experience great. Hands down BOMB FOOD.

Oh my damn! The fried chicken here is better than my mommas. (Don't tell her I said that) The minute you bite into this golden crispy juicy chicken you're transported to childhood nostalgia. In fact the adult kool aid and music they keep bumping reminds you of a cookout at your uncles house. Did I mention they have brunch!! Also the ox tails and red beans and rice are the bees knees... if bees had knees. Amazing friendly service and there is usually a small wait. However I gave this place 4 stars because of the sides. I don't believe you can call it soul food without some banging sides. The Mac and cheese and the grits need more seasoning. Also they are a bit over priced. If you're going to price your sides so high at-least make sure they are worth it. Considering these are the only two I've tried I'll leave that up to speculation because it is definitely a place I will be visiting again.

Went here for my first time last weekend. The wait was minimal on a Saturday morning. The food was great! Large portions and very tasty, great bang for the buck. The pancakes were very thick and fluffy. The French toast was nothing fancy, but still very good. The chicken sausage patties were very large and thick, would order those again.

I've already been here several times and have not been disappointed! Love all menu items and choice of items to add with your meal. The kool-aid reminds me of growing up back in the day! You really have to try Fixins and make it a regular feature! You will be glad you came.
staff was not attentive at all. waitress ignored us every time I tried to get her attention

Well to start off I give it 3 stars because the wait to get in was so long considering you could not use the link via Yelp to put your name on the wait list. That's what the 3 is for. This is a nice little place, the decor and location are cool. The food was tasty. I had the chicken and waffles my chicken was good. The waffles could have been a little harder or firm they were super soft. I enjoyed the Mac and cheese it was the way I like Mac and cheese. The greens were okay I do not like the stem in my greens but that is my preference. I had a drink called the cherry bomb, it was okay,  a little on the tart side I like my mixed drink a tad bit sweeter. The waitress was very nice. She and the rest of the staff were working really hard to keep up with the large crowd the was waiting to be served. The wait time for my food was good for as busy as it was. I also ordered the pimento cheese dip which I enjoyed a day later. All in all it is not a bad place to have a decent soul food dinner. Do not go on a Friday night unless you can call in to put your name on the waitlist. Enjoy!
WORTH THE WAIT!!!! Customer service and food is A1!!! Don't leave without trying the catfish!!!!

Not better than granny's but it's decent great environment and the wait wasn't too long. We order deviled eggs and fried green tomatoes for appetizers I got fried chicken to be safe my fiancé got shrimp and grits I was happy with my meal he wasn't too happy. I would return in the near future
Order the chicken and waffles.  Chicken was very flavorful.  Waffles were delicious and I don't what butter that was but it really went great together.  Also a plus to see Kevin and come talk to us!

author_location: Miramar Ranch North, San Diego, CA
text: We had our first visit (pickup) at Fixins last night and THE FOOD IS AMAZING!!! You can feel the love in their food. We had fried chicken, collard greens, red beans and rice and their Mac and cheese! It was so good...all of it! We absolutely cannot wait to go back! Another bonus...the staff are so sweet...I am so thankful they are open and bringing their passion for food to Sacramento!

Great food waffle and chicken was good . Thank you for seasoning the chicken . My husband had the fried catfish  red beans and rice and potato salad he said it was good. We shared the bread pudding it was ok little sweet .  Service was good . Whole atmosphere was great . We got to to take a picture with famous Kevin Johnson that was awesome. Thank you Kevin for that picture.
Food was fire, we had a table of 8 and no complaints, staff was prompt and accurate. Best soul food... wait best restaurant in town.

Absolutely amazing! We'll be back soon. The portions, food quality, and service here are all on-point. All of the owners(?) and waitstaff present were extremely friendly and attentive. Everything we ate there was delicious- we especially loved their biscuits and peach cobbler. Plus they have Kool-aid!

The food is outrageously good!! Fried catfish was good, fried green tomatoes were the bomb, black eyed peas and rice was delish, the charred okra was interesting but would have preferred it cooked differently. I grew up eating soul food and this is legit! We will be back!!!

My wife and I ate at Fixins for the first time today during their soft-opening period. I had the gumbo and my wife had fried catfish with collard greens and red beans and rice for her entree. We shared the peach cobbler for dessert. The gumbo was very tasty and well seasoned with an excellent roux. The catfish was absolutely amazing, with a thin, crispy and excellently seasoned batter and perfectly cooked catfish meat inside. The collard greens were slightly sweet and without the typical bitter bite, and the red beans and rice were well seasoned and tasty. The peach cobbler was absolutely awesome, mouthwatering and fresh but not too sweet, with the most perfect crust. We will definitely be back, many, many times!

Tried Fixins for the first time yesterday. Love the customer service! Food is freshly homemade everyday and boy was it good. We had fried chicken Mac n'cheese which is the BEST and coleslaw. The ranch and grape butter was also very yummy! Will be revisiting:)

Probably one of the worst service I had ever, walked in with four hostess standing around talking.  All girls looked at me then turned and continued to converse. One was kind enough to ask how many she would be seating but as she was doing so the one hostess talking stopped and looked at me as if I was interrupting her conversation. She rudely told a worker to seat us. We sat and a server come to take our order. Knowing what we wanted we asked if we could place the order now, he walked away and said he would return to take the order. After ordering we waiting 40 mins no food so we left.

My friend and I came here on a busy Friday night a few weeks ago and the food was pretty good. The wait was fast and we were seated before we finished our first drink at the bar. I would say their fried chicken was comparable to South. Their grits were amazing but their catfish bites and waffles were pretty standard.

Came here for the first time and it was BOMB!! Service was great and the food was even better!! Fried chicken, mac n cheese, and fried deviled eggs are amazing!!! My husband got the spicy fried chicken sandwich and loved it!! Definitely want to go back!!

Terrible experience here today.  It took over 4 hours from start to finish.  Once we finally got seated outside, we were told that the heating lamps were broken.  We only chose outside because of the lamps., then we were told we couldn't wait for a table inside. Ordered our food and it took over an hour for just our appetizers.  We waited another 20-30 minutes for our actual food to arrive even though other customers who came after us were seated after us ate, had dessert and and left. Finally got the food and it was cold.  Mac n cheese was cold on top and hot on the bottom, so I'm assuming it sitting under a lamp. Grits were cold and hard, I sent it back for a remake, but they didn't seem super excited about it.  No sauce for the food.  Asked for ketchup and bbq sauce which took another 10-15 minutes.  By then my food was all the cold.  No lids for the to go cups. Check comes out and they refuse to do a split check and they can only take 3 cards.  Gratuity was 18% but calculated on the receipt as 19.5% which we were taxed on. Horrible experience all around, plus our waitress Jasmine had a poor attitude.
Soooooo....Fixins' is all that!  Nice atmosphere, clean restrooms, delicious food; excellent and attentive service; food wait time-less than 10 minutes!  Will definitely be back!
I had a good experience I went with my sister. Cyd helped us out and she was doing her thing , it was busy so she did cool for it being that busy . Coming back when they have oxtails in stock ! Lol

A nice addition to Oak Park. We arrived on a Saturday night around 5 and our wait was about 45 mins. I had no idea the place was owned by former Mayor Kevin Johnson and team. We sat outside and ordered some drinks while we waited. Kevin Johnson went around and checked in on everyone to say hi and chat. He was super nice and genuine, not what I expected from a former politician.  Someone in our party mentioned the pie incident and he responded graciously as well. They have only been open for a month and a half so there were some kinks they were working out. On this particular night, the kitchen was backed up so they stopped seating people for about 30 minutes. We went in to get more drinks and Kevin Johnson had just come out of the kitchen with ipad in hand and working pretty hard. He came up to us and asked if we were still waiting, got our names again and seated us about 5 minutes after that. He said they were still working out kitchen logistics and they are going to open the outdoor area for dining soon too.  It's nice to see hard work even if he was a former NBA player and mayor. I gave this place 4 stars because the wait was so long. Hopefully the waiting area on the street will be nice and comfy if long waits are expected in the future. 

As for the food, we ordered the deviled eggs, fried green tomatoes, oxtail, fried catfish, and fried chicken. All very delicious and not greasy. I liked the Kool- Aid selection too! 

We will definitely come back. However, I do like the rib tips from Sandra Dees .  I hope to see more good things from Kevin Johnson and Oak Park!
Nice vibe, friendly staff, but I can tell they are still learning how to serve with excellence.  The food is good down home cooking. I will definitely return.

I've been to Fixins several times... and I love the food! The first 3 times where during the first two months the restaurant opened...  last time I went which was the Jan 2nd my son said the bacon was really salty... after talking to the waitress the cooks add salt to the bacon.  That day the service was also pretty slow as the restaurant was not crowded. I want Fixins to survive.. I love what Kevin Johnson is doing for Oak Park..
This place is amazing and I didn't mind the wait as it wasn't as long as expected, however, the food IS worth a wait.  It was really good and authentic.  I will be going back.

I would have given them no stars if I could. I've never been so annoyed with a company. For starters, the first point of contact Mayako, is not the best person to have up front. She wasn't very friendly, and quite unprofessional. Everyone that came in, regardless of party size, she told them it would be about a 45 minute wait. I watched her seat a gentleman she was familiar with, without him having to wait the 40-45 minutes like everyone else. He walked in, said hi, hugged her, and said he was there to eat. She told him she hated to put him on the wait list. She then waved a server over, had him clear a table off, and then seated that gentleman. Mayako and another person looked at the check in tablet together asking which person they should seat next, instead of seating people in the order of their arrival. Multiple customers went back to the post to check on their position, Mayako asked for their telephone numbers and often told them she didn't have them on the list. She asked if they would have checked in under another telephone number or name. Customers were so confused. Further, I sat to the right of Mayako my entire 50 minute wait, she finally decided to ask what my name was. She told me the system texted me 10 minutes ago and asked if I checked my phone, as it was in my hand. I asked her what number she texted, she proceeded to tell me it didn't matter, and she could seat me then. I again asked what number was texted. With an attitude, she showed me the tablet where she entered the telephone number incorrectly. Mayako took me to the table, and then stated it gets loud in there so sometimes she doesn't hear the telephone numbers. If that is in fact the case, she should confirm the telephone number, and/or show the customer the telephone number she heard. With that being the first point of contact, that was horrible. 
I decided to stay even though I was annoyed. The server, Toni, was actually very customer friendly. She took my order and told me my food would be out shortly. After about 5 minutes a plate came to me, however it was not my food. The guy picked the plate up and then took it to the correct customer. That same gentleman came out a second time, and proceeded to hand the wrong food to the wrong customer. I couldn't take it after that. I got up and left. I do not want to eat something that has been sat down in front of another as they both talk over it and discuss why that's not their meal. This was my first time there. I heard great reviews about the food, had I known about the customer service, I would have minded my business. I'm very disappointed that we finally have a business in our community worth celebrating, but the professionalism and customer service is horrible.

Went here for the first time today. We had the blue plate special with bacon and house potatoes and my husband has the 2 waffles + 3 pieces fried chicken. This was definitely enough food for us and our three small kids to share. Fried chicken was GREAT! Definitely coming back for that. The biscuits were also very good. NOTE - the butter that comes with everything is cinnamon flavored but you can ask for regular butter if you want - but you need to ask. I will say the house potatoes were the only miss - they were a little mushy and not very flavored. But everything else was great. They kept apologizing for the wait times but we thought it was very reasonable - I wonder if they are gunshot from some of the previous reviews? They even comped all our drinks - which I thought was unnecessary as the wait time for our food was really reasonable! Service was very friendly and helpful. We will definitely be back to try out their new outdoor seating when that opens up - and we want to try lunch. Definitely recommend this place.

WOW!!!!!!  delicious food.  Came here 1st time just before 5:00 pm and had catfish, greens, mac & cheese, coleslaw, bread pudding and peach cobbler.  Returned 3 days later at lunch time had eggs and cheese grits.  Had some issues with service and fortunately owner, chef and, I believe the manager happened to be having a discussion so politely interrupted to share my experience and was able to compliment the Chef on the wonderful food.  I so appreciated them hearing what i had to say.  I highly recommend this place.  I will be back soon.  Lovely environment and mix of people, senior to infants, ordinary folks like me to the professionals.  Please go!!!!

Definitely one of the best restaurants in town and definitely the best soul food restaurant we know of around here! The ox tail, and fried chicken are delicious. We have only been here a few times, but we definitely will be coming here for years!
I don't understand the hype for this place. But the food isn't worth coming back for at all! None of us (party of 8) liked our food. And that says a lot!

I've been here a few times now and really enjoy the food, atmosphere and the service. The fried okra and greens are amazing! And the fried chicken is my favorite! Really good!!! Our server, Jena, was delightful. Very relaxed and easy going, but also attentive and friendly and professional. It's a little loud and not always easy to have a conversation, but it's also lively and fun!

My husband and I tried Fixins for the first time. And they have a lot of fixing to do. The bartender Spencer told us we were confused by the menu. How can we both order chicken wings the same exact order but our dishes are totally different? No I think your cooks are confused. The service was bad and it's was like we asked for a cure for cancer when we asked for the bar area to be wiped off.
Great food, Fast service, and the place is spotless, bathrooms are super clean, just what we needed in Sacramento, I highly recommend this place
Down home southern soul food.  Koolaid Cocktail is a great choice

Went down to check out the new hot spot last about 530p. When we arrived I felt a good vibe as we entered , good music and impressive ambience. The crowed or should I say dinners was a good reputation of Sacramento. There was a wait time of 45 minutes for a party of four , so we headed to bar for drinks and chilled on the patio. They had a pretty cool notification system to let you know your wait time for being seated in real time. Wait staff was attentive and move with urgency. Bad service can kill your meal even if food yummy. Our server was Michal and she was great.  She answered our questions and was a great help picking our order. Had Deep fried Devil eggs to start, it came quickly and really good. We shared our entree's so I was able to enjoy a taste of everything. My favorite was the fried catfish, could have ate that entire plate but  I had to share.  Over all good and great service. Looking forward to going back.

We were seated quickly around 11 on a Saturday. The service was timely and very friendly. Kevin actually came out and mingles with people in the restaurant. They didn't have a drink menu, but did have bar seating. Also the temperature of the food could've been a tad warmer. The taste was good. I would go again.

So let's get the negatives out the way, they don't answer the phone they don't do togo and the weight is extremely long like a restaurant on Mother's Day lol.. Now the service was amazing Britney I believe is her name the front manager was awesome over and beyond to make our experience joyful for the 1st time, We definitely will be back the catfish and oxtails where to die for, next time I'll try to eat less so that I could enjoy some of the desert..  Clean establishments nice staff good food over all nice placw to have added in the community
The margarita was so good! It was my boyfriend and I's first time going to fixins soul kitchen and man oh man it did not disappoint!! I will gladly be coming back again!!! !

I love the vibe of this place! The decor is dope, all black art! The owner is extremely nice and the staff is very friendly and welcoming. The food was delicious! I recommend the green fried tomatoes, blue breakfast, and shrimp and grits. I definitely will be coming back. #Allblackeverything!

I've eaten here three times since their soft opening two weeks ago; the service is amazing, everyone works hard to make you feel like you're a part of their family and the FOOD, that's kinda what soul food is all about right?!  I had the fried chicken and two "Fixins"  creamy grits, and yams the first time and Mac and cheese and greens the second time. Then of course I had to try the chicken and waffles... everything is so good.    Ooooh and the dessert!!!!  I had the lemon bar, and now it haunts my dreams.   I honestly feel like this is going to be one of Sacramento's staple restaurants; they'll be around forever if they keep things up.  Good job Fixins crew, you have a customer for life.
Fixins is a new fabulous soul food restaurant.  Fried chicken was delicious.  Mac n cheese was awesome. Peach cobbler was fantastic!

This oxtails, yams, and macaroni and cheese was bomb!!! The catfish so good and seasoned like Cool Ranch Doritos!!! I'm part Texas and part Louisiana and this is the best soul food on the West Coast I've ever had!!! The drinks are bomb too!
I just loved the place. The food was well seasoned and tasty. And don't forget about the overall Vibe. I will return.

I've only been there twice, but the food is really good. I had the same thing both times that I was there. Prices are a little expensive but the food is excellent. It's always very busy there, however we were seated quickly, which was so appreciated because we only had one hour for lunch.

This is the best Soul food place ever!! Recently went to OLG in Atlanta, and Fixins beat their shrimp and grits!  And the fried green tomatoes..so good! I just wish they did take out because I work right around the corner, and would order out (and my fellow nurses), lunch there frequently!! Maybe that'll change

Went here on a Sunday after hearing all the great reviews from family and it did not disappoint. Arrived early and there was a short wait so we decided to sit and order from the bar. Ol' School provides the service and was on point. We ordered the fried chicken & catfish, red beans & rice, Mac & cheese, collard greens, and okra. Everything was seasoned well, cooked to perfection, and delicious as described. I would have to say my favorite was the red beans & rice which were the best I've ever tasted. Atmosphere and service was great with good folks and music from all walks of life. I will return many more times especially since we didn't leave room for dessert.

I went for Sunday brunch and had a great meal. I love the ambiance--modern but comfortable. The same goes for the food. All the southern classics with a few refinements to suit current tastes. I sampled a bunch of the sides and appetizers: deep fried deviled eggs, catfish nuggets, fried green tomatoes, collard greens, black eyed peas, grits and cheese. I  liked it all. Nothing was too heavy despite the fact that much of it was fried. My favorites were the fried green tomatoes, collard greens and deviled eggs. I also tried my friend's fried chicken and waffles and I am looking forward to ordering it for myself next time. That and the gumbo and the 12-layer devil's food cake. To drink, I had the New Orlean's Sweat Tea, which was an excellent choice. Everyone at the table ended up ordering this drink for their second round. Service was great. The staff were friendly and the orders came out promptly. I will definitely be back.
Everything on this menu is delicious! Great addition to the community of Oak Park. Keep up the quality and hospitality and this business will continue to be a hit spot!

The place is always busy but the food is great. The Mac and cheese was great as well as the yams. I liked the chicken but felt like it could have had a lil more flavor. Although the huge glass of sweet kool aid made up for it!

I frequently go to this restaurant. I love the mimosas and the setting. I don't mind paying for any food or beverages,  but yesterday was a real disappointment because I ordered the 3 piece fried chicken dinner and my chicken seemed to be overly fried. The server was nice enough to send my plate back to the kitchen 2x. Even though I got my chicken I was willing to just pay for it and leave. The Chef  personally came to my table w another plate, but what really upset me is that he picked the chicken off of the plate that he brought me and ,with his bare Hands and(NO GLOVES) to put on my plate. At that point it seemed a lot Sarcastic. Thank You to the Management for not charging me, and the Server was just doing an awesome job. But I'm no slacker I pay for what I order, so I felt bad that they had to take care of the tix. Really it was the Chef. Maybe he was having a bad day or something,  but he shouldn't take his Personal issue and bring it to a setting as Nice as your restaurant Mr. JOHNSON because I really like your restaurant and I'm glad that it's in the Community. Anyway I was able to still give the server a tip because it wasn't her fault, she didn't cook the food and she also was really professional as well as managment, but the Chef can really use some Training. Everyone knows that if the chicken is fried to Perfection the bones would be clean and the only thing that is left on the plate.
Amazing food, amazing staff and great service! Highly recommend. We have been there for brunch, lunch, dinner and have had a great experience every time- and very full!  We will be back!

They don't take reservations so we showed up with a party of 25. Manager (nicknamed Yellow Experience) & owner Kevin, were extremely accommodating giving us options and moving tables. Food was delicious and everybody enjoyed themselves. I'm a vegetarian but I was able to create a hearty meal out of their sides!
Great recommendation! The service was graceful. Wait time was awesome and the food was delicious. 5 Stars across the board! Continue to flourish....

Heard about this place through friends, thought I would try it...had chicken and waffles, the waffles were not crispy, the chicken was just ok. A friend ordered a drink from the bar only to get the bill, and realized it cost $35, (price of drink wasn't listed on drink menu) thinking it must've been a mistake because an entire bottle cost less than $35, we asked to speak to the manager...after several minutes of waiting, we were told, that there's nothing they can do...I was with a party of fifteen and we spent several hundreds of dollars at this establishment (won't be doing that again here) and  to not be offered an apology! RUDE!! Bad business KJ! Profiting off the blue collar workers, thought you were trying to support the community...not at those outrageous prices...and the foods not that good! VERY DISAPPOINTED !!!

I went to fixins for the second time drove from Natomas because my kids like their chickens and I like their oxtails. I was told that I could not place a to-go order it was all a lie dine in so I asked if I ordered can I box up the food myself the bartender asks Brian if it was okay and he said yes. When Brian brought my food to me mind you it was a hot plate of chicken I told him that if the policies of no two goals or put in place that the customers should be aware I then proceeded to told him that I do post on Yelp and I would be posting. Before I could finish my sentence Brian snatched a hot plate of chicken out of my hand and told me that she would not be serving me...ask him why and he said well you said that you were going to go on Yelp. I proceeded to tell him that I can put anything on Yelp that I felt that I wanted to. he asked me if we made a deal if I put nothing on Yelp that I could have my food. This was a ridiculous scene on the patio he's stashed all the plates away from me and said all you should have said was okay cool I won't put anything on Yelp and the problem would have been resolved. Mind you this is a predominantly soul food restaurant they're playing gospel music in the background all the customer there are mainly African-American and you treat your main customers and a way that's so horrific and so nasty I would never dinning there again I will tell anyone that I know not to ever go there it's not worth it is the humiliation it was utterly disgusting Brian if you see this or if corporate sees this they really need to find another manager to replace, retrain you I don't know if you're having a rough day but the world is already in chaos right now why treat each other with the disrespect that I was shown on Father's day. I was so embarrassed we're standing out on the patio while gospel music is playing and I'm getting food snatched from me mind you I drove all the way from Natomas. I've worked in the food industry for 10 years and the customers aren't always right the managers aren't always right but this situation could have been resolved quietly and quickly I was on my way out the door until I said the word Yelp and it pushed his buttons. Brian I hope you find peace in your life.

Fixins was pretty good overall but it could improve on a lot of things. The fries chicken was a little slimy but it was pretty good I really liked the creamed corn that was my favorite the green weren't that good I really wanted to try the okra that's one of the reasons I went there but was told they didn't have anymore so I was a little disappointed in that our server tried her best but you can tell she was young and didn't have much experience but she def tried her best but to wrap this up I probably won't come back here

The fried chicken was delicious and seasoned perfectly. All the food came out piping hot. The Mac n cheese was on point. Biscuit melts in your mouth. Iced tea was just the right sweetness. Sweet potatoes, creamed corn and the green beans tasted like my mother's cooking. Our server, Nathan, was very friendly and attentive. Fixins is the placed to go for a home cooked meal that you don't actually have to cook at home!
This was my first time there . I had the catfish it had great flavor , wasn't light and fluffy and wasn't greasy .
Great food and service.  The oxtail had great favor.  The chicken wings were very small. Love the ice tea!
This was my second visit and I will definitely be back again!  The service was fast, our food tastes so good.  The atmosphere is nice.  Everyone was nice and checked on us frequently.

I had fried chicken and waffles with a side of Mac and cheese. The chicken was just ok but the waffle was flat n floppy and the Mac and cheese was not good to me at all. Overall the atmosphere is good but the space is a little tight to move around and because of the size of the space playing music very loud was not good for conversation with your dinner mates.
I enjoyed my shrimp and grits and am glad to see more restaurants continue to come into the community.  I'll be back!
Never been here. My mistake, I thought I had a different restaurant loaded up. Sorry!

Thank goodness for the quality of food!! My first rime at this well reviewed establishment I was so excited for my birthday dinner! Strolling in at 7 pm was not the best decision but I was prepared to wait. The hostess quoted  a 2 hour wait but was not going to accept anymore on the list and we would be put on some other list. They seemed a little disorganized themselves. Luckily two seats opened up at the bar. Here is where it starts. We were not acknowledged even with eye contact or a smile from Spencer. 5 min later The other bartender D asked if we needed menus. We looked them over and chatted with patrons. When Spencer approaches I jokingly said smiling" you do have a voice!.. I was not expecting the response of " I do have other customers.  So anyway I gave him my entire order, then he took my husbands order after gathering his pad. And Mind you we did not receive a smile throughout the experience from him. 5 min later MY order came out in which it was not my order, it was my fellow patrons , in which we ordered the EXACT same thing. I preceded to clean the rim of the plate to make it picture perfect when he said I think that's mine... so  he proceeded to tell Spencer they give me his food in which I offered to pay for his food for the mistake and in conversations with him and Spencer I did not receive acknowledgment from Spencer and when I called him out for ignoring me his reply was" I was conversing with him not you" I was not going to make a scene. We ALL received our food  and enjoyed every bite of it ,my hubby's drink came way later though after the the food came. I understand that it was only one bad apple and was not going to let him ruin my experience. The food was wonderful I mean the Mac and cheese the oxtails the greens the fried chicken, I can't wait to go back and try more food. The end highlight was meeting Mr. Kevin Johnson himself, the restaurateur, Sacs former mayor, NBA Champion. I let him know the service fell short , but the food made up for the bad service from The bartender. Thank you Mr.Johnson for bringing your awesome cuisine to Oak Park.

Went here for lunch yesterday for the first time with three friends and was not disappointed. I had the gumbo and a sweet tea.  While I'm not an expert on gumbo, I would order this again!  Two of my friends had fried chicken and the third had catfish.  All of us enjoyed our meal.  The service was very good too.
Mia my server was amazing. Good authentic soul food, speedy service & good vibes. Def a returning customer

First time coming here. Waited about an hour to be seated ( should have joined the wait list). It was nice night so we waited outside. Well worth it. Had the Shrimp & Grits with gravy. Loved it. Kevin Johnson even stopped by to thank us for coming. Definitely coming back again.

Definitely a gem in Sacramento. I always can't wait until the next time I get to eat here. Their food is delicious. There is no other way to put it. My first visit had great service. We tried the chicken and waffles, fried catfish nuggets, Mac n cheese, peach cobbler (?), and mimosas. I could do without the mimosas but everything was amazing. The chicken was moist and perfectly crispy. Seasoned perfectly as well. Their Mac n cheese is probably my favorite Mac n cheese of all time. It does get a bit oily but I wish I could figure out their Mac n cheese recipe because it's sooooo good. I also ordered a banana cream pie to go for my sister and it was delicious. The service was great at my first visit as well!! My most recent visit we did take out due to COVID-19. I had their chicken sandwich, Mac n cheese (a must), and their alcoholic kool-aid. Their adult kool-aid was delicious & definitely could potentially be dangerous. You can't taste any of the alcohol! Their chicken sandwich was amazing, it was so good I began to like eating the raw onion in my sandwich. I hate eating raw onions with a passion but with this sandwich it's worth it. They serve the sandwich with a side of fries (generously salted), and a kool aid pickle. I love pickles so this kool aid pickle was a special treat. I'll definitely order more in the future! I liked the sweetness and tartness of the pickles. I love coming here and I want to come back more. I felt so bad for the server who helped me when I went on my most recent visit because they're clearly stressed with the demands of their take out orders now. They offer curbside pick up and make sure to check your order with you in person to make sure everything is there. A great place that I will continue to support!

I wasn't impressed with my visit to Fixins Soul Kitchen. I've eaten at numerous "Soul Food" restaurants and the food was just okay. Food lacked seasoning and serving sizes were small. Out of everything we tried( fried deviled eggs, greens, yams, red beans and rice, fritters, fried fish, fried and smothered chicken, biscuits) the coleslaw was the best. Waitress was very friendly and helpful however service was slow. 3 of our meals came and we were finished by the time the other 2 people were served. I was very disappointed in Kevin Johnson who was in the restaurant and we asked to speak to him. Not only did he not come talk to us he proceeded to talk to several people sitting near us, outside, and even took time to wipe off tables. I will say Thank you to the manager who ended up comping the 2 late meals.

This has become my Saturday go to! 4 weekends in a row and still have not been disappointed. I've told friends about it who also have been once a week. Thank you KJ for bringing opening this in Oak Park!
Exactly what I expected! Great soul food, excellent service and beautiful venue at the heart of Oak Park.. The food was excellent and seasoned to perfection!

I went in Sat afternoon. It was busy I get that. 2 people were trying to settle a diners tab. After that 2 female hosts were standing whispering things. I'm just standing there waiting to be helped. Obviously there little private conversation was more important than helping a customer. I will not come back. Get better hosts who care about the customers and not childish conversations.
Food was very good.  Service was good.  Loved the decor inside.  The peach Cobbler was to die for.  Everything was so much better than Sandra Dee's.  Highly recommend!!!
Friendly waitstaff, pleasant environment, reasonably price, quick service and delicious red velvet cake.

I was really excited about trying this restaurant. My family and I decided we would go check it out after church on Sunday. We arrived and found that the place was packed. Having driven from the Tracy/Stockton area I wasn't going to let a crowd turn me around. We were told our wait time was 1/2 hour to hour  before we would get seated.  After we Finally got seated and ordered our food we waited a while longer before our food came out.  When the waiter came out with our food I was quite shocked to find that it was our dessert that would come out first. I asked the waiter so we get dessert first?? He goes oh well it came out so I decided to bring it out. Being that we were already hungry we ate our dessert. As we are eating Our dessert another waiter comes out to ask how our dinner was. We informed him that we still hadn't received our dinner yet and that our dessert came out first. He apologized and immediately went into the kitchen and returned shortly with our dinner. I was glad the food tasted good but we still had not received the appetizers we ordered. After finally finishing our dinner my mom who is 85 realized she never got the fried green tomatoes she ordered, my fried deviled eggs or my nieces cat fish nuggets. At this point the trainee who was shadowing our waitress Jazmine, comes out and I inform him that our appetizers never came out. He apologizes and goes in the kitchen to tell someone.ANOTHER person comes to our table to apologize for not bringing out our order and asks if we still want the appetizers. I informed her that we didn't and just needed the check.

Overall I feel like the restaurant would be a very nice place to have a nice dinner with friends & family but they need to work out the kinks.  I think the crowd will die down soon but for now, you are going to have do better with getting people in & out and getting orders correct. I plan to give Fixins another chance (after the crowds die down).

My dissatisfaction is with customer service. I've came to the grand opening, called, left voicemails, and tried many times to get a reservation to host my birthday dinner there. I was told at first during the grand opening they were not doing reservations, then was told to contact a staff to see if I can get a reservation, and then once I found out that they hired someone to handle reservations by Kevin Johnson himself, he gave me two business cards and told me to outreach to them about reservations. I've already supported the business and wanted to bring my family and friends as well but when I finally got through to someone today to see what I can do. I was told they are not able to take anymore reservations for October. I'm disappointed. I came in, spoke to staff, called many times, left voicemails, and this is the outcome. Do better!
Love this place. Try the catfish. The cocktails are bomb AF. Oak park is becoming such a hip place.

If you like southern home style delicious food, Fixins is the place to go. I've had the shrimp and grits, fried catfish,  macaroni and cheese,  collards and the yams. Everything is fantastic! I've also had the peach cobbler for dessert and it was mouth watering.  Fixins is the place to go, if you're missing Momma"s cooking! Yummy, yummy

Was there for brunch with a group of girlfriends on a beautiful Saturday. All I can say is blessed! The food was outstanding, the service was top notch, and the vibe was high energy love! I will definitely go again. Thank you former Mayor Johnson for providing such quality for the Old School as well as the New School Oak Park. I am so proud of you!

I have visited this place three times. Very small dining area. Long wait list on Saturdays, Sundays, and evenings. Breakfast is the worst. Food not made to order. Bacon not as advertised (smoked).Dinner and lunch are the best meals. I informed the waitress I wanted to order to go, but I wanted to order as I finished eating. She brought the food while I was still eating. Young staff lacks customer service.
I loved this place!! They do a great job giving you the ultimate soul food experience. Great service, tasty soul food, what more could you ask for out of a place??
Excellent food service and atmosphere. We were Sat immediately and taken care of. The chicken and waffles were ex ellent
Bomb.com. Every time Is great. But I have to be honest I hate the wait! But apparently not to much cause I keep going back. Lol.
Yummy, fresh food great service. Ive visited twice I try the catfish plate and fried chicken plate both did not disappoint.
I ate at this restaurant yesterday and the food and service was as good as it gets. The prices matched the food. I will be returning,  hope to see you there. Enjoy

You don't come only here for the soul food, you come here for the soul of people. Once I stepped in the door I was welcomed just in a way as if you walk into a relatives home and they say "you've grown up so much I remember when you were just a baby!" and you don't know who they are but you still feel loved. Yeah that's how I felt here.
Amazing! Food that makes your soul happy! The catfish and Mac is extremely delicious! Can't wait to  visit again!

Decided to have brunch today at our fine neighborhood dining establishment. The fried chicken omelette with grits was absolutely delicious. Hydrated with green apple Kool-Aid; sour with a hint of sweetness yet very refreshing. The ambience and service was exceptional. Looking forward to another meal there very soon.

The decor was great. The seating was spacious. Everything was very comfortable. You could see your way to the bathroom very easily. There was a 45 minute wait, so they took our cell number and they texted us when it was ready. That was very convenient. We each got a drink. I got the New Orleans sweet tea and my husband got a vodka soda. We had to wait a bit to get our drinks, but both were very good. The server took our order. My husband got the hot chicken sandwich and I ordered the catfish with red beans and rice and green beans. It was taking a long time. I noticed people that were seated after us getting their food. I told my husband that I thought something happened with our order. A bit later our server came back and asked us what we had ordered. We told her again. No more green beans, so I ordered potato salad. Apparently, something did happen to our order. Once she took our order the second time our food came out quickly. It was so good! My husband asked for napkins. There weren't any on our table. I think she forgot. We finished pretty quickly. Then we waited for a very, very, very long time to get our check. I could see people waiting to get a seat and felt bad, because we were ready, but we were forgotten again. Everyone was nice, the place was beautiful, and the food was great. We just kinda felt forgotten. Otherwise it would have been 5 stars.

This one star is for the one sever actually came over to help us. We had a party of 5 2 adults 3 kids. We checked in at 1:24pm and didn't leave until 4 We waited a hr just to get seated which was expected. After we got seated we waiting nearly 45min before someone spoke to us. We kept trying to wave down someone to help us but they just kept passing us up. I seen some Of the servers standing around in a group Laughing so finally got up and asked why we haven't been helped we haven't even been offered water and it's been over 30 min. Everyone seemed confused. Someone Came over and finally helped us. She took our order we ordered deep fried deviled eggs (which she did comp)ox tails,shrimp and grits and two kids meals and then we wait another 30 min just to get our food. ( we also ordered two Togo orders but he didn't order those until we got our other food) those came out really fast because the place was empty by the time we got served. People who had been waiting to be seated after us had already got their  food and were basically finished by the time we got ours. The sever that served us was not the server for our section but she was extremely polite and helpful. Once we got our bill we asked our sever what happened and why were we waiting so long. She said that there was a large party in our section and the sever that was suppose to serve us asked another person to help us and they said no she then asked another person and they said no. So I guess at that point there was no one to serve us. The owner was there as all this was happing. You would think that they would be on their P'S and Q's while he was there but I guess not! aside from that the food was great the one server (sahdiah) I think was her name. Is the reason why we might come back she kinda save it. I'm not saying don't got eat there but there service could be a hit or miss. 

Team work makes the dream work.
Me and my family had a blast Nik the server was incredibly nice and attentive I would recommend this place to ANYONE. Family spot forsure me and my husband will be back for sure!!

Ive lived in Sac for quite some time now, and I am so delighted by Fixins as a new part of Sac's soul. I mean literally! This food is soo soul filling and we NEEDED this place! For our bellies and our community! I am rating this place a five for a few reasons. The customer service is unmatched. The last few times I have been in a wonderful waitress named Jena I believe has helped me and my family. You can tell her passion about Fixins. The more questions we asked her about the place, the more she lit up! It felt rare to meet someone so joyful as a server. Now let's get back to the food... mouth watering is an understatement!! I dont do reviews.. But this place. I had to. Some of the best soul food in the country!! And I went to school in the South! Highly recommend and a ten outta ten!

A colleague told me this was a really good place to try out so we did.  As it's still covid season, we opted to order ahead and pick it up.  The order was 10 minutes late. The hostess, Nikki, was pleasant and when I was checking in to let her know we were there she indicated we'd receive a text when the food was ready. We finally got the food and headed home. Upon arriving at home, mouth watering from the smell of the food in the car, we were shocked to see that 1/3 of the order was missing. We called Fixins right away and spoke with Nikki who said John, the manager, was on a call but would call me back, and we agreed that 20 minutes was sufficient time to wait. John did NOT call me back but I did call back. John apologized for the missing food and since we were over 20 minutes away said he'd "comp the meal". He comped the missing order and 2 desserts.  So for service... lots of room for improvement. The food was just ok.  I'm hoping the dessert is better as I love peach cobbler. Red beans n rice was bland, collard greens flavorful. Mac n cheese was ok. Chicken was average and catfish above average as well.
Dope spot. They will have bro Sunday ticket starting week 2 of nfl. Props to Kevin for getting don Julio 1942!!
Waffle was thinner than a pancake and but a little thicker than a crepe.  Fried chicken while moist lacked seasoning. Not sure if I would give them a second chance.

The wait time was comfortable. Service was great; thank you, Ness. I wish the coffee was a little stronger and the waffle thicker. Excellent chicken, very tasty and crispy. The atmosphere was nice and the music pleasing to my ears. Enjoyed brief chat with owner regarding Oak Park and SacHi. Yes, I will visit the restaurant again and bring a friend or family member(s).

1st time visit. Service was wonderful. The fried chicken was moist and flavorful, but the breading was tough to bite into. The potato salad and biscuits w/ honey-butter were excellent. The creamed corn was a little spicy for my taste. My dining companion said it was the best catfish she ever had. Will definitely be back.

Everything I tasted during my visit to Fixins was delicious! The fried chicken and the collard greens were amazing and definitely the best biscuits ever! I can't wait to return and will recommend this spot to everyone.

I am torn, and yet 24 hours and a phone call to verify my order later, I am pissed!  Yesterday a friend shared w/me an app to find black owned businesses in Sacramento, we find "Fixins".  Hungry, we take off to find the resto.  Finding it, we're further excited as the resto is full of people, lines outside-- food must be good.  I go inside to check on the wait time, an hour, ask if we can order to go, we can but online only.  I take the menu, we order: Catfish, and you get two sides (fries and red beans/rice) $19!!  The other item was oxtail.  We rush back, excited and hungry, open the box to find ONE (1) catfish filet and a small cup for the beans/rice, and a second good size container for the fries-- and 3 hushpuppies.  ONE (1) lonely fish filet, i thought it was a mistake.  Had to be.  I'm from Chicago-- J&J fish, any number of fish joints.  I recently lived in Texas-- Pappadeaux restaurant.  Firstly, CATFISH in the neighborhood in chicago aint $20.  Maybe half, probably less for 2-3 pieces.  At Pappadeaux, catfish dinner w/lots of redbeans/rice has 2 LARGE pieces isn't $20.  It was good, but I spent $20 for catfish and I spent $20 and was hungry after because there was ONE (1) lonely piece.  I Support Black Business.  But when I called back this morning to see if a mistake was made w/the order, she corrected me to say that the Catfish order comes w/ONE (1) piece.. TWENTY DOLLARS!!  Whereas I sought out a black business to patronize, I will never return.

Ordered some takeout for New Year' Eve yesterday, after my wife's eldest son gave us a gift certificate for Fixins as a Christmas gift. My wife had the 2-piece fried chicken with a couple sides; I had the shrimp and grits (a favorite of mine since I visited the Deep South for the first time three years ago),  and couple pieces of chicken on the side. We also ordered biscuits and banana pudding. They were really busy, so our food was a little late, but that's understandable given the holiday, and the chow was well worth a few additional minutes' wait. Wow. Their shrimp and grits was amazing. The sauce was perfectly seasoned -- very spicy without being hot. And the chicken -- normally I avoid restaurant fried chicken because it always reminds me of the chicken you used to get in TV dinners: scrawny chicken parts disguised with generic batter. Fixing wasn't like that at all. The chicken parts were plump and juicy and the batter flavorful. It wasn't the least bit greasy, either. My wife's son, who's a chef, says Fixins is his favorite Sacramento restaurant right now. I can see why. I think it may be mine too, although it could really mess with my 65-year-old waistline if I indulge too often. Anyway, it was a great dinner.
