We're looking forward to the place opening up for dine-in. The room and bar area looked really nice. One thing: I had originally intended to have the gumbo, which was highly recommended by my wife's son, but it wasn't on the menu when we ordered. Fixins said it was taken off the menu so they could work on the recipe, but that it would be back. The sooner the better.

I was slightly disappointed. My first experience was not a pleasant one. First, our wait was more than an hour, which is understandable due to it being the grand opening. Second, we were finally able to sit at the bar area but was told we HAD to purchase a drink or an appetizer to just sit there, slightly understandable, so I purchased a beverage and the fried green tomatoes (which were not so great). Finally, I was able to be seated and I wanted the check, so I got the bar hosts attention, and I let him know that I was going to my seat, he said nothing got my check, and proceeded to SNATCH my credit card out of my hand, and thew the recept and pen back at me, it was so obvious that the ladt that was sitting beside me made the comment of how non-personable this person was. That pretty much sealed the deal for me not to return. Thirdly, once seated, it took about 20  minutes for wait staff to come take our order, we order, food came and was disappointed in the portion size for the price we paid, it was about a cup full of gumbo in huge bowl.. very disappointing... I would like to think that the owner would not want to keep this type of service.

I'm giving 1 star only for their Mac and cheese but save your money. I'm from nyc. My older family members are from the south. NC SC GA. I know good soul food. This is not it. I hate to base my experience on one time so I went again tried something different even brought a friend so she could try something as well. The food taste horrible. I'm mad I spent all together about $90 just for two visit. Don't get the smothered chicken . I took two bites and couldn't finished my waitress didn't even offer to take it off my bill and sad part there was a couple next to me who  had the same thing and said the same thing. They was smart to ask for it off the bill smh. Second visit I did what they said. Their ox tails. And still horrible. A weird taste and I love ox tails. My friend had the catfish. Not good at all it just didn't even taste like real fish the batter was just off. I don't know who's cooking their food. But they lack skills completely I can make better soul food but sometimes you just want someone else cooking.

A welcome addition to Sacramento's dining scene. Finally a soul food restaurant with a vibrant hip ambiance, super friendly staff, and reasonable priced menu that is affordable and easy on the pocketbook. Fried chicken, oxtail, fried devil eggs, potato salad and homemade buttermilk biscuits to die for and among my favorites. All entrees priced less than 20.00 if I remember correctly and portions are perfect for the average eater. My husband had the burger and he said it was one of the best fresh patty 8 oz burger he has ever tasted.  Can't forget the Mac n cheese either.  They could expand their dessert and drink menu as the choices are limited.  best of all if you are lucky you can catch former Mayor Kevin Johnson walking around checking to see how you like the food and I would expect you will find some Kings players around from time to time.  Bobby Jackson was visiting when we dined here two days ago. Warning there are lines to get in during popular dining hours but the wait is well it for a fun and good eating experience.

This place needs a business management team and chefs that use seasoning. You walk in, everyone's sitting at a table after their 2+ hour wait, no food in sight and you can't smell any food being cooked. I never heard of a soul food place with no ribs, bbq etc. The greens are sweet and not very tasty, Mac and cheese was good, chicken had no flavor and tastes air fried, rice and beans at Popeyes has more flavor, candied yams were good, catfish was pretty good and potato salad was good as well. Aside from the 1hr and 30 minutes wait time and lack of seasoning the servers are kind, but they definitely need to rethink the menu, seating arrangement situation and offer food to be ordered while waiting, they discouraged it and wouldn't allow us to place order during wait because the "chef cooks everything from scratch" apparently they need to increase the number of chefs to help increase service. Oh & add some damn bbq please!

PISS, POOR customer service, the food ain't even all that! LOL, let's start there. Not to mention the "manager" Brian running the establishment while Kevin is gone is RACIST AF! YUCK!! You couldn't PAY me to eat here again. I would NEVER spend anymore of my hard earned money at this OVER-PRICED, FAKE BOOGIE, gentrified and corny establishment EVER again. And I will make sure that ALL my friends and family know to STAY FAR away from this business as well! They're RUDE, ALWAYS behind on their orders, then when the food FINALLY comes out it's lukewarm/cold, food is bland and has very little flavor, fried chicken you can tell isn't made to order, less than satisfactory customer service is an UNDERSTATEMENT and they don't know how to talk to their patrons. Getting a discount or a comped meal due to errors on their behalf and unusually long wait times is like pulling teeth. Stay away from this business, they don't understand what SUPREME customer service is-- a quality that is needed to RETAIN your consumer. They're argumentative when in the wrong and DEFENSIVE not wanting to own their mistakes. All they care about is money. Thee WORSE. **thumbs down**

Thank you Kevin Johnson for bringing this hip restaurant and the best soul food I have had since leaving Newark, NJ almost 8 years ago.  If this was your dream, your dream has come true. Steven ( M. and mom) and I go often and I think I have had almost everything on your menu. I can't name a favorite.  I brought about 25 plus, members of the Renaissance Society before the pandemic, and turned them on to soul food. I know of many who have returned with their friends and family.  During this pandemic, one of the few places I will go is Fixin's .  The food is great , service is spot on, and outside seating is fun, distancing and masks are adhered to, There is a little problem with visiting flies, apparently who also love the food.  One suggestion that I would love: it would be nice to be handed 2 hand wipes ( 60% or more alcohol) when being seated. One to be used before eating and one when finished with the meal.   I highly recommend this restaurant to everyone, and especially if you crave soul food with all the fixin's .

I'm fixin to tell you about my meal at Fixins Soul Kitchen.  Warm inviting atmosphere, comfortable and cool vibe all at the same time. Food smelled amazing in the restaurant and I have a terrible sense of smell only smell really good stuff or really bad stuff.  I couldn't decide what to order, I watched every plate as they came out the kitchen to the customers.  I wanted everything from breakfast, to appetizers, to sides and a meal. I decided on appetizer and Mac and Cheese severed in a cute cocotte pot  it looked similar to what my mom made growing up and the taste took me right back home again at my moms dinner table. I almost forgot the cornbread which tasted like cake it was so good.  Probably why I forgot to order dessert. The bill came and I Thought I'm not fixin to leave without dessert.  I decided to try the lemon bars, presentation was everything and the taste just melted in my mouth with a side of fresh whip cream.  It's my new favorite spot! And to think I almost settled for the standard chain restaurants to grab a bite to eat. If you Fixin to grab a bite to eat try something made from scratch at Fixins Soul Kitchen. We also saw KJ as he was fixin to leave.

What restaurant in the 21st century does dine in only?! I'm so upset. I've been trying to come here for literally weeks but the lines are always crazy. I finally come downtown in the morning hoping to pick up breakfast to take back home to my family. I call ahead of time to place an order and I was told sorry we aren't doing to-go orders right now. So I drive 15 minutes to the restraunt, waste another 10 minutes trying to find parking, and then this crap. I'm seriously like wtf?!! You are in SACRAMENTO, the farm to fork capital of the WORLD and you won't let customer enjoy the food how they please whether at the restaurant or at home with their families. Sad as I can imagine the food is great based on the reviews. But I have three kids under the age of three and trying to go to a restaurant is a nightmare. Let alone when they start crying and getting upset it takes away from the experience of others dining in. Well, I guess is one restaurant I won't get to try...

I have a message for those few complained about the wait time. In order to get GOOD southern food you may have to wait sometimes. For this restaurant to put out such good food in the amount of time they bring it out is remarkable (especially for a new place) are you kidding me. You just can't throw GOOD southern food together it is a real art it takes patients and care to put this kind of food together and there is no way around it. If you want GOOD southern food bring some patience with you I know your universe doesn't want to hear that but that is the way it is that is why this food is so special. I have been to this restaurant  10 times, sometimes my food came out fast, (which is most of the time) other times I had to wait a little. There is no other place in Sacramento where you can find Southern food like this so let's support it. And for those who say, "I am going to give them another chance give them as many chances you have given other restaurants over the years who don't support our community and not in our community and don't hire within our community.
this is going to be oak parks #1 sports bar & restaurant. all of the food is par excellance!! nothing was left on a single plate. im going back again.

I chose Fixins as a mid point for my son, his family and my grandsons to meet for brunch. We were a party of 10. We were told it would be a 30 minute wait so we waited. It turned out to be over an hour because the previous party at the large table was buying merchandise. We finally got the table but it took a while for anyone to ask us if we wanted anything to drink. Someone finally took our order at it was a good 30 minutes before we got our food. Then the owner sat someone at our table without even asking if it was okay for him to join us. It was perfectly fine but it was upsetting because they couldn't ask the people before us to look at the merchandise some where else. When we got the billed was $125. We knew that wasn't right for a party of 10 so we alerted the waitress. No thank you for being honest just a bill with the gratuity added. It was Bay Area price without the service. As I said, the food was amazing but they ti step up the service.

Great restaurant! Menu was short and easy to read.  I ordered the Catfish, and Fried Green Tomatoes for my appetizer which taste great. We waited over 15 minutes for the appetizer and our meal followed about 10 minutes later.  Needless to say our table was crowded.  I also ordered Lemonade and sweet Ice Tea wow was it sweet.  So sweet we couldn't finish it.  We received the bill and paid with a credit card. There was no receipt accompanying the credit card.  We asked the waiter for a receipt which he responded that he would get. It took him about 20 minutes. We grew tired of waiting and got up and walked towards the door. I just happened to see him waiting other tables and asked did he have our receipt. He quickly responded "oh yes, as he removed it from his pocket.  This is an easy fix...when a customer is paying, the waiter should either ask if we want a receipt or deliver the receipt to the customer along with the credit card and/or cash.  Overall our experience was awesome! Food was fresh and tasty!

This was honestly one of the worst dining experiences I've had in a long time. I actually won't even call it a dining experience because I didn't even make it that far. I came into this place to eat with my mother and younger brother. I should have realized something was wrong with this place when I walked in and saw at least a dozen empty tables yet there was still a group of people waiting to be seated. After about 5 minutes the hostess stumbles out of the back room and came to the front. She took an account of the people that were waiting to be seated and apparently they text you when your table is ready. Mind you, I can see at least 10 clean, clear, open tables that seat 4. We had to wait an additional 5-10 minutes for the hostess to tap into her tablet that we needed to be seated. And we make it to a table finally and we're given menus. We were there for at least another 20 minutes and they hadn't even taken our drink orders or at least brought glasses of water to the table. Completely ridiculous, wasted over 40 minutes waiting for ridiculously slow service. It's not as if they were understaffed or overbooked, they're simply careless and inattentive. Needless to say, I walked my family out of there and I wouldn't return because of the poor service alone.

I am being lenient on this review because they are New. Every restaurant has to go through their growing pains. I don't think they were anticipating the high volume of customers. To begin with the high notes, the fried chicken was good. The mac and cheese was great. The collard greens were great. That was enough because we had quite the wait, so monitored our place in line with the Yelp app while having cocktails at the adjacent Mexican restaurant. We would come back again after the hype slows down and after they are more fully staffed. His mother was a charm and very hospitable. There was one upset customer who verbalized her sentiments tonight and you could hear a pin drop. If the manager on duty would have simply been the more rational one, there would have been less of a scene perhaps. Sometimes it is ok to lose, and just give the customer her money back ... if that was the main issue. Know when to keep the peace. We thought a riot would have ensued if she kept going. Just saying. Rough Saturday, but our waiter had much heart and everyone seemed to try their best, though the kitchen was obviously behind. Keep working. Teamwork can make the dream work.

We have had an opportunity to have both Lunch/Dinner here on several occasions. 8 times to be exact.The first time..the food and experience was absolutely beyond amazing! Fried devil eggs..fried green tomatoes..the fried fish..oxtails..greens..potato salad..Fried chicken..mac and cheese..peach cobbler..incredibly delicious! The banana pudding is truly an insult to the culture. Seriously. The peace cobbler was insanely delicious. And then it all went down hill from there. Every time we go..seems something is always off. My guess is that someone different is clearly cooking each day? One day the sides are amazing. And the next..It's like who in the heck made this?! It's terribly disappointing to drive 40 minutes..and be completely unhappy with the food. Example. I've eaten at the Cheesecake Factory for over 10 years. I always order the exact same thing. Each and every time it's beyond on point. Perhaps..designate one or two people to cook/bake specific things. That way it'll never disappoint. I will say that the staff is incredibly friendly.

Amazing food, and everything we had taste great. No complaints except for the mix up when we arrived. Originally, when I checked in they told us the wait time would be 2 hours due to a mix up in the kitchen. So we checked in, set our timers and decided to take a walk around Oak Park. However, about 45 minutes later they text and was like your table is ready. It was going to take about 15 minutes to get back, so we called and we lost our table. We had to get back on the list again and we waited about another hour. However, the food was well worth the wait and our server was great. The owner (Mr. Johnson) was friendly, walking around, talking to guest, taking photos with those who requested, and thanking everyone for waiting. The whole entire vibe was awesome, and I forgot about how long we waited. Good service is hard to find and we were very pleasantly surprised. We will be back the next time we are in Sacramento.

The food was great and it came out quick. I originally came for banana pudding but was told that I couldn't place an order to go as they aren't setup to do so. So I asked to be seated. I was provided a menu and decided to take a look at it. I schemed through it and saw the smothered chicken breast and decided to order that as well. After I sat no one came to me for about 10 mins or more which was odd and rude as I was sitting alone and in the middle of the restaurant so I'm sure I couldn't be missed. Finally the bartender approached me and asked if I was ready to order I let him know I was and placed my order for the red kool-aid and smothered chicken and the banana pudding to come out last. Once I ordered the food came out quick and was pretty good. Along with the smothered chicken I had the option to choose two sides, I ordered Greens and Rice, the Greens I wasn't too much of a fan of because they were sweet. The chicken was good and the rice was cooked just right. And of course what I came for was good, the banana pudding although I asked for a container to take it with me in I sort of wish I hadn't because when she took it back the container didn't seem to me as if it held the amount the was brought out to me. Anyways great establishment and great music and vibes. I'll be coming back again just hope. They're more attentive to me after I've been seated.
